text: “Ma sumi Koosa, ma ta sumi a li gbampulim ni. Nara bɔnɛ daŋni ba chiŋi yau ni awa ba bar lagmi dɛɛni nɛɛ a sumi Koosa di nara na ba a pɛgi ba. Ma nu mu sɛma. Chenani nara pɛgi ba ya ba papaha,

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.

text: O fun ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Ba funɛ pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Baal ŋɔ, baŋ nibine ya un? Hali o koti a sɛm sɛma peha awa ni wati a nu o nɔ.” Yesu Fun Tiibi Alizin Tagna a Li Nara Balɛ Ni (Maak

text: O gbanko funɛ lisi Andiru, Filip, Batɔlɔmi, Matiu, Tɔmas, awa Jems, Alfius be, Tadius, Simɔn naa o pulum wɛ fun nyɛn o bambi ni,

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta bɔnɛ fun li Jɛrusalɛm a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Biɛlzɛbub, naa ya alizin tagna maga koora, nyɛn un ni a la nyɛ un kpok saha maga, chen ni o zi kotiu a duŋni alizin tagni ta a li nara ni.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Ba fun wa zu o wati a ŋɔ un di o niin ti awa o niinbisi ta nyɛn dentil ni a pɛɛsi o wɛ.

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Yesu fun yir ba a jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a nyɛ ba a pɛɛsi ba, “Yɛrɛ sintana kaa ya duŋni o titi a ta?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Zɛmɛ kinkan bur lagmi a wa. Hali Yesu awa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta wa fun na nyinbaan a di diwe.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Tɔhɔn tima vɛ kpak nɔ bɛgim, ba dɔma wa zi wa a chɔk ba tɔhɔn tii?

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Dam bɛgim nara burgi a yo dɔŋa, chen dam ti zi pisiu a chel.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Ma vɛ te kpak nikpok a vɔwi, yɛrɛ ma kaa ya a zu o dama a fuksi o kina?

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: “Ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma nan. Koosa koti a gil nara tum tagna maga awa chenani ba vɛ ya un jirima a nyɛ ba.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Nara vɛ nyɛ Koosa siim weri ti jirima, Koosa wa zi gil chen a nyɛ ba, hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: O funɛ sɛm a nama domi bɔnɛ ŋɔ di alizin tagin kani o nyuhu ni. Yesu Nitiŋɛ Ya O Wuti Walɛ (Mat.

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Doone Yesu niina awa o niinbisa fun wa tel a wa chiŋi dam ti dentil ni a tum nara di ba zu a yir un.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ kani a go un, o fun pɛɛsi ba, “Anti ya mu niin wuti awa mu niinbisa?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: O fun nyini nibinsa ba naa kanɛ a go un chen a ŋɔ, “Ma na mu numa awa mu niinbisi ta nɛɛnɛ.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Ba naa maga la tum chenani Koosa daŋni ba tum, ba waa ya mu numi ta awa mu niinbisi ta.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Yesu fun bu wa bɛɛŋ ti a biksi nara Koosa wɛ. Zɛmɛ kinkan fun wa o wati a wa go un. Domi ba funɛ iiu kinkan, o fun a nyini arŋ bar nyɛni a la zu, amaa zɛmɛ ta wa funɛ chiŋi jibaŋ nɛɛ.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: O funɛ zaasi chene, kiduhu ti bɔnɛ fun chesi nyinbaan ni. Zimebisa fun wa pini a naa chesi nyinbaan ni chen maga a di.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: O fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a biksi kidamata a nyɛ ba a ŋɔ,

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon a fun si a kaali o ŋmaha di o la zaasi kiduha.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Bɔnɛ ka fun chesi buŋkul tuhu ni. Hɛɛra wa fun to dooni kinkan. Chendaha a fun nyu lalaga.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Saha naa nha fun wa num a kpak a, a fun fiimi a siti. A wa fun koti a ta luta nɛnɛ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Kiduhu ti bɔnɛ ka fun chesi sɔta bar nyɛni ni a nyu a zini. Sɔti ta gbanko fun nyu a zini a tɔ a. Chendaha a wa fun koti a lul.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu fun bu pɛɛsi ba, “Baŋ kinnɛ ya zi koti a kpa lagmi Koosa koor ti ni a nyini a kpa maksi a koor ti?

ref: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
text: O funɛ wa ka o duŋduŋ, ba naa funɛ tiŋ un awa o gantɛl tiŋsa fidalɛ ta, ba fun wa a wa pɛɛsi un o jɛɛge ta mun.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: amaa laaboor weri ti wa fun ta lur ta ba ni. Ba funɛ tiŋ tamnɛ, amaa saha naa namsi wa kpak ba chen laaboor weri ti daha, ba vɛ un a bu vɛ tiŋi.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: ‘Chen ni ba zi nuu aa kidaŋni ba zim a muna amaa ba wa kizim. Ba zim a muna, ba bambe zi chɛrgiu, chen ni Koosa zi gil ba tum tagna a nyɛ ba.’ ” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Nipari Naa Zaasi Kiduha Chen Mun (Mat.

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Kiduhu naa funɛ chesi nyinbaan ni chen chi ba naa nu, amaa sintana funɛ siu lalaga a wa yisi laaboor ti a li ba bambe ni.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Baal naa funɛ zaasi kiduhu ti chen, Koosa laaboor weri tirɛ o fun a zaasi chen.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Yesu fun pɛɛsi ba, “Nar suk fitina, o zi bu kpa tempile a chik un niu koo o zi kpa un a chiŋsi o bar chiŋi niu o chaani kinkann?

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: “Maa nu chenani n kaa ŋɔ ma nan. Ma doso a nu mu sɛma a gbaari, Koosa zi suŋi mau ma zim a gbaari.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ di, “Koosa koor ti chi nipari naa funɛ si a la o ŋmaha di o la zaasi o kina chene.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Tɛɛnɛ ti titi yaa chɛrgi kiduhu ti a nyu a zini. A waa zini a peti chen, a zi kpaksi loona a lulsi.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: A wa bi a mak kumi chen, ba kpa chisa a kaali ŋmaha ti a la kum a.” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Kidusi Maga Kiwewe Wɛ (Mat.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: O chi kiduha naa ba yir ‘mastad’ ba yaa kpa aa du chene. Waa ya kidusi maga kiwewe.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: A gantɛl ni a si a zen a kaali bugil pum maga kɔha ni. Zimebisa yaa wa du a zaŋa a hulsi ni, domi a funɛ zenu a kpak basɔn were.”

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: O waa ya biksi ba wu digi a gbaari, see jɛɛgɛ duŋduŋ. O wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ nyɛn ba duŋduŋ, o fun a biksi ba a maga muna. Yesu Fun Jiŋ Peha (Mat.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: A wuzu ti nha funɛ wa chel, o fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Ma nyɛ ya la ji bɛɛŋ ti jibaŋ kinkin ti.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Yesu fun a jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ kinkan a nyɛ ba a biksi ba Koosa wɛ a chi chen, hali ba zima wa peti.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun gil zɛmɛ ta a zu pu Yesu arŋ tuhu ni ba kaali. Bɔnɛ gbanko fun zu arŋ bɔnɛ a tiŋ un.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Ba funɛ la chene, pe zen digi fun wa si a viigi. O fun a mo arŋ ti kinkan, a teŋ ni ti a du arŋ ti tuhu ni o daŋni o lim.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Yesu fun chɔ arŋ gantɛl ni a sul nyusula a chɔ do. O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun laasi un a pɛɛsi, “Ya kunyana, ya daŋni ya sou. I bak wa to ya nii?”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Yesu fun pɛɛsi o gantɛl tiŋsi ta, “Yɛrɛ maa ɔm chen ni? Ma se wa su minn?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: O fun a ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Ba funɛ pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Baal nɔ, baŋ nibine ya un? Hali o koti a sɛm sɛma peha awa ni wati a nu o nɔ.”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: “N fu koti a la gbaŋsi o kilaari nɔ, n fu zi na alaafɛu.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “N be, i si suuli nyɛ i na alaafɛu. Kila dama awa tu sɔɔn ni. I wichɔɔrti ti petiu. O wa zi bu bur wi.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Hali luri tima nidamata fun a tiibi un a wa ku. O fun chɔk o malɛ maga ka o nin ti vɛ peti a fun bukoo gbaari.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Yesu fun la zu dam ti a pɛɛsi ba, “Yɛrɛ ma chɛɛmi a la wi? Be ti wa sou. O chɔ dore.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Ba funɛ la ji bɛɛŋ jibaŋ kinkin ti, ba fun wa tel Gadaria tima pulum.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Nandigi wa fun koti a vɔu un. Ba yaa fun kpa zɔglɛ zenewa a vɔu un. O yaa fun a kasi a.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Ba yaa fun kpa zɔglɛ a vɔti o naŋgere awa o nisa hali ba wa ku, amaa o yaa fun kasi a magau. Nandigi wa fun kpak kpok a kaali un.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: O fun chɔ kaali di o la nyɛn doonani ba yaa sogli ba nawaalsa chen awa kuŋkule nyuuna bar nyɛni ni saha maga, a la huusi a zer buŋkula aa teŋsi o titi.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: O funɛ sumi Yesu kinkan di o ta duŋni a a li ba pulum ni.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Kurchusa funɛ nyɛn kuŋkule nyuhu ni a di diwe. A funɛ tel tusugsa lɛu.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Alizin tagni ta fun a sumi Yesu di o nyɛ a la zu kurchusi ta ni.

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Yesu fun nyɛ a nyinbaan. A fun li a la zu kurchusi ta ni. Kurchusi ta fun si a chɔ a la wilsi a chesi bɛɛŋ ni ti ni. Ni ti fun di a maga.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyini kurchusi ta chen, ba fun chɔ kaali dama a la ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni awa ba naa ba pu kɔha ti ni. Tɔhɔn ti tima fun li a wa Yesu wati di ba la na kin naa yɛɛ.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Ba fun wa na baal naa alizin tagna kinkan fun kani un ni chen laari gar a kani Yesu wati. O wa fun bu a zu galɛŋa. Hansura fun kpak ba.

ref: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni a na chenani funɛ ya alizin tagni ta awa kurchusi ta, ba fun ŋɔ ba naa wa chen.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Ba funɛ nu ba sɛm chen, ba wa Yesu wati a wa sumi un di o li ba pulum ni.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Aai. Kila i dam walɛ wati a la ŋɔ ba chenani i kunyana suŋi hin a ɔm hin siki.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: O fun kaali a la ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn Dɛkapɔlis pulum wati chenani Yesu fun ya nyɛ un. O ya ba maga alaazibu. Yesu Fun Tiibi Ju Tim Nihɛɛ Nihabichɛla (Mat.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Ba fun a mɔmsi un. O fun duŋni ba a ta, a kpa be ti nyana awa o niina awa o gantɛl tiŋsa nara batora duŋduŋ a la zu be ti dɛ chɔɔli ti. Be ti funɛ ya bun fidalɛwa.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yesu fun zu arŋa a la ji bɛɛŋ ti jibaŋ kinkin ti. O funɛ la tel dooni, zɛmɛ kinkan fun wa a wa go un bɛɛŋ ti nɔ ni.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ba bar lagmi dɛ nihɛɛ digi funɛ gbaari zɛmɛ ta ni. O sɔn funɛ ya Jairus. Saha naa o fun wa na Yesu, o fun la gbiri o suwachak ni,

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: a sumi un kinkan a ŋɔ, “Mu nihabichɛla la wi a daŋni o sou. Mu sumi hinu kinkan, kuwa a wa kpa i nisa a sak un ni, chen ni o zi na alaafɛu.”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Yesu fun tiŋ Jairus a kaali. Nara kinkan gbanko funɛ tiŋ un aa zaksi un.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Nihaan digi funɛ nyɛn doon a kpak wichɔɔr digi bun fidalɛ. O fun ya nin ka o vɛ peti.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: O fun a nu Yesu wɛ a la lisi o tuhu ni,

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Yesu fun zim di nandigi gbaŋsi unu domi o kpok ti bar digi funɛ liu. O fun burgi o gantɛl a pɛɛsi, “Anti gbaŋsi mu takta nan ni?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun burgi a pɛɛsi un, “I waa na zɛmɛ ta la murgi nan ni ka i pɛɛsi ya di, anti gbaŋsi hinu?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Yesu ha fun vɛɛn a nyini a go di o na nar gbaŋsi un chen.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Hansura fun kpak haan ti kinkan. O bara fun a jigiri. O fun zim di Yesu tiibi unu a fun wa gbiri o suwachak ni a sɛm o tuha chaani maga a nyɛ un.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Yesu ha funɛ vɛɛn a sɛm, nara bɔnɛ fun a li ba bar lagmi dɛ nihɛɛ dama a wa nihɛɛ ti wati a ŋɔ un, “I be nihabichɛl ti sowu. Ta bu muksi nibiksi ti di o waali.”

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu fun nu chenani ba sɛm chen a ŋɔ nihɛɛ ti, “Ta nyɛ o muksi hin. Nyɛ i si su min.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Yesu wa fun a daŋni nandigi tiŋ un amaa a nyɛ Pita, Jems awa o niinbe Jɔn ba duŋduŋ funɛ tiŋ un kaali.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Ba funɛ la tel ba bar lagmi dɛ nihɛɛ ti dama, ba fun a nu ba chɛɛma ka ba wi kinkan.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ di ba ya diwe a nyɛ be ti ni o di, a fun ŋɔ ba kpok ni, “Ma ta nyɛ nandigi zim kinkin naa yɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Ba fun la ji jibaŋ kinkin ti a fun la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Gɛnɛsarɛt a kpa arŋ ti a vɔu dooni.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Nara maga nyɛ Koosa witiina jirimau, amaa o sɛmbiri tɔhɔn nara wa o dam walɛ awa o lul, ba wara nyɛ un jirima.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Koor zen digi funɛ nyɛn doon. Ba funɛ yir un Hɛrɔd. O funɛ nu Yesu wɛ, o fun ŋɔ o titi ni, “Baal nɔ wa Jɔn naa la chaksi nara chen ya nibɛgimnɛ. Mu nyɛ ba fun teŋ o nyuha, amaa o bur si o sou niu.”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Yesu nitumɛ fidalɛ, ba naa o fun tum chen, bur wa a ŋɔ un kin naa maga ba tum awa kin naa maga ba biksi nara ta.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn dooni, ba hiisi wuzu fun wa tel. O fun kaali a la zu ba bar lagmi dɛ a biksi nara Koosa wɛ. Nara kinkan funɛ nyɛn doon a nu o biksi ti. O ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Ba funɛ pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Leene baal nɔ pu sɛm nama maga? Ka leene o vɛɛn pu zima nɔ? Ka yɛrɛ o zi yaa ya a tum o tum zene nama?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: O laata funɛ ya bugilsal a nyɛn bar nɔ ni chenn? O niina laata ya Mɛri chenn? Jems, Josɛf, Judas awa Simɔn laata ya o niinbisa chenn? Ya wa o toosa laata lagmi nyɛnn?” Ba wa un wa fun lɛm dɔŋ ni, domi ba funɛ zim unu.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma wa kaa la, ma fiɛŋ ma zer buna a chi diwe, malɛ awa kapursa.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Amaa ma wa zu tɔ digi ka tɔhɔn ti nara vɛ la ma duuli koo a vɛ nu nyɛ ma, ma li dooni, a piisi ma naasa hɛɛra a ta, chen ni ba zi zim di a li ba nɛɛ. O wa li ma ni.”

ref: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
text: Jɔn fun te ŋɔ un, “Chenani i ya chen, o wa wer.” O funɛ sɛm chen, Hɛrɔdias fun nyɛ Hɛrɔd tum nara ba la kpak Jɔn a tɔ un dɛ.

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Doone Hɛrɔdias fun a ha Jɔn a daŋ o kpu un.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Hɛrɔd funɛ ɔm Jɔnu a la nyɛ un jirima, domi o funɛ zim di Jɔn ya nibin werere. Adaha o fun vɛ la nɔɔni di ba kpu un. Hali Jɔn sɛm ta fun muksi un kinkan, amaa o ha funɛ vɛɛn a daŋni o nu o sɛm tau.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”

text: Ba tum nɛɛ ma zi wa zim ba naa se su min. Ba zi koti a kpa mu sɔnu a duŋni alizin tagna a li nara ni a bu sɛm sɛm falɛ,

ref: Many people have tried to tell the story of what God has done among us.
text: Min, Luk, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ mu kunyan Tɛɔfilus ni nan. Nidamata funɛ kpaŋi ba titiu a sɔbi kin naa maga funɛ ya ya wati.

ref: They wrote what we had been told by the ones who were there in the beginning and saw what happened.
text: Ba waa funɛ sɔbi chenani ba nu a li sɛhɛra tima ba naa funɛ piin na a o piili ti ni a fun la biksi nara a wɛ.

ref: So I made a careful study of everything and then decided to write and tell you exactly what took place. Honorable Theophilus,
text: Adaha, mu kunyana, n funɛ kpa laaboor nɔ magau a lagmi dɔŋ ni a nyini a kpa a a chiŋsi a sɛmbiri bar chiŋɛ ni.

ref: I have done this to let you know the truth about what you have heard. An Angel Tells about the Birth of John
text: Mu sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ hin nan, chen ni i zi zim di kin nawara ba fun biksi hin chen sir ya wutirɛ. Koosa Malaikasi Ta Digi Fun Sɛm Chenani Ba Zi Lul Jɔn

ref: When Herod was king of Judea, there was a priest by the name of Zechariah from the priestly group of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was from the family of Aaron.
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon. Ba yir un Zɛkaraya. O funɛ ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa nidigirɛ. O funɛ ya kɔkɔɔrsi ta digi walɛrɛ. Ba funɛ yir un Abia. O haan sɔn funɛ ya Ɛlizabɛt. O nyana gbanko funɛ ya kɔkɔɔrrɛ. Saha naa ba fun nyɛn chen, koor digi funɛ nyɛn doon a dɔ Judia pulum. O sɔn funɛ ya Hɛrɔd.

ref: Both of them were good people and pleased the Lord God by obeying all that he had commanded.
text: O wa o haan ti funɛ ya nibin werewa aa tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ nɛnɛ.

ref: But they did not have children. Elizabeth could not have any, and both Zechariah and Elizabeth were already old.
text: Ba funɛ hɛɛsiu kinkan a vɛ kpak bisa, domi Ɛlizabɛt funɛ ya nihaan barirɛ.

ref: One day Zechariah's group of priests were on duty, and he was serving God as a priest.
text: Wuzu digi o funɛ ya Zɛkaraya tum wuzurɛ ka o fun a tum Koosa tuma a li o bar jɛɛmi dam naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni chen.

ref: All at once an angel from the Lord appeared to Zechariah at the right side of the altar.
text: Zɛkaraya funɛ suk pora ti, Koosa malaikasi ta digi fun wa li o wati a wa chiŋi doonani ba suk pora ti nindil ni.

ref: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
text: Zɛkaraya funɛ na un, hansura fun kpak un.

ref: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
text: Amaa malaika ti funɛ ŋɔ unu, “Zɛkaraya, ta ɔmi. Koosa funɛ nu chenani i sumi un, ka i haan Ɛlizabɛt zi lulu nibabichɛla. O wa lul, i du o sɔn Jɔn.

ref: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
text: O zi nyɛ i wa i haan awa nidamata tusa sɔɔniu kinkan.

ref: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
text: Koosa zi wa nyɛ o ya nibin zenu o se ni. O wa zi wa nyɔ sin kpok, ka Koosa kpa o siim weri a du un ni. Saha naa ba zi wa lul un a kpa la, Koosa siim weri ti zi wa si un niu.

ref: John will lead many people in Israel to turn back to the Lord their God.
text: O zi wa nyɛ Ju tim nidamata bur wa tiŋ ba kunyana Koosau.

ref: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
text: Saha digi Koosa funɛ kpa o kpok a du o witiina Ɛlaija ni a nyɛ o ya o nitumi. Jɔn gbanko zi wa ya Koosa nitumi a chi un. O zi wa kpa bisa awa ba nyamau ba naa waligɛ dɔŋ ni a lagmi. Ba naa vɛ tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan, o zi wa nyɛ ba bur wau a wa tum tum were. O zi wa saksi Koosa narau ba la un.”

ref: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
text: Zɛkaraya fun ŋɔ malaika ti ni, “Yɛrɛ n kaa ya a zim di chenani i sɛm chen ya wutirɛ? Mu wa mu haan ti maga hɛɛsɛu.”

ref: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel, God's servant, and I was sent to tell you this good news.
text: Malaika ti fun ŋɔ un, “N sɔn ya Gabriɛl. N yaa chiŋi Koosa suwachak nɛɛ. Waa ya funɛ tum min di n wa sɛm hin laaboor weri nɔ.

ref: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
text: Chenani mu fun sɛm ma chen zi wa ya wutiu. I si wa su chen. Chendaha i wa zi koti a sɛm sɛma hali wuzu naa kin naa maga kaa wa ya.”

ref: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
text: Malaika ti funɛ sɛm sɛma Zɛkaraya wati chene, zɛmɛ ta funɛ chiŋi sal nɛɛ a pɔ un. Ba funɛ ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Yɛrɛ o vusi dam ti ni chen ni?”

ref: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
text: O tum saha funɛ wa peti a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni, o fun kaali o dama.

ref: Soon after this, his wife was expecting a baby, and for five months she did not leave the house. She said to herself,
text: Chen gantɛl nɛɛ, o haan Ɛlizabɛt fun kpak lo a fun vɛ li o dam ti ni pɛnsa nyuu.

ref: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
text: Doone o fun ŋɔ o chɛnsa maga ni, Koosa funɛ vusi di o suŋi min, amaa lalaknɔ o suŋi minu n na be. O yisɛ n hiisau a li nara se ni. Koosa Malaika Ti Fun Sɛm Chenani Ba Zi Lul Yesu

ref: One month later God sent the angel Gabriel to the town of Nazareth in Galilee
text: Ɛlizabɛt funɛ kpak lo pɛnsa nɔra, Koosa fun tum o malaika Gabriɛl a li tɔhɔn naa ba funɛ yir Nazarɛt. O nyɛn Galili pulum nɛɛ.

ref: with a message for a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to Joseph from the family of King David.
text: Nihabichɛla digi fun nyɛn dooni. Ba funɛ yir un Mɛri. O funɛ la nɔɔniu di o yu baal naa ba yir Josɛf. Josɛf funɛ ya koor Devid be lulirɛ.

ref: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
text: Malaika ti fun wa zaami Mɛri a ŋɔ un, “Koosa dugli hinu a daŋni o suŋi hin.”

ref: Mary was confused by the angel's words and wondered what they meant.
text: Mɛri funɛ nu o sɛm ta, hansura fun kpak un kinkan. O funɛ daŋni o zim o sɛm ta mun.

ref: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
text: Malaika ti fun bukoo ŋɔ un, “Mɛri, ta nyɛ hansura kpak hin. Koosa la daŋni o suŋi hin.

ref: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
text: O zi wa ya nibin zenu kinkan. O zi ya Koosa zen beu. Ya kunyana Koosa zi vɔu un koorau, a chi chenani o fun vɔu o nawaal Devid saha digi chen.

ref: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
text: O zi wa dɔ Jakɔb bi lulɛ magau hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. O koor ti wa zi wa kpak bɔ.”

ref: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
text: Mɛri fun ŋɔ malaika ti ni, “N wa zim nibaal, ka yɛrɛ Koosa kaa ya a nyɛ min lo?”

ref: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come down to you, and God's power will come over you. So your child will be called the holy Son of God.
text: Malaika ti fun ŋɔ un, “Koosa zi wa kpa o siim weri tiu a du hin ni a kpa o kpok a nyɛ hin. Chen la zi wa nyɛ i kpak lo. Be naa i kaa wa lul chen, o zi wa ya tu chaan tiinau a ya Koosa be.

ref: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
text: I funɛ nu chenani ya i towe Ɛlizabɛtu, ba funɛ ŋɔ di o ya nihaan barirɛ. O funɛ ya nihaan hɛɛrɛ, amaa lalaknɔ o kpak lore pɛnsa nɔra.

ref: Nothing is impossible for God!”
text: Koosa kpak kpok a tum tum maga.”

ref: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
text: Mɛri fun ŋɔ un, “Koosa dɔ min. Nyɛ o du min ni a chi chenani i ŋɔ chen.” O funɛ sɛm chen peti, malaika ti fun kaali. Mɛri Fun La Zaami Ɛlizabɛt

ref: A short time later Mary hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea.
text: Mɛri funɛ vaan saksiu di o la tɔhɔn naa kuŋkule nyuuna go un a li Judia pulum ni.

ref: She went into Zechariah's home, where she greeted Elizabeth.
text: O funɛ la tel dooni, o fun la zu Zɛkaraya dama a zaami o haan Ɛlizabɛt.

ref: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby moved within her. The Holy Spirit came upon Elizabeth.
text: Ɛlizabɛt funɛ nu ka Mɛri zaami, o be ti fun si a go o tuha. A saha ti Koosa fun nyɛ o siim weri ti si Ɛlizabɛt ni,

ref: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
text: a ŋɔ Mɛri ni wa ta kpok ni, “Koosa nyɛ hin tu sɔɔna a kaali nihaana maga. I wa lul be, Koosa zi nyɛ o gbanko tu sɔɔnau.

ref: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
text: O wa deŋli mu wati di Koosa be niina wa zu mu dam ti.

ref: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
text: N funɛ nu i zaami ti, n tuha funɛ sɔɔniu ka n be si a go mu tuha.

ref: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
text: I si funɛ su di chenani Koosa fun ŋɔ hin chen zi yau, o sir ya wuti. Chendaha i tuha gbanko sɔɔni.” Mɛri Fun Lɔ Bool A Pɛgi Koosa

ref: Mary said: With all my heart I praise the Lord,
text: Doone Mɛri fun ŋɔ, “N pɛgi Koosau wa mu bambi ni.

ref: and I am glad because of God my Savior.
text: Waa funɛ yisi min a li mu tum tagna ni di mu tiŋ un. Chendaha mu tuha sɔɔni.

ref: God cares for me, his humble servant. From now on, all people will say God has blessed me.
text: Min ya o bichɛl tumtumna ka o lisi mu wɛ. Saha nɔ kpa la, nara maga zi wa zimu di Koosa funɛ suŋi min,

ref: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
text: domi o tum tum zenewa a nyɛ min. O sɔn ti funɛ zenu kinkan, ka o ya tu chaan tiina.

ref: The Lord has used his powerful arm to scatter those who are proud.
text: O fun kpa o kpok kinkan a bur ba naa zer ba titi buudaha awa ba bambe sir yaali a tu tɛɛnɛ.

ref: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
text: O funɛ ur koor zeneu a ta, a ka kpa jɛrgɛ tima a te ba suwachak.

ref: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
text: Ba naa zimɛɛ di Koosa ya ba wu werewa, waa la zi suŋi ba, amaa o burgi o gantɛllɛ a chak ba naa vɛ zim un.

ref: God helps his servant Israel and is always merciful to his people.
text: O fun a tum chenani o vɔu a nyɛ ya nawaalsi ni saha digi. O fun wa suŋi o be Israɛl,

ref: The Lord made this promise to our ancestors, to Abraham and his family forever!
text: a kiɔm Abraham siki. O zi ɔm Abraham bi lulɛ maga gbanko sikiu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
text: Mɛri funɛ sɛm peti, o fun kpalimi Ɛlizabɛt wati pɛnsa tora. O saha funɛ wa tel, o fun a bur kaali o dama. Chenani Ba Fun Lul Jɔn

ref: When Elizabeth's son was born,
text: Ɛlizabɛt luli saha funɛ wa tel, o fun lul nibabichɛla.

ref: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
text: O walɛ awa o dɔŋtima funɛ nu chenani Koosa ɔm o siki kinkan ka ba wa un kpak tu sɔɔna kinkan.

ref: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
text: Be ti funɛ wa tel wuzu bunɔpɛ wuzu, nihɛɛta fun wa dam naa ba lul un chen di ba wa teŋ un. Ba fun a ŋɔ o nyan ni di o nyɛ ba yir be ti gbanko Zɛkaraya,

ref: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
text: amaa be ti niina funɛ vɛu, a ŋɔ di ba yir un Jɔn.

ref: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Nandigi wa kpak chen sɔn ti ya lul ni.”

ref: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
text: Doone ba fun kpa ba nisa a sɛm sɛma be ti nyan ti wati. Ba fun pɛɛsi un, “Sɔn baŋnɛ ii daŋni i du be ti?”

ref: At once, Zechariah started speaking and praising God.
text: Zɛkaraya funɛ sɔbi peti, o fun vaan piili a sɛm sɛma bura a la pɛgi Koosa.

ref: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
text: Ba naa maga funɛ nyɛn dɛ ti ni chen, ba funɛ bu nu ka Zɛkaraya sɛm sɛma, hansura fun kpak ba kinkan. Ba fun li a mɔɔli laaboor naa ba nu a su tɔsi na kuŋkule nyuuna go a li Judia pulum ni.

ref: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
text: Ba naa maga funɛ nu laaboor ti, ba fun lisi o wɛ a la pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Be nɔ wa ya nihɛɛ, yɛrɛ o kaa du?” Ba funɛ na di Koosa kpa o kpokre a nyɛ un. Chendaha ba funɛ pɛɛsi chen. Zɛkaraya Fun Sɛm A Chi Koosa Witinsi Ta Digi

ref: The Holy Spirit came upon Zechariah, and he began to speak:
text: Chen saha tirɛ Koosa siim weri ti fun si o nyan Zɛkaraya ti ni a sɛm a chi Koosa witinsi ta digi.

ref: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
text: O fun ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana naa ya Ju tim Koosa, funɛ la o nara nyuunau a nyɛ ba. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un nan daha.

ref: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
text: Waa funɛ nyɛ ya nibin zen naa ya Devid be luli. Waa la zi yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni ya tiŋ un.

ref: to save us from our enemies and from everyone who hates us.
text: a ŋɔ di o vɔu di o zi wa yisi yau a li ya dɔm nisa ni.

ref: God said he would be kind to our people and keep his sacred promise.
text: Ba gbanko fun ŋɔ di o zi ɔm ya nawaalsa sikiu a ta suglɛ chenani o wa ba ya nɔ bɛgim chen.

ref: that he would rescue us from our enemies. Then we could serve him without fear,
text: O funɛ vɔu vɔu kpokre a nyɛ ya nawaal Abraham ni di o zi yisi ya a li ya dɔm nisa ni, a nyɛ ya tiŋ un aa ta ɔmi.

ref: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
text: Doone Zɛkaraya fun ŋɔ, “Mu be, hin la zi ya Koosa zen witiina. Hin la zi te naa Koosa ŋɔ di o kaa tumu chen suwachak a saksi o nyinbaan a nyɛ un.

ref: You will tell his people that they can be saved when their sins are forgiven.
text: Hin la zi ŋɔ o nara ni di ba gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa, o zi yisi bau.

ref: God's love and kindness will shine upon us like the sun that rises in the sky.
text: Ya kunyan Koosa ya jɛrgi tiina a kpak ya siki. Chenani o daŋni o yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni chi nha yaa yɛli chene.

ref: On us who live in the dark shadow of death this light will shine to guide us into a life of peace.
text: O wara daŋni o na ya galimsa a ta ya yɔri, amaa o ka daŋni o te ya suwachakrɛ a biksi ya o bar sɔɔn nyinbaan.”

ref: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
text: Zɛkaraya be Jɔn ti fun bi a burgi nibaal. O fun li a la nyɛn nyabaan ni, hali saha naa o fun la li a biksi Ju tim ta gbampulim ni.

ref: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
text: A saha ti koor digi funɛ dɔ dooni. Ba funɛ yir un Ɔgastus. O funɛ daŋni o gur a zim nibinsa ba naa nyɛn o koor ti ni.

ref: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni. Ba yir un Simiɔn. O funɛ ya narrɛ a la tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ. O funɛ pɔ di Koosa tum naa kaa wa yisi Ju tim tum tagna. Koosa siim weri ti nyɛn un ni.

ref: These first records were made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
text: O funɛ gur nara tima maga, baal digi funɛ ya nihɛɛ a li Siiriya pulum ni. Ba funɛ yir un Sairinius.

ref: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
text: Nara tima maga funɛ kaali ba tɔsau di ba la sɔbi ba sɔna a du tɔn ni.

ref: So Joseph had to leave Nazareth in Galilee and go to Bethlehem in Judea. Long ago Bethlehem had been King David's hometown, and Joseph went there because he was from David's family.
text: Josɛf fun li Nazarɛt naa nyɛn Galili pulum ni a kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Bɛtlihɛm a li Judia pulum ni. Ba fun lul koor Devid dooni saha digi. Josɛf funɛ ya koor Devid bi lulirɛ. Chendaha o fun kaali dooni.

ref: Mary was engaged to Joseph and traveled with him to Bethlehem. She was soon going to have a baby,
text: Mɛri, naa la nɔɔni di o yu un, wa un fun kaali. O funɛ kpak lore.

ref: she gave birth to her first-born son. She dressed him in baby clothes and laid him on a bed of hay, because there was no room for them in the inn. The Shepherds
text: amaa dam naa hɔɔra fun nyɛn chen funɛ su wa nara ni. Chendaha o fun lul o tol be nibabichɛla a li sel bar nyɛni nɛɛ. O fun kpa gar a fɔu un a sak a diwe bar diili ni. Piisi Gbaŋsa Fun La Chem Koosa Malaikasa

ref: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
text: Piisi gbaŋsa bɔnɛ funɛ nyɛn dooni a nyini piisisi ta dusum a li kɔha ni.

ref: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
text: Koosa malaikasi ta digi fun wa li ba wati, ka Koosa chaani kinkan fun wa tɔ ba. Hansura fun kpak ba kinkan,

ref: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
text: amaa malaika ti funɛ ŋɔ bau, “Ma ta ɔmi. Mu kpak laaboor weri naa la zi nyɛ nara maga tusa sɔɔni kinkan a kaa ŋɔ ma.

ref: This very day in King David's hometown a Savior was born for you. He is Christ the Lord.
text: Dusum nɔwa ba lul bichɛla a li Devid tɔha Bɛtlihɛm ni. Waa la zi wa yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni maa tiŋ Koosa. Waa ya ya kunyan Kristo.

ref: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
text: Ma la dooni a la dɛu be ti. Ma zi na unu ka ba kpa gar a fɔu un a sak sela diwe bar diili ni. Chenani ma na dooni la biksi ma di mu sɛm ta ya wutirɛ.”

ref: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
text: Malaika ti funɛ sɛm chen peti, malaika damata fun li buudaha a wa gbaari un ni. Ba funɛ lɔ boola a pɛɛsi Koosa a ŋɔ,

ref: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
text: Malaikasi ta funɛ lɔ bool ta peti, ba fun bur a kaali Koosa tɔhɔn ti. Piisi gbaŋsi ta fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Ma nyɛ ya la Bɛtlihɛm a la na be naa Koosa malaika ti ŋɔ ya o wɛ chen.”

ref: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
text: Ba fun chɔ kaali a la na Josɛf awa Mɛri wa be ti ka o nyɛn sela diwe bar diili ti ni.

ref: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
text: Piisi gbaŋsi ta funɛ na be ti, ba fun ŋɔ Josɛf awa Mɛri ni chenani Koosa malaika ti ŋɔ ba o wɛ.

ref: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
text: Mɛri fun lisi ba sɛm ta mun kinkan a kpa a a du o bambi ni.

ref: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
text: Piisi gbaŋsi ta fun bur a kaali ba piisisi ta wati. Ba funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a la chene, ba fun lɔ boola a la pɛgi Koosa. Malaika ti sɛma awa be ti naali nyɛ ba tusa sɔɔni. Chenani malaika ti fun ŋɔ ba sir ya wutirɛ. Chenani Yesu Nyana Fun Du O Sɔn

ref: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
text: Wuzu bunɔpɛ gantɛl nɛɛ, ba fun teŋ be ti ka o nyan du o sɔn Yesu. Sɔn nɔwa malaika ti fun te du un ka o niin zi kpak o lo. Ba Fun Kpa Yesu A Nyɛ Koosa Ni A Li Jɛrusalɛm Ni

ref: The time came for Mary and Joseph to do what the Law of Moses says a mother is supposed to do after her baby is born. They took Jesus to the temple in Jerusalem and presented him to the Lord,

ref: The Law of the Lord also says parents have to offer a sacrifice, giving at least a pair of doves or two young pigeons. So that is what Mary and Joseph did.
text: Ma gbanko zi kɔrigi kambugilsa lɛ a nyɛ Koosa ni.” Chendaha Josɛf awa Mɛri funɛ daŋni ba ya chen.

text: Waa funɛ vɔu a nyɛ un di o wa zi wa sou, see o wa na Kristo naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen.

ref: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.

ref: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
text: “Mu kunyana, zinan nɔ i tum i vɔwi tiu. Lalaknɔ i zi nyɛ n waa naa ya i tumtumna, sou awa tu sɔɔn ni.

text: Mu funɛ na naa la zi yisi nara tima tum tagna a tau awa mu se ni.

text: ka ba wu lise naa sogli li gbampulim. I titi bambi zi botiu ka i na namsi kinkan. O zi chi ba kpa chise zenne di ba tau hin.”

ref: The prophet Anna was also there in the temple. She was the daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, and she was very old. In her youth she had been married for seven years, but her husband died.
text: Koosa witinsi ta digi funɛ nyɛn doon a ya nihaan hɛɛ. Ba funɛ yir un Ana. O nyɛn baal dama ni bun fu nɔpɛwa ka o baal sou. O nyan sɔn funɛ ya Fanuɛl a li Asa lul ni.

ref: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
text: Be ti funɛ biu a kpak kpok a gbaari. Koosa fun a suŋi un kinkan aa kpa o zima a nyɛ un. Yesu Fun Nu Ju Tim Nibiksɛ A Li Koosa Bar Jɛɛmi Dam Ni

ref: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
text: Ba naa maga funɛ nyɛn dooni a nu chenani o sɛm ba wa nɛha sɛm ni, alaazibu fun kpak ba kinkan.

ref: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
text: O nyan ti awa o niin ti funɛ na un, alaazibu fun kpak ba gbanko kinkan. O niin ti fun ŋɔ un, “N be, yɛrɛ i ya ya nan? I nyana awa min nyini i bar nyɛni a vusi. I namsi yau kinkan.”

text: O fun ŋɔ ba. “Yɛrɛ ma dɛu n bar nyɛni? Ma wa zim di o deŋlu wa mu fu nyɛn mu kunyan dam nii?”

text: Yesu funɛ bi a kpak Koosa zima a gbaari. O fun ya Koosa awa nara tima lɛmlɛm kinkan a gbaari.

ref: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”

ref: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
