text: Baal digi fun wa ŋɔ Yesu ni, “N zi tiŋ hinu, amaa nyɛ mu te la chausi mu dam walɛ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Nar tiŋ min a la nyini gantɛl ni, mu wa kilisi un o biksi nara Koosa koor ti wɛ.”

ref: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Kikumɛ damata funɛ nyɛn a daŋni kumi, amaa tumtumsi tama funɛ nyɛn. Ma sumi kikumɛ ta tiina di o kpa tumtumsi nidamata a wa gbaari ba ni.

ref: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
text: Mu la tum ma di ma la a chi chenani nar ya tum piisibisa gbesa bambaan ni chen.

ref: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
text: Ma waa la, ma ta zer buna a chi kapursa awa bɔtɛ koo natausa. Ma ta chiŋi aa sɛm sɛma nyinbaan ni.

ref: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
text: Ma wa la tel dam digi a zu ka ba la ma a du, ma zaami ba a ŋɔ, ‘Koosa nyɛ ma bar sɔɔna’. Ba ka vɛ la ma duuli, Koosa wa zi nyɛ ba.

ref: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
text: O deŋlu wa tumtumna kila o bɔta. Chendaha ma na dam naa nara zi zaami ma. Ma nyɛn dooni a di diwe aa kinyɔ kina maga ba kpa nyɛ ma. Ma ta nyɛn a go damsa.

ref: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
text: Ma la zu tɔha ka ba naa nyɛn dooni chen zaami ma, ma di diwe naa ba kpa nyɛ ma.

ref: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
text: Ma tiibi ba wichɔɔrsa aa ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni chen, ‘Saha ti telu di Koosa dɔ nara’.

ref: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
text: Ma la zu tɔha ka ba naa nyɛn dooni chen vɛ zaami ma, maa gba a val nyinbaanisi ni a ŋɔ nara maga ni,

ref: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
text: Maa nu mu sɛm na. A wuzu naa Koosa kaa wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nara sɛria, o zi wa kpak Sɔdɔm tima sikiu a kaali tɔhɔn ti tima.” Tɔsi Tima Wa Fun Nyɛ Ba Se Su Yesu (Mat.

ref: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
text: Koosa witinsa nidamata awa koorsa funɛ daŋniu kinkan di ba na kin nawara ma na, amaa ba wa fun koti a na a. Ba funɛ daŋniu kinkan di ba nu sɛm naa ma nu, amaa ba wa fun koti a nu a.” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Samaria Tim Baal Naa Funɛ Kpak Siki Wɛ

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
text: Koosa zi wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga tima sɛriau, o zi wa kpak Taaya awa Saidɔn tima sikiu a kaali ma.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
text: Ma Kapɛrnaum tima. Ma waa la daŋni ma zer ma titi buudaha. A wuzu ti wa tel, ba waa la zi zer ma a yo du din dam ni.”

ref: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “N fun na sintanau ka o li buudaha aa wa a chi nasɛha nyiksɛɛ. Mu sɔn funɛ kpok a kaali o sɔn.

ref: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
text: Maa nu mu sɛma. N funɛ kpa n kpoku a nyɛ ma, chen ni ma zi kpak kpoku a kaali sintan ti kpok saha maga. Hali ma zi kotiu a val duma awa numsi daha ka a ta ya ma wu digi.

ref: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta digi funɛ wa Yesu wati di o wa mo o lo. O fun pɛɛsi un, “Mu kunyana, yɛrɛ mu kaa ya a wa na miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔ?”

ref: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
text: Baal ti fun ŋɔ un, “Ba funɛ sɔbiu di nibine daŋni o na miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔ, o lawa o daŋni Koosa awa o bambi awa o siima, awa o lisi, awa o kpok maga ni. Ba gbanko funɛ sɔbiu di o lawa o daŋni o dɔŋtimau a chi chenani o daŋni o titi chen.”

ref: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Chen saha ti Koosa siim weri ti funɛ nyɛ Yesu tu sɔɔniu kinkan. O fun sumi Koosa a ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, koor naa dɔ buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ maga ni. Kin nawara i fun kpa sogli a li ninɛɛgɛ wati chen, i funɛ kpa biksi ba naa ya nyu bilim timwa. Mu kunyana, i funɛ tum chenani i titi daŋniu.”

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: Doone Yesu fun burgi a sɛm zɛmɛ ta ni, “Mu kunyana funɛ kpa kina magau a nyɛ min. Mu wa mu nyana duŋduŋ zim dɔŋa. Mu nyɛ ba naa mu lisi zim o gbanko.”

ref: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
text: Yesu wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun tur la li bar digi ba duŋduŋ. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma wa kpak nyusumu a na chenani mu fun tum.

ref: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
text: Yesu fun burgi a pɛɛsi un, “Ba naa funɛ sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chen, yɛrɛ ba fun biksi hin o wɛ?”

ref: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “I sɛm ta deŋlu. I tiŋ chenani i sɛm min, i zi na miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔu.”

ref: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
text: Amaa baal ti funɛ zimu o bambi ni di o bɔmu o wati di o daŋni nara maga. Adaha o fun bu pɛɛsi Yesu, “Anti ya mu dɔŋtiina?”

ref: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Baal digi funɛ nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni a gba aa la tɔhɔn naa ba yir Jɛriko chen. O funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni aa la chene, ŋmɛɛrsa fun wa go un a kpak un kpok ni. Ba fun a chɛɛsi o kilaarɛ a ur a, a tuk un kinkan. Ba fun kaali a ka un dooni a chɔ a hɔɔgi.

ref: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
text: O funɛ chɔ dooni a ya tama, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nidigi fun gba nyinbaan ti ni. O funɛ wa na baal ti ka o chɔ a hɔɔgi, o fun bɛ nyinbaan cheŋcheŋa a wa kaali.

ref: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
text: O wa fun bu vusi ka baal digi val wa. O funɛ ya Samaria tim baallɛ. O funɛ na baal ti, o we fun kpak un siki kinkan.

ref: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
text: O fun la li o wati a gbiri o suwachak ni. O fun chaksi o bar hɔɔsɛ ta a yisi nu awa kinyɔɔli a li o kapur ni a yoli a du a ni. Doone o fun yisi gar a mɛɛsi un a zer un a kansi o titi kokum daha ni. O fun kpa un kaali hɔɔra dama naa duglɛ nyinbaan nɔ a la nyini un.

ref: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, o fun a lisi malɛ aniita lɛ a nyɛ hɔɔra dam tiin ni a ŋɔ un, ‘N daŋni mu lau, adaha i nyini un. N wa bur wa, n zi bɔ hin malɛ naa i kpa a tiibi un chenu.’ ”

ref: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm a la mak o bɔ, o fun pɛɛsi Koosa chiksɛ nibiksi ti, “Nara bator ta tuhu ni, anti ya baal naa ŋmɛɛrsa fun kpak nyinbaan ni chen dɔŋtiina?”

ref: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
text: Nibiksi ti fun ŋɔ un, “Naa funɛ kpak un siki chen.” Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “La daŋni i dɔŋtiina a chi chenani o daŋni chen.” Yesu Fun La Zaami Marta Awa Mɛri

ref: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
text: Yesu wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni aa la, ba fun wa tel tɔ digi. Haan digi funɛ nyɛn dooni. Ba yir un Marta. O fun la ba du o dam ni.

ref: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
text: O funɛ kpak niinbere. Ba yir un Mɛri. Yesu funɛ piili a biksi o gantɛl tiŋsi ta, Mɛri fun wa kani o suwachak ni aa nu o sɛm ta.

ref: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
text: Marta funɛ ya diwewa hali o miisa wa mou un. O fun la pɛɛsi Yesu, “Mu kunyana, yɛrɛ i nyɛ min duŋduŋ ya diwe. Ŋɔ Mɛri ni di o si a wa suŋi min.”

ref: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Marta, yɛrɛ i nyɛ wɛ kinkan muksi hin?

ref: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
text: Chenani i ya diwe a nyɛ ya, chen weru, amaa ya zi di diwe ta petiu. Mu sɛm naa Mɛri nu, o ka zi kpak au o bambi ni hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
text: Ma sumi Koosa di o nyɛ ma kundɔŋ, o zi nyɛ mau. Ma daŋni kundɔŋ o wati, ma zi na unu. Ma yir un o zi wa puk zɔɔnɔu, ma zu o wati.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
text: Ma ya nibin tagna, amaa maa zim chenani ma zi kpa kiwere a nyɛ ma bisi niu. Ma kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni, waa kaali ma a kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ ba naa la sumi un ni.” Chenani Yesu Fun Duŋni Alizin Tagna A Li Nara Ni (Mat.

ref: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
text: Ma dɔŋtiin ti ŋɔ hinu a li sal tuhu ni, ‘Ta muksi min, kila. N piin far mu bulzɔɔnɔu ka mu wa n bisa chɔ do. N wa kisi a nyɛ ma kundɔŋ.’

ref: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ma naa ya nyama nan, lɔsa kpak ma bisa ka ba daŋni nyinsa di ba se, ma zi zer dum a nyɛ bauu?

ref: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Nan nyinbaan nɔ wa ma zi sumi Koosa, ‘Ya kunyan Koosa, ya daŋni i sɔn naa la chaani chiŋiu. Ya daŋni i wa dɔ narawa.

ref: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
text: aa gil ya taali a pɛŋ ya, ka ya ka gil aa pɛŋ ba naa laa tum ya wu tagna.’ ”

ref: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nara maga sumi Koosa di o nyɛ ba kundɔŋ, o zi nyɛ bau. Nara maga la daŋni kundɔŋ o wati, ba zi na unu. Ba yir un, o zi wa puk zɔɔnɔu ba zu o wati.

ref: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
text: Chenani Koosa fun suŋi un saha digi biksi ba naa funɛ nyɛn tɔhɔn naa ba yir Ninivi ni, di Koosa tum un ba wati. Chenani Koosa zi wa suŋi n waa nibine be zi biksi zinan narau di Koosa tum min ba wati.

ref: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm peti chen, nihaan digi a li zɛmɛ ta ni fun huusi a ŋɔ un, “Nihaan naa funɛ lul hin a dɛɛsi hin chen, yɛrɛ o tu fun sɔɔni kinkan.”

ref: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
text: Doone nara bɔnɛ fun kpa baal digi a wa Yesu wati. O funɛ ya womnɛ domi alizin tagin funɛ nyɛn un ni. Yesu fun tiibi un, chen ni o fun bur a sɛm. Alaazibu fun kpak zɛmɛ ta.

ref: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
text: Ba bɔnɛ ka funɛ daŋni Yesu tum alaazibu tuma a biksi ba di ba zim di o kpok ti sir li Koosa wa nɛɛ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
text: Yesu funɛ zim chenani ba lisiu a ŋɔ ba, “Tɔhɔn tima vɛ kpak nɔ bɛgim, ba dɔma wa zi wa a chɔk ba tɔhɔn tii? Dam bɛgim nara burgi a yo dɔŋa, chen dam ti zi pisiu a chel.

ref: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
text: Ma gantɛl tiŋsa gbanko koti a duŋni a a li nara ni. Anti nyɛ ba chen kpok ti? Ba waa biksi di chenani ma sɛm mu wɛ wa ya wuti.

ref: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
text: “Nikpok saksɛ o laal kina a dau a il o titi dama, nandigi wa zi wa ŋmɛ o kina.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
text: “Ma Farisi tim ta, yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ma ni? Ma yaa daŋni ma kani kor were ni a li ma bar lagmi dɛɛni ni. Ma yaa daŋni narawa kizaami ma a li yau ni a kinyɛ ma jirima.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ naa funɛ chiŋ doon digi fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Mu kunyana, chenani i sɛm chen, i pini ya gban zomwa.”

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
text: O fun bu ŋɔ ba, “Alizin tagin li nar nyuhu ni, o ya gbau a kaali nyabaan a la nyini doonani o zi nyɛn. O vɛ koti a na bar digi doonani o zi nyɛn, o zi ŋɔ o titi niu, ‘N zi bur la zu baal naa nyuha mu fun li chenu.’

ref: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
text: Adaha o bur kaali a la zu dooni a na un ka o chiŋi a wer.

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
text: O bukoo li a gba a dɛu o dɔŋtima alizin tagna kina nɔpɛ a kpa a a bur wa zu dooni. Alizin tagna nɔpɛ ta tum tagna kaali o tuma. Saha naa alizin tagin ti fun tum o tuma peti, chenani baal ti du fun bukoo tagini a kaali o piili ti ni.” A Li Wuti Bambi Sɔɔni

ref: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
text: Yesu fun burgi a ŋɔ un, “Ba naa laa nu Koosa sɛma a la tiŋ a, ba tusa sɔɔnɛ kinkan a kaali mu niina.” Nara Funɛ Daŋni Ba Na Alaazibu Tum (Mat.

ref: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
text: Farisi tim ta baal ti funɛ na di Yesu wa fun sɛm o nisa a la di, chen fun jɛri un kinkan.

ref: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
text: “Ma Farisi tim ta, yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ma ni? Ma nyɛ ya lisi kin nawara ma kpa du ma disa ni a lɛm. Koosa chiksɛ ta biksɛɛ di ma lisi kibɛgim a li kin fi ni a nyɛ Koosa ni. Ma tiŋ au ka chen wer. Koosa chiksɛ ta gbanko biksi di maa tum tum werewa a nyɛ nara ni a kidaŋni Koosa. Kin nama ya wu birigi a kaali suwachak kin tima kinkan, amaa ma wara tum a.

ref: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
text: Koosa wa wa di o wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nara sɛria, koor nihaan naa nyɛn doonani ba yir Siba chen, zi wa siu a fiksi ma. O funɛ li bar bolire a wa koor Solomon wati di o wa nu o zima sɛma. Mu waa naa nyɛn ma wati bar nɔ nan, min kaali Solomon kinkan.

ref: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
text: Koosa wa wa di o wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nara sɛria, Ninivi tima zi wa siu a fiksi ma. Joona funɛ biksi ba Koosa wɛ saha digi, ba fun gil ba tum tagna a tiŋ un. Mu waa naa nyɛn ma wati bar nɔ nan, min kaali Joona kinkan.” Chenani Ya Se Nyɛ Ya Na (Mat.

ref: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
text: “Nandigi wa zi suk fitina a kpa gbaŋa a chik un ni. O ka suk un a chiŋsi fitin bar chiŋi nɛɛ. Doonee o zi chaani nara maga ni a li dɛ ti ni.

ref: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
text: Ma se nyɛ ma bara la doonani ma daŋni. Ma se weree, ma zi na nyinbaanu nɛnɛ. Chen vɛɛna, ma tusa weree, ma zi tiŋ Koosa nyinbaanu nɛnɛ. Amaa ma ya nyuluma, ma wa zi zim doonani ma la. Chen vɛɛna, ma tusa vɛɛn biriu, ma wa zi na o nyinbaan a nyuksi.

ref: So be sure your light isn't darkness.
text: Ma doso, ma ta chi nyuluma.

ref: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
text: Ma ya womniwa. Koosa nyɛ ma diwe diili awa ma tusa gbanko.

ref: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
text: Yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ma ni? Ma fun kɔɔri witinsa ba naa ma nawaalsa fun kpu chen, hugilsa a wer.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm peti, Farisi tim ta baal digi fun yir un di o wa un di diwe o dam ni. Yesu fun la gbaari un ni ba di diwe ta.

ref: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
text: Yesu funɛ zim chenani o lisiu a ŋɔ un, “Ma Farisi tim ta daŋni ma di diwe, ma doso a sisi ma gbakmisa awa ma kurwasa, amaa ma tusa yaa kpak doŋau. Ma kpa kpoku a fuksi kina nara wati a ya tum tagna tima.

ref: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
text: Ma zer ma diwe awa ma kinyɔɔlɛ ta a nyɛ fara tim ni. Ma ya chen, ma tusa wa zi bu kpak doŋa.

ref: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
text: Yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ma ni? Ma chi hugilsa naa ba vɛ danirɛ. Chendaha nara val a daha ni aa vɛ zimi.”

ref: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Ma Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta, yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ma ni? Ma fun a saksi bun yuga a lagmi dɔŋ ni a nyɛ nara chuŋi a, amaa ma wa fun kpa ma nisa a gbaŋsi bun tima.

ref: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
text: Ma la nɔɔniu di chenani ma nawaalsa fun kpu Koosa witinsa ta chen, ba funɛ ya nɛnɛu. Chendaha ma fun kɔɔri a chen.

ref: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
text: Koosa naa ya zima tiina fun ŋɔ saha digi di o zi wa tum o witinsi ta awa o nitumɛ ta Ju tim watiu. O funɛ zimu di Ju tim ta zi wa kpu ba bɔnɛu a namsi ba bɔnɛ.

ref: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
text: Koosa zi lɛrgi Ju tima ba naa nyɛn zinan diŋsau a ŋɔ di ba wa dɔ chenani ba nawaalsi ta fun kpu o witinsi ta maga o piili saha ti kpa la.

ref: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
text: Ma nawaalsi ta funɛ te kpu nar ba yir Abɛl a fun wa tiŋ gantɛl a kpu Zɛkaraya gbanko a dugli bar kɔɔri ti a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni. Wutirɛ n ŋɔ ma chen, Koosa zi lɛrgi Ju tima ba naa nyɛn zinan diŋsau a ŋɔ di ba wa dɔ a naa maga siti nan.

ref: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
text: “Ma Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta, yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ma ni? Chenani maa biksi nara chi ma tɔ chenani ba zim di Koosa kaa wa yisi ba tum tagniwa. Ma titi wara daŋni Koosa yisi ma tum tagna a ta. Ma gbanko daŋni ma tɔ ba naa la wa Koosa wati nyinbaannɛ.”

ref: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm chen peti, Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta awa Farisi tim ta fun na paan kinkan a du un ni. Saha ti kpa la ba fun a pɛɛsi un wu damata wɛ,

ref: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
text: Nara tusugsa funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni aa zaksi dɔŋa, Yesu fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Farisi tim ta ya nusun timwa. Chendaha ma doso ba biksi ni.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
text: Koosa la nyɛ kɔsa wa kilaarɛ. A wa bi a vusi ka limaŋ wa pisi, din yaa di a. O wati ma wa kaali kɔsa kinkan. O wa zi bu koti a nyɛ ma kilaarɛɛ? Ma se wa su un nɛnɛ.

ref: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
text: “Ma ŋɔ mau, mu chɛnsa, ma ta ɔm ba nawara koti a kpu ma bara, amaa ba wa fun koti a tum chen a kaali.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
text: Ma wa wara hali Koosa funɛ gur ma nyuksau a li ma nyuhu ni. Adaha ma ta ɔm chenani nibinsa kaa ya ma. Ma wa kaali zimebisa kinkan Koosa wati.” Chenani Ya Zi Ŋɔ Nara Ni Yesu Wɛ (Mat.

ref: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
text: “Ma naa chi mu piisisa, ma ta ɔmi. Chenani ma kunyana Koosa dɔ ma ya un lɛmlɛmu kinkan.

ref: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
text: Ma kpa ma kina maga a suŋi ba naa la daŋni a. Ma ta chiŋsɛ kidamata a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Ma ka chiŋsɛ kidamata a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni. Chau wa zi wa chɔk a ka ŋmɛɛrsa wa chɔk dɛ ti a ŋmɛ a dooni.

ref: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
text: Koosa siim weri ti zi biksi ma chenani ma zi sɛmu.” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Malɛ Tiin Naa Funɛ Ya Wom Wɛ

ref: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
text: O funɛ peti chenani o lisi o bambi ni, Koosa fun wa ŋɔ un, ‘I ya nibin womnɛ. Dusum nɔ i zi sowu. I kin naa maga i kpa chiŋsi nan, a wa zi kpak wu digi wa hin.’ ”

ref: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
text: “Nibine naa maga ŋɔ nara ni di min dɔ ba, n waa nibine be zi sɛm chenu a nyɛ Koosa malaikasi ta ni.

ref: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
text: Nibine naa maga ka vɛ ŋɔ nara ni di min dɔ ba, n waa nibine be wa zi sɛm chen a nyɛ Koosa malaikasi ta ni.

ref: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
text: “Nara zi wa kpa ma a la ba bar lagmi dɛɛna awa koorsa awa nihɛɛta suwachak di ba di ma sɛria. Chen wa ya, ma ta nyɛ chenani ma kaa wa sɛmi kimuksi ma.

ref: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn zɛmɛ ta ni. O fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Mu kunyana, ya nyana sou a ka o kina. Ŋɔ mu maan ni di o purigi a bɔnɛ a nyɛ min.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “N chɛna, o wa deŋli wa mu di ma sɛria a purigi a a nyɛ ma.”

ref: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
text: Yesu fun burgi a ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni, “Nibine kpak un o bambi ni di o ya kin tiina, o tuha wara sɔɔni. Chendaha ma doso a ta tiŋ chen.”

ref: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
text: o fun lisi o tuhu ni, ‘N wa zim chenani mu kaa ya. N wa kpak bar chiŋsi a la zi koti a chiŋsi n kina magau.

ref: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
text: N zim chenani n zi tumu. N zi chɔk n kin bar chiŋsɛ tau a bukoo kɔɔri kinkin naa zene a kaali a. N ya chen, n zi koti a chiŋsi mu kikumɛ ta awa mu kina magau.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
text: Yesu fun bukoo ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Nan nyinbaan nɛɛnɛ n sɛm ma. Saha maga maa daŋni diwe awa kilaarɛ. Ma ta nyɛ kin nama muksi ma.

ref: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
text: Maa nyini chenani kɔsa yeligisa du. A wara tum tuma koo a ar kilaarɛ a nyɛ a titi ni. Amaa n ŋɔ mau, ma lisi koor naa ba funɛ yir Solomon chen. O funɛ ya malɛ tiinnɛ saha digi a kaali ba maga, amaa o wa fun laarɛ kilaarɛ a wer a chi kɔsa yeligisa na.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
text: Kin namawa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima yaa daŋni chen, amaa ma kunyana Koosa zimu di maa daŋni kin ta magau.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
text: Amaa tumtum naa o lisɛ chen vɛ ya wuti tiina, o titi niu di o kunyan ti zi vusiu. Adaha o zi tuk o daŋ tumtumsi ta. O gbanko zi la lagmi ba naa nyɔ sin a dɛɛsiu a di diwe.

ref: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
text: Ma ta lisi di mu biksi nɔ zi nyɛ nara lagmi a nyɛn wa bar sɔɔni. O ka zi nyɛ nara purigi dɔŋ niu.

ref: Be ready and keep your lamps burning
text: “Ma saksi a kirsi ma kilaarɛ aa suksi ma fitina,

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
text: Ba kunyan ti na ba ka ba si a saksi a pɔ un chen, o tumtumsi ta tusa zi sɔɔniu. O zi saksi o titiu a kpa diwe a wa nyɛ ba.

ref: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
text: O wa bur a wa tɔhɛɛsa koo saha naa zimal hɛɛta yaa yeligi chen ka o tumtumsi ta saksi a pɔ un, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu kinkan.

ref: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
text: Maa nu mu sɛm na. Dam tiin zimɛɛ di ŋmɛɛr kaa wa zu ŋmɛ un, o zi saksiu a pɔ un, chen ni ŋmɛɛr ti wa kiwa koti a zu.

ref: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
text: Saha naa ma vɛ wa lisi di n waa nibine be zi bur wau. Chendaha maa saksi saha maga.” Tumtumna Weri Awa Tumtumna Tagin Wɛ (Mat.

ref: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
text: Pita fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Mu kunyana, i biksi nan jɛɛgi nɔ a nyɛ ya nɛɛ koo nara maga ni?”

ref: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Ma anti la zi ya wuti tima awa ninɛɛgɛ a li kin nama maga ni? O chi dam tiin naa lisɛ o tumtumsa nidigi di o nyini o dam nara a kinyɛ ba diwe diili saha.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: O kunyan ti wa la a bur wa a na di o tum chenani o ŋɔ un, o tumtum ti tuha zi sɔɔniu.

ref: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: N ŋɔ ma wutirɛ, o kunyan ti zi nyɛ o nyini o kina magau a nyɛ un.

ref: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
text: A wuzu naa o vɛ lisi di o kunyan ti zi wau, o zi wau. O kunyan ti zi wa na paanu kinkan a kpa un a gbaari ba naa vɛ tum chenani ba ŋɔ ba a ta un yɔri. Dooni o zi wa wiu kinkan a se o nyina.

ref: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
text: “Tumtum zim chenani o kunyana daŋni o tum, ka o baga vɛ nyɛni di o tum chen, o kunyan ti zi tuk unu wa vurmsi niu kinkan.

ref: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
text: Tumtum naa vɛ zim chenani o kunyana daŋni o tum ka o kunyan ti tu vɛ sɔɔni wa chenani o tumi, o zi tuk unu wa vurmsi ni tama. Koosa zi pɛɛsi ba naa zim chenani o daŋni ba tum a kaali ba naa vɛ zim chenani o daŋni.” Chenani Nara Zi Purigi Dɔŋ Ni (Mat.

ref: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
text: O lawa mu namsiu kinkan. Mu tuha wa sɔɔni hali mu namsi saha wa peti.

ref: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
text: N zi wa burgi nyama a du ba nibabichɛsa ni, nihabichɛsa a du ba num ni, awa ba bisa haanasa a du ba hilsa ni.” Ju Tima Wa Zim Ba Saha Ti Mun (Mat.

ref: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
text: Yesu gbanko fun ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni, “Ma na dobaala ka a li nha bar yɛli a la nha bar cheli, ma zim di doon kaa mi. Ma sɛm wutirɛ.

ref: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
text: Ma ya na ka fufuk wa ya, ma yaa zimu di limaŋ saha pisu. O sir ya wuti.

ref: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
text: Ma ya nusun timwa. Ma zi nyini buudahau awa tɛɛnɛ aa zim chen mun. Yɛrɛ ma vɛ zim chenani mu duuli?” Ma Nyɛ Ma Wa Ma Dɔma Ya Chɛnsa (Mat.

ref: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
text: “Yɛrɛ ma titi vɛ lisi chenani were a tumi?

ref: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
text: Nar kpa ma la sɛria bar diili dɛ, nyɛ ma wa un ya chɛnsa a la nyinbaan ni. Ma vɛ ya chen ka ma la tel, o zi kpa mau a du sɛria nidiile tima digi nin ni. Nidiili ti ka kpa ma a nyɛ lanjima ni o tɔ ma dɛ.

ref: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
text: N ŋɔ mau, ma zi nyɛn dooniu hali ma wa bɔ ma chim maga.”

ref: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
text: A saha ti nara bɔnɛ funɛ wa ŋɔ Yesu ni Galili tim bɔnɛ wɛ. Ba funɛ kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni a li o bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni chene, gɔbnati nyuhu tiina naa ba funɛ yir Pailɔt fun tum o lanjimasa ba la kpu ba.

ref: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Chenani Galili tim ta fun siti biksɛɛ di ba tum tagna kaali ba dɔŋtim tum tagnaa?

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
text: Aai. N ŋɔ mau di ma vɛ gil ma tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan, ma maga zi sitiu a chi chenani ba fun siti chen.

ref: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
text: Aai. N ŋɔ mau di ma vɛ gil ma tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan, ma maga zi sitiu a chi chenani ba fun siti chen.” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Bugil Naa Vɛ Kpak Nɔn Chen Wɛ

ref: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
text: Doone Yesu fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a ŋɔ ba, “Baal digi funɛ du bugil o ŋmaha ni. Wuzu digirɛ o fun la di o la tɔti bugil ti nɔn. Amaa bugil ti ha wa fun nɔn.

ref: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
text: Amaa ŋmaha be ti funɛ ŋɔ unu, ‘Gil un o chiŋi bun bɛgim. N zi hur o munu a go a du kolum.

ref: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
text: Bun bɛgim ti gantɛl ni ka o nɔn, chen weru. O vɛ nɔni, n zi teŋ unu a lɔ a nyɛ hin.’ ” Yesu Fun Tiibi Nihaan Naa Huunɛ A Val

ref: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
text: Hiisi wuzu digi Yesu funɛ biksi narawa a li ba bar lagmi dɛ ni.

ref: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
text: Yesu funɛ na un, o fun a yir un a ŋɔ, “Nihaan, n daŋni i na alaafɛu.”

ref: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
text: Yesu fun nu o sɛm ta a ŋɔ un, “I wa ba naa tiŋ i nɔ ya nusun timwa. Ma zi pur ma nuha koo kokuma a kpa un la pul di o la nyɔ ni hiisi wuzu tiuu?

ref: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
text: Yesu dɔma funɛ nu chenani o sɛm, hiisa fun kpa ba kinkan. Zɛmɛ ba naa maga ka funɛ tiŋ un chen, ba tusa funɛ sɔɔniu o alaazibu tum daha. Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Kidusi Maga Kiwewe Wɛ (Mat.

ref: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
text: Chen saha ti Farisi tim ta bɔnɛ funɛ wa ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Hɛrɔd daŋni o kpu hinu. Si bar nɔ a la.”

ref: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
text: Mu nyɛn nyinbaan nɛɛ aa la Jɛrusalɛm. Wu digi wa zi kak min. Ju tim ta yaa funɛ daŋni di ba kpu Koosa witinsi ta digi, doonee ba yaa kpu ba chen.”

ref: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
text: Ba naa tiŋ gantɛl lalaknɔ nan, ba zi wa teu. Ba naa ka te suwachak lalaknɔ nan, ba zi wa tiŋ gantɛlu.” Chenani Yesu Funɛ Daŋni Jɛrusalɛm Tima (Mat.

ref: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Baal ti chi gbehere. Ma la ŋɔ un di zinan awa chɛ mu zi duŋni alizin tagnau a li nara ni aa tiibi wichɔɔrsa. Si chɔɔsirɛ n zi peti mu tuma.

ref: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
text: Koosa koor ti chi kiduha naa ba yir ‘mastad’ ba yaa kpa aa du chene. A gantɛl ni a bi a burgi bugila, zimebisa yaa wa du a zaŋa a hulsi ni.” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Sinmuna Wɛ (Mat.

ref: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
text: Yesu fun bukoo jɛɛgi jɛɛgi a ŋɔ ba,

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
text: Yesu funɛ la Jɛrusalɛm, o fun val a la zu tɔsa awa tɔ wisa a biksi nara.

ref: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
text: “Koosa koor ti bulzɔɔnɔ fɛmɛu. Ma kpaŋi ma titi kinkan a la zu dooni. Nidamata daŋni ba zu unu, amaa ba wa koti.

ref: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
text: Wuzu digi mu zi wa siu a far Koosa koor ti bulzɔɔnɔ ti. Ma zi wa chiŋi bulzɔɔnɔ ti gantɛl niu a yir, ‘Ya kunyana, puk bulzɔɔnɔ ti ka ya zu.’ Mu zi ŋɔ mau, ‘N wa zim doonani ma li.’

ref: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
text: Ma zi ŋɔ minu, ‘Ya wa hin funɛ lagmi a di diwe a la nyɔ chen. I gbanko funɛ biksi yau a li ya tɔhɔn ni.’

ref: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
text: Mu zi bukoo ŋɔ mau, ‘N wa zim doonani ma li. Ma naa la tum tum tagna nan, ma la. Ma wa kpak nyinbaan a la zi zu bar nɔ.’

ref: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
text: Ma wa na Abraham, Aizik, Jakɔb awa Koosa witinsi ta maga Koosa koor ti tuhu ni ka ma waa chiŋi o bulzɔɔnɔ ti gantɛl ni, ma zi namsiu aa wi kinkan.

ref: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
text: Nara zi wa li bawu magau a lagmi a di diwe Koosa koor ti ni.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: Doone Yesu fun burgi a ŋɔ zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ li Jɛrusalɛm chen, “Ma Jɛrusalɛm tima. Ma yaa kpu witinsa ba naa Koosa tum ma wati. Mu yaa daŋniu n kok ma maga bu damata a chi zimi kok o bisa awa o chɛnɛ ni. Amaa ma wara daŋni n kok ma.

ref: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: O wa vusi ma damsa zi wa chɔku. Mu ŋɔ mau lalaknɔ ma wa zi bu na min, hali n zi wa bur wa ka ma ŋɔ, ‘Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi naa Koosa tum nan.’ ”

ref: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn dooni a fun wa Yesu wati. O nisa awa o naŋgere funɛ fɔɔsiu.

ref: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
text: Yesu fun na un a pɛɛsi Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta awa Farisi tim ba naa nyɛn dooni, “Yɛrɛ Ju tim ta chiksɛ ta biksi ya? O deŋlu mu tiibi baal nɔ hiisi wuzu ti koo o wa deŋlii?”

ref: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
text: Amaa ba fun moori. Yesu fun a tiibi baal ti a nyɛ o kaali.

ref: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
text: Ba fun bu moori. Ya Ya Jɛrgɛ Tima, Koosa Zi Wa Kpa Yau A Kaali Buudaha

ref: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
text: Yesu fun na chenani nara tima bɔnɛ lisi doonani weree a kani. O fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a ŋɔ ba maga ni,

ref: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
text: “Nandigi yir ma di ma wa o haan fali dama a wa di diwe, ma ta zu kani bar weri ni. Haan fali baal ti yir nibirige a kaali ma,

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Nan jɛɛgi nɔ mun ya di nar zer o titi, o zi wa chelu. Nar ka ya jɛrgi, zi wa na zenzenu.”

ref: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
text: Doone Yesu fun burgi Farisi tim ta nihɛɛ ti wati a ŋɔ un, “I yir nara di ba wa i dama a wa di diwe, ta yir i chɛnsa koo i niinbisa koo i walɛ koo i dɔŋtima ba naa ya malɛ tima. Ba gbanko kpak nyinbaanu a la zi yir hin di i wa ba damsa a wa di diwe a bɔ hin chenani i fun ya ba chen.

ref: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
text: O tumtum ti fun li a kaali a la tum chenani o kunyan ti ŋɔ un. O funɛ bu bur wa, o fun ŋɔ un, ‘Mu tum chenani i daŋniu, amaa i dam ti ha wa vɛɛn su.’

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
text: Nara tima nidigi naa funɛ lagmi wa Yesu a li Farisi tim ta nihɛɛ ti ni ba di diwe chen, o funɛ nu Yesu sɛma, o fun ŋɔ un, “Ba naa kaa lagmi Koosa koor ti ni di ba di diwe nan, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu kinkan.”

ref: God sent a man named John,
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon. Ba yir un Jɔn.

ref: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
text: Koosa fun tum un di o biksi nara fitin ti wɛ, chen ni ba zi na nyinbaanu a nu o sɛma a nyɛ ba se wa su un.

ref: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
text: Waa funɛ ya fitin wuti naa funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ, chen ni nara maga zi na nyinbaanu a nyɛn nha ni.

ref: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
text: Yesu Kristo funɛ wa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ nɛɛ. Koosa fun nyɛ o naami un awa kin naa maga nyɛn un ni, amaa tɛɛnɛ nɔ nara wa fun zim nibine naa ya un.

ref: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
text: O funɛ wa nyɛn o sɛmbiri nara watɛɛ, amaa ba nidamata wa fun la un.

ref: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
text: Chenani ba yaa lul ba bichɛsa, o wa nyɛ ba ya Koosa bisa. O wa fun ya nibinsa tutɔrɛ di o burgi ba nyana. O ka ya o tutɔrɛ di o burgi ba nyana.

ref: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
text: Yesu Kristo ya ya kunyana Koosa duŋduŋ be. Koosa fun yisi o jirima awa o chaani kinkan a du un ni a nyɛ o burgi nibine a wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga di o wa ya lagmi nyɛn bunsa mɛna. Ya fun zim di Koosa jirima awa chaani kinkan nyɛn un ni awa chenani o sɛm maga ya wuti awa chenani o tuma suŋi nara.

ref: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
text: Chenani o fun koti a suŋi nara a la gali, o funɛ ya chene a nyɛ ya. O yaa suŋi yau a saksi dɔŋ ni.

ref: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
text: Ba fun bukoo pɛɛsi un, “I ya antirɛ? I ya Koosa witiina Ɛlaijarɛɛ?” Jɔn fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu laata.” Ba fun bu pɛɛsi un butora tiina, “Hin ya Koosa witiina naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi wa tumu ya wati chen?” Jɔn fun bukoo ŋɔ ba, “Mu laata.”

ref: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya wa bur la ba naa tum ya i wati, ba zi daŋni ya ŋɔ bau. O lawa i ŋɔ ya chenani i duu.”

ref: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
text: Ba fun bukoo pɛɛsi Jɔn, “I vɛ ya Kristo naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o kaa tumu a vɛ ya Ɛlaija koo Koosa witiin ti, yɛrɛ i chaksi nara wa ni ni?”

ref: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
text: Jɔn fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu chaksi nara wa ni ni a saksi ba a pɔ baal naa la wa chen. Ma ha wa zim un. Hali o wa ya lagmi zinan.

ref: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Waa nyɛn mu gantɛl ni aa wa. N purɛu wa mu ur o natausa a nyɛ un.”

ref: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
text: Ma lisi chenani mu fun ŋɔ ma o wɛ. Mu funɛ ŋɔ mau di nandigi nyɛn mu gantɛl ni aa wa. O funɛ piin nyɛnu ka ba lul min. Chendaha o ya nihɛɛ a kaali min.

ref: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
text: N wa fun zim naa o kaa wa ya. N funɛ te o suwachakrɛ a wa bar nɔ di mu chaksi Ju tim ta wa ni ni, chen ni mu zi kotiu a biksi ba o wɛ.”

ref: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.

text: Koosa naa funɛ tum min di mu wa chaksi nara wa ni ni chen fun te ŋɔ min, ‘Naa i na ka mu siim weri ti tu wa a zɔŋ un ni chen, waa kaa wa kpa mu siim weri ti a du nara ni.’

ref: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
text: Yesure mu fun na ka Koosa siim weri ti tu wa a zɔŋ o nyuhu ni. Wutirɛ mu ŋɔ ma di o ya Koosa bere.” Yesu Fun Te Yir O Gantɛl Tiŋsa

text: O fun kpa Simɔn a wa Yesu wati. Yesu fun a nyini un a ŋɔ, “Hin ya Simɔn, Jɔn be ti. Mu nyɛ hin sɔn faliu ba yir hin Sifas.” Sɔn ti mun ya ‘gbɛntɛl’. Yesu Fun Yir Filip Awa Nataniɛl Ba Wa Tiŋ Un

text: O fun lɛmsi ni naa funɛ burgi sin chen, a vɛ zim doonani o liili. Dam ti bisa ba naa funɛ zɔk a nyɛ un, ba waa funɛ zim doonani o li. O fun yir haan fali baal ti a ŋɔ un,

ref: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
text: Yesu funɛ sou ka Koosa lɛli un a li o sou ni, o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun lisi chenani o fun sɛm chen. Ba fun zim di o sɛma awa chenani ba fun sɔbi o wɛ a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chen, ya wutirɛ. Yesu Zim Chenani Nibinsa Bambe Duu

ref: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist

ref: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
text: Haan ti wa fun zim o sɛm ta mun a ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, i wa kpak guuga ka bugul nɔ luŋ. Leene i kaa la dɛu ni pali a wa nyɛ min?

ref: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
text: Ya nawaal, Jakɔb, funɛ hur bugul nɔ saha digi, chen ni o wa o walɛ awa o sela zi nyɔ o niu. Ya tiŋ gantɛlu a wa pu bugul ti a nyɔ un. I zen a kaali ya nawaal Jakɔbuu?”

ref: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
text: Ba naa maga ka nyɔ ni naa n nyɛ ba, nyɔksi wa zi bu kpak ba. Mu daŋni mu kpa Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ nara ni. O mi fali ti chi nisire naa nyɛ ni pal ba tuhu ni saha maga.”

ref: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
text: Haan ti funɛ nu Yesu sɛma, o fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, nyɛ min i ni ti, chen ni nyɔksa wa zi bu kpak min ka mu bur wa di mu wa dɔ ni.”

ref: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”

ref: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
text: Nara banyuu funɛ kpa hin a burgi ba haan. Baal nɔ wati i nyɛn nan ya i samnɛ. Chenani i ŋɔ min, chen ya wutirɛ.”

ref: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.

text: Kina bi naa chiŋi doon nan. Ya nawaalsa funɛ jɛɛmi Koosa o nyuhu nee, amaa ma Ju tima ŋɔ ya di Jɛrusalɛm ya Koosa bar jɛɛmi.”

ref: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.

text: Ma Samaritan tima, ma wa zim naa ma jɛɛmi nɛnɛ. Ya Ju tima ka zim naa ya jɛɛmi nɛnɛ. Waa la zi wa nyɛ Ju tim nidigi yisi tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nara tum tagna a li ba ni.

text: Yesu funɛ nu chenani ba sɛm dɔŋ ni, o fun si dooni a kaali kuŋkule nyuha bar nyɛni a la nyɛn doon o duŋduŋ. O funɛ zimu di ba kpak unu ba bambe ni di ba kpak un wa kpok ni a la vɔu un ba koora. Yesu Fun Val Ni Daha (Mat.

text: Ma ta kpa ma bambe a du diwe naa la zi wa chɔk, amaa ma ka kpa ma bambe a du Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ ni. Koosa naa ya mu nyan ti chiŋɛ mu gantɛl ni di mu ya nibine be. Chendaha mu koti a kpa chen mi fali ti a nyɛ ma.”

text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu wa mi fali diwe ta ya kibɛgimnɛ. Lɔsa wa zi bu kpak ba naa wa mu wati. Nyɔksa ka wa zi kpak ba naa nyɛ ba se su min.

ref: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.

text: ka ya se su a. Ya zimu di i ya Koosa tu chaan tiinnɛ.”

text: Mu waa zim unu. O wa min funɛ te lagmi nyɛn ka o fun tum min ma wati.”

ref: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.
text: Ba funɛ kaali doonani Yesu nyɛn chen, o fun ŋɔ ba naa maga funɛ nyɛn dooni, “Mu zi nyɛn ma watiu wuzu bumɛna a bur la naa tum min chen wati.

ref: You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Ma zi wa dɛu mu bar nyɛniu, amaa ma ka wa zi bu wa na min. Ma wa kitiŋ min la doonani mu nyɛn.”

ref: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
text: Ju tim ta wa fun zim o sɛm ta mun a la pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Leene o kaa gba la ka ya vɛ na un? O zi la Ju tim ba naa wa hɔɔra nyɛnɛɛ a la biksi ba chenuu?

ref: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
text: Waa funɛ ŋɔ ya di ya zi dɛu o bar nyɛniu ka ya ka wa zi na un. O gbanko funɛ ŋɔ yau di ya wa kitiŋ un a la doonani o nyɛn. O mun ya yɛrɛ?” Yesu Fun Kpa Ni A Nyɛ Ba Naa Nyɔksa Kpak Ni

ref: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, “If you are thirsty, come to me and drink!
text: Wuzu zene ta gantɛl tiŋna funɛ ya a kilɛmlɛm. A wuzu tirɛ Yesu fun si chiŋi zɛmɛ ta suwachak ni a tɛsi a ŋɔ, “Ba naa maga nyɔksa kpak, ma nyɛ ba wa mu wati mu kpa ni a nyɛ ba ba nyɔ.

ref: Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni saha digi a ŋɔ, ‘Ni pali zi li ba naa se su min bambe niu saha maga.’ ”

ref: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm ba ni pali ti wɛ chen, o funɛ ya Koosa siim weri ti wɛwa o sɛm. Yesu ha wa fun bur la Koosa tɔhɔn ti. Chendaha Koosa ha wa kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ ba naa se su Yesu ni. Zɛmɛ Funɛ Waligiu Yesu Biksi Ti Daha

ref: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ nu o sɛm ta, ba bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ, “Baal nɔ sir ya witiina naa Koosa ŋɔ di o zi tum ya wati wuzu digi chenu.”

text: Bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ, “Waa ya Kristo naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi tumu chen.” Bɔnɛ ka fun bukoo ŋɔ, “Kristo wa zi ya Galili tim baal.

ref: The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Ba zi lul Kristo a li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Bɛtlihɛm ka o nyɛn Judia pulum ni. O zi wa ya koor Devid bi luliu. Chendaha ba kaa wa lul un a li o tɔhɔn ti ni.’ ”

ref: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ waligiu Yesu daha.

ref: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him. The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
text: Bɔnɛ funɛ daŋni ba kpak un, amaa nandigi wa fun gbaŋsi un. Ju Tim Nihɛɛta Fun Vɛ Yesu

ref: When the temple police returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, they were asked, “Why didn't you bring Jesus here?”
text: Lanjimasa ba naa Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa Farisi tim ta fun tum di ba la kpak Yesu, fun bur wa wa nin hulɛ ni. Ju tim nihɛɛti ta fun pɛɛsi ba, “Yɛrɛ ma vɛ kpak un waali?”

ref: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
text: Lanjimasa ta fun ŋɔ ba, “Baal nɔ sɛma kaali chen naa maga ya nu.”

ref: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?
text: Farisi tim ta fun ŋɔ ba, “Wutirɛ o gbanko fun mur ma.

ref: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
text: Ma nihɛɛti ta awa Farisi tim ta nidigi wa to o si su un.

ref: And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.”
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa la nu o biksi ti wa zim Koosa chiksɛ ta ka Koosa fun ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba.”

ref: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
text: Nikɔdimus, naa funɛ te la sɛm sɛma Yesu wati, o wa ba funɛ lagmi. O fun ŋɔ ba,

ref: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
text: “Ya chiksɛ ta biksi ya di ya wa kina nar galimsa see ya nu a li o titi nɔ ni chenani o tum.”

ref: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
text: Ba fun pɛɛsi un, “I gbanko ya Galili tim narrɛɛ? Koosa sɛm tɔn ti bar digi wa to ba fun sɔbi di o witinsi ta digi ya Galili narrɛ.” ((

ref: but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives.
text: Yesu gbanko fun li dooni a kaali bi naa ba yir Ɔlif nyuhu daha.

ref: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi chɔsa, o fun si a bur kaali Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti. O funɛ la tel dooni, zɛmɛ ta fun wa lagmi a go un. O fun kani a biksi ba.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ awa Farisi tim ta fun kpak nihaan naa la daŋni nibaala yɔri yɔri. Ba fun kpa un wa Yesu wati a nyɛ o chiŋi ba maga suwachak ni.

ref: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
text: Ba nidigi fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, ya na unu ka o chɔ nibaal wati.

ref: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
text: Ya nawaalsi ta nihɛɛ, Mosis, fun a biksi ya a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di nihaan naa la ya chen, ya yaksi un wa buŋkula ni a kpu. Yɛrɛ i lisi o sɛm ti?”

ref: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
text: Ba funɛ daŋni ba mo o lo a na o galimsa. Amaa Yesu funɛ gur o nyuhau a la piigi tɛɛnɛ awa o nin ni.

ref: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
text: Ba ha funɛ pɛɛsi un chene, o zer a nyuha a nyini ba a ŋɔ, “Naa vɛ tum tum tagin, waa la zi te yak un wa buŋkul ni.”

ref: Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground.
text: O fun bu gur o nyuha a la piigi tɛɛnɛ awa o nin ni.

ref: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
text: Ba funɛ nu o sɛm ta, nihɛɛti ta fun te a li nibɛgim bɛgima hali ba wa bichɛsi ta maga wa li. Yesu wa nihaan ti duŋduŋ funɛ kpalimi dooni.

ref: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
text: Yesu fun bukoo zer o nyuha a nyini un a ŋɔ, “Leene ba naa la fiksi hin chen kaali? Nandigi wa to a ŋɔ di i kpak galimsauu?”

ref: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
text: Haan ti fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Mu kunyana, nandigi wa to.” O fun ŋɔ un, “Mu gbanko wa sɛm di i kpak galimsau. Kila dama. Ta bu tum tum tagna.”)) Yesu Chi Tɛɛnɛ Nɔ Tim Maga Fitina

ref: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
text: A saha tirɛ Yesu fun bukoo biksi zɛmɛ ta a ŋɔ ba, “Min chi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga fitinne. Ba naa maga tiŋ min wa zi bu val birsi ni. Mu zi chaani ba nyinbaan niu a kpa Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ ba.”

ref: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
text: Farisi tim ta bɔnɛ funɛ nyɛn dooni a ŋɔ un, “I sɛm ta wa ya wuti. I daŋni sɔnnɛ a nyɛ i titi ni.”

ref: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu zim doonani mu fun liu a zim doonani mu la. Chendaha sɛm naa mu sɛm mu titi wɛ nan ya wuti. Ma wa zim doonani mu fun li a zim doonani mu la.

ref: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
text: Ma yaa di nara sɛria dentil nee. Mu ka wara di nandigi sɛria.

ref: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
text: Mu di nara sɛria, mu duŋduŋ wa zi di un. Mu kunyan Koosa naa funɛ tum min chen, zi gbaari min niu a di sɛria ti. Chendaha mu sɛria ti ya wutiu.

ref: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
text: Mu sɛm mu titi wɛwa ka mu nyan naa tum min chen, gbanko sɛm mu wɛ. Chendaha ya sɛma ya wuti.”

ref: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
text: Ba fun pɛɛsi un, “Leene ya i nyan ti bar nyɛni?” Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma wa zim chenani mu du. Chendaha ma vɛ zim chenani mu nyan ti duuli. Ma fu zim min, chen fu zi nyɛ ma nyinbaanu ma zim mu nyan ti.”

ref: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
text: Yesu funɛ nyɛn doonani nara tima yaa kpa ba malɛ a dau a nyɛ Koosa ni a la biksi ba kin nama. O kpaki saha ha wa fun tel. Chendaha nandigi wa fun gbaŋsi un. Ju Tima Wa Kitiŋ Yesu A La Doonani O La

ref: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
text: Yesu fun bu ŋɔ ba, “Mu saksi di mu la bar digi. Mu wa la, ma wa kikoti a tiŋ min. Ma zi wa dɛu minu, amaa ma wa zi gil tum tagna tumi hali a wuzu naa ma wa siti.”

ref: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
text: Ju tim ta funɛ nu nan, ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “O fun ŋɔ ya di ya wa kikoti a tiŋ un a la doonani o la chen. O sɛm nɔ mun ya di o kaa kpu o titirɛɛ?”

ref: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma wa li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Mu ka li Koosa tɔhɔn tirɛ. Ma ya tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nibinsa. Mu wa ya tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nibine.

ref: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
text: Mu piin ŋɔ mau di ma wa zi gil tum tagna tumi hali a wuzu naa ma wa siti. Ma vɛ nyɛ ma se su min, chene ma kaa wa siti.”

ref: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Anti ya hin i sɛm nan sɛma a du ya ni?” Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu piin ŋɔ ma nibine naa ya minu.

ref: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
text: Naa funɛ tum min chen ya wuti tiinnɛ. Mu biksi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim magawa chenani o fun ŋɔ min duŋduŋ. Chendaha mu koti a di ma sɛria a biksi ma chenani ma bambe du.”

ref: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
text: Ba ha wa fun vɛɛn zim di Yesu ŋɔ ba o nyan Koosa wɛwa.

ref: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
text: Doone Yesu fun bukoo ŋɔ ba, “Ma zi wa kpak mu waa nibine beu a kpaasi mari daakakli mu sou, ma zi wa zimu di min ya naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen. Ma gbanko zi wa zimu di sɛm na wa li mu wati. A ya sɛm naa mu nyan ti fun kpa nyɛ min nɛ.

ref: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
text: Naa funɛ tum min chen, wa gil mu duŋduŋ. Waa nyɛn min ni. Mu yaa tiŋ o tutɔu a la sɔɔni o tuha.”

ref: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
text: Yesu fun sɛm peti, Ju tim ba naa funɛ nu o sɛm ta chen, nidamata se fun su un. Yesu Yisi Ya A Li Ya Yosi Ni

ref: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
text: Doone Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma tiŋ mu biksi nɔ, ma waa la zi ya mu gantɛl tiŋ wutɛ.

ref: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
text: Ma tiŋ nan, ma zi nu wuti tiu ka wuti ti yisi ma a li yosi ni.”

ref: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya waa ya Abraham bi lulɛ a ha vɛ ya nara yosa. I ŋɔ di wuti ti la zi yisi ya a li yosi ni. Nan mun ya yɛrɛ?”

ref: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ, “Mu ŋɔ ma, naa la tum tum tagna ya tum tagna yomne.

ref: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
text: Yosa wa kpak nyinbaan wa wu digi ni, amaa be ka kpak nyinbaan wa o nyan kin ni.

ref: If the Son gives you freedom, you are free!
text: Mu waa Koosa be koti a nyɛ ma hiisi wuti.

ref: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
text: Mu zim di ma ya Abraham bilulɛwa, amaa ma bɔnɛ daŋni ma kpu minu, domi mu biksi ti wara zu ma diŋsi ni.

ref: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
text: Mu wa mu nyan ti funɛ nyɛn, o funɛ biksi min wu damatiwa. Mu ŋɔ ma a wɛu lalaknɔ. Maa tum chenani ma nyan fun biksi marɛ.”

ref: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Abraham ya ya nyan ti.” Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma nyan ti sir ya Abraham, ma fu zi zer o naŋgeri weriu.

ref: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
text: Ma bɔnɛ daŋni ma kpu minu, domi mu la ŋɔ ma wuti naa Koosa fun biksi min chen daha. Chen laata Abraham fun du.

ref: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
text: Ma ya chen, nyan digi naŋgerire ma zer.” Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya wa ya sambisa a chi nibin bɔnɛ. Ya nyan wuti ya Koosa titi.”

ref: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma nyan ti sir ya Koosa, ma fu zi daŋni minu domi mu li o watɛɛ. Mu wa wa bar nɔ n titirɛ, amaa waa tum min.

ref: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
text: Ma wara koti a nu mu biksi ti, domi ma nyinbaan tɔu.

ref: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
text: Ma waa ya sintan bisa aa daŋni ma tiŋ o tutɔ. Saha naa Koosa fun naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga hali a wa tel lalaknɔ, sintana funɛ ya nara nikpuuli. O wa zim chenani wuti du. Waa ya nusun koora. O kaa suni wɛ, o wara bɔm un ni.

ref: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
text: Mu waa ka ŋɔ ma wuti ti wɛwa, amaa ma wa nyɛ ma se su chenani mu sɛm ma.

ref: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
text: Nandigi nyɛn ma ni a la zi biksi min di mu tum galimsi tumauu? Nandigi wa to. Mu yaa ŋɔ ma wuti tiu, ka yɛrɛ ma se vɛ su chenani mu ŋɔ ma?

ref: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
text: Ba naa ya Koosa bisa yaa nu o sɛmau. Ma waa wara daŋni ma nu a. Chen biksɛɛ di ma wa ya o bisa.” Yesu Awa Ju Tima Nawaal Abraham

ref: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
text: Doone Ju tim ta fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Ya ŋɔɛɛ di i ya Samaria tim nidigirɛ ka alizin tagin kani i nyuhu ni, wuti laata ya sɛm?”

ref: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Alizin tagin wa kani mu nyuhu ni. Mu nyɛ mu nyan ti jirimarɛ, amaa ma waa ka wara nyɛ min jirima.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
text: Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu chenani mu kaa sɛm ma. Ba naa maga yaa tiŋ mu nɔ, ba wa zi wa sou.”

ref: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
text: Ju tim ta fun ŋɔ, “Lalaknɔwa ya zim di i kpak alizin taginu. Hin ŋɔ ya di ba naa maga tiŋ i nɔ wa zi sou, amaa ya nawaal, Abraham, awa Koosa witinsi ta maga funɛ sitiu saha digi.

ref: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
text: I lisi di hin kaali baa? I lisu di baŋa ya hinn?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu wara nyɛ n titi wa jirima. Mu titi jirima nyɛɛli ya yɔrirɛ. Mu nyan ti ka yaa kpa o jirima a nyɛ min. O wa naa ma yir Koosa chen ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
text: Ma wa zim un, amaa mu waa ka zim unu. Mu fu ŋɔ di mu wa zim un, mu fu zi ya nusun tiinau a chi ma ya nusun tim chen. Mu zim unu aa tiŋ o nɔ.

ref: Your father Abraham was really glad to see me.”
text: Ma nawaal Abraham tuha funɛ sɔɔniu di mu zi wa tɛɛnɛ nɔu.”

ref: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Maa nu chenani mu kaa sɛm ma. Mu funɛ piin nyɛnu ka ba zi lul Abraham.”

ref: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
text: Ju tim ta funɛ nu o sɛm ta, ba fun pini buŋkula di ba yaksi un, amaa Yesu funɛ sogliu a la li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni.

ref: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
text: Wuzu digi Yesu funɛ val a la chene a la na baal naa ba fun lul wa nyulumsi ni chen.

ref: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun pɛɛsi un, “Ya kunyana, yɛrɛ ba fun lul un ka o nyulumi? O tum tagna daharɛ o nyulumi nan, koo o nyana awa o niin tum tagna daharɛɛ?”

ref: “No, it wasn't!” Jesus answered. “But because of his blindness, you will see God work a miracle for him.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “O nyulumsi ti wa kpak wu digi wa o tum tagna ni koo o nyana awa o niin tum tagna ni. O nyulumu chen ni nara zi na chenani Koosa kpok zi tumu.

ref: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
text: Lalaknɔwa nyinbaan puk a nyɛ ya di yaa tum tuma a nyɛ naa funɛ tum min ni chen. O wa zi vusi ka nyinbaan ti tɔ ya bu vɛ koti a tum wu digi.

ref: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
text: Chenani mu nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni nan, min chi fitina a chaani a nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ni.”

ref: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm peti, o fun tɔsi nantɔsa a du hɛɛra ni a gɔɔri a kpa tiksi baal ti se,

ref: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
text: a ŋɔ un, “Kila a la sɛm i se a li pul naa ba yir Sailom chen.” Sailom ti mun ya, ‘doonani mu fun tum hin’. Nyulum ti fun kaali a la sɛm o se ta dooni a lɛli. O fun bur wa.

ref: The man's neighbors and the people who had seen him begging wondered if he really could be the same man.
text: Doone o kandɔŋsa awa ba naa funɛ na un ka o sumi sumi kina chen fun a pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Baal nɔ, o laata yaa kani bar nɔ a sumi kina chenn?”

ref: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
text: Bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ, “Warɛ,” amaa bɔnɛ ka fun ŋɔ, “O laata. O chillɛ.” Baal ti titi fun ŋɔ ba, “Min chen.”

ref: “Then how can you see?” they asked.
text: Ba fun a pɛɛsi un, “Yɛrɛ i ya i se tima lɛli?”

ref: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Baal naa ba yir Yesu chen, waa funɛ tɔsi nantɔsa a du hɛɛra ni a gɔɔri a kpa tiksi mu se ni a ŋɔ min di mu la sɛm mu se ta a li Sailom pul ti ni. Mu fun la sɛm a dooni a puk.”

ref: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
text: Ba fun bukoo pɛɛsi un, “Leene baal ti ka?” O fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu wa zim.” Farisi Tima Wa Fun Zim Chenani Yesu Tiibi Un Chen

ref: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
text: A wuzu naa Yesu fun ya vɛri ti a du baal ti se ta ni di a puk chen funɛ ya Ju tim ta hiisi wuzurɛ.

ref: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
text: Farisi tim ta fun pɛɛsi un, “Yɛrɛ o ya i se nama lɛli?” O fun ŋɔ ba, “Baal naa ba yir Yesu chen, kpa vɛri a du mu se ta ni. Mu fun la sɛm a aa na.”

ref: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
text: Farisi tim ta funɛ nu o ta, ba bambe fun purigi. Ba bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Naa ya chen wa li Koosa wati, domi o tum ya hiisi wuzurɛ.” Ba bɔnɛ ka fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Tum tagna nitumi wa koti a tum tum zene a chi nan.”

ref: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
text: Ba fun bukoo pɛɛsi un, “Baal naa i ŋɔ di waa puk i se nan, yɛrɛ i lisi kpa la o wati?” O fun ŋɔ ba, “O ya Koosa witinsi ta digirɛ.”

ref: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
text: Farisi tim ta se wa fun su di baal ti funɛ ya nyulumnɛ a bur a na. Adaha ba fun yir baal ti nyana awa o niina,

ref: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
text: a pɛɛsi ba, “Ma be nɛɛ? Ma lul un nyulum, yɛrɛ o kaa ya a bur na?”

ref: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ, “Ya be ya un. Ya waa lul un nyulum,

ref: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
text: amaa ya wa zim chenani o ya a na koo naa lɛlɛ o se ta. Ma la pɛɛsi un chenani o ya o se ta lɛli. O ya nihɛɛu a la zi sɛm ma.”

ref: The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
text: Chendaha ba fun ŋɔ, “O ya nihɛɛu a la zi sɛm ma. Ma la pɛɛsi un chenani o ya o se ta lɛli.”

ref: The leaders called the man back and said, “Swear by God to tell the truth! We know that Jesus is a sinner.”
text: Farisi tim ta fun bu yir baal naa bu vɛ nyulumi chen a ŋɔ un, “Baal naa lɛlɛ i se chen ya tum tagna nitumirɛ. Ta nyɛ un jirima, amaa ka nyɛ Koosa wa jirima.”

ref: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
text: Baal ti fun ŋɔ ba, “O ya tum tagna nitumi koo o wa ya, mu wa zim. Mu zim di mu funɛ ya nyulumnɛ, amaa lalaknɔ mu nau.”

ref: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
text: Ba fun bu pɛɛsi un, “Yɛrɛ o ya i se na lɛli?”

ref: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu piin ŋɔ mau ka ma vɛ nuuli. Ma bur a daŋni mu ŋɔ ma a gbaariuu? Ma gbanko daŋni ma tiŋ unuu?”

ref: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
text: Ba funɛ nu o sɛm ta chen, ba funɛ zulimi un a ŋɔ, “Hin ya o gantɛl tiŋsi ta digi. Ya waa ka ya ya nawaal Mosis gantɛl tiŋsa.

ref: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
text: Ya zim di Koosa funɛ sɛm sɛma a nyɛ Mosis ni, amaa naa lɛlɛ i se ta chen, ya wa zim doonani o li.”

ref: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
text: Baal ti fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma wa zim doonani baal nɔ li a wa lɛli mu se na. Baŋ alaazibu wɛnɛ.

ref: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
text: Nara maga zim di Koosa wara nu tum tagna tiin nɔ. O ka nu ba naa la nyɛ un jirima aa tiŋ o tutɔ nɔrɛ.

ref: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga piili ni kpa la hali wa zinan, nandigi ha wa lɛli naa ba lul wa nyulumsa, se.

ref: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
text: Chenani o tum chen biksi di o li Koosa watɛɛ.”

ref: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ba funɛ lul hin di i yaa kitum tum tagniwa. I kpa i nyuhurɛ a mo yaa?” Ba fun zaksi un a li ba bar jɛɛmi dɛ ti ni a ta. Farisi Tima Wa Fun Koti A Nu Yesu Biksi Ti

ref: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
text: Yesu funɛ nu chenani ba ya un, o fun wa ŋɔ un, “I si su mu waa nibine beuu?”

ref: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
text: Baal ti fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, ŋɔ min chenani o du, chen ni mu gbanko si zi su unu.”

ref: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “N waa naa la sɛm nan ya un. Hali i koti a na minu lalaknɔ.”

ref: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
text: Baal ti fun gbiri Yesu suwachak ni a jɛɛmi un a ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, mu si su hinu.”

ref: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
text: Doone Yesu fun ŋɔ, “Mu funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga di mu wa nyɛ ba naa se vɛ lɛlirɛ lɛli a biksi ba naa ŋɔɛɛ di ba se lɛlɛu ka a vɛ lɛli.”

ref: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma se fu vɛ lɛli, mu fu wa zi na ma galimsa. Domi ma ŋɔ di ma se lɛlɛu ka a vɛ lɛli, mu zi na ma galimsau.”

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
text: Doone Yesu fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgi a ŋɔ ba, “Maa nu mu wati nɛnɛ. Nandigi zu piisisa bol dusum a vɛ tiŋ bol zɔɔnɔ a zuuli, ma zim di o ya ŋmɛɛrrɛ.

ref: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
text: Bol zɔɔnɔ niili yaa puk bol zɔɔnɔ ti a nyɛ un. O yaa zu a yir o piisisi ta sɔna. A tiŋ un a li domi a zim o tau.

ref: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
text: O wa a wa li, a yaa tiŋ o mun nee domi a zim o ta daha.

ref: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
text: A yaa tiŋ o duŋduŋnɛ. Nandigi wa te a suwachak, a zi pisiu domi a vɛ zim o ta daha.”

ref: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
text: Yesu fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgi ti peti, ba naa funɛ nu o sɛm ta chen wa fun zim o mun. Yesu La Dɛɛsi O Piisisa Nɛnɛ

ref: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
text: Nan daha, Yesu fun bukoo ŋɔ ba, “Maa nu mu wati nɛnɛ. O chi di min ya bol zɔɔnɔ ti.

ref: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
text: Nidamata ba naa te wa chiŋi mu suwachak ni chi ŋmɛɛrsi tawa. Mu piisisi ta wara nu ba sɛma.

ref: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
text: Ba naa maga nyɛ mu yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni chi piisisi naa yaa wa zu a bol a li di diwe chene.

ref: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
text: Ŋmɛɛr yaa wa zu bol di o ŋmɛ piisisi ta a kpu a. Min ka la dɛɛsi a. Min funɛ wa di mu wa kpa Koosa mi fali ti a nyɛ nara ni. Waa kaali ba titi miisa kinkan.

ref: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
text: “Min la dɛɛsi mu piisisi ta a kaali piisi gbaŋsi maga. Hali mu saksɛu di mu sou a nyɛ a.

ref: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
text: Tumtumna nyini piisisa, o wa zi nyini a nɛnɛ. Gbeha wa wa di o wa pisi a dɔŋ ni a kpak a, o zi chɔu a ka a, domi o wa dɔ a.

ref: Hired workers run away because they don't care about the sheep.
