text: Ba funɛ bɔ un o nyini ti daha. Chendaha o vɛ chiŋi a kok a.

ref: I am the good shepherd. I know my sheep, and they know me.
text: Min la dɛɛsi mu piisisi ta a kaali piisi gbaŋsi maga. Mu zim au ka a ka zim min.

ref: Just as the Father knows me, I know the Father, and I give up my life for my sheep.
text: Chen vɛɛna mu zim mu nyan tiu ka mu nyan ti ka zim min. Hali mu saksɛu di mu sou a nyɛ mu piisisi ta ni.

ref: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
text: Piisi bɔnɛwa mu dɔ ka a nyɛn bar digi ni. O lawa mu la a nu mu tau, chen ni a zi tiŋ minu. A wa mu piisisi ta lagmi dɔŋ ni, chen ni a zi ya kibɛgimu ka nigbaŋi bɛgim kinyini a.

ref: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
text: “Mu nyan ti daŋni minu, domi mu saksɛu di mu sou a nyɛ a. N wa sou, mu zi si a li mu sou niu.

ref: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
text: Nandigi wa kpak kpok a la zi yisi mu miisa. O ya mu tutɔrɛ nara zi wa ya chen. Mu nyan ti funɛ kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ min di mu nyini wuzu a sou. N wa sou, o kpok naa nyɛn min ni chen, zi nyɛ mu siu a li mu sou ni.”

ref: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
text: Farisi tim ta funɛ nu o sɛm ta, ba bambe fun bu purigi.

ref: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
text: Ba nidamata fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Baal nɔ, alizin tagin nyɛn o nyuhu ni ka o zu galɛŋa. Baŋnɛ ma chiŋi a nu di ma ya?”

ref: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
text: Ba bɔnɛ ka fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Alizin tagin nyɛn nar ni, o wa kikoti a sɛm nan. Alizin tagin wa kikoti a lɛli naa ba fun lul wa nyulumsa, se.” Ju Tima Fun Vɛ Yesu

ref: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
text: Wuzu zene ta bambaan ni, Yesu fun val Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam zaŋ naa ba yir koor Sɔlɔmɔn zaŋ.

ref: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
text: Ju tim ta bɔnɛ fun wa jiŋ un chiŋsi a pɛɛsi un, “Yɛrɛ i kaa ya sogli i titi a li ya ni? I ya naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi tumu wuzu digi chen, ŋɔ ya gbampulim ni.”

ref: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu piin ŋɔ ma chenani mu duu, amaa ma se wa fun su mu sɛma. Tum nawara mu tum nan a li mu nyan ti kpok nee, a wa la biksi ma di chenani mu sɛm ma chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
text: Mu zim mu piisisau ka a gbanko zim mu ta a la tiŋ min.

ref: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
text: Mu kpa Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ a, chen ni o wa zi bu na a galimsa a ta a yɔri. Nandigi wa zi fuksi a n wati.

ref: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
text: Mu nyana naa zen a kaali kin maga, kpa a a du mu nin ni. O wa zi nyɛ nandigi fuksi a a li mu nin ni.

ref: and I am one with the Father.
text: Mu wa mu nyan ti ya nibɛgimnɛ.”

ref: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
text: Ju tim ta funɛ nu o sɛm ta chen, ba fun bukoo pini buŋkula di ba yaksi un.

ref: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ, “Mu nyan ti funɛ nyɛ mu tum tum were kinkannɛ di ma na. O leema daharɛ ma daŋni ma kpu min?”

ref: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
text: Ju tim ta fun ŋɔ, “Ya wara daŋni ya kpu hin i tum were daha. I ya nibinere a la pini Koosa zoma a kpa i titi a maksi un. Chendaha ya daŋni ya kpu hin.”

ref: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
text: Mu nyan ti funɛ lisi min a tum min tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Mu ŋɔ ma di mu ya o bere, yɛrɛ ma ŋɔ min di mu pini o zomwa?

ref: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
text: Ma la nɔɔni di tum naa mu tum nan, ya mu nyan ti tumwa, ma nyɛ ma se su mu sɛm ta.

ref: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
text: Hali ma se vɛ su mu sɛm ta, ma nyɛ ma se su mu tum ta. Ma ya chen, ma zi zim nɛnɛu di mu nyan ti nyɛn min nɛɛ ka mu ka nyɛn un ni.”

ref: Again they wanted to arrest Jesus. But he escaped
text: Ju tim ta funɛ daŋni ba kpak un, amaa Yesu funɛ fuksi ba nin niu.

ref: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
text: O fun bur la ji Jɔɔdan mɔɔg jibaŋ kinkin ti a la tel doonani Jɔn fun te chaksi nara wa ni ni a nyɛn dooni.

ref: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
text: Nidamata fun wa o wati a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Jɔn wa fun tum tum zene, amaa chenani o fun ŋɔ ya baal nɔ wɛ, chen ya wutirɛ.”

ref: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
text: Doonee ba se fun su un.

ref: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
text: O toosi ta fun tum di ba la ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Ya kunyana, i chɛn birigi la wi.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he said, “His sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.”
text: Yesu funɛ nu laaboor ti, o fun ŋɔ ba naa kani o wati chen, “Lazarus fu kaa sou, o fu wa zi vusi nan. Chenani o du un ni chen zi nyɛ nara kinyɛ Koosa awa o be jirimau kinkan.”

ref: But he stayed where he was for two more days.
text: amaa o funɛ kpalimi doonani o nyɛn chenu wuzu bilɛ.

ref: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
text: Wuzu bilɛ ta gantɛl nɛɛ, o fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Ma nyɛ ya bur a la Judia pulum.”

ref: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya kunyana, chenani i kaa ya chen wa deŋli. O wa fun vusi ka Ju tima ba naa nyɛn dooni, fun pini buŋkula di ba yaksi hin.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Karifisa kin fidalɛ ya nha chela. Mu chi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga fitinne. Mu nyɛn ma wati, ma wa zi tu bɔsi ni, domi ma nau nɛnɛ.

ref: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
text: Amaa mu ka vɛ nyɛn ma wati ka ma gba birsi ni, ma zi tu a niu, domi ma wara na a.”

ref: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
text: Yesu fun bukoo ŋɔ, “Ya chɛn Lazarus chɔ dore. Mu zi la laasi unu.”

ref: They replied, “Lord, if he is asleep, he will get better.”
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun ŋɔ un, “Ya kunyana, o chɔ do, o zi bu na alaafɛu.”

ref: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
text: Ba fun lisi di o hiisiu, amaa Yesu sɛm ta mun funɛ ya di Lazarus sou.

ref: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
text: Mu tuha sɔɔnɛu mu vɛ la dooni, domi chenani o du un ni la zi nyɛ ma se su min a gbaari. Ma nyɛ ya la o wati.”

ref: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
text: Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta nidigi naa funɛ nyɛn o wati, ba yir un Tɔmas. Ba funɛ vɛɛn a yir un leure. O fun ŋɔ o dɔŋtim ta ni, “Ma nyɛ ya tiŋ ya kunyana, chen ni ya wa un zi sowu.” Yesu Zi Lɛli Narau A Li Ba Sou Ni

ref: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
text: Yesu awa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ la tel Bɛtani, ba naa nyɛn dooni, fun ŋɔ un di ba huk Lazarus o zinan ya bunaasi.

ref: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,
text: Bɛtani awa Jɛrusalɛm funɛ ya maalisa lɛwa.

ref: and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
text: Nan daha, Ju tim ta nidamata funɛ piin nyɛn dooni di ba zaami Marta awa Mɛri ba maan ti luha.

ref: When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house.
text: Marta funɛ nu di Yesu nyɛn nyinbaan ni aa wa, o fun si li di o la chem un. Mɛri duŋduŋ funɛ nyɛn ba dam ni.

ref: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
text: Marta fun la chem Yesu a ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, i fu nyɛn bar nɔ, mu maan ti fu wa zi sou.

ref: Jesus told her, “Your brother will live again!”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “I maan ti zi si o sou niu.”

ref: Martha answered, “I know he will be raised to life on the last day, when all the dead are raised.”
text: Marta fun ŋɔ un, “Mu zim di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa tel, o zi si o sou niu.”

ref: Jesus then said, “I am the one who raises the dead to life! Everyone who has faith in me will live, even if they die.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ, “Min ya naa la zi koti a lɛli nara a li ba sou ni a nyɛ ba bu kpak miisa. Naa si su min ka o sou, mu zi ya chenu a nyɛ un.

ref: “Yes, Lord!” she replied. “I believe you are the Christ, the Son of God. You are the one we hoped would come into the world.”
text: O fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, mu si su chenu. Mu gbanko si su di hin ya Koosa be awa Kristo naa o fun ŋɔ di o zi tumu tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni saha digi chen.” Yesu Funɛ Wi Lazarus Daha

ref: After Martha said this, she went and privately said to her sister Mary, “The Teacher is here, and he wants to see you.”
text: Marta funɛ sɛm nan peti, o fun bur la zu dam ti a ŋɔ Mɛri ni ba duŋduŋ, “Ya kunyana wau aa daŋni o na hin.”

ref: As soon as Mary heard this, she got up and went out to Jesus.
text: Mɛri fun vaan si a kaali o wati.

ref: He was still outside the village where Martha had gone to meet him.
text: Yesu ha wa fun wa zu tɔhɔn tuha a fun kpalimi cheŋi doonani Marta fun wa chem un.

ref: Many people had come to comfort Mary, and when they saw her quickly leave the house, they thought she was going out to the tomb to cry. So they followed her.
text: Ju tima, ba naa funɛ wa zaami Mɛri, funɛ na unu ka o si lalaga a li, ba gbanko fun si a tiŋ un. Ba fun baa di o la hugil ti munnɛ o la wi dooni.

ref: Mary went to where Jesus was. Then as soon as she saw him, she knelt at his feet and said, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
text: Meri fun la pu Yesu a gbiri o suwachak ni a ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, i fu nyɛn bar nɔ, mu maan ti fu wa zi sou.”

ref: When Jesus saw that Mary and the people with her were crying, he was terribly upset
text: Yesu funɛ na di o wa Ju tima ba naa funɛ tiŋ un chen, funɛ wiu, ba fun kpa ba tu chɔk ti a bu un o gbanko wi.

ref: and asked, “Where have you put his body?” They replied, “Lord, come and you will see.”
text: O fun pɛɛsi ba, “Leene ma huk un?” Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya kunyana, kuwa a wa na.”

ref: Jesus started crying,
text: Yesu fun a wi.

ref: and the people said, “See how much he loved Lazarus.”
text: Ju tim ta bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Wutirɛ o daŋni un kinkan.”

ref: Jesus was still terribly upset. So he went to the tomb, which was a cave with a stone rolled against the entrance.
text: Ba fun kpa Lazarus a du gbɛntɛl bɔ ni a zer buŋkul a tɔ o zɔɔnɔ. Yesu funɛ la tel hugil ti mun, ba fun bu kpa ba tu chɔk ti a bu un.

ref: Then he told the people to roll the stone away. But Martha said, “Lord, you know that Lazarus has been dead four days, and there will be a bad smell.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Ma zer buŋkul ti a ta.” Marta naa funɛ ya Lazarus towe, fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, ya funɛ huk un o zinan ya bunaasi ka o pɔ.”

ref: Jesus replied, “Didn't I tell you that if you had faith, you would see the glory of God?”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Mu piin ŋɔ hinu di i si su min, i zi na chenani Koosa tum tum zenu.”

ref: After the stone had been rolled aside, Jesus looked up toward heaven and prayed, “Father, I thank you for answering my prayer.
text: Ba fun zer buŋkul ti ta a li hugil ti nɔ ni, Yesu fun a nyini buudaha ni a ŋɔ, “Mu nyana, mu zaami hinu di i nu mu nɔ saha maga.

ref: I know that you always answer my prayers. But I said this, so the people here would believe you sent me.”
text: Mu titi zimu di i nu mu nɔu, amaa mu sɛm nan a nyɛ hin di ba naa nyɛn bar nɔwa se su di hin funɛ tum min.”

ref: When Jesus had finished praying, he shouted, “Lazarus, come out!”
text: O funɛ sɛm peti, o fun yir ta zaŋa a ŋo, “Lazarus, li wa.”

ref: The man who had been dead came out. His hands and feet were wrapped with strips of burial cloth, and a cloth covered his face. Jesus then told the people, “Untie him and let him go.” The Plot To Kill Jesus (Matthew
text: O fun si a li wa. Ba naa funɛ sogli un chen funɛ kpa gar pilimnɛ a mɛɛsi o nisa awa o naŋgere a tɔ o se a gbaari. Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma pur un a nyɛ o la.” Ba fun pur un a nyɛ o kaali. Chenani Ju Tima Funɛ Daŋni Ba Kpak Yesu (Mat.

ref: Many of the people who had come to visit Mary saw the things Jesus did, and they put their faith in him.
text: Ju tim nidamata ba naa funɛ wa di ba wa zaami Mɛri awa Marta, fun na chenani Yesu tum a nyɛ ba se su un.

ref: Others went to the Pharisees and told what Jesus had done.
text: Ba bɔnɛ ka fun kaali Farisi tim ta wati a la ŋɔ ba chenani Yesu tum.

ref: Then the chief priests and the Pharisees called the council together and said, “What should we do? This man is working a lot of miracles.
text: Doone Farisi tim ta fun yir Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta ba lagmi a na dɔŋa a zim chenani ba zi ya. Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Baal nɔ tum tum zene kinkannɛ.

ref: If we don't stop him now, everyone will put their faith in him. Then the Romans will come and destroy our temple and our nation.”
text: O lawa ya ya wu digiu o wati. Ya gil un o kaali, nara ta maga zi bur a tiŋ un. Ba ya chen, Roman tima ba naa dɔ ya, zi wa chɔk Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam tiu a kpu ya maga.”

ref: One of the council members was Caiaphas, who was also high priest that year. He spoke up and said, “You people don't have any sense at all!
text: Baal naa Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta fun lisi a li ba tuhu ni di o ya ba nihɛɛ, o sɔn funɛ ya Kaifas. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma nidigi wa kpak nɛha.

ref: Don't you know it is better for one person to die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed?”
text: Ya wara daŋni di Roman tim ta wa kpu ya maga. Ma nyɛ baal nɔ chiŋi ya maga bar chiŋi ni a sou nyɛ ya.”

ref: Caiaphas did not say this on his own. As high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the nation.
text: Chenani o fun sɛm chen wa li o titi ni. Domi waa funɛ ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti a bun ti, o funɛ chi Koosa witinsi ta digirɛ a sɛm di Yesu zi sou Ju tim ta daharɛ.

ref: Yet Jesus would not die just for the Jewish nation. He would die to bring together all of God's scattered people.
text: O sɛm ta mun funɛ zen a kaali chenu. Yesu sou daha, Koosa bisa maga, ba naa pisi a go tɛɛne nɔ maga zi wa lagmi dɔŋ niu a ya nibɛgim.

ref: Because of this plot against him, Jesus stopped going around in public. He went to the town of Ephraim, which was near the desert, and he stayed there with his disciples.
text: Yesu funɛ nu chenani ba daŋni ba ya, o wa fun bura gba gbampulim ni a li Judia pulum ti ni. O wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun li doon pulum ti ni a la nyɛn tɔhɔn naa ba yir Ifreem ka o dugli nyabaan chen.

ref: It was almost time for Passover. Many of the Jewish people who lived out in the country had come to Jerusalem to get themselves ready for the festival.
text: Ju tim ta wuzu zen naa ba yir val la gali ti chen funɛ wa dugli. Nidamata fun li ba tɔsa a kaali Jɛrusalɛm di ba te la chaksi ba tusa a li ba tum tagna ni ka wuzu zen ti zi tel.

ref: They looked around for Jesus. Then when they were in the temple, they asked each other, “You don't think he will come here for Passover, do you?”
text: Ba funɛ nyini di ba na Yesu dooni. Ba bɔnɛ fun lagmi a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Ma lisi di o zi wa lagmi ya ni a di wuzu zen tiu koo o wa zi wa lagmi?”

ref: The chief priests and the Pharisees told the people to let them know if any of them saw Jesus. This is how they hoped to arrest him.
text: Farisi tim ta awa Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta fun mɔɔli di naa zim doonani Yesu nyɛn, ba wa ŋɔ ba, chen ni ba zi la kpak unu.

ref: If you serve me, you must go with me. My servants will be with me wherever I am. If you serve me, my Father will honor you.
text: Ma la ŋɔ Girik tim ta ni di ba daŋni ba wa min tum tuma, ba wa tiŋ min, chen ni ba zi nyɛn doonani mu nyɛnu. Ba ya chen, mu nyan ti zi nyɛ ba jirimau.” Yesu Fun Sɛm O Sou Sɛma

ref: Judas did not really care about the poor. He asked this because he carried the moneybag and sometimes would steal from it.
text: Judas baga wa fun to di o suŋi fara tima. O funɛ nyini Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta malɛwa a la suŋi o titi a li malɛ ta ni.

ref: they took palm branches and went out to greet him. They shouted, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord! God bless the King of Israel!”
text: Ba kpa bone kpachaga a li di ba la chem un. Ba funɛ la o bar chemi chene a la chɛɛmi a ŋɔ, “Tabarkala. I waa naa Koosa tum di i ya ya koora, yaa zaami hinu kinkan.”

ref: Jesus found a donkey and rode on it, just as the Scriptures say,
text: Yesu funɛ na ka zɛmɛ ta wa, o fun nyɛ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta kpa kokum be pɔl a wa nyɛ un o zini a la.

ref: Mary took a very expensive bottle of perfume and poured it on Jesus' feet. She wiped them with her hair, and the sweet smell of the perfume filled the house.
text: Ba funɛ di chene, Mɛri fun kpak tulale awa gbakme suha naa malɛ fun a kpok kinkan, a biti o maga a du Yesu naŋgere ni a fiisi a awa o nyuksi ni. Dam ti maga fun su wa tulale ti sɔɔra weri ni.

ref: A lot of people came when they heard that Jesus was there. They also wanted to see Lazarus, because Jesus had raised him from death.
text: Laaboor funɛ pisi a su di Yesu nyɛn Bɛtani ni, Ju tim ta nidamata fun si di ba la na un. Ba gbanko funɛ daŋni ba la na Lazarus, naa Yesu fun lɛli a li o sou ni chen.

ref: A meal had been prepared for Jesus. Martha was doing the serving, and Lazarus himself was there.
text: Ba fun ya diwe di ba wa Yesu di. Yesu fun wa o wa Lazarus fun lagmi kani. Marta fun kpa diwe ta a wa nyɛ ba ba di.

ref: A disciple named Judas Iscariot was there. He was the one who was going to betray Jesus, and he asked,
text: Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta digi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Judas Iskariɔt. Waa funɛ ya naa kaa li Yesu gantɛl. O fun ŋɔ,

ref: Jesus replied, “Leave her alone! She has kept this perfume for the day of my burial.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Gil haan ti. O funɛ te biti tulale nɔ a du mu bara ni di ba wa sogli min.

ref: You will always have the poor with you, but you won't always have me.” A Plot To Kill Lazarus
text: Saha maga fara tim ta yaa nyɛn ma watɛɛ, amaa mu wa ma zi nyɛn vusi.” Chenani Ju Tima Funɛ Daŋni Ba Kpak Lazarus

ref: Philip from Bethsaida in Galilee was there too. So they went to him and said, “Sir, we would like to meet Jesus.”
text: Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta digi sɔn funɛ ya Filip. O tɔha funɛ ya Bɛtseida a li Galili pulum ni. Ba fun wa ŋɔ un, “Ya nihɛɛ, ya daŋni ya la pu Yesuu.”

ref: But the Pharisees said to each other, “There is nothing we can do! Everyone in the world is following Jesus.” Some Greeks Want To Meet Jesus
text: Farisi tim ta funɛ nu chenani zɛmɛ ta zaami un, ba funɛ ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “O di yau. Ma nyini a na nidamata ba naa tiŋ un.” Girik Tim Bɔnɛ Funɛ Daŋni Ba La Pu Yesu

ref: So Father, bring glory to yourself. A voice from heaven then said, “I have already brought glory to myself, and I will do it again!”
text: Doone o fun sumi Koosa a ŋɔ, “Mu nyana, nyɛ nara kinyɛ hin jirima chenani ba kaa ya min daha.” Ba fun vaan nu ta a li buudaha ni ka o ŋɔ, “Mu funɛ nyɛ nara nyɛ min jirima ka mu zi bu nyɛ ba ya chen.”

ref: “The Lord has blinded the eyes of the people, and he has made the people stubborn. He did this so that they could not see or understand, and so that they would not turn to the Lord and be healed.”
text: “Koosa funɛ nyɛ ba se tɔ a nyɛ ba bambe ya bambe sitɛ, chen ni ba wa zi koti a na aa zim chenani o ya. O vɛ ya chen, ba bambe zi chɛrgiu chen ni o gil ba tum tagna a nyɛ ba.”

ref: The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival,
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, zɛmɛ kinkan ba naa wa Jɛrusalɛm di ba wa di wuzu zen ti, fun nu di Yesu nyɛn nyinbaan ni aa wa.

ref: “People of Jerusalem, don't be afraid! Your King is now coming, and he is riding on a donkey.”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ma Jɛrusalɛm tima, ma ta nyɛ hansura kpak ma. Ma koora zi wa zini kokum be pɔlu a wa ma wati.”

ref: At first, Jesus' disciples did not understand. But after he had been given his glory, they remembered all this. Everything had happened exactly as the Scriptures said it would.
text: Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ kpa kokum be pɔl ti a nyɛ un, ba waa fun lisi chenani ba sɔbi o wɛ a du Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni. O funɛ sou a si o sou ni a bu kaali Koosa tɔhɔn ti, doone ba fun zim chenani ba fun sɔbi chen mun.

ref: A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle.
text: Chendaha zɛmɛ ta fun li di ba la chem Yesu. Ba funɛ daŋni ba na naa tum tum zen ti chen.

ref: Some Greeks had gone to Jerusalem to worship during Passover.
text: Girik tim bɔnɛ gbanko funɛ nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni di ba di wuzu zen ti.

ref: Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him. The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
text: Filip fun la ŋɔ Andiru ni chenani ba daŋni ba la pu Yesu. Nara balɛ ta fun tiŋ dɔŋa a kaali o wati.

ref: Jesus said: The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Saha telu di Koosa lisi a li o jirima awa chaani kinkan ni a nyɛ mu waa nibine be ni.

ref: I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat.
text: Maa nu mu wati nɛnɛ. Ma zim di kiduha tiina yaa kpa kibɛgim bɛgimwa a du. Kiduha ti titi yaa pɔu ka kifali nyu a zini a nɔn kidamata. O vɛ pɔɔli, o wa zi nyu a zini a nɔn.

ref: Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer.
text: Doone Yesu fun burgi a ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni, “Mu bambi bure min ni. Mu wa koti a sumi mu nyan ti di o lisi min a li kin naa kaa wa ya nan ni. O funɛ tum min di mu wa chem chene.”

ref: When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ chiŋi doon chen bɔnɛ fun nu ta ti a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “O chi nasɛha tɛsɛɛ.” Bɔnɛ ka ŋɔ, “Malaika ŋɔ un.”

ref: Then Jesus told the crowd, “That voice spoke to help you, not me.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ta naa ma nu chen, o ya ma kinnɛ. O wa ya mu kin.

ref: This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out!
text: Chenani Koosa zi di tɛɛne nɔ tim maga sɛria a nyaŋi sintana naa dɔ ba, duglɛu.

ref: If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.”
text: Ba wa kpaasi min a mari daakakli ni di mu sou, nidamata bambe zi nyɛn min niu.”

ref: Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
text: Chenani o sɛm ba nan biksi ba nyinbaan naa o zi wa sou.

ref: The crowd said to Jesus, “The Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
text: Zɛmɛ ta nidigi fun ŋɔ un, “Ba funɛ sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di Kristo naa Koosa fun vɔu di o tum chen zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Yɛrɛ i sɛm ya di nara zi kpak nibine beu a kpaasi mari daakakli ti ni o sou? Ya wa zim nibine naa ya un.”

ref: Jesus answered, “The light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu chi fitinne a nyɛn ma wati. O wa zi vusi ka birsi wa tɔ min. Ma kpak fitina a val doonani ma daŋni ma val. Birsi ti wa tɔ un, ma zi nyuksi nyinbaanu.

ref: Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.” The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding.
text: Mu ha nyɛn ma wati wuzu bilɛ nan, ma nyɛ ma se su min, chen ni ma nyinbaan zi chaaniu saha maga.” Yesu funɛ sɛm peti, o fun la sogli o titi. Chenani Ju Tim Ta Bɔnɛ Se Fun Vɛ Su Yesu

ref: He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him.
text: O funɛ tum tum zeneu kinkan a li ba suwachak ni, amaa ba nidamata se wa fun su un.

ref: This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true, “Lord, who has believed our message? And who has seen your mighty strength?”
text: Chendaha Koosa witiina Azaaya sɛma naa o fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni saha digi chen zi ya wutiu. O fun ŋɔ, “Koosa funɛ tum tum zeneu ba suwachak ni, amaa ba se wa fun su o laaboor weri naa ya fun sɛm ba.”

ref: The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
text: Azaaya fun bu sɔbi ba naa se vɛ su Yesu wɛ a ŋɔ,

ref: Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him.
text: Azaaya funɛ na Yesu jirima awa chaani kinkanu a chi o deesi. Chendaha o fun sɔbi nan.

ref: Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus.
text: Ju tim nihɛɛ damata funɛ nyɛ ba se su Yesu, amaa ba wa fun nyɛ ba se su un a li gbampulim ni, domi ba funɛ ɔm Farisi tim tawa. Farisi tim ta zim chenani ba ya, ba zi duŋni bau a li ba bar lagmi dɛɛni ni.

ref: And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God. Jesus Came To Save the World
text: Ba daŋni narawa kinyɛ ba jirima a kaali chenani ba daŋni Koosa kinyɛ ba jirima. Sɛm Nawara Yesu Fun Sɛm Zi Wa Di Nara Sɛriau

ref: In a loud voice Jesus said: Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me.
text: Doone Yesu fun zer o ta buudaha a ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni, “Naa si su min, o si su Koosa naa tum min chen gbanko.

ref: And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me.
text: Naa na min, o na Koosa naa tum min chen gbanko.

ref: I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
text: Mu funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ magau di ba naa maga se su min wa zi val birsi ni. Mu chi fitinne a chaani ba nyinbaan.

ref: I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge.
text: Mu wa zi di ba naa maga nu mu sɛma a vɛ tiŋ a nɔ sɛria. Mu wa fun wa di mu wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria. Mu ka funɛ wa di mu wa lisi ba a li ba tum tagni nɛɛ.

ref: But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa tel, sɛm nawara mu fun sɛm zi wa di ba naa funɛ vɛ min awa mu sɛma sɛriau.

ref: I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say.
text: Chenani mu sɛm ma nan wa ya mu titi sɛma. Mu nyan naa tum min chen, kpa sɛm ta nyɛ min di mu wa sɛm ma.

ref: I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.
text: Sɛm naa maga o fun kpa nyɛ min, mu kpa biksi mau. Ba naa maga nu o sɛm ta, o zi kpa o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔu a nyɛ ba.”

ref: It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
text: O funɛ ka wuzu bɛgimnɛ di val la gali wuzu zen ti tel. Yesu funɛ zimu di o kpaki saha ti duglɛu di o li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a bur kila o nyan ti wati. Chenani o funɛ daŋni ba naa o dɔ wa funɛ bur gantɛl.

ref: Even before the evening meal started, the devil had made Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, decide to betray Jesus.
text: Yesu wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ di dusum diwe ka sintana fun lisi di Judas Iskariɔt, Simɔn be, zi li Yesu gantɛlu.

ref: Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power.
text: Yesu funɛ zimu di o nyan naa tum un tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, funɛ kpa kina maga a du o nin ni. O gbanko funɛ zimu di o nyɛn nyinbaan nɛɛ a bur a la Koosa wati.

ref: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
text: A saha ti Yesu fun si diwe bar diili ti ni a ur o takta a zer bara kifiisi a niŋi.

ref: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
text: Doone o fun yoli ni a du katɛsa ni a funɛ sɛm o gantɛl tiŋsi ta naŋgere a kpa bara kifiisi ti a fiisi ba naŋgere ta.

ref: But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
text: O fun wa pu Simɔn Pita. O fun pɛɛsi un, “Mu kunyana, o wa deŋli wa i sɛm mu naŋgere.”

ref: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “I wa zim chenani mu kaa ya nyɛ hin nan mun lalaknɔ, amaa o gantɛl ni i zi wa zimu.”

ref: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
text: Pita fun ŋɔ un, “Mu wa zi kut nyɛ i sɛm mu naŋgere.” Yesu fun ŋɔ, “I vɛ nyɛ mu sɛm a, i wa zi bu ya mu gantɛl tiŋsi ta digi.”

ref: Peter said, “Lord, don't wash just my feet. Wash my hands and my head.”
text: Simɔn Pita fun fɛti a ŋɔ, “O ya di chene, ta chaksi mu naŋgere duŋduŋ. Chaksi mu nisa awa mu nyuha gbanko.”

ref: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Nar sɔ ni, chene i bara maga wer, amaa i ka val la bar digi, i naŋgere duŋduŋnɛ i yaa sɛm.” Doone o fun burgi a ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Lalaknɔwa ma maga bambe wer, amaa ma nibɛgim bambi vɛ weri.”

ref: Jesus knew who would betray him. That is why he said, “except for one of you.”
text: Yesu funɛ piin zim naa kaa li o gantɛlu. Chendaha o fun ŋɔ di ba nibɛgim bambi wa wer.

ref: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm ba naŋgere peti chen, o fun bur kaali o bar kani ti a la laari o takta ti a ŋɔ ba, “Mu zi ŋɔ ma chenani mu tum nyɛ ma nan munu.

ref: You call me your teacher and Lord, and you should, because that is who I am.
text: Ma yaa yir min ma kunyana naa la biksi ma Koosa wɛ. Chen deŋlee, domi mu sir ya ma kunyannɛ.

ref: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
text: Maa tiŋ chenani mu biksi ma di ma tiŋ chen.

ref: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
text: Ma nu mu sɛma nɛnɛ. Yom wa to a zen a kaali o kunyana. Nitumi wa to a zen a kaali naa tum un.

ref: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
text: “Sɛm naa mu sɛm ma nan, mu wa sɛm a a nyɛ ma maga nɛɛ. Min zim ba naa mu lisi a li ma tuhu niu. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Mu wa naa di diwe nan zi li mu gantɛlu.’ Chenani ba sɔbi, sɛm nama nan zi ya wutiu.

ref: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
text: Mu te ŋɔ mau, chen ni ka nan wa ya, ma se zi su di min ya naa ba fun sɔbi chen wɛ.

ref: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
text: Naa Yesu funɛ daŋni kinkan fun tur la mari un a pɛɛsi, “Mu kunyana, anti chen?”

ref: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm nan peti, o bambi buri un ni kinkan. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Wutirɛ mu ŋɔ ma, ma nidigi a li ma tuhu ni zi li mu gantɛlu.”

ref: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
text: Naa Yesu funɛ daŋni kinkan a li ba ni funɛ kani a mari un.

ref: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
text: Simɔn Pita fun baasi o nin di o nyɛ Yesu ŋɔ un chenani o du.

ref: No one at the meal understood what Jesus meant.
text: Ba naa funɛ lagmi di ba di diwe chen wa fun zim chenani Yesu sɛm un chen mun.

ref: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
text: Ba maga malɛ funɛ nyɛn Judas nin nee. Ba bɔnɛ fun lisi di Yesu ŋɔ un di o la yau wuzu zen ti diwe. Bɔnɛ ka fun lisi di Yesu ŋɔ un di o lisi malɛ ta bɔnɛ a la nyɛ fara tim ni.

ref: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
text: Judas funɛ la boroboro ti, o fun vaan si a li. O funɛ ya dusum saharɛ. Yesu Chiksi Fali Wɛ

ref: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
text: Mu bisa, mu wa ma wa zi nyɛn vusi. Mu wa kaali, ma zi dɛu mu bar nyɛniu. Ma lisi chenani mu fun ŋɔ Ju tim ta ni di ba wa kikoti a tiŋ min la doonani mu la chen. Chenani mu ŋɔ ba chen, ya ma gbanko kinnɛ.

ref: Simon Peter asked, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “You can't go with me now, but later on you will.”
text: Simɔn Pita fun pɛɛsi un, “Mu kunyana, leene i la?” Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “I wa kitiŋ min a la doonani mu la chen lalaknɔ. O zi ya tamnɛ i koti a tiŋ min.”

ref: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
text: Pita fun bu pɛɛsi un, “Mu kunyana, yɛrɛ mu vɛ ka koti a tiŋ hin lalaknɔ? Mu saksɛu di mu sou a nyɛ hin.”

ref: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
text: Yesu fun burgi a pɛɛsi un, “I sir saksɛ di i sou a nyɛ minuu? Nu mu wati nɛnɛ. I zi te ŋɔ nara niu dusum nɔ di i wa zim min nɔ butora ka zimal zi yeli.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
text: Doone Yesu fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Ma ta nyɛ ma bambe bure. Ma se su Koosa. Ma ta nyɛ ma se suuli bur gantɛl a li min ni.

ref: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
text: Damsi kinkan nyɛn doonani mu nyan ti nyɛn. Mu wa la dooni, mu zi kɔɔri bɔnɛu a chiksi ma. Nan vɛ yaali, mu fu wa zi ŋɔ ma.

ref: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
text: Mu wa kɔɔri a peti, mu zi bur wa kpa ma lau, chen ni ma wa min zi bu lagmi nyɛnu.

ref: You know the way to where I am going.”
text: Ma waa zim doonani mu la awa doon nyinbaan.”

ref: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
text: Tɔmas fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, ya ha wa kpak doonani i la chen ya bambe ni. Yɛrɛ ya kaa ya zim doon nyinbaan?”

ref: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Min ya doon nyinbaan ti. Mu ya wuti tiina awa Koosa mi fali tiina. Nandigi wa kila mu nyan ti wati aa vɛ na min.

ref: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
text: Ma fun zim chenani mu sir du, ma fu zi zim chenani mu nyan ti gbanko duu. Zinan kpa la ma na unu a zim un.”

ref: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Filip, yɛrɛ i pɛɛsi min chen ni? Ma wa min nyɛn vusiu. I ha wa vɛɛn zim nibine naa ya minn? Nara maga na min, mu nyan tirɛ ba na chen.

ref: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
text: Nyɛ i si su di mu nyan ti nyɛn min nɛɛ ka mu ka nyɛn un ni. Chenani mu biksi ma a la tum chen wa li min ni. A maga li mu nyan naa nyɛn min ni watɛɛ.”

ref: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
text: Doone Yesu fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Ma nyɛ ma se su di mu nyan ti nyɛn min nɛɛ ka mu ka nyɛn un ni. Nan bɔm ma ni, ma lisi tum zene naa ma na ka mu tum chen. A waa la zi suŋi ma ma se su.

ref: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
text: Wutirɛ mu ŋɔ ma, ba naa se su min, ba zi tum chenani mu tumu. Hali ba zi tumu tum zene a kaali chenani mu tum, domi mu zi wa bur la mu nyan ti watiu.

ref: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
text: Mu waa naa ya Koosa be, n daŋni narawa yaa kinyɛ mu nyan ti wa jirima. Chendaha kin naa maga ma ŋɔ di mu tum a nyɛ ma a li mu sɔn ni, n zi tum au.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
text: “Maa daŋni min, ma zi tiŋ chenani mu ŋɔ mau.

ref: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
text: Mu zi sumi mu nyan tiu o kpa o siim weri, naa ya wuti tiina, a du ma bambe ni. O zi nyɛn dooniu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ aa suŋi ma ma tiŋ chenani mu ŋɔ ma.

ref: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
text: O wa zi wa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga bambe ni, domi ba wa zim di o nyɛnu. Ma ka zim di o nyɛnu, domi o zi wa nyɛn ma bambe niu.

ref: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
text: “Mu wa zi gil ma duŋduŋ. Mu zi bur wa ma watiu.

ref: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
text: Saha naa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga vɛ kaa wa na min chen duglɛu, amaa ma waa ka zi bu na minu. Domi mu zi wa si mu sou niu, ma gbanko zi wa si ma sou niu.

ref: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
text: A wuzu naa mu wa si mu sou ni, ma zi zimu di mu nyɛn mu nyan ti nɛɛ ka ma nyɛn min ni ka mu gbanko nyɛn ma ni.

ref: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
text: “Ba naa la nu mu sɛma a la tiŋ a, chen biksi di ba daŋni minu. Mu gbanko zi daŋni bau a kpa mu titi a biksi ba. Ba la daŋni min chen daha, mu nyan ti gbanko zi daŋni bau.”

ref: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon. O sɔn ya Judas, amaa o laata fun ya Judas Iskariɔt. O fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Mu kunyana, yɛrɛ i kaa ya a kpa i titi a biksi ya a ta kpa i titi a biksi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga?”

ref: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Ba naa la daŋni min zi tiŋ mu nɔ sɛmau. Mu nyan ti gbanko zi daŋni bau, ka mu wa un wa nyɛn ba bambe ni.

ref: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
text: Ba naa vɛ daŋni min wa zi tiŋ mu nɔ sɛma. Chenani mu biksi ma wa li min ni. A maga li mu nyan naa tum min chen watɛɛ.

ref: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
text: “Mu wa ma funɛ nyɛn ka mu sɛm ma kin nama.

ref: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
text: Mu nyan ti wa tum o siim weri ti di o wa chiŋi mu bar chiŋi ni. Waa la zi biksi ma kin nama maga ma daŋni ma zim aa lisi ma chenani maga mu fun ŋɔ ma.

ref: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
text: “Mu zi nyɛ ma bar sɔɔnau a chi chenani mu bara du. A wa kaali tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga bar sɔɔnau kinkan. Ma ta nyɛ ma bambe bure a ta ɔmi.

ref: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
text: Mu piin te ŋɔ mau di mu zi la mu nyan ti watiu a bur wa. Ma sir a daŋni min, ma tusa zi sɔɔniu domi mu nyan ti zen kaali min.

ref: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
text: Mu te ŋɔ ma kin nama wɛwa ka a zi wa ya, chen ni a wa ya, ma se zi su minu a gbaari.

ref: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
text: Mu wa bura daŋni mu sɛm kinkan ma wati, domi sintana naa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga wau. Mu wa un wa kpak wu digi wa dɔŋ ni.

ref: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
text: Mu nyan ti funɛ ŋɔ min chenani mu zi tum. Mu zi tiŋ o nɔu a tum a, chen ni tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zi zimu di muu daŋni unu. Ma nyɛ ya si a la bar digi.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
text: Doone Yesu fun bu ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Mu chi bugil pum naa ba yir ‘vain’ chene a kpak wuti. Mu nyan ti chi naa la nyini unnɛ.

ref: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
text: O yaa ŋmɛnsi hulsa a naa vɛ nɔni chenu a ta, a ŋmɛnsi a naa la nɔn chen a bura nɔn kinkan.

ref: You are already clean because of what I have said to you.
text: Chene o fun piin ya a chaksi ma tusa a li mu sɛm nawara mu fun sɛm ma ni.

ref: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
text: Hulsi ta vɛ baŋsi bugil pum ti bara ni, a wa kinɔn nɔna. Chen vɛɛna, ma wa min vɛ ya nibɛgim a nyɛni, ma wa kikoti a tum tum were a nyɛ min. Chendaha o lawa ma wa min ya nibɛgimu a nyɛn.

ref: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
text: “Mu piin ŋɔ mau di mu chi bugil pum naa ba yir ‘vain’ chene. Ma chi mu hulsiwa. Ma wa min ya nibɛgim a nyɛn, ma zi kotiu a tum tum were kinkan a nyɛ min. Ma lisɛ ma titi a li min ni, ma wa kikoti a tum wu digi.

ref: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
text: Ma ya chen, mu nyan ti zi teŋsi mau a ta ma sou. Nara zi wa haasi ma a lagmi dɔŋ ni a fu.

ref: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
text: Ma wa min ya nibɛgim a nyɛn ka mu sɛm naa mu biksi ma nyɛn ma ni, ma zi kotiu a sumi Koosa kin naa ma daŋni o nyɛ ma.

ref: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
text: Muu daŋni mau a chi chenani mu nyan ti daŋni min. Maa lisi chen wɛ, chen ni ya lagmi nyɛni nɔ wa zi bur gantɛl.

ref: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
text: “Mu daŋni ma tusa sɔɔniu hali a wa peti a chi chenani mu tuha sɔɔni nan. Chendaha mu sɛm nan maga a nyɛ ma.

ref: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
text: Chiksi fali nyɛnɛɛ mu daŋni ma tiŋ. Ma zimu di mu daŋni mau kinkan. O lawa ma gbanko kidaŋni dɔŋau a chi chen.

ref: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
text: Nar chiŋi a chɛna bar chiŋi ni a sou, chenani o daŋni un chen kaali a maga.

ref: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
text: Ma tiŋ chenani mu fun biksi ma, mu zi zimu di ma ya mu chɛnsiwa.

ref: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
text: Chenani maga mu nyan ti fun ŋɔ min, mu funɛ kpa a magau a ŋɔ ma. Chendaha mu gbanko sɛm di ma ya mu chɛnsiwa. N wa zi bu ŋɔ di ma ya mu tumtumsiwa, domi tumtumsa wa zim chenani ba kunyana tum.

ref: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: Ma wa fun lisi min di mu ya ma kunyana, amaa mu ka funɛ lisi ma di ma tum tum were naa la zi vɛ chɔki a nyɛ min. Ma tiŋ min chen, ma zi kotiu a sumi mu nyan ti a li mu sɔn ti ni kin naa ma daŋni o nyɛ ma.

ref: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
text: Mu sɛm mau wa kpok ni, o lawa maa daŋni dɔŋau.” Chenani Tɛɛnɛ Nɔ Tim Maga Ha O Gantɛl Tiŋsa

ref: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
text: “Mu tiŋi nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ha ma, ma lisi di minnɛ ba te ha.

ref: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
text: Ma wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga fu lagmi a nyɛn dɔŋ wati, ba fu zi daŋni mau. Mu funɛ lisi ma di ma wa ba ta lagmi nyɛn dɔŋ wati. Chendaha ba ha ma.

ref: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
text: Ma lisi chenani mu fun ŋɔ ma di yom wa to a zen a kaali o kunyana. Ba funɛ muksi minu kinkan, adaha ba zi wa muksi ma gbanko zi nu ma sɛmau.

ref: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
text: Ba zi wa muksi mau a chi chen, domi ba wa zim naa funɛ tum min chen. Maa tiŋ min daha, ba gbanko ya ma chen.

ref: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
text: Mu funɛ wa ŋɔ ba di ba bur a li ba tum tagna tiŋi ni. Ba vɛ. Chendaha Koosa na ba galimsa. Ba wa zi wa koti a sɛm wu digi o suwachak ni.

ref: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
text: Nar ha min, mu nyan tirɛ o ha.

ref: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
text: Mu funɛ tum alaazibu tuma a naa nibine zi vɛ koti a tumiu, ba suwachak ni. Chendaha Koosa gbanko na ba galimsa. Hali chenani maga ba na ka mu tum nyɛ ba, a maga ya yɔrirɛ ba wati ka ba ha min awa mu nyan ti.

ref: I will send you the Spirit who comes from the Father and shows what is true. The Spirit will help you and will tell you about me.
text: “Mu funɛ piin ŋɔ mau di Koosa siim weri naa ya wuti tiina zi biksi nara mu wɛ. Mu wa bur la mu nyan ti wati, mu zi nyɛ o wa ma watiu a wa suŋi ma.

ref: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
text: Ma funɛ nyɛn mu wati o piili ti ni chen. Chendaha ma gbanko zi biksi narau mu wɛ.”

ref: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
text: “Mu ŋɔ ma kin nama wɛu, chen ni ma wa zi bur a ni.

ref: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
text: Ju tim ta zi wa duŋni mau a li ba bar lagmi dɛɛni ta ni. Hali saha zi wa wau, ba naa kpu ma lisi di ba tiŋ Koosa tutɔrɛ.

ref: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
text: Ba zi tum nanu a nyɛ ma, domi ba wa zim min koo mu nyan ti.

ref: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
text: Mu ŋɔ ma kin nama wɛu, chen ni saha wa tel di ba ya nan nyɛ ma, ma zi lisi chenani mu ŋɔ mau. Ma wa min lagmi nyɛn nan, o wa deŋli di mu ŋɔ ma a wɛ.” Chenani Koosa Siim Weri Ti Suŋi Ya

ref: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
text: “Lalaknɔwa mu saksi di mu bur la naa funɛ tum min ma wati. Ma nidigi ha wa wa pɛɛsi min di leene mu la.

ref: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
text: Mu ŋɔ ma kin nama wɛu ka a nyɛ ma tusa chɔk kinkan.

ref: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
text: Ma nyɛ mu sɛm ma wuti. O deŋlu di mu gil ma a bur la mu nyan ti wati, chen ni mu zi nyɛ o siim weri ti wa suŋi mau. Mu vɛ laali, o wa zi koti a wa.

ref: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
text: O wa wa, o zi nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zim di ba tum tum tagnau. O zi wa biksi ba di Koosa daŋni ba deŋliu o suwachak ni, chen ni o wa zi bu na ba galimsa.

ref: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
text: Ba se wa su min. Chendaha ba yaa tum tum tagna.

ref: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
text: Chenani mu zi chiŋi ba bar chiŋi ni a sou a nyɛ ba a bur la mu nyan ti wati, nan duŋduŋ la zi nyɛ ba deŋli Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
text: Koosa fun piin na sintana naa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga, galimsa a ha vɛ ta un yɔri. Kin namawa Koosa siim weri ti kaa wa biksi ba.

ref: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
text: “Mu kpak wu damatau a kaa ŋɔ ma, amaa ma wa kikoti a chiŋi a nu a maga.

ref: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
text: Koosa siim weri naa ya wuti tiina, wa nyɛn ma bambe ni, o zi nyini mau a li wuti nyinbaan ni. O biksi ti wa zi wa li o titi ni. Sɛm naa o zi wa nu mu wa mu nyan ti wati, o zi kpa au a sɛm ma. O gbanko zi biksi ma kin nawara zi wa yau wɛ.

ref: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
text: O zi nyɛ min jirima kinkanu, domi o zi nu mu sɛmau a kpa a a sɛm ma.

ref: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
text: Mu nyan ti sɛma awa mu sɛma ya kibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha mu ŋɔ ma di o siim weri ti zi nu mu sɛmau a kpa a a sɛm ma.” Yesu Gantɛl Tiŋsa Tu Chɔk Zi Burgi Tu Sɔɔnau

ref: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
text: “Mu wa zi nyɛn ma wati a vusi a bur la mu nyan ti wati. Mu wa la o wati, o wa zi vusi ka ma nu na min.”

ref: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm ba peti, o gantɛl tiŋsi ta bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “O fa ŋɔ ya di o wa zi nyɛn ya wati a wusi a bur la o nyan ti wati. O wa la o wati, o wa zi vusi ka ya bu na un. O sɛm ta mun ya baŋa?

ref: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
text: Ya wa zim chenani o fa ŋɔ ya di o wa zi vusi chen mun.”

ref: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
text: Yesu funɛ zimu di ba daŋni o suŋi bau. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma na dɔŋwa a li chenani mu fa sɛm ma chen, mu wa zi nyɛn ma wati a vusi a bur na ma lalaga.

ref: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
text: Wutirɛ mu ŋɔ ma, mu bu vɛ wa nyɛn ma wati, tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga tusa zi wa sɔɔniu kinkan, amaa ma tusa ka zi wa chɔku kinkan. Ma tu chɔk ti wa zi wa vusi, amaa a zi wa ya tu sɔɔnau lalaga.

ref: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
text: Nihaan wa duurɛ, o tuha ya chɔk. O wa lul peti, o ya sugliu wiili tiu ka o tuha sɔɔni o be ti daha.

ref: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
text: Ma tusa chɔku lalaknɔ. Ma bu wa na min, ma tusa ka zi bu sɔɔniu kinkan. Nandigi wa kilisi ma tu sɔɔn ti a li ma ni.

ref: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
text: “Chen saha ti ma wa zi wa mu wati di ma wa pɛɛsi min kundɔŋ wɛ. Wutirɛ mu ŋɔ ma, ma zi kotiu a sumi mu nyan ti kin naa ma daŋni mu sɔn daha o nyɛ ma.

ref: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
text: Hali wa zinan ma ha wa sumi un kundɔŋ mu sɔn daha. Maa sumi un o zi nyɛ mau ka ma tusa sɔɔni hali a la gali.” Yesu Kpok Ya Zen A Kaali Tɛɛnɛ Nɔ Tim Maga Kpok

ref: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
text: “Kin naa mu ŋɔ ma a wɛ nan, o bɔmu wa ma zim a mun. Saha wau di mu zi wa biksi ma mu nyan ti wɛ gbampulim ni ka o ta bu bɔm ma ni.

ref: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
text: Mu nyan ti daŋni mau kinkan a la zi ya chen a nyɛ ma. Ma daŋni minu ka ma se su di mu funɛ li o watɛɛ. Chendaha o daŋni ma a chi chen.

ref: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
text: Mu funɛ li o watɛɛ a wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Lalaknɔ mu saksi di mu li a bur a la o wati.”

ref: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
text: Doone o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun ŋɔ un, “Lalaknɔwa ya zim chenani i sɛm ya chen mun. O wa bu bɔm ya ni.

ref: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
text: Ya gbanko zimu di i zim kina magau, hali chenani ya lisi ya bambe ni. Chendaha ya se su di i li Koosa watɛɛ a wa ya wati.”

ref: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma se sir su minuu?

ref: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
text: Ma nu n wati. O wa zi vusi ka ma maga chɔ kaali ma damsa a ka min duŋduŋ, amaa mu nyan ti wa zi ya chen. O wa min zi nyɛnu.

ref: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
text: Chenani ma nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni zi nyɛ ma na namsiu kinkan. Chenani ma wa min ya nibɛgim a nyɛn ka zi nyɛ ma bara sɔɔniu. Chendaha mu fun ŋɔ ma kin nama. Ma kpak ma bambe. Mu kpok ti ya kizen a kaali tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga kpok.”

ref: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
text: Yesu funɛ sɛm peti, o fun nyini buudaha ni a ŋɔ, “Mu nyana, mu siwi saha ti duglɛu. Biksi nara i jirima awa i chaani naa i fun kpa a nyɛ min chen kinkan, chen ni mu zi nyɛ hin jirima kinkanu.

ref: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
text: Hin funɛ nyɛ min nyinbaan di mu kpa i mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ni. Mu kpa un a nyɛ ba naa i fun lisi a nyɛ min chen.

ref: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
text: Hin ya nibɛgim a ya wuti tiina. Nar zim hin awa mu waa naa i fun tum chen, o kpak i mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔu.

ref: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
text: Tum naa i fun kpa nyɛ min di mu tum nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga na i jirima awa i chaani kinkan. Lalaknɔ o petiu.

ref: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
text: “I funɛ lisi nara a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ni a nyɛ i titi ni. I fun kpa ba a nyɛ min ka mu fun biksi ba i wɛ ba tiŋ i nɔ.

ref: They know that you gave me everything I have.
text: Lalaknɔ ba zim di kin naa maga mu kpak li i watɛɛ.

ref: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
text: Sɛm naa i fun sɛm nyɛ min chen, mu funɛ kpa au a ŋɔ ba. Ba se funɛ su di hin tum min ba wati.

ref: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
text: “Mu wara sumi hin tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga daharɛ. Mu ka sumi hin ba naa se su min daharɛ. Hin dɔ ba a fun lisi ba a nyɛ min.

ref: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
text: Mu nara maga ya i narawa. I nara maga ka ya mu nara. Chenani ba tiŋ min biksi nara di mu kpak jirima kinkan.

ref: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
text: Lalaknɔ mu saksi di mu li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a wa i wati ka ba kpalimi gantɛl ni. Mu nyana naa ya tu chaan tiina, i funɛ lisi ba a nyɛ min. Kpa i kpok ti a nyini ba nɛnɛ a suŋi ba ba kpak nɔ bɛgim a chi ya.

ref: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
text: Mu wa ba lagmi nyɛn dɔŋ wati nan, i kpokre mu fun kpa a nyini ba. Ba nibɛgim duŋduŋ ya nibin yɔri a fun lisi o nin a li ya ni. Chenani ba fun sɔbi o wɛ a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
text: Lalaknɔ mu saksiu di mu bur wa i wati. Chendaha mu sumi hinu ba daha di ba tusa sɔɔni hali a la gali a chi mu kin.

ref: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
text: Sɛm naa maga i fun ŋɔ min chen, mu kpa au a ŋɔ ba. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ha bau, domi ba wa ba wa bu ya nibɛgim a chi chenani mu wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga wa ya nibɛgim.

ref: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
text: Mu wara sumi hin di i lisi ba a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Mu ka sumi hin di i kok bau a li sintani.

ref: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
text: “Mu ha vɛɛn a sumi hinu a nyɛ mu gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni a gbaari ba naa la zi nyɛ ba se su min a li ba biksi ti ni.

ref: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
text: Kisuŋi ba ba ya nɔ bɛgim dɔŋ ni. Mu nyana, i wa min ya nibɛgimnɛ. Suŋi ba ba wa zim ya a gbaari, chen ni tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga se zi su di hin funɛ tum min ba wati.

ref: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
text: I jirima awa i chaani naa i fun kpa nyɛ min chen, kinkan mu wa ba bɔnɛ un, chen ni ba zi ya nɔ bɛgimu dɔŋ ni a chi ya.

ref: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
text: Domi i wa min ya nibɛgimnɛ ka ya nyɛn ba bambe ni, nyɛ chenani ba ya nɔ bɛgim chen kigbaari. Ba ya chen, tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zi wa zimu di hin funɛ tum min ba wati. Ba gbanko zi zimu di ii daŋni bau a chi chenani ii daŋni min.

ref: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
text: “Mu nyana, hin funɛ te daŋni min ka i wa min bɔni i jirima awa i chaani kinkan ka i zi naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Mu sumi hinu di i nyɛ ba naa i fun lisi nyɛ min chen, wa lagmi min ni ya nyɛn i tɔhɔn ti ni wuzu digi, chen ni ba zi wa na mu jirima awa mu chaani kinkanu.

ref: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
text: Mu nyana naa ya tum were tiina, tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga wa zim hin. Mu ka zim hin ka mu gantɛl tiŋsi ta zim di hin funɛ tum min ba wati.

ref: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
text: Mu funɛ biksi ba chenani i bambi duu a ha vɛ peti. Mu zi nyɛn ba bambe niu a suŋi ba baa daŋni nara a chi chenani ii daŋni min.”

ref: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
text: Kaifas yom digi funɛ gbaari ba ni. O funɛ ya yom naa Pita fun teŋ o diŋna toti chen maannɛ. O fun ŋɔ Pita ni, “I wa baal ti laata mu na ŋma we ti ni chen?”

ref: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
text: Yesu fun bu pɛɛsi ba, “Antirɛ maa dɛu?” Ba fun vɛɛn ŋɔ un, “Ya dɛu Yesu Nazarɛt narrɛ.”

ref: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu piin ŋɔ mau di min ya un. O ya di minnɛ ma kaa wa kpak, ma nyɛ mu gantɛl tiŋsi nama kila ka ma kpak min.”

ref: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
text: Yesu fun te ŋɔ o nyan ti ni di ba naa o fun lisi nyɛ un chen, o wa nyuksi ba nibɛgim. Chendaha o fun sɛm chen.

ref: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
text: Doone Simɔn Pita fun foŋ o chisi zen a teŋ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti yom digi nindil diŋna a toti. O sɔn funɛ ya Malkus.

ref: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
text: Doone lanjimasi ta, ba nihɛɛ awa ba naa la il Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti, ba fun kpak Yesu a vɔu o nisa a ta o gantɛl ni.

ref: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
text: Pita fun kpalimi bulzɔɔnɔ ni. O wa fun vusi ka Yesu gantɛl tiŋna naa Anas fun zim nɛnɛ, fun wa li sal ti bulzɔɔnɔ ti. O fun sumi nihabichɛl naa pɔ bulzɔɔnɔ ti chen di Pita zu sal ti. O fun la nɔɔni.

ref: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
text: Dam bisa awa ba naa la il Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti, ba fun zɔk dinhala a buksi din sal ti bambaan ni domi wa funɛ nyɛn. Ba fun chiŋi a go un aa weeri. Pita fun la gbaari ba ni. Anas Fun Pɛɛsi Yesu O Biksi Ti Wɛ (Mat.

ref: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
text: Doone Anas fun pɛɛsi Yesu chenani o gantɛl tiŋsi ta awa o biksi ti du.

ref: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
text: Ta pɛɛsi min. Pɛɛsi ba naa mu fun biksi chen. Ba waa zim chenani mu fun ŋɔ ba.”

ref: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
text: Yesu nisa ha funɛ vɛɛn vɔu a ta o gantɛl ni. Anas funɛ peti o sɛma, o fun tum un kaali Kaifas naa ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti wati. Pita Fun Bu Vɛ Di O Wa Zim Yesu (Mat.

ref: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
text: Chɔɔsa fininirɛ Kaifas awa Yesu fun na dɔŋa peti. Ju tim ta funɛ saksiu di ba di ba wuzu zen wuzu naa ba yir val la gali ti diwe. Lanjimasi ta funɛ kpa Yesu a kaali gɔbnati nyuhu tiina sɛria bar diili dɛ. Ju tim ta fun kpalimi sal ni, domi o funɛ chiu di ba tiŋ zu.

ref: Pilate came out and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
text: Gɔbnati nyuhu tiin ti sɔn funɛ ya Pailɔt. O fun li sal ti a la pɛɛsi Ju tim ta, “Baŋ nyuhu nee ma fiksi baal nɔ?”

ref: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “O fu vɛ tum wu digi, ya fu wa zi kpa un a wa i wati.”

ref: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
text: Doone Pailɔt fun bur kaali sɛria bar diili dɛ ti a ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba kpa Yesu wa nyɛ un. O fun ŋɔ un, “Hin ya Ju tim koor chenn?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
text: Yesu fun burgi a pɛɛsi un, “Pɛɛsi nɔ li i watɛɛ koo nara ŋɔ hin di i wa pɛɛsi minn?”

ref: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
text: Pailɔt ŋɔ un, “Mu wa ya Ju tim nar ba ŋɔ min chenani mu zi pɛɛsi hin. I titi nara awa i Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta kpa hin a du mu nin ni. Tum tagna baŋwa i tum?”

ref: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Mu koor ti wa to tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. O fu nyɛnɛɛ, mu nara fu zi you a kok min, chen ni Ju tim ta wa zi kpak min. Mu bura ŋɔ hinu, mu koor ti wa to tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni.”

ref: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
text: Pailɔt fun ŋɔ un, “Chenani i sɛm nan ya wuti, i sir ya koorree?” Yesu fun ŋɔ, “Chenani i kut ŋɔ chen. Ba funɛ lul minu mu wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga di mu waa biksi nara wuti ti wɛ. Ba naa la daŋni ba ya wuti tima nu mu sɛma.”

ref: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
text: Doone Pailɔt fun bu ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni, “Ma wuzu zen wuzu naa ba yir val la gali ti wa tel bun kam, mu yaa lisi ba naa mu tɔ dɛ chen nibɛgimu a nyɛ ma. Ma daŋni mu lisi naa ya Ju tim koor tirɛ a nyɛ maa?”

ref: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
text: Doone Pailɔt fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba kpak Yesu a tuk un wa vurmsi ni.

ref: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
text: Ba fun kpa sɔta a wa sɔ a chi koora nichiga a kpa o chik o nyuha a zer ba gar sɛm digi a wa a wa laari un,

ref: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
text: a zaami un a ŋɔ, “Ju tim koora, ya zaami hinu,” a la mo un.

ref: Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, “I will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.”
text: Ba funɛ ya chene, Pailɔt fun bu li Ju tim wati a ŋɔ ba, “Mu bu kaa la kpa un wa biksi mau, chen ni ma zi zimu di mu nyini un di o nisa wa toli.”

ref: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”
text: Doone lanjimasi ta fun kpa Yesu a nyɛ o kaali zɛmɛ ta suwachak. O ha funɛ vɛɛn chik sɔta nichik ti awa gar sɛm tirɛ. Pailɔt fun ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni, “Ma na, baal ti nɛɛnɛ.”

ref: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa ba naa la il Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti funɛ na Yesu, ba fun tɛsi a ŋɔ Pailɔt ni, “Nyɛ ba kpaasi un a mari daakakli daha ni o sou.” Pailɔt fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu nyini un di o nisa wa toli. Ma kpaasi un a mari daakakli ti daha ni o sou.”

ref: The crowd replied, “He claimed to be the Son of God! Our law says that he must be put to death.”
text: Zɛmɛ ta fun ŋɔ un, “Ya chiksɛ ta biksi ya di o sou, domi o kpa o titirɛ a burgi Koosa be.”

ref: He went back inside and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus did not answer.
text: O fun nyɛ o lanjimasi ta kpa Yesu a te o suwachak a kaali sɛria bar diili dɛ ti. O fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Leene i li?” O fun moori.

ref: “Why won't you answer my question?” Pilate asked. “Don't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?”
text: Pailɔt fun bu ŋɔ un, “I wa kpak wu digi a la zi sɛm? Mu kpak kpoku a la zi nyɛ i la yɔri. Kpok ka vɛɛn nyɛnu mu zi ŋɔ mu lanjimasi ta ni ba kpaasi hin a mari daakakli ti ni i sou.”

ref: Jesus replied, “If God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Koosa fu vɛ nyɛ hin i kpok nɔ, i fu wa kikoti a ya min wu digi. Chendaha mu siwɛɛ, naa funɛ kpa min a du i nin ni, o galimsa la zi wa kaali i kin.”

ref: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”
text: Pailɔt funɛ nu Yesu sɛma, o fun bu chiŋsi o bambi bar bɛgim a yisi un. Zɛmɛ ta funɛ nu chen, ba fun bu tɛsi a ŋɔ Pailɔt ni, “I nyɛ baal nɔ kaali, i wa tiŋ koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza nɔ. Nar kpa o titi a burgi koora, o vɛ Siiza nɔrɛ.”

ref: When Pilate heard this, he brought Jesus out. Then he sat down on the judge's bench at the place known as “The Stone Pavement.” In Aramaic this pavement is called “Gabbatha.”
text: Pailɔt funɛ nu ba sɛma, o fun nyɛ o lanjimasi ta kpa Yesu a te o suwachak a kaali doonani chi biŋbiŋa. Ju tim ta funɛ yir dooni Gabata a li ba titi sɛm ni. Ba funɛ la tel dooni, Pailɔt fun kani sɛria bar diili kor ti ni.

ref: It was about noon on the day before Passover, and Pilate said to the crowd, “Look at your king!”
text: Nha funɛ wa tel sal baan nyuha. Ju tim ta funɛ saksi ba wuzu zen wuzu naa ba yir val la gali ti chen. Pailɔt fun ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni, “Ma na, ma koor ti nɛɛnɛ.”

ref: The soldiers first broke the legs of the other two men who were nailed there.
text: O fun la nɔɔni a nyɛ ba kaali. Ba fun la borigi nara balɛ ba naa ba fun kpaasi a gbaari Yesu ni chen naŋgere.

ref: So the chief priests went to Pilate and said, “Why did you write that he is King of the Jews? You should have written, ‘He claimed to be King of the Jews.’ ”
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta funɛ karimi sɛm ta, ba fun bur kaali Pailɔt wati a la ŋɔ un, “I fu wa lawa i ŋɔ i lanjimasi ta ni ba sɔbi di waa ya Ju tim koora. I ka fu lawa i ŋɔ ba ba sɔbi di o ŋɔ di waa ya Ju tim koora.”

ref: When Jesus saw his mother and his favorite disciple with her, he said to his mother, “This man is now your son.”
text: Yesu fun na o niin ti dooni awa o gantɛl tiŋna naa o daŋni kinkan ka o wa o niin ti dugli dɔŋa. O fun ŋɔ o niin ti ni, “Mu niina, kina be naa kaa chiŋi mu bar chiŋi ni.”

ref: and he carried his cross to a place known as “The Skull.” In Aramaic this place is called “Golgotha.”
text: Ba fun zer daakakli ti a nyɛ Yesu ni o baasi a la doonani Ju tim ta yir Gɔlgɔta a li ba titi sɛm ni. Chen mun ya, ‘nyukɔkri bar’.

ref: There Jesus was nailed to the cross, and on each side of him a man was also nailed to a cross.
text: Ba funɛ la tel dooni, lanjimasi ta fun kpaasi un a mari daakakli ti ni di o sou. Ba fun kpaasi nara balɛ gbanko a gbaari Yesu ni, digi o nindil ni, digi o ningal ni.

ref: Pilate ordered the charge against Jesus to be written on a board and put above the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews.”
text: Pailɔt fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba sɔbi o yaali a mari o daakakli ti ni. Chenani ba fun sɔbi ya, “Yesu Nazarɛt narrɛ. Waa ya Ju tim ta koora.”

ref: The words were written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek. The place where Jesus was taken wasn't far from the city, and many of the people read the charge against him.
text: Yesu bar kpaasi ti wa fun bol wa Jɛrusalɛm ni. Ju tim nidamata fun wa nyini chenani la ya a la karimi chenani ba sɔbi a mari daakakli ti ni. Sɛm tii butorawa ba fun sɔbi, a waa ya Ju tim ta sɛma, Girik tima awa Roman tima sɛma.

ref: But Pilate told them, “What is written will not be changed!”
text: Pailɔt fun ŋɔ ba, “Chenani mu nyɛ ba sɔbi, o du chen.”

ref: The soldiers said to each other, “Let's not rip it apart. We will gamble to see who gets it.” This happened so the Scriptures would come true, which say, “They divided up my clothes and gambled for my garments.” The soldiers then did what they had decided.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Ma ta nyɛ ya chɛɛsi un. Ma nyɛ ya yo walɛ a naa anti kaa di un.” Lanjimasi ta funɛ ya chene, di chenani ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni saha digi zi ya wutiu. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a ŋɔ, “Ba kpa mu kilaarɛu a purigi bɔni dɔŋa a ka mu takta we di ba yo walɛ a naa anti kaa di un.”

ref: Jesus' mother stood beside his cross with her sister and Mary the wife of Clopas. Mary Magdalene was standing there too.
text: Yesu niina, o towe, Mɛri naa ya Kilɛɔfas haan, awa Mɛri Magdala funɛ chiŋi a dugli Yesu.

ref: But when they came to Jesus, they saw he was already dead, and they did not break his legs.
text: Ba fun la di ba borigi Yesu naŋgere a na di o piin sou. Chendaha ba fun gil un.

ref: In the place where Jesus had been nailed to a cross, there was a garden with a tomb that had never been used.
text: Ŋma we funɛ dugli doonani ba fun kpa Yesu a mari daakakli ti ni. Gbɛntɛl bɔ fali funɛ nyɛn doon. Ba ha wa fun sogli nar un ni.

ref: Jesus knew that he had now finished his work. And in order to make the Scriptures come true, he said, “I am thirsty!”
text: Nan gantɛl nɛɛ Yesu fun zim di o tum petiu. O fun ŋɔ, “Nyɔksa kpak min.” O fun ŋɔ sɛm ta chenani ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chen zi ya wutiu.

ref: A jar of cheap wine was there. Someone then soaked a sponge with the wine and held it up to Jesus' mouth on the stem of a hyssop plant.
text: Kinyɔɔli haasi funɛ nyɛn gbaŋ ni. Ba naa funɛ chiŋi dooni nidigi fun zer kizɛrgi a tau un ni a kpa sak da ni a tɛ un di o nyɔ.

ref: kilograms of spices made from myrrh and aloes. This was the same Nicodemus who had visited Jesus one night.
text: Yesu funɛ nyɔ un a ŋɔ, “Mu tum petiu.” O fun gur o nyuha a sou. Lanjima Fun Kpa O Tim A Tau Yesu

ref: The next day would be both a Sabbath and the Passover. It was a special day for the Jewish people, and they did not want the bodies to stay on the crosses during this day. So they asked Pilate to break the men's legs and take their bodies down.
text: Ju tim ta wa funɛ daŋni di Yesu wa nara balɛ ta kpalimi dooni ba wuzu zen wuzu ti daha. Wuzu zen wuzu ti gbanko funɛ ya ba hiisi wuzurɛ. Chendaha ba fun vɛ daŋni chen yaali. Ba bɔnɛ fun bur kaali Pailɔt wati a la sumi un di o nyɛ o lanjimasi ta la borigi ba naŋgere a lisi ba.

ref: One of the soldiers stuck his spear into Jesus' side, and blood and water came out.
