ref: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
text: Ju tim ta wuzu zen naa ba funɛ yir Pɛntɛkɔst chen wa tel, Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta maga fun wa lagmi dɔŋ ni.

ref: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn doonee, chɛɛma fun vaan wa pirigi ba ni a li buudaha ni a chi do peha, a su doonani maga ba kani chen.

ref: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
text: ka Koosa siim weri ti fun wa si ba bambe ni, a nyɛ ba fun piili a sɛm sɛm naa vɛ ya ba titi sɛma.

ref: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
text: Ju tim nidamata funɛ gba a la Jɛrusalɛm wuzu zen wuzu ti daha. Ba funɛ li pulum damatiwa a la sumi Koosa saha maga.

ref: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
text: Laaboora funɛ mɔɔli chenani maga yɛɛ, zɛmɛ kinkan fun lagmi dentil ni. Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ wa li a sɛm ba, zɛmɛ ta se wa fun su di ba zi nu ba sɛm ta a li ba titi sɛm niu.

ref: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
text: Alaazibu fun kpak ba kinkan. Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Nara nama la sɛm sɛma ya wati nan ya Galili timwa.

ref: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
text: Yɛrɛ o du ya maga koti a nu sɛm nama a li ya titi sɛma ni?

ref: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
text: Ya ya Partia tima, Miid tima, Ilam tima, Mɛsɔpɔtemia tima, Judia tima, Kapadosia tima, Pɔntus tima, Azia tima,

ref: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
text: Ya bɔnɛ ka ya Krit tima awa Arab tima. Ya sɛm sɛm kurgɛwa, amaa ya nuu ka ba ŋɔ ya a li ya titi sɛma ni chenani Koosa fun tum alaazibu tuma.”

ref: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
text: Ba tusa maga funɛ buriu. Domi alaazibu funɛ kpak ba chen daha, ba bɔnɛ funɛ ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Kin nama mun ya baŋa?”

ref: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
text: Bɔnɛ ka fun mɔmsi Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Nara nama nyɔ sinnɛ a dɛɛsi.” Pita Fun Biksi Jɛrusalɛm Tima Koosa Laaboor Weri Wɛ

ref: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
text: Koosa nitumɛ nara fidalɛ ta funɛ nu chenani zɛmɛ ta sɛm, Pita fun si a chiŋi a sɛm ta zaŋa a ŋɔ ba, “Ma Ju tima awa ma naa maga nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, ma cheŋe ma diŋsa nɛnɛ a nu chenani mu kaa sɛm ma.

ref: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
text: Ma bɔnɛ ŋɔ dɔŋ ni di ya nyɔ sinnɛ a dɛɛsi, amaa o wa ya wuti. O ha ya chɔɔsau di nara ha vɛ di kundɔŋ a nyɔ sinu.

ref: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
text: ‘Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa dugli, mu zi kpa mu siim weri tiu a du nara maga ni. Ma bisa awa ma nihabichɛsa zi mɔɔli mu laaboorau a biksi nara. Ma bunɔɔna awa nihɛɛta zi la mu wutiu a chi ba deesi.

ref: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
text: Hali mu zi kpa mu siim weri tiu a du mu nibaala awa nihaana tumtumsi ni di ba mɔɔli mu laaboor ti a biksi nara.

ref: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
text: Mu zi kpa chɛl awa din wa nyɔsau a chɛrgi buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ magau.

ref: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
text: Nha wa zi bu chaani ka pɛna sɛri a chi chɛl. Kin nama maga zi wa te yau ka mu wuzu zen zi wa tel.

ref: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
text: Chen saha ti ba naa maga sumi min di mu yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni, n zi ya chenu.’

ref: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
text: “Ma Ju tima, maa nu chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma nan nɛnɛ. Tum zene awa alaazibu tuma a naa Koosa nyɛ Yesu Nazarɛt nar fun tum, biksi ma di Koosa funɛ lisi un. Ma titi zim kin nama wɛu domi a ya ma bambaan nɛɛ.

ref: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
text: Waa funɛ ya naa ma kpak a du tum tagna nitumɛ nisi ni chen di ba la kpaasi un a mari daakakli ti ni o sou chen. Koosa funɛ te zimu di ma zi ya chenu. O funɛ ya o tutɔrɛ di o sou chen ni.

ref: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
text: O fun lɛli Yesu a li o sou ni a nyɛ o namsi ti peti. O wa fun deŋli di o kpalimi o sou ni.

ref: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di koor Devid ŋɔ o wɛ, ‘Mu yaa na mu kunyana mu suwachak ni. Waa yaa suŋi min, chen ni nara wa zi muksi min.

ref: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
text: Domi o yaa suŋi min nan daha, mu sɛma biksi di mu tuha sɔɔnɛu. Mu ya nibinere a chi nara maga, amaa chenani mu kpak bambi bɛgim un ni nan zi nyɛ mu hiisiu.

ref: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
text: Mu zimu di o wa zi nyɛ mu kpalimi mu sou ni. O wa zi nyɛ o tumtum naa o fun lisi bara pɔ.

ref: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
text: Waa funɛ biksi min nyinbaan naa mu zi nyɛn wuti nyɛni. Domi waa nyɛn mu wati, mu tuha sɔɔni hali a la gali.’

ref: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
text: “Mu dɔŋ tima, n daŋni ma zim ya nawaal Devid wɛu nɛnɛ. O funɛ sou, ba fun kpa un a sogli. O hugil wa bol wa bar nɔ ni.

ref: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
text: O ha funɛ kpak miisa, o funɛ ya Koosa witiinne. Koosa funɛ vɔu a chiŋsi un di o zi nyɛ o bi lulɛ ta digi di o koorau.

ref: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
text: Waa ya naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen. Devid funɛ te zimu di Koosa zi lɛli unu a li o sou ni a fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Koosa wa fun gil un o kpalimi o sou ni di o bara pɔ.’

ref: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
text: Naa wɛ Devid fun sɔbi chen awa Yesu ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ya funɛ nau awa ya se ni ka Koosa lɛli un a li o sou ni,

ref: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
text: a kpa un a zini kaali Koosa nihɛɛti bar kani a li o tɔhɔn ti ni. O nyana funɛ kpa o siim weri tiu a nyɛ un a chi chenani o fun vɔu chen. Waa funɛ kpa chen siim weri ti a du ya bambe ni. Chenani ma nu a na zinan ya o waali ti mun.

ref: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
text: Koosa wa fun zer Devid a zini kaali o tɔhɔn ti, amaa ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di Devid ŋɔ, ‘Mu kunyana Koosa funɛ ŋɔ mu kunyan ni, kani mu nihɛɛti bar kani ni,

ref: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
text: ka mu nyɛ i dɔma burgi i yosa.’

ref: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
text: “Ma Ju tima, mu daŋni ma zimu nɛnɛ di Yesure ma fun kpu a li daakakli ti ni chen, waa ya nara maga nyuhu tiina. O gbanko ya naa Koosa fun vɔu di o zi tumu chen.”

ref: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
text: Pita sɛm ta funɛ zu zɛmɛ ta bambe niu kinkan. Ba fun ŋɔ un awa nitumɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni chen, “Ya dɔŋtima, yɛrɛ ya kaa ya?”

ref: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
text: Pita fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma te gil ma tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa, a nyɛ ya chaksi ma wa ni ni a li Yesu Kristo sɔn ni. Ma ya chen, Koosa zi gil ma tum tagnau a ta a kpa o siim weri ti a du ma ni.

ref: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
text: Sɛm ta maga laata Pita fun sɛm zɛmɛ ta. O funɛ kpaŋi o titirɛ di o sɛm ta zu ba bambe a ŋɔ, “Ma yisi ma titi a li tum tagna nitumɛ na wati, chen ni Koosa wa zi wa lɛrgi ma diŋsa a chi chenani o kaa wa lɛrgi ba kina.”

ref: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
text: Ba se funɛ sɛriu di nitumɛ ta biksi ba chenani ba zi tiŋ Yesu. Ba fun kpa ba kina a wa bɔni dɔŋa a yaa sumi Koosa a lagmi a di diwe. Chenani Yesu Nitiŋɛ Ta Lagmi Nyɛn

ref: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ o nitumɛ ta tum tum zene awa alaazibu tumwa kinkan. Tum na daha, Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta maga funɛ nyɛ ba jirima kinkan.

ref: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga fun kpalimi dɔŋ wati a kpa ba kina a bɔni dɔŋa.

ref: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
text: Ba fun yogli ba kina maga a nyɛ ba naa la daŋni a ni.

ref: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
text: Wuzu kam ba yaa fun la lagmi Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti niu a sumi Koosa aa go ba damsa a lagmi a di diwe awa tu sɔɔni. Ba nisa wa fun zuk wa ba lagmi a tum tuma.

ref: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
text: Ba yaa funɛ pɛgi Koosau ka Jɛrusalɛm tima lisi di ba ya nibin werewa. Wuzu kam Koosa fun kpa ba naa o yisi a li ba tum tagna ni, a gbaari ba ni.

ref: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
text: Wuzu digi zaazɔn saha naa Ju tim ta yaa la sumi Koosa chen, Pita awa Jɔn fun la kaali Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti. Chen saha ti ya niparɛ yaa si a li ba par ni chen.

ref: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
text: Ba fun wa tel bulzɔɔnɔ naa ba yir kiweri chen ka baal digi kani dooni. Ba funɛ lul un gbarigirɛ. Wuzu kam o walɛ yaa fun zer unu a wa kansi bulzɔɔnɔ ti ni di o sumi kina a li ba naa wa zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti.

ref: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
text: O funɛ na Pita awa Jɔn ka ba daŋni ba zu, o funɛ sumi kina ba wati.

ref: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
text: Ba fun chiŋi a nyini o se ni ka Pita ŋɔ un, “Nyini ya.”

ref: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
text: O fun zer o nyuha buudaha a nyini ba a lisi di ba kaa nyɛ un kundɔŋnɛ.

ref: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
text: Pita fun bu ŋɔ un, “Ya wa kpak malɛ a la zi nyɛ hin, amaa ya zi nyɛ hin kundɔŋu. Kpa Yesu Kristo Nazarɛt nar kpok a si a val.”

ref: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
text: Pita fun kpak o nindil a zer un chiŋsi. O naŋgere fun vaan kpok.

ref: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
text: Kpok ti funɛ vaan wa zu un, o si za chiksi a val. Pita awa Jɔn funɛ la Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti, o tiŋ ba a za a chiksi a la pɛgi Koosa.

ref: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
text: Ba fun zim di waa funɛ ya gbarigi a yaa kani bulzɔɔnɔ naa ba yir kiweri chen mun ni. Alaazibu fun kpak ba kinkan o si a val chen daha. Pita Fun Biksi Nara Koosa Bar Jɛɛmi Dam ni

ref: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Pita awa Jɔn funɛ wa tel zaŋ naa ba yir koor Solomon zaŋ, baal ti ha funɛ vɛɛn kpaksi ba nisawa. Nidamata fun chɔ a gor ba a du bambaan ni.

ref: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
text: Pita funɛ na ka zɛmɛ ta gbaari, o fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma Ju tima, yɛrɛ ma nyɛ alaazibu kpak ma chen? Yɛrɛ ma nyini ya se ni chen ni? O wa ya ya titi kpok nee koo chenani ya yaa jɛɛmi Koosa nɛnɛ nyɛ baal nɔ na alaafɛ.

ref: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
text: Koosa naa ma nawaalsa Abraham, Aizik awa Jakɔb funɛ jɛɛmi, kpa o jirima awa o chaani kinkan a nyɛ o tumtumna Yesu ni. Ma funɛ kpak unu a du gɔbnati nyuhu tiina naa ba yir Pailɔt nin ni. Pailɔt funɛ kpa un wa li ma suwachak a daŋni o gil un o la, ma fun vɛ.

ref: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
text: Ma ka fun sumi un di o kpa nara nikpuuli a nyɛ ma. Yesu nɔ funɛ ya tum were nitumirɛ ka Koosa fun lisi un di o tiŋ o tutɔ.

ref: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
text: Ma waa funɛ kpu naa kpa Koosa mi fali a nyɛ nara ni, amaa ya funɛ nau wa ya se ni ka Koosa lɛli un a li o sou ni.

ref: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
text: Yesu kpok ti funɛ nyɛ baal ti na alaafɛ. Chenani ma na aa zim kinkin naa ya zinan li chenani ya se su Yesu nee. Chendaha baal nɔ na alaafɛ bar nɔ ma suwachak ni.

ref: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
text: “Mu dɔŋtima, mu zimu di ma wa ma nihɛɛta wa fun zim chenani ma ya Yesu chen.

ref: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ o witinsi ta mɔɔli saha digi di naa o vɔu di o zi wa tumu chen, zi wa namsiu. Koosa fun nyɛ ba sɛm ta ya wuti.

ref: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
text: Adaha ma gil ma tum tagna a bur tiŋ ma kunyana Koosa, chen ni o wa zi bu na ma galimsa.

ref: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
text: Ma ya chen, o zi nyɛ ma bambe bukoo na kpoku a gbaari a tum o be Yesu naa o piin lisi di o kpa nyɛ ma. Waa ya naa o fun vɔu di o zi tumu wuzu digi.

ref: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
text: Ma nawaal Mosis funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Ma kunyana Koosa zi wa lisi o witinsi ta digiu a li ma tuhu ni a tum un ma wati a chi chenani o fun tum min ma wati nan. Maa tiŋ chenani maga o ŋɔ ma di ma tum.

ref: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
text: Ba naa maga vɛ tiŋ chen, Koosa zi waligi bau a li o nara ni a ta ba yɔri.’

ref: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
text: Koosa witiina naa ba yir Samuɛl awa ba naa maga tiŋ o gantɛl, ba fun mɔɔli Koosa laaboora a te ŋɔ di kin nama zi yau saha nɔ.

ref: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
text: Vɔwɛ naa Koosa fun vɔu a nyɛ o witinsi ta kpa a a mɔɔli, awa chenani o wa ma nawaalsi ta fun ya nɔ bɛgim, a ya ma gbanko kinawa zinan. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ Abraham ni, ‘Mu zi nyɛ i bi lulɛ kpa nyusumu a nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ni.’

ref: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
text: Chendaha Koosa fun lisi o be Yesu di o ya o tumtumna a te tum un ma wati di o kpa o nyusum a nyɛ ma ma gil ma tum tagni ta a bur tiŋ Koosa.”

ref: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
text: Pita awa Jɔn ha funɛ vɛɛna sɛm zɛmɛ ta ni chene, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta, ba naa la il Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti nihɛɛ, awa ba naa ba yir Sajusi tima fun wa ba wati.

ref: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
text: Ba fun na paan a du nitumɛ nara balɛ ta ni, domi ba waa ŋɔ zɛmɛ ta ni di Koosa lɛlɛ Yesu a li o sou ni. Nan funɛ biksi ba di Koosa zi wa lɛli narau wuzu digi a li ba sou ni.

ref: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
text: Ba fun kpak ba a tɔ ba dɛ zaazɔn saha hali chɔɔsa wa pisi.

ref: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
text: Zɛmɛ ta nidamata fun la nɔɔni di Pita sɛm ta ya wutiu a nyɛ ba se su Yesu. Nibaala duŋduŋ funɛ dugli nara tusugsa nyuunu.

ref: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta, Ju tim ta nihɛɛti ta, Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta,

ref: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
text: Anas, Kaifas naa ha funɛ ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ, Jɔn, Alɛzanda, awa ba naa wa Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti ya lul bɛgim, ba maga funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni a li Jɛrusalɛm ni.

ref: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
text: Ba fun nyɛ Pita awa Jɔn wa chiŋi ba suwachak ni a pɛɛsi ba, “Anti nyɛ ma kpok nɔ ma tiibi gbarigi ti o val nan? Anti nyɛ ma nyinbaan ma tum chen ni?”

ref: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun si Pita ni o ŋɔ ba, “Ma koorsi na awa ma nihɛɛta,

ref: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
text: ma daŋni ya ŋɔ mau chenani ya dɛ suŋi baal ti di o na alaafɛ.

ref: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
text: Ya daŋni ma awa Ju tim ta maga zim chenani mu kaa ŋɔ mau nɛnɛ. Yesu Kristo Nazarɛt nar kpokre ya kpa a tiibi un. Ma fun kpaasi Yesu a mari daakakli ti ni o sou, amaa Koosa fun lɛlɛ unu a li o sou ni. Yesu daharɛ baal nɔ na alaafɛ a wa chiŋi bar nɔ zinan nan.

ref: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbi Yesu wɛu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Buŋkul naa tɛsaalsa fun vɛ chen, waa ya buŋkul kpok aa ya kibirigi.’

ref: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
text: “O duŋduŋ koti a yisi nara a li ba tum tagna ni di ba tiŋ Koosa. Nandigi wa to tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a la zi koti a chiŋi o bar chiŋi ni a tum chen.”

ref: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
text: Ba naa funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni chen fun nu Pita sɛma, alaazibu fun kpak ba kinkan. Ba fun na di Pita awa Jɔn ya nyu bilim timwa, amaa hansura tama wa fun kpak ba. Kin nama funɛ nyɛ ba lisi di ba wa Yesu funɛ ya kpɛgsiwa.

ref: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
text: Baal naa Pita awa Jɔn fun tiibi chen fun chiŋi ba naa funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni, suwachak ni di ba na un. Chendaha ba fun moori a vɛ koti a sɛm wu digi.

ref: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
text: Ba fun ŋɔ ba kpa Pita awa Jɔn a li sal ka ba sɛm chenani ba zi ya ba.

ref: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Yɛrɛ ya kaa ya nara nama? Jɛrusalɛm tim ta maga zimu di Pita awa Jɔn tum tum zenu kinkan. Ya wa kikoti a tɔ chenani ba fun tum.

ref: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
text: Ma nyɛ ya tɛ ba nin di ba ta bu kpa Yesu sɔn a biksi nara, chen ni o wa zi mɔɔli a li tɔhɔn ti ni.”

ref: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
text: Ba funɛ sɛm dɔŋ ni peti, ba fun ŋɔ ba kpa Pita awa Jɔn a bu zu wa. Ba fun tɛ ba nin di ba ta bu kpa Yesu sɔn a sɛm koo a biksi nara tima.

ref: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
text: Amaa Pita awa Jɔn funɛ ŋɔ bau, “Ma ŋɔ ya chenani ya zi ya a deŋli Koosa suwachak ni. Ya tiŋ o nɔ koo ya tiŋ ma nɔɔ?

ref: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
text: Ya wa kigil di ya ŋɔ nara ni chenani ya fun na a nu.”

ref: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
text: Baal naa na alaafɛ a li tum zen nɔ ni funɛ kaali bun fumnaasiu. Yesu Nitiŋɛ Fun Sumi Koosa O Nyɛ Ba Kpak Ba Bambe

ref: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
text: Pita awa Jɔn fun vaan bur kaali di ba la lagmi Yesu gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni a ŋɔ ba chenani Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa Ju tim nihɛɛti ta fun ŋɔ ba.

ref: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: Ba funɛ nu chen, ba maga funɛ sumi Koosa a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana, i funɛ naami buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga wa teku awa kin naa maga kpak miisa a nyɛn.

ref: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
text: I funɛ nyɛ i siim weri ti ŋɔ ya nawaal Devid naa ya i tumtumna, chenani o zi sɔbi a li i sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Baŋnɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima fun na paan kinkan a tum tum yɔrisa a du hin ni?

ref: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga koorsa funɛ saksiu awa ba nihɛɛta funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni di ba chem ya kunyana Koosa awa naa o fun vɔu di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen a li laal ni.’

ref: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
text: Chen vɛɛna, Hɛrɔd awa Pontius Pailɔt wa Ju tim ta wa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima fun lagmi dɔŋ ni a go i be Yesu ni. I funɛ lisi un di o ya i tumtumna awa naa i fun ŋɔ di i zi wa tumu wuzu digi.

ref: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
text: I funɛ piin lisi chenani ba zi ya unu. I funɛ lisi chenu i kpok awa i tutɔ ti daha.

ref: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
text: Nyɛ wichɔɔrsa na alaafɛ, chen ni nara zi zim di i kpak kpoku. Nyɛ ya kpa i be Yesu naa i fun lisi, sɔn a tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma.”

ref: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
text: Ba naa maga se su Yesu funɛ ya nibɛgimnɛ. Nandigi wa fun ŋɔ di o titi kina ha vɛɛn ya o kinawa. Ba fun kpa a maga a bɔni dɔŋa.

ref: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
text: Nitumɛ ta fun ŋɔ nara tima ni chenani ba fun na ka Koosa lɛli ba kunyana Yesu a li o sou ni wa ba se ni. Koosa funɛ suŋi ba kinkan di ba sɛm ta zu nara tima bambe nɛnɛ.

ref: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
text: Kundɔŋ wa fun pɔɔgi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nidigi. Ba naa maga dɔ tɛɛnɛ koo damsa funɛ yogli au a kpa malɛ ta,

ref: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
text: a du nitumɛ ta nisa ni. Ba ka kpa a du ba naa la daŋni a nisa ni.

ref: Joseph was one of the followers who had sold a piece of property and brought the money to the apostles. He was a Levite from Cyprus, and the apostles called him Barnabas, which means “one who encourages others.”
text: O funɛ ya ba nidigirɛ a yogli o tɛɛnɛ a kpa malɛ ta a du nitumɛ ta nisa ni.

ref: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta digi sɔn funɛ ya Ananias. O haan sɔn funɛ ya Safaya. O gbanko fun yogli o tɛɛnɛ,

ref: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
text: a kpa malɛ ta bɔnɛ a du nitumɛ ta nisa ni, a ka kpa bɔnɛ pusi. O haan ti funɛ zim chenani o yau.

ref: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
text: Pita fun wa pɛɛsi un, “Ananias, i yogli i tɛɛnɛ ti chen, baŋnɛ i yisi malɛ ta bɔnɛ a pusi? Baŋnɛ i nyɛ sintana zu i bambi a daŋni o mur Koosa siim weri ti nan ni?

ref: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
text: I funɛ dɔ tɛɛnɛ ti chen, o funɛ ya i kinnɛ di i kpa un a tum chenani i daŋni. I funɛ yogli un a la malɛ ta chen, o ha funɛ vɛɛn ya i kinnɛ. Yɛrɛ i lisi i bambi ni di i ya nan? I daŋni i mur yau awa Koosa gbanko.”

ref: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
text: Ananias funɛ nu Pita sɛm ta, o fun vaan chel a sou. Ba naa maga funɛ nu chenani yɛɛ, hansura fun kpak ba kinkan.

ref: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
text: Bunɔɔn ta bɔnɛ fun si a zer gar a mɛɛsi un a la sogli un.

ref: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
text: O funɛ dugli hawasa torau, Ananias haan ti fun wa zu doonani ba maga fun lagmi chen. O wa fun zim o baal ti sou ni.

ref: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
text: Pita fun wa pɛɛsi un, “Tɛɛnɛ ti i yogli chen, malɛ ta maga nyɛnn mu wati nann?” O fun ŋɔ, “A maga chen.”

ref: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
text: Pita fun ŋɔ un, “Yɛrɛ i wa i baal la nɔɔni di ma zaamsi ya kunyana Koosa siim weri ti? Ba naa sogli i baal ti nyɛn dentil ni a pɔ di ba sogli i gbanko.”

ref: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
text: O funɛ nu o sɛm ta, o fun vaan chel a sou. Bunɔɔni ta fun zu a na di o sowu ka ba kpa un gbaari o baal ti ni a sogli.

ref: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
text: Hansura fun kpak Yesu nitiŋɛ ta awa ba naa maga nu chenani yɛɛ kinkan. Nitumɛ Fun Tum Alaazibu Tuma

ref: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
text: Nitumɛ ta fun tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma nara tima suwachak ni. Ba yaa funɛ zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti a lagmi Solomon zaŋ ti tuhu ni.

ref: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
text: Ba naa vɛ ya Yesu nitiŋɛ ta funɛ ɔm di ba wa ba lagmi dooni, amaa Jɛrusalɛm tim ta funɛ nyɛ ba jirimau.

ref: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
text: Chen saha ti nibaala awa nihaana nidamata se funɛ su Yesu a gbaari ba ni.

ref: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
text: Domi tum zene naa nitumɛ ta funɛ tum daha, nara tima fun kpa ba wichɔɔrsa a wa dau kichɔɔlɛ daha a li nyinbaanisi ni, chen ni Pita barsɔn zi val ba dahau ba na alaafɛ.

ref: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
text: Nidamata ba naa nyɛn tɔsi ni a go Jɛrusalɛm, fun kpa ba wichɔɔrsa awa ba naa alizin tagna nyɛn ba nyuuni ni a wa nitumɛ ta wati ba tiibi ba maga. Ju Tima Fun Muksi Nitumɛ Kinkan

ref: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
text: Doone Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti awa Sajusi tima ba naa funɛ nyɛn o wati, funɛ di handɔtirɛ a du nitumɛ ta ni ba tum were daha.

ref: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
text: Ba fun wa kpaksi ba maga a tɔ dɛ naa ba kpaksi nibin tagna duŋduŋ a tɔ chen.

ref: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
text: Dusum funɛ wa biri, Koosa malaikasi ta digi fun wa puk dɛ ti zɔɔnɔ a nyɛ ba li a la ŋɔ ba,

ref: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi chɔsa, nitumɛ ta fun tiŋ malaika ti nɔ a la zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti a biksi nara tima. Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti awa ba naa funɛ nyɛn o wati, fun yir sɛria nidiile ta awa Ju tim nihɛɛti ta maga ba lagmi o wati a tum Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti niilɛ di ba lisi nitumɛ ba naa ba tɔ dɛ chen, a wa.

ref: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
text: Ba funɛ la tel di ba lisi ba, ba wa fun na ba. Ba fun bur kaali ba naa tum ba chen wati a la ŋɔ ba,

ref: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
text: “Ya funɛ la tel dooni, zɔɔnɔ ti faru a pir ka lanjimasa chiŋi dooni a il. Ya fun la puk zɔɔnɔ ti a vɛ na nandigi.”

ref: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti awa Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti niilɛ ta nihɛɛ fun nu chenani ba sɛm a la lisi chenani ya ba.

ref: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
text: Ba funɛ sɛm dɔŋ ni chene, baal digi fun wa zu a ŋɔ ba, “Maa nu mu wati. Nitumɛ ta ma tɔ dɛ chen chiŋɛ Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni a biksi nara.”

ref: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
text: Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam niilɛ ta awa ba nihɛɛ ti fun kaali dooni lalaga a la kpa ba bur wa. Ba fun la kpa ba awa jirima ni a wa, domi hansura fun kpak ba di nara tima zi yaksi ba wa buŋkuli niu.

ref: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
text: Ba fun kpa ba wa nihɛɛta ba naa funɛ lagmi dooni suwachak. Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti fun pɛɛsi ba,

ref: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
text: “Ya wa fun tɛ ma nin di ma ta kpa Yesu sɔn a biksi nara timaa? Ma nyini na chenani ma ya. Ma mɔɔli o biksi tiu a li Jɛrusalɛm maga ni a la daŋni ya dɔ o sou.”

ref: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
text: Pita fun chiŋi nitumɛ ta bar chiŋi ni a sɛm a nyɛ ba, “O lawa ya tiŋ Koosa nɔu a kaali nibinsa nɔ.

ref: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
text: Ma waa funɛ kpaasi Yesu a mari daakakli ti ni o sou. Koosa, naa ya nawaalsi ta funɛ jɛɛmi, lɛli un a li o sou ni,

ref: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
text: a kpa un a zini kaali o tɔhɔn ti ni a nyɛ o kani Koosa nihɛɛti bar kani ni. Dooni o fun nyɛ o ya koora, chen ni Ju tim ta zi na nyinbaanu a gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa. Ba ya chen, Yesu zi yisi bau a li a ni ka Koosa zi gil a a ta.

ref: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
text: Kin nama wɛwa ya biksi nara tima. Koosa kpa o siim weri tirɛ a nyɛ ba naa tiŋ o nɔ. O gbanko la biksi ba chen.”

ref: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
text: Nihɛɛta ba naa funɛ lagmi chen, funɛ nu Pita sɛma, ba paan fun si ba ni ba daŋni ba kpu ba.

ref: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
text: Ba nidigi sɔn funɛ ya Gameliel. O funɛ ya Farisi tim ta digirɛ a ya Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta digi. Nara tima maga funɛ nyɛ un jirimau kinkan. O fun si chiŋi ba bambaan ni a ŋɔ ba lisi nitumɛ ta a li sal tama.

ref: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu dɔŋtima, ma doso chenani ma kaa ya nara nama.

ref: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
text: O gantɛl nɛɛ ba fun wa gur nara maga. Chen saha ti nidamata fun li tiŋ baal naa funɛ li Galili pulum chen. O sɔn funɛ ya Judas. Lanjimasi ta fun kpu o gbanko ka o gantɛl tiŋsi ta pisi dɔŋ ni.

ref: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
text: Nara nama nin nyɛn nan, ma ta ya wu digi a du ba ni. Nibine ya ba nyuhu tiina a li chenani ba lisi a la tum, ba yaali ti zi ya yɔriu.

ref: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
text: Koosa ka ya ba nyuhu tiina, ma wa zi koti a nyaŋi chenani ba ya. Ma wara daŋni di ya wa un yo dɔŋa.”

ref: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
text: Gameliel funɛ sɛm peti, ba maga fun la nɔɔni a nyɛ un. Ba fun ŋɔ ba ba kpa nitumɛ ta a zu a nyɛ Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti niilɛ ta tuksi ba wa vurmsi ni. Doone ba fun bukoo tɛ ba nin di ba ta bu kpa Yesu sɔn a sɛm nara ni a nyɛ ba kaali.

ref: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
text: Ba funɛ li, nitumɛ ta tusa fun sɔɔni kinkan di Koosa funɛ lisi ba di ba tuksi ba Yesu daha.

ref: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Wuzu kam ba yaa funɛ zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam tiu awa nara damsa di ba biksi nara di Yesu ya naa Koosa fun vɔu di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen.

ref: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
text: Chen saha ti Koosa yaa fun kpa nidamatau a gbaari Yesu nitiŋɛ ta ni. Wuzu kam nitumɛ ta yaa bɔni luhaana wa malɛu. Wuzu digi Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa la sɛm Girik tim sɛm chen funɛ zulimi ba naa la sɛm Ju tim sɛm chen, domi ba luhaani ta wa funɛ la ba tutɔ malɛ maga.

ref: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
text: Nitumɛ nara fidalɛ ta funɛ nu chenani la ya, ba fun yir Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga lagmi dɔŋ ni a ŋɔ ba, “Koosa wa fun lisi ya di ya nyini malɛ ta. O ka funɛ lisi yau di ya biksi nara tima o laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
text: Ya dɔŋtima, ma nyɛ ya lisi nara banɔpɛ ba naa nyɛn ya ni a kpak jirima awa nɛha kinkan a nyɛ Koosa siim weri ti te ba suwachak, kinyini malɛ ta.

ref: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
text: Ya tuma la zi ya yaa sumi Koosa a kibiksi nara o laaboor weri ti wɛ.”

ref: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
text: Ba sɛm ta funɛ nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta tu sɔɔnau kinkan. Doone ba fun lisi baal naa sɔn ya Stivin. O si funɛ su Yesu nɛnɛ ka Koosa siim weri ti fun te o suwachak. Ba gbanko fun lisi Filip, Prɔkɔrɔs, Nikanɔr, Timɔn, Parminas awa Nikolas naa funɛ si a bur tiŋ Ju tim jɛɛmi nyinbaan. O funɛ li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Antiɔk. Ba fun kpa ba a gbaari Stivin ni.

ref: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
text: Ba funɛ lisi ba peti a kpa ba a chiŋsi nitumɛ ta suwachak ni. Nitumɛ ta fun kpa ba nisa a saksi ba nyuuni ni a sumi Koosa a du ba ni.

ref: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
text: Koosa laaboor weri ti fun mɔɔli kinkan. Nara kinkan awa Koosa kɔkɔɔrsi ta nidamata fun nyɛ ba se su un a li Jɛrusalɛm ni a gbaari. Ju Tima Fun Kpak Stivin

ref: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
text: Nara tima funɛ na chenani Koosa funɛ suŋi Stivin kinkan a la nyɛ un kpok di o tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma a nyɛ ba.

ref: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
text: Ju tim ta bɔnɛ funɛ wau di ba wa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni a li Sairini pulum awa tɔhɔn naa ba yir Alɛzandria ni. Ba yaa yir ba bar lagmi dɛ ‘Yisi nibinsa a ta ba yɔri’ dɛ. Wuzu digi ba wa Ju tima ba naa li Silisia pulum awa Azia pulum ni fun lagmi a di Stivin amɔrɛ.

ref: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
text: Koosa siim weri ti funɛ suŋi Stivin o sɛm nɛha sɛma a nyaŋi ba.

ref: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
text: Ba fun gil un a la dɛu nara bɔnɛ a bɔ malɛ a nyɛ ba di ba ŋɔ nara tima ni di ba funɛ nu di Stivin chɔk Koosa awa Mosis sɔnnɛ.

ref: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
text: Ba fun burgi nara tima bɔnɛ, Ju tim nihɛɛti ta awa Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta a du un ni a wa kpak un a chiŋsi Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa sɛria nidiile ta suwachak ni.

ref: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
text: Ba fun kpa nusun tima di ba sɛm a du un ni. Ba fun ŋɔ, “Baal nɔ yaa chɔk Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam naa o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni, sɔn. O gbanko yaa chɔk Koosa chiksɛ naa o fun kpa nyɛ Mosis ni chen, sɔnu.

ref: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
text: Ya funɛ nuu ka o sɛm di Yesu Nazarɛt nar zi chɔk bar nɔu a chɛrgi nyinbaan naa Mosis fun ŋɔ ya nawaalsi ta di ba tiŋ.”

ref: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
text: Ba funɛ sɛm peti, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa sɛria nidiile ta maga funɛ nyini Stivin se ni a na ka o se chi Koosa malaikasi ta digi se.

ref: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
text: Doone Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti fun ŋɔ Stivin ni, “Chenani nara nama fiksi hin nan ya wutirɛɛ?”

ref: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu nyana awa mu dɔŋtima, maa nu chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma. Ya nyana Abraham funɛ te nyɛn Mɛsɔpɔtemia pulum nɛɛ a bu la nyɛn tɔhɔn naa ba yir Haran. Doonani o fun te nyɛn chen, wuzu digi Koosa naa kpak jirima awa chaani kinkan, fun wa,

ref: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
text: a ŋɔ un, ‘Gil i walɛ awa pulum nɔ ka mu biksi hin doonani i zi la.’

ref: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
text: Abraham fun tiŋ Koosa nɔ a li Mɛsɔpɔtemia pulum doonani Kaldia tima nyɛn, a la nyɛn Haran ni. O nyana funɛ sou, Koosa fun ŋɔ un o si a wa nyɛn bar nɔ.

ref: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
text: O funɛ wa tel bar nɔ, hali Koosa wa fun kpa tɛɛnɛ ti bar wewe a nyɛ un. Abraham wa fun kpak bisa, amaa Koosa funɛ vɔu di o wa o bi lulɛ zi wa dɔ unu wuzu digi.

ref: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
text: Chene Koosa fun ŋɔ un, ‘I bi lulɛ zi wa nyɛn hɔɔra pulum niu bunsa kɔksa naasi. Dooni ba zi wa ya nara tima yosau ka baa muksi ba kinkan.

ref: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
text: Bunsi ta wa peti, mu zi la di hɔɔri ta sɛriau a lɛrgi ba diŋsa a lisi i bi lulɛ ta di ba wa jɛɛmi min bar nɔ.’

ref: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
text: Koosa wa Abraham funɛ ya nɔ bɛgim ka Koosa fun ŋɔ un di o teŋsi nara tima ba zim chenani ba ya. Abraham fun lul be a du un Aizik. O funɛ wa ya wuzu mɛnasa ba fun teŋ un. Aizik fun wa bi a lul Jakɔb. Jakɔb gbanko fun wa bi a lul nara fidalɛ ba dɔ zɔɔnɔ.

ref: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
text: “Ba naa dɔ zɔɔnɔ ti chen fun di handɔta a du ba niinbe Josɛf ni a yogli un a nyɛ nara ni ba kpa un kaali Ijipt pulum a fun bu la yogli un doon o vɛɛn ya yoma. Koosa wa un funɛ nyɛn dooni,

ref: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
text: a na chenani o namsi a lisi un. O fun nyɛ o kpak zima kinkan a ya nibin weri Ijipt tim koora naa ba yir Fɛɛrɔ daŋni o na un. O funɛ wa o wati chene, o vɔu un di o ya pulum awa Fɛɛrɔ damsa koora a sak un ni.

ref: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
text: Chen saha ti lɔsa fun wa chel Ijipt pulum awa Kenan pulum naa ya nawaalsi ta fun nyɛn ni. Ba naa maga funɛ nyɛn doon, wa fun koti a na diwe a di a la namsi kinkan.

ref: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
text: Wuzu digi Jakɔb fun nu di diwe nyɛn Ijipt ni a fun tum o bisa di ba la yau diwe ta a wa.

ref: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
text: O funɛ tum ba a li Ijipt pulum ni bilɛ tima, Josɛf fun kpa o titi a la biksi ba. Fɛɛrɔ fun nu di Josɛf maanasa gbanko wau.

ref: Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were
text: Josɛf fun yir o nyana Jakɔb awa o walɛ di ba wa pu un Ijipt ni. Nara fumnɔpɛ danyuu funɛ ya o lul.

ref: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
text: Ba maga fun kaali a la nyɛn Ijipt ni. Doonee o wa o bisi ta fun siti.

ref: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
text: Abraham funɛ te yau tɛɛnɛ naa nyɛn doonani ba yir Sɛkɛm a li Kenan pulum ni a bɔ malɛ a nyɛ Hamor tim ni. Gbɛntɛl bɔ funɛ nyɛn dooni. Jakɔb wa o bisi ta funɛ siti, ba fun kpa ba a sogli un ni.

ref: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
text: “Saha funɛ dugli di Koosa ŋɔ Abraham ni chenani o vɔu chen mun, ya nara ba naa nyɛn Ijipt ni chen, funɛ gbaariu kinkan.

ref: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
text: Doone Ijipt tima fun bu vɔu koor fali dooni. O wa Josɛf wa fun zim dɔŋa.

ref: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
text: O fun mur ya nara a nyɛ ba na namsi. Hali o funɛ muksi bau di ba gil ba bichɛsa a ta kɔha ni ba siti.

ref: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
text: Saha tirɛ ba fun lul bichɛl pilim weri ba yir un Mosis. Ba fun dɛɛsi un o nyan dam ni pɛnsa tora.

ref: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
text: Ijipt tim ta fun muksi ba di ba kpa o gbanko a la ta kɔha ti ni. Doone Fɛɛrɔ nihabichɛla fun na un a la un a dɛɛsi a chi o ya o be.

ref: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
text: O funɛ wa bi, Ijipt tim ta fun biksi un a li ba zima ni o burgi nibin zen a li o sɛma awa o tuma daha.

ref: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
text: O funɛ la na ba chen, o fun na Ijipt tim digi ka o tuk Ju tim digi. O fun la gɔɔgi Ijipt tim baal a mo un a kpu.

ref: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
text: O fun baa di Ju tim ta zi zimu di Koosa zi nyɛ o yisi bau a li yosi ni. Amaa ba wa fun zim chen.

ref: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi Mosis fun na ka Ju tim nara balɛ yo dɔŋa. O fun la gɔɔgi ba dɔŋ ni a ŋɔ ba di ba ya nɔ bɛgim. O fun ŋɔ ba, ‘Ma nu mu wati. Yɛrɛ ma ya niinbisa a la yo dɔŋa?’

ref: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
text: Naa funɛ te mo o dɔŋtiin ti chen bum yo a zak Mosis a ta a ŋɔ un, ‘I wa dɔ ya a kpak nyinbaan di i di ya sɛria.

ref: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
text: I daŋni i kpu minu a chi chenani i dɛ kpu Ijipt tim baal ti dɛrati chen?’

ref: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
text: Mosis funɛ nu o sɛm ta, o fun si chɔ li Ijipt ni a la nyɛn pulum naa ba yir Midian. Dooni o wa o haan fun lul bisa lɛ.

ref: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
text: Alaazibu fun kpak un kinkan o la suwachak di o dugli un. Doone o fun nu ka ya kunyana Koosa ŋɔ un,

ref: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
text: ‘Min ya Koosa naa i nawaalsa Abraham, Aizik awa Jakɔb funɛ jɛɛmi chen.’ Hansura fun kpak Mosis o bara jigiri o vɛ kpak bambi di o nyini.

ref: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
text: Koosa fun bukoo ŋɔ un, ‘Ur i natausa. Doonani i chiŋi chen chi natausa duuliu.

ref: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
text: Mu funɛ na chenani mu nara na namsi Ijipt ni kinkan. Mu funɛ nuu ka ba fɔuli. Chendaha mu tu wa di mu wa yisi ba a li ba fɔuli ti ni. Kuwa mu kpa hin a la Ijipt.’ Koosa fun sɛm peti a kaali.

ref: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
text: “Ju tim nara balɛ ta Mosis fun gɔɔgi chen fun te ŋɔ un di o wa dɔ ba a kpak nyinbaan ti di o di ba sɛria. Koosa fun tum un di o bu bur kila Ju tima ba naa funɛ vɛ un, wati a nyɛ o malaika naa o na a li bugil pum din ti ni chen suŋi un o ya ba nyuhu tiina awa naa la zi yisi ba.

ref: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
text: Mosis nɔ fun ŋɔ Ju tim ta ni, ‘Koosa zi lisi witinsi ta digiu a li ma ni a tum un ma wati a chi chenani o fun tum min ma wati nan.’

ref: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
text: Mosis ya naa wa ya nawaalsa Ju tim ta funɛ lagmi a li nyabaan ti ni. Koosa malaika ti fun ŋɔ un dooni a li bi Sainai a kpa Koosa chiksɛ naa biksi ba chenani ba zi nyɛn, a nyɛ un. Nannɛ ba fun kpa nyɛ ya di ya tiŋ.

ref: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
text: “Ya nawaalsi ta fun vɛ di ba wa zi tiŋ Mosis nɔ a la daŋni ba bur la Ijipt pulum.

ref: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
text: Chen saha ti Ɛrɔn fun kɔɔri vuk o chi nupɔl ka ba fun kɔɔri kɔɔra a du un ni. Ba tusa fun sɔɔni kinkan a li chenani Ɛrɔn ya nyɛ ba chen ni.

ref: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
text: Koosa funɛ na chenani ba ya, o fun burgi o gantɛl a ta ba di ba jɛɛmi wula a li buudaha ni. Koosa witinsi ta digi fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, ‘Ma Ju tima, ma funɛ nyɛn nyabaan ni bun fumnaasi, ma wa fun kpa sela a kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ min.

ref: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
text: Ma funɛ kɔɔri vuku a yir un Mɔlɔk a kpa gar a tɔ un nha daha. Ma gbanko funɛ kɔɔri vuku a chi wul a yir un Refan. Domi ma funɛ kɔɔri vuk na a jɛɛmi a daha, mu zi nyɛ nara wa kpaksi mau a kpa ma kila a li pulum naa galɛ tɔhɔn naa ba yir Babilɔn ni.’

ref: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
text: “Ya nawaalsa funɛ gba a li nyabaan ni, ba yaa kpa ba lɛuu naa biksi ba di Koosa nyɛn ba watiu, a val. Mosis fun kɔɔri un a chi chenani Koosa fun ŋɔ un chen.

ref: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
text: Bun bɔnɛ tuhu ni Koosa fun duŋni ba dɔma a li pulum nɔ ni ka ba wa ba nyuhu tiina naa ba yir Jɔsua wa dɔ bar nɔ. Ba wa lɛu ti fun lagmi nyɛn bar nɔ, hali saha naa ba fun wa vɔu ya nawaal Devid koora.

ref: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
text: Chenani Devid fun tum sɔɔni Koosa tuha kinkan. O funɛ daŋni o sa bar jɛɛmi dama a nyɛ Koosa naa ya nawaal Jakɔb funɛ jɛɛmi chen o nyɛn.

ref: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
text: Amaa Koosa wa fun nyɛ un nyinbaan di o ya chen a nyɛ o be koor Sɔlɔmɔn sa un.

ref: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
text: “Amaa Solomon naa zenee kinkan a kaali a maga, o wa nyɛn damsi naa nibinsi sa ni. O witinsi ta digi fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ,

ref: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
text: ‘Mu nyɛn mu tɔhɔn nɛɛ a dɔ kina maga, ka tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga chi mu naŋgere bar zɔŋi. Ma wa kikoti a sa dama o wer a mak mu bar kani hiisi,

ref: I have made everything.”
text: Domi min funɛ naami kina maga.’ ”

ref: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
text: Hali Koosa witinsi ta digi wa fun ka ma nawaalsi ta vɛ namsi un. Koosa funɛ tum ba di ba wa ŋɔ ma nawaalsi ta ni di o zi wa tum o tumtumna naa tiŋ o tutɔu wuzu digi. Ma nawaalsi ta funɛ kpu ba chen a chi chenani ma vɛ a kpu o tumtum ti chen.

ref: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
text: amaa Koosa siim weri ti fun si Stivin bambi ni a nyɛ o nyini a na Koosa jirima awa o chaani kinkan a li o tɔhɔn ti ni. O funɛ vɛɛn nyɛ o na Yesu ka o chiŋi Koosa nihɛɛti bar kani ni dooni.

ref: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
text: Stivin fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma nu mu wati nɛnɛ. Ba puk Koosa tɔha bulzɔɔnɔu ka mu na Yesu naa ya nibine be ka o chiŋi Koosa nihɛɛti ti bar kani ni.”

ref: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
text: Ba maga fun tɔ ba diŋsa a la chɛɛmi, chen ni ba wa zi bu nu o sɛm ta a si a la nyirgi un.

ref: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
text: Ba fun lɛrgi un li tɔhɔn ti ni a ur ba takta zene a nyɛ bunɔɔn naa ba yir Sɔɔl nyini a. Doone ba fun pini buŋkula a yaksi Stivin.

ref: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
text: Ba funɛ yaksi un chene, Stivin fun sumi ya kunyan ti a ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana Yesu, la mu siima.”

ref: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
text: O fun gbiri a sɛm ta zaŋ ni, “Mu kunyana, gil ba tum tagna naa ba tum a du min ni nan a nyɛ ba.” O funɛ sɛm chene a sou.

ref: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
text: Sɔɔl funɛ namsi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta kinkan. O funɛ zu ba damsa bu bɛgim bɛgima a lɛrgi nibaala awa nihaana a li a tɔ ba dɛ. Filip Fun Biksi Samaria Tima Koosa Laaboor Weri Wɛ

ref: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ pisi chen fun kaali tɔ damata a la biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
text: Ba maga fun nu chenani o sɛm chen nɛnɛ a na tum zene naa o tum.

ref: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
text: O funɛ ŋɔ alizin tagna ni di a li nara tima bɔnɛ ni a fun huusi kinkan a li ba ni. O gbanko fun tiibi ba naa nisa awa naŋgere sitɛɛ awa ba naa la chel chɛrsa.

ref: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
text: Samaria tim ta maga tusa funɛ sɔɔniu kinkan chenani o ya chen daha.

ref: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Simɔn. Bunsa mɛna tuhu ni o yaa funɛ tum tu bilim tumwa a nyɛ Samaria tim ta ni a ŋɔ di o funɛ ya nibin zenne.

ref: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
text: Tɔhɔn ti maga nara, ba naa funɛ ya bichɛsa hali a wa nihɛɛta maga, funɛ nu o sɛmau nɛnɛ a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Baal nɔ kpok zen nyɛ o chi Koosa.”

ref: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
text: Chenani o yaa funɛ tum tu bilim tuma a nyɛ ba chen ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Chendaha ba fun nu o sɛm ta nɛnɛ.

ref: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
text: Filip funɛ wa biksi ba Yesu Kristo wɛ awa chenani Koosa daŋni o dɔ nara wɛ, nibaala awa nihaana kinkan fun nyɛ ba se su Koosa laaboor weri ti. Doone ba fun nyɛ o chaksi ba wa ni ni.

ref: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
text: Nitumɛ ba naa funɛ kpalimi Jɛrusalɛm ni chen, funɛ nu chenani Samaria tim ta nidamata se su Koosa laaboor weri ti, ba fun tum Pita awa Jɔn ba wati.

ref: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
text: Ba fun la tel doon ni a sumi Koosa di o kpa o siim weri ti a du ba ni.

ref: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Filip fun kpa ya kunyana Yesu sɔn a chaksi ba wa ni ni, amaa ba ha wa fun la Koosa siim weri ti.

ref: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
text: Pita awa Jɔn funɛ sumi Koosa peti, ba fun kpa ba nisa a saksi ba nyuuni ni Koosa siim weri ti zu ba ni.

ref: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
text: Simɔn fun chiŋi a nyini chenani Pita awa Jɔn kpa ba nisa a saksi ba ni ba la Koosa siim weri ti. O fun lisi malɛ a biksi Pita awa Jɔn,

ref: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
text: a ŋɔ ba, “Ma purigi ma kpok nɔ a nyɛ min, chen ni mu gbanko zi kotiu a kpa mu nisa a saksi nara ni di Koosa siim weri ti zu ba ni ka mu bɔ ma.”

ref: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
text: Pita fun ŋɔ un, “Kpa i malɛ kila. I wa kikoti a bɔ Koosa di o nyɛ hin kpok i kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ nara ni.

ref: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
text: I wa ya wa ya nibɛgim, domi i tuha wa deŋli Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you.
text: Gil tum tagin naa i lisi a sumi ya kunyana Koosa di o gil un aa ta.

ref: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
text: N zimu di i tu haasa nyɛ i di handɔta kinkan a du ya ni. I tum tagna tɔ hin a du a dɛ ni.”

ref: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
text: Simɔn fun ŋɔ Pita awa Jɔn ni, “Ma sumi ma kunyana Koosa a nyɛ min, chen ni o wa zi nyɛ mu namsi a li chenani mu tum ni.”

ref: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
text: Ba wa Simɔn funɛ sɛm peti, ba fun biksi nara tima Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ awa chenani ba zim Yesu wɛ a bur kaali Jɛrusalɛm. Ba funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a la chene, ba funɛ biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ tɔsi naa nyɛn Samaria pulum tim bɔnɛ ni. Filip Fun Pu Itiopia Tim Nihɛɛ

ref: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
text: Filip fun tum chenani malaika ti nɔ un. Nibin zen digi funɛ val nyinbaan ti ni a ya namugil a wuzu ti. O funɛ ya nihɛɛrɛ a li pulum naa ba yir Itiopia ka nihaan digi fun nyɛn doon a ya ba koora. Ba funɛ yir un Kandese. O fun kpa o malɛ a du nihɛɛ ti nin ni. O funɛ la Jɛrusalɛmnɛ a la jɛɛmi Koosa,

ref: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ Filip ni, “Kila a la tiŋ o gantɛl.”

ref: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
text: Filip fun chɔ la tiŋ un a nu ka o karimi a li Azaaya tɔn ti ni. O fun ŋɔ un, “I zim chenani i karimi chen munuu?”

ref: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
text: Baal ti fun ŋɔ un, “Nandigi vɛ ŋɔ min, mu wa kizim o mun.” Doone o fun yir Filip o wa zu a gbaari un ni.

ref: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
text: Nannɛ o funɛ karimi, “O funɛ chi piisire ba kpa un a kɔrigi. O gbanko funɛ chi piisi be naa ba ka funi o pusa ka o vɛ chɛɛmirɛ. O yaa fun mooriu.

ref: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
text: Nara fun nyini un a chi bichɛla. Nandigi wa fun to a chiŋi o gantɛl ni di ba kok un ka ba kpu un. Doone o miisa fun peti a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni ka o vɛ kpak bisa ba zi chiŋi o bar chiŋi ni.”

ref: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
text: Baal ti fun ŋɔ Filip ni, “Mu sumi hinu di i ŋɔ min naa wɛ Azaaya fun sɔbi chen. O funɛ sɔbi o titi wɛwa koo nandigii?”

ref: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
text: Filip fun te ŋɔ un chenani o karimi chen mun a zi biksi un Koosa laaboor weri ti awa Yesu wɛ.

ref: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
text: Filip fun ŋɔ, “I si su Yesu wa i bambi maga ni, mu zi chaksi hin wa ni ni.” Namugil ti fun jɛrgi a ŋɔ un, “Mu si su di Yesu Kristo ya Koosa bere.”))

ref: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
text: Ba fun la tel dooni, o nyɛ zaaga ti chiŋi. O wa Filip fun li loori munzuha ti ni a la tu ni ti ni. Filip fun chaksi un dooni.

ref: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
text: Ba funɛ wa li ni ti ni, ya kunyana Koosa siim weri ti fun vaan zer Filip a kaali, chen ni baal ti wa zi bu na un. Baal ti fun bu zu o loori munzuha ti a la wa tu sɔɔn kinkan ni.

ref: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun nyɛ Filip kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Asdɔd. O funɛ biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ a li tɔsa maga ni hali a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Siizaria.

ref: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
text: Nidamata funɛ nu Sɔɔl biksi ti a gbaari. O fun biksi ba di Yesu ya naa Koosa fun vɔu di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen nɛnɛ, chen ni Ju tim ba naa nyɛn dooni chen, wa kidi un amɔrɛ.

ref: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
text: Si a la zu tɔhɔn ti. Nandigi zi ŋɔ hinu chenani i zi yau dooni.”

ref: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
text: Mu zi biksi un chenani o zi biksi ba a na namsi kinkanu.”

ref: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.

text: a na a chi deesi ka i zu o dɛ a kpa i nisa a sak un ni di o se ta bu lɛli.”

text: O funɛ sɛm sɛm ta peti, Sɔɔl se ta fun vaan bu lɛli. O funɛ chi nyinsa pɛrɛ tɔ a a lɛligi. O fun si a nyɛ ba chaksi un wa ni ni.

ref: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
text: Sɔɔl ha funɛ vɛɛna namsi Yesu nitiŋɛ tau Jɛrusalɛm ni a daŋni o kpu ba. O fun la Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti wati,

ref: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
text: O funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a wa dugli Damaskus, kundɔŋ fun nyiksi a li buudaha ni a chaani a du un ni.

ref: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”

ref: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: O fun ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, anti ya hin?” Ta ti fun ŋɔ, “Min ya Yesu naa i namsi chen.

ref: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
text: Ba naa wa Sɔɔl funɛ gba chen fun chiŋi a gil chɛɛma. Ba fun nu ta a vɛ na nandigi.

ref: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,

ref: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
text: O wa funɛ na wuzu butora wuzu. O wa fun vɛɛna di diwe a la nyɔ.

ref: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta digi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Ananias. Ya kunyan ti fun wa ŋɔ un a chi o deesi, “Ananias.” O fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, minnɛ.”

ref: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
text: Ya kunyan ti fun bu ŋɔ un, “Si a la baal naa ba yir Judas chen dama a li nyinbaan naa ba yir deŋli. I la tel dooni, i pɛɛsi Sɔɔl naa li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Taasus chen wɛ. O la sumi Koosa,

ref: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
text: Ananias fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, nibin damata funɛ ŋɔ min baal ti wɛ awa chenani o nyɛ Koosa nara namsi kinkan a li Jɛrusalɛm ni.

ref: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
text: Lalaknɔ o chiŋi Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta bar chiŋi ni a wa bar nɔ di o kpaksi ba naa maga kpa i sɔn a jɛɛmi hin.”

ref: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
text: Ya kunyan ti fun bu ŋɔ un, “Mu funɛ lisi un di o tum mu tuma a kibiksi Ju tim ta, ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima awa ba koorsa mu wɛ. Adaha la tum chenani mu ŋɔ hin.

ref: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Ananias fun si a la zu dam ti a kpa o nisa a sak Sɔɔl ni a ŋɔ un, “Mu niinbe Sɔɔl, ya kunyana Yesu naa i na nyinbaan ni chen, funɛ tum min di mu wa lɛli i se. O gbanko funɛ tum min di o nyɛ Koosa siim weri ti zu hin ni a te i suwachak.”

ref: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
text: O fun di diwe o kpok bu bur a wa. Doone o fun nyɛn Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Damaskus ni chen wati wuzu bilɛ. Sɔɔl Fun Biksi Damaskus Tima Koosa Laaboor Weri Wɛ

ref: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
text: A funɛ ya tamnɛ, o fun vaan si a zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛɛna aa biksi ba di Yesu ya Koosa bere.

ref: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”

ref: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
text: Nha wa dusum maga ba yaa funɛ pɔu a li tɔhɔn ti bulzɔɔnɔ ni di ba kpak un kpu. Amaa Sɔɔl funɛ nu chenani ba daŋni ba ya unu.

ref: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
text: Doone Barnabas fun wa suŋi un a kpa un kaali nitumɛ ta wati. O fun ŋɔ ba chenani Sɔɔl fun chem ya kunyana Yesu nyinbaan ni ba sɛm sɛma. O gbanko fun ŋɔ ba chenani Sɔɔl fun kpak bambi a biksi Damaskus tima Yesu wɛ.

ref: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.

ref: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
text: O dɔŋtim ta funɛ nu chenani ba daŋni ba kpu un, ba fun tum Sɔɔl Siizaria ni o zu arŋa a kaali Taasus.

ref: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
text: Saha ti kpa la, Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Judia pulum, Galili pulum awa Samaria pulum ni, wa fun bu namsi. Koosa siim weri ti fun nyɛ ba se su a gbaari a la nyɛ ya kunyan ti wa jirima saha maga. Nidamata fun nyɛ ba se su un a gbaari ba ni. Pita Fun La Nyɛn Lida Awa Jɔpa Ni

ref: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
text: Saha tirɛ Pita fun gba a la tɔ damata a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Lida ka Koosa nara bɔnɛ nyɛn doon.

ref: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.

ref: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
text: Pita fun wa ŋɔ un, “Ɛnɛus, Yesu Kristo nyɛ i na alaafɛ. Si a guti i kichɔɔli.” Ɛnɛus fun vaan na alaafɛ a si a guti un.

ref: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
text: Lida tim ta awa ba naa maga nyɛn tɛɛnɛ naa mak dɔŋ ni fun nu chenani yɛɛ, a nyɛ ba se su ya kunyan ti.

ref: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nidigi funɛ ya nihaannɛ ba yir un Tabita. Girik tima sɛm ni ba ka yir un Dɔɔkas. O mun funɛ ya ‘cherga’. O fun nyɛn tɔhɔn naa ba yir Jɔpa. O yaa funɛ tum tum werewa a la suŋi fara tima.

ref: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
text: Chen saha ti wichɔɔrta fun wa kpak un o sou. O walɛ fun sɔ un ni a kpa un dau dɛ naa ba sa a zɔŋ o dɔŋtiina nyuhu ni.

ref: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
text: Jɔpa awa Lida wa fun bol wa dɔŋ ni. Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Jɔpa ni, funɛ nu di Pita nyɛn Lida ni, ba fun tum nara balɛ o wati ba la ŋɔ un di o ta vusi a kuwa.

ref: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
text: Pita fun vaan si a tiŋ ba. Pita wa ba funɛ wa tel Jɔpa, ba fun kpa un zini dɛ naa Dɔɔkas nyɛn chen. Nidamata fun lagmi dooni a wi. Luhaana gbanko fun wa gbaari ba ni a pa takta wisa awa takta zene naa o fun nyɛn o miisa ni a sɔ a biksi Pita.

ref: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
text: Pita fun nyɛ ba kpa a maga a li a gbiri a sumi Koosa. Doone o fun burgi o titi a chem Dɔɔkas a ŋɔ un, “Tabita, si kani.” O fun lɛli o se a na Pita a vaan si kani.

ref: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
text: Pita fun kpak o nin a suŋi un o si chiŋi. Doone o fun yir nara tima awa luhaana ba naa funɛ ya Koosa nara, ba zu wa. O fun kpa Dɔɔkas a nyɛ ba.

ref: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
text: Laaboor naa funɛ ya chen, fun mɔɔli Jɔpa tɔhɔn ti maga. Nidamata se fun su ya kunyan ti chen daha.

ref: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn Jɔpa ni ba yir un Simɔn. O tuma yaa fun ya sela tɔna tuma. Pita fun nyɛn o dam ni a vusi.

ref: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
text: Lanjima nihɛɛ digi funɛ nyɛn Siizaria ni. Ba funɛ yir un Kɔnilius. O funɛ ya lanjimasa kɔkwa nyuhu tiinnɛ. Ba maga funɛ li pulum naa ba yir Itali chene.

ref: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
text: O wa o walɛ yaa funɛ sumi Koosa a la jɛɛmi un. O gbanko yaa funɛ suŋi Ju tim fara tim tau.

ref: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.

ref: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: Kɔnilius fun kpa o se a zɔŋ un ni. Hansura kpak un a li o nyini ti ni. O fun ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, baŋnɛ i daŋni mu suŋi hin?” Malaika ti fun bu ŋɔ un, “Chenani ii sumi Koosa a la suŋi fara tim ta chen sɔɔnɛ o tuha. Chendaha o fun tum min i wati.

ref: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
text: Tum i walɛ ta bɔnɛ ba la yir baal naa ba yir Pita chen a li Jɔpa ni. O sɔn digi ya Simɔn.

ref: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
text: O nyɛn Simɔn naa la tum sela tɔna tuma chen dam nɛɛ a dugli teku nɔ.”

ref: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
text: Malaika ti funɛ kaali, Kɔnilius fun yir o dam bisa lɛ awa o lanjimasa nibɛgim ba wa o wati. Lanjima ti yaa funɛ jɛɛmi Koosau ka o si su un kinkan.

ref: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba chenani yɛɛ a tum ba Jɔpa ni.

ref: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi di ba nyɛn nyinbaan nɛɛ a wa dugli Jɔpa. Nha sal baan nyuha, Pita fun zini dɛ naa nyuha mak dɔŋa a li Simɔn dam ti ni di o sumi Koosa.

ref: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
text: Lɔsa fun kpak un kinkan o daŋni diwe o di. Ba funɛ ya diwe, o fun na kin bɔnɛ a chi o deesi.

ref: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.

ref: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
text: Sela awa zimebisa awa a nawara la nyɛ hala maga kikurgɛ funɛ nyɛn gar zen ti tuha ni.

ref: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: O fun nu ta ka o ŋɔ un, “Pita, si a kpu a se.”

ref: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Pita fun ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, mu wa kiya chen. N ha wa di diwe naa ba ŋɔ di mu chi.”

ref: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Ta ti fun bukoo ŋɔ un, “Ta chi diwe naa Koosa ŋɔ di o wa ya kichiili.”

ref: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
text: Ta ti funɛ sɛm un chene butora tiina, ka gar ti fun bu zini kaali Koosa tɔhɔn ti.

ref: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
text: Pita fun lisi chenani o fun na a chi deesi chen mun. O funɛ lisi chene, ba naa Kɔnilius fun tum chen fun na doonani Simɔn dam ti nyɛn a chiŋi o bulzɔɔnɔ ni.

ref: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
text: Ba fun pɛɛsi, “Simɔn Pita nyɛn bar nɔuu?”

ref: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
text: Pita ha funɛ vɛɛna lisi chenani o fun na chen mun ka Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ un, “Kinu mu wati. Nara batora chiŋɛ bulzɔɔnɔ ti ni a daŋni ba na hin.

text: Si tu la ba wati a ta ɔmi di i tiŋ ba, domi min tum ba i wati.”

ref: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
text: Pita fun si tu a la ŋɔ ba, “Min ya naa ma dɛu chen. Baŋnɛ maa daŋni mu suŋi ma?”

ref: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya kunyana Kɔnilius naa ya lanjimasa nihɛɛ, tum ya i wati. O ya nibin werire a yaa jɛɛmi Koosa ka Ju tim ta maga nyɛ un jirima kinkan. Koosa malaikasi ta digi funɛ wa ŋɔ un di o yir hin i wa o dama, chen ni o zi nu chenani i daŋni i ŋɔ unu.”

ref: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
text: Pita funɛ nu ba sɛm ta, o fun yir ba di ba wa chɔ. Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, ba maga fun si a tiŋ dɔŋa a kaali. Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Jɔpa ni, bɔnɛ fun gbaari ba ni a kaali.
