ref: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
text: Chɔɔsa fun bu wa pisi, ba funɛ tel Siizaria. Kɔnilius, o walɛ ta awa o pɛgsa ba naa o fun yir chen funɛ pɔ ba di ba zaami ba.

ref: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
text: Pita funɛ daŋni o zu dam ti, Kɔnilius fun wa chem un di o zaami. O fun gbiri a kpa o tila a baŋ tɛɛnɛ ni.

ref: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
text: Pita fun ŋɔ un, “Si chiŋi. Mu ya nibine a chi hin.”

ref: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
text: Ba funɛ zu la chene ba sɛm sɛma dɔŋ wati. Nidamata funɛ lagmi dam ti tuhu ni.

ref: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
text: Pita fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma zimu nɛnɛ di Ju tim ta chiksɛ biksɛɛ di ya wa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ta lagmi dɔŋ ni. Koosa ka funɛ biksi minu di mu zi kotiu a lagmi nara maga ni.

ref: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
text: Chendaha ma yir min, mu vɛ bur gantɛl. Ma ŋɔ min chenani mu zi suŋi ma.”

ref: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
text: Kɔnilius fun ŋɔ un, “Wuzu bunaasi gantɛl ni saha naa niparɛ yaa si ba par ni chen, mu funɛ nyɛn bar nɔwa a sumi Koosa. Chen saha ti awa lalaknɔ funɛ ya kibɛgimnɛ. Baal digi fun laari kichaani a vaan wa chiŋi mu suwachak ni,

ref: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
text: a ŋɔ, ‘Kɔnilius, chenani ii sumi Koosa a la suŋi fara tim ta chen sɔɔnɛ o tuha.

ref: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
text: Tum i walɛ ta bɔnɛ ba la yir baal naa ba yir Pita chen a li Jɔpa ni. O sɔn digi ya Simɔn. O nyɛn Simɔn naa la tum sela tɔna tuma chen dam nɛɛ a dugli teku nɔ.’

ref: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
text: Mu fun vaan tum ba di ba la yir hin. I ka ya nibin weri a wa. Lalaknɔ ya maga lagmi bar nɔwa Koosa suwachak ni a pɔ di i ŋɔ ya chenani o ŋɔ hin.” Pita Fun Biksi Kɔnilius Koosa Laaboor Weri Wɛ

ref: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
text: Doone Pita fun ŋɔ ba, “Lalaknɔwa mu zim di o ya wutirɛ di Koosa wara daŋni digi a ka digi.

ref: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
text: Ba naa maga yaa tum tum were a la nyɛ un jirima a li pulumsi maga ni, o zi la ba duu.

ref: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
text: Ma zim chenani o fun tum Yesu Kristo, naa ya kina maga nyuhu tiina, di o biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ Ju tim ta ni a nyɛ ba nyinbaan ba wa Koosa bu ya nɔ bɛgim.

ref: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
text: Ma gbanko zim chenani Jɔn fun te chaksi Yesu wa ni ni ka chenani zi ya a li Galili pulum awa Judia tɔsa maga niu.

ref: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
text: Yesu Nazarɛt narrɛ, Koosa fun lisi un a kpa o siim weri ti a du un ni. Koosa gbanko fun kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ Yesu ni di o tum tum were a yisi ba naa maga sintana fun dɔ a li o nin ni. Koosa funɛ nyɛn o wati. Chendaha o fun koti a ya chen.

ref: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
text: Ya waa funɛ na chenani maga o tum a li Jɛrusalɛm awa Ju tim ta pulum naa go un chen awa ya se ni. Ba fun kpa un a kpaasi a mari daakakli ti ni o sou.

ref: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
text: Wuzu butora wuzu, Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni a nyɛ ya bu na un.

ref: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
text: Nandigi wa fun bu na un, amaa ya naa Koosa fun lisi di ya na un wa ya se ni duŋduŋ na un, chen ni ya zi ŋɔ nara niu o wɛ. A gantɛl nɛɛ Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni, hali ya wa un lagmi a di diwe a nyɔ.

ref: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
text: O fun ŋɔ ya di o lawa ya lau a la biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ awa chenani Koosa fun lisi un di wuzu digi o zi wa di nisitɛ awa ba naa vɛ siti sɛriau.

ref: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
text: Koosa witinsi ta maga funɛ sɛm o wɛu saha digi. Ba fun biksi di ba naa maga se su un, Koosa zi gil ba tum tagnau a nyɛ ba Yesu sɔn daha.” Kɔnilius Awa O Walɛ Fun La Koosa Siim Weri

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
text: Pita ha funɛ vɛɛna sɛm chene, Koosa siim weri ti fun wa zu ba naa maga la nu chen, bambe ni.

ref: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
text: “Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a du nara nama ni a gbaari ya ni. Ya kaa ya chaksi ba wa ni ni, nandigi wa to a la zi ŋɔ di ya ta chaksi ba.”

ref: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
text: Doone o fun ŋɔ ba di ba la kpa Yesu sɔn a chaksi ba wa ni ni. Doone ba fun sumi Pita o nyɛn ba wati wuzu bilɛ.

ref: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
text: Pita fun li Jɔpa ni a kaali Jɛrusalɛm. Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ ya Ju tima a nyɛn dooni, bɔnɛ funɛ daŋni ba teŋsi Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim chen. Pita funɛ la tel dooni, ba fun wa zulimi un a pɛɛsi,

ref: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
text: “Baŋnɛ i la nyɛn ba naa vɛ teŋsi ni wati? Hali ba ŋɔ yau di i gbaari ba ni a di diwe.”

ref: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
text: “Mu funɛ nyɛn tɔhɔn naa ba yir Jɔpa nɛɛ a sumi Koosa a fun na kundɔŋ a chi mu deesi. O fun nyini a na gar zen ka o li Koosa tɔhɔn ti a tu wa tɛɛnɛ a chi nara kpaksi o nɔɔsa a wa dugli min.

ref: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
text: Mu fun nyini o tuhu ni a na naŋgere naasi tima awa zimebisa awa a nawara la nyɛ hala.

ref: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
text: Mu fun nu ta ka o ŋɔ min, ‘Pita, si a kpu a se.’

ref: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
text: Amaa mu funɛ ŋɔ, ‘Mu kunyana, mu wa kiya chen. N ha wa di diwe naa ba ŋɔ di mu chi.’

ref: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
text: Ta ti fun bu sɛm min a li buudaha ni, ‘Ta chi diwe naa Koosa ŋɔ di o wa ya kichiili.’

ref: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
text: Ta ti funɛ sɛm un chene butora tiina, ka gar ti fun bu zini kaali Koosa tɔhɔn ti.

ref: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ min di mu si tu la ba wati a ta ɔmi di mu tiŋ ba. Yesu nitiŋɛ nara banɔra ba naa nyɛn Jɔpa ni, fun wa gbaari ya ni. Ya maga fun kaali Siizaria a la zu Kɔnilius dama.

ref: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
text: Waa funɛ ŋɔ ya di o na Koosa malaikasi ta digiu o dam ti ni. Malaika ti fun ŋɔ un, ‘Tum i walɛ ta bɔnɛ ba la yir baal naa ba yir Pita chen a li Jɔpa ni. O sɔn digi ya Simɔn.

ref: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
text: Waa la zi ŋɔ ma chenani i wa i walɛ zi ya, chen ni Koosa zi wa yisi mau a li ma tum tagna ni ma bur a tiŋ un.’

ref: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
text: Mu funɛ la tel dooni a sɛm ba chene, Koosa siim weri ti fun wa zu ba naa maga la nu chen, bambe ni a chi chenani o fun te zu ya.

ref: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
text: Doone mu fun lisi chenani ya kunyan ti fun biksi ya a ŋɔ, ‘Jɔn funɛ kpa nire a chaksi nara, amaa Koosa ka kaa kpa o siim weri tire a chaksi ma bambe.’

ref: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
text: Ya se funɛ su ya kunyana Yesu Kristo, Koosa fun kpa o siim weri ti a du ya ni. Lalaknɔwa o ya chen a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni. Min ya anti a la zi nyɛ Koosa gil chenani o daŋni o tum?”

ref: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun nu o sɛm ta, ba fun gil Pita zulimi a la pɛgi Koosa a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Lalaknɔwa ya zim di Koosa funɛ nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nyinbaanu di ba gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ un, chen ni ba zi wa na o mi fali tiu.” Nidamata Fun Bur Tiŋ Yesu Antiɔk Ni

ref: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
text: Ju tim ta funɛ kpu Stivin a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, na namsi, ba nidamata fun pisi a kaali bar bole. Ba bɔnɛ fun kaali Fonisia pulum awa tɔhɔn naa ba yir Antiɔk. Bɔnɛ ka zu arŋa a kaali pulum naa ba yir Saiprus ka ni go un. Ba maga fun kpa Koosa laaboor weri ti a biksi Ju tim ta duŋduŋ.

ref: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
text: Nidamata kinkan funɛ nyɛ ba se su ya kunyan ti ka ba bur a tiŋ un, domi Koosa kpok ti funɛ nyɛn ba biksi ti ni.

ref: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, funɛ nu chenani ya Antiɔk ni a tum Barnabas dooni.

ref: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
text: O funɛ la tel a na chenani Koosa nyɛ ba nyusum, o tuha fun sɔɔni kinkan. O fun kpami ba di ba kpak ya kunyana nɛnɛ awa ba tuha bɛgim ni.

ref: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
text: Barnabas funɛ ya nibin werire. O si funɛ su Yesu nɛnɛ ka Koosa siim weri ti fun te o suwachak. Nidamata fun nyɛ ba se su ya kunyan ti o daha.

ref: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
text: O wa fun vusi ka Barnabas li dooni a kaali Taasus di o la dɛu Sɔɔl.

ref: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
text: O funɛ na un, Barnabas fun kpa un a bur kaali Antiɔk. Ba fun nyɛn doon bun bɛgim aa biksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa i kinkan chen. Saha tirɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn doon chen fun te chɛrgi chenani ba yir Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a du ba Yesu Kristo nitiŋɛ ta.

ref: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
text: Saha naa Barnabas awa Sɔɔl fun nyɛn Antiɔk ni, Koosa witinsi ta bɔnɛ fun li Jɛrusalɛm a kaali dooni.

ref: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
text: Ba nidigi sɔn funɛ ya Agabus. Wuzu digi Koosa siim weri ti fun nyɛ o si nara tima suwachak ni a fun te ŋɔ di lɔsa tagin zi wa chelu tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Bunsi naa Klɔdius fun di Koosi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza chen bambaan nɛɛ, lɔsa tagin ti fun wa chel.

ref: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
text: a kpa lagmi dɔŋ ni a du Barnabas awa Sɔɔl nin ni di ba kpa la nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nihɛɛti ni dooni.

ref: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
text: Chen saha ti koor Hɛrɔd funɛ namsi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta bɔnɛ.

ref: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
text: O fun kpak Jems naa ya Jɔn maana, a ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta digi ni o kpa o chise zen a kpu un.

ref: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
text: Ba fun kpa Pita a la tɔ dɛ a lisi lanjimasa pukpuk bu naasi di ba il un. Ba wuzu zene ta funɛ wa gali, Hɛrɔd funɛ lisi di o zi kpa Pitau a li nara tima suwachak a di un sɛria.

ref: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
text: Ba funɛ tɔ un dɛ chene, Yesu nitiŋɛ ta funɛ sumi Koosa awa tu bɛgim ni a nyɛ un. Koosa Malaika Fun Yisi Pita Nara Bar Tɔɔli Ni

ref: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
text: Koosa malaikasi ta digi fun vaan wa chiŋi dɛ ti ni ka o vaan chaani kinkan. Malaika ti fun la ziigi Pita hampɛɛm o yɛrsi dooni o ŋɔ un, “Si lalaga.” Zɔglɛ ta fun vaan turgi o nisa ni.

ref: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
text: Pita fun si chiŋi. Malaika ti fun bu ŋɔ un, “Vɔu i takta we a mari i titi a laari i takta zen a sak a du i natausa a tiŋ min a wa.” Pita fun tum chenani malaika ti ŋɔ un,

ref: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
text: Ba fun val lanjimasi ta bambaan a wa li dɛ ti zɔɔnɔ ti ni a bu la li lanjimasi ta bɔnɛ bambaan ni a zi la tel bulzɔɔnɔ naa nyinbaan chɔɛɛ a la li tɔha. Ba funɛ kpa lugɛ a kɔɔri bulzɔɔnɔ ti. O fun puk o titi ka ba val dooni a tiŋ nyinbaan ti a kaali. Ba funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ti ni a la chene, malaika ti fun vaan nyuksi a ka Pita.

ref: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
text: Doone Pita fun zim chenani yɛɛ a ŋɔ o titi ni, “O ya wutirɛ ya kunyan Koosa fun tum o malaikasi ta digi di o wa yisi min a li Hɛrɔd nin ni. O gbanko funɛ yisi minu a li chenani Ju tim ta kaa namsi min niu.”

ref: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
text: O funɛ vaan zim chenani yɛɛ, o fun kaali Mɛri, Jɔn Maak niina, dama. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nidamata funɛ lagmi dooni a sumi Koosa aa nyɛ un.

ref: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
text: O fun nu ta ti a zim di Pitarɛ. O tu sɔɔn fun nyɛ o sugli bulzɔɔnɔ ti pugi a chɔ bur kaali o dɔŋtim ta wati di o la ŋɔ ba di Pita wau.

ref: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “I zu galɛŋwa. O wa kiya un.” O fun bukoo ŋɔ ba di o ya wutirɛ. Ba fun ŋɔ un, “O ya Koosa malaika naa dɛɛsɛ Pitarɛ.”

ref: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
text: Ba funɛ kpak sɛm ta chene, ka Pita ha fun vɛɛn a mo bulzɔɔnɔ ti a la yir. Ba fun la puk bulzɔɔnɔ ti a na un ka o chiŋi. Alaazibu fun kpak ba kinkan.

ref: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
text: O fun kpa o nin a ya ba ba gil chɛɛma a ŋɔ ba chenani ya kunyan ti yisi un a li dɛ naa ba tɔ nara chen ni. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma la ŋɔ Jems awa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta chenani yɛɛ.” Doone o fun gil ba a kaali bar digi.

ref: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, lanjimasa ba naa funɛ il Pita dɛ ti ni chen, tusa fun buri kinkan. Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Leene o zi kaali nan?”

ref: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
text: Koor Hɛrɔd funɛ nu Pita wɛ, o fun ŋɔ ba di ba la dɛu un, amaa ba wa fun koti a na un. Doone o fun yir ba naa la il Pita chen di ba ŋɔ un chenani yɛɛ a ŋɔ di ba kpa ba a la kpu. A gantɛl nɛɛ, Hɛrɔd fun li Ju tim ta pulum a kaali Siizaria a la nyɛn doon wuzu bilɛ. Chenani Koor Hɛrɔd Fun Sou

ref: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
text: Wuzu digi Hɛrɔd fun na paan kinkan a du Taaya tima awa Saidɔn tim ni. Ba funɛ nu o paan ti wɛ, ba fun lisi nara a li ba tɔsi ta tuhu ni ba la na un. Ba funɛ daŋni ba wa un ya nɔ bɛgim, domi o pulum ti la nyɛ ba diwe kinkan. Baal naa Hɛrɔd fun kpa o dama a du o nin ni sɔn funɛ ya Blastus. Taaya tima awa Saidɔn tim ta funɛ te nyɛ o ya ba chɛnnɛ ka ba zi chem Hɛrɔd.

ref: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
text: A wuzu naa Hɛrɔd fun lisi di o di ba sɛria, o fun laari o takta were a la kani o kor ni a sɛm sɛma a nyɛ nara ni.

ref: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
text: Chenani o fun sɛm ba chen nyɛ ba chɛɛmi a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “O wa bura sɛm a chi nibine. O chi Koosarɛ.”

ref: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
text: Koosa malaikasi ta digi fun vaan nyɛ wichɔɔrta kpak Hɛrɔd dau, domi o funɛ la nɔɔniu chenani ba yir un chen a kpa o titi a maksi Koosa. O wa fun vusi ka katamsa se o tuha nawura o sou.

ref: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Barnabas awa Sɔɔl funɛ kpa Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Antiɔk ni, malɛ a du Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, nihɛɛti ta nin ni. Doone Barnabas awa Sɔɔl fun kpa Jɔn Maak a gbaari ba ni a kaali Antiɔk.

ref: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Antiɔk ni, bɔnɛ yaa mɔɔli Koosa laaboor weri ti. Bɔnɛ ka yaa biksi ba o mun. Ba funɛ yir ba sɔna, Barnabas, Simiɔn naa funɛ ya nibin bilim, Lusius naa funɛ li Sairini pulum, Manayɛn naa wa koor Hɛrɔd funɛ lagmi a bi, awa Sɔɔl.

ref: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
text: Wuzu digi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta funɛ jɛɛmi ya kunyan ti a la vɔu nɔ, Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ ba, “Min funɛ lisi Barnabas awa Sɔɔl di ba tum tum digi a nyɛ min. Ma waligi ba a nyɛ min.”

ref: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun sumi Koosa chen daha a kpa ba nisa a saksi ba ni a nyɛ ba kaali. Barnabas Awa Sɔɔl Fun Kaali Saiprus Pulum

ref: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ Barnabas awa Sɔɔl ni di ba la tɔhɔn naa ba yir Selusia a la zu arŋa a la Saiprus pulum naa ni go chen.

ref: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
text: Doone ba fun gba hali a bu la li ni ti nɔ digi a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Pafɔs. Dooni ba fun pu tu bilim tiina naa ba yir Bayesu. O funɛ ya Ju tim baallɛ a ŋɔ di o ya Koosa witinsi ta digirɛ.

ref: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
text: Baal naa ba funɛ yir Sɛjius Pɔlɔs funɛ ya Saiprus pulum ti gɔbnati nyuhu tiina. O funɛ ya nibin nɛɛgirɛ ka o wa Bayesu funɛ ya chɛnsa. Gɔbnati nyuhu tiin ti funɛ nu di Barnabas awa Sɔɔl wau, o fun yir ba ba wa biksi un Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
text: Sɔɔl sɔn digi funɛ ya Pɔɔl. O funɛ na chenani Ɛlimas ya, Koosa siim weri ti fun si un ni o nyini o se ni,

ref: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
text: a ŋɔ, “I waa naa chi sintana, yaa mur narawa a duŋni tum were aa ta. Saha maga i yaa chɛrgi ya kunyan ti sɛm wutɛu di a burgi nusuna.

ref: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
text: Nan daha, Koosa zi lɛrgi i diŋnau lalaknɔ. Nyulumsa zi kpak hinu hali wuzu naa Koosa fun lisi di o wa lɛli i se ta chen wa tel.” Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm chene, birsa fun vaan wa tɔ Ɛlimas se o daŋni nandigi o kpak o nin a te o suwachak.

ref: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
text: Gɔbnati nyuhu tiin ti funɛ na chenani yɛɛ, o se fun su Koosa laaboor weri ti. Alaazibu fun kpak un kinkan a li chenani Barnabas awa Sɔɔl fun biksi un ya kunyan ti wɛ. Barnabas Awa Pɔɔl Fun La Tel Antiɔk A Li Paisidia Ni

ref: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
text: Pɔɔl awa o dɔŋtima fun la zu arŋa a li Pafɔs ni a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Pɛɛga a li Pamfilia pulum ni. Doonee Jɔn Maak fun ta ba a bur kaali Jɛrusalɛm.

ref: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas fun la gali Pɛɛga ni a kaali Antiɔk a li Paisidia pulum ni. Ju tim ta hiisi wuzu funɛ wa tel, ba fun la zu ba bar lagmi dɛ a kani.

ref: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
text: Nandigi funɛ karimi a li chiksɛ naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ Mosis ni awa tɔni naa Koosa witinsi ta fun sɔbi ni. Doone bar lagmi dɛ ti nihɛɛta fun tum nandigi di o la ŋɔ ba, “Ya niinbisa, ma zim chenani ma zi sɛm a kpami nara tima, ya nyɛ ma nyinbaanu di ma ya chen.”

ref: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
text: Doone Pɔɔl fun si chiŋi a kpa o nin a viigi ba suwachak ni di ba moori a ŋɔ ba, “Mu niinbisa Ju tima awa ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima a la jɛɛmi Koosa, maa nu mu wati nɛnɛ.

ref: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
text: Koosa naa ya Ju tim ta jɛɛmi, funɛ lisi ya nawaalsi ta a nyɛ o titi ni a nyɛ ba ya hɔɔra a li Ijipt pulum ni. Ba funɛ nyɛn doon chene, ba fun i a gbaari. Doone o fun kpa o kpok zen ti a lisi ba li dooni,

ref: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
text: Ma doso chenani Koosa witinsi ta fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni wa zi wa ya ma.

ref: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
text: O fun suŋi ba ba chɔk buuri bunɔpɛ a li pulum naa ba funɛ yir Kenan ni a nyɛ ba dɔ un.

ref: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
text: Doone Koosa fun lisi un a li o koor ti ni a nyɛ Devid chiŋi o bar chiŋi ti ni. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di Koosa funɛ ŋɔ o wɛu, ‘Mu zimu di Devid, Jɛsɛ be, ya naa mu bambi daŋni, domi o zi tum chenani maga mu daŋni o tumu.’

ref: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
text: Koosa funɛ vɔu saha digi di o zi wa tum Devid bi lulɛ ta digiu o lisi Ju tim ta tum tagna a li ba ni. Yesu funɛ ya naa Koosa vɔu di o tum chen.

ref: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
text: Jɔn fun te biksi ba di o lawa ba gil ba tum tagnau a bur tiŋ Koosa a nyɛ o chaksi ba wa ni ni, ka Yesu zi wa.

ref: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
text: Jɔn tuma funɛ wa dugli peti, o fun ŋɔ nara tima ni, ‘Ma nu mu wati nɛnɛ. Mu laata ya naa maa lisi di mu yau. Nandigi tiŋ mu gantɛl a wa. Mu purɛu wa mu ur o natausa.’

ref: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
text: “Mu niinbisa, ma naa ya Abraham bi lulɛ, awa ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima a la jɛɛmi Koosa. Laaboor weri naa la biksi chenani Koosa daŋni o lisi nara a li ba tum tagna ni ba tiŋ un ya ya kinnɛ.

ref: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
text: Ba funɛ ya chenani maga Koosa witinsi ta fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a du un ni peti, Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun yisi un a tu daakakli ti ni a kpa un a zu sogli gbɛntɛl bɔ ni.

ref: But God raised him from death!
text: Doone Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni,

ref: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
text: ka ba naa funɛ piin te tiŋ un a li Galili pulum ni a kaali Jɛrusalɛm, ba fun bu na un wa ba se ni bu damata. Laaboor weri nɔ ya ba tuma lalaknɔ ba kpa un a biksi Ju tim ta.

ref: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
text: Chendaha ya wa bar nɔ zinan. Chenani Koosa fun vɔu a nyɛ ya nawaalsi ta ni chen,

ref: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
text: ya chenani o fun lɛli Yesu a li o sou ni a nyɛ ya naa ya ba bi lulɛ ni chen. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti bar digi naa ba yir Saams chen ni di Koosa ŋɔ, ‘Zinan mu fa biksi narau gbampulim ni di mu ya i nyana.’

ref: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
text: Koosa gbanko biksi yau a li tɔn ti tuha ni chenani o zi wa lɛli Yesu a li o sou ni a ta nyɛ o pɔɔli. O fun ŋɔ, ‘Mu zi sir kpak ma sikiu a chi chenani mu kpak koor Devid siki chen.’

ref: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
text: Naa funɛ sɔbi tɔn ti gbanko funɛ sɛm Koosa wɛwa, ‘I wa zi nyɛ i tumtum naa i fun lisi, bara pɔ.’

ref: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
text: Devid funɛ kpak miisa, o funɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔu hali a wa sou. Ba fun kpa un a sogli a dugli o nawaalsi ta hugilsa ka o fun pɔ,

ref: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
text: amaa naa Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni chen, o bara wa fun kpalimi hugil ti ni a pɔ.

ref: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni ma zimu nɛnɛ di baal nɔ sɔnnɛ ya kpa a biksi ma di Koosa daŋni o gil ma tum tagnau a nyɛ ma.

ref: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ, ‘Ma naa la pini Koosa sɛma zoma chen, ma nu mu wati. Tum nawara mu tum ma se ni nan, hali nandigi wa ŋɔ ma a mun, ma se wa zi su a. Chendaha Koosa zi wa na ma galimsau a ta ma yɔri, alaazibu zi kpak mau chen daha.’ ”

ref: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm peti, nara ta maga fun si a li. Ba bɔnɛ fun wa ŋɔ Pɔɔl awa Barnabas ni di ba bur wa biksi ba a gbaari a li ba hiisi wuzu kinkin ti ni.

ref: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
text: Ba fun si a la damsa, ba naa ya Ju tim ta awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima a la jɛɛmi Koosa, ba nidamata fun tiŋ nara balɛ ta. Ba funɛ la chene, Pɔɔl awa Barnabas fun sɛm ba a kpami ba di ba kpak chenani Koosa suŋi ba chen nɛnɛ.

ref: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
text: Ba hiisi wuzu ti funɛ wa tel, o funɛ duuti ba tɔhɔn ti maga lagmi dooni di ba nu ya kunyan ti laaboor weri ti.

ref: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
text: Ju tim ta bɔnɛ funɛ na zɛmɛ ta, ba funɛ di handɔta kinkan. Ba funɛ pini Pɔɔl zoma a ŋɔ o sɛm ta wa ya wuti.

ref: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
text: Chenani Pɔɔl fun sɛm nyɛ ba sɔɔni ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima tusa kinkan ka ba pɛgi ya kunyan ti o laaboor weri ti daha. Doone ba naa Koosa fun lisi di o kpa o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ ba, ba se fun su o laaboor weri ti.

ref: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
text: Koosa laaboor weri ti fun mɔɔli tɔsi naa maga go dooni.

ref: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
text: Amaa Ju tim ta funɛ nyɛ tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛta awa nihaana ba naa la jɛɛmi Koosa a ya malɛ tima, paan fun siu a du Pɔɔl awa Barnabas ni. Ba fun muksi ba kinkan a duŋni ba a li dooni.

ref: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a la chene, ba fun pisi ba naŋgere hɛɛra a ta di Ju tim ta zim di o wa li ba ni, a kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Ikonium.

ref: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa se su Koosa laaboor weri ti a li Antiɔk ni, ba tusa funɛ sɔɔniu kinkan ka Koosa siim weri ti te ba suwachak.

ref: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas fun la tel Ikonium a la zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ a sɛm ba a chi chenani ba fun sɛm a li Antiɔk ni. Ju tim ta awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nidamata fun nyɛ ba se su Koosa laaboor weri ti ba sɛm ta daha.

ref: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
text: Ju tima ba naa se funɛ vɛ suuli funɛ nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ta, bɔnɛ paan fun si a du nara balɛ ta ni.

ref: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas fun nyɛn dooni a vusi a kpak bambe kpok di ba la biksi nara ya kunyan ti wɛ. Waa funɛ nyɛ ba o kpok ti di ba tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma a nyɛ un, chen ni nara zi zimu di chenani ba biksi ba di Koosa daŋni o suŋi bau funɛ ya wuti.

ref: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
text: Tɔhɔn ti tima ba naa funɛ lagmi dooni, a purigi a li chenani ba lisi ni. Bɔnɛ fun nyɛn Ju tim ta wati. Bɔnɛ ka fun nyɛn Pɔɔl awa Barnabas wati.

ref: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
text: Doone Ju tim ta, ba nihɛɛta awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ka ba se vɛ suuli, fun si a la chem ba wa yo ni a pini buŋkula a yaksi ba.

ref: But when the two apostles found out what was happening, they escaped to the region of Lycaonia. They preached the good news there in the towns of Lystra and Derbe and in the nearby countryside. Paul and Barnabas in Lystra
text: Doonee ba fun biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ba nara ni. Barnabas Awa Pɔɔl Fun Nyɛn Listira Awa Dɛɛbɛ

ref: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas funɛ la tel Listira, ba fun la pu baal naa ba lul wa gbarigisa ka o kani dooni. O ha wa piin maksi vali.

ref: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
text: O fun nu chenani Pɔɔl biksi tɔhɔn tima Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ. Doone Pɔɔl fun wa zaami un a zim di o si su di o zi kotiu a tiibi un. O fun nyini o se ni,

ref: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
text: a sɛm wa ta zaŋa ni, “Si a chiŋi.” Gbarigi ti fun vaan si za a chiksi a val a go.

ref: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ na chenani Pɔɔl fun tiibi un, ba funɛ tɛsi a la ŋɔ dɔŋ ni a li ba titi sɛm ni, “A naa ya yaa jɛɛmi chen funɛ burgiu a chi nibinsa a li buudaha ni a tu wa.”

ref: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
text: Ba fun du Barnabas sɔn digi Zuus. Pɔɔl ka funɛ di sɔn digi Hɛmis, domi waa yaa funɛ sɛm sɛm ta.

ref: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
text: Zuus bar jɛɛmi dama funɛ dugli tɔhɔn tirɛ. O dam kɔkɔɔrsa funɛ nu chenani nara tima sɛm, o fun la kpaksi nuwala awa bugilfita a wa tɔhɔn ti bulzɔɔnɔ di o wa zɛmɛ ta kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Pɔɔl awa Barnabas ni.

ref: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
text: Ba funɛ nu chenani tɔhɔn ti tima daŋni ba ya nyɛ ba, ba fun chɛɛsi ba kilaarɛ di zɛmɛ ta zim di ba wa la nɔɔni a chɔ zu ba bambaan a tɛsi a ŋɔ,

ref: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
text: “Ma ta kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ ya a kijɛɛmi ya. Ya ya nibinsiwa a chi ma. Ya wa bar nɔu di ya wa biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ. Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ funɛ naami buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ wa teku wa kin naa maga nyɛn a ni. Waa la daŋni ma gil kin nawara ma jɛɛmi a ya yɔri a bur tiŋ un.

ref: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
text: Hali wa zinan waa nyɛ nara maga jɛɛmi a li chenani baa daŋni ni.

ref: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
text: Tum were naa o yaa tum a nyɛ ya chen biksi ya chenani o du. Waa nyɛ doon mi ya kiparɛ bi, chen ni ya zi na diwe a di wa tu sɔɔni.”

ref: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
text: Hali ba funɛ sɛm ba chen maga, o funɛ bɔmu a li Pɔɔl awa Barnabas wati di ba nyɛ zɛmɛ ta gil chenani ba daŋni ba tum amaa ba funɛ koti a gil.

ref: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
text: O wa fun vusi ka Ju tim ta bɔnɛ li Antiɔk a li Paisidia pulum awa Ikonium a la tel Listira. Ba fun nyaŋi zɛmɛ ta awa ba sɛm ni ka ba wa ba fun kpak Pɔɔl a yaksi un wa buŋkula ni. Ba fun baa di o sowu a lɛrgi un a li tɔhɔn ti ni a ta.

ref: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun go un wa kaali di ba na ba kisuŋi unu, amaa o funɛ kotiu a si chiŋi. O wa ba bur kaali tɔhɔn ti tuha. Chɔɔsa fun bu wa pisi o wa Barnabas fun li dooni a kaali Dɛɛbɛ. Barnabas Awa Pɔɔl Fun Bur Kaali Antiɔk A Li Siiriya Ni

ref: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
text: Ba fun la biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ nara ni dooni ka nidamata fun nyɛ ba se su un. Doone ba fun vɛɛn bur a chi chenani ba wa chen a la gali Listira awa Ikonium a la tel Antiɔk a li Paisidia pulum.

ref: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a la chene, ba fun nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta se suuli gbaari a kpami ba di ba kpak Yesu nɛnɛ. Ba fun ŋɔ ba, “O deŋlu wa ya te namsiu kinkan ka Koosa zi wa dɔ ya a peti.”

ref: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
text: Doone ba fun lisi nihɛɛta a li Yesu nitiŋɛ ta tuhu ni di ba nyini ba a li tɔsi ta ni. Pɔɔl awa Barnabas funɛ vɔti ba nɔɔna a sumi Koosa peti, ba fun kpa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a du ya kunyan ti nin ni a kaali.

ref: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
text: Ba fun la gali Paisidia pulum ni a la zu Pamfilia pulum.

ref: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
text: Ba fun bu la tel Pɛɛga a biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti doon a li a kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Atalia,

ref: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
text: Ba fun la tel Antiɔk a yir Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga a lagmi dɔŋ ni a ŋɔ ba chenani maga Koosa suŋi ba ba tum awa chenani Koosa nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nyinbaan di ba se su Koosa laaboor weri ti.

ref: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
text: Ba fun nyɛn Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wati dooni a vusi.

ref: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas wa ba fun di amɔrɛ kpok a li ba biksi nɔ ni. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun lisi Pɔɔl, Barnabas awa ba bɔnɛ a li ba tuhu ni ba la Jɛrusalɛm ba wa nitumɛ ta awa nihɛɛti ta na dɔŋa chenani ba zi ya.

ref: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
text: Ba funɛ la tel Jɛrusalɛm, nitumɛ ta, nihɛɛti ta awa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga fun wa zaami ba. Doone ba fun ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni, chenani maga Koosa yaa funɛ suŋi ba ba tum o tuma.

ref: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
text: Saha ti Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa ya Farisi tim ta bɔnɛ fun si chiŋi ba suwachak ni a ŋɔ, “O lawa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta tiŋ Ju tim chiksɛ tau a nyɛ ba teŋsi ba.”

ref: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
text: Nitumɛ ta awa nihɛɛti ta fun li ba duŋduŋ a na dɔŋa chenani ba zi ya.

ref: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
text: Ba funɛ sɛm chene a vusi, Pita fun si chiŋi ba bambaan ni a ŋɔ ba, “Mu niinbisa, ma zimu di Koosa funɛ lisi min a li ma ni saha digi di mu la biksi o laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni, chen ni ba zi nu unu a nyɛ ba se su un.

ref: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
text: Koosa naa zim chenani nibinsa bambe du, fun kpa o siim weri ti a du ba ni a chi chenani o fun te kpa du ya ni chen. Nan funɛ biksi ya di ba naa funɛ vɛ ya Ju tima zi kotiu a wa zim un a chi ya.

ref: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
text: Koosa funɛ gil ba tum tagna a nyɛ ba domi ba se su o laaboor weri ti daha. Nan biksi ya di ya naa ya Ju tima awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
text: Chenani ba fun teŋsi ya nawaalsi ta awa ya titi awa chenani ba wa ya fun kpaŋi di ya tiŋ Ju tim ta chiksɛ ta, chen wa fun suŋi ya. Chendaha Koosa vɛ daŋni ya ya kpa kin naa vɛ ya kibirigi a gbaari Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima.

ref: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
text: Chenani ya kunyana Yesu fun suŋi ya ya se su un, ka Koosa fun yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni ya tiŋ un awa chenani o tum nyɛ ba ya kibɛgimnɛ. Kin nama ya kibirige.”

ref: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
text: Pita funɛ sɛm peti, ba maga fun moori di ba nu Barnabas awa Pɔɔl wati. Ba fun ŋɔ ba chenani Koosa fun suŋi ba ba tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni.

ref: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
text: Ba sɛm ta gantɛl nɛɛ, Jems fun ŋɔ ba maga ni, “Mu niinbisa, ma nu mu wati.

ref: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
text: Simɔn Pita fa ŋɔ ya di Koosa funɛ lisi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim chen nidamata a nyɛ o titi ni. Nan biksɛɛ di Koosa daŋni bau.

ref: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
text: ‘Koor Devid walɛ chi damnɛ mu fun sa ka o chel. Wuzu digi mu zi wa bur wa sa unu,

ref: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
text: chen ni ba naa maga vɛ ya Ju tima mu fun lisi a nyɛ mu titi ni zi dɛu mu bar nyɛniu.

ref: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
text: Jems fun bukoo ŋɔ ba, “Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nidamata bur aa tiŋ Koosa. O ya mu tutɔrɛ ya vɛ kpa kin nama a gbaari ba.

ref: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ma nyɛ ya sɔbi tɔn a ŋɔ ba di o wa lawa ba di vursi diwe domi a ya doŋwa a vɛɛn ŋɔ ba di ba ta tum chɔkumsi tuma a ta se nɛmɛ naa ba vɛ kɔrigi o li chɛl.

ref: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
text: Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima funɛ piin zimu di chenani mu sɛm chen ya wu birigirɛ, domi Ju tim ta yaa funɛ biksi chenani Mosis fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni saha digi a li ba bar lagmi dɛɛni ni tɔsi maga ni a vusi.” Nitumɛ Fun Sɔbi Tɔn A Nyɛ Ba Naa Vɛ Ya Ju Tim Ni

ref: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
text: Jems funɛ sɛm peti, nitumɛ ta, nihɛɛti ta awa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga fun lisi nara balɛ a li ba tuhu ni di ba la lagmi Pɔɔl awa Barnabas ni ba la Antiɔk. Ba sɔna funɛ ya Judas Barsabas awa Sailas. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga fun nyɛ ba jirima kinkan.

ref: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
text: Nannɛ ba fun sɔbi a li tɔn nɔ ni. “Nitumɛ ta awa Yesu nitiŋɛ nihɛɛta, ba naa ya ma niinbisa, sɔbi tɔn nɔ a zaami Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima a nyɛn Antiɔk, Siiriya pulum awa Salisia pulum ni.

ref: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
text: Ya funɛ nu di ba naa li ya tuhu ni bɔnɛ funɛ wa buti ma awa ba biksi ni. Ya wa fun nyɛ ba nyinbaan di ba ya chen.

ref: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
text: Zinannɛ ya maga lagmi di ya na dɔŋa a zim chenani ya zi ya a lisi nara balɛ di ba lagmi Pɔɔl awa Barnabas ni ba la ma wati. Ya daŋni Pɔɔl awa Barnabasrɛ kinkan,

ref: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: domi baa tum ya kunyan Yesu Kristo tumau a li bar tagni ni.

ref: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
text: Nara balɛ ta ya lisi a gbaari ba ni chen sɔna ya Judas awa Sailas. Chenani ba zi sɛm ma awa chenani ya sɔbi a li tɔn ti ni ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
text: Koosa siim weri ti te ya suwachak di ya ta bu kpa chiksɛ a gbaari ma, amaa ma doso aa tiŋ ya sɛm na.

ref: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
text: Ma ta di vursi diwe a ta se nɛmɛ naa ba vɛ kɔrigi o li chɛl a ta tum chɔkumsi tuma. Ma gil nan kin nama yaali, ma zi tiŋ Yesuu nɛnɛ. Ma zaami dɔŋa a nyɛ ya.”

ref: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
text: Nitumɛ ta awa nihɛɛti ta fun kpa tɔn ti a la nyɛ nara banaasi ta ni ba kpa kaali Antiɔk. Ba fun la tel doon a lagmi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga dɔŋ ni a kpa tɔn ti a nyɛ ba.

ref: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
text: Chenani ba fun karimi tɔn ti ni, chen fun kpami ba a sɔɔni ba tusa kinkan.

ref: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
text: Judas awa Sailas funɛ ya Koosa witinsi ta nara balɛwa. Ba gbanko fun kpami Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni, di ba kpak Yesu nɛnɛ a vusi.

ref: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
text: Doone Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun tiŋ ba a li ta nyinbaan ni a bur kaali Jɛrusalɛm. ((

ref: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas fun kpalimi Antiɔk ni. Ba wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ. Pɔɔl awa Barnabas Fun Waligi Dɔŋ Ni

ref: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
text: Wuzu digi Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ Barnabas ni, “Ya funɛ biksi narau a li tɔ damati ni. Ma nyɛ ya bur kila a la na chenani Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn dooni, se su.”

ref: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
text: Barnabas funɛ daŋni o yir Jɔn Maakrɛ a gbaari ba ni,

ref: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
text: amaa Pɔɔl wa fun la nɔɔni, domi Maak funɛ waligi o titiu a li ba ni a li Pamfilia pulum ni a vɛ peti tum ta.

ref: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
text: Pɔɔl awa Barnabas funɛ di amɔrɛ kpok o daha a waligi dɔŋ ni. Barnabas fun kpa Maak a la zu arŋa ba kaali Saiprus pulum.

ref: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun kpa Pɔɔl awa Sailas naa o fun lisi, a du Koosa nin ni di o suŋi ba. Ba fun li dooni,

ref: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
text: Pɔɔl awa Sailas fun bur kaali Dɛɛbɛ awa Listira. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta digi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Timoti. O niina gbanko funɛ ya Yesu nitiŋirɛ. O niin ti funɛ ya Ju timwa ka o nyana ka ya Girik tima.

ref: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
text: Ya niinbisa ba naa maga nyɛn Listira awa Ikonium ni, funɛ sɛm di Timoti ya narrɛ.

ref: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
text: Ju tima ba naa maga funɛ nyɛn dooni, fun zim di ba wa teŋ Timoti, domi o nyan ti wa fun ya Ju tima. Chendaha Pɔɔl fun te nyɛ ba teŋ un ka o kpa un gbaari ba ni.

ref: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
text: Ba funɛ li tɔsi ni, ba yaa fun ŋɔ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta ni chenani nitumɛ ta awa nihɛɛta ba naa funɛ nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, fun sɔbi a li ba tɔn ti ni. Ba gbanko fun ŋɔ ba di o lawa ba tiŋ chenani ba sɛmu.

ref: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
text: Saha tirɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun kpak Yesu nɛnɛ a gbaari ka nidamata nyɛ ba se su un a gbaari ba ni wuzu kama. Pɔɔl Fun Kaali Troas

ref: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
text: Ba fun wa tel doonani ba yir Misia a daŋni ba zu pulum naa ba yir Baitinia. Ya kunyan Yesu siim weri ti ha wa fun vɛɛn la nɔɔni di ba zu.

ref: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
text: Ba fun la gali Misia a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Troas naa nyɛn teku nɔ ni.

ref: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
text: A wuzu ti dusum Pɔɔl funɛ na baal digirɛ a chi o deesi ka o li pulum naa ba yir Masɛdonia. O fun chiŋi Pɔɔl suwachak ni a sumi un, “Ji a wa suŋi ya.”

ref: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
text: Baal ti funɛ kaali, ya wa Pɔɔl fun saksi di ya la Masɛdonia, domi Koosa funɛ biksi ya di ya ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni o laaboor weri ti wɛ. Lidia Si Funɛ Su Yesu Filipai Ni

ref: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
text: Ya fun kpa teku ti a ta ya gantɛl ni a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Filipai. O funɛ ya tɔ birigirɛ a li Masɛdonia pulum ti ni. Roman tima funɛ sa un. Ya fun nyɛn doon wuzu damata.

ref: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
text: Ju tim ta wuzu hiisi funɛ wa tel, ya fun li tɔhɔn ti ni a la val pul nɔ ni, a dɛu doonani Ju tim ta yaa lagmi a sumi Koosa. Ya funɛ na un, ya fun la lagmi nihaani ni a kani dooni a sɛm.

ref: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
text: Nihaani tima digi sɔn funɛ ya Lidia ka o nu ya sɛm ta. O yaa funɛ yogli gar naa ya ti bɛgim a laksi kibilima awa kisɛma ka a malɛ kpok kinkan. O funɛ li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Tiataya a li Azia pulum ni. O yaa funɛ jɛɛmi Koosau ka ya kunyan ti puk o bambi di o nu Pɔɔl sɛm ta.

ref: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
text: Ya funɛ zim di o nu au, ya fun chaksi o wa o walɛ wa ni ni. Doone o fun ŋɔ ya, “Ma zimu di mu si su ya kunyan ti wuti, adaha wa nyɛn mu wati wuzu bilɛ.” O fun nyɛ ya bambe chɛrgi a la o wati. Ba Fun Tɔ Pɔɔl Awa Sailas Dɛ Filipai Ni

ref: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
text: Wuzu digi ya funɛ la doonani Ju tim ta yaa sumi Koosa a la chem nihabichɛl naa ya yoma. Alizin tagin funɛ nyɛn o nyuhu ni a nyɛ o yaa ŋɔ nara ni chenani kaa wa ya. O tima fun na nyɔɔra kinkan a li chenani o ya a nyɛ nara ni chen.

ref: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
text: O fun tiŋ ya wa Pɔɔl gantɛl a tɛsi a la ŋɔ, “Nara nama ya Koosa zen tumtumsiwa. Ba waa funɛ mɔɔli ma a li chenani Koosa zi wa yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni ma tiŋ un.”

ref: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
text: Nihabichɛl ti fun tiŋ ya a gba wuzu bilɛ. O fun wa tel o bɔ, Pɔɔl fun burgi a ŋɔ alizin tagin ti ni, “N kpa Yesu Kristo sɔnnɛ a ŋɔ hin di i li un ni.” Alizin tagin ti fun vaan li un ni.

ref: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
text: Baa naa dɔ nihabichɛl ti chen funɛ na chenani Pɔɔl ya, ba fun zim di ba wa zi bu na nyɔɔra a li un ni. Ba fun kpak Pɔɔl awa Sailas a zaksi ba a la yawa doonani ba di sɛriasa chen.

ref: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
text: Ya waa ya Roman tima. Ya chiksɛ ka wa nyɛ ya nyinbaan di ya tiŋ ba biksi.”

ref: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
text: Ba funɛ sɛm chene, zɛmɛ fun go ba a wa kaali a tuksi Pɔɔl awa Sailas. Doone sɛria nidiile ta fun ŋɔ lanjimasa ba naa nyɛn dooni, di ba ur ba kilaarɛ bɔnɛ a chɛɛsi a la tuk ba wa vurmsi ni.

ref: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
text: Lanjimasi ta fun tuk Pɔɔl awa Sailas kinkan, a kpa ba a la tɔ dɛ a ŋɔ niili ni di o nyini ba nɛnɛ.

ref: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
text: Niili ti funɛ nu ba sɛm ta, o fun kpa Pɔɔl awa Sailas a zu la far dɛ digi tuhu ni a furgi bɔɔsa a li dagumsi ni a kpa ba naŋgere a du a ni.

ref: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
text: O fun wa dugli tɔhɛɛsa, Pɔɔl awa Sailas funɛ sumi Koosa a lɔ boola a pɛgi un. Ba naa ba fun kpa gbaari ba ni dɛ ti ni fun kani a nu ba wati.

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
text: Tɛɛnɛ fun vaan zigsi kinkan. Hali dɛɛna gbanko zɛchigsa funɛ zigsi. Zɔɔnɔsa maga puk a titi ka kin nawara funɛ vɔti ba kasi.

ref: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
text: Niili ti fun yɛrsi a na di zɔɔnɔsi ta maga puku. O fun foŋ o chise zen a daŋni o kpu o titi, domi o funɛ lisi di ba naa maga o il chen liu.

ref: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
text: Doone Pɔɔl fun tɛsi kinkan a ŋɔ un, “Ta kpu i titi. Ya maga nyɛnu.”

ref: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
text: Niili ti fun ŋɔ di ba kpa fitin kuwa a chɔ a la gbiri Pɔɔl awa Sailas suwachak ni. Hansura fun kpak un kinkan o bara jigiri.

ref: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
text: O bara ta funɛ gil jigiri, o fun te ba suwachak a li a pɛɛsi ba, “Mu kunyana, yɛrɛ mu kaa ya ka Koosa yisi min a li mu tum tagna ni?”

ref: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Nyɛ i si su ya kunyana Yesu ka Koosa zi yisi i wa i walɛ a li ma tum tagna ni.”

ref: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
text: Doone Pɔɔl awa Sailas fun biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ un awa ba naa maga nyɛn o dam ni.

ref: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
text: Tɔhɛɛsa funɛ wa gali tama, niili ti fun kpa ni a wa chaksi ba bar huusɛ. O funɛ ya chen peti, ba fun kpa Yesu sɔn a chaksi o wa o walɛ ta wa ni ni.

ref: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
text: Doone niili ti fun kpa Pɔɔl awa Sailas a kaali o dam ti a la ya diwe ba di. O wa o walɛ ta tusa fun sɔɔni kinkan, domi ba se su Koosa.

ref: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, sɛria nidiile ta fun tum lanjimasi ta di ba la ŋɔ niili ti ni o lisi Pɔɔl awa Sailas a nyɛ ba la.

ref: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
text: Niili ti fun wa ŋɔ Pɔɔl ni, “Sɛria nidiile ta funɛ tum ba wa ŋɔ min di mu lisi i wa Sailas a nyɛ ma la. Maa la a ta chɛɛmi.”

ref: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
text: Pɔɔl fun la ŋɔ lanjimasi ta ni, “Ma sɛria nidiile ta wa dɛ na ya galimsa, amaa ba nyɛ ma tuksi ya wa vurmsi ni nara tima suwachak ni a kpa ya tɔ dɛ. Ya ya Ju narawa a vɛɛn ya Roman tima, adaha ba wa kpak nyinbaan wa ba nyɛ ma ya ya chen. Lalaknɔ ba daŋni ba sogli a lisi ya a ta. Ya wa la nɔɔni chenani ba daŋni ba ya chen. Ma bur la ŋɔ ba di ba titi kuwa a wa lisi ya.”

ref: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
text: Lanjimasi ta fun bur kaali sɛria nidiile ta wati a la ŋɔ ba chenani Pɔɔl sɛm ba. Sɛria nidiile ta funɛ nu di Pɔɔl awa Sailas ya Roman timwa, hansura fun kpak ba.

ref: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
text: Pɔɔl awa Sailas fun kaali Lidia dama di ba la na ba niinbisa ba naa lagmi dooni. Ba fun sɛm sɛma a kpami ba a li tɔhɔn ti ni.

ref: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
text: Ba fun la gali tɔsa naa ba yir Amifipolis awa Apolonia a la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Tɛsalɔnika. Ju tim ta funɛ kpak ba bar lagmi dɛrɛ dooni.

ref: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
text: O fun biksi ba di Yesu ya Kristo naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di o zi wa tumu wuzu digi chen a karimi Koosa sɛm tɔn ti bar digi doonani ba fun sɔbi di o zi wa namsiu a sou ka Koosa lɛli un a li o sou ni.

ref: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
text: Ju tim ta bɔnɛ se funɛ suu di chenani Pɔɔl sɛm chen ya wutirɛ a chiŋi o wa Sailas gantɛl ni. Girik tima ba naa funɛ jɛɛmi Koosa awa nihaana ba naa ya nibirige nidamata gbanko funɛ ya chene.

ref: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
text: Ju tima ba naa funɛ ka chen funɛ di handɔta a du Pɔɔl awa Sailas ni. Ba fun lagmi ba naa chiŋi a ya yɔri a li yau ni a nyɛ ba dam tɔhɔn ti. Ba fun lisi di Pɔɔl awa Sailas nyɛn baal naa ba yir Jesɔn dam nɛ a wa chɔk dam ti di ba lisi ba a li zɛmɛ ta suwachak.

ref: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
text: Ba funɛ vɛ na ba, ba fun kpak Jesɔn awa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta bɔnɛ a zaksi ba a la tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛta suwachak a tɛsi a ŋɔ, “Doonani nara nama la, ba yaa muksi narawa kinkan. Lalaknɔwa ba tel ya tɔha,

ref: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
text: a zu Jesɔn dama a nyɛn doon. Ba waa chɔk chiksɛ naa koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza fun kpa nyɛ ya. Hali ba yaa biksi di ba kunyana Yesu ya koorsi ta nyuhu tiina a chi un.”

ref: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
text: Tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛti ta awa zɛmɛ ta funɛ nu ba sɛma, o fun muksi ba kinkan.

ref: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
text: Nihɛɛti ta fun nyɛ Jesɔn awa Yesu nitiŋɛ, ba naa ba kpa wa ba wati, fun bɔ malɛ a nyɛ ba kaali. Pɔɔl Fun Kaali Bɛrɛa

ref: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
text: Dusum funɛ wa biri, Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun tum Pɔɔl awa Sailas a li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Bɛrɛa. Ba fun la tel doon a la zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ.

ref: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
text: Ba naa nyɛn dooni chen, bambe funɛ puku a kaali ba naa nyɛn Tɛsalɔnika ni, domi ba funɛ kpak un ba bambe ni di ba nu chenani Pɔɔl kaa sɛm. Wuzu kam ba yaa fun karimi Koosa sɛm tɔn tiu di ba na chenani Pɔɔl biksi ba chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
text: Ju tim ta awa nibaala wa nihaan birige, ba naa maga ya Girik tima nidamata, fun nyɛ ba se su Koosa laaboor weri ti.

ref: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
text: Ju tima ba naa funɛ nyɛn Tɛsalonika ni, funɛ nu di Pɔɔl biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ Bɛrɛa tim ta ni, ba bɔnɛ fun wa gbaari Bɛrɛa tim ta ni a lagmi zɛmɛ di ba dam tɔhɔn ti.

ref: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
text: Ya niinbisi ta fun vaan lisi ba bɔnɛ a li ba ni di ba wa Pɔɔl kila ni nɔ. Sailas awa Timoti fun kpalimi Bɛrɛa ni.

ref: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
text: Ba funɛ tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Atɛns, Pɔɔl fun tum ba naa tiŋ un chen, ba bur kaali Bɛrɛa di ba la ŋɔ Sailas awa Timoti ni di ba wa gbaari un ni lalaga. Pɔɔl Fun Nyɛn Atɛns

ref: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ pɔ di ba wa gbaari un ni, o fun gba a go tɔhɔn ti a na di vursu damata funɛ nyɛn dooni.

ref: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
text: O yaa fun la zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ a biksi ba awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima a la jɛɛmi Koosa, Yesu wɛ. O biksi ti fun nyɛ ba wa un di amɔrɛ. O gbanko yaa fun la yawa a ŋɔ ba naa chiŋɛɛ di ba nu o sɛm ta.

ref: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
text: Nibiksɛ ba naa ba yir Ɛpikurian awa Stɔik wa Pɔɔl gbanko funɛ di amɔrɛ. Ba bɔnɛ fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Nyu bilim tiin nɔ wa zim chenani o biksi chen mun.” Ba bɔnɛ ka fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “O chi o biksi vursa a naa hɔɔra nyɛ a jirima wɛwa.” Ba funɛ sɛm chene, domi Pɔɔl funɛ biksi ba Yesu wɛ awa chenani Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni.

ref: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
text: Tɔhɔn nihɛɛta yaa lagmi a li kuŋkule nyuhu naa ba yir Arɛɔpɔgus ni. Nibiksɛ ta fun kpa Pɔɔl a kaali ba suwachak a ŋɔ un, “Ya maga daŋni i ŋɔ ya i biksi fali ŋɔ wɛu.

ref: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
text: Ya ha wa nu un. Chendaha yaa daŋni ya zim o mun.”

ref: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
text: Atɛns tima awa hɔɔra ba naa nyɛn dooni, maga funɛ kpa ba saha magawa a sɛm sɛma awa biksi fali ti tiŋ gantɛl a wa chen wɛ.

ref: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
text: Pɔɔl fun chiŋi dooni a ŋɔ nihɛɛti ta awa nibiksɛ ta, “Ma Atɛns tima, mu zimu di ma daŋni vursau kinkan.

ref: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
text: Mu funɛ valu a go ma tɔhɔn ti a na ka ma nyɛ a jirima. Hali mu fun na digi ka nandigi sɔbi un ni, ‘Koosa naa ya vɛ zimi nɛɛnɛ.’ Koosa naa ma nyɛ jirima a vɛ zimi nan, o wɛwa mu kaa ŋɔ ma nan.

ref: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
text: Koosa funɛ naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa kin naa maga nyɛn un ni a dɔ o wa buudaha. Chendaha o wa koti a nyɛn dam naa nibinsa sa a nyɛ un ni.

ref: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
text: Waa kpa miisa a nyɛ kihiisɛ maga ni a la nyɛ nibinsa kin naa maga ba daŋni. O wara daŋni kundɔŋ a li nibinsa wati ba zi nyɛ un.

ref: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
text: Koosa funɛ te naami nibaal bɛgimnɛ. O tuhu nɛɛ nibinsa maga li a purigi a pisi a su tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. O fun te lisi ba bar nyɛni wɛ awa saha naa ba zi li dooni a zi naami ba.

ref: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
text: O funɛ ya chene, chen ni ba zi na nyinbaanu a dɛu un a na. O wa ya wa bol dɔŋ ni.

ref: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
text: Nandigi funɛ sɔbi chen wɛ saha digi a ŋɔ, ‘Koosa kpa o kpok ti a du ya ni, chen ni ya zi koti nyɛnu.’ Ma walɛ ta digi funɛ sɔbi a ŋɔ di, ‘Ya ya o bisiwa.’

ref: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
text: Chenani o fun sɛm chen ya wuti, ya wa zi lisi di nara zi kpa salma akoo aniita koo buŋkul a naami vura wa nɛha ni a ŋɔ di waa chen.

ref: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
text: Hali saha nɔ Koosa wa fun lɛrgi ba naa vɛ zim o wɛ diŋsa, amaa o fun ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn bawu maga ni lalaknɔ di ba gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ un.

ref: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
text: O fun lisi a wuzu naa o nyɛ naa o fun lisi chen wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria. Koosa fun lɛli Yesu a li o sou ni di o biksi ya di waa ya naa la zi tum chen.”

ref: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
text: Nihɛɛti ta awa nibiksɛ ta bɔnɛ nu ka Pɔɔl sɛm di Koosa funɛ lɛli Yesu a li o sou ni, ba funɛ mɔmsi un. Bɔnɛ ka fun wa ŋɔ un, “Ya daŋni i bur wa biksi yau a gbaari.”

ref: When Paul left the council meeting,
text: Doone Pɔɔl fun gil ba.

ref: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni chen bɔnɛ nyɛ ba se su Koosa a chiŋi Pɔɔl gantɛl ni. Ba bɔnɛ sɔna funɛ ya Diɔnisius naa ya tɔhɔn nihɛɛti ta digi, awa Damaris naa ya nihaan.

ref: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
text: O fun la tel dooni a pu Ju tim baal naa ba yir Akwila. Ba funɛ lul un Pɔntus pulum nɛɛ ka o wa o haan naa ba yir Prisila, fun nyɛn Room ni a li Itali pulum ni. Koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza Klɔdius fun ŋɔ Ju tim ta maga ni di ba li Room ni. Chendaha Akwila awa o haan ti fun nyɛn Kɔrint ni.

ref: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
text: Pɔɔl fun la nyɛn ba dam ni, domi o wa Akwila funɛ ya sela tɔna nikɔɔrɛwa.

ref: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
text: Pɔɔl yaa funɛ zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛu ba wuzu hiise wuzu o wa ba awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima di amɔrɛ di o nyɛ ba se su o sɛma.

ref: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
text: Ju tim ta wa fun la nɔɔni a nu chenani o biksi ba chen a sɛm sɛm tagna a du un ni. Pɔɔl fun burgi o gantɛl a chak ba a ŋɔ ba, “Koosa na ma galimsa a ta ma yɔri a li ma nɛɛ. O wa li min ni. Saha nɔ kpa la n wa zi bu biksi ma. Mu ka zi la biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim tau.”

ref: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
text: Pɔɔl fun gil ba a la nyɛn baal naa ba yir Titius Jastus dam ni. O funɛ ya ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ta nidigirɛ a la jɛɛmi Koosa. O dam ti funɛ sak Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ ti nɛɛ.

ref: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
text: Ba bar lagmi dɛ ti nihɛɛta digi sɔn funɛ ya Krispus. O wa o walɛ fun nyɛ ba se su ya kunyan ti. Ba naa funɛ nyɛn Kɔrint ni, nidamata fun nu Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ba se su un. Doone Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun chaksi ba wa ni ni.

ref: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
text: Dusum digi Pɔɔl fun na ya kunyan ti a chi o deesi ka o ŋɔ un, “Ta ɔmi. Ta bur gantɛl a li laaboor weri ti biksi ti ni,

ref: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
text: domi mu nyɛn i wati. Min dɔ ba naa nyɛn tɔhɔn nɔ nidamata. Chendaha nandigi wa zi koti a tum hin tum tagin.”

ref: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
text: Ya kunyan ti funɛ kaali, Pɔɔl fun si a nyɛn doon a biksi nara bun birigi awa pɛnsa nɔra.

ref: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ Galio ni, “Baal nɔ yaa biksi ya nara di ba jɛɛmi Koosa aa chɔk ya chi.”

ref: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ daŋni o sɛmu amaa Galio wa fun nyɛ o na nɔ a sɛm a ŋɔ Ju tim ta ni, “Baal nɔ fu tum tum naa vɛ deŋli, mu fu zi sɔɔni mu barau a nu chenani ma zi sɛm.

ref: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
text: Ma la sɛm di o biksi ti chɔk ma chire. Ma waa la zi lisi chenani ma zi ya. Mu wa zi di chen sɛria.”

ref: Then he sent them out of the court.
text: O funɛ sɛm peti chen, o fun nyɛ o lanjimasa duŋni ba a ta.

ref: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
text: A gantɛl nɛɛ Pɔɔl fun nyɛn Kɔrint ni a vusi. Doone Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun tiŋ o wa Akwila awa Prisila a la ta nyinbaan ni ba la tɔhɔn naa ba yir Kɛnkrɛa. Doonee Pɔɔl fun vɔu vɔwi a nyɛ Koosa ni di o zi tiŋ unu a gbaari a nyɛ ba funi o nyuha di nara zim. Ba fun zu arŋ naa le la Siiriya pulum.

ref: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
text: Ba fun la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Ɛfɛsus ka Pɔɔl fun la zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ di o wa ba di o biksi ti amɔrɛ.

ref: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
text: O fun la tel Siizaria a li arŋ ti ni a la zaami Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni a gali kaali Antiɔk a li Siiriya pulum ni.

ref: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
text: O funɛ ya tama o fun li dooni a gba a go Frijia awa Galesia pulum a kpami Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn dooni di ba kpak Yesu nɛnɛ. Apɔlɔs Fun Kaali Ɛfɛsus Awa Kɔrint

ref: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
text: Ju tim ta nidigi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Apɔlɔs. Ba funɛ lul un a li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Alɛzandria a li Ijipt pulum ni. O funɛ zim Koosa sɛm tɔn ti munu kinkan a koti a kpa un a biksi nara nɛnɛ. Wuzu digi o fun wa Ɛfɛsus.

ref: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
text: O funɛ zim chenani nara tiŋ ya kunyan ti, amaa ba wa fun kpa Yesu sɔn a chaksi un. Ba ka funɛ kpa nire a chaksi un a chi chenani Jɔn fun chaksi nara wa ni ni chen. O funɛ biksi Ɛfɛsus tima wuti awa si sɛm nɛɛ Yesu wɛ.

ref: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
text: O fun la zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ a kpak o bambi a sɛm sɛma a nyɛ ba. Akwila awa Prisila funɛ nu o sɛm ta, ba fun kpa un kaali dama a la biksi un chenani nara zi tiŋ ya kunyan ti a kaali chenani o zim.

ref: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: O fun wa ya wuzu bilɛ Apɔlɔs fun lisi di o zi la Girik tim pulumu. Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni, daŋni ba suŋi un a sɔbi tɔn a nyɛ ba niinbisa ba naa nyɛn dooni. Ba fun sɔbi a ŋɔ di o wa tel ba la un nɛnɛ a du. O fun la tel doon a suŋi ba naa Koosa fun suŋi a nyɛ ba, se su un chen kinkan.

ref: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
text: Apɔlɔs funɛ nyɛn Kɔrint ni, Pɔɔl fun gba a la li Ɛfɛsus. O fun la pu Yesu nitiŋɛ ta bɔnɛ dooni,

ref: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
text: a pɛɛsi ba, “Ma funɛ nyɛ ma se su Koosa laaboor weri ti chen, ma la Koosa siim weri tiuu?” Ba fun ŋɔ un, “Ya ha wa nu un di Koosa siim weri ti nyɛnu.”

ref: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ ba, “Jɔn funɛ chaksi ba naa gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa chene. O gbanko funɛ biksi Ju tim ta di ba nyɛ ba se wa su naa tiŋ o gantɛl a wa chen. Chen tiina ya Yesu.”

ref: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni, funɛ nu Pɔɔl sɛm ta, ba fun nyɛ o kpa ya kunyan Yesu sɔn a chaksi ba wa ni ni.

ref: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
text: Pɔɔl fun kpa o nisa a saksi ba ni di Koosa siim weri ti wa zu ba bambe ni. Ba fun piili a sɛm sɛm kurgɛ a la sɛm nara ni Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: There were about twelve men in this group.
text: Ba funɛ ya nara fidalɛwa.

ref: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
text: Pɛnsa tora Pɔɔl yaa fun la zu Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛ a kpak o bambi a biksi nara. O wa ba di amɔrɛ di o nyɛ ba se su di saha naa Koosa dɔ nara chen telu.

ref: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
