text: Ba bɔnɛ bambe funɛ kpoku ka ba se vɛ su o sɛm ta. Ba fun sɛm wu tagna a du ya kunyan ti nyinbaan tiŋi ti ni a li nara tima maga suwachak ni. Pɔɔl funɛ nu chenani ba sɛm, o wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun gil ba a la zu dɛ zen digi. Nibiksi naa ba yir Tiranus gbanko yaa funɛ biksi narau dooni. Wuzu kam Pɔɔl yaa funɛ biksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta dooni ka ba lɛusi un.

ref: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
text: O funɛ biksi dooni bunsa lɛwa hali Ju tim ta maga awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima aa nyɛn Azia pulum ni fun nu ya kunyan ti laaboor weri ti wɛ. Chenani Funɛ Ya Skiiva Bisa

ref: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
text: Chen saha ti Koosa fun suŋi Pɔɔl o tum tum zene naa nara ha vɛ naali.

ref: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun kpa Pɔɔl tum kilaarɛ a la gbaŋsi wichɔɔrsa ba na alaafɛ. Ba fun vɛɛn kpa a a gbaŋsi ba naa alizin tagna nyɛn ba nyuuni ni a li ba ni.

ref: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
text: Ju tim ta bɔnɛ yaa funɛ gbau a go a duŋni alizin tagna a li nara ni. Ba funɛ nu chenani Pɔɔl funɛ ya, wuzu digi ba fun ŋɔ alizin tagin ni, “Ya kpa Yesu naa Pɔɔl sɛm o wɛ chen, sɔnnɛ a ŋɔ hin di i li.”

ref: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
text: Alizin tagin ti fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu zim Yesu awa Pɔɔl, amaa anti ya ma?”

ref: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
text: Alizin tagin ti fun si baal ti ni a la muksi ba wa kpok ni a huusi ba a puuni ba kilaarɛ a nyɛ ba chɔ kaali.

ref: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
text: Ju tim ta maga awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima aa nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni fun nu chenani yɛɛ. Hansura fun kpak ba kinkan, chen ni ba fun nyɛ ya kunyana Yesu awa jirima kinkan a gbaari.

ref: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nidamata fun lagmi dɔŋ ni a sɛm chenani ba tum tuma a vɛ sɔɔni Koosa tuha.

ref: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
text: Ba naa funɛ ya tu bilim tima nidamata fun kpa ba tɔna a wa dau nara maga suwachak ni a fu a. Tɔn ta malɛ funɛ tel sidi tusugsa fumnyuunu.

ref: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
text: Chenani maga funɛ ya nyɛ nidamata se su ya kunyan ti laaboor weri ti a gbaari. Ɛfɛsus Tɔhɔn Tima Fun Dam

ref: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
text: A gantɛl nɛɛ Pɔɔl fun lisi di o zi gbau a la Masɛdonia pulum awa Girik tima pulum a bur wa Jɛrusalɛm. O gbanko fun lisi di chen gantɛl ni o zi li dooniu a la Room.

ref: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
text: O fun tum o nisuŋi nara balɛ, Timoti awa naa ba yir Ɛrastus chen, ba te o suwachak a kaali Masɛdonia pulum. O fun kpalimi Ɛfɛsus naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni.

ref: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
text: Chen saha ti nidamata funɛ tiŋ ya kunyan ti nyinbaan. Chendaha ba naa funɛ nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni nyɛ o dam.

ref: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
text: Wuzu digi o fun yir o tumtumsi ta a lagmi dɔŋ ni a ŋɔ ba, “Mu tumtum dɔŋsa, ma zimu di yaa na nyɔɔra kinkannɛ a li ya dam wisi ta kɔɔri nɔ ni.

ref: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
text: Ma zimu chenani Pɔɔl nɔ biksi nara tima di ba bur a li vursi naa nibinsa kɔɔri jɛɛmi ni, domi a ya yɔrirɛ. Nidamata funɛ tiŋ o nɔ a li Ɛfɛsus nɔ awa Azia tim pulum maga ni.

ref: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Dimitrius tum dɔŋsi ta funɛ nu o sɛm ta, ba fun na paan kinkan a la tɛsi a ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ yaa nyɛ Ɛfɛsus tim ta vuk naa ba yir Atimis jirima.”

ref: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
text: Ba fun li a tɛsi a go tɔhɔn tuha a chi chenani ba fa te tɛsi chen a nyɛ tɔhɔn ti dam. Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum ni chen, nara balɛ wa Pɔɔl funɛ wa Ɛfɛsus. Ba sɔna funɛ ya Gaiyus awa Aristakus. Zɛmɛ ta fun kpaksi nara balɛ ta a ka Pɔɔl a chɔ kaali doonani tɔhɔn ti tima yaa lagmi.

ref: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ nu chenani yɛɛ, o funɛ daŋni o la sɛm zɛmɛ ta ni a la ba, amaa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta funɛ lisiu di chen wa kpak nyinbaan.

ref: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
text: Azia tim koorsa ba naa funɛ nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni, bɔnɛ funɛ ya Pɔɔl chɛnsiwa. Ba funɛ nu chenani Pɔɔl daŋni o ya, ba fun tum nara ba la ŋɔ un di o ta la dooni.

ref: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ lagmi a dam chen funɛ tɛsi kinkan. Ba nidamata wa fun zim kinkin naa nyuhu ni ba ya chen. Chendaha ba maga fun tɛsi a kurgɛ.

ref: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
text: Ju tim ba naa funɛ nyɛn zɛmɛ ta ni, bɔnɛ fun kpak baal naa ba yir Alɛzanda a zak un li zɛmɛ ta suwachak di o ŋɔ ba chenani o kpak. O fun a viigi o nin di ba gil chɛɛma.

ref: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
text: Ba funɛ wa zim di o ya Ju narrɛ, ba chɛɛm ti fun wa lagmi kibɛgim ka ba chɛɛmi aa la ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ yaa nyɛ Ɛfɛsus tim ta vuk naa ba yir Atimis jirima.” Hali karifisa lɛ ba funɛ chɛɛmi chene.

ref: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
text: A gantɛl ni tɔhɔn ti witiina fun wa chiŋi zɛmɛ ta suwachak ni a nyɛ ba gil chɛɛma. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma Ɛfɛsus tima, ba naa maga nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni zimu di wuzu digirɛ buŋkul zen fun li buudaha a chel awa chenani ya fun sa dama a kpa un du a du un sɔn Atimis a la jɛɛmi un.

ref: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
text: Nara maga zimu di chenani ya chen ya wutirɛ. Chendaha o wa lawa ma lagmi a chɛɛmi kinkan a kitum tum naa vɛ kpak nyuha.

ref: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
text: Nara balɛ na ma kpa wa bar nɔ nan wa ŋmɛ kina a li vuk damsi ni koo a chɔk ya vuk nihaan ti sɔn.

ref: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
text: Dimitrius awa o tum dɔŋsi ta daŋni ba fiksi ba, ma nyɛ ba kpa ba a la a wuzu naa ba puk sɛria bar diili dɛ ti. Doonee nara koti a fiksi nara sɛria nidiili ti suwachak ni.

ref: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
text: Wu digi nyɛn Dimitrius awa o tum dɔŋsi ta bambe ni, ba pɔ a wuzu naa ya tɔhɔn ti tim maga yaa lagmi a na dɔŋa a nyɛ a deŋli.

ref: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
text: Roman tim ta nu chenani tɔhɔn ti dam zinan, ba zi wa pɛɛsi o munu. Ya wa zi koti a ŋɔ ba chenani o du ka ba muksi ya.”

ref: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
text: O funɛ sɛm nan peti, o fun nyɛ ba pisi a li lagmi ti ni.

ref: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
text: Wuzu bilɛ gantɛl ni, Pɔɔl fun yir Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a lagmi dɔŋ ni a ŋɔ ba di o daŋni o la Masɛdonia pulumnɛ. O fun kpami ba a chausi ba a kaali.

ref: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
text: O fun gba a go Masɛdonia pulum a kpami Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn dooni. O fun li dooni a kaali Girik tim pulum,

ref: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
text: a la nyɛn dooni pɛnsa tora. O funɛ nyɛn doon a saksi di o bur la Siiriya pulum, o fun nu di Ju tim ta dɛu nyinbaannɛ di ba kpu un. Chendaha o fun chɛrgi o bambi a lisi di o bur la tiŋ Masɛdonia pulum a la.

ref: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
text: Ju tim ta wuzu zen naa ba yaa se boroboro ka luri vɛ nyɛni chen, petiu, ya fun li Girik tim pulum a kaali Filipai a la zu arŋa a kaali Troas. Wuzu bunyuu gantɛl ni ya fun tel doon a nyɛn wuzu bunɔpɛ. Chenani Funɛ Ya Yutikus Troas Ni

ref: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
text: Asibri wuzu zaazɔn ya wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Troas ni, fun lagmi a di diwe. Ya funɛ di peti, Pɔɔl fun si a ŋɔ ya ni. O funɛ sɛm chene hali tɔhɛɛsa. Chɔɔsa zi wa pisi, o funɛ daŋni ya li dooni. Chendaha o fun sɛm a vusi.

ref: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
text: Ba funɛ sa dɛ naa tuhu ni ya lagmi chen a zɔŋ dɛ naa lɛ nyuhu nɛɛ. Fitin damata funɛ chiŋi dooni.

ref: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
text: Bunɔɔn digi fun nyɛn dooni ba yir un Yutikus. O fun kani tokoro mun ni. Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm chene, ka o chuk do hali a wa chɔ peti a tu wa chel tɛɛnɛ ni. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta fun taŋsi a tu wa suŋi un, amaa o funɛ sou.

ref: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
text: Pɔɔl ka fun tiŋ ba gantɛl a tu wa a zer un a kur a ŋɔ ba, “Ma ta nyɛ o muksi ma. O bu kpak miisau.”

ref: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
text: Ya funɛ kaali chene, Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wa zer bunɔɔn naa funɛ sou a bu si chen a kaali dama. Chenani o fun bu na miisa chen sɔɔni ba tusa kinkan. Pɔɔl Fun Kaali Mailitus

ref: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
text: Pɔɔl fun waligi ya ni a kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Asɔs o duŋduŋ. Ya ka fun zu arŋa a la pu un dooni a chi chenani o fun ŋɔ ya chen.

ref: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
text: Ya funɛ la tel Asɔs, o fun wa gbaari ya ni a li arŋ ti tuhu ni. Ya fun li dooni a kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Mitilini.

ref: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
text: Pɔɔl wa funɛ daŋni o nyɛn Azia pulum ti ni. O ka funɛ daŋni o gali Ɛfɛsus nɛɛ a la tel Jɛrusalɛm lalaga. Ju tim ta wuzu zen naa ba yir val la gali ti chen funɛ dugli. O daŋni o te la dooni ka o zi tel. Pɔɔl Fun Chausi Ɛfɛsus Nihɛɛta

ref: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ nyɛn Mailitus nɛɛ a tum nara ba la ŋɔ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni, nihɛɛta di ba wa pu un.

ref: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
text: Ba funɛ la tel dooni, Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ ba, “Saha naa mu wa ma fun nyɛn Azia pulum ni, ma zim chenani mu fun duu.

ref: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
text: Mu yaa funɛ tum ya kunyan ti tuma awa jɛɛgisi nɛɛ. Hali Ju tim ta funɛ na nyinbaanasa di mu namsi kinkan chen ni mu zi wiu, nannɛ mu funɛ tum.

ref: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
text: Mu funɛ biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ma nara suwachak ni a vɛɛn biksi ma a li ma damsi ni. Kinkin naa la zi suŋi ma wa ka mu vɛ ŋɔ ma.

ref: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
text: Lalaknɔ mu tiŋ Koosa siim weri ti nɔ a le la Jɛrusalɛm. Mu wa zim chenani maga kaa ya min dooni.

ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: Mu zimu di doonani maga mu funɛ gba, waa funɛ ŋɔ min di mu zi na namsiu ka nara tɔ min dɛ.

ref: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Mu baga wa to kin naa ya min ni. Ya kunyana Yesu fun lisi min di mu biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ awa chenani Koosa daŋni o suŋi ba. Mu bambi maga nyɛn tum nɔ o kpa nyɛ min di mu tum a peti nannɛ.

ref: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
text: “Mu wa ma funɛ nyɛn, mu funɛ biksi ma di saha naa Koosa kaa wa dɔ nara telu. Mu zimu di saha nɔ kpa la, ma wa zi bu na min.

ref: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
text: Nan daha, mu sɛm ma wutirɛ, a wuzu naa Koosa wa na ma galimsa a ta ma yɔri, o wa ya mu wi.

ref: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
text: Kin naa maga Koosa daŋni o tum wa ka mu vɛ biksi ma.

ref: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
text: Waa funɛ nyɛ o be sou a nyɛ ya, chen ni ya zi kotiu a bur a tiŋ un a ya o nara. O siim weri ti funɛ lisi ma di ma ya Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni, nihɛɛta. Ba waa chi piisisa ka ma ka chi ba nigbaŋɛ. Ma kpak Yesu nɛnɛ, chen ni ma zi kotiu a kok ba.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
text: Mu funɛ kaali chen gantɛl nɛɛ, mu zimu di nara zi wau a chi gbesa a chem ba naa ma nyini chen a muksi ma kinkan.

ref: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
text: Hali saha zi wa telu di ma Yesu nitiŋɛ ta bɔnɛ zi wa siu a suni wɛ, chen ni bɔnɛ zi purigiu a la tiŋ ba.

ref: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
text: Ma doso a kilisi chenani mu fun kpak un mu bambi ni di mu biksi ma bunsa tora a ta hiisi tama.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: “Lalaknɔ mu kpa ma a du Koosa nin ni. O laaboor weri ti chenani o suŋi nara wɛ zi nyɛ ma kpak unu nɛnɛ a gbaari. Wuzu digi o zi wa kpa kin nawara o fun kpa chiŋsiu a nyɛ ba naa maga o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni.

ref: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
text: Mu wa ma funɛ nyɛn, mu wa funɛ daŋni nandigi salma koo aniita koo gar.

ref: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
text: Mu yaa funɛ tum a li mu bara wilim nee a nyini mu wa mu pɛgsa.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
text: Mu funɛ tum nannɛ di mu biksi ma chenani ma gbanko zi wa tum a koti a suŋi fara tima. Ma lisi chenani ya kunyana Yesu fun biksi ya. O funɛ ŋɔ, ‘Nar lisi a li o kin ni a nyɛ nandigi ni, o tuha zi sɔɔniu a kaali nara nyɛ un kina.’ ”

ref: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm peti, o wa ba fun gbiri tɛɛnɛ ni a sumi Koosa.

ref: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
text: Chenani o fun te ŋɔ ba di o wa zi bu na ba fun chɔk ba tusa kinkan, ka ba gil un o zu arŋ ti.

ref: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
text: Ya fun li arŋ ti ni dooni a la zu arŋ naa la daŋni o la Fonisia pulum. Ya fun li Patara ni,

ref: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
text: a la gali Saiprus pulum naa ni go ka o nyɛn ya ningal ni a la tel Taaya a li Siiriya pulum ni. Bun tima arŋ ti fun chuŋi chen funɛ ya doon kinawa. Ya fun gil arŋ ti,

ref: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
text: Arŋ ti funɛ bu saksi di o si, ba wa ba haanasa awa ba bisa fun tiŋ ya a la li ni ti nɔ a gbiri a sumi Koosa.

ref: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
text: Ya fun chausi ba a bu zu arŋ ti ka ba bur kaali dama.

ref: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
text: Ya fun li dooni a tiŋ ni nɔ kaali tɔhɔn naa ba yir Tɔlimeis. Ya fun bu gil arŋ ti dooni a la zaami Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a nyɛn ba wati wuzu bɛgim.

ref: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
text: Wuzu bilɛ gantɛl ni Koosa witiinsi ta digi fun li Judia pulum a wa. O sɔn funɛ ya Agabus.

ref: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
text: O fun kaali Pɔɔl wati a la ŋɔ un di o kpa o chɛra kikirisa a nyɛ un. Pɔɔl fun kpa a nyɛ un. Agabus fun kani a kpa un a vɔu o nisa awa o naŋgere a ŋɔ, “Ma nu chenani Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ minu. Chɛra kikirisi nɔ tiina la tel Jɛrusalɛm, Ju tima ba naa nyɛn dooni, zi wa vɔu unu a chi chenani mu vɔu mu titi nan, a kpa un a du ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim nisa ni.”

ref: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
text: Ya funɛ nu o sɛm ta, ya wa ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni, fun sumi Pɔɔl di o ta la Jɛrusalɛm.

ref: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
text: O fun ŋɔ ya, “Yɛrɛ ma nyɛ mu wɛ muksi ma a nyɛ mu bambi buti? N saksɛu di ba kpak min a vɔu ya kunyan Yesu daha. Hali mu saksɛu di mu sou a nyɛ un.”

ref: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
text: Ya zim di ya sɛm na wa zi nyɛ o gil laali, ya fun ŋɔ un, “I zimɛɛ di ya kunyana daŋni i ya chen, o deŋlu wa i ya chen.”

ref: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
text: Wuzu bilɛ gantɛl ni, ya fun lagmi ya kina a kaali Jɛrusalɛm.

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn Siizaria ni, bɔnɛ fun gbaari ya ni a kaali. Ya funɛ la tel Jɛrusalɛm, ba fun te ya suwachak a kpa ya a la baal naa ba yir Manasɔn, dama di ya nyɛn dooni. O funɛ li Saiprus pulum nɛ ka o se su ya kunyan ti a vusi. Pɔɔl Fun La Zaami Jems Jɛrusalɛm Ni

ref: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni fun la ya du num num ni.

ref: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, ya wa Pɔɔl fun kaali a la zaami Jems. O wa Yesu nitiŋɛ nihɛɛti ta maga funɛ lagmi dɔŋa a kani.

ref: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
text: Pɔɔl fun zaami ba maga a ŋɔ ba chenani maga Koosa fun suŋi un di o tum a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni.

ref: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
text: O funɛ sɛm peti, Jems awa nihɛɛti ta fun pɛgi Koosa chenani o tum chen daha. Ba fun ŋɔ Pɔɔl ni, “Ya niinbe, i zimu di Ju tima ba naa nyɛn bar nɔ, tusugsa funɛ nyɛ ya se su ya kunyan ti a kpak un ba bambe ni kinkan di ba tiŋ ba chiksɛ ta.

ref: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
text: Nara funɛ ŋɔ ba chenani i yaa biksi Ju tima ba naa nyɛ ba se su ya kunyan ti ka ba nyɛn bar boli ni di ba ta bu tiŋ ba chiksɛ ta. I gbanko yaa biksi bau di ba ta bu teŋsi ba bisa a ta bu tiŋ ba nawaalsi ta nyinbaan.

ref: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
text: Nara nama zi nu i wɛu a daŋni ba wa zaami hin. Ya wara daŋni i wa ba wa di amɔrɛ kpok.

ref: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
text: Adaha tiŋ chenani ya kaa ya ŋɔ hin. Nara banaasi nyɛn ya ni a vɔu vɔwi a nyɛ Koosa ni di ba zi tiŋ unu nɛnɛ a gbaari.

ref: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
text: Kpa i titi a gbaari ba ni a nyɛ nara chaksi ma wa ni ni a funi ma nyuuna a biksi nara di Koosa la chaani ma bambe ni o suwachak ni. Bɔ a nyɛ ma maga ni, chen ni nara maga zi zimu di chenani ba sɛm i wɛ chen wa ya wuti. Ba gbanko zi zimu di i ha wa gil ya chiksɛ ta tiŋi.

ref: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
text: Ya funɛ piin sɔbi tɔnu a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni a nyɛ ba se su Yesu. Ya funɛ ŋɔ bau di ba ta di vursi diwe a ta se nɛmɛ naa ba vɛ kɔrigi o li chɛl a ta tum chɔkumsi tuma.”

ref: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi Pɔɔl fun la gbaari nara tima ni a nyɛ nara chaksi ba wa ni ni a biksi nara di Koosa la chaani ba bambe ni o suwachak ni. Doone ba fun la zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti a ŋɔ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta ni di wuzu bunɔpɛ wuzu ba zi wa kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni a biksi nara di chenani o funɛ chaani ba bambe ni chen mak o bɔu. Ju Tima Fun Kpak Pɔɔl Koosa Bar Jɛɛmi Dam Ni

ref: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
text: Wuzu bunɔpɛ ta funɛ wa dugli peti, Ju tima ba naa funɛ li Azia pulum, bɔnɛ fun na Pɔɔl a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni. Ba fun nyɛ ba naa maga nyɛn dooni chen dam a la kpak un.

ref: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
text: Ba fun tɛsi a ŋɔ, “Ma Ju tima, ma wa suŋi ya. Baal naa ya kpak nan, waa ya naa yaa biksi nara bar damati ni di Ju tim ta, ba chiksɛ ta awa Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam nɔ ya yɔrirɛ. Hali lalaknɔ o kpa ba naa vɛ ya Ju timwa a wa zu bar nɔ a chɔk doonani Koosa fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni chen.”

ref: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
text: Ba funɛ piin te na Pɔɔl awa Trofimus naa li Ɛfɛsus ni ka ba lagmi a gba tɔhɔn ti ni. Ba fun baa di Pɔɔl kpa un a wa zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti. Chendaha ba fun ŋɔ chen.

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ saksi di ba kpu Pɔɔl chene, nandigi fun chɔ a la zini a ŋɔ Roman tim lanjimasa nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ni di Jɛrusalɛm tim maga funɛ damu.

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: O wa o lanjimasa awa ba nihɛɛta fun vaan chɔ tu a la doonani zɛmɛ ta tuk Pɔɔl chen. Zɛmɛ ta funɛ na lanjimasa nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti ka o wa o lanjimasi ta wa, ba fun gil Pɔɔl tuki.

ref: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
text: Lanjimasa nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba kpa zɔglɛ abalɛ a vɔu Pɔɔl a burgi pɛɛsi zɛmɛ ta, “Anti ya baal nɔ? Baŋnɛ o tum?”

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Nara tima bɔnɛ fun tɛsi a ŋɔ wu digi. Ba bɔnɛ ka fun tɛsi aa sɛm kundɔŋ. Chɛɛm ti fun lagmi dɔŋ ni o vɛ nu chenani ba sɛmi. Nan daha o fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni di ba kpa Pɔɔl a la zu ba dam kpok naa nara vɛ zu yɔri.

ref: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ chɛɛmi kinkan a ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ ya kpu un.” Pɔɔl Fun Kok O Titi Zɛmɛ Suwachak Ni

ref: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
text: Lanjimasi ta awa Pɔɔl funɛ la dugli zuuli, Pɔɔl fun burgi a ŋɔ lanjimasi ta nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti ni, “I zi nyɛ mu sɛm hin wu digiuu?” O fun ŋɔ un, “Mu wa zim di i sɛm Girik tim sɛm tau.

ref: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ un, “Ju tima lul min tɔ birigi naa ba yir Taasus a li Sailisia pulum ni. Mu sumi hinu, nyɛ mu sɛm a nyɛ zɛmɛ ta ni.”

ref: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
text: Lanjimasi ta nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun nyɛ un nyinbaan. Pɔɔl fun chiŋi naŋgere bar nɔsɛ nyuhu ti ni a viigi o nin di zɛmɛ ta gil chɛɛma. Ba fun moori ka o sɛm sɛma a nyɛ ba a li Ju tim sɛm ni.

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ nu ka o sɛm ba a li ba titi sɛm ni, ba fun bukoo moori a gbaari.

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: Pɔɔl fun bukoo ŋɔ ba, “Ju tima lul min tɔhɔn naa ba yir Taasus a li Sailisia pulum ni, amaa mu funɛ biu a burgi nihɛɛ a li Jɛrusalɛm nɔ ni. Ya nibiksi zen naa ba yir Gameliel funɛ biksi min di mu tiŋ ya nawaalsi ta chiksɛ ta nɛnɛ. Chenani mu se fun sɛri di mu tiŋ Koosa chen saha ti awa chenani ma kina sɛri zinan ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
text: Nara funɛ ŋɔ min chenani nara bɔnɛ funɛ tiŋ Yesu nyinbaan, mu fun namsi ba kinkan a fun kpaksi ba nidamata a tɔ ba dɛ a kpaŋi mu titi a kpu bɔnɛ.

ref: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
text: “Nha funɛ wa tel sal baan nyuha, mu funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a wa dugli Damaskus. Kundɔŋ fun vaan nyiksi a li buudaha ni a chaani du min ni.

ref: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
text: Mu fun chel tɛɛnɛ ni a nu ta ka o ŋɔ min, ‘Sɔɔl, baŋnɛ ii namsi min chen ni?’

ref: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
text: Mu fun ŋɔ, ‘Mu kunyana, anti ya hin?’ Ta ti fun ŋɔ, ‘Min ya Yesu Nazarɛt nar naa i namsi chen.’

ref: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
text: Ba naa wa min funɛ gba chen fun na chaani ti, amaa ba wa fun nu ta ti.

ref: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
text: Mu fun bukoo ŋɔ, ‘Mu kunyana, baŋnɛ ii daŋni mu ya?’ Ta ti fun ŋɔ min, ‘Si a la zu Damaskus. Nandigi zi wa ŋɔ hinu chenani Koosa daŋni i yau dooni.’

ref: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
text: Kundɔŋ naa funɛ chaani a du min ni chen nyɛ mu burgi nyulumnɛ. Adaha mu dɔŋtim ta nidigi fun kpak mu nin a te mu suwachak a la zu tɔhɔn ti tuha.

ref: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
text: “Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Ananias. Waa yaa funɛ jɛɛmi Koosa wa wuti ni aa tiŋ ya chiksɛ ta nɛnɛ. Ju tim ba naa maga nyɛn Damaskus ni, funɛ nyɛ un jirima kinkan.

ref: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: O fun bukoo ŋɔ min, ‘I funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ti ni a wa chene, Koosa naa ya nawaalsi ta funɛ jɛɛmi, nyɛ i na Yesu ka i wa un sɛm sɛma. Waa ya o tumtumna naa yaa tiŋ o tutɔ. Koosa funɛ lisi hin di i tum chenani o daŋni,

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: a ŋɔ nidamata ni naa i na ka i wa un sɛm sɛma wɛ.

ref: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
text: “Mu fun bur kaali Jɛrusalɛm a la zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti di mu sumi un. Mu sumi ti bambaan ni,

ref: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
text: Mu fun ŋɔ un, ‘Ju tim ta zimu nɛnɛ di mu gba a go ba bar lagmi dɛɛna a kpaksi ba naa se su hin a tuksi.

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
text: Saha naa ba fun kpak Stivin anti funɛ biksi ba i wɛ, a kpu un, mu titi funɛ nyɛn dooniu a na. Mu funɛ nyini ba naa la yaksi un chen taktasa a la nɔɔni chenani ba funɛ ya chen ni.’

ref: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
text: Mu kunyan ti fun bukoo ŋɔ min, ‘Mu kaa tum hin i la ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim watɛɛ a li tɔ bar bole ni. Adaha kila.’ ”

ref: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
text: Ba fun tɛsi kinkan a chɛsi ba kilaarɛ a zɔk hɛɛra a yo buudaha ni di ba biksi nara di chenani Pɔɔl sɛm chen pini Koosa zomwa.

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: Roman lanjimasa nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ba kpa Pɔɔl a la zu ba dam kpok naa nara vɛ zu yɔri, a tuk un wa vurmsi ni. O funɛ ŋɔ ba di ba ya chene, chen ni Pɔɔl zi ŋɔ unu kin naa ya zɛmɛ ta chɛɛmiu.

ref: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
text: Lanjimasi ta fun kpa ganɛsa a vɔu Pɔɔl a saksi di ba tuk un. O fun burgi a ŋɔ ba nihɛɛ naa chiŋi dooni, “Mu ya Ju tim baallɛ a vɛɛn ya Roman tim gbanko. Ma wa kpak nyinbaan naa ma zi tuk min wa vurmsi ni, domi ma ha wa kpa min la sɛria bar diili dɛ a na mu galimsa.”

ref: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
text: Lanjimasi ta nihɛɛ ti funɛ nu o sɛm ta, o fun kaali ba nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti wati a la ŋɔ un, “Mu sumi hinu doso baal ti ni. O ŋɔ min di o ya Roman tim baallɛ.”

ref: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
text: Lanjimasi ta nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun la pɛɛsi Pɔɔl, “O ya wutirɛ i ŋɔ di i ya Roman tim baallɛ nann?” Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ, “O ya wutirɛ.”

ref: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
text: Nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun ŋɔ un, “Mu funɛ bɔ malɛ damatiwa ka ba nyɛ mu burgi Roman tim baal.” Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ un, “Roman tima funɛ lul min.”

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: Lanjimasa ba naa funɛ daŋni ba tuk Pɔɔl chen, fun vaan bur gantɛl. Nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti funɛ zim di Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm wutirɛ, hansura fun kpak un, domi o wa fun kpak nyinbaan di o kpak un a kpa zɔglɛ ta a vɔu un. Ju Tima Nihɛɛta Fun Lagmi Di Ba Di Pɔɔl Sɛria

ref: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
text: Nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti funɛ daŋni o zim chenani Pɔɔl ya Ju tim ta fiksi un. Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, o fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba pur zɔglɛ ta Pɔɔl ni a fun ŋɔ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛti ta ni ba lagmi. Doone o fun kpa un a chiŋsi ba suwachak ni.

ref: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
text: Pɔɔl fun nyini ba se ni a ŋɔ, “Mu niinbisa, chenani mu nyɛn hali wa zinan, mu kpak tuha kibɛgimu awa Koosa ni.”

ref: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ naa ba yir Ananias funɛ nu o sɛm ta, o fun ŋɔ naa chiŋi a dugli un digi o chɛɛgi o lɛha.

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti ni, “I chi zɛgbɛra naa yɛlɛɛ ba pɛɛrirɛ. Koosa zi wa bɔ hinu a li chenani i ya min chen. I kani bar nɔwa aa di mu sɛria a li ya chiksɛ ta nyinbaan ni ka i titi chɔk a a ŋɔ baal nɔ di o chɛɛgi mu lɛha.”

ref: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
text: Ba naa funɛ chiŋi a dugli Pɔɔl chen nidigi fun ŋɔ un, “Yɛrɛ i nyuha kpok i pini Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti zoma?”

ref: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ ba maga ni, “Mu niinbisa, chenani ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chen ya wutirɛ. Ba funɛ ŋɔ, ‘Ma fiɛŋ ma wa pini koor naa dɔ ma zoma.’ Amaa chenani o fun ya min chen, mu wa fun zim di waa ya ya koor ti.”

ref: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
text: Pɔɔl fun na di tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛti ta wa ya nibɛgim. Bɔnɛ funɛ ya Farisi timwa. Bɔnɛ ka ya Sajusi tima. O fun sɛm ta zaŋa a ŋɔ ba, “Mu niinbisa, Farisi tima funɛ lul min. Mu kpak bambi bɛgimu di wuzu digi Koosa zi wa lɛli nisitɛu a li ba sou ni. Chendaha ma lagmi bar nɔ zinan di ma di mu sɛria.”

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: O funɛ sɛm nan, Farisi tim ta awa Sajusi tima fun vaan purigi a di amɔrɛ wa dɔŋ ni.

ref: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
text: Doone Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ba naa funɛ ya Farisi tim chen bɔnɛ fun chɛɛmi a tɔ chɛɛm ti a ŋɔ, “Ya se su di Koosa funɛ tum a malaikasi ta digi koo o siimsi ta digi a wa sɛm nyɛ un. Chendaha o nin wa toli.”

ref: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
text: Chenani Farisi tim ta awa Sajusi tima funɛ di amɔrɛ chen wa burgi yo. Hansura funɛ kpak lanjimasi ta nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti di ba zi huusi Pɔɔlu. O fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba zu kpak un yo ti bambaan ni a li a bur la zu ba dam kpok ti.

ref: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
text: Dusum funɛ wa biri, Pɔɔl fun bu na ya kunyan ti a chi o deesi. Ya kunyan ti fun ŋɔ un, “Kpak bambi. Mu zi nyɛ i la Roomu a la ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni, mu wɛ a chi chenani i ya bar nɔ nan.” Ju Tima Fun Vɔti Nɔɔna Di Ba Kpu Pɔɔl

ref: The next morning more than
text: Ba naa funɛ vɔti nɔɔni chen funɛ kaali nara fumnaasiu.

ref: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
text: Ba fun la ŋɔ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa nihɛɛti ta ni, “Ya fun lagmi dɔŋ ni a vɔti nɔɔna a ŋɔ di ya vɛ kpu Pɔɔl, ya wa zi di diwe koo a nyɔ ni.

ref: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
text: Ya sumi mau di ma tum nandigi o la ŋɔ Roman tim nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti ni di o kpa Pɔɔl a wa, domi ma daŋni Pɔɔllɛ ŋɔ ma a gbaari. Ya zi chem un nyinbaan niu a kpa un.”

ref: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
text: Pɔɔl towe be fun nu chenani ba daŋni ba ya a la zini a zu lanjimasi ta dam kpok ti a ŋɔ Pɔɔl ni.

ref: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
text: Pɔɔl funɛ nu o sɛm ta, o fun yir lanjimasi ta nihɛɛti ta digi o wa. O fun ŋɔ un, “Kpa bunɔɔn ti kila i nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti wati. O kpak wu digirɛ a kaa ŋɔ un.”

ref: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
text: Nihɛɛ ti fun kpa un kaali o ŋɔ nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti ni, “Pɔɔl naa i tɔ dɛ chen yir min, a ŋɔ min di mu kpa bunɔɔn nɔ a wa i wati. O kpak wu digirɛ a kaa ŋɔ hin.”

ref: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
text: Nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun kpa bunɔɔn ti nin a kpa un a la ba digi a la pɛɛsi un, “Yɛrɛ i kpak a kaa ŋɔ min chen?”

ref: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
text: O fun ŋɔ, “Ju tim ta nihɛɛti ta la nɔɔniu di ba chɛ lagmi chɛ a ŋɔ hin i bu kpa Pɔɔl a wa, amaa ba funɛ zigu a la daŋni o ŋɔ ba a gbaari.

ref: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
text: Nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti fun ŋɔ un, “Ta bu ŋɔ nandigi ni chenani i ŋɔ min nan,” a nyɛ o kaali. Ba Fun Tum Pɔɔl Filiks Wati

ref: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
text: Ma dɛu zaagna a nyɛ Pɔɔl ni o zini a kok un a la tel gɔbnati nyuhu tiin nihɛɛ dama dooni. O sɔn ya Filiks.”

ref: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
text: Min la tum baal nɔ awa tɔn nɔ ma wati. Ju tim ta fun kpak un a daŋni ba kpu un, ka mu wa mu lanjimasi ta fun la lisi un ba bambaan ni. Ba funɛ ŋɔ min di o ya Roman tim baallɛ. Chendaha mu fun ya chen.

ref: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
text: Mu fun ŋɔ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛti ta di ba lagmi dɔŋ ni a kpa un kaali ba suwachak di mu zim chenani o ya Ju tim ta fiksi un.

ref: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
text: Mu fun wa dɛu a na di o wa tum tum tagin naa la zi nyɛ mu di o sou sɛria koo a tɔ un dɛ. Ba ka fun ŋɔ di o chɔk ba titi chire.

ref: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
text: Mu funɛ nu di Ju tim ta bura daŋni ba kpu unu, mu fun lisi di mu nyɛ mu lanjimasi ta kpa un wa i wati. Mu fun ŋɔ ba naa la fiksi un chen di ba tiŋ o gantɛl a wa fiksi un i suwachak ni. Zaami i walɛ maga a nyɛ min.”

ref: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
text: Lanjimasi ta fun tiŋ ba nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti nɔ a kpa Pɔɔl a kaali. Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi di ba tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Antipatris.

ref: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
text: Lanjimasa ba naa funɛ val naŋgere awa ba naa la chem laal wa tima ni, fun purigi ba titi a bur kaali Jɛrusalɛm. Lanjimasa ba naa la zini zaagna fumnɔpɛ fun kpa Pɔɔl ba wa un kaali.

ref: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
text: Ba fun la tel Siizaria a zer ba nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti tɔn a nyɛ Filiks ni a kpa Pɔɔl a du o nin ni.

ref: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
text: Filiks fun karimi tɔn ti a pɛɛsi Pɔɔl pulum naa o li. O funɛ zim di o li Sailisia pulum nɛ,

ref: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
text: o fun ŋɔ un, “O wa zi vusi ka ba naa la fiksi hin chen wa. Ba wa wa, mu zi di i sɛriau.” O fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ni di ba kpa Pɔɔl a la tɔ un dɛ a li dam zen naa koor Hɛrɔd fun sa chen ni a la il un.

ref: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
text: Wuzu bunyuu gantɛl ni, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ, Ananias, tɔhɔn nihɛɛta, ba naa funɛ li Jɛrusalɛm, awa naa la chiksi ba sɛria ka ba yir un Tatolos, fun wa tel Siizaria. Filiks funɛ nu di ba wau, o fun ŋɔ a lanjimasi ta ni ba la kpa Pɔɔl a wa chiŋsi o suwachak ni.

ref: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
text: Doone o fun ŋɔ di Tatolos gbanko wa chiŋi o suwachak ni a fiksi Pɔɔl. Tatolos fun ŋɔ, “I waa naa ya ya gɔbnati nyuhu tiina nihɛɛ, ya zaami hinu. I waa naa kpak ya wa nɛha ni a nyɛ ya bara sɔɔni, hin vɛɛn tum tum were a nyɛ ya pulum tim ni.

ref: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
text: Ya tusa sɔɔnɛu kinkan i koti a suŋi ya chen.

ref: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
text: Ya wara daŋni ya kpak hin a vusi. Ya sumi di i nu ya sɛm tam nɔ.

ref: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
text: Pɔɔl nɔ chiŋɛ i suwachak ni nan, yaa muksi ya narau a huusi ma Roman tima ma naa dɔ ya. Doonani maga o la, ka Ju tim ta nyɛn, o yaa nyɛ tɔhɔn ti damu. O gbanko ya ba naa la tiŋ Nazarɛt nar, nidigi nyuhu tiinnɛ.

ref: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
text: I wa pɛɛsi un, i zi zim kin naa nyɛ ya fiksi unu.”

ref: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
text: Doone Filiks fun ŋɔ Pɔɔl ni di o saha telu wa o sɛm. Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ, “Mu zimu di i di ya nara sɛriau bun damata. Chendaha mu tuha sɔɔni di mu kok mu titi i suwachak ni zinan.

ref: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
text: Nyɛ mu ŋɔ hin chenani mu na. O funɛ dugli wuzu fidalɛ gantɛl nɛɛ, mu fun la Jɛrusalɛm a la zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti di mu jɛɛmi un.

ref: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
text: Mu funɛ nyɛn dooni mu wa nandigi wa fun di amɔrɛ. Hali a wa li a gba tɔhɔn ti a zu ba bar lagmi dɛɛna, mu wa fun nyɛ tɔhɔn tima dam.

ref: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
text: Mu zimu di ba wa koti a biksi hin di chenani ba fiksi min chen ya wutirɛ,

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: amaa chenani mu kaa ŋɔ hin chen ya wutirɛ. Mu yaa tiŋ Koosa jɛɛmi nyinbaan naa Ju tim ta ŋɔ di o wa ya wuti. Waa funɛ ya naa ya nawaalsi ta jɛɛmi a kpa a chiksɛ ta a nyɛ Mosis ni a nyɛ o witinsi ta sɔbi o sɛm ta a du o sɛm tɔn ti ni. O chiksɛ ta awa chenani maga ba sɔbi a du o sɛm tɔn ti ni ha vɛɛn ya mu kinawa.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: Mu yaa fun kpak unu mu bambi ni di mu tum mu tuma wa tu bɛgim ni Koosa awa nara suwachak ni.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: “Saha naa mu fun nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, o funɛ vusiu ka mu la dooni. Ju tim ta bɔnɛ fun lagmi malɛ a du mu nin ni di mu kpa la Jɛrusalɛm. Mu gbanko fun la dooni di mu jɛɛmi Koosa.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: Ju tima ba naa funɛ li Azia pulum a wa chen, bɔnɛ funɛ nyɛn dooni a kpak min. Ba kpak wu digi a du min ni, ba waa fu la zi nyɛn i suwachak ni bar nɔ ni a fiksi min.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: Ba vɛ waali nan, nyɛ ba naa lagmi dɔŋ ni a li Jɛrusalɛm ni chen ŋɔ hin chenani ba fun di mu sɛria a na mu galimsa.

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
text: Mu fun ŋɔ ba di mu kpak bambi bɛgimu di wuzu digi Koosa zi wa lɛli nisitɛu a li ba sou ni. Ba na mu galimsa nan dahauu?”

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
text: Filiks funɛ zim chenani nara tiŋ Yesu nyinbaanu kinkan. O fun ŋɔ ba di lagmi ti petiu a vɛɛn ŋɔ, “Nihɛɛ nyuhu tiin ti, Lisias, wa wa, mu zi lisi chenani mu kaa yau.”

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: O fun ŋɔ o lanjimasa ba naa funɛ il Pɔɔl chen, nihɛɛ di ba ta bu tɔ un dɛ a nyɛ o chɛnsa wa nyinbaan ba kpa kin naa o daŋni a wa nyɛ un. Pɔɔl, Filiks Awa Drusila Fun Na Dɔŋa Ba Duŋduŋ

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Wuzu bilɛ gantɛl ni Filiks wa o haan naa ba yir Drusila, fun ŋɔ ba kpa Pɔɔl a wa ba wati. O haan ti funɛ ya Ju tim nihaannɛ. Ba funɛ daŋni ba wa un sɛm chenani nara se su Yesu Kristo.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ ba chenani ba naa se su Yesu fun kpaŋi ba titi di ba tum tum were a nyɛ un a ya bambe tima. O gbanko funɛ ŋɔ bau chenani Koosa zi wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛriau wuzu digi. Pɔɔl sɛm ta fun nyɛ hansura kpak Filiks ka o ŋɔ un, “O magu. Kila. Mu wa na nyinbaan, mu zi yir hinu i wa sɛm ya gbaari.”

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: Filiks fun kpak bambi bɛgim di Pɔɔl zi kpa malɛu a nyɛ un a nyɛ o la. Chendaha o wa un fun lagmi a sɛm sɛma bu damata.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Filiks funɛ daŋni o sɔɔni Ju tim ta tusau. Chendaha o nyɛ o lanjimasi ta il Pɔɔl a vɛ nyɛ o laali. Bunsa lɛ gantɛl ni, baal naa ba funɛ yir Pɔsius Festus fun burgi gɔbnati nyuhu tiina nihɛɛ a chiŋi Filiks bar chiŋi ni.

ref: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
text: Wuzu butora gantɛl ni, Fɛstus fun li Siizaria a kaali Jɛrusalɛm.

ref: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛti ta funɛ nu di o wau, ba fun la fiksi Pɔɔl o suwachak ni,

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: a sumi un di ba kpa Pɔɔl kuwa Jɛrusalɛm o di o sɛria. Ba funɛ daŋni ba kpu un nyinbaan nɛɛ. Chendaha ba fun sɛm chen.

ref: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Fɛstus fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu wa la nɔɔni di mu kpa un wa bar nɔ. O wa zi vusi ka mu bur kaali Siizaria.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
text: Baal nɔ fu tum tum tagin ka ma daŋni ma fiksi un mu suwachak ni, ma lisi nara a li ma tuhu ni a gbaari min ni ya la nu chenani sɛm ta kaa du.”

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Fɛstus funɛ kpalimi Jɛrusalɛm ni wuzu mɛnasa koo wuzu fi a li dooni a bur kaali Siizaria. Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, o fun la kani sɛria bar diili dɛ ni a ŋɔ ba kpa Pɔɔl a wa.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ zu dɛ ti, Ju tima ba naa funɛ li Jɛrusalɛm a wa, fun vaan go un a fiksi un a ŋɔ di o tum tum tagna damatiwa, amaa ba wa fun koti a biksi di chenani ba sɛm chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
text: Pɔɔl fun la chiŋi Fɛstus suwachak ni a ŋɔ un, “Mu wa fun chɔk Ju tim ta chiksɛ koo a sɛm sɛm tagin a du Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni koo a chɔk koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza sɔn.”

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
text: Festus funɛ daŋni o sɔɔni Ju tim ta tusa a pɛɛsi un, “Mu nyɛ ba kpa hin a la Jɛrusalɛm di mu di i sɛria dooni, yɛrɛ o zi du hin ni?”

ref: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ un, “Yɛrɛ ma vɛ kaa di min sɛria ti bar nɔ a kaa kpa min la bar digi? Hin ya gɔbnati nyuhu tiin zen a chiŋi koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza bar chiŋi ni. I zimu nɛnɛ di mu wa tum tum tagin digi a du Ju tim ta ni.

ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
text: Mu fu chɔk chiksɛ ta koo a tum tum tagin naa i zi di mu sou sɛria, mu saksɛu di mu namsi. Chenani ba fu fiksi min chen vɛ ya wuti, i wa kpak nyinbaan naa i zi kpa min a du ba nisa ni. Mu sumi hinu di i nyɛ i lanjimasi ta kpa min a la koor Siiza wati o di mu sɛria.”

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: Fɛstus fun burgi o wa ba naa la suŋi un o di sɛria chen sɛm sɛma dɔŋ wati. Ba funɛ sɛm peti, o fun burgi Pɔɔl wati a ŋɔ un, “I sumi minu di mu nyɛ ba kpa hin a la koor Siiza wati o di i sɛria. Mu zi nyɛ ba kpa hin a la o watiu.” Fɛstus Fun Ŋɔ Agripa Awa O Haan Ni Pɔɔl Wɛ

ref: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
text: Wuzu bilɛ gantɛl ni, koor naa ba yir Agripa awa o haan naa ba yir Bɛrnisi, fun wa Siizaria di ba zaami Fɛstus.

ref: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn doon tama, Fɛstus fun ŋɔ koor ti Pɔɔl wɛ. O fun ŋɔ, “Saha naa Filiks fun ya gɔbnati nyuhu tiin zen bar nɔ chen, o fun kpak baal naa ba yir Pɔɔl chen a tɔ un dɛ.

ref: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
text: Saha naa mu fun te la Jɛrusalɛm, Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta awa tɔhɔn ti nihɛɛti ta fun fiksi un mu suwachak ni a sumi min di mu na o galimsa.

ref: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
text: Mu fun ŋɔ ba di Roman tima wara di nar sɛria hali o wa na nyinbaan a kok o titi a li ba naa la fiksi un chen suwachak ni.

ref: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
text: Ba naa funɛ fiksi un fun gbaari min ni a wa bar nɔ, mu wa fun vusi chen yaali ni. Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, mu fun la kani sɛria bar diili dɛ ti ni a ŋɔ ba kpa Pɔɔl a wa mu wati.

ref: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
text: Ju tim ta fun vaan chiŋi di ba fiksi un. Tum tagna naa ba fun ŋɔ min di o tumu chen, wa ya chenani mu funɛ lisi.

ref: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
text: Mu wa vaan zim chenani ba sɛm chen mun. Chendaha mu fun pɛɛsi Pɔɔl di yɛrɛ o lisi mu kpa un a la Jɛrusalɛm a la di o sɛria dooni.

ref: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
text: Pɔɔl fun vaan sumi min di mu nyɛ ba kpa un a la koor Siiza nuha tiina wati o di o sɛria. Mu fun la nɔɔni a ŋɔ mu lanjimasa ta ni di ba il un hali saha naa mu kaa wa tum un.”

ref: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
text: Fɛstus funɛ sɛm peti, Agripa fun ŋɔ un, “Mu daŋni n pu baal nɔ a nu chenani o zi sɛm.” Fɛstus fun ŋɔ un, “Mu chɛ zi nyɛ ba kpa un wau i nu o wati.”

ref: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, Agripa awa o haan, Bɛrnisi, lanjimasa nihɛɛ nyuhu tima, awa tɔhɔn ti nibin birige fun si lagmi a la zu bol zen. Zɛmɛ fun lagmi a lɔ boola a la gɔ di ba nyɛ Agripa awa o haan ti jirima. Ba maga funɛ wa gil chɛɛma, Festus fun ŋɔ ba kpa Pɔɔl a wa.

ref: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
text: Fɛstus fun ŋɔ, “Koor Agripa awa ba naa maga lagmi ya wati bar nɔ. Ma na baal nɔ chiŋɛ ya suwachak ni nan. Ju tima ba naa maga nyɛn bar nɔ awa Jɛrusalɛm ni daŋni mu na o galimsau a di o sou sɛria. Ba fɔuli di o ta bu nyɛn vusi.

ref: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
text: Mu zaamsɛ unu a na di o wa tum tum tagin digi. Chendaha mu wa koti a di o sou sɛria. O funɛ sumi minu di mu nyɛ ba kpa un a la koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza wati o di o sɛria. Mu fun la nɔɔni a nyɛ o kaali.

ref: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
text: Mu zi sɔbi tɔnu a ŋɔ Siiza ni o wɛ, amaa mu si ha wa su chenani mu zi sɔbi. Chendaha mu kpa un wa ma maga wati zinan, chen ni i waa, koor Agripa, zi di o sɛriau a na wu digi a nyɛ min mu du mu tɔn ti ni.

ref: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
text: Mu wa lisi di o deŋliu wa mu tum Pɔɔl Room ni a vɛ sɔbi tɔn a biksi chenani o tumu a gbaari un.”

ref: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
text: “Koor Agripa, mu tuha sɔɔni di mu kok mu titi i suwachak ni a li chenani maga Ju tim ta fiksi min chen zinan ni.

ref: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
text: I zimu nɛnɛ chenani Ju tim ta nyɛn awa chenani ba yaa lagmi dɔŋ ni a sɛm ba amɔrɛ sɛma. N sumi hinu di i kpak kanyita a kinu mu wati.

ref: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
text: Ba zimu di mu ya Farisi tim nidigirɛ a li o piili ni. Ba waa kpak si sɛm a jɛɛmi Koosa a kaali kinkin tima. Ju tima ba naa nyɛn bar nɔ daŋni, ba zi ŋɔ hinu di chenani mu sɛm chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
text: Ya buuri nara fidalɛ ba naa maga yaa jɛɛmi Koosa, kpak bambi bɛgimu a li vɔwi ti ni. Koor Agripa, Ju tim ta fiksi minu i suwachak ni zinan, domi mu gbanko kpak bambi bɛgimnɛ un ni.

ref: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
text: Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta funɛ nyɛ min kpok di mu kpaksi Koosa nara ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni, nidamata a tɔ ba dɛ. Ba funɛ di ba sou sɛria, mu gbanko fun la nɔɔni a nyɛ ba.

ref: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
text: Mu fun gba a go Ju tim ta bar lagmi dɛɛna a lɛrgi Koosa nara ba naa nyɛn dooni, diŋsa a kpaŋi mu titi di mu nyɛ ba bur a li Yesu nyinbaan tiŋi ni. Ba fun nyɛ mu paan si kinkan ka mu kaali tɔ bar bole di mu la nyɛ ba namsi dooni.’ ” Pɔɔl Fun Ŋɔ Agripa Chenani O Si Su Yesu (Tuma

ref: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
text: Koor Agripa, nha funɛ wa tel sal baan nyuha, mu funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a wa dugli Damaskus. Kundɔŋ fun vaan nyiksi a li buudaha ni a chaani du mu wa ba naa la val chen ni. O funɛ chaaniu a kaali nha.

ref: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
text: Ya maga fun chesi tɛɛnɛ ni. Mu fun nu ta ka o ŋɔ min a li Ju tim ta sɛm ni, ‘Sɔɔl, baŋnɛ ii namsi min chen ni? I nyɛ i titi dɛŋarɛ a chi nuwal naa yaa galimi a pa o kunyan chen.’

ref: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
text: Mu fun pɛɛsi un, ‘Mu kunyana, anti ya hin?’ O fun ŋɔ min, ‘Min ya Yesu naa i namsi chen.

ref: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
text: Si a chiŋi. Mu wa i watiu di mu wa lisi hin i ya mu tumtumna. Mu daŋni i la ŋɔ nara niu chenani i wa min pu dɔŋa zinan awa kin nawara mu zi biksi hin.

ref: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
text: Mu zi tim hin ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima watiu a yisi hin a li ba wa Ju tim ta nisa ni.

ref: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
text: Mu funɛ te biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ Damaskus tim ni a zi kaali Jɛrusalɛm awa Judia pulum awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima a la biksi doonma ni. Mu fun biksi ba di o lawa ba gil ba tum tagnau a bur tiŋ Koosa a tum tum were di ba biksi nara chenani ba tum.

ref: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
text: Mu biksi nɔ daha, Ju tim ta fun kpak min a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni a daŋni ba kpu min.

ref: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a ŋɔ di nara wa kpu un, Koosa zi lɛli unu a li o sou ni, chen ni Ju tim ta awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima zi na nyinbaanu a gil ba tum tagna a bur a tiŋ un. Nan chi fitinne a chaani ba ni a li birsi ni.”

ref: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
text: Pɔɔl funɛ kok o titi chene, Fɛstus fun teŋ o nɔ a tɛsi a ŋɔ, “Pɔɔl, i zima kinkan nyɛ i zu galɛŋa.”

ref: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ un, “Festus naa nara nyɛ jirima, mu wara zu galɛŋa. Mu kpak alaafɛu ka mu sɛma ya wuti.”

ref: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
text: Pɔɔl fun burgi a ŋɔ koor Agripa ni, “Koor Agripa, i zim chenani mu sɛm chen munu. Chendaha mu kpak bambi a sɛm hin. Nara fu zi ŋɔ hin kin nama wɛu, domi a wa funɛ sogli a ya.

ref: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
text: Koor Agripa, mu zim di i si su chenani Koosa witinsi ta fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chenu.”

ref: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
text: Agripa fun ŋɔ un, “Mu lisi di sɛm tam nɔ i sɛm min nan i daŋni mu ya Yesu nitiŋirɛ.”

ref: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
text: Pɔɔl fun ŋɔ, “Mu na nyinbaan a sɛm sɛm tama koo sɛm damata, mu zi sumi Koosau di i wa ba naa maga nyɛn bar nɔ nan zi burgi Yesu nitiŋɛu a chi chenani mu ya Yesu nitiŋi, amaa mu wa zi sumi un di ba tɔ ma dɛ a chi chenani ba fun tɔ min dɛ chen.”

ref: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
text: Doone Agripa awa o haan, Bɛrnisi, awa Fɛstus fun si li. Ba funɛ li peti chene ka nara tima maga pisi.

ref: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
text: Nibin birige nara batora ta funɛ li sal, ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Pɔɔl ha wa tum tum tagin naa la zi nyɛ ba tɔ un dɛ koo a di o sou sɛria.”

ref: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
text: Agripa fun ŋɔ Fɛstus ni, “O fu vɛ sumi hin di i nyɛ ba kpa un la koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza wati o di un sɛria, i fu zi nyɛ o lau.”

ref: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
text: Ya fun la zu arŋa naa la daŋni o si a la Azia pulum ni nɔ a kaali. O funɛ li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Adramitiumnɛ. Baal naa ba funɛ yir Aristakus funɛ gbaari ya ni. O funɛ li Tɛsalɔnika naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum nɛɛ.

ref: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, ya fun la tel Saidɔn. Julius funɛ ya nibin werire Pɔɔl wati a nyɛ un nyinbaan di o gil arŋ ti a la zaami o chɛna, chen ni ba zi nyɛ un kin nawara o daŋniu.

ref: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
text: Pɔɔl fun bur wa zu arŋ ti ka ya burgi a kaali. Peha funɛ mo ya suwachak ni, ya fun val Saiprus pulum ni nɔ a la doonani pehe ti vɛ vali.

ref: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
text: Ya fun val la li ni naa chɔ gbampulim ni a la tel Sailisia pulum awa Pamfilia pulum ni nɔ a la li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Maira a li Lisia pulum ni.

ref: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
text: Doonee lanjimasa nihɛɛ ti fun na arŋa a li Alɛzandria ni ka o saksi di o la Itali pulum. O fun bur la zu arŋa naa kpa ya li Siizaria a wa lisi ya a bur du arŋ digi ni.

ref: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
text: Ya fun li dooni a val tama wuzu bilɛ, domi pehe ti wa funɛ suŋi ya. Chendaha o funɛ bɔm ya niu di ya la dugli tɔhɔn naa ba yir Sinidus. Ya wa fun bu koti a la suwachak tama a burgi a la gali Krit pulum zuuli bar digi doonani ba yir Salmone.

ref: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
text: Ya fun val a la dugli ni nɔ ka o fun bɔm ya ni di ya tel doonani ba yir, bar hiisi weri. O wa tɔhɔn naa ba yir Lasɛa chen, wa bol dɔŋ ni.

ref: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
text: “Mu nivalɛ dɔŋtima, mu zimu di ya chiŋɛ bar nɔ aa la, o zi tagini ya niu. Bun nawara arŋ ti chuŋi chen zi wa chɔku ka ya bɔnɛ siti.”

ref: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
text: Fufuk saha funɛ dugliu. Doonani arŋ ti fun chiŋi chen wa fun wer di fufuk saha wa pu un dooni. Niduse ta nidamata funɛ daŋni ba li doon a kpaŋi a tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Finiks chen. O funɛ chem nha bar chelire. Doon funɛ ya bar weri di arŋsa chem fufuk saha. Chenani Peha Fun Teŋ Ni Kinkan A Du Pɔɔl Ni

ref: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
text: Wuzu digi peha naa vɛ kpoki fun ya a li pul mun. Niduse ta fun lisi di ba si awa arŋ ti ni a la a lisi lugɛ yuga naa kpak arŋ ti o vɛ laali a li ni ti ni. Ya fun si dooni a val a dugli Krit pulum ni nɔ.

ref: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
text: O funɛ mo arŋ ti a teŋ ni ti kinkan. Niduse ta fun luk a ku chenani ba zi ya o deŋli. Pehe ti fun nyɛ arŋ ti la doonani ba vɛ daŋni o laali.

ref: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
text: Ya fun la gali pulum we naa ni go chen ya nindil ni ka pehe ti fun baligi a bu si. Ba funɛ yir dooni Kɔdɔ. O funɛ bɔmu kinkan ya wati ka ya kpak arŋ we naa kizen ti lɛrgi chen.

ref: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
text: Ya fun lisi un a nyɛ o zɔŋ arŋ zen ti buudaha ni a kpa ŋmɛnsa a vɔu a a go a mari arŋ zen ti, chen ni ni naa pehe ti teŋ wa zi chɔk un. Hansura fun kpak niduse ta di arŋ ti zi la fir hɛchɛ naa duglɛ Libia pulum ni nɔ niu. Ba fun pur gata naa yaa suŋi arŋ ti o val kinkan chen, chen ni arŋ ti wa zi val kinkan.

ref: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
text: Pe kpok naa funɛ teŋ ni ti kinkan chen fun ya hali dusum maga. Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi, niduse ta fun luksi bun ta bɔnɛ a yo du ni ti ni.

ref: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
text: Chɔɔsa fun bu wa pisi, ba fun pirigi kin nawara suŋi arŋ ti o la chen, bɔnɛ a du ni ti ni.

ref: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
text: Pe kpok ti funɛ ya chene wuzu damata. Dobaala fun tɔ buudaha ya vɛ na nha koo wula. Ya lɔk fun wa li peti, ya maga funɛ lisi di ya kaa ya sitiu.

ref: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
text: Nandigi wa fun koti a ya diwe a di a vusi. Chen saha ti Pɔɔl fun si chiŋi ya maga suwachak ni a ŋɔ, “Mu dɔŋtima, ma fu nu mu wati a nyɛn Krit pulum ni, ma fu wa zi namsi nan a nyuksi ma kin ta maga.

ref: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
text: Mu sumi mau di ma kpak ma bambe. Arŋ ti zi wa chɔku, amaa nandigi wa zi wa sou.

ref: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
text: Dusum ti Koosa naa dɔ min awa naa mu jɛɛmi, fun tum o malaikasi ta digi o wa ŋɔ min,

ref: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
text: ‘Pɔɔl, ta ɔmi. Koosa zi nyɛ i la koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza watiu o di i sɛria. O ya niweri daha, o wa zi nyɛ hin koo ba naa wa hin lagmi nyɛn nan nidigi sou.’

ref: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
text: Mu dɔŋtima, chendaha mu ŋɔ ma di ma kpak ma bambe. Mu si su di Koosa zi tum chenani o ŋɔ minu.

ref: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
text: O wa zi vusi ka pehe ti kizak ya a la li pulum naa ni go chen nɔ.”

ref: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
text: Lugɛ yuga a naa yaa nyɛ arŋ ti chiŋi, ha funɛ vɛɛn vɔti arŋ tiu. Niduse ta fun la teŋsi lugɛ yuga ŋmɛnsi ta a ta a pur kinkin naa yaa nyɛ arŋ ti deŋli aa val. Ba fun kpa gata naa la suŋi arŋ ti o val kinkan a tɛ buudaha pehe ti mo un a la dugli ni ti nɔ.

ref: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
text: Doone ba fun kpa arŋ we ti a wa mari arŋ zen ti chakpal ni ti ni di ba zu a ŋɔ di ba kaa la zer lugɛ yuk kinkin tawa a la du ni ti ni arŋ ti suwachak ni.

ref: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
text: Hansura fun kpak niduse ta di arŋ ti zi la mo gbɛntɛlau. Ba fun kpa lugɛ yuga a naa yaa nyɛ arŋ ti chiŋi, a banaasi a kaali arŋ ti gantɛl a la du ni ti ni a tu kaali ni mun a funɛ pɔ di chɔɔsa pisi.

ref: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
text: Pɔɔl funɛ na ka niduse tima daŋni ba gil arŋ ti ta a ta bu bur laali, o fun ŋɔ lanjimasi ta awa ba nihɛɛ ti ni, “Ma ta nyɛ niduse tima kaali a ka ya. Ba kaalɛɛ, ya maga zi sitiu.”

ref: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
text: Lanjimasa nihɛɛ ti fun ŋɔ o lanjimasi ta ni ba teŋsi ŋmɛnsa a naa vɔu arŋ we ti a mari arŋ zen ti chakpal ni a waligi dɔŋ ni.

ref: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
text: Mu sumi mau di ma di diwe, chen di arŋ ti wa chɔk, ya zi kotiu a li awa ya miisi ni. Mu si su di ya nidigi wa zi huusi.”

ref: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm peti, o fun zer boroboro a sumi Koosa a du a chɔk se.

ref: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
text: Nan funɛ kpami kinkin tima ba gbanko di diwe.

ref: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
text: Ya maga funɛ di puŋ, niduse ta fun luksi bun nawara kaɛɛ a ya mɛ chen a du ni ti ni, ka arŋ ti fuŋsi a zɔŋ ni daha ni. Pɔɔl Arŋ Zuuli Fun Mo Ni Nɔ A Chɔk

ref: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
text: Chɔɔsa funɛ wa pisi peti, ya maga fun koti a na jibaŋ, amaa niduse ta wa fun zim doonani ya nyɛn. Ba fun na ni ti nazaŋa ka hɛchɛ kinkan nyɛn dooni. Ba fun lisi di ba zi nyɛ arŋ ti val hɛchɛ ti dahau dooni.

ref: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn ni ti ni a la chene, arŋ ti fun vaan la zini hɛchɛ a li ni ti ni. Arŋ ti suwachak fun la kak ka ni naa pehe ti teŋ kinkan chen funɛ mo arŋ ti mun o kpɛligi.

ref: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
text: Lanjimasi ta fun lisi di ba kpu ba naa maga ba il chen, chen ni ba wa zi la tu ni ti a di a chɔ kaali.

ref: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
text: Lanjimasi ta nihɛɛ ti wa fun nyɛ lanjimasi ta kpu ba, domi o wa funɛ daŋni Pɔɔl sou. O fun purigi ya maga chen bilɛ. O fun ŋɔ ba naa zim ni diili chen ba te tu ni ti a di a la zini jibaŋ,

ref: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
text: a ka fun ŋɔ ba naa vɛ zim ni diili chen ba tu ni ti a kpaksi arŋ kpɛligi ta a kpa ba a la tel jibaŋ ti. Ya maga fun ya chen a fosi ni ti ni.

ref: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn doon ka ni ti go ba chen, fun ŋɔ ya di doon sɔn ya Mɔltɔ.

ref: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
text: Ba funɛ ya nibin werewa ya wati a la ya a du. Doon funɛ mi ka wa nyɛn. Chendaha ba fun biksi din di ya weeri.

ref: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
text: Pɔɔl funɛ lagmi daasa a du din ti ni chene, dum fun li dooni a gasi o nin. Din ti nunum funɛ nyɛ dum ti li.

ref: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn pulum ti ni fun na dum ti ka o gasi Pɔɔl nin ti. Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Baal nɔ funɛ kpu nandigirɛ. Hali ni ti vɛ di un chen, amaa lalaknɔwa o na o nyɔɔra a zi sou.”

ref: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
text: Pɔɔl fun misi dum ti o chel din ti ni, ka o vɛ ya un wu digi.

ref: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
text: Ba funɛ nyini un a la pɔ di o fɔɔsi a chel tɛɛnɛ ni a sou. Ba fun nyini a vusi a na di wu digi wa ya un. Ba fun chɛrgi ba bambe a li chenani ba fun te ŋɔ di o kpu nandigirɛ chen a ŋɔ di Koosa funɛ kok un.

ref: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
text: Ya wa pulum ti koor bar nyɛni wa fun bol dɔŋ ni. O sɔn funɛ ya Publius. O fun la ya a du ya nyɛn o wati wuzu butora.

ref: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
text: Kabilima funɛ kpak Publius nyana ka o chaari a chɔ o kichɔɔli ni. Pɔɔl fun nu o wichɔɔrti ti wɛ a fun la zu o dɛ chɔɔli ti a sumi Koosa a nyɛ un a kpa o nisa a sak un ni a tiibi un.

ref: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
text: Chenani o fun tum chen laaboora mɔɔli pulum naa ni go chen maga. Ba naa maga funɛ wi a nyɛn dooni fun wa Pɔɔl wati o tiibi ba.

ref: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
text: Alɛzandria tima arŋa funɛ nyɛn doone fufuk saha ti. Ba funɛ yir un, ‘vursa a naa ya leusisa’. Ya fun la zu un a kaali.

ref: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
text: Ya fun la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Sirakus a nyɛn dooni wuzu butora.

ref: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn dooni, fun nyɛ ya wa nyɛn ba wati. Wuzu bunɔpɛ gantɛl ni, ya fun li dooni a kaali Room.

ref: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn dooni, fun val nyinbaan ziŋi a wa chem ya. Bɔnɛ fun chem ya a li yawa naa ba yir Apius.
