Ba wa ya fun val tama ka ba naa tiŋ ba gantɛl a wa chen, fun chem ya a li hɔɔra bar chɔɔli damsi butora. Pɔɔl funɛ na ba chen, o kpami un o zaami Koosa chen daha. Chenani Pɔɔl Fun Na Room Ni

ref: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
text: Ya funɛ la tel Room, ba fun nyɛ Pɔɔl nyɛn o duŋduŋ ka lanjima il un.

ref: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
text: Wuzu butora gantɛl, o fun yir Ju tima ba naa nyɛn dooni, nihɛɛta ba lagmi a wa o wati. O fun ŋɔ ba, “Mu niinbisa, mu wa fun tum tum tagin a du ya walɛ ni. Mu ka wa fun vɛɛn biksi nara di ba ta bu tiŋ ya nawaalsi ta nyinbaan. Hali nan daha, ba fun kpak min a tɔ dɛ a li Jɛrusalɛm ni a kpa min a du Roman tim nisa ni.

ref: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
text: Ba fun di mu sɛria a na di mu wa ya wi naa la zi nyɛ mu sou a la daŋni ba lisi min.

ref: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
text: Ya Ju tima kpak bambi bɛgimu di Koosa zi tum naa o fun vɔu di o tumu ba wati wuzu digi. Roman tim ta funɛ kpa zɔgliu a vɔu mu nisa a nyɛ lanjima nɔ il min domi naa ya nara kpak bambi bɛgim a nyɛ chen daha. Chendaha mu yir ma di ma wa min na dɔŋa.”

ref: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
text: Ju tima ba naa funɛ nyɛn Room ni, fun ŋɔ un, “Ya niinbisa ba naa nyɛn Judia pulum ni, digi wa to a sɔbi i wɛ a du tɔn ni a nyɛ ya. Nandigi wa gba a li dooni a wa ŋɔ ya i laaboora koo a chɔk i sɔn ya wati.

ref: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
text: Ya zimu di ba naa nyɛn bawu maga ni ka ba sɛm sɛm tagna a du ba naa wa hin la tum tum ni. Chendaha ya daŋni ya nu chenani i lisi.”

ref: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
text: Ba fun la nɔɔni di ba wa Pɔɔl zi bu lagmiu. A wuzu ti fun wa tel, ba fun i a gbaari a wa doonani o nyɛn chen. A piili chɔɔsa hali nha wa chel, o fun biksi ba di saha naa Koosa zi wa dɔ nara telu mun. O fun karimi Koosa sɛm tɔn ti bar digi a li chiksɛ naa o fun lisi a nyɛ Mosis ni chen awa sɛm naa Koosa witinsi ta fun sɔbi di o biksi ba chenani o fun ŋɔ Yesu wɛ chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
text: Ba bɔnɛ fun nyɛ ba se su o biksi ti. Ba bɔnɛ ka wa fun nyɛ ba se su un.

ref: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
text: O fun sɛm di Koosa ŋɔ un, ‘La ŋɔ Ju tim ta ni, ba kpak seu a vɛ naali. Mu ŋɔ bau, ba ka wa zim mu sɛm ta mun.

ref: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
text: Nara nama bambe situ. Ba tɔ ba diŋsiwa awa ba se gbanko. O vɛ ya chen, ba bambe fu zi lisiu ka ba nu mu sɛm ta a koti a na. Ba bambe zi chɛrgiu, chen ni mu gil ba tum tagna a nyɛ ba.’ ”

ref: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
text: Pɔɔl funɛ sɛm chen Ju tim ta funɛ di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ a ta.))

ref: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
text: Pɔɔl fun la nyɛn nandigi dam ni a la bɔ un dam ti nyuhu ni bunsa lɛ. O fun la ba naa maga wa di ba zaami un a du.

ref: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
text: O fun kpak bambi a biksi ba di saha naa Koosa kaa wa dɔ nara chen telu awa ya kunyana Yesu Kristo wɛ. Nandigi wa fun to a tɔ un nyinbaan di o ta sɛmi.

ref: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
text: Mu waa, Pɔɔl, naa ya Yesu Kristo tumtumna, min la sɔbi tɔn nɔ. Koosa fun a yir min a lisi min di mu ya o nitumi a la ŋɔ nara ni o laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
text: Laaboor weri nɔ ya kin nawara o fun vɔu saha digi chen a fun nyɛ o witinsi ta sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni.

ref: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
text: amaa Koosa funɛ kpa o kpok zen ti a lɛli un a li o sou ni di o biksi ya di o gbanko ya o bere.

ref: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
text: Yesu Kristo fun a suŋi min di mu ya o nitumi aa la sɛm tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ni a nyɛ un, chen ni ba se zi su unu ka baa tiŋ o nɔ.

ref: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
text: Ma gbanko funɛ gbaari ba niu. Koosa fun a yir ma di Yesu Kristo dɔ ma.

ref: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
text: Mu zaami ma naa maga Koosa daŋni a fun lisi a nyɛ o titi niu a li Room ni. Ya kunyana Koosa awa ya kunyana Yesu Kristo suŋi ma a kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Pɔɔl Fun Zaami Koosa Ba Daha

ref: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
text: Mu daŋni n te ŋɔ di Yesu Kristo kpa chenani mu kunyana Koosa ma maga daha a nyɛ un. Nara sɛmu bawu maga ni chenani ma se su Yesu Kristo.

ref: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
text: Koosa naa muu tum a nyɛ awa mu bambi maga ni a la biksi o laaboor weri ti o be wɛ a nyɛ nara ni, waa ya mu sɛhɛra tiina di mu yaa sumi unu ma daha,

ref: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
text: di o ya o tutɔ, o zi nyɛ min nyinbaanu wa ma wati.

ref: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
text: Mu daŋni mu na mau kinkan, chen ni mu wa ma zi kotiu a bɔni Koosa siim weri ti papaha bɛgim di ma kpak nɛnɛ.

ref: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
text: Chen yaali ni, ya maga zi kotiu a suŋi dɔŋa. Chenani mu se su Koosa zi kpami mau. Chenani ma gbanko se su un zi kpami minu.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni ma zimu di mu funɛ daŋniu mu wa ma wati bu damata, amaa mu wa fun koti a wa. Mu daŋni mu lisi narawa ma wati a nyɛ Koosa ni a chi mu funɛ lisi ba a li ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim wati bawu maga ni.

ref: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
text: Chendaha mu kpak un mu bambi ni di mu biksi un a nyɛ ma naa nyɛn Room gbanko ni. Koosa Fun Kpa O Kpok Ti A Du O Laaboor Weri Ni

ref: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
text: O waa na paan a du ba naa vɛ o nɔ a la tum tum tagna a zi lɛrgi ba diŋsa. Ba tum tagna daha, ba kpa o wuti ti a ta.

ref: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
text: Kin nawara nyɛ nibinsa zim Koosa nyɛn gbampulim ni. Koosa titi funɛ kpa a a li chen.

ref: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
text: Saha naa Koosa fun naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga hali wa zinan, ba kotiu a na chenani o bambi du a li kin nawara o naami ni. Ba waa funɛ zim o kpok naa vɛ kpak bɔ awa chenani o ya Koosa. O fun kpa kin nama a li gbampulim, chen ni ba wa zi koti a sɛm sɛma.

ref: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
text: Ba fun koti a zim Koosa, amaa ba wa la nɔɔni di ba zaami un a nyɛ un jirima. Ba fun burgi womna a la lisi lisi yɔrisa. Ba se wa fun lɛli.

ref: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
text: Ba fun a yir ba titi ninɛɛgɛ, amaa ba funɛ burgi womniwa.

ref: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
text: Ba fun a nyɛ kin nawara chi nibinsa awa zimebisa awa sela awa kipalɛ jirima a kpa a chiŋsi Koosa bar jɛɛmi bar chiŋsi ni. Waa nyɛnɛɛ hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
text: Chendaha Koosa fun kpa ba a ta di ba tiŋ ba titi daŋnɛ a tum tum tagna a tum dɔŋa aa du dɔŋ ni.

ref: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
text: Ba fun a piŋi Koosa wuti a la nusuna a la nyɛ kin nawara o fun naami jirima a kaali o titi naa naami a. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
text: Koosa fun kpa ba a ta di ba tiŋ ba titi daŋnɛ naa nyɛ ba na hiisa. Hali ba nihaana fun vɛ di ba wa nibaala wa zi chɔ aa la chɔ dɔŋa.

ref: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
text: Nibaala gbanko ka fun vɛ di ba wa nihaana wa zi chɔ a la chɔ dɔŋa. Chenani ba funɛ tum nyɛ ba na hiisa kinkan. Ba tum tagna zi buru a di ba.

ref: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
text: Ba wa fun lisi di o deŋlu di ba zim Koosa. Chendaha o fun kpa ba a ta a nyɛ ba burgi nara a lisi wu tagna a la tum tum tagna a naa vɛ deŋli,

ref: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
text: a la tum tum tagna damata a ya se tima, a kpak handɔta wa dɔŋa a la kpu nara a la yo dɔŋa la mur ba dɔŋtima a ha dɔŋa kinkan, a la lɔ nara gantɛl,

ref: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
text: a la chɔk nara sɔna a la pini nara zoma aa ha Koosa. Baa daŋni sɔniwa a la zer ba titi a la nyini tum tagna falɛ di ba tum saha maga. Ba wa tiŋ ba nyama awa ba numa nɔɔna,

ref: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
text: a wa kpak nɛha aa vɛ kpak se suuli. Hali ba wara daŋni ba dam walɛ a vɛ kpak nara siki.

ref: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
text: Ba zimu di Koosa funɛ ŋɔ di o deŋlu wa ba naa la tum nan sitiu, amaa ba ha vɛɛn a tum au a ŋɔ di ba naa la tum chen tum tum werewa.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: I dɔŋtiina tum tum tagna ka i di un sɛria, i wa zi koti a ŋɔ wu digi. I tum galimsau a du i titi ni. Tum tagna naa i fiksi un chen, ii gbanko tum chene vɛɛna.

ref: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
text: Ya zimu di o deŋlu wa Koosa di ba naa la tum nan sɛria.

ref: Do you really think God won't punish you, when you behave exactly like the people you accuse?
text: I di chenani i dɔŋtiina tum sɛria chen, i zi koti a chɔ li Koosa sɛria diili ti niuu?

ref: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
text: I baa di chenani Koosa ya niwerire kinkan i wati a kpak hin wa kanyita, chen ya yɔrirɛɛ? I wa zim di Koosa ya niwerire kinkan i wati, chen i zi gil i tum tagna a bur tiŋ unn?

ref: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
text: Amaa i si kpoku kinkan. I tuha wa zi chɔk i tum tagna daha. I tum tum tagniwa aa chiŋsi. Koosa wuzu wa tel di o wa di hin sɛria wa wuti, o zi na paanu kinkan a du hin ni a lɛrgi i diŋsa a gbaari.

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: Chen wuzu ti o zi wa bɔ nara maga wa ba chimu a li chenani ba tum ni.

ref: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
text: Ba naa maga la tum tum were wa kanyita saha maga, chen ni ba zi wa la jirima awa chaani kinkan awa miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔu Koosa wati, Koosa zi kpa kin namau a nyɛ ba.

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: Amaa ba naa ka kpak se a la vɛ di ba tiŋ wuti nyinbaan a la tum tum tagna saha maga, Koosa zi na paanu kinkan a du ba ni a lɛrgi ba diŋsa.

ref: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Muksi awa wiili zi wa pu ba naa maga la tum tum tagnau. A zi wa te pu ba naa ya Ju tima a zi pu ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima.

ref: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: Ba naa ka la tum tum were zi wa la jirima awa chaani kinkan awa bara sɔɔnau. Ba naa ya Ju tima zi wa te la ka ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ka zi la.

ref: God doesn't have any favorites!
text: Koosa wara daŋni bɔnɛ a kaali bɔnɛ.

ref: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
text: Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima wa la Koosa chiksɛ ta a la tum tum tagna, Koosa wa zi wa kpa o chiksɛ ta a wa di ba sɛria. Amaa Ju tima ba naa la o chiksɛ ta, chiksɛ tawa Koosa zi wa kpa a wa di ba sɛria.

ref: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
text: Ba tuma biksɛɛ di Koosa funɛ sɔbi o chiksɛu ba bambe ni. Ba bambe lise gbanko di ba sɛria a ŋɔ ba di ba galimu akoo ba wa galimi.

ref: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
text: Ma naa ba yir Ju tima, maa nyini Koosa naa ya ma jirima tiina, chiksɛ ta se ni,

ref: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
text: aa zim o tutɔ awa kin naa weree, domi ba funɛ biksi o chiksɛ tau a nyɛ ma.

ref: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
text: Ma kpak bambeu di ma koti a ya nyuhu tima a la biksi ba naa nyuksɛ nyinbaan a vɛ zim Koosa,

ref: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
text: aa ŋɔ di ma koti a biksi ba naa ya womniwa awa ba naa ya bichɛsa. Ma kpak bambeu di ma kpak zima awa wuti maga hali a wa peti a li o chiksɛ ta ni.

ref: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
text: Ma naa la biksi nara, yɛrɛ ma vɛ biksi ma titi? Ma ŋɔ di ba ta ŋmɛɛli a la ŋmɛ.

ref: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
text: Ma ŋɔu di ba ta tum chɔkumsa tuma a la tum chɔkumsa tuma. Ma ŋɔu di ba nyɛ Koosa jirima a vɛ ya chen ma titi.

ref: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
text: Koosa chiksɛ ta ya ma jirima kina ka maa chɔk a a du un hiisa.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
text: Ba funɛ a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ba naa vɛ zim Koosa chɔk o sɔnnɛ ma daha.”

ref: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
text: Ba teŋ ma ka maa tiŋ o chiksɛ ta gbanko, ma teŋi nyɛ ma na nyɔɔrau. Amaa maa chɔk o chiksɛ ta, ma burgi a chi ba naa vɛ teŋsire.

ref: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
text: Nara vɛ teŋsi aa tiŋ o chiksɛ ta, Koosa zi nyini bau a chi ba wa teŋsi.

ref: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
text: Chendaha ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nyɛ ma na galimsa, domi nara funɛ sɔbi Koosa chiksɛ tau a nyɛ ma aa teŋsi ma, amaa maa chɔk au. Ba ka tiŋ o chiksɛ tau a vɛ teŋsi.

ref: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
text: Nibine bara teŋee, chen wa biksi di o ya Ju tim nidigirɛ wuti. Teŋi naa Koosa baga nyɛn, o wa ya bara teŋsire.

ref: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
text: O siim weri ti wa nyɛn nibine bambi ni, chen nyɛ o ya Ju tim nidigi wuti. Koosa chiksɛ ta wa koti a tum chen. Nibinsa wa zi pɛgi naa kpak Koosa siim weri ti. Koosa ka la zi pɛgi tiina.

ref: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
text: O ya chen ni, yɛrɛ Ju tim ta kpak nyusum a kaali ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima? Baŋ nyɔɔrrɛ ba na a li ba teŋsi ti ni?

ref: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
text: Ju tim ta kpak nyusumu a kaali ba a li wu damata ni. Wu birigi naa Koosa fun biksi o sɛma a du Ju tim ta nin ni.

ref: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
text: Aai. Nara maga ya nusun tima, Koosa sɛm ta ka zi ya wutiu saha maga. Ba fun a sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ba zi ŋɔ di i sɛm ta ya wutiu. Ba wa di i sɛria, i zi na nyusumu a li bar diili dɛ ti ni.”

ref: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
text: Ya tum tagna nyɛ Koosa tum deŋli a li a gbaari, yɛrɛ ya zi sɛm? Ma nyɛ mu kpa nibinsa sɛma a pɛɛsi ma, “O ya chen ni, o lɛrgi ya diŋsa ya tum tagna daha, o tum wu taginuu?”

ref: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
text: Aai. Koosa tum wu tagin, yɛrɛ o ka ya a di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria?

ref: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
text: Ya nusuna nyɛ Koosa wuti li a gbaari, chen ni nibinsa zi pɛgi unu, yɛrɛ o ŋɔ di o zi na ya galimsau a ta ya yɔri ya tum tagna daha?

ref: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
text: O ya chen, ya zi kotiu a ŋɔ ma nyɛ ya tum tum tagna, chen ni tum were zi li a niu. Nara bɔnɛ fun a chɔk mu sɔn a ŋɔ di mu sɛmu, amaa ba sɛma wa fun ya wuti. O deŋlu di Koosa zi na ba galimsau a ta ba yɔri. Nandigi Wa To A Ya Tum Weri Nitumi

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
text: Adaha ya naa ya Ju tima ya ya nibin werewa a kaali ba naa vɛ ya Ju timaa? Aai. N piin biksi mau di ba naa ya Ju tim ta awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima, ba maga ya tum tagna timwa.

ref: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
text: Nandigi wa toli a nɛɛgi. Nandigi wara daŋni o dɛu Koosa.

ref: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
text: Ba maga fun a nyuksi ba nyinbaan a ya yɔri. Nandigi wa toli a tum jirima tuma, hali nibɛgim wa to.

ref: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
text: Ba nɔɔna chi hugilsi naa ba vɛ tɔɔlirɛ. Ba pɛgi nara wa ba nyina nɛɛ a la mur ba. Ba sɛma chi duma sambosiwa.

ref: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
text: Ba ŋɔ nɔrɛ a nyɛ nara ni wa tuha sa ni.

ref: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
text: Chɔki awa muksi pisi ba nyinbaan maga.

ref: They don't know how to live in peace.
text: Ba wa zim bara sɔɔna nyinbaan.

ref: They don't even fear God.”
text: Ba wara nyɛ Koosa jirima.”

ref: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
text: Ya zimu di ba naa la biksi Koosa chiksɛ ta, ba waa la biksi ba naa la tiŋ a, di ba na galimsa. Chendaha nara maga zi wa la nɔɔniu di ba tum galimsau Koosa suwachak ni. Ba wa zi wa koti a sɛm wu digi.

ref: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
text: O chiksɛ ta biksi ya di ya maga ya tum tagna nitumɛwa. Koosa ha zi vɛɛn na ya galimsau o chiksɛ ta tiŋi daha. Chenani Koosa Zi Bu Vɛ Na Nibinsa Galimsa

ref: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
text: Lalaknɔ Koosa fun a biksi ya o nyinbaan chenani o zi bu vɛ na nibinsa galimsa o wati. Chenani o tum chen wa kpak wu digi wa o chiksɛ ta tiŋi ni. O chiksɛ naa ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni awa o witinsa ba naa nyɛn saha digi, funɛ biksi nara o wɛ.

ref: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Koosa wa bu a na ba naa maga se su Yesu Kristo galimsa.

ref: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
text: Nara maga yaa tum tum tagnau a pɔɔgi chenani Koosa daŋni ba du.

ref: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
text: Koosa fun a kpak ya siki a nyɛ Yesu Kristo la ya nyuuna a nyɛ ya. Koosa fun ya chen a nyɛ ya yɔri. Chendaha o bu vɛ na ya galimsa o wati.

ref: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
text: Lalaknɔ o kpak nyinbaan naa o zi bu vɛ na nibinsa galimsau o wati. Nan biksi ya di o ya tum weri tiin naa la zi vɛ na ba naa maga se su Yesu galimsa.

ref: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
text: Ya zi kotiu a pɛgi ya titi domi ya funɛ tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ dahaa? Ya wa kikoti. Ya zi koti a pɛgi ya titi kundɔŋ wɛuu? Aai. Koosa wa zi bu na ya galimsa a li ya se suuli ni. Chendaha ya vɛ koti a pɛgi ya titi.

ref: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
text: Ya kpak bambiu di nibine se su Kristo, Koosa wa na o galimsa. Chen tiina tiŋ o chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ, Koosa wa zi koti a ya chen.

ref: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
text: Ju tim ta duŋduŋ koti aa tiŋ Koosaa? Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima, ba wa koti aa tiŋ unn? Ba gbanko kotiu.

ref: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
text: Tɔ. Ya se suuli nyɛ Koosa chiksɛ ta ya yɔrii? Aai. Ya chiŋi a gantɛl nɛɛ.

ref: Well then, what can we say about our ancestor Abraham?
text: Ma nyini ya nawaal Abraham tuma.

ref: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
text: Koosa wa fun bu na Abraham galimsa o tum were daha, o fu zi pɛgi o titiu. Amaa o fu wa zi pɛgi o titi Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
text: Nandigi tumɛɛ ka o kunyan bɔ un, chimnɛ o bɔ. O wa ya papaha.

ref: But you cannot make God accept you because of something you do. God accepts sinners only because they have faith in him.
text: Nibine nyɛ o si su Koosa a vɛ nyini o tum weri se ni, Koosa wa zi bu na o galimsa. Waa koti a bu vɛ na tum tagna nitumɛ galimsa.

ref: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
text: Chenani ya nawaal Devid gbanko fun ŋɔ, o mun ya di nibine vɛ nyini o tum were se ni a nyɛ o se su Koosa, o wa zi bu na o galimsa. Chen tiina tuha zi sɔɔniu kinkan.

ref: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
text: Nibinsa ba naa Koosa zi vɛ lisi a bu fiksi ba tum tagna ni, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu.”

ref: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
text: Saha leemawa chen fun ya? Ba funɛ piin teŋ un chen saha tiu akoo ba wa fun teŋ unn? Ba ha wa fun teŋ un.

ref: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
text: Koosa wa fun te na o galimsa o si suuli daha ka ba zi teŋ un. Ba funɛ teŋ un, chen biksi nara di Koosa wa piin na o galimsa o si suuli daha. Chendaha ba naa maga Koosa vɛ na ba galimsa o wati ba se suuli daha, ba waa ya Abraham bi lulɛ, hali ba naa ba vɛ teŋsi.

ref: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
text: Ba naa ba teŋsi nyɛ ba se su Yesu a chi Abraham si funɛ su Koosa chen, ba gbanko ya Abraham bi lulɛwa. O Lawa Ya Gbanko Se Su Koosa A Chi Abraham

ref: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
text: Koosa funɛ vɔu Abraham awa o bi lulɛ di ba zi wa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ magau, o wa fun vɔu chen Abraham chiksɛ tiŋi daha. O si funɛ su Koosa, chen ni o wa fun koti a bu na o galimsa. Chendaha Koosa fun vɔu chen.

ref: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
text: Ba naa tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta zi dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ magau, chenani nibinsa se su Koosa awa o vɔwi ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
text: Koosa na paan a du ba naa la chɔk o chiksɛ ta a wa lɛrgi ba diŋsa. Chiksɛ ta fu vɛ nyɛni, nara fu wa zi koti a chɔk a.

ref: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
text: Koosa vɔwi nyini nara se suuli nee, chen ni Abraham bi lulɛ maga ba naa teŋsee awa ba naa vɛ teŋsi, zi kpak bambiu di Koosa zi tum chenani o fun vɔu. O vɔwi ti ya papaharɛ. Abraham ya ya maga nawaal.

ref: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
text: Ba fun sɔbi chen wɛ a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di Koosa ŋɔ, “Mu funɛ nyɛ i burgi nidamata nyannɛ.” Abraham si fun su Koosa naa koti a lɛli nara a li ba sou ni a sɛm a naami kina. Chendaha Koosa vɔwi ti weru a li o wati.

ref: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
text: Chenani o fun du wa nyɛ Abraham kpak bambi, amaa o kpak bambiu a nyɛ o si su Koosa. Chendaha o fun burgi nibin damata nyana a chi chenani ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di Koosa ŋɔ, “I bi lulɛ zi ya nidamatau.”

ref: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
text: O wa fun a beeli chenani Koosa fun vɔu chen, amaa o si suuli funɛ nyɛ un kpok o nyɛ Koosa jirima.

ref: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
text: O fun kpak bambi kinkan di Koosa zi tum chenani o sɛmu.

ref: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
text: Ba wa fun sɔbi sɛm nama a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a nyɛ Abraham duŋduŋnɛ.

ref: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
text: O funɛ ya ya gbanko daharɛ ba fun sɔbi chen. Ya se su Koosa naa funɛ lɛli ya kunyana Yesu Kristo a li o sou ni. Chendaha o gbanko wa zi bu na ya galimsa.

ref: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ Yesu sou ya tum tagna daha a bu lɛli un a li o sou ni, chen ni o wa zi bu koti a na ya galimsa.

ref: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
text: Lalaknɔ Koosa wa piin na ya galimsa ya se suuli daha. Ya wa un kpak nɔ bɛgimnɛ ya kunyan Yesu Kristo daha.

ref: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
text: Waa funɛ kpa ya a kansi bar zen nɔ ni ya se suuli daha. Waa nyɛ ya kpak bambi bɛgim di wuzu digi Koosa wa ya zi wa bɔni o jirimau. Chendaha ya tusa sɔɔni kinkan.

ref: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
text: Hali nara namsi ya, ya tusa gbanko zi sɔɔniu. Ya zimu di namsi ti nyɛ ya na kanyita.

ref: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
text: Koosa funɛ daŋni ya kinkan a kpa o siim weri ti a du ya ni kinkan. Waa ya o papaha ya wati. Chendaha ya bambi bɛgim naa ya kpak un ni wa zi nyɛ ya buri.

ref: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
text: Nar ya nibin weri, amii nandigi zi la nɔɔniu a chiŋi o bar chiŋi ni a sou a nyɛ un.

ref: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ biksi ya chenani o daŋni ya kinkan. Ya ha funɛ vɛɛn a tum tum tagnau, chen saha ti Kristo funɛ chiŋi ya bar chiŋi ni a sou a nyɛ ya.

ref: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
text: O sou daha, o funɛ nyɛ Koosa wa nyinbaanu di o ta bu na ya galimsa. Chendaha ya koti a kpak bambi kinkan di Kristo zi yisi yau a li Koosa paan ni awa chenani o zi lɛrgi ya diŋsau.

ref: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
text: Ya funɛ ya Koosa dɔmawa, amaa lalaknɔ o be ti nyɛ ya wa Koosa kpak nɔ bɛgim o sou daha. Koosa funɛ lɛli un a li o sou ni. Chendaha ya gbanko kpak bambi di o zi wa yisi yau a li ya tum tagna ni hali o wa peti.

ref: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
text: Chen maga laatanɛ. Lalaknɔ Koosa ya ya jirima tiina ya kunyana Yesu Kristo naa nyɛ ya wa un ya nibɛgim daha. Chenani Yesu Suŋi Ya Kaali Adam Tum Tagna Kinkan

ref: Adam sinned, and that sin brought death into the world. Now everyone has sinned, and so everyone must die.
text: Tɔ. Nibin bɛgim funɛ te nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni saha digi a tum tum tagna. O fun sou a daha. Chendaha ba naa maga funɛ tiŋ o gantɛl gbanko fun siti ba tum tagna daha.

ref: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
text: Nibinsa funɛ te tum tum tagna ka Koosa zi kpa o chiksɛ ta a nyɛ ba. Hali o chiksɛ ta funɛ wa, o wa fun fiksi ba di ba chɔk a,

ref: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
text: amaa saha naa Adam fun nyɛn hali a wa pu Mosis saha ti, nara maga funɛ sitiu. Hali ba naa ha funɛ vɛ tum tum tagin a chi Adam gbanko funɛ sou. Chenani Adam fun tum bu nara maga. Chenani Kristo fun tum bu nara maga gbanko.

ref: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
text: Chenani Koosa fun suŋi ya yɔri, awa chenani Adam tum tum tagna wa ya kibɛgim. Nibin bɛgim tum tagna daha, nidamata fun siti. Nibin bɛgim Yesu Kristo daha, Koosa ka fun koti a suŋi nidamata kinkan yɔri.

ref: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
text: Chenani Adam fun tum tum tagin awa chenani Koosa fun suŋi ya yɔri wa ya kibɛgim. Adam funɛ tum tum tagin, Koosa fun di o sɛria a na o galimsa. Amaa lalaknɔ ya naa funɛ tum tum tagna kinkan, o zi wa di ya sɛriau a bu vɛ na ya galimsa.

ref: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
text: Nibin bɛgim tum tagna daha, nidamata fun siti. Lalaknɔ Koosa koti a suŋi ya kinkan a bu vɛ na ya galimsa a nyɛ ya o mi fali ti Yesu Kristo daha. Chenani Koosa tum kaali nibin bɛgim ti tuma kinkan.

ref: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
text: Adam funɛ tum tum tagin saha digi, Koosa fun na nara maga galimsa a la zi wa ta ba yɔri. Yesu funɛ sou o daakakli ti ni, chen nyɛ Koosa bu vɛ koti a na nara maga galimsa a kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ba.

ref: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
text: Adam funɛ vɛ di o wa zi tiŋ Koosa nɔ, chen funɛ nyɛ nidamata ya tum tagna nitumɛ o wati. Yesu ka funɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔrɛ, chen funɛ nyɛ o bu vɛ koti a na nidamata galimsa.

ref: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o chiksɛ ta a nyɛ Ju tim ta ni, a waa funɛ nyɛ nara tum tum tagna a gbaari kinkan. Ba funɛ tum au a gbaari, chenani Koosa suŋi ba gbanko gbaari a kaali chen.

ref: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
text: Nibinsa tum tagna funɛ nyɛ nara maga siti, amaa lalaknɔ Koosa suŋi yau a bu vɛ na ya galimsa a nyɛ ya o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ ya kunyana Yesu Kristo daha.

ref: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
text: O ya chen, yɛrɛ ya zi sɛm? Ya zi tum tum tagnau a la chen ni Koosa zi suŋi ya a gbaariuu?

ref: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
text: Aai. Ya wa ya tum tagna wa kpak wu digi wa dɔŋ ni, yɛrɛ ya kaa tum tum tagna aa la?

ref: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
text: Mu daŋni ma zimu di ba funɛ chaksi yau wa ni ni, chen ni ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgim, o chi ya wa un siwɛɛ.

ref: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
text: Ba funɛ chaksi ya, o chi ya wa un funɛ sou ka ba sogli ya, ka Koosa fun lɛli ya a li ya sou ni a nyɛ yaa tiŋ o mi fali ti nyinbaan. O funɛ kpa o kpok kinkan tirɛ a lɛli Kristo a li o sou ni. Chendaha o fun koti a ya chen a nyɛ ba.

ref: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
text: Ya wa un funɛ sou, Koosa lɛlɛ ya a nyɛ ya o mi fali ti a chi o funɛ lɛli un.

ref: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
text: Ya zimu di o chi ya gantɛl tuha kina a naa yaa tum tum tagna, wa un funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni, chen ni a wa zi bu kpak kpok a dɔ ya.

ref: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
text: Nibine siwɛɛ, o wa zi bu koti a tum tum tagna.

ref: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
text: Ya wa Kristo funɛ sou, ya se su di ya wa un nyɛnu.

ref: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
text: Koosa funɛ lɛli Kristo a li o sou ni. Ya zimu di o wa zi kut bu sou. Sou wa bu kpak kpok un ni.

ref: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
text: O funɛ sou bu bɛgim duŋduŋnɛ a sou tum tagna wati. Koosa funɛ lɛli un a li o sou ni, chen ni lalaknɔ o nyɛn Koosa watɛɛ.

ref: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
text: Maa zim di ma wa ma tum tagna wa bu kpak wu digi wa dɔŋ ni. Koosa funɛ kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ma, domi ma wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Maa nyɛn a sɔɔni o tuha.

ref: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
text: Ma ta tiŋ ma titi daŋnɛ a nyɛ tum tagna nyaŋi ma.

ref: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
text: Ma ta kpa ma titi a nyɛ tum tagna ni. Ma ka kpa ma titi a nyɛ Koosa ni. O chi ma funɛ sitiu. O fun wa lɛli ma a li ma sou ni a nyɛ ma o mi fali ti. Chendaha ma kpa ma titi a tum tum were aa nyɛ un.

ref: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
text: Koosa chiksɛ ta wa bu dɔ ma. Lalaknɔwa Koosa suŋi ma. Chendaha tum tagna wa zi bu dɔ ma. Koosa Funɛ Nyɛ Ya O Kpok Di Ya Tum Tum Were

ref: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
text: Mu ŋɔɛɛ di chiksɛ ta wa dɔ ma amaa Koosa suŋi mau, o deŋlu wa yaa tum tum tagnaa? Aai.

ref: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
text: Ma wa zim di ma kpa ma titi a nyɛ nandigi ni, ma burgi o yosawaa? Ma kpa ma titi a nyɛ tum tagna ni, Koosa zi ta ma yɔriu. Ma ka kpa ma titi a tiŋ o nɔ, o wa zi bu na ma galimsa.

ref: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
text: Ma nyɛ yaa zaami Koosa. Saha digi ma funɛ kpa ma titirɛ a tum tum tagna saha maga, amaa lalaknɔ maa kpak unu ma bambe ni di ma tiŋ Koosa wuti nyinbaan naa ba fun wa biksi ma chen.

ref: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
text: O bɔmu di ma zim kin nama mun. Chendaha mu ŋɔ ma a li ma nyɛni ni. Saha digi ma yaa fun kpa ma titiu a tum chɔkumsa tuma awa tum tagna bɔnɛ a gbaari. Lalaknɔ ma kpa ma titi aa tum tum were.

ref: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
text: Ma yaa funɛ tum tum tagna chen ni, ma wa tum were wa kpak wu digi wa dɔŋ ni.

ref: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
text: Tum tagna naa ma funɛ tum chen, nyɛ ma na hiisa lalaknɔ. Baŋ nyɔɔrrɛ ma na a li a ni? Ba naa maga la tum a, Koosa zi wa ta ba yɔriu.

ref: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma maga zim chiksɛ tau di o dɔ nara hali ba nyɛni saha.

ref: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
text: Nihaan yuɛɛ, chiksɛ ta sɛm di o wa o baal lagmi dɔŋ ni nyɛn, amaa o baal ti wa sou, o wa un wa bu kpak wu digi wa dɔŋ ni.

ref: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
text: O baal ti nyɛnɛɛ, ka o la chɔ baal digi wati, nara zi yir un chɔkumsa tiinau. O baal ti wa sou, chiksɛ ta wa bu kpak kpok wa un ni. O la yu baal digi, o wa ya chɔkumsi tiina.

ref: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
text: Mu niinbisa, chen vɛɛna chiksɛ ta wa bu kpak kpok ma ni. O chi ma wa Kristo funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni. Lalaknɔ naa Koosa fun lɛli a li o sou ni chen, waa dɔ ma, chen ni ma zi tum tum wereu a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
text: Ya funɛ tiŋ ya gantɛl tuha kinawa, chiksɛ ta kpa kpok a nyɛ ya titi daŋnɛ ni di ya tiŋ a. Ba naa maga tiŋ chen, Koosa zi ta ba yɔriu,

ref: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
text: amaa lalaknɔ Koosa chiksɛ ta wa bu kpak kpok ya ni. O chi ya funɛ sou chiksɛ ta watɛɛ. Koosa chiksɛ naa ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni, ya nyinbaan bunirɛ. Ya naa kpak o siim weri ti o nyɛn ya ni, la tiŋ o nyinbaan fali ti. Chiksɛ Biksi Nara Di Ba Ya Tum Tagna Nitumɛwa

ref: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
text: O ya chen, yɛrɛ ya kaa ŋɔ? Chiksɛ ta awa tum tagna nitumɛ ya kibɛgimnɛɛ? Aai. Chiksɛ ta fu vɛ waali, mu fu wa zi zim di muu tum tum tagniwa. Chiksɛ ta wa fun biksi min di mu ta ya si tiina, n fu wa zi zim chen.

ref: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
text: Mu wa fun zim chiksɛ ta, tum tagna fun a na nyinbaan aa nyɛ mu ya si tiina kinkan. Chiksɛ ta fu vɛ waali, tum tagna fu wa zi na chen nyinbaan.

ref: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
text: Mu ha wa fun zim chiksɛ ta wuti wɛ muna, o chi mu funɛ nyɛnu, amaa mu funɛ wa zim a wuti muna, o chi mu funɛ sou. Tum tagna fun na nyinbaan di a tum chen.

ref: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
text: Tum tagna funɛ na a nyinbaanu chiksɛ tima daha a fun mur min di o deŋlu di Koosa tum chen.

ref: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
text: A bar peti nɛɛ, Koosa chiksɛ ta maga chaani a deŋli a wer.

ref: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
text: Kin nawara weree funɛ nyɛ mu na galimsa Koosa watii? Aai. Tum tagna funɛ ya chen. A funɛ kpa chiksɛ naa weree a nyɛ mu na galimsa o wati. Kin nama daha, tum tagna fun a tagini a la gali. Chenani Mu Yaa Daŋni Muu Tum Tum Were A Luk Ku

ref: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
text: Ya zimu di chiksɛ ta ya Koosa siim weri ti kinawa. Mu waa ya nibine. Tum tagna funɛ di min a nyɛ mu fun burgi a yoma.

ref: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
text: Mu wa zim mu tum ta muna. Mu wara tum chenani muu daŋni. Tum nawara mu harɛ, muu ka tum.

ref: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
text: Mu tum chenani mu vɛ daŋni mu tumirɛ, chen biksi di mu la nɔɔniu di chiksɛ ta weru.

ref: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: O wa ya di min la tum tum nama. Tum tagna naa nyɛn min ni nan, la tum a.

ref: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
text: Mu zimu di tum were wa nyɛn mu gantɛl tuha wɛ ni hali tama. Saha maga mu yaa daŋni muu tum tum werewa a luk ku.

ref: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
text: Mu wara tum tum were naa muu daŋni, amaa mu ka tum tum tagni naa mu vɛ daŋni mu tumirɛ.

ref: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
text: Muu tum chenani mu vɛ daŋni mu tumi, o wa ya di min la tum a, amaa tum tagna naa nyɛn min ni la tum a.

ref: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
text: Mu daŋni muu tum tum were, saha maga tum tagna yaa nyɛn mu bambi ni. Nannɛ o yaa du saha maga.

ref: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
text: Mu bambi zimu di Koosa chiksɛ ta weru,

ref: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
text: amaa mu zimu di mu bara kuru aa yo mu bambi lise a kpak min, chen ni mu zi la nɔɔniu a tum mu bara tum tagna.

ref: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
text: Ei. Muksi fun wa pu min kinkan. Anti la zi koti a yisi min a li mu bara kpok naa nyɛ Koosa na mu galimsa nii?

ref: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
text: Koosa funɛ kotiu a yisi min a li un ni ya kunyana Yesu Kristo daha. Chendaha mu zaami un kinkan. Ma nyɛ mu lagmi a nama a ŋɔ ma. Muu tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta wa mu bambi lise ni aa la tiŋ tum tagna wa mu bara ni.

ref: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
text: Ba naa maga wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgim, Koosa wa zi bu na ba galimsa.

ref: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
text: O siim weri naa funɛ kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ya, funɛ yisi ya a li tum tagna kpok ni awa chenani Koosa zi ta ya yɔri, domi ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
text: Nibinsa wa kpak kpok. Chendaha Koosa chiksɛ ta fun vɛ koti a ya chen. Koosa funɛ nyɛ o be burgi nibine a tum un o wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a sou nibinsa tum tagna daha. O funɛ ya chen, Koosa chɔk tum tagna kpok ti,

ref: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
text: chen ni ya naa vɛ tum tuma a li ya bara kpok ti ni, amaa a la tum a a li Koosa siim weri kpok ti ni, zi kotiu aa tum tum were a naa maga chiksɛ ta biksi.

ref: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
text: Ba naa nyɛ ba bara dɔ ba, ba waa la lisi di ba bara kina ya kibirigewa. Amaa ba naa ka nyɛ Koosa siim weri ti dɔ ba, ba waa la lisi di o kina ya kibirige.

ref: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
text: Ba naa la lisi ba bara kina wɛ, Koosa zi wa ta ba yɔriu, amaa ba naa ka la lisi Koosa siim weri ti kina wɛ, ba waa kpak bara sɔɔna awa o mi fali ti.

ref: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
text: Ba naa la lisi ba bara kina wɛ, ba waa ya Koosa dɔma. Ba wa tiŋ o nɔ. Hali ba daŋni baa tiŋ o nɔ, ba wa kikoti.

ref: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
text: Amaa ma ka wa tum tuma a li ma bara kpok ti ni. Maa tum au a li Koosa siim weri ti kpok ni. Waa nyɛn ma ni. Nar vɛ kpak Kristo siim weri ti nyɛn un ni, Kristo dɔ un.

ref: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
text: Kristo nyɛn ma ni, ma bara zi sou ma tum tagna daha. Amaa Koosa wa bu na ma galimsa. Chendaha ma siima kpak o mi fali tiu.

ref: Yet God raised Jesus to life! God's Spirit now lives in you, and he will raise you to life by his Spirit.
text: Koosa siim weri ti funɛ lɛli Yesu Kristo a li o sou ni chen. O nyɛn ma bambe ni, wuzu digi o gbanko zi wa lɛli ma barau a li ma sou ni.

ref: My dear friends, we must not live to satisfy our desires.
text: Mu niinbisa, chendaha o wa lawa ya tiŋ ya bara kina.

ref: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
text: Yaa tiŋ ya bara kina, Koosa zi wa ta ya yɔriu. Amaa ma ka vɛ ma bara tum tagna tiŋi Koosa siim weri ti kpok daha, ma zi kpak o miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔu.

ref: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
text: Koosa siim weri ti ya ya nyuhu tiina, ya waa ya Koosa bisa.

ref: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
text: Koosa siim weri ti wa fun nyɛ ya burgi o yosa di ya ɔm un. O ka funɛ nyɛ ya burgi Koosa bisiwa, chen ni ya koti a yir Koosa ya kunyana.

ref: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
text: O siim weri ti ya ya sɛhɛra tiina a li ya bambe ni di ya ya Koosa bisiwa.

ref: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
text: Chendaha wuzu digi ya zi wa la kin naa o fun vɔu di o kpa nyɛ yau. Kristo wa ya gbanko zi bɔni kin naa Koosa fun vɔu di o kpa nyɛ unu. Ya namsi lalaknɔ Kristo sɔn daha, wuzu digi o wa ya zi wa bɔni o jirima awa o chaani kinkanu. Koosa Wa Ya Zi Wa Bɔni O Jirimau Wuzu Digi

ref: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
text: Mu zimu di o jirima awa o chaani naa o wa ya zi wa bɔni gbampulim ni wuzi digi kaali namsi naa ya na lalaknɔ nan hali a la gali.

ref: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
text: O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ kin naa maga o fun naami zi wa ya chenani o daŋniu hali a wa chel. O wa ya a tutɔrɛ, amaa Koosa funɛ kɔɔri nyinbaanu,

ref: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
text: Ya zimu di o kinaamɛ maga namsiu hali wa zinan a chi chenani nihaan ya durɛ.

ref: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
text: A duŋduŋ laata. Ya naa piin kpak o siim weri ti nyɛn ya ni, ya gbanko namsiu a pɔ a la saha naa Koosa zi wa ŋɔ gbampulim niu di ya ya o bisiwa. Chen saha ti o zi wa lɛli ya barau a li a sou ni.

ref: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
text: Koosa funɛ yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni, o fun nyɛ ya kpak bambi bɛgim di wuzu digi o zi wa ya chenu. Ya piin dɔ kundɔŋ, ya wa ya kpak bambi bɛgim un ni.

ref: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
text: Ya kpak bambi bɛgim a li kin naa ya ha vɛ kpaki ni, ya pɔ a wa kanyita ni.

ref: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
text: Ya wa kpak kpok a la zi zim chenani ya zi sumi Koosa, amaa o siim weri ti yaa suŋi yau di ya sumi un. O sumi ya daha, o chi o pɛmu ka o sɛm kaali chenani sɛm.

ref: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
text: Koosa naa la nyini nibinsa bambe a zim chenani a du, waa zim chenani o siim weri ti lise. O siim weri ti zim Koosa tutɔ aa sumi chen a nyɛ o nara ni.

ref: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
text: Ya zimu di saha maga Koosa nyɛ kina maga burgi kiwere a nyɛ ba naa la daŋni un ni. O funɛ ya o tutɔrɛ o yir ba di ba wa o wati. Chendaha oo tum chen.

ref: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
text: Koosa funɛ daŋni o bere ya nihɛɛ a li nibin damata ba naa chi un ni. Saha digi o piin zim bau a lisi ba di ba chi o be.

ref: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
text: Kin nama maga daha, yɛrɛ a du ya ni? Koosa chiŋi ya gantɛl ni nan ni, anti la zi koti a kpak ya kpok?

ref: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
text: Hali waa funɛ lisi o titi be a nyɛ o sou a li o daakakli ti ni ya daha. Chendaha o kpa kina maga a gbaari un a nyɛ ya yɔri.

ref: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
text: Anti la zi koti a ŋɔ di ba chelu? Yesu Kristo funɛ ya naa siwɛɛ ka Koosa lɛli un a li o sou ni. Lalaknɔ o kanɛ Koosa nihɛɛta bar kani nɛɛ a la sumi un saha maga a nyɛ ya.

ref: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
text: Anti la zi waligi ya a li chenani Kristo daŋni ya? Namsi awa muksi kinkan wa lɔsa wa fara wa bar tagna wa kpuuli, kin nama wa kikoti a waligi ya a li chenani Kristo daŋni ya ni.

ref: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
text: Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ya nyɛn sou bar nyɛni nɛɛ saha maga ma daha. Ya chi piisisiwa a naa ba yaa lisi di ba kɔrigirɛ.”

ref: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
text: Hali kin nama kibɛgim zi wa pu ya, ya kpok kaali a kpok domi Yesu Kristo daŋni yau.

ref: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
text: Mu kpak bambi kinkanu di sou awa miisa wa malaika were wa kitagna kpok wa kin nawara la ya lalaknɔ wa kin nawara la zi wa ya,

ref: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
text: wa buudaha kina wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina, wa kin nawara maga Koosa fun naami, kin nama wa kikoti a waligi ya a li chenani Koosa daŋni ya, domi ya wa ya kunyana Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
text: Domi mu wa Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ, mu wara suni wɛ. Koosa siim weri ti ya mu sɛhɛra tiina. Chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma nan, o ya wutirɛ.

ref: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
text: Mu titi nara se wa su Yesu Kristo. Chendaha mu tuha chɔk aa kpak muksi kinkan.

ref: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
text: Mu fu koti a chiŋi ba bar chiŋi ni a nyɛ Koosa ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ min a waligi min a li Kristo ni, mu fu zi ya chenu.

ref: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
text: Ba waa ya Ju tim ta. Saha digi Koosa fun lisi ba di ba ya o bisa a biksi ba o kpok ti awa o chaani ti kinkan. O wa ba funɛ ya nɔ bɛgim. O gbanko fun kpa o chiksɛ naa nyɛ ba jɛɛmi un nɛnɛ awa o vɔwɛ a nyɛ ba.

ref: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
text: Ba waa ya Abraham awa Aizik awa Jakɔb bi lulɛ. Ba waa funɛ lul Kristo. O gbanko ya Koosa a dɔ kina maga. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
text: Ju tim ta nara tama se su Yesu Kristo. Chen mun ya di vɔwi naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ ba chen chɔkuu? Aai. Ju tim ta maga laata ya Koosa titi nara.

ref: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
text: Chen mun ya di Koosa wa fun lisi Ju tim ta maga domi ba ya Abraham lul bisiwa. Abraham wuti lul bisa ya ba naa ba fun lul a li Koosa vɔwi ni.

ref: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
text: Saha digi Koosa funɛ vɔu a ŋɔ Abraham ni, “Sara luli saha wa tel, mu zi nyɛ o lul nibabichɛlau.”

ref: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Chen vɛɛna, Rɛbɛka funɛ ya ya nawaal Aizik haannɛ a lul leusisa.

ref: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ te ŋɔ unu leusisi ta wɛu ka o zi lul. O fun ŋɔ un, “Nihɛɛ zi wa tiŋ bichɛla nɔu.” Bichɛsa ha wa tum tum were koo tum tagna, amaa Koosa funɛ ŋɔ ba wɛu, chen ni ya zim di o yisi narawa a nyɛ o titi ni, o wa yisi ba ba tuma daharɛ.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
text: Ba fun a sɔbi leusisi ta wɛ a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Jakɔbrɛ muu lisi a vɛ Isɔ.”

ref: Are we saying God is unfair? Certainly not!
text: Ya zi koti a ŋɔ di Koosa laata ya wuti tiinaa? O ya wuti tiinnɛ.

ref: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
text: O fun ŋɔ Mosis ni saha digi, “Mu zi kpak sikiu a du ba naa mu fun lisi ni.”

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
text: Ba funɛ bukoo sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chenani Koosa fun ŋɔ Ijipt tim koor ni, “Mu funɛ daŋni mu biksi hin mu kpokre, chen ni tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zi nu mu wɛu a nyɛ min jirima. Chendaha mu fun vɔu hin koora.”

ref: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
text: Koosa kpak o siki a du ba naa o daŋni ni. Ba naa se o daŋni a kpok, a kpok. Ya Chi Tempilsa A Naa Koosa Fun Kɔɔrirɛ

ref: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
text: Tɔ. Ma zi ŋɔ minu Koosa nyɛ nibine si kpok, yɛrɛ o zi ŋɔ di chen tiin tum galimsau? Nandigi wa kikoti a burgi o gantɛl a chak chenani Koosa tum.

ref: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
text: Ma naa ya nibinsa, yɛrɛ ma daŋni ma lɛusi Koosa? Nibine kɔɔri tempile, tempile ti zi koti a burgi a pɛɛsi naa kɔɔri un chen di, yɛrɛ i kɔɔri min nan?

ref: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
text: Tempile nikɔɔri daŋni o lagmi chuha a kɔɔri tempile naa ba zi kpa a tum tum were, a ka kɔɔri tempile naa ba zi kpa a tum tum tagna, waa kpak chen kpok.

ref: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
text: Chen vɛɛna, Koosa funɛ daŋni o biksi nara o kpokre awa chenani o na paan a du ba ni a lɛrgi ba diŋsa. Saha ti funɛ telu di o deŋlu di o wa chɔk ba, amaa o funɛ kpak ba kanyitau a vusi.

ref: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
text: O gbanko funɛ daŋni o biksi ba naa o kpak siki o jirima awa o chaani kinkan. Saha digi o funɛ saksi bau di ba la nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni a la un.

ref: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
text: Koosa witiina naa ba yir Hosia, fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu zi yisi ba naa vɛ ya mu narau di ba ya mu nara, a yisi ba naa vɛ ya mu nidaŋnɛ, ba ya mu nidaŋnɛ.

ref: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
text: Doonani ba fun ŋɔ di nara nama wa ya mu nara, lalaknɔ ba zi sɛmu di ba ya Koosa naa nyɛn bisiwa.”

ref: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
text: Koosa witiina Azaaya gbanko funɛ ŋɔ Ju tim ta wɛu gbampulim ni, “Hali Ju tim ta iiee a chi mɔɔga hɛɛra, Koosa zi yisi ba nara tamau a li ba tum tagna ni ba tiŋ un.

ref: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
text: O wa zi vusi a tum chenani o fun ŋɔ di o zi tumu a lɛrgi ba diŋsa lalaga.”

ref: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
text: Azaaya fun te ŋɔ, “Koosa naa buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga tiina, wa fun gil ya nara tama, ya maga fu zi wa petiu a chi Sɔdɔm awa Gamɔra tima peti chen.” Ju Tima Nidamata Funɛ Vɛ Koosa Laaboor Weri

ref: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
text: Tɔ. Yɛrɛ ya zi sɛm? Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima wa funɛ dɛu di Koosa wa zi bu na ba galimsa o wati. Amaa ba se funɛ su un. Chendaha o fun ya chen.

ref: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
text: Yɛrɛ? Ba se wa fun su Koosa. Chendaha ba fun vɛ koti a la chen. Ba wa funɛ daŋni di Koosa na ba galimsa ba tum daha.

ref: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
text: Ba fun sɔbi buŋkul digi wɛ a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni. O funɛ chi ba mo un wa ba naŋgere nee. Ba fun sɔbi di Koosa ŋɔ, “Mu kpa mu buŋkullɛ a chiŋsi Jɛrusalɛm ni. Ba naa maga nyɛ ba se su un, ba bambe wa zi buti.”

ref: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋniu kinkan di Koosa yisi Ju tim ta a li ba tum tagna ni. Muu sumi unu saha maga di o ya chen.

ref: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
text: Ba ha wa fun zim Koosa nyinbaan naa nyɛ o vɛ na nibinsa galimsa a kpaŋi aa kɔɔri ba titi nyinbaan di o du chen. Chendaha ba fun vɛ la chenani o koti a tum a nyɛ ba.

ref: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
text: Kristo funɛ kpa Koosa chiksɛ ta tiŋi a nyɛ a peti, chen ni Koosa wa zi bu koti a na ba naa maga se su un galimsa. Koosa Daŋni O Lisi Nara Magwa A Li Ba Tum Tagna Ni

ref: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
text: Mosis fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni ba naa la daŋni ba tiŋ chiksɛ ta wɛ, chen ni Koosa wa zi bu na ba galimsa, a ŋɔ, “Nibine tiŋ chiksɛ ta hali a wa peti, o zi na miisau a li o tiŋi ti ni.”

ref: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
text: Ma ta bu pɛɛsi, “Anti la zi zu tɛɛnɛ di o zu a dɛu Kristo awa nisitɛ ta, chen ni o zi li wa ma watiu?”

ref: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
text: Maa nu chenani ba fun sɔbi. “Koosa sɛma wa ma duglɛu kinkan. A nyɛn ma nɔɔni niu awa ma bambe ni.” A wa o laaboora weri naa ba fun biksi ma chenani ma se su un, ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
text: Ma ŋɔɛɛ di Kristo ya ma kunyana ka ma se su di Koosa funɛ lɛli unu a li o sou ni, Koosa zi yisi mau a li ma tum tagna ni.

ref: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
text: Koosa sɛm tɔn ti bukoo a biksi ya di o wa zi buti ba naa maga se su un.

ref: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
text: Koosa wara daŋni bɔnɛ a ka bɔnɛ. Ba naa ya Ju tima awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima, ba maga ya kibɛgimnɛ o wati. Ba maga kunyana yaa nu a la suŋi ba naa la yir o sɔn kinkan.

ref: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
text: Ba se vɛ un, yɛrɛ ba kaa yir o sɔn di o yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni? Ba fu vɛ nu o wɛ, yɛrɛ ba se fu zi koti a su unn? Nara fu vɛ biksi ba o wɛ, yɛrɛ ba fu kaa ya a nu o wɛ?

ref: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
text: Koosa fu vɛ tum nandigi, anti fu zi koti a biksi nara tima o wɛ? Ma zim chenani ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ba naa la gba a la mɔɔli Koosa laaboor weri ti, ba waali weru kinkan.”

ref: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
text: Amaa nara maga ha wa la nɔɔni Koosa laaboor weri ti ni. O witiina Azaaya funɛ sɔbiu, “Mu kunyana, Ju tim tama la nɔɔni sɛm naa i fun kpa a du ya nisi ni.”

ref: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
text: Amaa ma nyɛ mu pɛɛsi ma, Ju tim ta nu Koosa wɛuu? Ba funɛ nuu. Ba funɛ sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Laaboor ti funɛ pisi tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Nara funɛ nu o wɛu bawu maga ni.”

ref: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
text: Ma nyɛ mu bu pɛɛsi ma, Ju tim ta wa zim laaboor ti munn? Ba zimu. Mosis titi funɛ sɔbi di Koosa ŋɔ, “Mu zi nyɛ ma kpak handɔtau a du ba naa mu vɛ lisi a chi ma. Mu zi nyɛ ma na paanu a du ba naa ma yir womna ni.”

ref: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
text: Azaaya funɛ kpak bambi kpok a sɔbi di Koosa ŋɔ, “Ba naa funɛ vɛ dɛu min, ba waa na min. Ba naa funɛ vɛ pɛɛsi mu wɛ, mu fun ŋɔ ba mu titi wɛ.”

ref: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
text: Amaa o funɛ sɔbi Ju tim ta wɛu di Koosa ŋɔ, “Mu funɛ bɛrigi mu nisau chɔɔsa hali zaazɔn wa tel di ba zi wa mu watiu. Ba se funɛ kpok. Chendaha ba fun vɛ.”

ref: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
text: Koosa funɛ vɛ o nara magauu? Aai. Mu gbanko ya Ju tim narrɛ awa Abraham lul bisa digi a li Bɛnjamin walɛ ni.

ref: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
text: Koosa wa koti a vɛ ba naa o fun lisi o piili ni a nyɛ o titi ni. Ma wa zim chenani ba fun sɔbi Koosa witiina Ɛlaija wɛ a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chenn? O funɛ sumi Koosau a fiksi Ju tim ta ba tum tagna daha.

ref: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
text: O fun ŋɔ, “Mu kunyana, ba funɛ kpu i witinsau a chɔk i bar kɔɔrɛ. Mu duŋduŋ kaɛɛ ka ba daŋni ba kpu min gbanko.”

ref: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
text: Yɛrɛ o du lalaknɔ? Kin naa Ju tim ta funɛ dɛu, ba wa fun na un. Ba nara tama naa Koosa fun yisi a nyɛ o titi ni, ba waa funɛ wa na un. Ba naa funɛ vɛ na un, o fun nyɛ ba se kpok.

ref: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
text: Chene o du lalaknɔ. Koosa fun a kpak siki a du Ju tim tama ni a lisi ba a nyɛ o titi ni.

ref: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: O funɛ yisi ba o siki daharɛ. O wa fun yisi ba ba tum were daharɛ. O yisi nara ba tum were daha, o wa fun koti a kpak ba siki.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
text: Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa funɛ nyɛ ba bambe siti, chen ni ba se wa kikoti a na. Ba diŋsa gbanko wa kikoti a nu hali wa zinan.”

ref: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
text: Ya nawaal Devid fun ŋɔ, “Koosa zi wa kpa kin naa ba nyini a se niu a mo ba lo a kpak ba a lɛrgi ba diŋsa a daha.

ref: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
text: Ba se zi nyulumiu, chen ni ba wa zi koti a na ka ba muksi nyɛ ba ta kpak kpok hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
text: Mu zi bukoo pɛɛsi mau, ba funɛ vɛ Koosa, o gil bauu? Aai. Ba funɛ ya chen daha, o fun bur yisi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima a li ba tum tagna ni. Ju tim ta kpak ba handɔta chen daha.

ref: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
text: Ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima, maa nu mu wati lalaknɔ. Chenani Koosa nyɛ mu ya o nitumi di muu biksi ma o wɛ ya mu jirima kin.

ref: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
text: Nan daha, amii mu titi nara bɔnɛ kpak ma handɔtau, chen ni Koosa zi koti a yisi bau a li ba tum tagna ni.

ref: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
text: Koosa funɛ vɛ Ju tim tau ba vɛ kpak se suuli daha, o fun burgi a daŋni o wa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ya nɔ bɛgim. O wa la Ju tim ta, chen la zi nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima na nyɔɔra hali a la gali.

ref: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
text: Ma lisi kikumɛ ta tama a nyɛ Koosa ni, o chi a magawa ma kpa nyɛ un. Bugil luta weree, o hulsa gbanko zi weru.

ref: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
text: Ju tim ta chi bugil naa Koosa fun chɔɔsirɛ. Ba yir un ‘ɔlif’. Koosa fun a teŋsi o hulsi ta bɔnɛ a ta a kpa ma a chiŋsi a bar chiŋɛ ni. O funɛ ya chene, chen ni ma wa o hulsi ta zi lagmi dɔŋ niu a la weri naa la li luti ta ni.

ref: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
text: Ma ta lisi di ma wa bɔ hulsi a naa Koosa fun teŋsi a ta chen. Ma waa ya hulsi ta bɔnɛ duŋduŋ. Ma nyini luti ta se nɛɛ di ma wer. Chendaha ma wa kikoti zer ma titi.

ref: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
text: Ma zi ŋɔ minu, Koosa funɛ teŋsi bugil ti hulsi bɔnɛ a ta, chen ni o zi kpa ya a chiŋsi a bar chiŋɛ niu.

ref: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
text: Chenani ma sɛm ya wuti. Ju tim ta wa fun a nyɛ ba se su Koosa. Chendaha o fun teŋ a a ta. Ma se su un. Chendaha ma ka chiŋi a bar chiŋi ni. Ma ta daŋni sɔn a li chenani Koosa tum ni. Ma doso a ta wa cheli a chi Ju tim ta.

ref: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
text: Koosa wa fun te kpak siki a li bugil ti hulsi ta ni a teŋsi a a ta. Ma doso ka o zi wa teŋsi mau a ta.

ref: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
text: Maa lisi chenani Koosa kpok awa bɔnɛ ni a kpak siki wa bɔnɛ ni. O funɛ kpoku wa ba naa chel chen ni, amaa o kpak ma sikiu. Ma nyɛ o kpak ma siki aa la. Ma ta nyɛ o wa teŋ ma a taali.

ref: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
text: Ju tim ta burɛɛ a nyɛ ba se su Koosa, o zi bu kpa bau a zɔŋ bugil ti ni. Koosa kpak kpoku a la zi tum chen.

ref: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
text: O funɛ kotiu a kpa ma naa ya hul kurgɛ a du bugil ti ni, o wa zi bɔm o wati di o bu kpa bugil ti titi hulsi ta a du un ni. Koosa Zi Kotiu A Kpak Nara Maga Siki

ref: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni mu zimu wuti naa Koosa fun biksi ya gbampulim ni, chen ni ma wa zi daŋni sɔn o funɛ lisi ma daha. Wuzu digi ba naa maga vɛ ya Ju tima Koosa fun lisi chen, zi wa o watiu. Hali chen saha ti o zi nyɛ Ju tim nidamata se kpoku.

ref: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
text: Nannɛ Koosa zi wa yisi bau a li ba tum tagna ni. Ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Naa la zi wa yisi Jakɔb lul bisa a li ba tum tagna ni, zi wa li Jɛrusalɛm niu wuzu digi.

ref: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
text: Mu zi wa yisi bau a li ba tum tagna ni, ba wa min ya nɔ bɛgim.”

ref: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
text: Lalaknɔ Ju tim ta vɛu di ba wa zi nu Koosa laaboor weri ti a ya o dɔma. Nan nyɛ ma nyinbaan di ma tiŋ Koosa. Amaa saha digi o funɛ lisi bau a nyɛ o titi ni. Chendaha o zi wa kpak ba siki ba naa dɔ ba zɔɔnɔ daha.

ref: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
text: O wara chɛrgi o bambi a li chenani o fun lisi ba a nyɛ ba o papaha yɔri.

ref: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
text: Ma wa fun te tiŋ Koosa nɔ, amaa lalaknɔ o funɛ kpak ma sikiu, domi Ju tim ta wa fun tiŋ o nɔ.

ref: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
text: Chen vɛɛna Ju tim ta funɛ vɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔ, o fun bur a kpak ma siki. Chendaha wuzu digi Koosa gbanko zi wa kpak ba sikiu.

ref: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
text: O fun nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga na galimsa domi ba wa tiŋ o nɔ, chen ni o zi kotiu a kpak ba maga siki. Chenani Koosa Du Kaali Kina Maga

ref: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
text: Ei. Koosa siki awa nɛha wa zima zenu kinkan. A wa kpak bɔ. Nandigi wa kikoti a zim chenani o lisi a la tum.

ref: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
text: Anti nyɛn Koosa la kina o wati chen ni Koosa zi kpa a bur nyɛ unuu? Nandigi wa toli.”

ref: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
text: Waa funɛ naami kina maga. O kpok ti daha, a nama maga ha vɛɛn nyɛnu hali wa zinan a nyɛ un jirima. O jirima zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
text: Mu niinbisa, Koosa funɛ kpak ma sikiu kinkan. Chendaha mu sumi ma di ma kpa ma titi a nyɛ un a chi nara yaa kpa kina a kɔɔri kɔɔra chen. Ma ya chen a ya nibin were, ma zi tiŋ unu nɛnɛ a sɔɔni o tuha kinkan.
