ref: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
text: Ma ta bu tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim nara nyinbaan, amaa ma nyɛ Koosa ka chɛrgi ma bambe, chen ni ma nyinbaan zi ya kifaliu. Ma nyɛ o ya chen, ma zi kotiu a zim o tutɔ naa weree hali a wa peti, a sɔɔni o tuha.

ref: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
text: Koosa la suŋi min di mu biksi ma nan. Ma ta daŋni sɔna a kaali chenani ma fu zi du. Koosa nyɛ ma se su un. Ma kpa ma se suuli ti a kidi ma titi sɛria wa nɛha ni.

ref: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
text: Nibine bara ya kibɛgimnɛ, amaa o kpak luk pɛk damatiwa. Luk pɛksi ta maga kpak a tumawa.

ref: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
text: Ya gbanko iiu. Domi ya wa Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ, ya maga chi bara bɛgimnɛ. Chendaha ya lagmi dɔŋ ni a ya nibɛgim.

ref: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
text: Koosa fun kpa o papaha kurgɛ a nyɛ ya di ya tum tuma a nyɛ un. O fun nyɛ ya burgi o witinsa chen ni ya zi kotiu a biksi nara o wɛ, ma nyɛ ya ya chen. Ma nyɛ ya biksi a chi chenani ya se su un.

ref: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
text: Koosa nyɛ ya tum a nyɛ ya dɔŋ tim ni, ma nyɛ ya tum chen. O nyɛ ya biksi narawa, ma nyɛ ya biksi ba.

ref: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
text: O nyɛ ya kpami nara ba tiŋi ni, ma nyɛ ya kpami ba chen ni. O nyɛ nibine kpa o kina a bɔni nara, o lawa o ya chenu a li o bambi ni. O nyɛ nibine ya nyuhu tiina, o lawa o kpak si sɛmu a tum nɛnɛ. O nyɛ nibine kpak nara siki, o lawa o kpak tu sɔɔnau.

ref: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
text: Maa daŋni dɔŋa a li ma bambe ni a bur gantɛl a li tum tagna ni a la suwachak a li tum were nyinbaan saha maga.

ref: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
text: Ma wa ma dɔŋtima ya nibɛgimnɛ Kristo daha. Chendaha maa daŋni dɔŋa nɛnɛ aa kpak un ma bambe ni di ma nyɛ dɔŋa wa jirima.

ref: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
text: Ma ta ya zanyɛrka tima, amaa ma kpak se sɛm a tum a nyɛ ma kunyan ti ni saha maga.

ref: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
text: Ma kpak bambi bɛgim awa tu sɔɔna un ni, a kinamsi wa kanyita ni aa kpak un ma bambe ni a sumi Koosa saha maga.

ref: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
text: Maa suŋi Koosa nara ba naa vɛ kpak kina, a la hɔɔra a du ma damsi ni.

ref: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
text: Maa sumi Koosa di o nyɛ ba naa la namsi ma na alaafɛ, a ta ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba.

ref: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
text: Maa nyɛ ma tusa wa ba naa tusa sɔɔnɛɛ sɔɔni. Ma bukoo nyɛ ma tusa wa ba naa tusa chɔkɛɛ chɔk.

ref: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
text: Maa kpak nɔ bɛgim wa dɔŋ ni. Ma ta daŋni sɔna. Ma saksi a nyɛ ma wa ba naa la tum tum wisa lagmi kitum. Ma ta lisi di maa zim kina magau.

ref: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
text: Nandigi tum tum tagin a du ma ni, ma ta bu kpa tum tagin a bɔ un. Maa ka tum tum naa nibinsa maga zim di a weru.

ref: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
text: O nyɛn ma watɛɛ, maa kpaŋi aa kpak bara sɔɔna awa nara maga ni.

ref: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
text: Ba gbanko funɛ sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Lɔsa kpak ma dɔma, maa ya diwe a nyɛ ba ba di. Nyɔksa kpak ba, ma zɔk ni a nyɛ ba ba nyɔ. Ma ya chen, hiisa kinkan zi kpak bau.” Ma tiŋ a chi chenani ba fun sɔbi chen.

ref: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
text: Nara maga kitiŋ pulum nihɛɛta nɔɔna. Nandigi waa kikoti a ya nihɛɛ see Koosa la nɔɔni. Waa funɛ nyɛ ba ya nihɛɛta,

ref: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
text: chen ni nara maga vɛ tiŋ ba nɔɔna, Koosa nɔrɛ ba vɛ chen. Waa la zi di ba sɛria a na ba galimsa.

ref: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
text: Ba naa la tum tum were wa zi ɔm pulum ti nihɛɛti ta. Ba naa ka la tum tum tagna, ba zi ɔmu. Ma wara daŋni ma ɔm nihɛɛti taa? Maa tum tum were ba zi pɛgi mau.

ref: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
text: Ba waa ya Koosa tumtumsa a tum a dɛu ma tum were. Maa tum tum tagna, ma zi ɔm bau. Ba kpak kpok naa ba zi lɛrgi ma diŋsau. Ba waa chiŋi Koosa naa na paan a du tum tagna nitumɛ ni a la lɛrgi ba diŋsa bar chiŋi ni.

ref: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
text: Chendaha o lawa maa tiŋ pulum ti nihɛɛti ta nɔɔnau. Ma ta tiŋi di ma ɔm ba paan ti duŋduŋ, amaa maa nyɛ ba jirima domi ma la tiŋ Koosa wa tu bɛgim ni daha.

ref: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
text: Chendaha o weru di ma bɔ lamposa. O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ ma ya chen, chen ni pulum ti nihɛɛti ta zi kotiu a tum ba tuma a nyɛ un.

ref: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
text: Ma ta pɛŋ nandigi kina, amaa maa daŋni dɔŋa saha maga. Nar la daŋni nibinsa, waa la tiŋ chenani Koosa chiksɛ ta maga biksi un.

ref: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: A waa ya Koosa chiksɛ ta bɔnɛ. “Ma ta tum chɔkumsa tuma, ma ta kpu nara, ma ta ŋmɛɛli, ma ta kpak si.” Chiksɛ nama awa a naa kaɛɛ, a maga tiŋ kibɛgimnɛ, maa daŋni nibinsa a chi ma titi.

ref: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
text: Maa daŋni nibinsa, ma wa zi la nɔɔni a tum ba tum tagna. Maa daŋni dɔŋa, maa tiŋ chenani chiksɛ ta magawa biksi ma.

ref: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
text: Ma zimu di tɛɛne nɔ maga siili saha duglɛu. Chendaha maa kpak un ma bambe ni aa tiŋ chen. Saha naa Koosa zi wa yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni hali a wa peti duglɛu a kaali saha naa ya se fun te su Yesu.

ref: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
text: O chi dusum la daŋni o peti, ka chɔɔsa pisa dugli. Ma nyɛ ya gil ya tum tagna a naa chi dusum a ta aa nyɛ ya tum tum were a naa chi nha.

ref: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
text: Ya wa bu nyɛn dusum ni. Chendaha o lawa ya tum chenu. Ma ta nyɛ ya lagmi dɔŋ ni a nyɔ sin a dɛɛsi. Ma ta tum chɔkumsa awa sakaal tuma a kidi dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ a kpak handɔta wa dɔŋ ni.

ref: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
text: Ma burgi chenani ya kunyana Yesu Kristo du. Ma ta tiŋ ma titi daŋnɛ a tum tum tagna.

ref: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
text: Nar vɛ kpak kpok a li chenani o se su Yesu a la daŋni o lagmi ma ni, ma la un, amaa ma ta di chenani o lisi amɔrɛ.

ref: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
text: Nibin bɛgim naa si su Yesu nɛnɛ lisi di o zi kotiu a di kina maga. Naa vɛ kpak kpok a li chenani o si su un, waa ka lisi di o zi kotiu a di disa duŋduŋ.

ref: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
text: Anti ya ma ma koti a ŋɔ di nandigi tumtumna tum galimsau? O titi kunyana la zi zim di o chelu koo o wa chel. Ya kunyan ti zi chiŋi o tumtumsa gantɛl niu, chen ni ba wa zi chel.

ref: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
text: Nibine bɛgim la nyɛ wuzu digi wa jirima a kaali wuzu bɔnɛ. Digi ka zi ŋɔu di wuzuna maga ya kibɛgimnɛ. Mu daŋni ma nidigirɛ zim ma bambe ni chenani ma zi tum.

ref: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
text: Naa nyɛ wuzu digi wa jirima, ya kunyannɛ o nyini a la nyɛ un jirima. Naa la di kina maga awa naa la chi diwe bɔnɛ, ya kunyannɛ ba nyini a la nyɛ un jirima.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
text: Ya nidigi wa dɔ ya titi miisa. Ya nidigi wa dɔ ya titi sou.

ref: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
text: Ya nyɛnɛɛ a kpak miisa, ya kunyan ti daha ya nyɛn. Ya sitɛɛ, ya kunyan ti daha ya sou. Ya kpak miisa koo ya sitɛɛ, ya kunyan ti ha vɛɛn dɔ ya.

ref: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
text: Kristo funɛ sou a si o sou ni di o burgi ba naa kpak miisa awa ba naa sitɛɛ kunyana.

ref: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
text: Chen ya wuti, yɛrɛ maa di ma dɔŋtima sɛria saha maga? Yɛrɛ ma nyini ba ba ya chi bichɛsa? Wuzu digi ya maga zi wa chiŋiu a li Koosa sɛria bar diili ni.

ref: In the Scriptures God says, “I swear by my very life that everyone will kneel down and praise my name!”
text: Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Wuzu digi nara maga zi wa gbiriu mu suwachak ni a pɛgi min. Beeli wa to hali tama.”

ref: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
text: Wuzu digi Koosa zi wa pɛɛsi ya chenani ya fun tumu. O Wa Lawa Ya Nyɛ Ya Dɔŋtima Tum Tum Tagna A Cheli

ref: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
text: Chendaha o wa lawa ya di dɔŋa sɛria. Ma doso ma ta wa ya wi naa la zi nyɛ ma dɔŋtima tum tum tagna a cheli.

ref: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
text: Mu kpak bambiu di kundɔŋ wa to a chi, domi mu wa ya kunyan ti ya nibɛgimnɛ. Amaa bɔnɛ lisiu di diwe bɔnɛ ya chire. Ba di a a nyɛ ba doŋwa.

ref: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
text: O ya di ma diwe daharɛ ma nyɛ ma dɔŋtima bɔnɛ tusa chɔk, ma wara tiŋ nyinbaan daŋni ti. Kristo fun sou a nyɛ ba. O wa werwa ma chɔk ba chenani ma di diwe daha.

ref: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
text: Ma ta nyɛ nandigi nyinbaan di o la ŋɔ nara ni chenani ma lisi di o weru kpak sɔn tagin.

ref: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
text: Koosa dɔ ma, diwe naa ma di a la nyɔ wa bu ya kibirigi o wati. Chenani o siim weri ti nyɛ ma bara sɔɔna awa tu sɔɔna a la suŋi ma di ma tum tum were a nyɛ Koosa ni, kin nama ka ya kibirigi a li o wati.

ref: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
text: Ba naa maga tiŋ Kristo nɔ chen, zi sɔɔni Koosa tuhau. Nibinsa gbanko zi nyɛ ba jirimau.

ref: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
text: Ma nyɛ ya yaa nyini kin nawara nyɛ ya kpak bara sɔɔna awa kin nawara la zi nyɛ ya suŋi dɔŋa a kpak nɛne.

ref: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
text: Ma ta chɔk Koosa tuma diwe daha. Diwe wa toli a chi. Amaa o wa wer di nibine kidi diwe bɔnɛ, chen ni o dɔŋtima nyuksi nyinbaan.

ref: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
text: O deŋlu wa ma gil nɛmɛ seeli awa sin nyɔɔli awa kin naa maga la zi nyɛ ma dɔŋtima nyuksi nyinbaanu.

ref: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
text: Chenani ma lisi kin nama wɛ, ma nyɛ chen nyɛn ma wa Koosa duŋduŋ wati. Nar zim di o wa tum galimsau diwe naa o di chen daha, o zi kpak tu sɔɔnau.

ref: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
text: Nar ka la beeli diwe naa o di wɛ, o lisi di o tum galimsirɛ. O si wa zu Koosa a li chenani o di ni. Tum naa nibine tum ka o si vɛ su un ni, o ya tum taginnɛ.

ref: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
text: Ya naa se su Kristo nɛne, o lawa ya suŋi ba naa se vɛ su un nɛne. Ma ta nyɛ yaa lisi ya duŋduŋ kina wɛ,

ref: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
text: amaa ma nyɛ ya gbanko kilisi ya dɔŋtima kina wɛ, chen ni ba gbanko zi kpoku a chi ya.

ref: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
text: Kristo wa fun lisi o duŋduŋ kina wɛ. Ba funɛ sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ Koosa ni, “Zoma naa nara fun pini a du hin ni, ba pini a du min nɛɛ.”

ref: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
text: Koosa naa funɛ kpa o kanyita a nyɛ ya a kpami ya, la zi nyɛ ma kpak nɔ bɛgim wa dɔŋ ni a chi chenani Yesu Kristo fun biksi ya chen,

ref: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
text: chen ni maga zi lagmiu a pɛgi Koosa naa ya ya kunyana Yesu Kristo nyan ti wa ta bɛgim ni. Ba Naa Vɛ Ya Ju Tima Pɛgi Koosa

ref: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
text: Maa la dɔŋa a chi Kristo funɛ la ma chen. Ma ya chen, nara zi nyɛ Koosa wa jirimau.

ref: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
text: Ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma, Kristo funɛ wa tum a nyɛ Ju tim ta ni, chen ni Koosa vɔwɛ a naa o fun ŋɔ a nyɛ Abraham, Aizik awa Jakɔb ni, zi burgi wutiu.

ref: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
text: O gbanko fun wa di ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima zi kotiu a nyɛ Koosa wa jirima o siki naa o kpak ba ni daha. Ba fun sɔbi Koosa wɛ a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Mu wa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima zi lagmiu a nyɛ hin jirima a lɔ boola aa pɛgi hin.”

ref: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
text: Ba fun bu sɔbi, “Ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima, ma nyɛ ma wa o titi nara tusa maga sɔɔni.”

ref: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
text: Ba gbanko fun sɔbi, “Ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima, maa pɛgi Koosa. Nibinsa maga kipɛgi un.”

ref: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
text: O witiina Azaaya fun a sɔbi, “Jɛsɛ be luli zi siu a dɔ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima. Ba zi kpak bambi bɛgim un niu.”

ref: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
text: Ma se su Koosa naa ya bambi bɛgim tiina. Chendaha o zi nyɛ ma tu sɔɔna awa bara sɔɔna kinkan, chen ni ma bambi bɛgim naa ma kpak un ni zi zenu aa gbaari a li o siim weri ti kpok ni. Kin Naa Nyɛ Mu Kpak Bambi A Sɛm Ma Nan

ref: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu kpak bambiu di ma ya nibin werewa kinkan a kpak zima nɛnɛ a koti aa biksi dɔŋa.

ref: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ suŋi minu di mu kpak bambi kpok a lisi ma kin bɔnɛ a li tɔn nɔ mu sɔbi nan a nyɛ ma.

ref: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
text: Waa funɛ nyɛ mu ya Yesu Kristo tumtumna ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim daha. Chendaha mu sɔbi a nyɛ ma chen. Mu chi Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa ba naa yaa kpa kina a nyɛ Koosa ni digirɛ. Mu biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima o laaboor weri ti wɛ, chen ni mu zi kpa ba naa o siim weri ti fun lisi chen a nyɛ unu.

ref: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
text: Chenani mu tum a nyɛ Koosa ni ya mu jirima kin, domi mu wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
text: Mu daŋni mu ŋɔ nara duŋduŋnɛ chenani Kristo fun suŋi min di ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima wa tiŋ Koosa nɔ. A li mu sɛma awa mu tuma,

ref: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
text: wa o tum kpok wa o siim weri ti kpok nee, Kristo fun ya chen a nyɛ ba. Mu funɛ gbau a li Jɛrusalɛm ni o wa tel bar boli naa ba yir Ilrikum pulum. Mu fun a biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti Kristo wɛ bawu maga ni.

ref: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
text: Mu funɛ kpaŋi mu titiu di mu ŋɔ nara ni Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ doonani ba ha fun vɛɛn nu Kristo wɛ, chen ni mu fu wa zi tum doonani nandigi piin a piili.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
text: Chenani ba fun sɔbi Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni yau. Ba fun ŋɔ, “Ba naa ba vɛ biksi o wɛ zi wa zim unu.” Mu Daŋni Di Mu Wa Zaami Ma Room Ni

ref: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
text: Mu funɛ daŋni mu wa ma watɛɛ bu damata, amaa chenani mu biksi nara saha maga funɛ tɔ mu nyinbaan.

ref: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
text: Amaa tum naa muu tum bar nɔ ni petiu. O nyɛn mu bambi niu di mu wa na ma bun damata.

ref: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
text: Chendaha mu daŋni mu te wa ma watɛɛ a zi gali a la Speen pulum. Mu nyɛn ma wati hali a wa puŋ, mu zi nyɛ ma suŋi minu mu la Speen.

ref: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
text: Ba dɔŋtima ba naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum awa Girik tim pulum ni, ba fun lisi di o weru wa ba lagmi ba kin bɔnɛ a kpa nyɛ fara tim ni a li Jɛrusalɛm ni.

ref: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn chim nɛɛ ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni. Chenani ba fun ya chen weru. Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima funɛ nu Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ a li Ju tim ta wati, o deŋlu di ba kpa ba kina a suŋi ba.

ref: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
text: Mu wa tel dooni a kpa kina nama a nyɛ ba peti, mu zi la Speenu a la zaami ma nyinbaan ni.

ref: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
text: Mu wa pu ma, mu zimu di Kristo zi nyɛ mu wa ma kpak tu sɔɔnau kinkan.

ref: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
text: Tɔ. Mu niinbisa, mu sumi mau di ma biksi min chenani Koosa siim weri ti nyɛ ma daŋni min. Ma nyɛ mu wa ma lagmi a kpak si sɛm a sumi Koosa a nyɛ min ya kunyana Yesu Kristo daha.

ref: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
text: Ma sumi Koosa, chen ni o zi yisi minu a li ba naa vɛ un a li Judia pulum ni nisa ni. Ma gbanko kisumi un di o nara ba naa nyɛn Jɛrusalɛm ni zi la kin nawara mu kpa wa ba watiu.

ref: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
text: O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ mu zi wa na nyinbaanu a wa ma wati awa tu sɔɔni a wa hiisi tama.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
text: Koosa naa ya bara sɔɔn tiina, nyɛn ma maga wati. Ami.

ref: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
text: Mu daŋni mu ŋɔ mau di ya dɔŋtiina towe, Fibi, ya nibin werire. O tum aa nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta ni a li tɔhɔn naa ba yir Kɛnkrɛa ni.

ref: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
text: O wa pu ma, ma la un a du ya kunyan ti daha. O deŋlu di Koosa nara kiya chen. O funɛ suŋi nidamatau a suŋi min gbanko. Chendaha ma suŋi un wa kin naa o vɛ kpaki ni.

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
text: Ma zaami Prisila awa Akwila ba naa wa min la tum Yesu Kristo tuma.

ref: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
text: Ba fun a kpiŋi ba miisa mu daha. Chendaha mu wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa maga vɛ ya Ju tima, kpak tu sɔɔna ba ni.

ref: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
text: Ma gbanko zaami Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa lagmi ba dam ni, a zaami mu nidaŋni Ɛpainitus naa si funɛ te su Yesu a li Azia pulum ni.

ref: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
text: Ma zaami Mɛri naa funɛ suŋi ma kinkan,

ref: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
text: awa Andrɔnikus awa Junias ba naa wa min ba fun tɔ dɛ chen. Ba ya Ju timwa a chi min. Ba se funɛ te min a su Yesu, ka Koosa nitumɛ ta nyɛ ba jirima.

ref: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
text: Ma zaami Amipliatus. Mu daŋni unu ya kunyan ti daha.

ref: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
text: Ma zaami Urbenus naa wa ya la tum Kristo tuma, awa Stakis naa mu daŋni.

ref: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
text: Ma zaami Apeles. Koosa funɛ zaamsi un, o fun di a li Kristo tiŋi ni. Ma zaami Aristɔbɔlɔs dam walɛ,

ref: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
text: awa Hɛrɔdiɔn naa ya Ju tiina a chi min. Ma zaami ba naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a nyɛn Nasisus dam ni,

ref: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
text: awa ya toosa Trifena awa Trifosa ba naa la tum ya kunyan ti tuma kinkan, awa ya towe Pɛrsis naa ya maga daŋni. O gbanko funɛ tum kinkan a nyɛ ya kunyan ti ni.

ref: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
text: Ma zaami Rufus naa te suwachak a li ya kunyan ti tiŋi ni awa o niina naa mu gbanko yir mu niina.

ref: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
text: Ma zaami Asinkritus, Flɛgɔn, Hɛrmis, Patrobas, Hɛrmas awa ba naa maga nyɛn ba wati.

ref: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
text: Ma zaami Filologus, Julia, Nɛrɛus awa o toosa, Olimpus, awa Koosa nara ba naa maga nyɛn ba wati.

ref: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
text: Ma zaami dɔŋa nɛnɛ. Kristo nitiŋɛ ta maga zaami mau. Mu Daŋni Mu Mak Mu Tɔnu Bar Nɔwa

ref: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
text: Mu niinbisa, mu sumi mau ma il aa zim ba naa la daŋni ba waligi ma a li dɔŋ ni a bur ma a li Yesu tiŋi ni. Chenani baa biksi awa chenani nara fun te wa biksi ma yo dɔŋa. Ma ta lagmi ba ni.

ref: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
text: Nara nama ba naa la tum nan, ba wara tum a nyɛ ya kunyana Kristo nɛɛ. Ba titi daŋnɛ tum tagniwa baa tum, a ŋɔ sɛm were a pɛgi nara wa ba nyina ni aa mur ba naa vɛ ili.

ref: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
text: Nara nyɛ ma jirima bawu maga ni domi chenani maa tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ. Chendaha mu tuha sɔɔni. Mu daŋni ma zim wu werewa a ya womna a li wu tagna ni.

ref: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Koosa naa ya bara sɔɔna tiina, waa la zi wa chɔk sintana a nyɛ ma lalaga. Ya kunyana Yesu zi suŋi mau.

ref: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
text: Timoti naa wa min la tum tuma zaami mau. Lusius, Jesɔn awa Sosipata ba naa ya Ju tim ta, gbanko zaami mau.

ref: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
text: Min, Tɛrsius, naa la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ Pɔɔl ni, zaami mau ya kunyan ti daha.

ref: Gaius welcomes me and the whole church into his home, and he sends his greetings. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our dear friend Quartus send their greetings too. Paul's Closing Prayer
text: Ya kunyana Yesu Kristo la zi suŋi ma.)) Ma Nyɛ Ya Pɛgi Koosa O Laaboor Weri Daha

ref: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
text: Tɔ. Koosa koti a nyɛ ma kpak nɛnɛ a tiŋ laaboor weri naa mu fun a biksi nara Yesu Kristo wɛ. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un chen daha. Koosa funɛ te kpa o laaboor weri nɔ a sogli saha digi,

ref: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
text: a zi kpa un a li gbampulim a nyɛ o witinsi ta ni ba sɔbi o wɛ. Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, ŋɔu lalaknɔ di ya kpa un a la biksi ba naa maga vɛ ya Ju tima, chen ni ba zi na nyinbaanu a nyɛ ba se su Koosa a tiŋ o nɔ.

ref: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
text: Koosa duŋduŋ kpak zima maga. O zi la jirimau Yesu Kristo daha hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
text: Mu waa, Pɔɔl, awa ya niinbe Sɔstenis zaami mau. O funɛ sɔɔni Koosa tuhau o fun nyɛ mu ya Yesu Kristo nitumi.

ref: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ya kunyan Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo zi suŋi mau aa nyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Koosa Funɛ Kpa O Siim Weri Papaha Maga A Nyɛ Ya

ref: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
text: Mu pɛgi Koosa saha maga ma daha. Waa la suŋi ma di ma tiŋ un Yesu Kristo daha.

ref: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
text: Chenani ma fun nu Yesu Kristo wɛ chen zu ma bambe niu nɛnɛ,

ref: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
text: domi Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti papaha maga a nyɛ ma, a biksi di ma funɛ la unu. Maa pɔ ya kunyan Yesu Kristo bur waali.

ref: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
text: Waa la zi kpa o kpok a nyɛ ma maa tiŋ un nɛnɛ, chen ni o wa bur wa, o wa zi bu na ma galimsa.

ref: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
text: Koosa ya wuti tiina. O funɛ yir ma chen di o nyɛ ma wa o be Yesu Kristo ya chɛnsiwa. Ma Titi Burgi Aa Di Dɔŋa Wa Amɔrɛ

ref: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu sumi mau ya kunyan Yesu Kristo sɔn daha ma maga kiya nɔ bɛgim. Ma ta waligi ma titi, chen ni ma sɛma awa ma lise ni ma zi kpak nɔ bɛgimu.

ref: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
text: Mu niinbisa, haan naa ba yir Kloi chen, walɛ wau a wa ŋɔ min di ma titi burgi aa di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ.

ref: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
text: Ma amɔrɛ ti biksi di ma wa tiŋ nyinbaan bɛgim. Ma bɔnɛ ŋɔ di maa tiŋ Pɔɔllɛ, bɔnɛ Apɔlɔs, bɔnɛ Sifas, bɔnɛ ka ŋɔ di maa tiŋ Kristore.

ref: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
text: Ma na, ma purigi Kristo tiu. Minnɛ ba fun kpaasi a mari daakakli ti a nyɛ ma chenn? Ba funɛ chaksi ma wa ni nɛ di ma tiŋ minnɛɛ?

ref: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
text: Mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati, mu fun chaksi Krispus awa Gaiyus duŋduŋnɛ,

ref: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
text: chen ni ma nidigi wa kiŋɔ di mu chaksi unu ka o tiŋ min.

ref: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
text: Mu gbanko funɛ chaksi Stɛfanas awa o dam walɛ magau. Mu wa lisi di mu funɛ chaksi nandigi a gbaariu.

ref: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
text: Kristo wa fun tum min di mu wa chaksi narawa. O funɛ tum minu di mu wa biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ nara maga ni. O wara daŋni mu sɛm nibinsa zimarɛ. Mu kpa chen a biksi Yesu daakakli ti siwi wɛ, chen zi tɔ o daakakli siwi ti kpoku. Yesu Kristo Ya Koosa Kpok Awa O Zima

ref: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
text: Ba naa vɛ Koosa laaboor weri ti Yesu daakakli ti siwi ti wɛ, o wa kpak nyuha ba wati. Ba naa la ya chen tiŋ din dam nyinbaannɛ, amaa ya naa vɛ nyuksi aa tiŋ Koosa, Yesu daakakli ti siwi ti ya Koosa kpok naa o kpa nyɛ ya.

ref: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu zi nyɛ nibin nɛɛgɛ zima wa ya yɔriu.”

ref: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
text: Ba naa la tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima, ba wa zim Koosa. Koosa nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima ti ya yɔriu.

ref: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
text: Ba zima ti wa nyɛ ba zim Koosa. Koosa nyɛ chen ya a li o zimi ni. Amaa o funɛ ŋɔ o zi la ba naa se su o laaboor weri tiu ka ya kpa un a biksi nara chen. Ba naa la tiŋ nibinsa zima ti lisiu di o laaboor weri ti ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
text: Ju tim ta daŋni ya la biksi ba alaazibu wɛ, ka Girik tima daŋni ya la biksi ba zima ti.

ref: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
text: Amaa ya wa la biksi ba Yesu daakakli ti siwi ti wɛ duŋduŋ. Chen ya tuzɔgrɛ Ju tim ta wati, a vɛɛn ya yɔri a li ba naa maga vɛ ya Ju tim wati.

ref: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
text: Amaa Ju tim ta maga awa Girik tima ba naa maga Koosa fun yir di ba wa o wati, Kristo ya Koosa kpok ti awa o zima ti ba wati.

ref: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
text: Koosa zima naa nibinsa lisi di o ya yɔrirɛ kaali ba zima ti kinkan. Koosa kpok naa nibinsa lisi di o wa kpok kaali ba kpok kinkan.

ref: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
text: Mu niinbisa, Koosa funɛ yir ma di ma wa o wati, ma lisi chenani ma fun du. Ma nara tama funɛ ya nibin nɛɛgɛ a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga se ni. Tam ka fun ya nibin zene tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga se ni. Tam ka ya nibin zene bisa.

ref: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
text: Koosa tutɔ nɛɛ o fun lisi ba naa nibinsa yir womna di ba filimi nibin nɛɛgɛ ta, a lisi ba naa nara funɛ ŋɔ di ba wa kpak kpok di ba filimi ba naa kpak kpok.

ref: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
text: O fun lisi nibinsa ba naa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zɛɛmi awa ba naa ba ŋɔ di ba ya yɔrirɛ, chen ni o zi wa ta kin nawara tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ŋɔ di a ya kibirigewa yɔri.

ref: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
text: Chendaha nibine wa to a la zi koti a nyɛ o titi jirima Koosa wati,

ref: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ma ta nyɛ ma titi jirima. Ma ka nyɛ Koosa jirima.”

ref: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu funɛ wa ma wati a sɛm Koosa sɛma a naa funɛ sogli ma wati, mu wa fun kpak nɔ lɛma awa nibinsa zima ti a biksi ma.

ref: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
text: Mu funɛ daŋni mu sɛm mau a li Yesu Kristo siwi daakakli ti daha ni duŋduŋnɛ.

ref: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
text: Biksi ti maga mu fun biksi ma, o wa fun tiŋ nibinsa zimirɛ di o ya ma lɛmlɛm, amaa Koosa siim weri ti funɛ ya mu sɛm ta sɛhɛra a nyɛ a kpak kpok.

ref: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
text: O wa fun ya nibinsa zimarɛ nyɛ ma se su Yesu. Koosa kpok ti ka funɛ nyɛ ma se su un. Koosa Siim Weri La Biksi Ya Koosa Zima Wɛ

ref: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
text: Mu yaa vɛɛn a sɛmu zima sɛmau a nyɛ ba naa la tiŋ Koosa ni nɛnɛ. O wa ya tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zimarɛ akoo nibin zene zima mu sɛm a nyɛ ba. A nama ya yɔrirɛ a la zi vɛ vusi.

ref: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
text: Ya ka biksi ma Koosa zima ti wɛwa. O funɛ te lisi unu ya jirima daha a zi naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Hali saha nɔ Koosa ha vɛɛna sogli unu a li nibinsi ni.

ref: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
text: Ba naa maga dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga wa fun zim zima nɔ wɛ. Ba fu zimɛɛ, ba fu wa zi kpu ya kunyan ti o daakakli ti ni.

ref: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa funɛ kpa wu werewa a chiksi ba naa la daŋni un. Nibinsa se ha wa na a, akoo nibinsa diŋsa ha wa nu a wɛ. Nibinsa wa lisi di o zi wa ya chenu.”

ref: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
text: Amaa lalaknɔ Koosa funɛ nyɛ o siim weri ti biksi ya kin nama wɛ. O siim weri ti nyini kina maga a zim Koosa wu sogle gbanko.

ref: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
text: Nibine siima zim o titi tuha lisi. Koosa siim weri ti duŋduŋ zim Koosa tuha lisi gbanko.

ref: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
text: Ya wara tiŋ nibinsa zimirɛ. Koosa funɛ zer o siim weri ti a du ya bambe ni, chen ni ya zi kotiu a zim o siim weri papaha naa o fun kpa nyɛ ya.

ref: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
text: Ya wara sɛm sɛma a li nibinsa zima ti ni. Koosa siim weri ti la biksi ya chenani ya zi sɛm. O sɛmwa ya kpa la biksi ma.

ref: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
text: Nibine naa vɛ kpak siim weri ti o nyɛn un ni, o vɛ Koosa siim weri ti kinawa. A ya yɔrirɛ o wati ka o vɛ zim a muna.

ref: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
text: Koosa siim weri ti nyɛn nibine ni, waa la zi koti a zim kina maga nyinbaan. Koosa siim weri ti ka vɛ nyɛn nibine ni, o wa kikoti a zim naa kpak Koosa siim weri ti.

ref: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a pɛɛsi, “Anti zim chenani Koosa lisi du? Anti fun biksi un kundɔŋ?” Amaa ya kpak Kristo lisiu.

ref: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu wa fun koti a biksi ma a chi chenani mu funɛ biksi ba naa tiŋ Koosa siim weri ti nɛnɛ. O chi maa tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga nyinbaannɛ. Chendaha mu sɛm ma a chi bichɛsa ba naa nyɛ ba se su Yesu.

ref: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
text: O funɛ chi illɛ mu kpa aa dɛɛsi ma domi ma ha wa mak ku diili. Ma ha wa mak ku diili,

ref: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
text: domi ma ha vɛɛna tiŋu a chi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga. Chenani maa di handɔta awa dɔŋa a la di amɔrɛ wa dɔŋ ni biksi di ma wara tiŋ Koosa siim weri ti nyinbaan. Ma ka tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga nyinbaannɛ.

ref: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
text: Ma bɔnɛ sɛm di ma tiŋ Pɔɔllɛ, bɔnɛ ka ŋɔ di ma tiŋ Apɔlɔsrɛ. Chenani ma ya chen biksi ma tuma.

ref: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
text: Apɔlɔs wa Pɔɔl ya nibin zenewa maa tiŋ baa? Aai. Ya waa ya Koosa tumtumsa a nyɛ ma nyinbaan di ma se su un. Waa funɛ nyɛ ya nara balɛ na tuma yaa tum.

ref: What matters isn't those who planted or watered, but God who made the plants grow.
text: Naa chɔɔsɛ awa naa la du ni, ba wa ya kundɔŋ, amaa Koosa duŋduŋ nyɛ bugil ti zini.

ref: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
text: Naa chɔɔse awa naa la du ni, ba maga ya nibɛgimnɛ. Koosa la zi bɔ ba ba chim.

ref: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
text: Chenani Koosa dɔ ya chen, nyɛ ya wa un tum tuma. Waa dɔ ma gbanko. Ma chi o ŋmaharɛ oo kɔɔri, a chi o dɛ oo sa.

ref: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
text: Koosa nyɛ min o kpok ti mu ya tɛsal weri a baŋ o dɛ ti sɛchik a nyɛ un. Nandigi wa wa sa zɔŋ o daha. Ba naa maga sa Koosa dɛɛna, ba doso a ya tɛsal were.

ref: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
text: Saha naa Kristo wa bur wa, o zi wa kpa dinu a zaamsi chenani nara maga tuma duu. Chen la zi wa biksi a kiwere koo a wa wer.

ref: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
text: Din ti wa bu kaa vɛ koti a chɔk nar tuma, Koosa zi wa bɔ tum nawara o fun tumu.

ref: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
text: Amaa din ti wa di o tum ta, o wa zi na bɔta. Koosa zi wa yisi unu, amaa o zi wa chi nibine naa ba lisi un o dɛ ni ka din di o kina maga chenu.

ref: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
text: Ma wa zim di ma tusa ya Koosa bar nyɛnirɛɛ? O siim weri ti nyɛn ma niu.

ref: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
text: Nibine naa la chɔk Koosa bar nyɛni ti, Koosa zi chɔk o gbankou, domi o bar nyɛni ti ya bar birigirɛ o wati. Ma titi tusa ya Koosa bar nyɛnirɛ.

ref: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
text: Ma ta nyɛ nandigi wa mur o titi. O lisi di o kpak tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima, o kpa o zima ti a yo ta ka burgi wom, chen ni o zi kpak Koosa zimau.

ref: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima ti ya yɔrirɛ Koosa wati. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “O wa nyaŋi ba naa yaa kpa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima ti aa tum tum tagna.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
text: Ba funɛ vɛɛn sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa zim di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima ti lisi ya yɔrirɛ.”

ref: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
text: Pɔɔl, Apɔlɔs, Pita, tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga, miisa awa sou, a naa maga nyɛn lalaknɔ awa a naa vɛɛn kaa wa, ma kpak a magau.

ref: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
text: Yesu Kristo dɔ ma, ka Koosa ka dɔ Kristo.

ref: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
text: O weru di ma la yir ya Yesu tumtumsi chen. Koosa funɛ kpa o wu sogle a du ya nisa ni. Ya ka kpa a aa biksi nara.

ref: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
text: Tumtum naa la tum nɛnɛ a nyɛ o kunyan ni, chen weru. O kunyan ti zi wa ŋɔ di o ya wuti tiinnɛ.

ref: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
text: Mu bak wa to chenani ma wa nibinsa sɛm mu wɛ a la di min sɛria. Mu gbannɛ wa zi di mu titi sɛria.

ref: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
text: Hali mu ŋɔ di mu tuha chaanu di mu funɛ tum nɛnɛu, chen wa zi biksi di mu zi wa liu. Kristo ya naa la zi wa di min sɛria.

ref: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
text: Ma ta di sɛria ti lalaknɔ. Ma nyɛ Yesu wa bur wa. O wa wa, o zi kpa nibinsa wu sogle a li gbampulimu a biksi ba muna. O wa ya chen, o zi wa nyɛ ba jirimau a li ba tum were ni.

ref: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu kpa mu tuma awa Apɔlɔs tuma di mu biksi ma ma se lɛli ma tiŋ chenani Koosa sɛm tɔn ti biksi ya chen. Maa tiŋ chen, ma wa zi lisi nibin digi a zer un a kaali digi.

ref: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
text: Anti ŋɔ ma di ma kaali ma dɔŋtima? Koosa funɛ nyɛ ma kin naa maga ma kpak. A maga li Koosa watɛɛ, ka yɛrɛ maa daŋni sɔn di ma titi kpokre ma kpak a nan?

ref: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
text: Ma lisi di ma piin kpak kin naa maga ma daŋniu a ya kin tima a burgi a chi koorsa. Chenani maa lisi, chen wa ya wuti. Ya fu daŋni ya wa ma burgi a chi koorsiwa.

ref: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
text: Mu wati Koosa nyɛ o nitumɛ ta tiŋ gantɛl, a chi ba naa ba funɛ di ba sou sɛria. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga awa Koosa malaikasa nyini ya a chi ya ya nibin tagniwa.

ref: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
text: Ya ya womniwa a li nibinsa wati Kristo daha, amaa ma waa ya o nibin nɛɛgɛwa. Ya waa ya o nibin ba naa vɛ kpak kpok a li nibinsa wati, amaa ma waa ya o nibin kpokwa. Nara la filimi ya, amaa ba waa la nyɛ ma jirima.

ref: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
text: Lɔsa awa nyɔksa yaa funɛ kpak yau hali wa zinan ni. Ya yaa laari kizɛrgɛwa. Nara yaa funɛ tuk yau. Ya wa fun kpak bar nyɛni a nyɛn aa go.

ref: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
text: Ya bara wilim nɛɛ ya funɛ di. Nara yaa pini yau, amaa ya yaa sumi Koosau di o suŋi ba. Ba yaa muksi yau kinkan, amaa ya yaa kpak kanyitau ba wati.

ref: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
text: Ba yaa chɔk ya sɔnau, amaa ya yaa fun sɛm sɛm wereu aa nyɛ ba. Ya yaa fun chi chaasiwa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga wati hali wa zinan.

ref: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
text: Mu wa sɔbi nannɛ a nyɛ ma di mu nyɛ hiisa kpak ma, amaa mu daŋni mu dɛɛsi mau a chi mu titi bisa.

ref: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
text: Ma kpak nibinsa ba naa la zi biksi ma Kristo wɛ, nidamatau, amaa nibin bɛgim duŋduŋ ya ma nyana. Mu funɛ te biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛu ma nu un a nyɛ ma se su Yesu Kristo. Chendaha mu ya ma nyana.

ref: and I want you to be like me.
text: Adaha mu sumi mau, maa tiŋ chenani muu tiŋ.

ref: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
text: Mu zi kpa Timotiu a tum ma wati di o wa biksi ma nyinbaan naa ma zi tiŋ wɛ. Waa ya mu bi daŋni a li Kristo ni a ya wuti tiina. Waa la zi wa lisi ma chenani muu tiŋ aa tum tuma Yesu Kristo wati. Chen nyinbaannɛ mu fun kpa biksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn bawu maga ni.

ref: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
text: Ma bɔnɛ zer ma titi kinkan, domi ma lisi di mu wa zi bur wa ma wati,

ref: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
text: amaa mu zi wau. Koosa la nɔɔni, mu zi wau lalaga a zim ba naa la zer ba titi chen. Anti la sɛm nan ni? Ba kpak kpok akoo ba wa kpak kpok, mu daŋni mu zim chen.

ref: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
text: Koosa koor ti wa ya nɔ sɛmi wɛwa. O ya o kpok ti wɛ wa.

ref: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
text: Ma biksi min chenani mu zi wa ma wati. Mu wa ma wati awa da ni a wa tuk ma akoo mu wa ma wati balimm a wa biksi ma chenani mu daŋni maa?

ref: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
text: Mu nuu ka ba ŋɔ min di maa tum chɔkumsi tumiwa. Ma nidigi funɛ kpa o nyan haan a la chɔ. Hali ba naa vɛ zim Koosa chi chenu.

ref: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
text: Baŋnɛ ma zer ma titi nan? O fu lawa ma tusa chɔku kinkan, ka ma lisi baal naa ya chen a li ma lagmi ti ni.

ref: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
text: Chendaha mu ŋɔ mau ma yir ma titi a lagmi dɔŋ ni wa ya kunyan Yesu kpok ti ni. Ma wa lagmi, o zi chi mu gbanko nyɛn ma wati.

ref: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
text: Ma kpa un a du sintana nin ni o muksi un a chɔk o bara, chen ni a wuzu naa ya kunyan ti wa bur wa, Koosa zi wa yisi o miisau.

ref: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
text: Ma sɔn daŋni ti wa wer. Ma wa zim di luri tama yaa nyɛn boroboro sau ni, o yaa nyɛ sau ti maga fɔɔsiu chenn?

ref: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
text: Yaa tiŋ Yesu, o wa werwa ya bur a tum ya titi tum bunɛ a lisi lise tagna a kitum tum tagna. Ma nyɛ ya kpa a nama maga a ta. A waa chi luri ti. Ma nyɛ ya sɛm wuti duŋduŋ a nyɛ ya tusa chaani nɛnɛ. A waa chi boroboro naa vɛ kpak luri ti.

ref: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
text: Mu funɛ te sɔbi tɔnu a nyɛ ma a ŋɔ di ma ta nyɛ ma wa ba naa la tum chɔkumsa tuma kilagmi.

ref: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
text: Mu wa ŋɔ ma di ma wa ba naa vɛ tiŋ Yesu a la tum nan kilagmi dɔŋ ni. Ba naa la daŋni nihaana yɔri yɔri a la daŋni malɛ a burgi ba kina a la ŋmɛ a la jɛɛmi vuga, ba waa nyɛn bawu maga ni.

ref: Why should I judge outsiders? Aren't we supposed to judge only church members?
text: Ba naa vɛ ya Yesu nitiŋɛ ta, mu laata kaa wa di ba sɛria. Koosa kaa wa di ba sɛria,

ref: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
text: amaa ba naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ, ma waa dɔ ba di ma wa di ba sɛria. “Adaha ma yisi chen nibin tagin tiina a li ma ni.”

ref: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
text: Ma naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ nan ka ma wa ma dɔŋtima kpak dɔŋa, baŋnɛ maa yaa vɛ kpa la Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wati ba la di ma sɛria? Amaa ma yaa la ba naa vɛ tiŋ Yesure. Chen deŋluu?

ref: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
text: Ma wa zim di wuzu digi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta zi wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛriauu? Ma kaa wa ya chen wuzu digi, yɛrɛ ma vɛ koti a di wu wisa sɛria lalaknɔ?

ref: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
text: Ma wa zim di wuzu digi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta zi wa di malaika tagna sɛriauu? Ma kaa wa ya chen wuzu digi, yɛrɛ ma vɛ koti a di ma nyɛni kina sɛria?

ref: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
text: Ma wa ma dɔŋtima kpak dɔŋa, ma yaa la ba naa vɛ ya Koosa nara di ba di ma sɛria.

ref: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
text: Chen deŋluu? Hiisa wa kpak ma. Ma wa kpak nibin nɛɛgɛ ba naa la zi koti a kɔɔri ma wu wisa a li ma nii?

ref: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
text: Ma naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ ta yaa kpa dɔŋa a la sɛria bar diili dɛ di ba naa vɛ tiŋ Yesu di ma sɛria.

ref: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
text: Ma wa zim di nibin tagna wa kizu Koosa koor tii? Ma mur ma titirɛ. Ba naa la tum chɔkumsa tuma a la nyɛ vursa jirima, a la daŋni nihaana yɔri yɔri,

ref: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
text: a la ŋmɛ, a kpa malɛ a burgi ba kina, a la nyɔ sin a dɛɛsi, a la chɔk nara sɔna a la fuksi nara a la kpaksi nara kina kpok ni, ba nidigi wa kikoti a zu Koosa koor ti.

ref: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
text: Ma bɔnɛ funɛ ya chene, amaa lalaknɔ Koosa funɛ chaksi ma tum tagnau a li ma ni. Koosa dɔ ma. Ma wa bu kpak galimsa o wati Yesu Kristo awa Koosa siim weri ti tum daha. Ma Kpa Ma Bara Aa Nyɛ Koosa Jirima

ref: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
text: Ma bɔnɛ yaa ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Ya kpak nyinbaanu ya ya kina maga ya daŋniu.” Amaa mu ŋɔ ma di kina maga la ta wer wa ya. Ya wa zi la nɔɔni a nyɛ kin nama dɔ ya.

ref: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
text: Ma bɔnɛ gbanko sɛm dɔŋ ni, “Lo daha diwe nyɛn. Diili daha lo gbanko nyɛn.” Amaa wuzu digi Koosa zi wa chɔk a magau. Ya bara wa nyɛn chɔkumsa tuma daharɛ. Ya kunyan daharɛ a nyɛn. Ya kunyan ti gbanko dɔ ya bara di ya kpa a aa tum a nyɛ un.

ref: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
text: Koosa funɛ lɛli ya kunyan ti a li o sou ni. Chene o zi wa kpa o kpok ti a wa lɛli ya gbanko.

ref: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
text: Ma wa zim di ma bara ta ya Kristo bara luk pɛksiwaa? Mu kpa Kristo bara luk pɛkha a la lagmi a chɔ nihaan sakaal, chen weruu? Aai.

ref: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
text: Ma wa zim di nar wa sakaal chɔ, ba bara ya nibɛgimnɛɛ? Koosa sɛm tɔn ti biksi ya nan wɛ, a ŋɔ, “Nara balɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni a chɔ, a burgi nibɛgim.”

ref: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
text: Amaa ba naa wa ya kunyan ti ya nibɛgim, ba siimsa gbanko ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
text: Adaha ma chɔ li doonani chɔkumsi nyɛn. Wu tagin maga naa nara tum, a wa ya ba barawa baa ya chen. Amaa nibine naa la daŋni nihaana yɔri yɔri, o chɔk o titi barawa.

ref: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
text: Ma wa zim di ma bara ya Koosa siim weri ti bar nyɛnirɛɛ? Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ ma.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
text: Ma wa dɔ ma titi, domi Koosa yau ma o titi. Ma yawi funɛ iiu kinkan. Adaha ma doso a kpa ma bara ta aa nyɛ un jirima.

ref: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
text: Ma funɛ sɔbi tɔnnɛ a pɛɛsi min di nibaal vɛ kpa haan, chen weruu?

ref: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
text: O weru. Amaa chɔkumsi kinkan daha, o deŋlu di nibaal kpak o titi haanu. Nihaan gbanko kpak o titi baal.

ref: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
text: Nihaan wa dɔ o bara. O baal ti dɔ o bara. Chen vɛɛna, o baal ti wa dɔ o titi bara. O haan ti dɔ o bara.

ref: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
text: Adaha ma ta vɛ dɔŋa. Nibaal awa o haan vɔwɛɛ a ŋɔ di ba ha gil dɔŋa wuzu bilɛ, chen ni ba zi sumi Koosau awa tu kibɛgim ni a bu wa lagmi dɔŋ ni, chen weree. Amaa o vɛ ya chene, ma ta waligi dɔŋ ni, chen ni sintana wa zi koti a zaamsi ma, domi ma wa kikoti a kpak ma titi nɛnɛ.

ref: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
text: Mu daŋni mu ŋɔ bazooma, luhaana awa butoosa watɛɛ. Ma fu tiŋ min a vɛ deu haanasa awa baalasa yuuli, chen bɔɛɛ.

ref: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
text: Amaa ma vɛ koti a kpak ma titi, ma kpa haanasa aa yu baalasa. Chen bɔɛɛ ma vɛ koti a kpak ma titi.

ref: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
text: Mu daŋni mu ŋɔ nihaana ba naa piin kpak baalasa ba ta vɛ ba baalasi ta. A wa ya mu titi sɛmwa. A ya Koosa sɛmwa.

ref: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
text: Mu ŋɔ nannɛ a nyɛ mu dɔŋtim ta ni. Nibaal naa ya Yesu nitiŋi aa kpak haan naa vɛ ya Yesu nitiŋi, ka ba lagmi nyɛn wa dɔŋ ni ka chen wer wa haan ti ni, o baal ti ta duŋni un taali. Mu titi zima nɛɛ mu ŋɔ nan. Ya kunyan ti laata ŋɔ.

ref: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
text: Nihaan gbanko ya Yesu nitiŋi ka o baal vɛ ya Yesu nitiŋi, ka ba lagmi nyɛn wa dɔŋ ni ka chen wer wa baal ti, o haan ti ta wa vɛ un.

ref: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
text: Amaa nibaal koo nihaan ba naa vɛ ya Yesu nitiŋɛ, daŋni o vɛ nihaan koo nibaal ba naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ, ma ta tɔ ba nyinbaan. Naa vɛ ya Yesu nitiŋɛ daŋni o ya chen, Yesu nitiŋɛ ta la nɔɔni a nyɛ un. Koosa funɛ yir ma di ma wa o wati a wa kpak bara sɔɔna.

ref: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
text: Ma nihaana, ma wa zim di Koosa zi kotiu a yisi ma baalasa a li ba tum tagna ni ma dahaa? Ma nibaala, ma wa zim di Koosa zi kotiu a yisi ma haanasa a li ba tum tagna ni ma dahaa? Ma Ta Chɛrgi A Li Ma Nyɛni Ni

ref: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
text: Adaha nara maga kitum aa di a chi chenani o fun piinsa tum aa di hali Koosa fun yir un. Chene Koosa daŋni nara maga kitum aa di. Mu biksi Koosa nitiŋɛ ta bawu maga ni a chi chen.

ref: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
text: Ba teŋ un o ya yɔrirɛ. Ba vɛ teŋ un, chen gbanko ya yɔrirɛ. Amaa Koosa nɔ tiŋi ya wu birigi.

ref: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
text: Adaha ma tum aa di a chi chenani ma fun piinsa tum aa di hali Koosa fun yir ma.

ref: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
text: I funɛ ya yoma ka Koosa yir hin, ta nyɛ chen muksi hin. Amaa ba daŋni ba lisi hinu a chausi, i la nɔɔni.

ref: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
text: Domi nibine naa ya yoma ka ya kunyan ti yir un, o ya nibine naa dɔ o titirɛ o wati. Nibine naa dɔ o titi ka ya kunyan ti yir un, o ya Kristo yomne.

ref: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
text: Waa funɛ yau ma. Ma yawi funɛ iiu kinkan. Adaha ma ta lisi di maa tum a nyɛ nibinsi nee.

ref: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
text: Ma funɛ sɔbi tɔnnɛ a pɛɛsi min ba naa vɛ kpak baalasa awa ba naa vɛ kpak haanasa wɛ. Ya kunyan ti wa nyɛ min chen chiksi, amaa mu zi sɛm ma chenani mu yaa funɛ lisiu. Waa funɛ ɔm min siki, chen ni mu ya o wuti tiina.

ref: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
text: Wu namsɛ zi wa pu yau lalaga. Chendaha o weru mu wati chenani nibine nyɛn nan, o kut kpak chen.

ref: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
text: I kpak haan, ta vɛ un. I ka vɛ kpak haan, ta dɛu haan.

ref: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
text: I kaa kpa haan, o wa ya di i tum galimsau. I kaa yu baal, o wa ya di i tum galimsau. Ba naa kpak baalasa awa haanasa, ba waa la na namsi a kaali ba naa vɛ kpak baalasa awa haanasa. Mu wara daŋni ma namsi a chi ba.

ref: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
text: Mu niinbisa, wu namsɛ saha duglɛu. Nan daha ma naa kpak haanasa, ma nyɛn a chi ba wa kpak haanasa.

ref: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
text: Ma naa la wi nan, ma nyɛn a chi ma tusa sɔɔnɛu. Ma naa kpak tu sɔɔna, ma nyɛn a chi ma tusa wa sɔɔni. Ma naa la yausi kina gbanko, ma nyɛn a chi kin ta wa ya ma kina.

ref: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
text: Ma naa kpa ma bambe maga a du tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina ni, ma nyɛn a chi ma se wa sɛri a ni, domi o wa zi vusi ka tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga wa chɔk.

ref: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
text: Mu wara daŋni tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina kimuksi ma tusa kinkan. Nibaal naa vɛ kpak haan, o nyɛ o si sɛri a du ya kunyan ti tum ni, chen ni o tuha zi sɔɔniu.

ref: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
text: Nihaan naa vɛ yu baal, o si yaa sɛri a li ya kunyan ti tum ni, chen ni o zi dɔ o bara awa o siimau. Nihaan naa yu baal, o si yaa sɛri a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina ni, chen ni o zi sɔɔni o baal ti tuhau.

ref: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
text: Mu la sɛm ma nan, mu daŋni mu suŋi mau. Mu wara daŋni mu tɔ ma nyinbaan. Mu daŋni ma ya wi naa deŋlee ka nyɛ ma si sɛri aa kitum ya kunyan ti tuma duŋduŋ.

ref: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
text: Nibaal naa kpak butowe a vɔu di o wa kpa un ka o bambi chɛrgi di chen wa deŋli wa o kpa un, amaa o bambi nyɛn un ni kinkan, o kpa un. Chen wa ya wu tagin,

ref: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
text: Adaha nibaal kpa butowe naa o vɔu di o kpa, o ya nɛnɛu. Amaa o vɛ kpa un, chen bɔɛɛ.

ref: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
text: Nihaan naa kpak baal, o wa bu kpak nyinbaan wa o gil o baal ti a la yu baal digi. Amaa o baal ti sowɛɛ, o kpak nyinbaanu wa o yu baal naa o daŋniu. Chen tiina lawa o ya Yesu nitiŋirɛ.

ref: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
text: Mu wati, o gil baal yuuli, o tuha zi sɔɔniu a kaali o yu baal. Mu la sɛm nan, mu lisu di Koosa siim weri ti biksi min.

ref: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
text: Ma funɛ sɔbi tɔnnɛ a pɛɛsi min sela a naa ba yaa kɔrigi a nyɛ vursi ni chen wɛ. Mu zim di ya maga kpak Koosa zima ti a chi chenani ma sɛm, amaa zima nyɛ nara zer ba titi. Amaa yaa daŋni dɔŋa, chen la zi suŋi ya.

ref: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
text: Nar lisi di o nɛɛgɛu, o wa zim kundɔŋ a chi chenani o fu zi zim,

ref: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
text: amaa nar la daŋni Koosa, warɛ Koosa zim.

ref: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
text: Ma nyɛ ya bur gantɛl a la sela a naa ba yaa kɔrigi a nyɛ vursi ni. Ya zimu di vursa wa to tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a kpak miisa. Ya zimu di Koosa bɛgim duŋduŋ nyɛnɛɛ.

ref: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
text: Kin nawara nibinsa yir ba vursa awa ba kunyama, a iiu kinkan. Ba sɛm di a nyɛn buudaha ni awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni.

ref: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
text: Amaa ya wati, Koosa bɛgim nyɛnɛɛ. Waa ya ya kunyan ti a fun naami kina maga. Ya ya o bisiwa. Ya kunyana Yesu Kristo gban ya nibɛgimnɛ. Koosa funɛ nyɛ o naami kina maga a nyɛ ya miisa.

ref: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
text: Amaa nara nidamata laata zim di nan ya wutiu. Nibinsa ba naa funɛ nyɛ vursa jirima kinkan, bɔnɛ wa se vursa nɛmɛ, ba yaa lisi di ba jɛɛmi vursi tawa. O fun muksi ba di nɛmɛ naa ba se chen, zi nyɛ ba doŋu.

ref: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
text: Diwe wa kikoti a kpa ya la Koosa wati. Ya di a koo ya chi, a mak ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
text: Amaa ma doso. Ma vɛ chi a a la se vursa namsa, ma ta nyɛ ma yaali ti mur ba naa ŋɔ di namsi ta zi nyɛ ma dɔŋu, a wa chel.

ref: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
text: Ma nibɛgim zim wuti nɔ a la doonani ba kɔɔri vursa a la se namsa ka i dɔŋtiina naa vɛ zim wuti nɔ nɛnɛ, na hin dooni, o gbanko zi kpak bambiu a la zu dooni a se namsi ta. O wa se peti, o tuha zi chɔku kinkan namsi ta daha.

ref: Then what you know has destroyed someone Christ died for.
text: Kristo funɛ wa sou i dɔŋtiin ti daharɛ, amaa ma chɔk unu ma zima ti daha.

ref: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
text: Adaha nɛmɛ mu zi se bur mu dɔŋtima gantɛl, mu wa zi bu se un, chen ni mu dɔŋtim ta wa zi wa chel.

ref: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
text: Mu wati, mu wa dɔ mu titii? Mu wa ya Yesu Kristo nitumɛ ta digirɛɛ? Mu wa fun na ya kunyan Yesuu? Ma waa tiŋ Yesu domi mu funɛ wa ma wati a biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛɛ?

ref: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
text: Bɔnɛ wara la nɔɔni di mu ya Kristo nitumirɛ. Yɛrɛ ma lisi? Chenani maa tiŋ ya kunyan ti biksi nara di mu ya o nitumirɛ.

ref: When people question me, I tell them
text: Bɔnɛ beeliu di mu wa ya o nitumi ti, nannɛ mu ya ŋɔ ba.

ref: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
text: Kristo nitumɛ ta kpak nyinbaan di nara nyɛ ba diwe awa kinyɔɔli ba tum daha. Mu wa kpak chen nyinbaan?

ref: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
text: Kristo nitumɛ ta, ya kunyan ti niinbisa awa Pita, ba maga funɛ kpak haanasiwa. Ba wa ba haanasi ta la tiŋ dɔŋa a gba, mu gbanko wa kpak chen nyinbaan?

ref: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
text: Mu wa Barnabas duŋduŋ kaa tum aa di ya bar wilim nii?

ref: Do soldiers pay their own salaries? Don't people who raise grapes eat some of what they grow? Don't shepherds get milk from their own goats?
text: I ya lanjima a li la laal ni, o wa ya i kunyan malɛwa i kaa la dii? I par i ŋmaha, i wa zi di o diwee? I gbaŋ nuhɛ, i wa zi nyɔ nuhɛ ta nuill?

ref: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
text: Mosis funɛ sɔbiu a ŋɔ, “Ma ta vɔu nuha naa la sɔrgi niŋmɛna di o chɛri a, nɔ.” Koosa funɛ daŋni nuhɛ ta duŋduŋ suŋirɛ daha o fun nyɛ Mosis sɔbi nan nii?

ref: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
text: Akoo o funɛ kaa suŋi nibinsiwa a fun nyɛ o sɔbi nann? Wuti ni o chi ya daharɛ o fun sɔbi chen, chen ni ya zi zimu di ba naa la par ba ŋmaha ba kipar wa bambi bɛgim, ka ba naa la kum, ba kikum wa bambi bɛgim.

ref: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
text: Chenani ya fun a biksi ma Koosa wɛ nan, o wa deŋli di ya la diwe ma watii?

ref: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
text: Ma nyɛ bɔnɛ ni chene. Ba kpak chen nyinbaan a la diwe ma wati, ya waa wa kpak chen nyinbaan a kaali baa? Ya ha wa ŋɔ ma di ma ya chen a nyɛ ya. Ya kpak kanyitarɛ kina maga ni, chen ni ya wa zi tɔ Kristo laaboor weri ti nyinbaan.

ref: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
text: Ma zimu di ba naa la tum Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni a li Jɛrusalɛm ni di a li ba tum nɛɛ. Ba naa la kɔɔri kɔɔra, baa di kɔɔri ti bar digirɛ.

ref: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
text: Chene ya kunyan ti fun sɛm a nyɛ ba naa la biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ di ba di a li ba tum ta ni.

ref: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
text: Mu kpak nyinbaan naa mu zi tum a nama magau, amaa mu wa tiŋ chen. Mu wa sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ma di ma ya chen a nyɛ min. Mu daŋni mu te biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri tiu a sou wa lɔsa a zi ŋɔ ma ma nyɛ min diwe.

ref: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
text: O wa deŋli wa muu daŋni sɔn domi mu la biksi nara Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ daha. O funɛ kpak minu a ŋɔ min mu ya chen. Mu vɛ biksi o laaboor weri ti, yɛrɛ o zi du min ni?

ref: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
text: O fu ya mu titi tutɔrɛ mu biksi nan, o fu deŋlu wa mu la nyɔɔra a niu. Amaa Koosa funɛ kpa o laaboor weri ti a du mu nin ni a ŋɔ di mu kpa un aa nyɛ nara ni. Adaha mu laata kpa mu titi aa tum nan ni.

ref: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
text: Mu tum ta nyɔɔra ya baŋa? Mu nyɔɔra ya mu kpa Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ nara ni a ta la kundɔŋ. Saha maga mu wara tiŋ nyinbaan naa mu kpak, domi mu ya Yesu Kristo nitiŋɛ ta digirɛ.

ref: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
text: Nandigi wa dɔ min, amaa mu funɛ kpa mu titirɛ a burgi yoma a tum a nyɛ nara maga ni, chen ni mu zi kotiu a la ba nidamata a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
text: Mu wa Ju tim ta lagmi nyɛn, mu yaa kpa mu titiu a burgi ba, chen ni mu zi la ba bɔnɛu a nyɛ Koosa ni. Mu wa ba naa tiŋ Ju tim ta chiksɛ ta wa lagmi, mu gbanko yaa tiŋ ba chiksɛ tawa, chen ni mu zi la ba bɔnɛu a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
text: Mu wa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima a vɛ tiŋ Ju tim chiksɛ ta, wa lagmi, mu yaa kpa mu titiu a burgi ba, chen ni mu gbanko zi la ba bɔnɛu a nyɛ Koosa ni. Mu kpa mu titi a burgi nara nama, mu ha vɛɛn tiŋ Koosa nɔrɛ. Muu tiŋ chenani Yesu fun biksi ya di yaa tiŋnɛ.

ref: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
text: Mu wa ba naa se vɛ su Yesu nɛnɛ, wa lagmi, mu yaa burgi ba warɛ, chen ni mu zi suŋi bau di ba tiŋ un nɛnɛ. Chenani nibinsa du, mu gbanko du chene, chen ni mu zi la ba bɔnɛu a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
text: Mu ya chene o laaboor weri ti daha, chen ni mu zi wa na kiwere a naa Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ ya chenu.

ref: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
text: Ma zim di nara yaa chɔ chɔchɔha di ba la di chen, nara kinkan yaa chɔ a la, amaa nibɛgim yaa la di. Ma Yesu tiŋi chi chɔchɔharɛ. Maa chɔ nɛnɛ, chen ni ma zi wa diu.

ref: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
text: Chendaha mu yaa chɔu a deŋli a la tel o bɔ. Mu gban chi nara nitukirɛ. Mu wa mou a kru.

ref: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
text: Mu yaa muksi mu barawa aa kpak mu titi nɛnɛ, chen ni mu titi wa zi wa nyuksi laaboor weri ti papaha naa mu kpa biksi nara chen.

ref: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni ma lisiu di Koosa dobaal funɛ kok Ju tim nawaalsa ba naa maga funɛ tiŋ Mosis saha digi. Ba maga funɛ val a gali ni sɛm ti niu a kaali.

ref: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
text: Koosa funɛ tum alaazibu tuma a kpa diwe a nyɛ ba di ba di,

ref: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
text: a kpa ni a nyɛ ba di ba nyɔ. Ba fun na ka Mosis nyɛ ni li gbɛntɛl ni. Kristo chi gbɛntɛl tirɛ a tiŋ ba aa la,

ref: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
text: amaa Koosa tuha wa fun sɔɔni a li ba nidamata ni, ka o fun chɔk ba nyabaan ni di ba siti.

ref: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
text: Chenani ba funɛ siti chen biksi ya di ya doso a ta tum tum tagna a chi ba.

ref: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
text: Ya ta nyɛ vursa wa jirima a chi chenani ba bɔnɛ ya. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Nara kanɛɛ a di kikɔrigɛ ta a nyɔ sin kpoka a si a gɔ.”

ref: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
text: Ya ta tum chɔkumsa tuma a chi chenani ba bɔnɛ funɛ tum chɔkumsa tum chen. Koosa fun kpu ba nara tusugsa fumlɛ datura wuzu bɛgim.

ref: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
text: Ma bɔnɛ yaa ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Ya kpak nyinbaanu wa yaa ya kina maga ya daŋniu.” Amaa kina maga laata la suŋi ya di ya tiŋ Koosa aa gbaari.

ref: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
text: Ya ta zaamsɛ ya kunyan ti a chi chenani ba bɔnɛ funɛ zaamsi un chen. O fun nyɛ duma dusi ba ba siti.

ref: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
text: Kin na funɛ ya bau di a biksi ya. Ba fun sɔbi chenani funɛ ya di o biksi ya naa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga peti saha, di ya fiɛŋ ya wa tiŋ a chi chenani ba funɛ tiŋ.

ref: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
text: Chendaha naa lisɛɛ di o chiŋɛ nɛnɛu, o doso a ta wa cheli a chi ba.

ref: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
