text: Sintana zaamsi nibine magau di ba tum tum tagna. Chenani o zaamsi ba, o zaamsi ma gbankou. Amaa Koosa ya wuti tiina. O wa zi nyɛ wu zaamsɛ a naa kaali ma kpok, wa pu ma, chen ni ma wa zi wa chel. O zi nyɛ ma kpoku ma kpak kanyita wa wu zaamsɛ ni a yisi ma a li a ni.

ref: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma gil vursa jirima nyɛɛli.

ref: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
text: Ma ya nibin nɛɛgɛwa. Adaha ma nu chenani mu ŋɔ ma nan nɛnɛ.

ref: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
text: Ya wa lagmi di ya lisi ya kunyan ti siwi we, kinyɔɔli naa ya sumi Koosa a du a kpa nyɔ chen, ya wa yaa lisi o chɛl naa funɛ li a suŋi ya chenee? Boroboro naa ya yaa kpa se chen, ya wa yaa lisi o bara naa funɛ sou a suŋi ya chenee?

ref: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
text: Ma lisi Ju tim wɛ. Ba naa la di kikɔɔrɛ a naa ba kɔɔri a nyɛ Koosa ni chen, bɔnɛ ba wa a ya kibɛgimnɛɛ?

ref: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
text: Chenani mu daŋni mu ŋɔ ma ya di vursi ta kidigi wa ya kundɔŋ, akoo kikɔɔrɛ ta ba kɔɔri aa nyɛ a chen, a wa ya kundɔŋ.

ref: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
text: Mu ŋɔ mau di ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima, ba yaa kɔɔri kɔɔrɛ a nyɛ alizin tagna nɛɛ. O wa ya Koosarɛ ba kɔɔri aa nyɛ. Mu wara daŋni ma wa alizin tagni ta ya kibɛgim.

ref: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
text: Ma wa kilagmi a nyɔ ya kunyan kinyɔɔli ti a bu la nyɔ alizin tagna kinyɔɔlɛ. Ma wa kilagmi a se ya kunyan boroboro ti a bu la se alizin tagna namsa.

ref: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
text: Ya daŋni ya kunyan ti na paannɛ a du ya nii? Ya waa kpok a kaali unn?

ref: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
text: O wa werwa nibine kidaŋni o duŋduŋ suŋi, amaa o lawa o daŋni o dɔŋtima suŋi gbankou.

ref: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
text: Ma kpak nyinbaan wa ma se nɛmɛ a naa maga ba yogli a li yau ni, a ta beeli a pɛɛsi doonani o liili.

ref: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyan ti dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa kin naa maga nyɛn un ni.”

ref: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
text: Ba naa se vɛ su Koosa digi yir ma di ma wa un lagmi dɔŋ ni a di ka ma vɔu di ma la, ma di diwe naa o kpa nyɛ ma chen, a ta beeli a pɛɛsi doonani o liili.

ref: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
text: O wa ya ma lisi daharɛ, amaa ma dɔŋtim ta lise daha. Chendaha mu ŋɔ ma di ma ta diili. Ma nidigi zi ŋɔ minu, “Mu vɛ chi chen, baŋnɛ mu kaa kpak mu titi mu dɔŋtim ta lisi daha?

ref: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
text: Mu zaami Koosa a la di, yɛrɛ nandigi kaa galimi min diwe naa mu sumi Koosa a du a la di ni?”

ref: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
text: Amaa kina maga maa ya a la di koo a nyɔ, maa ya a maga a kinyɛ Koosa wa jirima.

ref: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
text: Adaha maa tiŋ min a chi chenani mu ya Yesu nitiŋi a la tiŋ un nan. O Lawa Nihaana Tɔ Ba Nyuunau A Kijɛɛmi Koosa

ref: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
text: Mu pɛgi mau domi maa tiŋ biksi naa maga Koosa fun biksi min chen ka mu kpa un a biksi ma.

ref: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
text: Mu daŋni ma zimu di Kristo ya nibaal nyuhu tiina ka nibaal ka ya o haan nyuhu tiina. Koosa gbanko ka ya Kristo nyuhu tiina.

ref: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
text: Ma wa lagmi dɔŋ ni, nibaal naa la sumi Koosa akoo a kpa o siim weri ti kpok a sɛm sɛma a kpa kundɔŋ a tɔ o nyuha, o du Kristo wa hiisau.

ref: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
text: Nihaan naa la sumi Koosa akoo a kpa o siim weri ti kpok ti a sɛm sɛma a ka vɛ kpa kundɔŋ a tɔ o nyuha, o du o baal wa hiisau. O haan ti chi nibine naa yaa funi o nyuhurɛ.

ref: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
text: Nihaan vɛ kpa kundɔŋ a tɔ o nyuha, o vaan funi o nyuksi ta a ta. Chenani o ya hiisa wa nihaan funi o nyuha, o werwa o kpa kundɔŋu aa tɔ o nyuha.

ref: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
text: O wa werwa nibaal tɔ o nyuha, domi o ya Koosa chɛmsarɛ a biksi chenani o kpok ti jirima du. Nihaan ya o baal chɛmsarɛ a biksi chenani o kpok ti jirima du.

ref: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
text: Koosa wa fun naami nibaal a li nihaan wati, amaa o funɛ naami nihaan a li nibaal watɛɛ.

ref: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
text: Koosa wa fun naami nibaal nihaan daharɛ, amaa o funɛ naami nihaan nibaal daharɛ.

ref: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
text: Chendaha o lawa nihaan yaa zer kundɔŋu a tɔ o nyuha. Chen la zi biksi di nibaal dɔ un. Koosa malaikasa la nyini ma, adaha maa ya chen.

ref: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
text: Chenani ya tiŋ ya kunyan ti nan, nibaala awa nihaana ya nibɛgimnɛ o wati.

ref: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
text: Saha digi nihaan funɛ li nibaal watɛɛ, amaa lalaknɔ nihaan la lul nibaal. Kina maga li Koosa watɛɛ.

ref: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
text: Maa lisi nɛnɛ a ŋɔ min. O werwa nihaan kisumi Koosa ka kundɔŋ vɛ tɔ o nyuhauu?

ref: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
text: Amaa nihaan nyuksa nyu aa ziŋ, chen weru. Chen la zi nyɛ o wer. Koosa nyɛ un chen di o tɔ o titi.

ref: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
text: Amaa nandigi daŋni o di amɔrɛ, chene ya wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn bawu maga ni, yaa tiŋ. Chenani Ma Zi Di Ya Kunyana Diwe Wa Wuti Ni (Mat.

ref: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
text: Kin namawa mu daŋni mu biksi ma maa tiŋ a. Mu wa zi pɛgi ma kin ta daha. Ma wa lagmi dɔŋ ni chen wara suŋi ma, amaa o ya tau barigi mau a kaali kiwere.

ref: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
text: Nara funɛ ŋɔ min ma wɛu di ma wa lagmi dɔŋ ni a sumi Koosa, ma wara kpak nɔ bɛgim. Mu lisi di a bɔnɛ ya wutirɛ ba sɛm.

ref: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
text: O wa kpak beeli waligi nyɛn ma ni, chen ni ma zi kotiu a na ba naa la tiŋ wuti ti.

ref: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
text: Domi ma bɔnɛ yaa di ma titi diwewa lalaga. Lɔsa yaa kpak bɔnɛ. Bɔnɛ ka yaa nyɔu hali a dɛɛsi.

ref: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
text: Saha naa maga ma wa se boroboro ti a la nyɔ kinyɔɔli ti, nara zi lisi chenani ya kunyan ti sowu. Maa ya chen hali a wuzu naa o kaa wa bur wa.

ref: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
text: O vɛ ya chen ka nibine se a la nyɔ a, o nyɛ Koosarɛ na paan a du un ni, domi o wa te zaamsi o titi nɛnɛ a lisi di o ya ya kunyan ti barawa awa o chɛllɛ.

ref: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
text: Ba funɛ di diwe ta peti, o zer gbakme a ŋɔ, “Kinyɔɔli naa chi mu chɛl nan, biksi ya wu fali bɛgimnɛ di ya wa Koosa ya nɔ bɛgimnɛ. Maa nyɔ un a kilisi mu wɛ.”

ref: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
text: Nannɛ ya kunyan Yesu fun biksi min mu fun kpa un a nyɛ ma. Dusum nɛ Judas fun li ya kunyan Yesu gantɛl, o wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun lagmi a di diwe. O fun kpa boroboro,

ref: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
text: a sumi Koosa a du a chɔksi a ŋɔ, “Mu bara nɛɛnɛ mu kpa a nyɛ ma. Maa ya chen a kilisi mu wɛ.”

ref: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
text: Adaha o lawa nibine maga te zaamsi o titiu nɛnɛ a zi se boroboro ti aa nyɔ kinyɔɔli ti.

ref: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
text: Chendaha ma nidamata wi a vɛ kpak kpok ka ma bɔnɛ fun siti.

ref: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
text: Ya fu te zaamsi ya tusa, Koosa fu wa zi di ya sɛria a kimuksi ya.

ref: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
text: Wuzu digi ya kunyan ti zi wa bur wau a na tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga galimsa a ta ba yɔri. Chendaha o di ya sɛria a lɛrgi ya diŋsa lalaknɔ, chen ni ya wa zi gbaari ba ni.

ref: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma yaa lagmi dɔŋ ni di ma di ya kunyan ti diwe ta, ma yaa kipɔ dɔŋa. Ma ta yaa kitaŋsi a kidiili.

ref: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
text: Lɔsa kpak ma nidigi, o yaa di o dam ni a zi wa. Ma ta di diwe naa vɛ kpak nyinbaan wa ma diili, chen ni Koosa wa zi na paan a du ma ni. Mu wa bur wa ma wati, mu zi biksi ma wɛ naa kaɛɛ.

ref: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
text: Ma zimu di saha naa ma ha fun vɛ zim Koosa chen, vursa a naa vɛ kpak miisa chen, funɛ dɔ ma a nyɛ ma nyuksi.

ref: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
text: Adaha mu daŋni ma zimu di nibine naa Koosa siim weri ti nyɛn, o wa zi koti a ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ Yesu ni. Nibine wa to a la zi koti a sɛm nara ni di Yesu ya o kunyannɛ, see Koosa siim weri ti nyɛn un ni.

ref: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
text: Koosa siim weri papaha a naa o kpa nyɛ ya chen, kurgu. Amaa siim weri bɛgim ti, waa nyɛ a.

ref: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
text: Tum ta ya kunyan ti bɔni ya chen kurgu. Amaa ya maga tum ya kunyan bɛgim ti watɛɛ.

ref: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
text: Kpok naa maga Koosa bɔni ya chen, a maga kurgu, amaa Koosa bɛgim ti waa nyɛ ya kpok ti maga.

ref: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
text: Koosa siim weri ti funɛ kpa o papaha a nyɛ ma nibɛgim bɛgim ni di maa suŋi dɔŋa.

ref: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
text: Koosa bɛgim siim weri ti funɛ nyɛ bɔnɛ wa nɛha sɛma a nyɛ bɔnɛ kpak zima.

ref: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
text: Waa nyɛ bɔnɛ se suuli a nyɛ bɔnɛ kpak kpok a koti a tiibi nara,

ref: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
text: a nyɛ bɔnɛ kpok di baa tum alaazibu tuma. Bɔnɛ koti aa sɛm Koosa wɛ a li o kpok ti ni, ka bɔnɛ koti aa zim papaha a naa li Koosa wati awa papaha a naa vɛ li o wati. Bɔnɛ sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, ka bɔnɛ koti a biksi a muna aa nyɛ nara ni.

ref: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
text: Amaa Koosa bɛgim siim weri ti kpa papaha nama maga a nyɛ ya. O tutɔ nɛɛ, o nyɛ ya papaha a kurgi. Ya Bara Luk Pɛksa Iiu

ref: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
text: Ya bɔnɛ ya Ju timwa. Ya bɔnɛ ka wa ya Ju tima. Ya bɔnɛ ya yosa ka ya bɔnɛ ka dɔ ya titi. Amaa Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri tiu a du ya maga ni ya burgi nibɛgim.

ref: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
text: Nibine bara luk pɛkha duŋduŋ laata ya bara, amaa a iiu.

ref: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
text: Nibine naŋgeri ŋɔɛɛ di o wa ya nin, chendaha o wa to o bara ni, o sunu. O wa ya nin, amaa o gbanko nyɛn a bara nɛɛ.

ref: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
text: Nibine diŋna gban ŋɔɛɛ di o wa ya si, chendaha o wa to o bara ni, o sunu. O wa ya si, amaa o gbanko nyɛn o bara nɛɛ.

ref: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
text: Bara fu kpak se duŋduŋ a vɛ kpak diŋsa, yɛrɛ o fu kaa ya aa nu? Bara fu kpak diŋ duŋduŋ a vɛ kpak misa, yɛrɛ o fu kaa ya aa nu sɔɔra?

ref: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
text: Amaa chen laata Koosa fun naami ya. O funɛ kpa ya bara luk pɛksiwa a lagmi a chi chenani o daŋni.

ref: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
text: Bara fu ya luk pɛk bɛgim duŋduŋ, bara fu wa zi nyɛn.

ref: It takes many parts to make a single body.
text: Amaa bara luk pɛksa iiu. A maga ka ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
text: Se wa kikoti a ŋɔ nin ni di o wara daŋni un. Nyuha gbanko wa kikoti a ŋɔ naŋgere ni di o wara daŋni un.

ref: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
text: Aai. Bara luk pɛksa a naa vɛ kpoki, a ya kibirige kinkan.

ref: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
text: A naa vɛ kpak jirima, a waa ya a naa ya daŋni aa la nyɛ jirima kinkan. Bara luk pɛksa a naa vɛ weri nan, ya waa la nyini a.

ref: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
text: Amaa a naa weree, ya ta bu nyini a kinkan. Chendaha Koosa fun kpa ya bara luk pɛksa a lagmi dɔŋ ni, chen ni ya zi nyɛ a naa vɛ kpak jirima chen, wa jirimau.

ref: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
text: Adaha waligi wa to bara titi ni, amaa o luk pɛksa a naa kurgɛ chen, a maga baga nyɛn dɔŋ niu.

ref: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
text: Bara luk pɛkha vɛ kpak alaafɛ, o bara maga la wi chen. Nara pɛgi bara luk pɛkha digi, ba pɛgi bara magawa.

ref: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
text: Ma maga ya Kristo barawa. Ma maga nibɛgim bɛgim ya o bara luk pɛksiwa.

ref: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
text: Ma maga wa ya Koosa nitumɛ ta. Ma maga wa ya o witinsi ta. Ma maga wa ya o nibiksɛ ta. Ma maga wara tum alaazibu tum ta.

ref: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
text: Ma maga wara tiibi nara akoo a sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, koo a biksi sɛm kurgɛ ta muna,

ref: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
text: amaa ma nyɛ ma se sɛri Koosa papaha birige ta ni. Ma nyɛ mu biksi ma kinkin naa kaali a nama maga.

ref: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
text: Mu koti a sɛm sɛm kurgɛ hali awa malaikasa sɛma a vɛ daŋni nara, mu chɛɛmiu a chi nibine naa ba yaa mo luŋa ka o vɛ zim o mun.

ref: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Mu koti a biksi nara Koosa wɛ nɛnɛ o kpok ti ni, a nɛɛgi kinkan a zim Koosa kisogle a kpak kpok a zi koti a ŋɔ bi ni di o si o bar chiŋi ti ni aa la bar digi. Mu koti aa tum nan maga a vɛ daŋni nara, o ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
text: Mu kpa mu kina maga a nyɛ fara tim ni a kpa mu titi a la du din ni a sou Koosa daha a vɛ daŋni nara, o nyɔɔra ya baŋa?

ref: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
text: O wa zi sɛm wu yɔrisa a nyɛ nara ni. O wa zi ya si tiina koo paan tiina. O wa zi lisi wu tagna naa nara tum a nyɛ un.

ref: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
text: Lalaknɔ ya wa zim kina maga a vɛ koti a sɛm sɛma a li Koosa kpok ti ni nɛnɛ.

ref: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
text: Yesu wa bur wa, ya zi wa ya kina maga petiu.

ref: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
text: Mu funɛ ya bichɛla, mu funɛ sɛmu aa la lisi bichɛl lise. Amaa mu funɛ wa bi a burgi nihɛɛ, mu funɛ gil bichɛsi yaaliu.

ref: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
text: Kin nawara ya yaa na nan, a chi chenani ya yaa nyini diga buni ni chene. Amaa wuzu digi ya zi wa na kina magau nɛnɛ. Zima zen naa ha nyɛn gantɛl ni aa wa nan, kaali zima naa maga ya kpak nanu. Saha wa tel, mu zi wa zim kina magau nɛnɛ a chi chenani Koosa zim min nan.

ref: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
text: Adaha kina tora nyɛnɛɛ a ya wu birige. A waanɛ, si suuli, bambi bɛgim kpaki, awa daŋni. Amaa daŋni kaali a maga.

ref: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
text: Ma nyɛ ma se sɛri awa nara daŋni ni. Ma gbanko nyɛ ma se sɛri wa papaha a naa Koosa siim weri ti kpa nyɛ ma ni. Papaha naa nyɛ yaa sɛm Koosa sɛma a li o kpok ti ni, waa kaali a maga.

ref: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
text: Nibine naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, o wa ya di nar nɛɛ o sɛm aa nyɛ, amaa Koosa nɛɛ o sɛm aa nyɛ. A ya wu soglewa o sɛm a li Koosa siim weri ti kpok ti ni, amaa nara wa zim a muna.

ref: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
text: Amaa naa la sɛm a nyɛ nara ni a li Koosa kpok ti ni, waa la suŋi ba di ba zim Koosa wɛ a la kpami ba a la belimi ba.

ref: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
text: Nibine naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, o suŋi o titirɛ, amaa naa la sɛm a li Koosa kpok ti ni, waa la suŋi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga.

ref: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
text: Ma maga kote aa sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, mu fu daŋni chenu. Amaa ma kote aa sɛm a li Koosa kpok ti ni, chen zi sɔɔni mu tuhau kinkan. Nibine naa la sɛm a li Koosa kpok ti ni, waa bɔ wa naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, see o koti a biksi a muna a suŋi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta.

ref: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
text: Mu niinbisa, mu wa wa ma wati a la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, yɛrɛ mu kaa ya suŋi ma? Mu ya chen ni, mu wa kikoti a sɛm ma chenani Koosa fun biksi min akoo biksi ma zima koo a sɛm ma a li Koosa kpok ti ni koo a nyɛ ma zim Koosa nyinbaan wɛ.

ref: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
text: Kina nawara vɛ kpak miisa, a waa la biksi ma kin nama. Ba hu lewa akoo ba mo luŋa amaa a kuuli wara li nɛnɛ, yɛrɛ nara tima kaa yaa nu a kuuli ti?

ref: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
text: Ba wa hu laal kaabul nara zi nuu aa saksi a la laal ni, amaa ba vɛ hu un nɛnɛ, anti la zi koti a zim aa saksi?

ref: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
text: Chen ya ma gbanko ma sɛm sɛma a naa ba zi vɛ koti a nuuli, yɛrɛ ba kaa ya zim a muna? O ya di chene, ma chɛɛmi aa du ba nɛɛ.

ref: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
text: Amaa mu vɛ nu nandigi sɛma, o duuti mu ya womne o wati ka o gbanko duuti o ya womne mu wati.

ref: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
text: Chenani ma se sɛri wa ma kpak papaha a naa Koosa siim weri ti nyɛ ma, o lawa ma daŋni a naa la zi suŋi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta ba tum tum were a gbaari.

ref: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
text: Adaha naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, o lawa o sumi Koosau o nyɛ un papaha naa la zi biksi un a muna.

ref: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
text: Mu sumi Koosa wa sɛm kurgɛ ni, mu siima ya naa la sumi chen, amaa mu bambi wa zim chenani mu sumi chen.

ref: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
text: Yɛrɛ mu kaa ya? Mu zi sumiu awa mu siim ti ni a sumi awa mu bambi gbanko ni. Mu zi lɔ boolau awa mu siim ti ni, a lɔ wa mu bambi gbanko ni.

ref: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
text: Ma vɛ ya chen, a la zaami Koosa awa ma siima duŋduŋ ni, yɛrɛ nara kaa ya a la nɔɔni a ŋɔ ami. Ba wa zim chenani maa zaami chen.

ref: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
text: Ma zaami nɛnɛ, amaa ba bambe wa to ma wati.

ref: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
text: Mu zaami Koosau di o nyɛ mu gbanko sɛm sɛm kurgɛ a kaali chenani ma maga sɛm nan.

ref: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
text: Amaa mu wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wa lagmi di ya jɛɛmi Koosa, mu yaa daŋni mu sɛm ba sɛm tama naa la zi suŋi bau. Chen bɔ wa mu sɛm ba sɛm damata a naa vɛ kpak suŋi.

ref: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu zi nyɛ hɔɔra ba naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, sɛm mu tuma wɛu a nyɛ nara nama ni, amaa ba wa zi nu mu wati.”

ref: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ nara sɛm sɛm kurgɛ di ba biksi ba naa se vɛ su un di o sɛm ta ya wutiu, amaa o funɛ nyɛ nara sɛm a li o kpok ti ni di ba biksi ba naa se su un di o sɛm ta ya wutiu.

ref: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
text: Adaha ma Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wa lagmi dɔŋ ni ka ma maga sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, ba naa vɛ nu ba sɛm ta chen awa ba naa se vɛ su Koosa wa zu, ba wa zi ŋɔ di ma zu galɛŋwaa?

ref: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
text: Amaa ma maga sɛm a li Koosa kpok ti ni, ba naa se vɛ su Koosa awa ba naa maga vɛ zim sɛm kurgɛ ta wa zu, ba zi nuu a la nɔɔni di ba ya tum tagna nitumɛwa. Ma sɛm maga zi zu ba bambeu.

ref: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
text: Ba tuha maga wu sogle zi liu ka ba zi gbiri Koosa suwachak ni a sumi un aa kiŋɔ nara ni di wuti Koosa nyɛn ma watiu. O Lawa Ya Lagmi, Yaa Vɛ Kpak Chɛɛmau

ref: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
text: Mu niinbisa, yɛrɛ mu zi bu a chiksi ma maa zim? Ma wa lagmi dɔŋ ni, ma nibɛgim yaa lisi Koosa pɛgi boola maa lɔ, digi biksi ma, digi ka biksi sɛm sogle naa Koosa biksi un, muna, digi ka sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, ka digi ka biksi a muna. Ma nyɛ a nama maga maa ya chen kisuŋi dɔŋa.

ref: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
text: Ba naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, ma nyɛ ba ya nara balɛ akoo nara batora duŋduŋ a nyɛ ba nibɛgim bɛgima nyinbaan ba sɛm, aa nyɛ nibɛgim kibiksi a muna.

ref: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
text: Ma wa lagmi dɔŋ ni, nandigi vɛ nyɛn dooni a la zi koti a biksi a muna, ma nyɛ ba gil, amaa ma nyɛ ba sɛm ba bambe ni a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
text: Nandigi kanɛɛ ka Koosa biksi un kundɔŋ di o wa sɛm, naa la sɛm chen gil a nyɛ nikani ti si sɛm.

ref: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
text: Ma maga kisɛm a li Koosa kpok ti ni bu bɛgim bɛgima, chen ni ma maga zi buksi dɔŋ watiu a na kpami.

ref: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
text: Ba naa la sɛm a li o kpok ti ni, o lawa ba kpak ba titiu nɛnɛ.

ref: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
text: Koosa ha wa yir ya di ya ya diŋ vɛ nuusawa, amaa di ya du balimmnɛ.

ref: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
text: Doonani maga Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wa lagmi, o lawa nihaana gil sɛmau. O wa deŋli di ba ya kisɛm dooni. Koosa chiksɛ naa o fun kpa nyɛ Ju tim ta ni chen biksi di nihaana tiŋ nibaala nɔɔna.

ref: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
text: Nihaani ta kpak wu digi di ba pɛɛsi, ba yaa la ba damsa ba pɛɛsi ba baalasa. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta yaa lagmi dɔŋ ni di ba sumi Koosa, nihaana sɛm sɛma dooni, ba zi na hiisau.

ref: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
text: Ma watɛɛ Koosa wɛ piilii? Ma duŋduŋ daharɛ nara fun wa biksi ma o wɛɛ?

ref: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
text: Ma naa la sɛm di maa sɛmu a li Koosa kpok ti ni, akoo ma kpak o siim weri ti papaha tirɛ, o lawa ma gbanko zimu di chenani mu sɔbi a nyɛ ma nan ya ya kunyan ti chiksirɛ.

ref: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
text: Ma bɔnɛ vɛ daŋni ma nu mu sɛm nama, ma naa kaɛɛ ta nu ba sɛma.

ref: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma nyɛ ma se sɛri a kisɛm Koosa sɛma a li o kpok ti ni. Amaa ma ta tɔ ba naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ chen nyinbaan.

ref: But do everything properly and in order.
text: O lawa ma kiya kin maga balimmnɛ.

ref: My friends, I want you to remember the message I preached and that you believed and trusted.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma nyɛ mu lisi ma Koosa laaboor weri naa mu fun kpa nyɛ ma chen wɛ. Ma funɛ la nɔɔniu ka ma ha vɛɛn kpak un nɛnɛ.

ref: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
text: Ma nu laaboor weri ti, Koosa zi wa yisi mau a li ma tum tagna ni hali o wa peti. Ma se vɛ su un wuti, o wa zi wa ya chen a nyɛ ma.

ref: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
text: Wu birigi zen naa mu fun la Koosa wati chen, mu funɛ kpa biksi mau. Waa nɛɛnɛ. Yesu funɛ sou ya tum tagna daharɛ a chi chenani Koosa sɛm tɔn ti fun sɛm di o zi wa tumu saha digi chen.

ref: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
text: Ba funɛ sogli unu, amaa o funɛ si o sou niu wuzu butora wuzu a chi chenani Koosa sɛm tɔn ti fun sɛm saha digi chen.

ref: Christ appeared to Peter, then to the twelve.
text: Pita fun bu te na un. Doone o nitumɛ fidalɛ ta fun bu na un.

ref: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
text: Chen saha ti o nitiŋɛ tusugsa nyuu gbanko funɛ lagmi a na un. Ba nidamata ha vɛɛn nyɛn dooni, amaa bɔnɛ situ.

ref: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
text: Jems gbanko fun bu na un. Doone o nitumɛ ta maga gbanko fun bukoo na un.

ref: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
text: Min tiŋ gantɛl a na un. O funɛ duuti ba tiŋ gantɛllɛ a lul min mu na un.

ref: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
text: Min tiŋ gantɛl a li Yesu nitumɛ ta ni. O wa deŋli di ba yir min o nitumi, domi min funɛ muksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta kinkan chen.

ref: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ ɔm min sikiu a suŋi min. O funɛ suŋi min chen daha, mu koti a du chenani mu du nan. Amaa chenani o fun suŋi min, mu wa kpa a a ta. Mu funɛ siu aa tum o tuma kinkan hali a kaali ba maga. Mu wara tum o tuma a li mu titi kpok ni, amaa Koosa la suŋi min.

ref: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
text: Chenani mu biksi awa chenani ba biksi, a maga ya kibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha ma se fun su un. Chenani Yesu Zi Wa Lɛli Ya A Li Ya Sou Ni

ref: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
text: Ya funɛ biksi ma Kristo wɛ di Koosa funɛ lɛli unu a li o sou ni, yɛrɛ ma bɔnɛ sɛm di Koosa wa kilɛli nara a li ba sou ni?

ref: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
text: Koosa vɛ wa koti a lɛli nara a li ba sou ni, o wa fun lɛli Kristo a li o sou ni.

ref: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
text: Koosa vɛ lɛli Kristo a li o sou ni, ya biksi ti ya yɔrirɛ awa ma Yesu se suuli ti gban ya yɔri.

ref: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
text: Chen sir ya wuti, ya suni Koosa nɔ nɛɛ. Nibinsa vɛ wa si ba sou ni, Koosa wa fun lɛli Yesu a li o sou ni. Ya fun ŋɔ ma di Koosa funɛ lɛli unu a li o sou ni.

ref: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
text: Nara vɛ wa si ba sou ni, Koosa wa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni.

ref: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
text: Koosa vɛ lɛli Kristo o sou ni, ma Yesu se suuli ti ya yɔrirɛ, ka ma tum tagna ha vɛɛn nyɛn ma ni.

ref: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
text: Ba naa se funɛ su Kristo gbanko ka ba siti chen, ta yɔriu.

ref: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
text: Ya nyɛni nɔ duŋduŋ ya kpak bambi bɛgim wa Kristo niu, o lawa nara kiɔm ya sikiu kinkan a kaali tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga.

ref: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
text: Amaa wuti Koosa funɛ te lɛli Kristo a li o sou ni. Chendaha Koosa zi wa lɛli ba naa funɛ siti chen gbankou a li ba sou ni.

ref: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
text: Nibin bɛgim daha sou wa. Nibin bɛgim daha sou siili nyɛn.

ref: Adam brought death to all of us, and Christ will bring life to all of us.
text: Domi ya wa Adam ya nibɛgimnɛ, chendaha yaa siti. Ya naa wa Kristo ya nibɛgim nan, ya ka zi wa si ya sou niu a kpak miisa.

ref: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
text: Amaa ya kaa wa si ya sou ni nibɛgim bɛgimwa. Yesu funɛ ya naa funɛ te si. O wa bur wa, ba naa o dɔ chen gbanko zi wa siu.

ref: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha zi wa tel, Kristo zi wa nyaŋi o dɔma ba naa maga dɔ ba nara kpok. O ya chen, o zi kpa o koor tiu a nyɛ o kunyana Koosa ni.

ref: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
text: Kristo zi dɔ kina magau, hali Koosa wa nyaŋi o dɔma maga.

ref: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
text: O gantɛl dɔm naa o zi nyaŋi ya sou.

ref: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa zi wa nyɛ Kristo dɔ kina magau.” Chen wa ya di o zi dɔ Koosau.

ref: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
text: Koosa wa nyɛ Kristo naa ya o be, wa dɔ kina maga a peti, o gbanko zi la nɔɔniu di Koosa dɔ un, chen ni Koosa duŋduŋ la zi dɔ Kristo awa kina maga.

ref: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
text: Nibin bɔnɛ yaa chiŋi siwɛ bar chiŋi nɛɛ aa ŋɔ di nara kichaksi ba wa ni ni ba daha. Yɛrɛ ba yaa ya chen? Nara nama ŋɔɛɛ di nisitɛ vɛ si ba sou ni, yɛrɛ ba yaa ya chen a nyɛ ba?

ref: And why do we always risk our lives
text: Nisitɛ ta vɛ si ba sou ni, yɛrɛ ya gbanko nyɛn bar tagna ni saha maga?

ref: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu hɛɛsɛ ma naa la tiŋ ya kunyan Yesu Kristo a ya mu jirima nara, muu zu sou bar nyɛni nɛɛ wuzu kam.

ref: What do you think I gained by fighting wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead are not raised to life, “Let's eat and drink. Tomorrow we die.”
text: O duuti mu wa sel zene funɛ yo dɔŋa a li Ɛfɛsus ni. O ya di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina duŋduŋ daha mu wa a funɛ yo dɔŋa, baŋ nyɔɔra mu na? Nisitɛ vɛ si ba sou ni, ma nyɛ yaa di diwe aa kinyɔ kinyɔɔlɛ domi ya wa zim chɛ.

ref: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
text: Ma bur wa ma nɛha ni a gil ma tum tagna. Ma bɔnɛ wa fun zim Koosa nɛnɛ. O wa kpak ma hiisaa? Yesu Zi Kpa Bara Falɛu A Nyɛ Ya

ref: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
text: Ma nidigi zi wa pɛɛsi minu, “Yɛrɛ nisitɛ ta kaa ya si ba sou ni? Baŋ barawa ba kpak?”

ref: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
text: Ma ya womniwa kinkan. Kiduha naa ma du chen yaa nyu tɛɛnɛ niu a zini, ka kibuni ti pɔ.

ref: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
text: Ma kpa mɛ a du chen, kiduhu titi wara zini. Kiduha naa ma du chen laata ma kum a di.

ref: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
text: Koosa kpa kiduha a nyɛ a be chi chenani o daŋni, ka o nyɛ a maga kurgɛ dɔŋ ni.

ref: People, animals, birds, and fish are each made of flesh, but none of them are alike.
text: Bara maga wa ya kibɛgim. A maga kurgu. Nibinsa bara nyɛn doon a kurgi sela bara ni. Nyinsa gbanko bara kurgu zimebisa bara niu.

ref: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
text: Buudaha kina nyɛn doon ka tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina gbanko nyɛn. Buudaha kina bunɔɔta kurgi tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina bunɔɔta niu.

ref: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
text: Nha bunɔɔta kur pɛna bunɔɔta niu. Wula bunɔɔta kurgi nha awa pɛna bunɔɔta niu. Wula gbanko chaaniu a kaali dɔŋa.

ref: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
text: Chene nara zi si ba sou ni. Ba wa siti, bara naa ba kpak chen zi wa pɔu. Ba wa si ba sou ni, ba bara falɛ ta wa zi kut pɔ.

ref: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
text: Bara naa ba sogli, o ya bara titirɛ, amaa bara fali ti ya siim barawa. Bara titi nyɛnu ka siim bara ka nyɛn.

ref: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa funɛ te naami nibine naa ba funɛ yir Adam ka o kpak miisa.” Amaa Adam naa funɛ tiŋ gantɛl chen, waa ya siim naa la nyɛ nara miisa. Waa ya Kristo.

ref: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
text: Ma zimu di ya wa fun te kpak siim bara. Ya funɛ te kpak bara titirɛ a zi wa kpak siim bara.

ref: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
text: Koosa funɛ te kpa hɛɛra a naami Adam, amaa Adam naa tiŋ gantɛl chen, Koosa funɛ tum un a li o tɔhɔn ti nɛɛ.

ref: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
text: Ba naa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, ba chi Adam naa Koosa fun kpa hɛɛra a naamirɛ. Ba naa nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni, ba chi Adam naa funɛ li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni chene.

ref: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
text: Lalaknɔ ya chi Adam naa Koosa fun kpa hɛɛra a naamirɛ, amaa ya zi wa chi Kristo naa funɛ li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni chenu.

ref: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma nan. Ya bara titi wa kikoti a zu Koosa koor ti ni. Kinkin naa la zi wa pɔ wa kikoti a wa nyɛn doonani kin naa vɛ pɔɔli ni.

ref: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
text: Maa nu. Mu zi biksi ma Koosa wu bɛgim sogli wɛu. Ya maga laata kaa sou. Amaa ya sitɛɛ akoo ya wa siti, wuzu digi Koosa zi wa chɛrgi ya maga barau bu bɛgim.

ref: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
text: Saha naa Koosa kaabul naa wa tiŋ gantɛl, wa hu, o zi lɛli nisitɛ tau a li ba sou ni. Lalaga a chi se kamsɛɛ, o zi wa chɛrgi ya bara titiu a nyɛ ya bara falɛ a naa vɛ pɔɔli.

ref: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
text: Koosa wa chɛrgi bara a burgi a naa la zi vɛ pɔɔli awa a naa la zi vɛ wa siti, kin naa ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni zi wa ya wutiu. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a ŋɔ, “Koosa fun kpa o kpok ti a nyaŋi sou.”

ref: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
text: Tum tagna daharɛ sou koti aa muksi ya. Koosa chiksɛ daha, ya daŋni ya tum tum tagna kinkan.

ref: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
text: Ya kunyana Yesu Kristo funɛ nyaŋi sou. Chendaha ya zaami Koosa.

ref: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma kpak nɛnɛ aa tum ya kunyan ti tuma kinkan. Ma zim di chenani maa tum tuma a nyɛ un, a wa zi wa ya yɔri.

ref: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
text: Ma funɛ sɔbi tɔnnɛ a pɛɛsi min malɛ naa mu daŋni ma la a nyɛ Koosa nara ba naa nyɛn Judia pulum ni chen wɛ. Maa tum chenani mu fun ŋɔ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Galesia pulum ni, di baa tum chen.

ref: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
text: Aleedi wuzu kam, ma maga yaa zer kinkin naa maa daŋni ma la a li kinkin naa Koosa nyɛ ma chen aa dau. Maa ya chen, mu wa wa ma zi la petiu.

ref: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
text: Mu wa wa, mu zi sɔbi tɔnau a nyɛ ba naa ma se su a li ma wati ba kpa a awa ma papaha a la Jɛrusalɛm.

ref: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
text: O deŋlu wa mu la dooni, mu wa ba zi lagmi dɔŋ ni a lau. Chenani Pɔɔl Funɛ Lisi Doonani O Zi La

ref: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
text: Mu daŋni mu la Masɛdonia pulumnɛ. Mu wa li dooni, mu zi wa nyɛn ma watiu wuzu bilɛ.

ref: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
text: Ami mu zi nyɛn ma watiu hali wa saha wa gali. Mu saha wa tel di mu la, ma zi kotiu a suŋi min mu la doonani maga mu kaa la.

ref: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
text: Mu wara daŋni mu wa gali ma wati lalaga. Mu kunyan ti fu la nɔɔni, mu fu zi daŋni mu nyɛn ma watiu a vusi.

ref: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
text: Mu kpak nyinbaanu bar nɔ a zi koti a tum Koosa tuma kinkan a na nyɔɔra. Amaa ba naa la daŋni ba tɔ mu tum nyinbaan, ba iiu.

ref: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
text: Timoti wa wa ma wati, ma la un a du nɛnɛ, chen ni o wa zi ɔm, domi o gbanko tum Koosa tumwa a chi min.

ref: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
text: Ma ta lisi di o ya bichɛllɛ, amaa ma suŋi un o koti a li nyinbaan ti awa bar sɔɔni ni a bur wa mu wati. Mu nyini o wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nyinbaan niu.

ref: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
text: Mu funɛ sumi Apɔlɔs kinkan di o tiŋ ya dɔŋtim ta a la ma wati, amaa o wara daŋni o la doon ni lalaknɔ. O wa na nyinbaan, o zi wau. Pɔɔl Mak O Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
text: Ma se su Yesu. Chendaha ma il a kpak ma titi nɛnɛ awa bambi kpok ni.

ref: Show love in everything you do.
text: Maa tum ma tum maga aa nyɛ nara ni awa daŋni ni.

ref: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
text: Ma zimu di Stɛfanas awa o dam walɛ funɛ te nyɛ ba se su Yesu a li Girik tim pulum ni a fun piinsa tum si sɛm tuma a nyɛ Koosa nara ni.

ref: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu sumi mau maa tiŋ ba naa maga wa un la tum nɔ.

ref: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
text: Stɛfanas, Fortunatus awa Akaikus funɛ wa sɔɔni mu tuhau kinkan, domi ba waa funɛ wa chiŋi ma bar chiŋi ni.

ref: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
text: Ba waa kpami min kinkan a chi chenani ba fun kpami ma chen. Maa nyɛ nara nama wa jirima.

ref: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni zaami mau. Akwila, Prisila awa Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa yaa lagmi ba dam ni, ba gbanko zaami mau kinkan ya kunyan Yesu daha.

ref: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
text: Ya niinbisa maga zaami mau. Ma zaami dɔŋa nɛnɛ.

ref: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
text: Mu kpa mu ninne a sɔbi a zaami ma gbanko nan.

ref: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
text: Nibine naa vɛ daŋni ya kunyan ti, Koosa zi ŋɔ nɔu a nyɛ un. Ya kunyana, kuwa.

ref: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
text: Ya kunyana Yesu la zi suŋi ma.

ref: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
text: Mu kpa mu daŋniu a nyɛ ma maga Yesu Kristo sɔn daha.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
text: Min waa, Pɔɔl, naa Koosa fun a lisi di mu ya Yesu Kristo nitumi, awa ya niinbe, Timoti, zaami mau. Min, Pɔɔl, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ma naa Koosa fun lisi di ma tiŋ un a li Kɔrint ni awa ba naa maga ya o nara a nyɛn Girik pulum ni.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
text: Ya kunyan Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma a kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Pɔɔl Fun Zaami Koosa Chenani O Suŋi Ya

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
text: Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa naa ya ya kunyan Yesu Kristo nyana. Waa ya siki tiina awa belimi tiina.

ref: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
text: O yaa belimi yau a li ya namsi maga ni. Domi o yaa suŋi ya chene, ya koti a chiŋi ya dɔŋtima gantɛl ni a li ba namsi ni.

ref: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
text: Ya namsi awa Kristo namsi ya kibɛgimnɛ. Ya namsi gbaari, o yaa belimi yau a gbaari.

ref: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
text: Mu wa Timoti funɛ namsi di Koosa koti a suŋi ma a yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni ma tiŋ un. Ma namsi awa ya namsi ti ya kibɛgimnɛ. Domi Koosa belimi ya, ya gbanko koti a belimi ma a la kpami ma, chen ni ma zi si ma namsiu wa kanyita ni.

ref: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
text: Ya se su di ma zi kpaku a kpok. Ya zimu di ma namsi ti awa ya namsi ya kibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha Koosa belimi ma awa ya.

ref: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni maa lisi chenani ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni, fun nyɛ ya na namsiu kinkan. Chen saha ti ya namsi funɛ zenu a kaali a maga. Hali ya fun lisi di ya zi sitiu.

ref: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
text: O chi ba funɛ di ya sou sɛriau. Koosa funɛ nyɛ kin nama ya yau, chen ni o zi biksi yau di ya ta nyini ya titi se ni, amaa ya nyini o waa naa lɛlɛ nara a li ba sou ni se ni saha maga.

ref: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
text: Chen saha ti, Koosa fun a yisi ya a li sou ni. Ya se su di o zi yisi yau a li wɛ niu.

ref: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
text: Maa sumi un ya daha. Koosa bu wa yisi ya a li ya namsi ni kinkan, ma wa nidamata kipɛgi un chen daha. Kin Naa Nyɛ Pɔɔl Vɛ La Zaami Ba

ref: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
text: Ya tusa sɔɔniu domi ya zimu di ya ha wa tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zima. Koosa ka suŋi ya di ya nyɛn wa tu chaana awa tu bɛgim ni nara maga suwachak ni. Ya wa ma funɛ nyɛn chen, ya fun doso a kaali chenani ya fun nyɛn.

ref: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
text: Chenani mu sɔbi a nyɛ ma, o wa bɔm wa ma zim.

ref: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
text: Ma ha wa zim min nɛnɛ, amaa mu daŋni ma zim min nɛnɛ, chen ni mu zi ya ma jirima naru. Ya kunyan Yesu wa bur wa, ma zi kut ya mu jirima narau.

ref: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
text: Kin nama daha, mu funɛ daŋni mu wa gali ma ni a la Masɛdonia pulum a bur wa na ma, chen ni ma zi suŋi minu a li Judia pulum nyinbaan ni. Mu fu ya chen, mu fu zi suŋi mau bilɛ.

ref: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
text: Mu funɛ te daŋni mu wa ma watiu a bu vɛ daŋni, nan nyɛ mu burgi a chi naa chɛrgi o bambi saha magaa? Mu kpak sire a te daŋni mu wa ma wati a bu vɛ daŋnii?

ref: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
text: Koosa ya mu sɛhɛra tiina di mu wa ya chen tiina. Mu ya nibine naa ya wuti tiinnɛ.

ref: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
text: Min, Sailas awa Timoti funɛ nyɛn ma wati chen, ya fun a biksi ma Yesu Kristo naa ya Koosa be, wɛ. Yesu wara vɔu a nyɛ ma wa o nyina ni. O ka tum o tum magawa wa tu bɛgim ni.

ref: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
text: Koosa funɛ tum Yesu di o nyɛ vɔwɛ naa maga Koosa fun kpa nyɛ nara ni a li o piili ti ni, chen ya wuti. Ya zaami Koosau a la nyɛ un jirima kinkan kin nama daha.

ref: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
text: Waa nyɛ ya wa ma kpak nɛnɛ di Kristo nyɛn ya nɛɛ. O titi funɛ lisi ya,

ref: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
text: Mu daŋni Koosarɛ ya mu sɛhɛra tiina ma zim di mu sɛm ta ya wutiu. Mu wa fun wa di mu wa na ma, chen ni ma wa zi nu mu sɛm kpoka.

ref: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
text: Ya wara daŋni ya wa gbaari chenani ma se su Yesu Kristo. Ma se piin su unu nɛnɛ. Ya ka daŋni ya wa suŋi mau ma tum were la suwachak ka ma tusa sɔɔni a gbaari.

ref: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
text: Mu wara daŋni di mu sɛm kpok ta nyɛ ma tusa bu wa chɔk. Chendaha mu vɛ wa ma wati.

ref: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
text: Mu fu wa ma wati ka mu sɛma nyɛ ma tusa wa chɔk, anti fu zi nyɛn a kpami min? Ma nawara tusa chɔk chen fu wa zi ya chen.

ref: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
text: Chendaha mu fun te sɔbi tɔn a nyɛ ma. Chen saha ti mu wa fun wa di mu wa na ma, domi ma naa fu zi nyɛ mu tuha sɔɔni, ma waa nyɛ o chɔk. Mu zim mu bambi niu di kinkin naa la sɔɔni mu tuha, waa la sɔɔni ma gbanko tusa.

ref: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
text: Mu funɛ sɔbi a nyɛ ma, o funɛ muksi minu kinkan ma daha. Mu tuha funɛ chɔku kinkan mu wi. Amaa mu wa funɛ daŋni mu tɔn ti sɛma chɔk ma tusa. Mu funɛ daŋni mu biksi mau chenani muu daŋni mau. Ma Ta Bu Lɛrgi Tum Tagna Nitumɛ Diŋsa

ref: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
text: Ma gil a nyɛ un a la kpami un, chen ni o tu chɔk ti wa zi nyɛ un gbaŋchɛlyɛla.

ref: You should make them sure of your love for them.
text: Mu sumi mau di ma ŋɔ un di maa daŋni unu kinkan.

ref: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
text: Chendaha mu fun sɔbi tɔn ti a nyɛ ma. Mu funɛ daŋni mu zimu di ma zi tiŋ mu nɔu a li kina maga ni. Mu daŋni mu zaamsi mau.

ref: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
text: Ma gil a nyɛ nar ni, mu zi chiŋi ma gantɛl niu. Mu daŋni mu suŋi mau. Chendaha ma ya chen. O chi Kristo nyini chenani mu yarɛ.

ref: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
text: Ya zim chenani sintana tumu saha maga. Chendaha ma zer chen nyinbaan o wa kikoti a nyaŋi ma. Kin Naa Muksi Pɔɔl Bambi Troas Ni

ref: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
text: Mu funɛ wa tel Troas, ya kunyan ti fun a nyɛ min nyinbaan kinkan di mu biksi nara Yesu Kristo laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
text: Amaa o funɛ muksi minu kinkan mu vɛ koti a dɛu Taitus dooni. Chendaha mu vɛ nyɛn Troas tima wati a vusi a kaali Masɛdonia pulum. Koosa Funɛ Nyɛ Pɔɔl Biksi Nara Ba Zim Kristo

ref: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
text: Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa kinkan. Waa funɛ nyɛ ya wa Kristo ya nibɛgim. Lalaknɔ waa dɔ ya. Saha maga waa nyɛ ya gba a biksi nara, chen ni ba zi na nyinbaanu a zim un.

ref: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
text: Doon naa maga ya wa tel, ya chi sɔɔr taginnɛ a li ba naa Koosa zi wa ta yɔri wati. Ya ka chi sɔɔr lɛmnɛ a li ba naa wa zim Kristo. Anti ya nikpok a la zi tum tum na a nyɛ Koosa ni?

ref: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
text: Ya bu a zer ya titirɛ ma suwachak nii? Nibiksɛ ba naa na nyɔɔra a li ba biksi ni kpa tɔnawa a nyɛ ma, chen ni ma zi zim chenani ba duu. Ba wa li, ba zi bukoo la tɔnau ma wati a kaali bar digi. Ya chi ba warɛɛ?

ref: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
text: Aai. Ya wara daŋni ya kpa tɔna a nyɛ ma. Chenani maa zim a tiŋ Koosa mu biksi daha biksi nara maga di Koosa sir tum min.

ref: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
text: Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a du ma ni. Chendaha ma chi Kristo tɔn naa ya fun sɔbi chene. Chenani Koosa ya nyɛ ma kaali chenani o fun sɔbi o chiksɛ ta a sak buŋkula ni a nyɛ Mosis saha digi chen.

ref: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
text: Ya se su Koosa Kristo daha. Chendaha ya ŋɔ ma nan ni.

ref: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
text: Ya wa kikoti a kpa ya kpok a tum tuma a nyɛ Koosa ni. Waa kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ ya ya tum tuma a nyɛ un.

ref: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o chiksɛ ta a nyɛ Mosis ni saha digi, Koosa fun sɔbi a a sak buŋkula daha ni. Mosis fun na Koosa jirima awa o chaani kinkan, chen ni o gbanko se fun chaani kinkan. Mosis funɛ bur a la chem Ju tim ta, o se ta chaani ti wa fun bu hi, amaa ba wa fun koti a nyini a. Koosa fun kpa o jirima awa o chaani kinkan a du o chiksɛ naa nyɛ nara siti ni,

ref: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
text: O chiksɛ naa nyɛ Koosa na nara galimsa a ta ba yɔri, dɔ Koosa jirima awa o chaani kinkan. Amaa o siim weri naa nyɛ nibinsa deŋli Koosa suwachak ni, dɔ o jirima awa o chaani kinkan kaali o te tel ti.

ref: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
text: Jirima awa o chaani naa Koosa kpa nyɛ o siim weri ti ni nyɛ jirima awa o chaani naa o fun kpa nyɛ o chiksɛ ta ni saha digi chen, chi yɔrirɛ.

ref: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
text: Koosa chiksɛ naa funɛ dɔ o jirima awa o chaani kinkan wa fun vusi. O siim weri ti ka nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Chendaha o jirima awa o chaani kaali Koosa chiksɛ ta jirima awa chaani kinkan.

ref: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
text: Kin nama daha, ya kpak bambe kpok a biksi nara chenani Koosa fun tum nyɛ ba.

ref: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
text: Ya wa chi Mosis naa funɛ kpa gar a tɔ o se, chen ni Ju tim ta wa zi koti a na Koosa chaani kinkan a li o se ni.

ref: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
text: Nara maga nyɛ ba se su ya kunyan ti, ba bambe sitɛ zi wa zim Koosau.

ref: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
text: Ya kunyan ti awa Koosa siim weri naa wa nyɛn ba ni chen ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ba waa lisɛ ba.

ref: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
text: Ya naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ ta, ya se wa tɔ. A chi diga naa la nyiksirɛ ya kunyan ti jirima awa o chaani kinkan. O siim weri ti tum ya bambe nee a chɛrgi ya di ya chi un a gbaari.

ref: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
text: Koosa naa funɛ kpak ya siki chen, funɛ lisi yau di yaa biksi nara kin nama wɛ. Ya wa zi bur gantɛl a li tum naa o kpa nyɛ ya nan.

ref: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
text: Ya wa fun tum tum tagna a sogli di ya na hiisa. Ya wa fun la mur nara. Ya wa fun kpa ya titi biksirɛ a gbaari chenani Koosa fun biksi ya ni. Yaa ka biksi nara o wuti a li gbampulim nɛɛ saha maga, chen ni nara maga zi la nɔɔniu ba bambe ni di ya waa ya Koosa nitiŋɛ ta.

ref: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
text: Nidamata wara nu Koosa laaboor weri naa ya kpa biksi ba. Koosa zi wa na ba galimsau a ta ba yɔri.

ref: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
text: Ba wa koti a nyɛ ba se su o laaboor weri ti. O chi nharɛ a chaani nara bambe ni, amaa sintana naa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga, tɔ ba lise. Koosa laaboor weri ti ya Kristo wɛ. O bambi chi Koosa bambire. Koosa awa un bɔnɛ o jirima awa o chaani ti kinkan.

ref: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
text: Ya wara biksi narawa ya titi wɛ. Ya ka biksi ba Yesu Kristo naa ya ya kunyan ti wɛwa. O daha ya wa ya ma tumtumsa.

ref: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
text: Koosa funɛ naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga saha digi, o fun ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ birsa burgi nha.” O be Yesu Kristo funɛ kpa Koosa jirima awa o chaani ti kinkan a biksi ya. Nan chi fitina a chaani ya bambe ni.

ref: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ naami ya naa kpak o fitin birigi nɔ kinkan a chi choha tempile, chen ni o zi kotiu a biksi nara di o kpok kinkan nɔ wa li ya ni. O li un nɛɛ.

ref: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
text: Yaa namsi kinkan saha maga, amaa ya kote a di au. Ya wa fun zim nyinbaan naa ya zi tiŋ, amaa chen wa fun muksi ya.

ref: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
text: Dɔma la dɛu ya, amaa Koosa wara daŋni ya chel ba nin ni. Hali nara mosi yau di ya chel, amaa ba wa kpu ya.

ref: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
text: Saha maga ya nyɛn sou bar nyɛni nɛɛ Yesu daha, chen ni nara zi zimu di o miisa naa nyɛn ya ni kok ya dooni.

ref: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
text: Domi ya nyɛn sou bar nyɛni nɛɛ a chi nan saha maga, ma waa ka nyɛn Koosa miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔ bar nyɛni nɛɛ.

ref: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Min funɛ ŋɔ Koosa ni domi mu si su un.” O siim weri ti funɛ nyɛ ya se su un, chen ni ya zi biksi narau o wɛ.

ref: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
text: Ya zimu di Koosa naa funɛ lɛli ya kunyana Yesu a li o sou ni chen, o gbanko zi lɛli yau a li ya sou ni a chi Yesu, a nyɛ ya wa ma lagmi o suwachak ni.

ref: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
text: Yaa di namsiu a chi nan ma Yesu nitiŋɛ ta daha. Chenani Koosa suŋi nidamata ba wa zim un a gbaari, ma zaami awa ma Koosa pɛgi zi lagmiu a gbaari. Ya Bara Chi Lɛuwa A Zi Wa Chɔk

ref: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
text: Kin nama daha, ya wa zi bur gantɛl a li tum naa Koosa kpa nyɛ ya nan. Ya bara hɛɛsiu aa gbaari, amaa wuzu kam Koosa waa ka gbaari o miisa naa o nyɛ ya chenu.

ref: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
text: Ya namsi naa ya namsi nan, a wa zi vusi. A wa peti, Koosa zi zeru a li o jirima zenzen naa vɛ kpak bɔ ni, a nyɛ ya. O jirima zenzen nɔ kaali ya lalaknɔ namsi kinkan.

ref: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
text: Ya baga wa to tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kin nawara vɛ vusi ni. Ya baga ka nyɛnu a li kin nawara Koosa zi wa kpa nyɛ ya wuzu digi ni. A waa la zi nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
text: Ya bara chi lɛwa naa nibinsa kɔɔrirɛ. A wa chɔk, Koosa zi kpa ya kaali o tɔhɔn tiu a la nyɛ ya bara falɛ naa o titi naami chenu. Ya zi kpak au hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
text: Lalaknɔ ya wiu a la daŋni Koosa zer ya kinkan a la o tɔhɔn ti a la nyɛ ya o bar falɛ ta dooni.

ref: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
text: O wa ya chen, ya siimsa wa zi pɔɔgi bara.

ref: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
text: Ya lalaknɔ bara naa chi lɛwa chen, a nyɛ ya na namsirɛ. Ya wara daŋni ya ta ya lalaknɔ bara lalaga, amaa ya kpak unu ya bambe ni di Koosa zi kpa o bara falɛ tau a nyɛ ya a chiŋi a bar chiŋɛ ni. O ya chen, ya lalaknɔ bara a naa sitɛɛ, zi burgi o bara falɛu. A waa kpak miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
text: Koosa fun naami ya di ya la o bara falɛ na. O funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ ya di o ya o purigiri bɔɔli di o sir zi ya chenu.

ref: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
text: Chendaha ya kpak tu sɔɔna saha maga. Ya zimu di ya nyɛn lalaknɔ nan, ya ha wa kikoti a chiŋi ya kunyan ti suwachak ni.

ref: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
text: Ya tusa sɔɔnɛu kinkan. Ya wa kpak hansura di ya sou. Chen yɛɛ, ya zi la dugli ya kunyan tiu.

ref: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
text: Ya nyɛn bar nɔ koo ya duglɛ un a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni, o lawa ya kpak unu ya bambe ni o ya Koosa wa tu sɔɔna.

ref: After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies. Bringing People to God
text: Ya wa siti, ya maga zi la Kristo suwachaku di o di ya sɛria. Chenani ya fun a tum tum were koo tum tagna a li ya lalaknɔ bara ni, chene o zi wa bɔ ya chim. Koosa Daŋni O Wa Nara Maga Bu Ya Nɔ Bɛgim

ref: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
text: Domi ya ɔm ya kunyan tiu, chendaha muu daŋni mu biksi ma di yaa tum Koosa tuma wa wutirɛ. Waa zim di ya tum o tuma wutirɛ. Mu si su di ma gbanko zi tum chenu.

ref: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
text: Ya wa bur a daŋni sɔna a nyɛ ya titi ni. Ya waa ka la nyɛ ma nyinbaan di ya ya ma jirima tima, chen ni ma zi kotiu a sɛm ba naa la nyini nibinsa se ni a vɛ nyini ba bambe ni.

ref: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
text: Ya la sɛm nan, ma bɔnɛ baa di yaa zu galɛŋwaa? Ya zuɛɛ, ya daŋni ya nyɛ Koosa wa jirimarɛ daha. Ma bɔnɛ baa di ya kpak alaafɛuu? Ya kpakɛɛ, o ya ma daharɛ.

ref: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
text: Yesu Kristo daŋni yau kinkan. Ya zimu di waa chiŋɛ nara maga bar chiŋɛ ni a sou ba daha. O la daŋni ya chen daha, ya se sɛrɛu di ya tum o tuma.

ref: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
text: O funɛ sou chene, chen ni ba naa la o mi fali ti wa zi bu tiŋ ba titi tutɔ, amaa ba zi tiŋ unu naa funɛ sou a si o sou ni ba daha.

ref: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
text: Chendaha ya wara bur a di nibinsa sɛria a chi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga. Ya funɛ di Kristo sɛria chene, amaa lalaknɔ ya wa bur a di un.

ref: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
text: Nara burgi tiŋ Yesu Kristo, Koosa ya chɛrgi ba bambe a ya kifalɛ. Ba gantɛl tu kina wɛ wa bu toli.

ref: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
text: Kin nama magawa Koosa fun ya nyɛ ya. Yesu Kristo siwi daha, Koosa wa ya fun bu ya nɔ bɛgim a daŋni ya la ŋɔ nara ni o wɛ, chen ni o wa ba gbanko zi bu ya nɔ bɛgimu.

ref: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
text: Kristo siwi ti daha, Koosa fun na nyinbaan a nyɛ o wa nara maga bu ya nɔ bɛgim, chen ni o wa zi bu lisi ba tum tagna wɛ. Laaboor weri nɔwa o fun kpa du ya nisa ni di ya biksi nara.

ref: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
text: Yesu Kristo funɛ tum ya ma wati, chen ni Koosa zi kotiu a sɛm sɛma a nyɛ ma. Ya sumi mau Yesu Kristo daha di ma wa Koosa ya nɔ bɛgim a peti.

ref: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Min, Timoti awa Koosa lagmi a tum o tuma. Chendaha ya sumi ma di ma kpa chenani Koosa fun suŋi ma a tum tuma a nyɛ un.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
text: Ya wara daŋni nandigi nyini ya tuma a na ya galimsa. Chendaha ya doso di ya ta wa tum wu digi a tɔ nara nyinbaanasa.

ref: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
text: Ya tum maga yaa biksi nara di ya ya Koosa tumtumsiwa. Ba namsi yau kinkan, amaa ya di ya namsiu wa kanyita ni.

ref: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
text: Ba yaa fun tuksi yau a tɔ ya dɛ. Tɔhɔn dami yaa funɛ li ya nɛɛ. Ya yaa funɛ tumu hali a wa gbarigi a gil ya diwe awa ya do.

ref: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
text: Chenani ya ya bambi chaanɛ tima a zim Koosa nɛnɛ a kpak kanyita wa nara ni a ya jɛrgɛ tima, a kpak o siim weri ti a li ya bambe ni a la daŋni ya dɔŋtima wa tu bɛgim ni,

ref: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
text: a la biksi nara wa wuti a li Koosa kpok ti ni, a nama maga biksi nara di ya ya o tumtumsiwa. Ya chi lanjimasiwa. Koosa nyɛ ya tum tum were a nyɛ un, chen ni ya zi koti a yo ya dɔmau a kok ya titi.

ref: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
text: Nara nyɛ ya jirimarɛ aa nyɛ ya na hiisa. Ba pɛgi yau a la pini ya zoma. Ya sɛm sɛm wutɛu, amaa ba ŋɔ di ya ya nusun timwa.

ref: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
text: Ya wa ya nibin zene, amaa nara maga zim ya wɛu. Ya chi nibin sitɛwa, amaa ya ka ha vɛɛn kpak miisau. Ba nyɛ ya na namsiu kinkan, amaa ba ha wa kpu ya.

ref: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
text: Ya tusa yaa chɔku a bu sɔɔni kinkan. Ya ya fara timwa, amaa ya suŋi nidamatau kinkan di ba wa zim Koosa. Nibinsa lisi di ya wa kpak kundɔŋ, amaa ya waa ka dɔ kina maga.

ref: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
text: Ma Kɔrint tima, wu digi wa ka mu vɛ sɔbi a du tɔn nɔ ni. Ya chasi ya tusau a nyɛ ma.

ref: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
text: Ya daŋni ya ŋɔ mau lalaknɔ a chi ma ya ya bisa. Ma biksi ya di maa daŋni yau a chi chenani yaa daŋni ma. Ma gbanko ka chasi ma tusa a nyɛ ya. Ma Ta Nyɛ Ma Wa Ba Naa Yaa Jɛɛmi Vursa Lagmi

ref: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
text: Ma wa ba naa se vɛ su Koosa, ta lagmi a tum tuma. Tum tagna awa tum were wa kilagmi dɔŋ ni. Dusum wa nha wa kilagmi dɔŋ ni.

ref: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
text: Kristo awa sintana kikoti a ya nɔ bɛgimuu? Nara se su Koosa awa nara se vɛ su Koosa kikoti a ya nɔ bɛgimuu?

ref: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
text: Ya chi Koosa naa nyɛn, damnɛ. Ya chi chen, yɛrɛ ya wa vursa kaa ya nyɛn? Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu wa mu nara zi nyɛnu a lagmi aa val. Min la zi ya ba Koosa.”

ref: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
text: O gbanko fun ŋɔ, “Ma waligi ma titi a li ba naa la jɛɛmi vursa ni, chen ni ma wa zi bu gbaŋsi a kina. Ma ya chen, mu titi zi la ma a duu.

ref: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
text: Mu zi ya ma nyanau, ka ma kiya mu bisa. Mu waa, Koosa, naa kpak kina maga kpok, ŋɔ nan.”

ref: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, sɛm nama ya vɔwi naa o fun vɔu a nyɛ ya. Ma nyɛ ya chaksi ya titi, chen ni ya zi yisi doŋau a li ya bara awa ya bambe ni a ta. Ma nyɛ ya kpaŋi saha maga hali a wa peti a ya tu chaan tima, domi ya nyɛ Koosa wa jirima. Ma Nyɛ Mu Tuha Sɔɔni A La Gali Saha Maga

ref: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
text: Ma chasi ma tusa a nyɛ ya. Ya ha wa tum tum tagna a nyɛ nandigi ni. Ya ha wa di nandigi koo a mur ba.

ref: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
text: Ya wara sɛm nannɛ a nyɛ ma di ya na ma galimsa. Ya funɛ piin ŋɔ mau di ya daŋni mau kinkan. Ya nyɛnɛɛ koo ya sitɛɛ, ma zi vɛɛn nyɛn ya bambe niu.

ref: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
text: Ya se su ma kinkan, domi ma waa ya ya jirima nara. Ma kpami ya a li ya namsi maga ni, chen ni ya tusa zi sɔɔniu a la gali saha maga.

ref: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
text: Ya funɛ wa tel Masɛdonia pulum, ya wa fun koti a hiisi. Doonani maga ya yaa fun burgi, ya yaa na namsiu. Nidamata yaa funɛ muksi yau kinkan. Chendaha tu chɔk yaa fun su ya tusau,

ref: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
text: amaa Koosa naa la belimi ninamsɛ chen, waa funɛ tum Taitus di o wa kpami ya.

ref: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
text: Ya tusa funɛ sɔɔniu ya na un chen a nu o laaboor chenani ma fun a kpami un. O fun ŋɔ ya di maa daŋni ma na minu kinkan, awa chenani ma tusa chɔk domi muksi naa ma kpa du min chen daha. O gbanko funɛ ŋɔ yau di lalaknɔ ma se sɛrɛu di ma tiŋ ya biksi ti. O funɛ ŋɔ ya o laaboor ti peti, ya tusa fun sɔɔni kinkan a kaali o te tel ti ni.

ref: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
text: Mu tuha funɛ chɔk kinkan a li tɔn naa mu fun te sɔbi a nyɛ ma chen ni, amaa mu funɛ nu di ma tusa wa chɔk vusi, chen fun bu ya min tu sɔɔna.

ref: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
text: Chenani mu fun chɔk ma tusa wa fun nyɛ mu tuha sɔɔni, amaa chenani ma gil ma tum tagna a bur a tiŋ Koosa nyɛ o sɔɔniu. Chenani ma tusa fun chɔk sɔɔni Koosa tuha gbankou.

ref: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
text: Chenani ma tusa fun chɔk nyɛ ma gil ma tum tagna a bur a tiŋ un. Ya ya chen, wuzu digi o zi kotiu a yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. O wa ya kin naa ya zi vɛ. Naa vɛ nyɛ Koosa chɔk o tuha, wuzu digi Koosa zi wa na o galimsau a ta un yɔri.

ref: Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter.
text: Domi ma waa funɛ nyɛ Koosa chɔk ma tusa, ma se sɛrɛu di ma kɔɔri ma sɔna a bu kɔɔri wi naa kaɛɛ. Ma daŋni di ma bu na ya kinkan a kpak un ma bambe ni di ma lɛrgi naa la tum tum tagin diŋna. Tum naa maga ma fun tum, ya wa na ma galimsa.
