ref: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
text: Ya funɛ sɔbi tɔn tiu di ya suŋi naa funɛ tum tum tagin ti awa naa funɛ la tum tagin ti. Ya gbanko funɛ sɔbi chen ni di Koosa biksi ma chenani ma daŋni ya. Nan kaali kin naa balɛ na maga.

ref: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
text: Ma waa funɛ kpami ya kinkan a sɔɔni ya tusa. Taitus funɛ ŋɔ ya chenani ma kpami un, ya tusa fun sɔɔni a kaali chenani a fun te sɔɔni.

ref: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
text: Taitus funɛ li bar nɔ di o wa ma wati, ya funɛ ŋɔ unu di ma ya ya jirima narawa. Ma wa fun nyɛ ya na hiisa. Ya ha vɛɛn a sɛm sɛm wutɛwa a nyɛ ma. Chen vɛɛna, chenani ya fun ŋɔ Taitus ni, chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
text: O lisi chenani ma fun la un a du a kpa ma titi a nyɛ un a saksi di ma tiŋ o nɔ. Lalaknɔ oo daŋni mau a gbaari chen daha.

ref: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
text: Mu tuha sɔɔnɛu kinkan di mu se su ma a chi nan.

ref: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya daŋni ma zimu chenani Koosa fun a suŋi Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum ni.

ref: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
text: Min ya ba sɛhɛra tiina hali ba funɛ kpa ba kina a nyɛu ka a bu vɛ tel ba. Nandigi wa fun muksi ba di ba ya chen.

ref: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
text: Ba fun sumi ya di ya la ba papaha, chen ni ya zi kotiu a suŋi Koosa nara ba naa nyɛn bar dumsi ni.

ref: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
text: Ba papaha ti funɛ kaali chenani ya lisi. Ba fun te kpa ba titi a nyɛ ya kunyan ti ni a tiŋ Koosa nɔ a lisi ba kin ta a nyɛ ya.

ref: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
text: Chendaha yaa sumi Taitus naa piin piili di o la ma papaha ti, di o suŋi ma a kpa o maga a nyɛ un. Mu zimu di maa ya nannɛ, domi maa daŋni ma suŋi ma dɔŋtimwa kinkan.

ref: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
text: Mu wara daŋni mu ŋɔ ma chenani ma zi ya, amaa mu piin ŋɔ mau chenani ba naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum ni fun kpak un ba bambe ni ba suŋi ba dɔŋtima kinkan. Mu daŋni mu zaamsi mau a na ma daŋni ma dɔŋtim tau a chi chenani ba daŋni ba.

ref: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
text: Ma zimu chenani ya kunyan Yesu Kristo funɛ daŋni ya a la suŋi ya kinkan. O funɛ kpa o kidamatiwa a ta, a fun a ya fara tiina ma daha, chen ni ma zi lau a li o kidamati ta ni.

ref: A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice.
text: Ta lɛm nɛɛ mu ŋɔ ma kin nama wɛ. Bunaani ma kpak un ma bambe ni a piili a pa ma kin ta a nyɛ Taitus ni. Lalaknɔ ma pa a petiu. Ma waa funɛ ya nitele a lisi a vɛɛn piili.

ref: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
text: Ma la a la peti chenani ma fun piili. Ma funɛ piili wa si sɛm nɛ. Ma vɛɛn peti un wa si sɛm ni a nyɛ chenani ma zi koti.

ref: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
text: Mu wara daŋni mu nyɛ o bɔm ma wati, a vɛ bɔm ba naa ma suŋi chen wati.

ref: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
text: Lalaknɔ ma kpak kidamatau. Chendaha o weru di ma lisi a li a ni a suŋi ba naa la daŋni a. Ba kpak kidamata ka ma wa daŋni a, ba zi suŋi mau. Nan ni ma wa chel a li kundɔŋ ni.

ref: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni chenani o fun kpa diwe a nyɛ o nara ni a li nyabaan ni a ŋɔ, “Nibine naa funɛ na diwe kinkan funɛ bɔni a nyɛ naa na tam nɛɛ. Ba maga fun ya kibɛgim.”

ref: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
text: Ya pɛgi Koosau di Taitus fun kpak un o bambi ni a suŋi ma a chi chenani ya kpak un a li ya bambe ni nan.

ref: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
text: Mu funɛ ŋɔ Taitus ni di o bu wa zaami ma, o tuha funɛ sɔɔniu kinkan. O daŋniu kinkan di o bu na ma hali o wara daŋni mu ŋɔ un.

ref: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
text: Ya funɛ tum ya niinbe digirɛ awa un di ba wa ma wati. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga nyɛ un jirima kinkan, domi o funɛ biksi nidamatiwa Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
text: Ba funɛ lisi unu di o wa ya kpa ma papaha ti a la nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Judia pulum ni. Nan la zi nyɛ ya kunyan ti wa jirima a biksi nara di yaa daŋni ya suŋi bau.

ref: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
text: Ya daŋni ya dosou a li ya tum nama ni di nandigi wa zi koti a na ya galimsa a daha. Chendaha o wa ya kaa gba.

ref: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
text: Ya daŋni ya ya chenani deŋli ya kunyan ti awa nara maga suwachak niu.

ref: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
text: Ya gbanko tum nara balɛ tiina di o wa ma wati. O wa ba lagmi a kaali. Ya funɛ zaamsi unu bu damata a zim di o kpak unu o bambi ni di o suŋi ya a li ya tum ni. O zimu di mu si su mau kinkan. Chendaha o kpak un o bambi ni a gbaari di o wa suŋi ma kinkan.

ref: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
text: Nandigi wa pɛɛsi ma nara nama wɛ, ŋɔ ba di mu wa Taitus la tum tum bɛgim. Ma bu ŋɔ ba di Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn bar kurgɛ ni, funɛ lisi nara balɛ ta di ba wa chiŋi ba bar chiŋɛ ni. Ba tuma la nyɛ Yesu Kristo wa jirima kinkan.

ref: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
text: Ba wa tel, ma la ba a du a nyɛ ba zim di maa daŋni bau. Ma ya chen, Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ tum ba, zi la nɔɔniu di ma waa ya ya jirima nara.

ref: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
text: O wa lawa mu bu sɔbi a nyɛ ma di ma suŋi Koosa nara.

ref: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
text: Mu zim di ma daŋni ma suŋi bau. Mu funɛ ŋɔ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum ni, di ba niinbisa ba naa nyɛn Girik tim pulum ni, funɛ piiliu a saksi ba papaha ti bunaani. Ba funɛ nu chenani ma kpak un ma bambe ni di ma tum nan, ba nidamata fun saksi ba papaha gbanko.

ref: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
text: Lalaknɔwa mu tum ya niinbisa na ma wati, chen ni ma zi lagmi ma papaha tiu a peti. Mu funɛ ŋɔ bau di ma zi ya chenu. Ma ta du min hiisa.

ref: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
text: Mu wa Masɛdonia nara tima bɔnɛ wa na di ma ha wa ya wu digi, chenani ya se su a li ma ni zi burgi hiisau. Ma gbanko zi na hiisau kinkan.

ref: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
text: Chendaha mu fun te tum ya niinbisa na ya suwachak ni, chen ni ba zi wa suŋi mau ma saksi papaha naa ma fun vɔu di ma nyɛ ya chen a chiŋsiu. Ya wa pu ma, a zi vaan chiŋiu a pɔ ya. Chen la zi biksi ya di ma nisa wa zuk, amaa ma nisa ka fuŋsɛu wa ma nyɛ ya.

ref: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
text: Ma zimu di niparɛ du kin tama, o zi kum tamau, amaa o du kidamata, o zi kum kinkanu.

ref: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
text: Ma maga kpa chenani ma lisi di ma nyɛ ya. Ma ta nyɛ ma nisa zugi a li ma papaha ti nyɛɛli ni. Ba naa la nyɛ awa nin fuŋsɛ ni yaa sɔɔni Koosa tuha kinkan.

ref: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
text: Ma ya chen, Koosa zi bu gbaari mau a i a kaali chen, chen ni saha maga ma zi kpaku a mak ma titi ka ma nyɛ nara ni.

ref: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
text: Koosa kpa kiduha aa nyɛ niparɛ ni di ba du, chen ni a zi nyɛ ba diweu. Chen vɛɛna, o yaa lisiu a li o kin ni a nyɛ ma a gbaari, chen ni ma zi kotiu a suŋi ma dɔŋtima.

ref: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
text: O yaa daŋni ma lisi a li kidamata a naa o fun nyɛ ma ni di ma suŋi ma dɔŋtima. Ya kpa ma papaha ti a nyɛ ba naa la daŋni un ni, ba zi pɛgi Koosau ma daha.

ref: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
text: Kina lɛ wa liɛɛ a li chenani ma tum ni. Ma suŋi bau a li ba wu muksi ni ka ba pɛgi Koosa kinkan.

ref: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
text: Ma suŋi ba kinkan chen ni, ma biksi narawa di maa tiŋ Koosa laaboor weri ti Yesu Kristo wɛ nɛnɛ. Ba zi nyɛ Koosa jirimau, domi ma waa la suŋi ba awa nara maga kinkan.

ref: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
text: Ba zi daŋni mau a sumi Koosa a nyɛ ma, domi o suŋi mau kinkan di ma suŋi ba.

ref: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o beu a nyɛ ya. Wuti waa kaali a maga kinkan. Chendaha ma nyɛ yaa zaami un.

ref: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
text: Ma bɔnɛ ŋɔ dɔŋ ni di mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati mu funɛ ya jɛrgɛrɛ, amaa mu funɛ bol, mu funɛ sɔbi sɛm kpokwa a nyɛ ma. Yesu Kristo daŋni mu ya jɛrgirɛ ma wati. Chendaha mu waa, Pɔɔl, sumi ma lalaknɔ,

ref: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
text: mu wa wa ma wati, ma ta wa muksi min mu sɛm sɛm kpoka a nyɛ ma. Mu wa pu ba naa la ŋɔ di yaa tiŋ ya tutɔ daŋnɛrɛ, mu zi sɛm sɛm kpokau a nyɛ ba.

ref: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
text: Ya wara nyini ya titi kpok se nee, amaa Koosa kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ ya, chen ni ya zi chiŋiu a du nibiksɛ ba naa vɛ nu Koosa laaboor weri ti, biksi ni,

ref: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
text: a lisi chenani nara zer ba titi a vɛ zim Koosa a ta. Ya kpa o kpokre a biksi ba, chen ni ba zi burgi a tiŋ Yesu Kristo nɔu awa ba bambe ni.

ref: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
text: Ma bɔnɛ nyini ya se nee, a vɛ nyini ya bambe ni. Ma bɔnɛ lisi di Yesu Kristo dɔ maa? Ma lisi di waa dɔ ya gbanko a chi o dɔ ma chen.

ref: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
text: Ya kunyan ti kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ ya di yaa suŋi ma a ta lɛrgi ma diŋsa. Ya fa sɔbi nan kinkan, hiisa wa zi kpak ya.

ref: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
text: Ya wara daŋni ya tɔn nama kpak ma hansura.

ref: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
text: Mu funɛ piin sɔbiu di ma bɔnɛ ŋɔ di mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati, mu funɛ ya jɛrgɛrɛ ka mu sɛma ya yɔri. Mu funɛ bol a sɔbi ma, tɔni ta maga funɛ suu wa sɛm kpoka ni.

ref: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
text: Nara nama ba naa la sɛm nan, mu daŋni ba zimu di mu wa wa ma wati mu zi kpa mu tɔn ta sɛm kpokau a sɛm ma.

ref: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
text: Nara nama ya womniwa kinkan. Ba lisiu di ba ya nibin zenewa. Ba wara daŋni ba kpa ba titi a lagmi ya ni. Ba funɛ naami ba titi chiksɛ a la tiŋ a.

ref: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
text: Ya wara daŋni sɔna a kaali chenani Koosa fun lisi ya di ya tum o tuma. Chenani ya suŋi ma ya tum ta bar digirɛ.

ref: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
text: Ya funɛ te wa biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri naa ya Yesu Kristo wɛ chen. Chendaha ya kpak nyinbaan di yaa suŋi ma.

ref: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
text: Ya wara daŋni nyɔɔra a li chenani nara biksi ma a li Koosa laaboor weri ti ni. Ya kpak bambi bɛgimu di ma se zi su unu ka ya wa ma tuma pisi a gbaari.

ref: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
text: Ma maga tiŋ chen, ya wa ma zi lagmiu a te biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ tɔsi naa go ma ni. Ya ya chen, nandigi wa zi ŋɔ di ya daŋni nyɔɔrrɛ a li nara tum ni.

ref: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
text: Ma pi ma titi, chen wa deŋli. Ya kunyan ti ka pi ma, chen deŋlee kinkan.

ref: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
text: Muu daŋni ma kinkannɛ a chi chenani Koosa daŋni ma. Ma chi nihabichɛllɛ mu fun a vɔu di mu nyɛ baal naa sɔn ya Yesu Kristo chen.

ref: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
text: O muksi minu kinkan ma daha. Ma bɔnɛ nyɛ nara burgi ma bambeu a li chenani ma fun te tiŋ Yesu Kristo a li tu bɛgim ni. Ma burgi nɔ chi chenani sintana fun nɛɛgi a mur Iif saha digi chen.

ref: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
text: Ma bɔnɛ la nu ba naa vɛ tiŋ ya wuti biksi Yesu wɛ. Ba funɛ chɛrgi Koosa laaboor weri tiu a biksi ma siim naa vɛ ya Koosa siim weri ti wɛ. Ma funɛ la nɔɔniu ka o vɛ bɔm ma wati.

ref: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
text: Ma lisu di nibiksɛ nama ya nibirigewa a kaali min. Mu wa la nɔɔni a nyɛ ma.

ref: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
text: O ya wutirɛ mu vɛ koti a sɛm sɛma nɛnɛ nara suwachak ni, amaa mu zima wa pɔɔgi min. Mu yaa biksi mau di nan ya wutirɛ.

ref: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
text: Mu funɛ te nyɛn ma wati a biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ, mu wa funɛ daŋni bɔta. Mu funɛ bur mu titirɛ gantɛl di ma zer ma nyuuna buudaha. Chen wa fun deŋlii?

ref: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
text: Wuzu digi mu malɛ fun wa peti, amaa mu wa fun wa pɛɛsi ma di ma suŋi min. Mu niinbisa ba naa nyɛn Masɛdonia pulum ni, funɛ zer kin naa maga mu daŋniu a wa nyɛ min. Mu yaa fun dosou di mu wa zi nyini ma se ni a li kundɔŋ ni. Chene o kaa nyɛn kpa la saha wa wa.

ref: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
text: Mu sɛm ta ya wutirɛ domi Yesu Kristo nyɛn min ni. Ba naa maga nyɛn Girik tim pulum ni zi nuu di mu wara nyini ma se ni a li kundɔŋ ni.

ref: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
text: Ma lisi di mu la tum nan, mu wara daŋni maa? Koosa zimu di mu daŋni mau kinkan.

ref: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
text: Mu wa zi chɛrgi chenani mu tum nan, chen ni nibiksɛ nama wa zi na nyinbaan a ŋɔ di ba tuma awa mu tuma ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
text: Hali sintana gbanko koti a chɛrgi o titi, chen ni nara zi lisiu di o ya Koosa malaikasi ta nichaani digirɛ.

ref: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
text: Sintana koti a tum chen, o wa bɔm wa o tumtumsa chɛrgi ba titi, chen ni ma zi lisiu di ba ya Koosa tumtumsiwa. Koosa wa di ba sɛria, o zi na ba galimsau a ta ba yɔri. Chenani Pɔɔl Fun Namsi A Biksi Nara Koosa Wɛ

ref: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
text: Mu piin sɔbiu di ma bɔnɛ zi lisiu di mu sɛm ta wa kpak nyuha. Mu bur a sumi mau di ma kpak min kanyita. Lalaknɔ ma nyɛ mu daŋni sɔn tama.

ref: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
text: Ya kunyan ti wa fun biksi ya di ya daŋni sɔna. Womna la daŋni chen duŋduŋ.

ref: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
text: Nibiksɛ nama la tiŋ ba titi daŋnɛ aa daŋni sɔna a du ba titi ni. Chendaha mu gbanko daŋni sɔn.

ref: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
text: Ma lisu di ma kpak zima kinkannɛ, amaa ma wa vusi a nu womsi biksi nɔ.

ref: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
text: Ba wa kpa tuha sa a du ma ni a dɛɛsi ma a chi bichɛsa, a nyɛ ma chɔk ma malɛ awa diwe kinkan a pini ma aa gbaari. Ma la kin nama maga.

ref: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
text: Ya wa ma funɛ nyɛn, Koosa wa fun nyɛ ya bambi kpoka di ya ya chen a nyɛ ma. Baa daŋni sɔna, mu gbanko zi ya womu a tum chen.

ref: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
text: Ba ya Ju timwaa? Mu gbanko ya Ju timwa. Ba ya Abraham bi lulɛwaa? Mu gbanko ya ba warɛ.

ref: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
text: Ba ŋɔ ma di ba ya Yesu Kristo tumtumsiwaa? Ma zi ŋɔ di mu zu galɛŋwa, amaa mu daŋni mu ŋɔ ma di mu ya o tumtumnɛ a kaali ba kinkan. Mu funɛ tum tumwa kinkan a nyɛ un. Ba fun a tɔ min dɛ bu damata a kpa vurmsa a tuk min. Mu fun wa dugli siwi bu damata. Mu kaali bau a li kina maga ni kinkan.

ref: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
text: Nara fun kpa daasa a tuk min naval butora. Nara fun kpa buŋkula a yaksi min bu bɛgim. Mu funɛ nyɛn arŋ zen nee, peha fun wa mo un a la zini gbɛntɛla o pɛksi. O funɛ ya chene naval butora. Mu funɛ nyɛn teku ni ti nee aa di nha wa dusum maga bu bɛgim.

ref: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
text: Mu funɛ tumu a la gali a gil mu do bu damata. Nyɔksa awa lɔsa fun a kpak min. Mu kilaarɛ wa fun mak min ka mu vɛ kpak bar chɔɔli wuzu kama.

ref: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
text: Kin nawara nyɛnɛɛ mu ha vɛ sɛm ma a wɛ. Mu baga nyɛnu kinkan saha maga di Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga zi tiŋ unu nɛnɛ.

ref: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
text: Nar vɛ tiŋ un nɛnɛ, o muksi minu. Nar ka tum tum tagna, mu tuha zi chɔku kinkan a daha.

ref: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
text: Koosa naa ya ya kunyan Yesu nyana, zimu di mu wa ya nusun tiina. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
text: Mu funɛ nyɛn Damaskus nɛɛ, gɔbnati nyuhu tiina naa ba funɛ yir Aretas, fun tum o lanjimasa di ba la il tɔhɔn ti nɔɔna a kpak min.

ref: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
text: Ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma tama a gbaari mu titi wɛ ni. Chenani mu zi ŋɔ ma wa zi suŋi ma kinkan, amaa nyinbaan wa to mu zi vɛ sɛm ma. Wuzu digirɛ Koosa fun suŋi min di mu na ya kunyan ti.

ref: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
text: Mu funɛ piin a wa ma watiu bilɛ. Lalaknɔ mu bur a saksiu di mu wa. Mu wa pu ma, mu wara daŋni ma wa lisi kundɔŋ a li ma kin ni a nyɛ min. Mu daŋni ma kpa ma titirɛ a ka nyɛ min. Ma chi mu bisiwa. Bichɛsa wara dɛɛsi ba nyama awa ba numa, amaa ba nyama awa ba numa ka la dɛɛsi ba.

ref: As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time.
text: Ma nyɛ mu bukoo ŋɔ ma. Koosa funɛ lisi min a zini a zu bar weri kinkan. O duŋduŋ zim di o funɛ ya nan koo o chi mu funɛ deesiu.

ref: But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell.
text: Mu funɛ zu dooni, mu funɛ nu sɛmau a naa mu vɛ koti a sɛm ma.

ref: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
text: Mu fu zi daŋni sɔnu a daha, amaa mu wa zi bu daŋni sɔn. Mu ka daŋni mu biksi mau di mu titi kpok ti ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
text: Mu fu ŋɔ ma chenani mu du, ma fu wa zi ŋɔ di mu sɛma wa kpak nyuha. Mu fu zi ŋɔ ma wutiu. Mu wara daŋni nara zim di mu ya nibin zenne a kaali ba, domi chenani Koosa fun ya a nyɛ min daha. Mu daŋni ba ka di min sɛriarɛ mu tuma awa mu sɛma daha.

ref: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
text: Koosa wa funɛ daŋni mu zer mu titi, domi o funɛ nyɛ mu nu alaazibu sɛmau. Chendaha o fun kpa wichɔɔrta a nyɛ min o wi mu bara ni saha maga. O chi Koosa nyɛ sintana tuksi minu saha maga.

ref: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
text: Mu funɛ sumi ya kunyan ti kin nɔ wɛ butora a ŋɔ un di o kpa un a ta.

ref: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
text: O fun vɛ la nɔɔni a ŋɔ min, “Mu zi nyɛ hin mu kpok tiu i di un. Nibine kpok bur gantɛlu, kpok naa mu kpa du un ni chen la gbaari.” Chendaha mu tuha sɔɔni ka mu daŋni sɔn di mu titi kpok ti ya yɔrirɛ, chen ni Yesu Kristo kpok ti zi nyɛn min niu kinkan.

ref: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
text: Nara pini min a nyɛ mu na namsi a la muksi min kinkan Yesu Kristo daha ka mu vɛ koti a nyini mu titi kpok se ni, mu tuha sɔɔnɛu. Pɔɔl Baga Funɛ Nyɛn Ba Ni

ref: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
text: Ma muksi minu di mu sɔbi a nyɛ ma a chi mu ya wom. Yɛrɛ ma bɔnɛ vɛ nyɛ min jirima? Mu titi kpok ti ya yɔrirɛ, amaa mu wara tiŋ nusun nibiksɛ ba naa ma lisi di ba wa ya nibirige, gantɛl.

ref: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
text: Mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati, ma wa funɛ beeli di mu ya Koosa nitumirɛ wuti. Waa funɛ suŋi min di mu tum tum zene a kitum alaazibu tuma awa kanyita kinkan ni a nyɛ ma.

ref: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
text: Mu daŋni mu kpu mu titirɛ a nyɛ ma, chen ni mu zi suŋi mau. Chenani mu daŋni ma la suwachak, ma daŋni zi bur gantɛluu?

ref: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
text: Ma zimu nɛnɛ di mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati, mu wa funɛ nyini ma diwe se ni. Ma bɔnɛ zi buru a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni di mu sɛm ta ya wutirɛ amaa mu nɛɛgiu a mur ma.

ref: Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you?
text: Yɛrɛ mu nɛɛgi a la mur ma? Ba naa mu fun tum ma wati chen, ba funɛ di mauu?

ref: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
text: Mu funɛ sumi Taitusu di o la ma wati. O fun la nɔɔni, mu fun tum o wa ya niinbe digi ma wati. Taitus funɛ di mauu? Ma wa zim di chenani mu wa Taitus bambe lisi awa chenani yaa tum, ya kibɛgimnɛɛ?

ref: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
text: Ma lisi di ya kɔɔri ya sɔniwa ma wati a li tɔn nɔ nii? Chen wa ya wuti. Koosa dɔ ya ya sɔbi sɛm naa Yesu Kristo fun kpa nyɛ ya. Mu bi daŋnɛ, ya tum tagna la zi suŋi ma.

ref: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
text: O muksi minu mu wa wa ma wati, ma bɔnɛ wa zi tiŋ chenani mu daŋni maa tiŋ. Ma zi wa di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛu a kpak dɔŋa handɔta a na paan a du dɔŋ ni a ya se tima a kichɔk dɔŋ sɔna a kilɔ dɔŋ gantɛla a zer ma titi a vɛ tiŋ ma nihɛɛta nɔɔna. O du chen, ma wa zi daŋni chenani mu wa ma zi nyɛn.

ref: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
text: O muksi minu mu wa wa ma wati, Koosa zi nyɛ mu na hiisau ma daha. Mu tuha zi chɔku kinkan domi ma nidamata wa zi gil ma chɔkumsa tuma awa ma sakaal tuma awa ma tum doŋa kinkan a bur wa tiŋ Koosa.

ref: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
text: Mu funɛ piin wa ma watiu bilɛ. Lalaknɔ mu bur a saksiu di mu wa. Maa lisi chenani ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni. Ba funɛ ŋɔ, “Ma daŋni ma fiksi nandigi, ma kpak nara alɛ akoo nara batora ba ya ma sɛhɛra tima.”

ref: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
text: Mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati bilɛ tiin chen, mu funɛ tɛ ma naa la tum tum tagna chen ninu. Lalaknɔ mu bol ma niu, mu ha vɛɛn a tɛ ma awa ba naa ka, ninu. Mu saksiu di mu wa lɛrgi ma diŋsa kinkan.

ref: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
text: Yesu Kristo kpak kpok kinkan. Mu wa pu ma, ma zi zim di o kpok ti nyɛn min niu.

ref: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
text: Ba funɛ wa kpa un a la kpaasi a mari o daakakli ti ni o sou, o wa fun kpak kpok. Amaa lalaknɔ o bu nyɛn Koosa kpok naa funɛ lɛli un a li o sou ni, daharɛ. Chen vɛɛna, mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati saha digi, mu funɛ ya jɛrgirɛ ma wati. Mu wa kaa bur wa, mu wa Koosa kpok kinkan zi bur wau.

ref: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
text: Maa zaamsi ma titi di ma se su Yesu Kristo nɛnɛ. Ma zim di o nyɛn ma bambe niu akoo ma higliu di ma tiŋ unuu?

ref: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
text: Ma zimu di ya wara higli. Ya kpak unu nɛnɛ.

ref: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
text: Yaa sumi Koosau di ma bur a li ma tum tagna ni, chen ni mu wa zi wa lɛrgi ma diŋsa. Ma bur a tum tum were, chen bɔɛɛ di mu biksi ma chenani Yesu Kristo kpok ti nyɛn min ni.

ref: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
text: Ya yaa kpami mau di ma tum were a nyɛ Koosa ni a tiŋ o wuti nyinbaan.

ref: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
text: Ya vɛ kpak kpok ka ma wa kpak kpok, ya tusa zi sɔɔniu. Chendaha ya yaa sumi Koosau di kin naa nyɛn ma wati maga kɔɔri.

ref: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
text: Mu sɔbi tɔn nɔwa a te nyɛ ma di ma kɔɔri kin nama, chen ni ka mu wa wa ma wati, mu wa zi bu kpa kpok naa ya kunyan ti fun kpa nyɛ min chen a lɛrgi ma diŋsa. O funɛ kpa unu a nyɛ min di mu suŋi ma ma zim un a gbaari.

ref: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
text: Ya niinbisa, lalaknɔ ya daŋni ya mak tɔn nɔ sɔbi bar nɔwa. Ma nu chenani mu sɛm ma chen a kɔɔri kin nawara vɛ deŋli maga. Ma kpak nɔ bɛgim wa dɔŋ ni a kpak bara sɔɔna awa dɔŋ ni. Koosa naa la daŋni ma a la nyɛ ma bara sɔɔna, o zi suŋi mau a chi nan.

ref: From the apostle Paul and from all the Lord's followers with me. I was chosen to be an apostle by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised him from death. No mere human chose or appointed me to this work. To the churches in Galatia:
text: Mu wa ya niinbisa ba naa maga nyɛn bar nɔ, zaami ya dɔŋtima ma naa maga lagmi dooni aa jɛɛmi Koosa a li Galesia pulum ni.

ref: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
text: Ya kunyan Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma aa kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna.

ref: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
text: Kristo funɛ tiŋ ya kunyan Koosa tutɔrɛ a sou ya daha, chen ni o zi kotiu a yisi ya a li lalaknɔ wu tagna saha ni.

ref: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
text: Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa naa kpak jirima kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, chen daha. Ami. Koosa Laaboor Weri Ya Kibɛgim Duŋduŋnɛ

ref: I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message,
text: Mu funɛ nuu di ma wa tiŋ Koosa naa funɛ yir ma, awa Kristo naa suŋi ma, a vusi. Ma buru a tiŋ biksi digi lalaga. O jɛri minu kinkan.

ref: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
text: Nara bɔnɛ funɛ chɛrgi Koosa laaboor weri tiu Kristo wɛ a wa muksi ma saha maga. O laaboor weri ti ya kibɛgim duŋduŋnɛ. Digi wa bu toli.

ref: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
text: Koosa malaikasi ta digi zi wa li o tɔhɔn tiu koo min wa biksi ma laaboor weri naa kurgɛɛ a li Koosa laaboor weri naa ya piin biksi ma chen, Koosa zi na ya galimsau a ta ya yɔri.

ref: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
text: Mu funɛ te ŋɔ ma nanu. Lalaknɔ mu bur a ŋɔ mau. Nandigi biksi laaboor weri ti ka o kur a li Koosa laaboor weri naa ma fun a la nɔɔni di o ya wutirɛ, Koosa zi na o galimsau a ta un yɔri.

ref: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
text: Ma lisu di muu daŋni mu ya nara lɛmlɛmnɛɛ? Aai. Mu daŋni mu sɔɔni Koosa duŋduŋ tuharɛ. Mu daŋni mu ya nara lɛmlɛm, mu wa ya Kristo tumtumna. Chenani Koosa Fun Lisi Pɔɔl O Ya O Nitumi

ref: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni mu ŋɔ ma wu digirɛ. Nibinsa wa fun naami laaboor weri naa mu fun biksi ma chen.

ref: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
text: Nandigi wa fun a biksi min hali tama. Yesu Kristo titi funɛ kpa un a biksi min gbampulim ni.

ref: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
text: Ma zimu di saha digi mu funɛ tiŋ Koosau a chi Ju tim ta, mu fun a muksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta kinkan. Mu wa fun a kpak ba siki a la daŋni mu chɔk ba maga.

ref: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
text: Mu funɛ tiŋ chenani Ju tim ta tiŋu nɛnɛ a kaali ba naa nyuuna mak dɔŋa. Mu se funɛ sɛriu kinkan di mu wa tiŋ mu nawaalsi ta nyinbaan.

ref: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
text: a nyɛ mu wa zim o be Yesu ti gbampulim ni, chen ni mu zi la biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju timau o laaboor weri ti wɛ. Mu wa fun lagmi nandigi ni di o ŋɔ min chenani mu zi tum,

ref: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
text: amaa mu fun vaan kaali Arabia pulum. Mu wa fun la di mu la na ba naa funɛ piin ya Yesu nitumɛ ta a li Jɛrusalɛm ni. Mu funɛ nyɛn Arabia ni peti, mu fun bur a kaali Damaskus.

ref: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
text: Bunsa tora gantɛl ni, mu fun kaali Jɛrusalɛm di mu la na Pita, a nyɛn o wati wuzu fidanyuu,

ref: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
text: Yesu nitumɛ ta maga ni, mu funɛ pu Jems naa ya ya kunyan ti niinbe, duŋduŋnɛ.

ref: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
text: Chenani mu sɔbi nan ya wutirɛ. Koosa ya mu sɛhɛra tiina mu wara suni wɛ.

ref: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
text: Mu funɛ li Jɛrusalɛm ni, mu fun gba Siiriya pulum awa Silisia pulum.

ref: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
text: Chen saha ti maga, Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Judia pulum ni, wa fun pu min.

ref: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
text: Ba funɛ nu di mu ya naa funɛ nyɛ ba namsi kinkan a la daŋni mu dusi Koosa laaboor weri ti a ta, ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Baŋa ya baal nɔ burgi a suŋi ya nan?”

ref: And because of me, they praised God.
text: Ba fun a pɛgi Koosa mu daha.

ref: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
text: Bun fidanaasi gantɛl ni, min, Barnabas awa Taitus fun bur a kaali Jɛrusalɛm.

ref: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
text: Koosa titi funɛ biksi min di ya la dooni. Ya wa Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn dooni, nihɛɛta fun wa lagmi dɔŋ ni ya duŋduŋ. Mu fun ŋɔ ba chenani mu fun biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ. Mu wara daŋni ba lisi di tum naa mu tum a nyɛ Koosa ni chen ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
text: Taitus naa funɛ nyɛn mu wati, funɛ ya Girik timwa, amaa ba wa fun muksi un di ba teŋ un.

ref: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
text: Nara ba naa funɛ ŋɔ di ba ya Yesu nitiŋɛwa, bɔnɛ funɛ daŋni di ba teŋ un, amaa ba funɛ suniu. Ba fun a sogli a zu di ba na chenani ya tiŋ Yesu a ta nyɛ ba teŋsi ya, domi ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ba daŋni ya burgi ba yosiwa.

ref: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
text: Ya wa ba wa fun la nɔɔni hali tama, chen ni ya zi kotiu a kpak Koosa laaboor weri naa ma piin nu wuti.

ref: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
text: Ba naa funɛ chi nihɛɛti ta dooni fun la nɔɔni di chenani mu biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ya wutirɛ. Ba wa fun kpa biksi digi a gbaari mu biksi ti ni. Mu baga wa fun to di ba wa ya nihɛɛti ta koo ba laata. Koosa wara di ya sɛria a li ya bara nɛɛ.

ref: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
text: Ba funɛ zimu di Koosa funɛ kpa o laaboor weri ti a du mu nin ni a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni. O gbanko funɛ kpa o laaboor weri ti a du Pita nin ni a nyɛ ba naa ya Ju tim ni.

ref: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
text: Jems, Pita awa Jɔn ba naa chi nihɛɛti ta, funɛ zimu di Koosa suŋi minu di muu tum o tuma chen ni, ba fun la nɔɔni di ya wa ba ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ba wa ya gbanko fun la nɔɔniu di ba zi la biksi ba naa ya Ju timau. Ya ka zi la biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju timau.

ref: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
text: Wuzu bilɛ Pita fun wa dooni. O funɛ nyɛn doon chene, mu fun a fiksi un gbampulim ni di mu na o galimsau.

ref: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
text: Mu funɛ na ka ba vɛ tiŋ chenani Koosa laaboor weri ti biksi ya wuti, mu fun ŋɔ Pita ni ba maga suwachak ni, “I ya Ju tima, amaa i nyɛnu a chi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima. Yɛrɛ ii daŋni i muksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima di ba nyɛn a chi Ju tim nyɛnɛɛ?” Kin Naa Nyɛ Koosa Vɛ Na Nibine Galimsa

ref: But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
text: Ya zimu di nar tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ, chen tiina ha zi vɛɛn kpak galimsau o wati, amaa nar nyɛ o si su Yesu Kristo, Koosa wa zi bu na o galimsa. Ya se su Yesu Kristo. Ya wara tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ. Chendaha o wa bu na ya galimsa.

ref: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
text: Lalaknɔ Koosa wa bu na ya galimsa, domi ya wa Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ, chen biksi nara di ya funɛ te kpak galimsau o wati a la tum tum tagna. Chen gbanko biksi di ya Ju tim ta funɛ te kpak galimsau Koosa wati a la tum tum tagna, a chi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima. Chen biksi di Kristo nyɛ ya tum tagna nitumɛɛ? Aai.

ref: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
text: Amaa mu bu biksi di nar tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ di Koosa wa zi bu na o galimsa, chen biksi di mu chɔk o chiksɛ tawa.

ref: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
text: Mu tiŋ o chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ, mu wa kikoti a tiŋ a a peti, a na galimsa o wati. Mu wa bu tiŋ chen. Lalaknɔ mu nyɛn a nyɛ Koosa nɛɛ. O chi mu wa Kristo sowɛɛ a li o daakakli ti ni,

ref: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
text: chen ni mu wa zi bu nyɛn, amaa Kristo kinyɛn min ni. Mu si su Koosa be naa la daŋni min a fun sou mu daha. Chene muu tiŋ lalaknɔ.

ref: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
text: Mu wara tɔ Koosa suŋi nyinbaan. Nar ka tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ, chen ni Koosa wa zi bu na o galimsa, chen biksi di Kristo sou funɛ ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
text: Mu daŋni mu pɛɛsi ma wu digirɛ. Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ ma, ma funɛ tiŋ o chiksɛ ta hali a wa petiu koo ma funɛ nu o laaboor weri tiu a nyɛ ma se su unn?

ref: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
text: Ma ya womniwa kinkan. Ma funɛ te tiŋ a li Koosa siim weri ti kpok ni, amaa lalaknɔ maa daŋni ma tiŋu a li ma titi kpok ni hali a wa peti.

ref: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
text: Ma namsi maga ya yɔrirɛ ma watii? Mu kpak bambi di o wa ya yɔri.

ref: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
text: Koosa kpa o siim weri ti a tum tum zene a nyɛ ma, o ya chenu domi maa tiŋ o chiksɛwa hali a wa peti koo domi ma funɛ nu o laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ma se su unn?

ref: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
text: O lawa ma wa zimu di ba naa se su Koosa ya Abraham wuti bi lulɛwa.

ref: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
text: Koosa funɛ te biksi ba naa funɛ sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o wa zi bu na ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima galimsa o suwachak ni, domi ba se zi wa su unu. Abraham funɛ te nu nan laaboor weri nɔ di, “Koosa zi suŋi ba naa maga nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga niu a chi nan o daha.”

ref: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
text: Abraham si funɛ su Koosa. Chendaha Koosa fun suŋi un chen. Ba naa maga se su Koosa, o zi suŋi bau a chi chenani o fun a suŋi Abraham chen.

ref: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
text: Ba naa la nyini Koosa chiksɛ ta tiŋi se ni aa tiŋ a nɔ, Koosa zi ŋɔ nɔu a nyɛ ba. Ba fun a sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Nar tim Koosa chiksɛ hali a wa peti a chel, Koosa zi ŋɔ nɔu a nyɛ un.”

ref: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
text: Kristo funɛ sou, waa funɛ chiŋi doonani Koosa kaa ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ya bar chiŋi ni a la ya nyuuna a nyɛ ya. Ba fun bukoo sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa zi ŋɔ nɔu a nyɛ naa ba yisi a tɛ daakakli ti ni.”

ref: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu daŋni mu ŋɔ mau a li ya nyɛni ni. Ma zim di nara balɛ ya nɔ bɛgim a la nɔɔni dɔŋ ni, nandigi wa kikoti a chɔk un koo ma gbaari.

ref: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
text: Ba fun bukoo sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa funɛ kpa o vɔwe a nyɛ Abraham awa o be luli ni.” Ba wa fun sɔbi di o kpa a a nyɛ Abraham awa o bi lulɛ ni. O mun ya di o funɛ kpa a a nyɛ nibin bɛgim duŋduŋ nɛɛ. Chen tiina ya Kristo.

ref: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
text: Nara la chenani Koosa fun vɔu chen domi ba waa la tiŋ o chiksɛ ta duŋduŋ, chen la zi chɔk o vɔwi naa o nyɛ Abraham ni a nyɛ o ya yɔri.

ref: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
text: Yɛrɛ Koosa fun kpa o chiksɛ ta a nyɛ Mosis ni a gbaari o vɔwi naa o fun piin kpa a nyɛ Abraham ni? Koosa funɛ ya chene, chen ni nibinsa zi zimu chenani tum tagna duu, hali saha naa Abraham be luli naa Koosa fun vɔu di o tum, fun wa. Koosa funɛ kpa o chiksɛ ta a nyɛ nara ni, o funɛ te kpa a a nyɛ o malaikasi ta nɛɛ. Ba fun a kpa a a nyɛ Mosis ni o ka fun kpa a a nyɛ nara ni.

ref: There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him. Slaves and Children
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ kpa o vɔwi ti a nyɛ Abraham ni, o wa fun te kpa un a nyɛ malaikasi ta koo nandigi ni. Kin Naa Nyɛ Koosa Fun Nyɛ Nara O Chiksɛ

ref: Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law.
text: Koosa chiksɛ ta awa o vɔwi ti yo dɔŋwaa? Aai. O chiksɛ ta fu nyɛ o koti a kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ nibinsa ni, o fu wa zi na ba galimsa ba chiksɛ ta tiŋi daha.

ref: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga na galimsa ba tum tagna daha. Chendaha ba naa se su Yesu Kristo, la Koosa papaha naa o fun vɔu a nyɛ Abraham ni chen.

ref: The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith.
text: O chi Koosa chiksɛ ta funɛ te kpak ya wa tum tagna a tɔ dɛ hali Koosa fun wa biksi ya chenani ya se zi su Yesu.

ref: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
text: Amaa Yesu Kristo funɛ wa, chiksɛ ta wa bur a il ya. Waa funɛ nyɛ ya se su un, chen ni Koosa wa zi bu na ya galimsa.

ref: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
text: Lalaknɔ ya se su Yesu, waa ya ya kunyana. Koosa chiksɛ ta wa bu ya ya kunyana.

ref: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Chenani ma se su Yesu Kristo nyɛ ma ya Koosa bisa.

ref: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
text: Lalaknɔ ma wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ma funɛ burgi chenani o duu.

ref: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
text: Ma naa ya Ju tima awa ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima, ma naa ya yosa awa ma naa vɛ ya yosa, ma nibaala awa ma nihaana, ma maga ya nibɛgimnɛ, domi ma wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
text: Kristo dɔ ma. Chendaha ma zi na kin naa maga Koosa fun vɔu a nyɛ Abraham bi lulɛ ni chenu.

ref: Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs.
text: Mu daŋni mu ŋɔ mau a gbaari. Bichɛla naa la zi wa dɔ o nyan kina, o nyana yaa fun a dɛɛsi unu a chi yoma.

ref: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
text: O nyan ti zi nyɛ nara kiil unu, hali saha naa o zi wa dɔ o nyan ti kina maga.

ref: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
text: Chen vɛɛna, wu damata awa chiksɛ funɛ te dɔ ya a nyɛ ya burgi a yosa ka ya zi wa zim Yesu Kristo.

ref: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
text: Amaa o saha funɛ wa tel, Koosa fun tum o titi be tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Nihaan digi funɛ lul o be. O fun bi aa tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta.

ref: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
text: O funɛ ya chene di o la nara ba naa chiksɛ ta dɔ, nyuuna a nyɛ ba, chen ni Koosa zi kotiu a kpa ba a burgi o titi bisa.

ref: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
text: Ma ya o bisiwa. Chendaha Koosa fun kpa o be siim weri ti a du ma bambe ni. Chen siim weri ti nyɛ ma yir Koosa ma kunyana.

ref: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
text: Lalaknɔ ma wa bu ya yosa, amaa ma ya Koosa bisiwa. Chendaha o zi kpa o kin nawara maga o fun vɔu chen a nyɛ mau. O Fun Muksi Pɔɔl Kinkan Ba Daha

ref: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
text: Saha digi ma wa fun zim Koosa. Ma waa funɛ zaami a tiŋ kin naa chi a ya Koosarɛ. A wa ya Koosa hali tama.

ref: But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers?
text: Lalaknɔ ma zim Koosa a ya o bisa ka o zim ma, yɛrɛ maa daŋni ma bur aa tiŋ chiina awa chiksɛ? Ma bur a daŋni ma ya a yosiwaa?

ref: You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years.
text: Ma waa funɛ lisi pɛn bɔnɛ awa bun bɔnɛ a di ma wuzu zene a ni.

ref: I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
text: O muksi minu kinkan ma daha. Tum naa mu fun tum a nyɛ ma, chen ya yɔrirɛɛ?

ref: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
text: Mu niinbisa, mu sumi mau di maa tiŋ a chi chenani muu tiŋ chen. Mu funɛ nyɛn ma wati, mu fun burgi a chi chenani ma du. Ma fun la min du nɛnɛ.

ref: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
text: Ma zimu di mu funɛ te wa biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ, mu bara wa fun lɛm.

ref: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
text: Mu wichɔɔrta fu zi duŋni mau wati, amaa ma wa fun vɛ min. Ma waa ka funɛ nyini min a chi mu ya Koosa malaikasi ta digi. Hali a vɛɛn chi mu ya Yesu Kristo titi.

ref: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
text: Ma tusa funɛ sɔɔni kinkan. Hali ma kpa ma bambe a nyɛ min. Baŋa ya?

ref: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
text: Mu funɛ biksi ma wa wuti ni. Chen nyɛ mu ya ma dɔm lalaknɔɔ?

ref: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
text: Nusun nibiksɛ tima kpak se sɛm di ba purigi ma a li min ni, chen ni ma zi vɛ mu biksi tiu aa tiŋ ba.

ref: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
text: O weru wa nar kpak si sɛmu wa wu were ni saha maga. Mu nyɛn ma wati koo mu wa to ma wati, chen ya wutirɛ.

ref: My children, I am in terrible pain until Christ may be seen living in you.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, mu chi nihaan naa la daŋni lulirɛ aa la wi ma daha. Mu daŋni kinkan di Kristo wa nyɛn ma ni hali o wa peti.

ref: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
text: Mu gbanko daŋniu kinkan di mu wa nyɛn ma wati, chen ni mu zi kotiu a zim chenani mu zi wa nɔ ma. O muksi minu kinkan ma daha. Chenani Hega Awa Sara Fun Biksi Ya

ref: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
text: Ma naa la daŋni di Koosa chiksɛ ta dɔ ma, maa lisi chenani a biksi ma.

ref: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
text: Ya karimɛu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di Abraham funɛ kpak bisa lɛwa. Yoma naa ba funɛ yir Hega chen, lul digi. Naa ka funɛ vɛ ya yom chen, lul digi.

ref: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
text: Koosa funɛ vɔu vɔwiu a nyɛ naa vɛ ya yoma chen di o zi lul beu, amaa o wa fun vɔu vɔwi di yom ti zi lulu.

ref: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
text: Nan la biksi ya wu digi. Saha digi Koosa awa Ju tim ta funɛ te ya nɔ bɛgimnɛ a li bi naa ba yir Sainai a li Arabia pulum ni. Nihaan naa funɛ ya yoma, o chi chenani ba fun ya dooni chene. Ba naa maga tiŋ chenani Koosa awa o nara fun ya chen wuzu ti, ba chi yosiwa.

ref: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
text: Hega gbanko funɛ chi Ju tima ba naa Koosa chiksɛ ta dɔrɛ. Ba funɛ yir ba tɔ zen Jɛrusalɛm. Chendaha ba ya yosa,

ref: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
text: amaa nihaan naa funɛ vɛ ya yom chen, o chi Jɛrusalɛm naa nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni chene. Ya waa ya o bisa.

ref: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Nihabari naa vɛ na wiili a lul be, nyɛ i tuha sɔɔni hali a la gali. Naa baal funɛ duŋni un, waa la zi lul a kaali naa kpak baal.”

ref: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
text: Chen saha ti yom ti be funɛ muksi naa vɛ ya yom chen be kinkannɛ, domi o funɛ lul un Koosa siim weri kpok ti daharɛ. Hali wa zinan, ba naa Koosa chiksɛ ta dɔ muksi ba naa la tiŋ Koosa o siim weri kpok ti daharɛ.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
text: Ba fun bukoo sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Nihaan yom ti be awa naa vɛ ya yom chen be wa zi bɔni ba nyana kina. Chendaha ma duŋni nihaan yom ti awa o be ti a ta.”

ref: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya zimu di ya wa ya naa vɛ ya yom chen bi lulɛwa. Ya waa ka ya nihaan yom ti bi lulɛ.

ref: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
text: Kristo funɛ lisi yau a li yosi ni. Maa kpak chen nɛnɛ, a ta bu nyɛ nara kpak ma a ya yosa.

ref: I, Paul, promise you that Christ won't do you any good if you get circumcised.
text: Ma nu mu sɛma. N waa, Pɔɔl, la ŋɔ ma di ma nyɛ nusun nibiksɛ nama teŋsi ma, Kristo ya yɔrirɛ ma wati.

ref: And if you try to please God by obeying the Law, you have cut yourself off from Christ and his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Ma naa la kpaŋi ma titi di maa tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta, chen ni o wa zi bu na ma galimsa, ma waa la vɛ Kristo. Chendaha Koosa vɛ koti a suŋi ma hali tama.

ref: If you are a follower of Christ Jesus, it makes no difference whether you are circumcised or not. All that matters is your faith that makes you love others.
text: Domi ya wa Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ, Koosa baga wa to di nara teŋsi yau koo ba wa teŋsi. Amaa o ka daŋni kinkannɛ di ya se su un, chen ni ya zi daŋni dɔŋau.

ref: You were doing so well until someone made you turn from the truth.
text: Ma funɛ tiŋ Koosa nyinbaanu nɛnɛ. Lalaknɔ ma nyɛ nusun nibiksɛ nama wa nyɛ ma buru a li o wuti ti tiŋi ni.

ref: And that person was certainly not sent by the one who chose you.
text: Koosa naa funɛ yir ma, wa ya chen.

ref: A little yeast can change a whole batch of dough,
text: Nandigi fun ŋɔ di ma kpa luri tama a du boroboro damata ni, o maga zi fuusiu.

ref: but you belong to the Lord. This makes me certain you will do what I say, instead of what someone else tells you to do. Whoever is causing trouble for you will be punished.
text: Domi ya wa ya kunyan ti ya nibɛgimnɛ, mu kpak bambiu di ma zi tiŋ mu sɛmau. Mu gbanko kpak bambiu di Koosa zi wa na ba naa la muksi ma galimsau a ta ba yɔri. Hali chenani ba mak.

ref: My friends, if I still preach that people need to be circumcised, why am I in so much trouble? The message about the cross would no longer be a problem, if I told people to be circumcised.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu biksi di o lawa ba teŋsi ba naa la tiŋ Yesu, yɛrɛ Ju tim ta ha vɛɛn a muksi min kinkan? Mu biksi nara nan ni, ba wa zi muksi min. Mu zi kotiu a biksi nara di Kristo funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni a ta na muksi.

ref: I wish everyone who is upsetting you would not only get circumcised, but would cut off much more!
text: Hali mu daŋni kinkan di ba naa la muksi ma, zi ya ba titi namugilisau.

ref: My friends, you were chosen to be free. So don't use your freedom as an excuse to do anything you want. Use it as an opportunity to serve each other with love.
text: Mu niinbisa, Koosa funɛ yir ma di ma ta ya yosa. Amaa lalaknɔ o yisɛ mau, ma ta bu tiŋ ma titi daŋnɛ. Ma ka daŋni dɔŋa a kisuŋi dɔŋa.

ref: All the Law says can be summed up in the command to love others as much as you love yourself.
text: Koosa chiksɛ ta digi ya a koora a biksi ya di, “Maa daŋni ma dɔŋtima a chi chenani maa daŋni ma titi.”

ref: But if you keep attacking each other like wild animals, you had better watch out or you will destroy yourselves. God's Spirit and Our Own Desires
text: Ma yaa na paan a du dɔŋ ni a la di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ, ma doso. Wuzu digi ma zi wa chɔk dɔŋau. Koosa Siim Weri Yo Ya Tutɔ Daŋnɛ

ref: The Spirit and your desires are enemies of each other. They are always fighting each other and keeping you from doing what you feel you should.
text: Ma tutɔ la yo chenani Koosa siim weri ti daŋni maa tiŋ. Chenani o siim weri ti daŋni ma nyɛn yo ma tutɔ daŋnɛ tau. O siim weri ti awa ma tutɔ daŋnɛ ya dɔmawa. Chendaha o bɔm ma wati di ma tum chenani maa daŋni.

ref: But if you obey the Spirit, the Law of Moses has no control over you.
text: Koosa siim weri ti ya ma nyuhu tiina, Koosa chiksɛ ta wa bu ya ma nyuhu tiina.

ref: People's desires make them give in to immoral ways, filthy thoughts, and shameful deeds.
text: Ma zim chenani nara titi daŋnɛ duu. Saha maga baa tum sakaal tumwa awa chɔkumsi tuma.

ref: They worship idols, practice witchcraft, hate others, and are hard to get along with. People become jealous, angry, and selfish. They not only argue and cause trouble, but they are
text: Ba yaa nyɛ vursiwa jirimarɛ a la tum hiti tuma. Ba yaa ha dɔŋwa a la di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ a na paan a du dɔŋ ni a kpak handɔta wa dɔŋa a ya se tima. Ba purigi dɔŋwa,

ref: envious. They get drunk, carry on at wild parties, and do other evil things as well. I told you before, and I am telling you again: No one who does these things will share in the blessings of God's kingdom.
text: a nyɔ sin a dɛɛsi a lagmi a di diwe kinkan a la tum tum tagna kinkan. Mu funɛ te tɛ ma ninu kin nama daha. Mu bukoo tɛ ba naa la tum tuma a chi nan ninu di ba wa zi wa zu Koosa koor ti.

ref: And because we belong to Christ Jesus, we have killed our selfish feelings and desires.
text: Ba naa Yesu Kristo dɔ, ba funɛ kpa ba titi daŋnɛu a ta.

ref: But don't be conceited or make others jealous by claiming to be better than they are.
text: Ya ta daŋni sɔna a daŋni bara bora aa kidi handɔta a du dɔŋ ni.

ref: My friends, you are spiritual. So if someone is trapped in sin, you should gently lead that person back to the right path. But watch out, and don't be tempted yourself.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma nidigi tum tum tagin, ma naa la tiŋ ya kunyan ti nɛnɛ ya jɛrgi o wati a kpaŋi a burgi o nyuha. Maa doso a ta cheli o chi un.

ref: You obey the law of Christ when you offer each other a helping hand.
text: Maa suŋi dɔŋa a li ma tuma maga ni. Ma ya chen, ma zi tiŋ chenani Kristo fun a biksi yau.

ref: If you think you are better than others, when you really aren't, you are wrong.
text: Nar lisee di o ya nibin zenne a vɛ yaali, o mur o titirɛ.

ref: Do your own work well, and then you will have something to be proud of. But don't compare yourself with others.
text: Maa zaamsi ma titi chenani maa tiŋ saha maga. Maa tiŋ nɛnɛ, chen weru. Ma ta kpa chenani maa tiŋi awa ma dɔŋtima tiŋi a lagmi dɔŋ ni a ŋɔ di maa tiŋu nɛnɛ.

ref: We each must carry our own load.
text: Nibine maga dɔ ba titi tumwa Koosa wati.

ref: Share every good thing you have with anyone who teaches you what God has said.
text: Naa funɛ nu Koosa laaboor weri ti Kristo wɛ, o wa o nibiksɛ bɔni o kina.

ref: You cannot fool God, so don't make a fool of yourself! You will harvest what you plant.
text: Ma ta mur ma titi. Nandigi wa kikoti a nyɛ Koosa ya wom. Chenani nibine tum, a zi bur wa o watiu.

ref: Don't get tired of helping others. You will be rewarded when the time is right, if you don't give up.
text: Ma ta nyɛ ya tum tum were a kuuli. Ya kpak chen nɛnɛ, Koosa zi bɔ ya ya chimu.

ref: We should help people whenever we can, especially if they are followers of the Lord. Final Warnings
text: Ma nyɛ ya dɛu nyinbaan aa suŋi nara maga. O lawa ya te a suŋi ya dɔŋtima ba naa se su ya kunyan tiu. Pɔɔl Mak O Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: You can see what big letters I make when I write with my own hand.
text: Ma na chenani kisɔbɛ naa zene mu titi sɔbi bar nɔ nan.

ref: Those people who are telling you to get circumcised are only trying to show how important they are. And they don't want to get into trouble for preaching about the cross of Christ.
text: Ba naa la daŋni sɔna nara suwachak ni, ba waa la daŋni ba muksi ma di ba teŋsi ma. Ba daŋni ba teŋsi ma chene, chen ni nandigi wa zi nyɛ ba na namsi. Ba wa la nɔɔni di chenani ma se su Kristo naa funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni, magu.

ref: They are circumcised, but they don't obey the Law of Moses. All they want is to brag about having you circumcised.
text: Hali ba naa teŋsee tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ ta a chel. Ba daŋni ba teŋsi ma, chen ni ma waa la zi ya ba jirima nara.

ref: But I will never brag about anything except the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ. Because of his cross, the world is dead as far as I am concerned, and I am dead as far as the world is concerned.
text: Mu daha, chenani ya kunyana Yesu Kristo fun sou a li o daakakli ti ni chen, chen ya mu jirima kin. Chendaha mu wa fun bukoo tiŋ chenani tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga tiŋ. Mu funɛ vɛ ba nyinbaanu.

ref: It doesn't matter if you are circumcised or not. All that matters is that you are a new person.
text: Koosa baga wa to di nara teŋsi dɔŋa koo ba wa teŋsi. O baga ka nyɛnu di o kɔɔri ma ma ya nibin falɛ.

ref: If you follow this rule, you will belong to God's true people. God will treat you with undeserved kindness and will bless you with peace.
text: Ba naa maga la lisi nan, Koosa zi kpak ba sikiu a kinyɛ ba bara sɔɔna. Ba waa ya Ju tima ba naa Koosa dɔ wuti ni.

ref: On my own body are scars that prove I belong to Christ Jesus. So I don't want anyone to bother me anymore.
text: Mu kpak funfukniwa mu bara ni. A wa biksi nara di mu ya Yesu tumtumnɛ. Chendaha nandigi ta bu gbaari mu namsi nɔ ni.

ref: My friends, I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you! Amen.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma maga. Ami.

ref: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. To God's people who live in Ephesus and are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
text: Min waa, Pɔɔl, naa Koosa fun lisi di mu ya Yesu Kristo nitumi, mu la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ Koosa nara ma naa nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni. Ma gbanko la tiŋ Yesu Kristo nɛnɛ.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Christ Brings Spiritual Blessings
text: Ya kunyan Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma aa kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Chenani Koosa Fun Nyɛ Kristo Suŋi Ya

ref: Praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ for the spiritual blessings that Christ has brought us from heaven!
text: Koosa naa ya ya kunyan Yesu Kristo nyana, waa funɛ kpa o papaha a naa nyɛn ya wa un bambaan ni a nyɛ ya, domi ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa chenani o fun tum chen ni.

ref: Before the world was created, God had Christ choose us to live with him and to be his holy and innocent and loving people.
text: O fun te lisi ya a nyɛ o titi ni Yesu Kristo daha ka o fun a naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga, chen ni ya zi kotiu a ya bambi chaan tima ba naa o vɛ na ba galimsa o suwachak ni.

ref: God was kind and decided that Christ would choose us to be God's own adopted children.
text: Domi o la daŋni ya daha, o fun piin ya o tutɔ chen saha ti o nyɛ ya burgi o bisa Yesu Kristo daha. Chen nyɛ o tuha sɔɔni kinkan.

ref: God treated us with undeserved grace because of the Son he dearly loves, and so we should praise God.
text: Koosa funɛ suŋi yau a kaali chenani o daŋni. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un o funɛ kpa o be daŋni a nyɛ ya yɔri chen.

ref: Then when the time is right, God will do all he has planned, and Christ will bring together everything in heaven and on earth.
text: Saha naa o fun lisi chen wa tel, o zi lagmi kin nawara maga o fun a naamiu, buudaha kina awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina, a nyɛ Yesu Kristo di a maga koora.

ref: He did this so we Jews would bring honor to him and be the first ones to have hope because of him.
text: Ma nyɛ ya naa te kpak bambi bɛgim Yesu Kristo ni, pɛgi Koosa naa kpak jirima kinkan.

ref: Christ also brought you the truth, which is the good news about how you can be saved. You put your faith in Christ and were given the promised Holy Spirit to show you belong to God.
text: Ba funɛ wa biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri naa ya wuti wɛ, ma fun a nu ba biksi ti, ka Koosa fun a yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni ma tiŋ un. Chen saha ti ma se funɛ su Yesu Kristo. Chendaha Koosa fun kpa o siim weri naa o fun vɔu di o nyɛ ma a dani ma.

ref: The Spirit also makes us sure we will be given what God has stored up for his people. Then we will be set free, and God will be honored and praised. Paul's Prayer
text: Koosa nyɛ o siim weri tirɛ ya o purigiri bɔɔli. Chendaha ya zim di o zi la ya nyuunau a nyɛ ya hali o wa peti a chi o funɛ vɔu chen. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un nan daha. Pɔɔl Fun Sumi Koosa A Nyɛ Ba

ref: I have heard about your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for all God's people.
text: Mu funɛ nu chenani ma se su ya kunyan Yesu awa chenani maa daŋni Koosa nara maga.

ref: So I never stop being grateful for you, as I mention you in my prayers.
text: Mu pɛgi Koosa a yaa sumi un saha maga ma daha,

ref: I ask the glorious Father and God of our Lord Jesus Christ to give you his Spirit. The Spirit will make you wise and let you understand what it means to know God.
text: chen ni naa ya ya kunyan Yesu Kristo nyana a kpak jirima kinkan, o zi kpa o zimau a nyɛ ma a biksi ma a biksi ma o titi wɛ gbampulim ni, chen ni ma zi zim unu nɛnɛ.

ref: My prayer is that light will flood your hearts and you will understand the hope given to you when God chose you. Then you will discover the glorious blessings that will be yours together with all God's people.
text: Mu sumi unu di ma lise zi la o wutiu, chen ni ma zi kpak bambi bɛgim a li chenani o fun yir ma ni a zim di kin nawara o fun vɔu di o kpa nyɛ o nara ni zenu kinkan.

ref: I want you to know about the great and mighty power that God has for us followers. It is the same wonderful power he used
text: Mu gbanko sumi unu di ma zim o kpok naa o fun kpa a nyɛ ya naa se su un ni kinkan. Kpok naa ya kpak nan awa kpok kinkan,

ref: when he raised Christ from death and let him sit at his right side in heaven.
text: naa o fun kpa a lɛli Yesu Kristo a li o sou ni a kansi o nihɛɛ bar kani ni a li o tɔhɔn ti ni, ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: There Christ rules over all forces, authorities, powers, and rulers. He rules over all beings in this world and will rule in the future world as well.
text: Yesu Kristo nyɛn dooni lalaknɔ a dɔ siim kpoka awa kpok maga a naa nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ naa la wa nan ni.

ref: God has put all things under the power of Christ, and for the good of the church he has made him the head of everything.
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ o ya kina maga koora awa ba naa tiŋ un nyuhu tiina.

ref: The church is the body of Christ and is filled with Christ who completely fills everything.
text: Ba waa chi o bara. Waa la su kina maga, amaa see ba wa gbaariu a su a peti.

ref: In the past you were dead because you sinned and fought against God.
text: Saha digi ma yaa funɛ tum tum tagniwa a vɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔ. Ma funɛ chi nisitɛwa.

ref: You followed the ways of this world and obeyed the devil. He rules the world, and his spirit has power over everyone who doesn't obey God.
text: Ma yaa funɛ tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nyinbaan taginnɛ awa siim tagna koora. Waa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tima ba naa maga vɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔ.

ref: Once we were also ruled by the selfish desires of our bodies and minds. We had made God angry, and we were going to be punished like everyone else.
text: Saha digi ya maga funɛ tiŋ chene. Lise tagna a naa funɛ nyɛn ya bambe ni awa ya titi daŋnɛ nyɛ ya yaa funɛ tum tum tagna damata. Chendaha Koosa fun na paan a du ya ni. Chenani o funɛ daŋni di o wa lɛrgi ya diŋsa awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga diŋsa funɛ ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: But God was merciful! We were dead because of our sins, but God loved us so much he made us alive with Christ, and God's gift of undeserved grace is what saves you.
text: a fun wa na ya ka ya yaa chi nisitɛ ya tum tagni ta daha. O fun yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni di ya tiŋ un o lɛli ya a li ya sou ni a chi chenani o fun lɛli Yesu Kristo chen. Koosa funɛ suŋi yau kinkan a la gali chen ni.

ref: God raised us from death to life with Christ Jesus, and he has given us a place beside Christ in heaven.
text: O fun a lɛli Yesu Kristo a li o sou ni di o zini a zu Koosa tɔhɔn ti. Domi o funɛ nyɛ ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ, o chi ya wa un piin nyɛn dooni.

ref: God did this so in the future world he could show how truly good and kind he is to us because of what Christ Jesus has done.
text: Koosa funɛ ya chen maga a nyɛ ya a biksi ba naa maga nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni awa ba naa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, chenani o funɛ daŋni ya kinkan a la gali a tum Yesu Kristo di o wa suŋi ya. O biksi nɔ nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: You were saved by faith in God, who treats us much better than we deserve. This is God's gift to you, and not anything you have done on your own.
text: Koosa funɛ suŋi ma ma se su un, chen ni o zi yisi mau a li ma tum tagna ni. Ma wa kikoti a ya chen a nyɛ ma titi ni. Chenani o tum a nyɛ ma ya o papaha ti.

ref: It isn't something you have earned, so there is nothing you can brag about.
text: Chendaha ma vɛ kpak nyinbaan a daŋni sɔn.

ref: God planned for us to do good things and to live as he has always wanted us to live. This is why he sent Christ to make us what we are. United by Christ
text: Koosa fun nyɛ ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgim, chen ni ya zi tum tum wereu a nyɛ un. O funɛ lisi yau saha digi di ya tum nan a nyɛ un. Ya Wa Kristo Ya Nibɛgimnɛ

ref: Don't forget that you are Gentiles. In fact, you used to be called “uncircumcised” by those who take pride in being circumcised.
text: Ba funɛ lul ma a li ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim nɛɛ. Ju tim ta yir ma ba naa ba vɛ teŋsi. Ba ka yir ba titi ba naa ba teŋsi. Ma lisi chenani ma fun nyɛn saha digi.

ref: At that time you did not know about Christ. You were foreigners to the people of Israel, and you had no part in the promises God had made to them. You were living in this world without hope and without God,
text: Chen saha ti ma wa Yesu Kristo wa fun ya nibɛgim. Ma funɛ ya hɔɔrawa a vɛ lagmi Ju tima ba naa Koosa fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni. Ba wa fun gur ma lagmi ba naa wa Koosa kpak nɔ bɛgim a li o vɔwɛ ta o fun kpa nyɛ ba chen. Chenani ma yaa nyɛn wuzu kam, ma wa fun zim Koosa a vɛ koti a kpak bambi bɛgim a li un ni.

ref: and you were far from God. But Christ offered his life's blood as a sacrifice and brought you near God.
text: Ma funɛ nyɛn bar boli nee. Yesu Kristo fun sou di o kpa ma a wa dugli.

ref: Christ has made peace between Jews and Gentiles, and he has united us by breaking down the wall of hatred that separated us. Christ gave his own body
text: Ju tima awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima wa kpak wu digi wa dɔŋ ni. O chi chechepure purige ba dɔŋ ni ba ya dɔma. Yesu Kristo funɛ sou ya daha, o chi o funɛ zak chechepure tire a lɔ a nyɛ Ju tima awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ya nɔ bɛgim.

ref: to destroy the Law of Moses with all its rules and commands. He even brought Jews and Gentiles together as though we were only one person, when he united us in peace.
text: O fun nyɛ chiksɛ naa Koosa fun nyɛ Ju tim ta ni chen peti, chen ni o zi lagmi Ju tim ta awa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima ba burgi lul bɛgim o wati. Ba wa zi bu ya dɔma.

ref: On the cross Christ did away with our hatred for each other. He also made peace between us and God by uniting Jews and Gentiles in one body.
text: Yesu Kristo funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni, o fun lagmi ba dɔŋ ni a nyɛ ba wa Koosa ya nɔ bɛgim. O fun kpa chenani ba fun te ha dɔŋa a yo ta.

ref: You Gentiles are no longer strangers and foreigners. You are citizens with everyone else who belongs to the family of God.
text: Ma naa vɛ ya Ju tima, wa bu ya hɔɔra. Ma wa Koosa nara lagmi dɔŋ ni a li Koosa walɛ ni.

ref: Christ is the one who holds the building together and makes it grow into a holy temple for the Lord.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa ya a gbaari Yesu Kristo ni a sa dɛ ti o wer Koosa wati o nyɛn.

ref: And you are part of that building Christ has built as a place for God's own Spirit to live.
text: Koosa siim weri ti nyɛ ma gbaari ba naa wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgim ni a burgi dɛ naa Koosa nyɛn chen bar digi.

ref: You have surely heard about God's gift of undeserved grace in choosing me to help you.
text: Ma wa fun zim di Koosa funɛ kpa o kina a naa la zi suŋi ma a du mu nin nee?

ref: In fact, this letter tells you a little about how God has shown me his mysterious ways.
text: Koosa funɛ te kpa o tutɔ ti a sogli a zi kpa un a li gbampulim a biksi min. Mu funɛ piin sɔbi nan wɛu tama a nyɛ ma.

ref: As you read the letter, you will also find out how well I really do understand the mystery about Christ.
text: Ma bukoo a karimi tɔn naa mu fun sɔbi a nyɛ ma chen, ma zi zim chenani mu zim Yesu Kristo wu sogle mun wɛu.

ref: And the mystery is this: Because of Christ Jesus, the good news has given the Gentiles a share in the promises God gave to the Jews. God has also let the Gentiles be part of the same body.
text: Ma nyɛ mu biksi ma a muna. Ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima nyɛ ba se su Koosa laaboor weri ti, ba wa Ju tim ta ya nɔ bɛgim a bɔni Koosa papaha naa o fun vɔu di o nyɛ Ju tim ta ni chen. Ba wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ a zi la kin naa maga Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ ba naa se su Yesu Kristo ni.

ref: God treated me with kindness. His power worked in me, and it became my job to spread the good news.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o kpok tiu a nyɛ min di mu burgi o tumtumna a biksi nara o laaboor weri ti wɛ. O funɛ suŋi minu kinkan hali a la gali. O funɛ ya o papaharɛ.

ref: I am the least important of all God's people. But God was kind and chose me to tell the Gentiles that because of Christ there are blessings that cannot be measured.
text: Min ya bichɛla a kaali o nara maga, amaa o funɛ lisi minnɛ di mu la biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima o laaboor weri ti wɛ. O laaboor weri ti biksi ya Yesu Kristo wu were damata naa vɛ kpak peti wɛ.

ref: God, who created everything, wanted me to help everyone understand the mysterious plan that had always been hidden in his mind.
text: Koosa gbanko funɛ lisi min di mu biksi nara maga nyinbaan naa Koosa tutɔ ti tiŋ. Waa funɛ naami kina maga, a kpa o tutɔ ti a sogli o piili ni,

ref: Then God would use the church to show the powers and authorities in the spiritual world that he has many different kinds of wisdom.
text: a kpa a li gbampulim lalaknɔ, chen ni Koosa nitiŋɛ ta zi biksi siim kpoka awa kpok a naa maga nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti niu chenani o zima ti tum a kurgi.

ref: God did this according to his eternal plan. And he was able to do what he had planned because of all Christ Jesus our Lord had done.
text: Koosa funɛ tiŋ o tutɔrɛ a li o piili ti ni a kpa du ya kunyana Yesu Kristo nin ni di o tum un.

ref: Christ now gives us courage and confidence, so we can come to God by faith.
text: Domi ya se su un ka ya wa un ya nibɛgim, ya kpak bambe a vɛ ɔmi di ya la Koosa suwachaku.

ref: This is why you should not be discouraged when I suffer for you. After all, it will bring honor to you. Christ's Love for Us
text: Mu namsi bar nɔu ma daha, amaa mu wara daŋni ma tusa chɔk chen daha. Ma ka nyɛ mu namsi nɔ nyɛ ma jirima a nyɛ ma kpak bambe. Chenani Kristo Daŋni Ya Hali A La Gali

ref: I kneel in prayer to the Father.
text: Kin nama daha muu sumi ya kunyan Koosa ma daha.
