ref: We believe Jesus died and was raised to life. We also believe that when God brings Jesus back again, he will bring with him all who had faith in Jesus before they died.
text: Ya se su di Yesu funɛ sou ka Koosa lɛli un a li o sou ni. Chendaha ya se su di Koosa zi wa lɛli ba naa se funɛ su unu a sou a chi o funɛ lɛli Yesu chen. Saha naa o wa wa, o wa ba zi wa bur wau.

ref: Our Lord Jesus told us that when he comes, we won't go up to meet him ahead of his followers who have already died.
text: Ya waa la biksi ma chenani ya kunyan ti fun a biksi ya. Wuzu digi ya kunyan ti zi wa bur wau. O wa wa, ya naa kpak miisa bɔnɛ wa zi wa te la a ka ba naa sowɛɛ.

ref: With a loud command and with the shout of the chief angel and a blast of God's trumpet, the Lord will return from heaven. Then those who had faith in Christ before they died will be raised to life.
text: Wuzu digi nara zi wa nu ya kunyan ti tau awa Koosa malaikasa nyuhu tiin ta wa o kaabul kuuli. Ba zi na ya kunyan ti titi ka o li Koosa tɔhɔn ti a tu wa tɛɛnɛ. O zi te a lɛli ba naa maga se funɛ su unu a li ba sou ni.

ref: Next, all of us who are still alive will be taken up into the clouds together with them to meet the Lord in the sky. From that time on we will all be with the Lord forever.
text: O zi vaan lagmi ya naa kaɛɛ a kpak miisa, chen ni ya maga zi siu a la zini dobaala ni a la pu ya kunyan ti dooni a nyɛn o wati hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Encourage each other with these words.
text: Ma ŋɔ laaboor weri nɔ a nyɛ dɔŋ ni aa kibelimi dɔŋa.

ref: I don't need to write you about the time or date when all this will happen.
text: Mu niinbisa, o wara daŋni mu bu sɔbi saha naa kin nama zi wa ya wɛ a nyɛ ma.

ref: You surely know that the Lord's return will be as a thief coming at night.
text: Ma titi yaa zim di a wuzu naa ya kunyan ti kaa wa bur wa, o waa chi chenani ŋmɛɛr yaa wa dusum chene.

ref: People will think they are safe and secure. But destruction will suddenly strike them like the pains of a woman about to give birth. And they won't escape.
text: A saha naa nara yaa sɛm di alaafɛ nyɛnu ka ba vɛ ɔm kundɔŋ, muksi kinkan ya vaan wa pu ba lalaga. Ba nidigi wa zi li muksi ti ni. O zi jɛri bau a chi nihaan naa kpak lo. O wa kaa lul, wiili ti yaa jɛri unu.

ref: My dear friends, you don't live in darkness, and so that day won't surprise you like a thief.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma zim a wuzu naa Yesu zi wa bur wau. Chendaha o wa zi jɛri ma a chi ŋmɛɛr.

ref: You belong to the light and live in the day. We don't live in the night or belong to the dark.
text: Ma maga chi fitin timwa aa tum gbampulim ni. Ya wa chi birsa tima a sogli aa tum.

ref: People sleep during the night, and some even get drunk.
text: Dusum nɛ nara nyɔ sin aa dɛɛsi a la chɔ do.

ref: But we belong to the day. So we must stay sober and let our faith and love be like a suit of armor. Our firm hope that we will be saved is our helmet.
text: Ya chi fitin tima, ma ta nyɛ ya dɛɛsi chen. Ma nyɛ ya se suuli a kidaŋni nara a kpak bambi bɛgim di Koosa zi yisi yau a li ya tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. Ya ya chen ni, sintana wa zi nyaŋi ya.

ref: Christ died for us, so we could live with him, whether we are alive or dead when he comes.
text: O funɛ sou ya daharɛ chen ni o wa bur wa, ka ya maga ha vɛɛn nyɛn akoo a siti, ya zi wa kpak miisau a nyɛn o wati.

ref: This is why you must encourage and help each other, just as you are already doing. Final Instructions and Greetings
text: Nan daha, ma kpami dɔŋa aa kisuŋi dɔŋa a chi chenani ma piinsa ya. Pɔɔl Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: My friends, we ask you to be thoughtful of your leaders who work hard and tell you how to live for the Lord.
text: Mu niinbisa, yaa sumi mau di ma nyɛ ba naa ya kunyan ti fun lisi di ba ya ma nihɛɛta chen, wa jirima. Ba waa la tum o tuma kinkan ma wati a la nyini ma.

ref: Show them great respect and love because of their work. Try to get along with each other.
text: Ma nyɛ ba jirima kinkan aa kidaŋni ba, domi tum naa baa tum nan daha. Ma la nɔɔni dɔŋ ni.

ref: My friends, we beg you to warn anyone who isn't living right. Encourage anyone who feels left out, help all who are weak, and be patient with everyone.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya ŋɔ mau wuti ni, ma ŋɔ nibin yarɛ ni a kikpami ba, a kisuŋi ba naa la ɔm wa ba kpak bambi, a kisuŋi ba naa se suuli gbarɛgɛɛ, aa kpak nara maga wa kanyita.

ref: Don't be hateful to people, just because they are hateful to you. Rather, be good to each other and to everyone else.
text: Ma ta tum tum tagin a bɔ tum tagin. Maa kpaŋi a tum tum were duŋduŋ aa nyɛ dɔŋ ni awa nara maga ni.

ref: Always be joyful
text: Ma kut kpak tu sɔɔna kinkan,

ref: and never stop praying.
text: a kisumi Koosa saha maga,

ref: Whatever happens, keep thanking God because of Jesus Christ. This is what God wants you to do.
text: aa pɛgi un a li kin naa maga ya ma ni. Chene Koosa daŋni ma tum aa kitiŋ Yesu Kristo.

ref: Don't turn away God's Spirit
text: Ma ta tɔ Koosa siim weri ti tuma.

ref: Put everything to the test. Accept what is good
text: Ma nyini ba sɛma maga a nɛɛ a weru. A were ma kpak a.

ref: and don't have anything to do with evil.
text: Koosa siim weri ti kpok vɛ nyɛn ba sɛm ta ni, ma vɛ a.

ref: I pray that God, who gives peace, will make you completely holy. And may your spirit, soul, and body be kept healthy and faultless until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
text: Koosa naa la nyɛ ma bara sɔɔna, zi nyɛ maa tum tum wereu a gbaari. O zi kok ma awa ma lisi wa ma siimau a li wu tagna ni, chen ni a zi weru hali saha naa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo wa bur wa.

ref: The one who chose you can be trusted, and he will do this.
text: O waa naa funɛ yir ma di ma wa o wati, ya wuti tiina. Chendaha o zi kok ma.

ref: Friends, please pray for us.
text: Mu niinbisa, maa sumi Koosa a nyɛ ya gbanko ni,

ref: Give the Lord's followers a warm greeting.
text: a kizaami ya niinbisa maga nɛnɛ.

ref: In the name of the Lord I beg you to read this letter to all his followers.
text: Mu kpak ya kunyan ti kpoku di mu ŋɔ ma ma karimi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa maga nyɛn dooni.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma.

ref: From Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Mu waa, Pɔɔl, awa Sailas wa Timoti zaami mau. Min, Pɔɔl, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ma naa tiŋ Yesu a li Tɛsalɔnika ni. Ya kunyan Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo dɔ ma.

ref: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! When Christ Returns
text: Ba suŋi ma a kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Ya Kunyana Wa Wa, O Zi Di Nara Sɛriau

ref: My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya yaa zaami Koosau ma daha saha maga. O weru di yaa zaami un chen, domi ma se suuli gbaariu kinkan ka maa daŋni dɔŋa.

ref: This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
text: Chendaha ya yaa ŋɔ Yesu nitiŋɛ doonani maga ya nyɛn di ma ya ya jirima narawa. Hali nara namsi ma kinkan, ma se ha vɛɛn su Koosau kinkan ka maa di ma namsi.

ref: All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering.
text: Koosa wa wa di o wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria wa wuti, o zi wa ŋɔ di ma wa zu o koor ti, domi namsi naa ma namsi nan daha.

ref: but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels
text: Koosa ya wuti tiina. Ya kunyan Yesu awa o malaikasa ba naa chi di a kpak kpok, wa li Koosa tɔhɔn ti a wa, o zi nyɛ ma namsi ti awa ya namsi petiu a kpa namsi ti a du ba naa la namsi ma chen ni.

ref: and with a flaming fire. Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message.
text: Ba naa vɛ zim Koosa awa ba naa se vɛ su o laaboor weri ti ya kunyan Yesu wɛ, Koosa zi wa lɛrgi ba diŋsau a ta ba yɔri.

ref: Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength.
text: Koosa wa yisi ba a li o bar nyɛni awa o jirima zen ti ni, ba zi wa namsiu a ta yɔri hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
text: A wuzu naa ya kunyan ti kaa wa bur wa, ba naa maga se su un zi nyɛ un jirimau kinkan. Ma gbanko zi wa nyɛn dooniu a ya chen, domi ma se funɛ su Koosa laaboor weri naa ya fun wa biksi ma chen.

ref: God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do.
text: Chendaha ya yaa sumi un ma daha. Waa funɛ lisi ma di ma la o mi fali ti. Yaa sumi unu di o nyɛ ma o kpok ti yaa tiŋ o mi fali ti nɛnɛ. Ya vɛɛn a sumi unu di o suŋi ma ma kitum tum were a naa maga ma daŋni ma tum hali a wa peti, domi ma se su un.

ref: Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
text: Ma tiŋ chen nɛnɛ, ya kunyan Yesu sɔn ti zi na jirimau ma daha, ka ma gbanko kaa na jirima, domi ma wa un ya nibɛgimnɛ. Ya Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma ma nyɛn chen.

ref: When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends,
text: Mu daŋni mu bu ŋɔ mau lalaknɔ saha naa ya kunyan Yesu wa bur wa ka ya wa un wa lagmi wɛ. Mu niinbisa, mu sumi mau di,

ref: not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters.
text: nara ŋɔ ma di ya kunyan ti piin bur wau, ma ta nyɛ o jɛri ma a li ma lise ni a ta nyɛ ba sɛma kimuksi ma. Nara nama ŋɔ ma di ba ya Koosa witinsiwa akoo ba karimɛ tɔnnɛ a ŋɔ di min sɔbɛɛ, ma ta nu ba sɛm ta.

ref: He will brag and oppose everything holy or sacred. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God.
text: Tum tagna tiin nɔ zi wa ŋɔu di kinkin naa nibinsa jɛɛmi ya yɔrirɛ, a ŋɔ di o ya nibin zenne a kaali ba maga. Hali o zi la zu Koosa bar jɛɛmi damau a kani a ŋɔ di o ya Koosarɛ.

ref: Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
text: Ma wa lisi saha naa mu fun nyɛn ma wati chenn? Mu funɛ ŋɔ ma a nama maga wɛu.

ref: You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come.
text: Lalaknɔ wu digi nyɛ a vɛ yaali, ka ma zim chen kin ti. Koosa wa lisi wu digi ti, tum tagna tiin ti zi wau.

ref: His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way.
text: Nibinsa piin sogli a vɛ Koosa. Chen kin ti tɔ ba nyinbaan di ba vɛ Koosa gbampulim ni chen, hali o saha wa tel o kaali.

ref: Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
text: Doone tum tagin tiin ti zi na nyinbaan a wa. Ya kunyan Yesu wa bur wa, o zi wa kpa o kpoku awa o jirima wa o hiisa a hiisa a du un niu o sou.

ref: When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs.
text: Tum tagin tiin ti wa wa, o zi kpa sintana kpoku a tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma kinkan,

ref: Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it.
text: di o mur nara ba naa Koosa zi wa na ba galimsa a ta ba yɔri. O zi wa na ba galimsa chenu, domi ba wara daŋni wuti ti, chen ni o fu zi kotiu a yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni ba tiŋ un.

ref: So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie.
text: Chendaha Koosa zi nyɛ sintana kpa o kpok tiu a tum tum tagna, chen ni ba se zi su kinkin naa ya nusunau.

ref: My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya yaa zaami Koosau ma naa o daŋni nan daha. Ma se funɛ su o wuti ti. Chendaha o fun te kpa o siim weri kpok ti a yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni a nyɛ o titi ni.

ref: God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Ya funɛ wa biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ, o fun a yir ma, chen ni wuzu digi ma zi wa bɔni ya kunyan Yesu Kristo jirima awa o chaaniu kinkan.

ref: My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
text: Mu niinbisa, chen daha, maa kpak nɛnɛ aa tiŋ wuti biksi naa ya fun biksi ma saha naa ya fun nyɛn ma wati awa chenani ya fun sɔbi tɔn ni a nyɛ ma.

ref: God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father
text: Ya kunyana Koosa la daŋni ya a fun suŋi ya, a kpami ya ya kpak bambi bɛgim un ni.

ref: will encourage you and help you always to do and say the right thing.
text: O wa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo la kpami ma di ma tum tum were a kisɛm wu were duŋduŋ.

ref: Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do.
text: Mu niinbisa, maa sumi Koosa a nyɛ ya di ya koti a mɔɔli Koosa laaboor weri ti bawu maga ni, chen ni nidamata se zi su unu a chi chenani maa ya chen.

ref: Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith.
text: Ma vɛɛn kisumi un di o yisi ya a li ba naa la tum tum tagna nisa ni, domi nara kinkan se wa su laaboor weri ti.

ref: But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm.
text: Amaa ya kunyan ti ya wuti tiina. O zi kpa o kpoku a nyɛ ma a yisi ma a li tum tagna naa sintana tum a du ma ni.

ref: He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying.
text: Ya kunyan ti nyɛ ya se su ma di ma zi tiŋ kin naa maga ya fun biksi mau a chi ma la tiŋ lalaknɔ nan.

ref: I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ. Warnings against Laziness
text: O suŋi ma ma daŋni Koosa nɛnɛ a kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ ma, chen ni ma zi kotiu a di ma namsi. Ma Nyɛ Ma Se Sɛri Ya Kunyan Tum Ni

ref: My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you.
text: Mu niinbisa, ya kunyan Yesu Kristo ŋɔ ya di ya ŋɔ ma kin nama wɛ. Ma ta nyɛ ma wa ba naa kpak zanyɛrka lagmi, domi ba wara tiŋ ya biksi ti.

ref: You surely know that you should follow our example. We didn't waste our time loafing,
text: Ma titi zimu nɛnɛ chenani ya nyɛni fun du ma watiu. Ma gbanko kinyɛn chen. Ya wa ma funɛ nyɛn, ya wa fun kpak zanyɛrka.

ref: and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
text: Ya ka funɛ tum nha wa dusumu kinkan, chen ni ya zi diu a li ya bara wilim ni. Ya wa fun a daŋni di ma suŋi ya wa diwe ni. Kin naa maga ma yaa fun a nyɛ ya, ya yaa bɔ a nyuuniu.

ref: We had the right not to work, but we wanted to set an example for you.
text: O funɛ deŋliu wa ya ŋɔ ma di ma suŋi ya wa diwe ni, amaa ya bara wilim nee yaa di a la biksi ma chenani ma zi tiŋ.

ref: We also gave you this rule: If you don't work, you don't eat.
text: Ya funɛ nyɛn ma wati, ya yaa fun a ŋɔ mau di nara vɛ daŋni tum tumi, o wa lawa ba di diwe.

ref: Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody.
text: Ya funɛ nu di ma bɔnɛ ya zanyɛrka timwa a la zu ba dɔŋtima wɛ ni. Chendaha ya sɔbi nan a nyɛ ma.

ref: So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living.
text: Ya ŋɔ bau ya kunyana Yesu Kristo sɔn daha ba nyɛ ba se sɛrɛ ka ba di ba titi bara wilim aa kitum ba tuma balimm.

ref: Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma ta wa ku wa tum were tumi ni.

ref: Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them.
text: Nandigi nyɛn dooni a vɛ daŋni o tiŋ chenani ya sɔbi tɔn nɔ nan, ma ta nyɛ o lagmi ma ni, chen ni o zi na hiisau.

ref: Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower. Final Prayer
text: Ma ta ya un a chi o ya ma dɔmnɛ, amaa ma kpaŋi a burgi o nyuha o tiŋ nyinbaan ti a chi ma niinbe. Pɔɔl Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
text: Ya kunyana naa ya bara sɔɔn tiina, waa la zi nyɛ ma bara sɔɔna saha maga, a nyɛn ma wati.

ref: I always sign my letters as I am now doing: PAUL.
text: Min, Pɔɔl, zaami mau. Mu titi la sɔbi sɛm na. Mu tɔna maga ni nannɛ mu yaa sɔbi mu sɔn.

ref: I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
text: Ya kunyana Yesu Kristo suŋi ma maga.

ref: From Paul. God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
text: Min waa, Pɔɔl, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ. Koosa naa funɛ yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni di yaa tiŋ un, awa Yesu Kristo naa nyɛ ya kpak bambi bɛgim un ni, ba funɛ ŋɔu di mu ya Yesu Kristo nitumi.

ref: Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace! Warning against False Teaching
text: Mu la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ Timoti naa si funɛ su Yesu wuti ni a li mu biksi ni. Ya kunyana Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kpak hin siki a kisuŋi hin a nyɛ hin bara sɔɔna. Doso Ba Naa La Biksi Chenani Vɛ Ya Wuti

ref: When I was leaving for Macedonia, I asked you to stay on in Ephesus and warn certain people there to stop spreading their false teachings.
text: Mu daŋni i kpalimi Ɛfɛsus nɛɛ a chi chenani mu fun ŋɔ hin saha naa mu wa hin fun nyɛn ka mu gali Masɛdonia pulum ni chen. Nara ba naa nyɛn Ɛfɛsus ni, bɔnɛ biksi chenani vɛ ya wutirɛ. La ŋɔ ba di ba gil chen biksi.

ref: You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
text: Ŋɔ ba di ba kpa ba mura awa ba nawaalsa sɔn biksi a ta, domi a wa nyɛ nara di dɔŋa amɔrɛ saha maga. Chenani baa tum wa nyɛ nara se su Koosa awa o wuti biksi ti.

ref: You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith.
text: Mu ŋɔ hin nannɛ, chen ni ya tusa zi chaaniu ka ya kpak tuha bɛgim wa Koosa ni ka ya se su un wuti ya daŋni nara maga.

ref: There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk.
text: Nibiksɛ nama funɛ bur wuti biksi ti niu a nyuksi ba nyinbaan. Saha maga baa di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ yɔri yɔri.

ref: They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
text: Ba kpak unu ba bambi ni a biksi nara Koosa chiksɛ wɛ, amaa ba wa fun zim sɛm naa ba biksi chen mun.

ref: We know the Law is good, if it is used in the right way.
text: Ya zimu di Koosa chiksɛ ta weru, amaa o lawa nara zim a wuti munu.

ref: We also understand it wasn't given to control people who please God, but to control lawbreakers, criminals, godless people, and sinners. It is for wicked and evil people, and for murderers, who would even kill their own parents.
text: Koosa wa fun nyɛ o chiksɛ ta nibin were daharɛ. O fun kpa a a nyɛ ba naa vɛ tiŋ a, a vɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔ a vɛ daŋni ba zim un a la tum tum tagna a kpu nara hali ba nyama awa ba numa,

ref: The Law was written for people who are sexual perverts or who live as homosexuals or are kidnappers or liars or won't tell the truth in court. It is for anything else that opposes the correct teaching
text: a la tum chɔkumsi tuma a daŋni ba wa ba dɔŋtima nibaala kichɔ, a kpaksi nara a la yogli a la sɛm nusun sɛma a la chɔk nara sɔna a la tum tum naa vɛ tiŋ wuti nyinbaan biksi ti.

ref: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me the strength for my work because he knew he could trust me.
text: Mu zaami ya kunyan Yesu Kristo, domi o funɛ lisi minu di mu ya o tumtumna a kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ min di muu tum o tuma.

ref: I used to say terrible and insulting things about him, and I was cruel. But Christ had mercy on me because I didn't know what I was doing, and I had not yet put my faith in him.
text: Saha digi mu fun zim un a la chɔk o sɔn a la pini o zoma a la muksi o nitiŋɛ ta kinkan. Mu si wa fun su un. Chendaha mu wa fun zim chenani mu tum. Amaa Koosa funɛ kpak min sikiu,

ref: Christ Jesus our Lord treated me with undeserved grace and has greatly blessed my life with faith and love just like his own.
text: a suŋi min hali o vɛ kpak bɔ di mu si su un, chen ni mu zi kotiu a daŋni nara. Ba naa maga wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgim, o suŋi ya nan.

ref: “Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners.” This saying is true, and it can be trusted. I was the worst sinner of all!
text: Chenani mu kaa ŋɔ nan ya wuti sɛma a naa ya maga zi la nɔɔni. Yesu Kristo funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga di o wa yisi nara a li ba tum tagna ni baa tiŋ Koosa. Min funɛ tum tum tagna a kaali nara maga.

ref: But since I was worse than anyone else, God had mercy on me and let me be an example of the endless patience of Christ Jesus. He did this so that others would put their faith in Christ and have eternal life.
text: Chendaha Koosa fun a kpak min siki ka muu tum tum tagna a kaali nara maga ka Yesu Kristo fun kpak kanyita kinkan min ni. Chenani ba fun ya a nyɛ min biksi ba naa la zi wa a nyɛ ba se su un, chenani ba zi wa la o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: I pray that honor and glory will always be given to the only God, who lives forever and is the invisible and eternal King! Amen.
text: Ma nyɛ ya yaa kipɛgi Koosa a kinyɛ un jirima kinkan. Waa ya ya koora naa nyɛnɛɛ hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, a ya nibɛgim duŋduŋ. Nandigi wa koti a na un. Ami.

ref: Timothy, my son, the instructions I am giving you are based on what some prophets once said about you. If you follow these instructions, you will fight like a good soldier.
text: Mu be, Timoti, mu kpa sɛm nama a du i nin nee. Saha digi nara bɔnɛ funɛ sɛm i wɛ a li Koosa kpok ni. Nyɛ ba sɛm ta kpami hin i tum i tuma nɛnɛ a nyɛ Koosa ni,

ref: You will be faithful and have a clear conscience. Some people have made a mess of their faith because they didn't listen to their consciences.
text: a kpak nɛnɛ aa tiŋ un wa tuha bɛgim ni. Bɔnɛ ka wa fun tiŋ wa tuha bɛgim a bur a chɔk ba se suuli kinkan.

ref: Two of them are Hymenaeus and Alexander. I have given these men over to the power of Satan, so they will learn not to oppose God.
text: Nara balɛ naa ba yir Haiminius awa Alɛzanda, nyɛn ba ni. Mu fun lisi ba a du sintana nin ni, chen ni ba zi buksiu a ta chɔk Koosa sɔn.

ref: This kind of prayer is good, and it pleases God our Savior.
text: Ya tum chen maga, o zi sɔɔni Koosa tuhau,

ref: God wants everyone to be saved and to know the whole truth, which is,
text: domi o daŋni o yisi nara magwa a li ba tum tagna ni baa tiŋ un a wa zim o wuti ti.

ref: There is only one God, and Christ Jesus is the only one who can bring us to God. Jesus was truly human, and he gave himself to rescue all of us.
text: Koosa ya nibɛgimnɛ. Yesu Kristo gbanko ya nibɛgimnɛ a fun wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a wa ya nibine, chen ni o zi la mo ninu Koosa wati a nyɛ ya.

ref: This is why God chose me to be a preacher and an apostle of the good news. I am telling the truth. I am not lying. God sent me to teach the Gentiles about faith and truth.
text: Chendaha o fun nyɛ mu ya o nitumi a tum min di mu la biksi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima di ba se su un ka ba wa zim wuti ti. Mu sɛma ya wutirɛ. Mu wa ya nusun tiina.

ref: I want everyone everywhere to lift innocent hands toward heaven and pray, without being angry or arguing with each other.
text: Chendaha mu daŋni nibaala ba naa maga tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ a nyɛn bawu maga ni ba sumi un. Ba ta na paan a kidi dɔŋ wa amɔrɛ.

ref: I would like for women to wear modest and sensible clothes. They should not have fancy hairdos, or wear expensive clothes, or put on jewelry made of gold or pearls.
text: Mu gbanko daŋni nihaana ba ta niŋi gata a chi sakaalsa a kiniŋi wa nɛha ni. Ba ta kpa malɛ damata a vɔti ba nyuksa a ta laari salma kina awa buŋkul were awa gar were.

ref: Women who claim to love God should do helpful things for others,
text: O ka bɔu di baa tum tum were domi baa tiŋ Koosarɛ.

ref: and they should learn by being quiet and paying attention.
text: O lawa nihaana ya jɛrgɛu a moori aa kibuksi.

ref: They should be silent and not be allowed to teach or to tell men what to do.
text: Mu wa la nɔɔni di nihaana kibiksi koo a dɔ nibaala. O lawa ba mooriu.

ref: After all, Adam was created before Eve,
text: Koosa funɛ te naami Adam awa Iif.

ref: and the man Adam wasn't the one who was fooled. It was the woman Eve who was completely fooled and sinned.
text: Sintana wa fun mur Adam, amaa nihaan tirɛ sintana fun mur o chɔk Koosa chiksɛ ta.

ref: It is true that anyone who desires to be a church official wants to be something worthwhile.
text: Mu sɛm wutirɛ. Nar kpak un o bambi ni di o ya Yesu nitiŋɛ nyuhu tiina, o daŋni kiwerere.

ref: That's why officials must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. They must be self-controlled, sensible, well-behaved, friendly to strangers, and able to teach.
text: O lawa o kpak sɔn weriu nara wati a kpak nihaan bɛgim duŋduŋ a kpak o titi nɛnɛ a ya bambi tiina a tiŋ nɛnɛ a la hɔɔra a du o dam ni a koti aa biksi nara.

ref: They must not be heavy drinkers or troublemakers. Instead, they must be kind and gentle and not love money.
text: O wa lawa o nyɔ sin a dɛɛsi a kpak nara wa tuha sa a kidaŋni o ya malɛ tiina. O lawa o ka ya jɛrgiu a kpak bara sɔɔna saha maga,

ref: Church officials must be in control of their own families, and they must see that their children are obedient and always respectful.
text: a koti a nyini o walɛ nɛnɛ a nyɛ o bisa tiŋ o nɔ aa nyɛ un jirima.

ref: If they don't know how to control their own families, how can they look after God's people?
text: Nar vɛ koti a nyini o walɛ nɛnɛ, yɛrɛ o kaa ya a koti a nyini ba naa tiŋ Koosa?

ref: They must not be new followers of the Lord. If they are, they might become proud and be doomed along with the devil.
text: O si su Koosa tama, o wa kiya Yesu nitiŋɛ nyuhu tiina. O ya nyuhu tiina lalaga, o zi zer o titiu ka Koosa zi na o galimsa a chi o funɛ na sintana galimsa chen.

ref: Finally, they must be well-respected by people who are not followers. Then they won't be trapped and disgraced by the devil. Church Officers
text: O lawa ba naa vɛ zim Koosa, kinyɛ un jirimau, chen ni sintana wa zi wa mo o lo o di hiisa. Ba Naa I Zi Lisi Ba Ya Yesu Nitiŋɛ Nisuŋɛ

ref: Church officers should be serious. They must not be liars, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money.
text: O lawa Yesu nitiŋɛ nisuŋɛ kpak nara jirimau a tum ba tuma a li ba bambe ni. Ba gbanko ta nyɔ sin a dɛɛsi a kidaŋni ba ya malɛ tima.

ref: And they must have a clear conscience and hold firmly to what God has shown us about our faith.
text: Amaa o lawa ba kpak wuti naa funɛ sogli ka Koosa kpa un li gbampulim chenu a kpak tu bɛgim wa Koosa ni.

ref: They must first prove themselves. Then if no one has anything against them, they can serve as officers.
text: O lawa Yesu nitiŋɛ ta te a zaamsi ba, chen ni ka ba di, ba zi ya ba nisuŋɛu.

ref: Women must also be serious. They must not gossip or be heavy drinkers, and they must be faithful in everything they do.
text: Nisuŋi naa ya nihaan, o lawa o kpak nara jirimau a kpak o titi nɛnɛ a ya wuti tiina a li kina maga ni. O ta lɔ nara gantɛl.

ref: Church officers must be faithful in marriage. They must be in full control of their children and everyone else in their home.
text: Nisuŋi lawa o kpak nihaan bɛgim duŋduŋnɛ a kinyini o bisa awa o walɛ nɛnɛ.

ref: Those who serve well as officers will earn a good reputation and will be highly respected for their faith in Christ Jesus. The Mystery of Our Religion
text: Ba naa la suŋi nɛnɛ, zi na jirimau a koti a kpak bambi aa ŋɔ nara ni chenani ya se su Yesu Kristo domi ya wa un ya nibɛgimnɛ. Yɛrɛ Yesu Kristo Zen Mak

ref: I hope to visit you soon. But I am writing these instructions,
text: Mu lisu di o wa zi vusi ka mu wa i wati.

ref: so if I am delayed, you will know how everyone who belongs to God's family ought to behave. After all, the church of the living God is the strong foundation of truth.
text: Mu vusɛɛ, tɔn nɔ zi biksi hinu chenani ya zi nyɛn wa dɔŋ niu. Ya naa la tiŋ Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ, ya waa ya o wuti niilɛ.

ref: Here is the great mystery of our religion: Christ came as a human. The Spirit proved that he pleased God, and he was seen by angels. Christ was preached to the nations. People in this world put their faith in him, and he was taken up to glory.
text: Wuti naa funɛ sogli ka Koosa lisi un a li gbampulim, yɛrɛ o zen mak. Yesu Kristo fun wa burgi nibine, ka Koosa siim weri ti fun biksi nara tima di chenani o ŋɔ o titi wɛ chen ya wutirɛ. Koosa malaikasa funɛ nyini un ka Koosa wa lɛli un a li o sou ni a zini o tɔhɔn ti. O nitiŋɛ ta fun a mɔɔli o laaboor weri ti bar damata ni ka nara se su un.

ref: God's Spirit clearly says that in the last days many people will turn from their faith. They will be fooled by evil spirits and by teachings that come from demons.
text: Koosa siim weri ti biksi nyɛn gbampulim ni di saha kurgɛ wau, nibin bɔnɛ zi wa buru a tiŋ Koosa. Ba ka zi wa nu alizin tagna biksiu a kitiŋ ba naa la mur ba nɔ.

ref: They will also be fooled by the false claims of liars whose consciences have lost all feeling. These liars
text: Biksi nɔ li nusun tima ba naa la daŋni ba mur nara nɛɛ. O bak ka wu bu to wu were koo wa tagna ni.

ref: will forbid people to marry or to eat certain foods. But God created these foods to be eaten with thankful hearts by his followers who know the truth.
text: Nara nama la biksi di nara kichi diwe bɔnɛ ka nibaala ta kpa haanasa. Amaa Koosa daŋni ba naa se su un a zim wuti ti di ba kpa diwe naa maga o fun naami chen a sumi un aa di.

ref: Everything God created is good. And if you give thanks, you may eat anything.
text: Kin nawara maga Koosa fun naami weru. O wara daŋni ya chi diwe bɔnɛ, amaa o daŋni ya kpa diwe magwa a sumi un aa kidi.

ref: What God has said and your prayer will make it fit to eat. Paul's Advice to Timothy
text: Ya ya chen, Koosa zi kpa o ninu a sak diwe ta ni ka ya di. Pɔɔl Daŋni Timotiu O Ya Yesu Tumtum Weri

ref: If you teach these things to other followers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show you have grown up on the teachings about our faith and on the good instructions you have obeyed.
text: I yaa lisi Koosa wuti biksi naa i fun la aa tiŋ, a kpa a biksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta, i ya Yesu Kristo tumtum werire.

ref: We have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of everyone, but especially of those who have faith. This is why we work and struggle so hard.
text: Chendaha yaa tum ya tuma kinkan a nyɛ Koosa ni, domi ya kpak bambi bɛgimu un ni. O nyɛnu a daŋni o yisi nara maga a li ba tum tagna ni baa tiŋ un. O piin ya chenu a nyɛ ya naa se su un ni.

ref: Teach these things and tell everyone to do what you say.
text: Kiŋɔ a kibiksi kin nama a nyɛ nara ni.

ref: Don't let anyone make fun of you, just because you are young. Set an example for other followers by what you say and do, as well as by your love, faith, and purity.
text: Nyɛ i sɛma awa i nyɛni wa chenani i daŋni nara wa chenani i si su wa i tum were, biksi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta chenani ba zi tiŋ Koosa. I ya chen, nandigi wa zi ŋɔ di i ya bichɛllɛ.

ref: Until I arrive, be sure to keep on reading the Scriptures in worship, and don't stop preaching and teaching.
text: Hali mu wa wa i wati, kpak si sɛm a karimi Koosa sɛm tɔn ti nara suwachak ni a kibiksi ba Koosa wɛ.

ref: Use the gift you were given when the prophets spoke and the group of church leaders blessed you by placing their hands on you.
text: Ta suglɛ papaha naa Koosa fun nyɛ hin saha naa o wa yir hin di i ya o tumtumna a nyɛ o witiina sɛm a li o kpok ni ka Yesu nitiŋɛ nihɛɛti ta kpa ba nisa a sak hin ni.

ref: Remember these things and think about them, so everyone can see how well you are doing.
text: Nyɛ kin nama nyɛn i bambi ni i tiŋ a, chen ni nara maga zi na chenani i tum tum were a gbaariu.

ref: Be careful about the way you live and about what you teach. Keep on doing this, and you will save not only yourself, but the people who hear you.
text: Kidoso i nyɛni ni awa chenani i biksi nara. I kpak kin nama nɛnɛ, wuzu digi Koosa zi wa yisi i wa ba naa la nu i nɔu a li ma tum tagna ni hali a wa peti.

ref: Don't correct an older man. Encourage him, as you would your own father. Treat younger men as you would your own brother,
text: Ta zulimi nihɛɛ, amaa sumi un a chi o ya i nyana. Kisɛm sɛma bunɔɔna wati a chi ba ya i niinbisa,

ref: and treat older women as you would your own mother. Show the same respect to younger women that you would to your sister.
text: awa nihaana a chi ba ya i niina, awa butoosa a chi ba ya i toosa. Kilisi chenani weree aa nyɛ ba.

ref: Take care of any widow who is really in need.
text: Kinyɛ luhaana ba naa vɛ kpak nara ba nyini ba, wa jirima.

ref: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should learn to serve God by taking care of her, as she once took care of them. This is what God wants them to do.
text: Amaa luhaan kpak niisisa akoo bisa, ba nyini un a bɔ chenani ba nyana awa ba niina fun ya nyɛ ba. Chene ba zi tiŋ Koosau ba dam ni a sɔɔni o tuha.

ref: A widow who is really in need is one who doesn't have any relatives. She has faith in God, and she keeps praying to him night and day, asking for his help.
text: Luhaan vɛ kpak nandigi o nyini un ka o kpak bambi bɛgim Koosa ni a la sumi un di o suŋi un nha awa dusum, o ya luhaan wutirɛ.

ref: A widow who thinks only about having a good time is already dead, even though she is still alive.
text: Amaa luhaan naa ka tiŋ o titi daŋni, o nyɛnu a chi o wa zim Koosa.

ref: Tell all this to everyone, so they will do the right thing.
text: Kibiksi kin nama a nyɛ i nara ni, chen ni nandigi wa zi koti a nyɛ ba sɔn tagin.

ref: People who don't take care of their relatives, and especially their own families, have given up their faith. They are worse than someone who doesn't have faith in the Lord.
text: Nandigi vɛ nyini o titi walɛ, o wa tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ. O tum tagna a kaali ba naa vɛ zim Koosa, domi ba waa koti a nyini ba walɛ.

ref: She must also be well-known for doing all sorts of good things, such as raising children, giving food to strangers, welcoming God's people into her home, helping people in need, and always making herself useful.
text: Luhaan bunsa kaali bun fumnɔra, ka o fun kpak baal bɛgim duŋduŋ a dɛɛsi o bisa nɛnɛ a la Koosa nara awa hɔɔra a du o dam ni a la suŋi ba naa nyɛn muksi ni a kpak un o bambi ni di o tum tum werewa, Yesu nitiŋɛ ta zi nyini unu.

ref: Don't put young widows on the list. They may later have a strong desire to get married. Then they will turn away from Christ
text: Luhaana vɛ tel bun fumnɔra, ta nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta kinyini ba. Ba zi daŋni ba yu baal falɛu, ba wa zi bu kpak un ba bambi ni di ba tum Kristo tuma.

ref: Besides, they will become lazy and get into the habit of going from house to house. Next, they will start gossiping and become busybodies, talking about things that are none of their business.
text: Luhaan butoosa gbanko zi kpak zanyɛrkau a gba nara damsa a zu ba wɛ ni aa lɔ nara gantɛl.

ref: I would prefer that young widows get married, have children, and look after their families. Then the enemy won't have any reason to say insulting things about us.
text: Mu daŋni luhaan butoosa kiyu baalasau a lul bisa a kinyini ba dama, chen ni ya dɔma wa zi na nyinbaan a chɔk ya sɔn.

ref: Look what's already happened to some of the young widows! They have turned away to follow Satan.
text: Luhaan butoosi ta bɔnɛ piin bur Koosa nyinbaan tiŋi niu di ba tiŋ sintana.

ref: If a woman who is a follower has any widows in her family, she should help them. This will keep the church from having that burden, and then the church can help widows who are really in need. Church Leaders
text: Nihaan naa tiŋ Kristo a kpak luhaana o walɛ ni, o lawa o nyini bau. Yesu nitiŋɛ ta ka nyini luhaana ba naa vɛ kpak nara.

ref: Church leaders who do their job well deserve to be paid twice as much, especially if they work hard at preaching and teaching.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ nihɛɛta ba naa la nyini ba nara nɛnɛ a kpak si sɛm aa biksi nara Koosa wɛ kinkan, o lawa ba na jirima bilɛu.

ref: It is just as the Scriptures say, “Don't muzzle an ox when you are using it to grind grain.” You also know the saying, “Workers are worth their pay.”
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ma ta vɔu nuha naa la sɔrgi niŋmɛna di o chɛri a, nɔ.” Ba fun bukoo sɔbi, “Tumtumna kila o bɔta.”

ref: Don't listen to any charge against a church leader, unless at least two or three people bring the same charges.
text: Nandigi daŋni o fiksi Yesu nitiŋɛ nihɛɛ, nyɛ o kpa nara balɛ koo nara batora ba wa a ya sɛhɛra tima, chen ni i zi nu ba sɛmau. O vɛ ya chen, ta nu o wati.

ref: But if any of the leaders should keep on sinning, they must be corrected in front of the whole group, as a warning to everyone else.
text: Ba naa la tum tum tagna, kpaŋi a burgi ba nyuuna nara suwachak ni, chen ni ba naa nuɛɛ zi ɔmu.

ref: In the presence of God and Christ Jesus and their chosen angels, I order you to follow my instructions! Be fair with everyone, and don't have any favorites.
text: Mu chiŋi Koosa awa Yesu Kristo awa o malaikasa naa o lisi suwachak ni, mu ŋɔ hinu di ii tiŋ mu biksi ŋɔ. Ta nyini digi a kaali digi.

ref: Don't be too quick to accept people into the service of the Lord by placing your hands on them. Don't sin because others do, but stay close to God.
text: Ta kpa i nisa a saksi nara ni lalaga di ba ya ya kunyan ti tumtumsa. Ta lagmi ba naa la tum tum tagna wati, amaa i kpak i titi wa tu chaan ni saha maga.

ref: Stop drinking only water. Take a little wine to help your stomach trouble and the other illnesses you often have.
text: Ta nyɔ ni duŋduŋ, amaa i yaa kinyɔ sin tama, domi i lo wi yɔri yɔri. O zi suŋi hinu.

ref: Some people get caught in their sins almost at once, even before the time of judgment. But other people's sins don't show up until later.
text: Bɔnɛ tum ba tum tagna gbampulim nɛɛ, chen ni ya zi zim a wɛu. Bɔnɛ ka tum ba tum tagna a sogli, chen ni ya wa zi zim a wɛ. Amaa wuzu digi Koosa zi wa di ba maga sɛriau.

ref: It is the same with good deeds. Some are easily seen, but none of them can be hidden.
text: Ya zim tum were naa nibinsa tumu wɛ, hali a naa sogli wɛ, domi a zi wa li gbampulimu.

ref: If you are a slave, you should respect and honor your owner. This will keep people from saying bad things about God and about our teaching.
text: O lawa yosa kinyɛ ba kunyama awa jirimau, chen ni nandigi wa zi koti a chɔk Koosa sɔn a kisɛm di ya biksi ti ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: If any of you slaves have owners who are followers, you should show them respect. After all, they are also followers of Christ, and he loves them. So you should serve and help them the best you can. False Teaching and True Wealth These are the things you must teach and tell the people to do.
text: Ba kunyam ta ya Yesu nitiŋɛ, ba ta vɛ ba jirima nyɛɛli, domi ba ya ba dɔŋtima daha. Ba tum tuma kinkan a gbaari, domi ba dɔŋtim ta ba daŋni chen la zi na nyɔɔra a li ba tum ta ni. Kibiksi kin nama a nyɛ i nara ni. Nusun Nibiksɛ Daŋni Ba Na Nyɔɔrrɛ A Li Ba Biksi Ni

ref: Anyone who teaches something different disagrees with the correct and godly teaching of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Ba naa maga vɛ la nɔɔni ya kunyana Yesu Kristo wuti sɛma a naa nyɛ ya tiŋ un nɛnɛ, amaa a kpak ba titi biksi a nyɛ nara ni,

ref: Those people who disagree are proud of themselves, but they don't really know a thing. Their minds are sick, and they like to argue over words. They cause jealousy, disagreements, unkind words, evil suspicions,
text: ba zer ba titi a vɛ zim kundɔŋ. Saha maga ba yaa daŋni ba di amɔrɛrɛ kinkan a kidaŋni tɛl sɛma, chen ni ba naa la nu ba sɛm ta ya handɔta timawa a waligi dɔŋ ni a la chɔk dɔŋ sɔna a ya beeli tima.

ref: and nasty quarrels. They have wicked minds and have missed out on the truth. These people think religion is supposed to make you rich.
text: Nibiksɛ nama ya ba naa vɛ zim wuti ti, ka ba lisi lisi tagna saha maga. Ba yaa daŋni bara borare a du dɔŋ ni, a la lisi di ba naa tiŋ ba biksi ti chen zi wa ya malɛ timau.

ref: And religion does make your life rich, by making you content with what you have.
text: O ya wutirɛ di ba naa tiŋ Koosa wuti biksi ti zi na nyɔɔrau kinkan, amaa ba kpak tu sɔɔna awa kin naa ba kpak chen ni.

ref: We didn't bring anything into this world, and we won't take anything with us when we leave.
text: Ba funɛ lul ya, ya wa fun koti a kpa kundɔŋ a wa. Ya wa sou, ya wa zi vɛɛn koti a kpa kundɔŋ aa la.

ref: So we should be satisfied just to have food and clothes.
text: Ya kpak diwe a di a kpak kilaarɛ a laari, chen la zi puŋ ya.

ref: People who want to be rich fall into all sorts of temptations and traps. They are caught by foolish and harmful desires that drag them down and destroy them.
text: Amaa ba naa la daŋni ba ya malɛ tima, sintana zi wa zaamsi bau a mo ba lo a li ba womsa awa ba tum tagna daŋni ni, chen ni ba zi wa chɔku kinkan.

ref: The love of money causes all kinds of trouble. Some people want money so much they have given up their faith and caused themselves a lot of pain. Fighting a Good Fight for the Faith
text: Domi tum tagna damata li malɛ daŋni nɛɛ. Bɔnɛ kpak a ba bambe nee kinkan di ba na malɛ a la bur Koosa nyinbaan ni. Chen nyɛ ba tusa chɔk kinkan. O Lawa Timoti Tiŋ Pɔɔl Biksiu

ref: Timothy, you belong to God, so keep away from all these evil things. Try your best to please God and to be like him. Be faithful, loving, dependable, and gentle.
text: Amaa i waa naa la tiŋ Koosa, ta tum tum tagna chen ni. Nyɛ i si sɛri wa tum were tumi ni ka i tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ. Nyɛ i si su un kinkan a ya jɛrgi a kidi i namsi a kidaŋni nara maga.

ref: Fight a good fight for the faith and claim eternal life. God offered it to you when you clearly told about your faith, while so many people listened.
text: I funɛ gil i tum tagna a chiŋi Koosa sɛhɛra tima nidamata suwachak ni, o fun kpa o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ hin. Kpak un nɛnɛ. Nyɛ i si su un kinkan saha maga, chen ni i zi tiŋ unu nɛnɛ.

ref: Now I ask you to make a promise. Make it in the presence of God, who gives life to all, and in the presence of Christ Jesus, who openly told Pontius Pilate about his faith.
text: Mu chiŋi Koosa naa funɛ naami kina maga, awa Yesu Kristo naa funɛ sɛm a kok o titi nɛnɛ a nyɛ Pɔntius Pailɔt ni a li ba suwachak ni, mu ŋɔ hinu di,

ref: Promise to obey completely and fully all that you have been told until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
text: ii tiŋ chenani mu fun biksi hin. Nyɛ i tuha kichaani, chen ni nandigi wa zi koti a chɔk i sɔn, hali ya kunyan Yesu Kristo wa bur wa.

ref: The glorious God is the only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords. At the time that God has already decided, he will send Jesus Christ back again.
text: Koosa naa zenee a dɔ kina maga a ya koorsa maga kaŋkoora, waa la zi tum un saha naa o daŋni.

ref: Only God lives forever! And he lives in light that no one can come near. No human has ever seen God or ever can see him. God will be honored, and his power will last forever. Amen.
text: Koosa duŋduŋ nyɛnɛɛ saha maga a li chaani naa nandigi zi vɛ koti a zuuli. Nandigi ha wa na un koo zi koti a na un. O kpok zen awa o jirima zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Warn the rich people of this world not to be proud or to trust in wealth that is easily lost. Tell them to have faith in God, who is rich and blesses us with everything we need to enjoy life.
text: Kibiksi ba naa kpak kidamata di ba ta daŋni sɔn a daha. Ba ta kpak bambi bɛgim a li ba kin naa la zi wa chɔk ni, amaa ba ka kpak bambi bɛgim Koosa naa la nyɛ ya kina maga di ya tusa sɔɔni ni.

ref: Instruct them to do as many good deeds as they can and to help everyone. Remind the rich to be generous and share what they have.
text: Kibiksi ba baa tum tum were nɛnɛ a kidaŋni ba bɔni ba kina a nyɛ ba dɔŋtima ni.

ref: This will lay a solid foundation for the future, so they will know what true life is like.
text: Ba ya chen, ba zi kotiu a chiŋsi ba kina saha naa la wa nan a koti a kpak Koosa wuti miisa naa o zi kpa nyɛ ba.

ref: Timothy, guard what God has placed in your care! Don't pay any attention to godless and stupid talk that sounds smart but really isn't.
text: Timoti, kiil kin tima mu fun kpa a du i nin ni chen. Ta lagmi ba naa la sɛm di ba biksi nara chenani ba zi wa zim Koosa. Ba biksi ti ya yɔrirɛ. Saha maga nara nama di ba titi womsi amɔrɛrɛ a la sɛm sɛm tagna.

ref: Some people have even lost their faith by believing this talk. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: Bɔnɛ funɛ tiŋ ba a bur a tiŋ Koosa wuti nyinbaan. O suŋi ma maga.

ref: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus. God himself chose me to be an apostle, and he gave me the promised life that Jesus Christ makes possible.
text: Mu waa, Pɔɔl, naa Koosa fun lisi di mu ya Yesu Kristo nitumi a tum di mu la ŋɔ nara ni o miisa naa o piin kpa nyɛ ya wɛ, domi ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: Timothy, you are like a dear child to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Christ Jesus will be kind and merciful to you and will bless you with peace! Do Not Be Ashamed of the Lord
text: Mu la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ mu be daŋni Timoti naa si funɛ su Yesu a li mu biksi ni. Ya kunyana Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo suŋi hin a kpak hin siki aa nyɛ hin bara sɔɔna. Pɔɔl Fun Zaami Koosa Timoti Daha

ref: Night and day I mention you in my prayers. I am always grateful for you, as I pray to the God my ancestors and I have served with a clear conscience.
text: Min la tum Koosa tuma awa tuha bɛgim ni a chi chenani mu nawaalsa fun tum chen. Mu yaa sumi Koosa a la zaami un i daha nha awa dusum.

ref: I remember how you cried, and I want to see you, because this would make me truly happy.
text: Mu lisu chenani i fun li mu watiu ka i tuha chɔku kinkan. Mu daŋni mu bu na hinu kinkan, chen ni i zi nyɛ mu tuha sɔɔniu hali a la gali.

ref: I also remember the genuine faith of your mother Eunice. Your grandmother Lois had the same sort of faith, and I am sure you have it as well.
text: Mu lisu di i niina, Yunis, awa i naha, Lois se su Yesu wuti ni. Mu zimu di i gbanko si su unu a chi ba.

ref: So I ask you to make full use of the gift God gave you when I placed my hands on you. Use it well.
text: Chendaha mu daŋni i lisi saha naa mu fun wa kpa mu nisa a sak hin ni, ka Koosa kpa o papaha a nyɛ hin chen. Ta tɔ un, amaa kitum un nɛnɛ.

ref: Don't be ashamed to speak for our Lord. And don't be ashamed of me, just because I am in jail for serving him. Use the power that comes from God and join with me in suffering for telling the good news.
text: Ta nyɛ hiisa kpak hin a li chenani i ŋɔ nara ni ya kunyan ti wɛ. Ta nyɛ hiisa gbanko kpak hin a li chenani ba tɔ min dɛ Kristo wɛ daha. Kpa kpok naa Koosa nyɛ hin aa saksi a na namsi o laaboor weri ti daha.

ref: God saved us and chose us to be his holy people. We did nothing to deserve this, but God planned it because he is so kind. Even before time began God planned for Christ Jesus to show kindness to us.
text: Koosa funɛ yir ya di ya ya o titi nara a fun yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni yaa tiŋ un. O wa fun ya chen di ya ya nibin werewa daha. Ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha o funɛ ya Koosa tutɔrɛ a li o piili ni o suŋi ya chen.

ref: Now Christ Jesus has come to offer us God's gift of undeserved grace. Christ our Savior defeated death and brought us the good news. It shines like a light and offers life that never ends.
text: Doone o fun tum o be Yesu Kristo di o wa lisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni yaa tiŋ un. Nan biksi ya di o ya Koosa tutɔrɛ o suŋi ya. Kristo funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni, o fun a nyaŋi sou. Waa funɛ kpa Koosa laaboor weri naa funɛ sogli chen a biksi ya o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ, wɛ.

ref: My work is to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher.
text: Koosa fun lisi min di mu ya o nitumi a la biksi nara o laaboor weri ti wɛ.

ref: This is why I am suffering now. But I am not ashamed! I know the one I have faith in, and I am sure he can guard until the last day what he has trusted me with.
text: Chendaha ba fun kpak min a la tɔ dɛ ka mu namsi nan. Amaa mu ha vɛɛn kpak bambi kpoku, domi mu zim naa mu si suu. Mu zimu di o zi il o kin nawara o fun kpa a du mu nin ni hali saha naa o wa bur wa.

ref: Now follow the example of the correct teaching I gave you, and let the faith and love of Christ Jesus be your model.
text: I funɛ nyɛn mu wati, mu fun a biksi hin Koosa wuti wɛ di ii tiŋ un. Lalaknɔ kpak mu wuti biksi nɔ nɛnɛ. Nyɛ i si yaa su Yesu Kristo aa kidaŋni nara nɛnɛ, domi o wa ya ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: You have been trusted with a wonderful treasure. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives within us.
text: Koosa siim weri naa nyɛn ya ni nan, zi kpa o kpoku a nyɛ hin di i kpak Koosa papaha naa o fun kpa a du i nin ni chen nɛnɛ.

ref: I pray that the Lord will be kind to the family of Onesiphorus. He often cheered me up and wasn't ashamed of me when I was put in jail.
text: Ɔnɛsifɔrɔs funɛ kpami minu bu damata. Chendaha mu sumi ya kunyan ti di o kpak o walɛ siki. O wa kpak hiisa a du min ni domi ba funɛ kpak min a tɔ dɛrɛ daha.

ref: Then after he arrived in Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me.
text: O funɛ wa tel Room, o fun vaan piili a dɛu mu bar nyɛni hali a wa na min.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus will ask God to show mercy to Onesiphorus on the day of judgment. You know how much he helped me in Ephesus.
text: I gbanko zimu nɛnɛ a li chenani o fun suŋi min kinkan a li Ɛfɛsus ni. Ya kunyan ti wa bur wa, o zi kpak un sikiu kinkan.

ref: Timothy, my child, you must let Christ Jesus make you strong by his gift of undeserved grace.
text: Mu be, ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha Koosa la zi suŋi hin i kpak kpok.

ref: You have often heard me teach. Now I want you to tell these same things to followers who can be trusted to tell others.
text: I funɛ nu ka muu biksi nara bu damata. I gbanko tiŋ mu nɔ sɛma aa kibiksi ba naa ya wuti tima ba zi kotiu a la biksi ba dɔŋtima.

ref: As a good soldier of Christ Jesus you must endure your share of suffering.
text: I chi Yesu Kristo lanjimarɛ. Adaha i saksi a na namsi o daha.

ref: Soldiers on duty don't work at outside jobs. They try only to please their commanding officer.
text: Lanjima la laal ni, o yaa gil o kina magau a ta gantɛl ni. O yaa daŋni o tuma sɔɔni o nyuhu tiina tuhau.

ref: No one wins an athletic contest without obeying the rules.
text: Nar awa o dɔŋtima chɔ chɔchɔha a daŋni o di, o lawa o tiŋ ba nihɛɛti nɔu.

ref: And farmers who work hard are the first to eat what grows in their field.
text: Niparɛ naa la tum kinkan a li o ŋmaha ni, o zi na kiparɛ naa ba te kum chenu.

ref: If you keep in mind what I have told you, the Lord will help you understand completely.
text: Kilisi chenani mu sɛm hin nan. Koosa zi suŋi hinu ii zim mu sɛm ta muna.

ref: Keep your mind on Jesus Christ! He was from the family of David and was raised from death, just as my good news says.
text: Kilisi Yesu Kristo naa funɛ ya koor Devid lul be, a si a li o sou ni. Mu yaa ŋɔ nara niu Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ, o wɛwa mu yaa ŋɔ ba.

ref: And because of this message, I am locked up in jail and treated like a criminal. But God's good news isn't locked in jail,
text: Koosa laaboor weri ti daha ba fun kpak min a la tɔ dɛ di mu namsi a chi tum tagna nitumi. Amaa ba wa koti a kpa o laaboor weri ti a tɔ dɛ.

ref: and so I am willing to put up with anything. Then God's special people will be saved and given eternal glory because they belong to Christ Jesus.
text: Chendaha mu di mu namsi maga, chen ni Koosa zi wa yisi ba naa o fun lisiu a li ba tum tagna a kpa o jirima zen naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ ba. Ya wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha Koosa koti a ya chen.

ref: Here is a true message: “If we died with Christ, we will live with him.
text: Ba funɛ sɛm wutirɛ, “Ya wa un siwɛɛ, wuzu digi ya wa un zi nyɛnu.

ref: If we don't give up, we will rule with him. If we deny that we know him, he will deny that he knows us.
text: Ya di ya namsi maga, wuzu digi ya wa un zi wa ya koorsau. Ya vɛ un, o gbanko zi vɛ yau.

ref: If we are not faithful, he will still be faithful. Christ cannot deny who he is.” An Approved Worker
text: Ya vɛ ya wuti tima, o waa ya wuti tiinnɛ saha maga. O wa koti a vɛ o titi.” Kpaŋi I Titi A Kitum Koosa Tuma Nɛnɛ.

ref: Don't let anyone forget these things. And with God as your witness, you must warn them not to argue about words. These arguments don't help anyone. In fact, they ruin everyone who listens to them.
text: Kibiksi kin nama a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ta ni aa kitɛ ba nin Koosa suwachak ni di ba ta di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ yɔri yɔri. Ba naa la di amɔrɛ chen ni, ba sɛma zi nyɛ ba naa la nu a chen bur Koosa tiŋi niu.

ref: Do your best to win God's approval as a worker who doesn't need to be ashamed and who teaches only the true message.
text: Kpak un i bambi ni aa kitum Koosa tuma nɛnɛ, chen ni o wa zaamsi hin a peti, o zi ŋɔ di i kpaŋi i titiu. I biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ nara ni wa wuti ni, i wa zi wa na hiisa.

ref: That sort of talk is like a sore that won't heal. And Hymenaeus and Philetus have been talking this way
text: Chenani ba yaa biksi nara chi chɔ naa la di nibine barawa. Nara balɛ naa ba yir Haiminius awa Failitus, nyɛn ba ni.

ref: by teaching that the dead have already been raised to life. This is far from the truth, and it is destroying the faith of some people.
text: Nara balɛ na funɛ gil Koosa wuti nyinbaanu a nyɛ ba naa se su Kristo bɔnɛ bur o tiŋi ni, domi ba biksiu di ya piin siu a li ya sou ni.

ref: In a large house some dishes are made of gold or silver, while others are made of wood or clay. Some of these are special, and others are not.
text: Kidamata nyɛn sal zen ni. Ba kpa aniita awa salmasiwa a kɔɔri bɔnɛ a kpa daasa awa choha a kɔɔri bɔnɛ. Ba kpa aniita awa salma kin tau a chiŋsi a pɔ wuzu zene wuzu. Ba ka kpa daasa awa choha kin tau a tum tuma wuzu kam.

ref: This is also how it is with people. The ones who stop doing evil and make themselves pure will become special. Their lives will be holy and pleasing to their Master, and they will be able to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: Ba naa maga gil ba tum tagna, ba chi aniita awa salma kin nawara ba kpa chiŋsi a pɔ wuzu zene chene. Ba saksi ba titiu di ba tum tum were a nyɛ un.

ref: Run from temptations that capture young people. Always do the right thing. Be faithful, loving, and easy to get along with. Worship with people whose hearts are pure.
text: Ta tiŋ i titi daŋni a chi ba naa ya bunɔɔna, amaa kpak un i bambi ni a tum tum were kinkan a nyɛ i si su Yesu aa kidaŋni nara awa bara sɔɔni ni. Kilagmi ba naa kpak bambi chaanɛ a la sumi Koosa di o suŋi ba.

ref: and God's servants must not be troublemakers. They must be kind to everyone, and they must be good teachers and very patient.
text: Ya kunyan ti tumtumna fiɛŋ o daŋni yo. O lawa o ya jɛrgiu a koti a biksi nara nɛnɛ awa kanyita ni.

ref: Be humble when you correct people who oppose you. Maybe God will lead them to turn to him and learn the truth.
text: Saha naa o wa burgi womna nibiksɛ na nyuuna, o ta na paan a du ba ni. Ami Koosa zi nyɛ ba nyinbaanu di ba gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ un a wa zim o wuti ti.

ref: They have been trapped by the devil, and he makes them obey him, but God may help them escape.
text: Ba ya chen, ba zi bur wa ba nɛsa niu a koti a chɔ a li sintana naa funɛ kpak ba a mo ba lo di ba tiŋ o nɔ ni.

ref: You can be certain that in the last days there will be some very hard times.
text: Kinu mu sɛma. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga saha wa dugli o peti, o zi wa bɔmu kinkan nara wati.

ref: People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud, stuck-up, rude, and disobedient to their parents. They will also be ungrateful, godless,
text: Nara zi wa nyɛn dooniu a kpak si a kidaŋni malɛ a zer ba titi aa ya diŋ vɛ nuulisi tima. Ba zi wa pini nara zomau a kivɛ ba nyama awa ba numa nɔɔna a ta zaami nara aa vɛ tiŋ Koosa.

ref: heartless, and hateful. Their words will be cruel, and they will have no self-control or pity. These people will hate everything good.
text: Ba zi vɛ ba dɔŋtimau a ya bambi kpok tima a ya tuha sa tima a chɔk ba sɔna a kitum chi garaŋzur tuma aa du ba ni. Ba zi ha tum wereu,

ref: They will be sneaky, reckless, and puffed up with pride. Instead of loving God, they will love pleasure.
text: a li nara gantɛl a ya wu zene tima aa lisi di ba ya nibin zenewa. Ba zi ya kidaŋni ba titi daŋnɛu a kaali chenani ba fu zi daŋni Koosa.

ref: Even though they will make a show of being religious, their religion won't be real. Don't have anything to do with such people.
text: Ba zi jɛɛmi Koosa wa ba nyina niu aa vɛ kpak o kpok ti. Ta lagmi ba naa la tum chen ni.

ref: Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires.
text: Ba bɔnɛ la zu damsa a mur nihaana ba naa vɛ kpak nɛha. Ba banchisa la yaali ba tum tagna daha. Hali ba titi daŋnɛ la lɛrgi ba di ba ya chen.

ref: These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth.
text: Nihaan nama yaa kpaŋi di ba buksi wu digi, amaa ba ka wa fun koti a zim un nɛnɛ.

ref: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real.
text: Nibiksɛ nama burgi ba gantɛlwa a chak Koosa wuti ti a chi nara balɛ naa ba funɛ yir Janis awa Jambrɛs, burgi ba gantɛlwa a chak Mosis saha digi. Nibiksɛ nama ya lise tagin timwa a nyuksi Koosa nyinbaan.

ref: But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out. Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
text: Ba wa zi wa na nyɔɔra kinkan a li ba tum ni. Nara maga zi wa zimu di nibiksɛ na gbanko ya womniwa a chi ba funɛ zim di Janis awa Jambrɛs ya womniwa saha digi. Ta Bur Gantɛl A Li Koosa Wuti Nyinbaan Ni

ref: Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with
text: Amaa i chau chenani muu biksi nara nɛɛ awa chenani mu nyɛni du wa chenani mu kpak un a li mu bambi ni di muu tiŋ Koosa. I gbanko zimu di mu si yaa su Koosau ka mu daŋni mu dɔŋtima awa kanyita ni wa chenani mu di mu namsi.

ref: trouble and suffering in the cities of Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. Yet the Lord rescued me from all those terrible troubles.
text: I gban zimu chenani mu dɔma muksi minu kinkan a li tɔsi a naa ba yir Antiɔk, Ikonium awa Listira ni, ka mu di namsi dooni. Amaa Koosa funɛ bu wa lisi minu a li mu namsi maga ni.

ref: In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others.
text: Nibinsa zi yaa wa muksi ba naa la daŋni ba tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ, domi ba wa Yesu Kristo ya nibɛgim.

ref: But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
text: Amaa nimurɛ ba naa la tum tum tagna zi wa tumu aa gbaari. Ba wa la mur nara awa ba titi.

ref: Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things.
text: Amaa ii ka tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan wuti ti. I zimu nɛnɛ ba naa funɛ te biksi hin o wɛ chen, di i si su un kinkan.

ref: Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved.
text: I gban funɛ zim Koosa sɛm tɔn tiu a li i bichɛsi ni. Waa funɛ koti a nyɛ i si su Yesu Kristo, chen ni o zi yisi hinu a li i tum tagna ni.

ref: The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
text: chen ni o tumtumsa zi saksiu di ba tum tum were maga.

ref: When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you
text: Mu chiŋe Koosa awa Yesu Kristo naa la zi wa di miisa tima awa nisitɛ sɛria a di o koor ti suwachak ni,

ref: to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient.
text: Mu ŋɔ hinu di o lawa i biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ aa nyɛ nara ba naa la nu awa ba naa vɛ nuuli ni. I yaa la burgi ba naa la tum tum tagna nyuuna, aa kikpami ba a bur tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan. Saha maga ii biksi ba wa kanyita kinkan ni.

ref: The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear.
text: Saha wau nibinsa zi vɛ wa nu Koosa wuti biksi ti, amaa ba zi wa tiŋ ba titi daŋnɛu a lagmi ba naa la zi wa biksi ba chenani ba daŋni aa gbaari.

ref: They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories.
text: Ba zi wa buru a li Koosa wuti biksi ti ni, a kidaŋni baa nu mura.

ref: But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
text: Amaa ii kpak o wuti ti nɛnɛ saha maga. I di namsi aa kibiksi Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ a nyɛ nara ni aa kitum i tuma nɛnɛ a nyɛ un.

ref: I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful.
text: Mu miisa chi chɔchɔharɛ. Mu kpaŋu a chɔ peti ka mu si ha vɛɛn su Koosa.

ref: So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power. Personal Instructions
text: Ya kunyan ti wa bur wa di o di ya sɛria wa wuti, o zi bɔ min mu chimu, domi mu tum tum werewa a nyɛ un. O zi wa ya chenu a nyɛ ba naa maga la daŋni un a la pɔ un di o bur wa. Ta Vusi A Wa Kpami Min

ref: Come to see me as soon as you can.
text: Kpaŋi i titi a bur wa mu wati lalaga.

ref: Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
text: Dimas naa la daŋni tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina, funɛ gil minu a kaali Tɛsalɔnika. Krɛsɛns funɛ a gbau a kaali Galesia pulum. Taitus ka kaali Dalmesia pulum.

ref: When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus. Don't forget to bring the scrolls, especially the ones made of leather.
text: I wa wa, i zer mu kuntu naa mu kpa ta Karpus wati a li Troas ni a kpa un kuwa, a pa mu tɔna gbanko. Doso a naa ya sela tɔna chen ni.

ref: Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
text: Machɛrɛ naa ba yir Alɛzanda chen funɛ muksi min kinkan. Ya kunyan ti zi wa di o tum tagna sɛriau.

ref: Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
text: Doso kinyini un, domi ya funɛ nyɛn dooni, o yaa fun vɛ ya biksiu wa kpok ni.

ref: When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them.
text: Mu funɛ kok mu titi a li sɛria bar diili dɛ ti ni, nandigi wa fun chiŋi mu gantɛl ni. Mu si su di Koosa wa zi wa lisi chenani ba fun gil min.

ref: But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions.
text: Amaa ya kunyan ti funɛ nyɛn mu watiu a kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ min, a fun nyɛ mu koti a sɛm Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ a nyɛ ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim ni a lisi min a li bar tagin kinkan ni.

ref: The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: O gbanko zi lisi minu a li tum tagin naa maga nibinsa zi wa tum a du min ni a nyɛ mu zu o koor naa nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni. O la jirima kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami. Pɔɔl Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
text: Mu zaami Akwila awa Prisila. Mu gbanko zaami Ɔnɛsifɔrɔs walɛ.

ref: Erastus stayed at Corinth. Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
text: Ɛrastus fun kpalimi Kɔrint ni ka mu fun gil Trofimus a li Mailitus ni, domi o wa fun kpak alaafɛ.

ref: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
text: Kpaŋi i titi a wa ka wa saha wa tel. Yubulus, Pudɛns, Linus, ya towe Klɔdia, awa ya dɔŋtima ba naa maga nyɛn bar nɔ, zaami hinu.

ref: I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
text: Ya kunyan ti zi nyɛn i watiu. Koosa kisuŋi ma maga.

ref: From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I encourage God's own people to have more faith and to understand the truth about religion.
text: Mu waa, Pɔɔl, naa ya Koosa tumtumna awa Yesu Kristo nitumi, min la sɔbi tɔn nɔ. Koosa funɛ lisi min a tum di mu wa kpami ba naa Koosa fun lisi di ba se su un a wa zim o wuti, chen ni ba zi tiŋ unu nɛnɛ.

ref: Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! What Titus Was To Do in Crete
text: Min la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ mu be Taitus naa si funɛ su Koosa wuti ti a li mu biksi ni. I wa min la tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan. Ya kunyan Koosa awa Yesu Kristo naa funɛ yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni, zi suŋi hinu a nyɛ hin bara sɔɔna. Chenani Taitus Zi Tum A Li Krit Pulum Ni

ref: I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you,
text: Mu funɛ gil hin Krit ni, i kpalimi dooni a kɔɔri Yesu nitiŋɛ kina a lisi nihɛɛta a li doonani maga baa lagmi. Lisi chenani mu fun ŋɔ hin.

ref: they must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. Their children must be followers of the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild and disobedient.
text: O lawa nihɛɛ kpak sɔn weri wa nara niu a kpak nihaan bɛgim duŋduŋ a kpak bisa ba naa se su Koosa. Ba ta chɔk o malɛ aa kitiŋ o nɔ.

ref: Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business.
text: Chenani o ya Yesu nitiŋɛ nyuhu tiina aa tum Koosa tuma nan, o lawa o kpak sɔn weri wa nara niu. O ta ya diŋ vɛ nuuli tiina a ta na paan lalaga a du nara ni, a ta nyɔ sin kinkan a dɛɛsi, a ta kpak nara wa tuha sa, a ta daŋni o ya malɛ tiina.

ref: Instead, they must be friendly to strangers and enjoy doing good things. They must also be sensible, fair, pure, and self-controlled.
text: O lawa o la nara a du o dam niu aa kidaŋni o tum tum were saha maga. O lawa o ya nɛha tiinau a ya nibin weri naa tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ a ya bambi tiina.

ref: They must stick to the true message they were taught, so their good teaching can help others and correct everyone who opposes it.
text: O lawa o kpak Koosa laaboor weri naa ba fun te biksi unu nɛnɛ. Waa ya wuti tiina. O kpak chen nɛnɛ, o zi kotiu a kpa un a biksi nara a kpami ba, aa koti a burgi ba naa vɛ un nyuunau.

ref: There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers.
text: Nibin damata vɛ tiŋ Koosa nɔ a kpa ba sɛm yɔrisa a la mur ba dɔŋtima. Ju tima ba naa ŋɔ di ba se su Yesu, bɔnɛ la ya chen a kaali ba maga.

ref: But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not.
text: Ba biksi tagna a naa lawa ba ta biksi, waligi narawa awa ba walɛ a ta a li Koosa nyinbaan wuti ti ni. Chendaha i ŋɔ ba di ba gil ba muri. Ba gbanko biksi chene di ba na malɛ.

ref: It is like one of their own prophets once said, “The people of Crete always tell lies. They are greedy and lazy like wild animals.”
text: Ba sɛmbiri witinsi ta digi a li Krit ni funɛ ŋɔ saha digi, “Krit tima ya nusun timwa saha maga. Ba chi sel tagna a naa ya pumpo tima a kpak zanyɛrkarɛ.”

ref: This surely is a true saying. And you should be hard on such people, so you can help them grow stronger in their faith.
text: O sɛm ta ya wutirɛ. I lawa i burgi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nyuunau wa kpok ni, chen ni ba zi kpak Koosa wuti tiu nɛnɛ.

ref: Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
text: Ba ta nu Ju tim ta mura awa chenani nibinsa ba naa vɛ wuti ti, ŋɔ ba.

ref: Everything is pure for someone whose heart is pure. But nothing is pure for an unbeliever with a dirty mind. That person's mind and conscience are destroyed.
text: Kina maga weru a li ba naa tusa Koosa fun chaksi ni. Amaa ba naa tusa o fun vɛ chaksi ka ba se vɛ su un, kina maga ya doŋwa ba wati, domi ba lise awa ba tusa ya kitagniwa.

ref: Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
text: Nibiksɛ na la ŋɔ di ba zim Koosau, amaa ba tuma biksɛɛ di ba wa zim un. Ba vɛ o nɔ a vɛ koti aa tum tum were. Chendaha ba chi kipɔtiwa o wati.

ref: Titus, you must teach only what is correct.
text: Amaa o lawa i kpa Koosa wuti biksi tiu aa biksi nara.

ref: Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
text: Kibiksi nibaal hɛɛta di ba ta nyɔ sin aa dɛɛsi. O lawa ba ya nɛha timau awa bambe tima, chen ni nara zi nyɛ ba jirimau. Kibiksi ba di ba se su Yesu kinkan a kidaŋni nara maga a kidi ba namsi.

ref: Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper,
text: Kibiksi nihahɛɛta gbanko di baa tum tum were, chen ni ba dɔŋtima zi nyɛ ba jirimau. Biksi ba ba ta chɔk ba dɔŋtim sɔna a ta nyɔ sin aa dɛɛsi. O lawa nihahɛɛti ta biksi nihaana baa tum tum wereu,

ref: so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers.
text: chen ni ba se zi sɛriu ka baa daŋni ba baalasa awa ba bisa,

ref: Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
text: a ya nɛha tima awa tu chaan tima a ya dam nihaan birige a ya jɛrgɛ tima aa tiŋ ba baalasa nɔɔna, chen ni ba naa vɛ tiŋ Kristo wa zi chɔk Koosa laaboor weri ti sɔn.

ref: Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
text: O lawa i gbanko biksi bunɔɔnau ba ya nɛha tima.

ref: Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
text: Kpa wuti sɛma aa biksi ba, chen ni i dɔma wa zi koti a ŋɔ di i tum galimsau. I sɛm chen, hiisa zi kpak bau.

ref: Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners
text: O lawa yosa tiŋ ba kunyama nɔɔnau saha maga aa nyɛ ba tusa kisɔɔni kina maga ni. Yosa ta lɛusi ba kunyama,

ref: or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior. God's Kindness and the New Life
text: akoo a ŋmɛ ba kina. O lawa baa tum tum wereu a nyɛ ba a ya wuti tima, chen ni ya Koosa naa funɛ yisi ya a li ya wu tagna ni, biksi ti zi kpak sɔn weriu.

ref: He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world.
text: Nan biksi ya di ya ta tum tum tagna a kidaŋni ya titi daŋnɛ a chi ba naa vɛ zim un. Lalaknɔ o daŋni ya ya bambi timwa awa wuti tima aa kitum tuma a naa la zi sɔɔni Koosa tuha.

ref: He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
text: O funɛ sou a daakakli ti nɛɛ a nyɛ ya, chen ni o zi la ya nyuunau a nyɛ ya a chaksi ya a dɔ ya a nyɛ ya se sɛm di ya tum tum were a nyɛ un.

ref: Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful
text: Kilisi nara di baa kpa ba bambe a du ba koora awa ba nyuhu tim ni a tiŋ ba nɔɔna a kisaksi ba titi aa tum tum were saha maga.

ref: and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone.
text: Kibiksi ba di ba fiɛŋ ba chɔk nibinsa sɔna, amaa o lawa ba ya nibin wereu awa nibin jɛrgɛ a kpak bara sɔɔna nara maga wati.

ref: We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
text: Ya ha wa fun zim Koosa a ya womna saha digi. Ya funɛ tiŋ ya titi nyinbaannɛ a vɛ tiŋ o nɔ. Saha maga ya yaa daŋni ya titi daŋnɛwa ka ya tusa sɔɔni. Ya yaa chɔk ya dɔŋtima sɔniwa a di handɔta a du ba ni. Nidamata fun ha ya. Ya gbanko ka fun ha ba.

ref: God our Savior showed us how good and kind he is.
text: Koosa naa weree a la daŋni ya, fun wa pu ya,

ref: He saved us because of his mercy, and not because of any good things we have done. God washed us by the power of the Holy Spirit. He gave us new birth and a fresh beginning.
text: o fun yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni yaa tiŋ un. O wa fun ya chen ya titi tum were daharɛ, amaa o ka funɛ ɔm ya sikirɛ. O fun kpa o siim weri a du ya ni, di o chaksi ya tum tagna a li ya ni a kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ya.

ref: Jesus treated us much better than we deserve. He made us acceptable to God and gave us the hope of eternal life.
text: O fun a suŋi ya di ya wa bu kpak galimsa o wati a nyɛ ya kpak bambi bɛgim di ya la o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: This message is certainly true. These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds.
text: Chenani mu ŋɔ hin, nan ya wutirɛ. Mu daŋni i yaa kibiksi narawa kin nama wɛ kinkan, chen ni ba naa se su Koosa se zi sɛri tum were tumi niu. Ba tum chen, ba zi wa na nyɔɔrau.

ref: But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
text: Amaa ta di amɔrɛ wa ba naa la biksi ba nawaalsa sɔna wɛ a la yo dɔŋa Koosa chiksɛ daha. Kin nama ya yɔrirɛ a wa kpak nyɔɔra.

ref: You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are. Personal Instructions and Greetings
text: Nibine naa la tum chen, biksi nara magawa di o tum tum tagniwa a bur tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan ti. Pɔɔl Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
text: Mu zi tum Artimas koo Tikikus i watiu. Ba nidigi wa pu hin, i gil un a kuwa mu wati Nikɔpɔlis ni. Mu daŋni mu nyɛn doonee hali wa saha wa peti.

ref: When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
text: Kpaŋi i titi a kisuŋi Zinas naa ya sɛria nidiili, awa Apɔlɔs ba saksi a pɔ ba val ti. Kpa kina maga baa daŋni a nyɛ ba.

ref: Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
text: Ba naa maga nyɛn mu wati zaami hinu. Ya gbanko zaami ba naa maga tiŋ Yesu a la daŋni ya a nyɛn i watiu. Koosa la zi suŋi ma maga.

ref: From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith. Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you
text: Mu waa, Pɔɔl, naa ba fun tɔ dɛ domi mu ya Yesu Kristo sɛhɛra tiinnɛ, awa ya dɔŋtiin, Timoti, zaami hinu. Min, Pɔɔl, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ i waa hin Failimɔn ni. I wa ya tum Yesu Kristo tumwa.

ref: and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
text: Ya gbanko zaami ya towe Afia, Arkipus awa ba naa la tiŋ Kristo a lagmi i dam ni chen. Arkipus gbanko tum Yesu Kristo tumwa kinkan.

ref: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Philemon's Love and Faith
text: Ya kunyana Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu Kristo suŋi ma maga a kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Failimɔn Funɛ Daŋni Koosa Nara Maga Kinkan

ref: Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God.
text: Mu niinbe, Failimɔn, mu yaa sumi Koosau a la zaami un i daha.

ref: I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
text: Nibinsa yaa ŋɔ minu di i si su ya kunyan Yesu kinkan ka ii daŋni ba naa maga tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan. Chendaha mu ya chen.

ref: My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people. Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
text: Mu niinbe daŋni, hin la kpami min a la nyɛ mu tuha sɔɔni kinkan, domi ii daŋni ba naa maga la tiŋ Koosa nyinbaannɛ a la kpami ba. Pɔɔl Fun Sumi Failimɔn Di O Suŋi Un

ref: Christ gives me the courage to tell you what to do.
text: Domi Kristo dɔ min nan, mu zi kpak bambi kpoku a ŋɔ hin chenani i zi ya.

ref: But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ,
text: Muu daŋni hinu. Chendaha mu sumi hin di i suŋi min. Kilisi di mu ya nibaalhɛɛrɛ ka ba fun kpa min la tɔ dɛ domi mu ya Yesu Kristo sɛhɛra tiinnɛ.

ref: I beg you to help Onesimus! He is like a son to me because I led him to Christ here in jail.
text: Mu sumi hinu i yoma Onisimus daha. O chi mu bere mu wati, domi o si funɛ su Kristore a li mu biksi ti ni dɛ bar tɔɔli nɔ ni.

ref: Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
text: Saha naa Onisimus naa sɔn mun ya ‘nisuŋi’, fun nyɛn i wati, o funɛ ya yɔrirɛ. Lalaknɔwa o kaa tum chenani o sɔn ti mun du a nyɛ mu wa hin ni.

ref: Sending Onesimus back to you makes me very sad.
text: Min la tum un di o bur la i wati. O chi mu tum mu titirɛ.

ref: I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news.
text: Mu daŋni o nyɛn mu watɛɛ dɛ nɔ ni a chiŋi i bar chiŋi ni a suŋi min.

ref: But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
text: Mu wara daŋni o nyɛn mu wati ka i vɛ nyɛ un nyinbaan. Mu wara daŋni mu muksi hin i suŋi min, amaa kitum i tutɔ.

ref: Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good,
text: Kilisi chenani o chɔ a ka hin nan. I wa un wa fun nyɛn a ya tama. O wa bur wa, i wa hin zi lagmi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
text: Lalaknɔ o wa bu ya yoma kinkan. O ya ya niinbe daŋnirɛ. Mu daŋni unu kinkan. Lalaknɔ i zi daŋni unu a kaali min domi o ya i yomne ka o si su ya kunyana.
