ref: If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me.
text: I wa min lagmɛɛ a tum Koosa tuma. Chendaha i la un a du a chi chenani i la min chen.

ref: If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
text: O chɔk i kundɔŋ akoo o pɛŋ i malɛ, ŋɔ min mu bɔ hin.

ref: With my own hand I write: I, PAUL, WILL PAY YOU BACK. But don't forget you owe me your life.
text: Mu titi la sɔbi nan a nyɛ hin. O pɛŋ i malɛ, mu vɔu di mu zi bɔ hinu. Mu biksi ti funɛ nyɛ i si su Yesu Kristo. Chendaha o chi min dɔ hin.

ref: My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
text: Adaha, mu niinbe, kisuŋi min a chi chen aa kikpami min, domi ya maga tiŋ ya kunyana Kristore.

ref: I am sure you will do all I have asked, and even more.
text: Mu zim di i zi tumu a kaali chenani mu sumi hin chen.

ref: Please get a room ready for me. I hope your prayers will be answered, and I can visit you.
text: Onisimus wa tel i wati, i kɔɔri dɛ a chiksi min. Mu kpak bambi bɛgimu di mu zi koti a wa ma watiu domi ma sumi Koosau a nyɛ min. Pɔɔl Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings,
text: Ɛpafras naa nyɛn mu wati dɛ nɔ ni domi o si su Yesu Kristo, o zaami hinu.

ref: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
text: Maak, Aristakus, Dimas awa Luk gbanko zaami hinu. Ba wa min la tum Koosa tuma.

ref: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
text: Ya kunyan Yesu Kristo kisuŋi ma.

ref: Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors.
text: Saha digi Koosa fun nyɛ o witinsa biksi ya nawaalsa o tutɔ wɛ. Nyinbaan damatiwa o fun kpa a biksi ba a gbaari tamtama.

ref: But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son.
text: Amaa bunsa nama nɛɛ o fun nyɛ o be Yesu Kristo biksi ya o wɛ. Koosa funɛ nyɛ o naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa buudaha, a fun lisi un di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa buudaha siili saha wa tel, o zi wa dɔ kina magau.

ref: God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together. After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o jirima awa o chaani kinkan a nyɛ un. O bɔni chenani Koosa du, a kpa o kpok kinkan a sɛm a kpak tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa buudaha a lagmi dɔŋ ni. O funɛ sou, chen ni Koosa zi koti a chaksi nibinsa tum tagnau a li ba bambe ni baa tiŋ un. O funɛ si o sou ni a zini a zu Koosa tɔha, a fun la kani Koosa naa kpak kpok kinkan a kaali a maga, nihɛɛta bar kani ni. Yesu Ya Nizen A Kaali Koosa Malaikasa

ref: He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs. God's Son Is Greater than Angels
text: Chenani o fun ya nan biksi ya di waa zenee a kaali Koosa malaikasa. Sɔn naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ un chen, zenee a kaali a sɔna kinkan.

ref: God has never said to any of the angels, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!” Neither has God said to any of them, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son!”
text: Ba funɛ sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Zinan mu fa biksi narau gbampulim ni di mu ya i nyana.” Ba gbanko fun sɔbi di o ŋɔ, “Mu zi ya o nyanau ka o kaa ya mu be.” Koosa wa fun ŋɔ nan o malaikasi ta digi daharɛ. O ka funɛ ŋɔ nan o be ti daharɛ.

ref: When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
text: Koosa funɛ tum o be bɛgim tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, a fun ŋɔ o wɛ, “Nyɛ mu malaikasi ta maga kijɛɛmi un.”

ref: And when God speaks about the angels, he says, “I change my angels into wind and my servants into flaming fire.”
text: Ba gbanko fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o funɛ sɛm o malaikasi ta wɛwa, “A chi peha awa dinne a saksi di a tum chenani mu daŋni lalaga.”

ref: But God says about his Son, “You are God, and you will rule as King forever! Your royal power brings about justice.
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ ŋɔ o be ti wɛwa, “I waa naa ya Koosa, i koor ti zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. I dɔɔli maga deŋlu.

ref: You loved justice and hated evil, and so I, your God, have chosen you. I appointed you and made you happier than any of your friends.”
text: I si yaa sɛriu di i tum tum were a vɛ kpak wu digi wa tum tagna ni. Chendaha mu waa naa ya i Koosa, funɛ lisi hin di i kpak jirima kinkan awa tu sɔɔna a kaali kina maga.”

ref: The Scriptures also say, “In the beginning, Lord, you were the one who laid the foundation of the earth and created the heavens.
text: Ba gbanko fun a sɔbi di Koosa funɛ ŋɔ o be ti wɛwa, “I waa naa zen kaali kina maga, min funɛ nyɛ i naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a li o piili ni. Hali min nyɛ i kpa i nisa a naami buudaha gbanko.

ref: They will all disappear and wear out like clothes, but you will last forever.
text: Wuzu digi a maga zi wa chɔku, amaa i zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. A maga chi takta naa vusɛɛ a la kusirɛ.

ref: You will roll them up like a robe and change them like a garment. But you are always the same, and you will live forever.”
text: Wuzu digi i zi wa guti unu a ta a kpa digi a chiŋsi o bar chiŋi ni. I ka wa zi kut chɛrgi a nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: God never said to any of the angels, “Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you!”
text: Ba gbanko fun sɔbi di Koosa ŋɔ, “Kuwa a wa kani mu nihɛɛta bar kani ni, ka mu nyɛ i dɔma burgi i yosa.” Koosa wa fun ŋɔ nan o malaikasi ta digi daharɛ. O ka funɛ ŋɔ nan o be ti daharɛ.

ref: We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away.
text: Chendaha o lawa ya kpaku nɛnɛ a li wuti biksi naa ya fun a nu a gbaari ni. Ya ya chen, ya wa zi a bur Koosa wuti nyinbaan ti ni.

ref: So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true.
text: Ya kunyan Yesu titi funɛ te biksi nara chenani Koosa daŋni o yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni baa tiŋ un. Ba naa funɛ nu o biksi ti, ba waa funɛ wa ŋɔ ya di o ya wutirɛ. Ya vɛ o wuti biksi naa ya kibirigi nan, yɛrɛ ya kaa ya a chɔɔli chenani Koosa zi na ya galimsa a lɛrgi ya diŋsa?

ref: God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to. The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ ba tum tum zene awa alaazibu tuma a tiŋ o tutɔ a kpa o siim weri ti papaha a nyɛ ba di o biksi nara di o sɛm ta ya wutiu. Chenani Yesu Sou Nyɛ O Burgi Ya Nyuhu Tiina

ref: We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels.
text: Lalaknɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga wa chɔk ka Koosa kɔɔri kifali, o wa zi nyɛ o malaikasi ta dɔ un.

ref: Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God, “What makes you care about us humans? Why are you concerned for weaklings such as we?
text: Ba funɛ sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di ba biksi ya di sɛm na ya wutiu. Ba fun ŋɔ, “Koosa, hin funɛ naami nibinsa a nyɛ ba kpok tama, amaa i baga nyɛn ba niu ka i nyini ba.

ref: You made us lower than the angels for a while. Yet you have crowned us with glory and honor.
text: Saha naa la zi vɛ vusi, i funɛ nyɛ i malaikasi ta zenu a kaali ba, a kpa jirima awa chaani a nyɛ ba.

ref: And you have put everything under our power!” God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control.
text: Hali i funɛ nyɛ ba dɔ kin naa maga i naamiu.” Ba funɛ sɔbi di nibinsa dɔ kina maga, o mun ya Koosa wa kpa kundɔŋ a ta. Amaa ya zimu di chenani Koosa sɛm chen ha wa ya,

ref: What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
text: amaa ma lisi Yesu wɛ. Koosa funɛ tum unu tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a nyɛ o malaikasi ta zen a kaali un wuzu bilɛ, a nyɛ o sou nara maga daha, chen ni Koosa zi kotiu a suŋi ba. O funɛ sou chene, Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni a nyɛ un o jirima awa o chaani kinkan.

ref: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
text: O funɛ deŋliu di Koosa naa funɛ naami kina maga di a nyɛ un jirima, nyɛ o be Yesu namsi a sou a peti o tuma a chi chen, chen ni Koosa zi koti a yisi nidamatau a li ba tum tagna ni a nyɛ ba burgi o bisa ba naa wa Yesu la zi wa bɔni o jirima awa o chaani kinkan. Chenani Yesu sou funɛ nyɛ o burgi ba nyuhu tiina birigi.

ref: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
text: Naa funɛ nyɛ Koosa yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni, o nyana awa ya nyana ya nibɛgimnɛ lalaknɔ. Chendaha Yesu vɛ kpak hiisa di o yir ya o niinbisa.

ref: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
text: Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “O Koosa, mu zi biksi mu niinbisau i wɛ. Mu wa ba zi lagmi dɔŋ niu a lɔ boola a pɛgi hin.”

ref: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
text: Ba fun bukoo sɔbi di o ŋɔ, “Mu zi nyɛ mu si su Koosau.” Ba fun bukoo sɔbi butora tiina di o ŋɔ, “Mu wa bisa naa Koosa fun kpa a nyɛ min, nyɛn bar nɔ.”

ref: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
text: Ba naa o funɛ yir Koosa bisa, ba waa ya nibinsa. Chendaha Yesu fun burgi nibine naa kpok purɛɛ a chi ya, chen ni o zi sou a kpa sintana awa o sou kpoku a ta,

ref: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
text: a yisi ba naa la ɔm sou a li un ni nidamatau a ta. Ba funɛ chi yosiwa ba ɔmi ti daha.

ref: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
text: Yesu wa fun burgi Koosa malaikasi ta digi di o suŋi ba. O ka funɛ burgi Ju tima ba naa ya Abraham bi lulɛ, digirɛ.

ref: And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
text: Sintana fun a zaamsi Yesu a nyɛ o na namsi kinkan. Chendaha o koti a suŋi ba naa sintana zaamsi lalaknɔ.

ref: My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest!
text: Mu niinbisa, Koosa funɛ yir ma a lisi a nyɛ o titi ni. Ma lisi Yesu naa ya se su wɛ. Koosa funɛ tum un di o wa ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra.

ref: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
text: Ju tim ta chi damnɛ. Mosis funɛ ya dam ti bar digi, amaa Yesu funɛ ya naa sa un. Chendaha o jirima kaali Mosis jirima kinkan.

ref: Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
text: Damsi maga kpak a nisalɛwa, amaa Koosa duŋduŋ funɛ naami kina maga.

ref: Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future.
text: Mosis funɛ nyini Koosa dam walɛ nɛnɛ, amaa o funɛ ya Koosa tumtumnɛ. O fun te biksi ba chenani Koosa zi wa tum wuzu digi.

ref: But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope. A Rest for God's People
text: Amaa Kristo naa ya Koosa be, waa ya dam ti tiina. Ya kpak un nɛnɛ a nyɛ chenani ya kpak bambi bɛgim un ni ya ya jirima kin, ya ya o dam walɛwa. Koosa Fun Vɔu Di Ya Zi La Zu O Bar Hiisiu

ref: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
text: Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o siim weri ti funɛ ŋɔ, “Koosa sɛm sɛma ma wati zinan,

ref: don't be stubborn! Don't rebel like those people who were tested in the desert. *
text: ma ta nyɛ ma se kpoki a chi ya nawaalsa ba naa funɛ nyɛn nyabaan ni saha digi. Chen saha ti ba funɛ zulimi un kinkan a zaamsi un.

ref: For forty years your ancestors tested God and saw the things he did.
text: Hali o fun nyini ba dooni bun fumnaasi, ka ba ha fun vɛɛna muksi un kinkan.

ref: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
text: Chendaha o fun a na paan a du ba ni a ŋɔ, ‘Ba yaa tum tum tagniwa saha maga, a vɛ tiŋ mu wuti nyinbaan.

ref: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
text: Mu funɛ na paanu a ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba a ŋɔ, ba wa zi wa zu mu bar hiisi.’ ”

ref: My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma doso. Ma ta nyɛ ma se suuli bur gantɛl ka ma tum tum tagna, a bur tiŋ Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ, nyinbaan.

ref: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
text: Chendaha maa kpami dɔŋa wuzu kama, chen ni ma nidigi wa zi nyɛ sintana nyɛ ma se kpok a kimur ma maa tum tum tagna. Koosa koti a suŋi ya lalaknɔ, amaa wuzu digi o wuzu suŋi ti wa peti.

ref: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
text: Ma lisi chenani ma se fun te su Kristo, ka ma fun wa zim un. Ma kpak un chen nɛnɛ hali ma nyɛni maga wa peti, ma zi wa bɔni o kinau wuzu digi.

ref: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
text: Anti watɛɛ Koosa fun sɛm sɛma saha digi ka ba vɛ un chen? O funɛ sɛm sɛmwa a nyɛ ba naa maga Mosis fun kpa li Ijipt pulum ni. Ba waa ya ba naa funɛ vɛ un.

ref: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
text: Yɛrɛ o fun ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba a ŋɔ di ba wa zi wa zu o bar hiisi? Ba funɛ vɛ unu. Chendaha o fun ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba a chi chen.

ref: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
text: Yɛrɛ Koosa fun ŋɔ di ba wa zi zu o bar hiisi ti? O fun ŋɔ ba chene domi ba se wa fun su un.

ref: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
text: Chenani Koosa fun vɔu di ya zi wa zu o bar hiisi ti ha vɛɛn chiŋiu. Ma nyɛ ya doso a ta nyɛ ya se suuli bur gantɛl, chen ni Koosa zi nyɛ ya zu dooniu.

ref: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
text: Ba funɛ wa biksi ya Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ a chi chenani Mosis gbanko fun biksi ba chen. Amaa ba wa fun na nyɔɔra a li un ni, domi ba se wa fun su un.

ref: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu funɛ na paanu a ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba a ŋɔ ba wa zi wa zu mu bar hiisi ti.” O funɛ naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga peti, Koosa fun zu doon. Saha ti kpa la, o funɛ pɔ ba naa se su un di ba wa gbaari un ni dooni.

ref: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Wuzu bunɔpɛ wuzure Koosa fun naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a peti a zu o bar hiisi ti.” Chendaha ya zim di o pɔ nannɛ.

ref: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
text: Amaa ba funɛ sɔbiu di o ŋɔ, “Ba wa zi wa zu mu bar hiisi ti.”

ref: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
text: Mosis funɛ biksi Ju tim ta Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ a li nyabaan ti ni saha digi, ba se wa fun su un. Chendaha ba fun vɛ zu o bar hiisi ti. Nara bɔnɛ zu doon, domi Koosa vɔwi ti ha vɛɛn chiŋiu.

ref: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
text: Wuzu bunɔpɛ wuzurɛ Koosa fun naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a peti a zu o bar hiisi ti. Doonee ba naa maga se su un zi zu.

ref: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
text: Ba naa zu dooni zi hiisiu a li ba tum ni, a chi chenani Koosa gbanko fun a hiisi a li o tum ni chen.

ref: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
text: Ma nyɛ ya kpak un a li ya bambe ni di ya zu dooni. Ma ta nyɛ ya vɛ o nɔ a chi ba naa funɛ tiŋ Mosis chen, chen ni Koosa wa zi wa nyɛ ya zu dooni.

ref: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
text: Koosa kpak miisa awa kpok kinkan. Chendaha o sɛm maga tum. A diu a kaali chi nɔsa lɛ a zu nara bambe ni a zim chenani ba lise maga du.

ref: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
text: Kin naa maga Koosa fun naami chen, puku a li gbampulim ni Koosa se ni. Wuzu digi o zi wa pɛɛsi ya magau chenani ya tumu. Yesu Ya Ya Koosa Dam Kɔkɔɔra Nizen

ref: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
text: Ya kpak naa ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra nizenu. O ya Koosa be, Yesu ti. O fun bur a zini a zu Koosa suwachak ni ya daha. Nan daha, o lawa ya kpak chenani ya se su unu nɛnɛ.

ref: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
text: Domi ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔr ti chiŋɛ ya bar chiŋi ni Koosa suwachak ni, ma nyɛ ya kpak bambi. Koosa zi wa kpak ya sikiu a kisuŋi ya saha naa maga ya daŋni un.

ref: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
text: Domi o titi wa fun kpak kpok, o kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni o tum tagna daha a gbaari nara kɔɔra ni.

ref: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
text: Nandigi wa fun lisi o titi di o ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra, chen ni nibinsa zi nyɛ un jirimau. Koosa duŋduŋ lisi un, a chi chenani o fun te lisi Ɛrɔn saha digi chen.

ref: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
text: Chen vɛɛna, Kristo wa fun lisi o titi di o ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra, chen ni ya zi nyɛ un jirimau. Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ un, “Zinan mu fa biksi narau gbampulim ni di mu ya i nyana.”

ref: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
text: Ba fun bukoo sɔbi di o ŋɔ un, “Hin la zi ya mu dam kɔkɔɔra hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, a chi Mɛlkizidɛk.”

ref: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
text: Yesu funɛ nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a wa dugli siwi, o fun a sumi Koosa naa kpak kpok di o lisi un a li o sou ni. Koosa funɛ nu o sumiu, domi Yesu funɛ ya nibin jɛrgɛrɛ a la nyɛ un jirima.

ref: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
text: Yesu ya Koosa bere, amaa Koosa funɛ biksi unu di o tiŋ o nɔ a li o namsi ni.

ref: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
text: Ma lisi di Koosa piin lisi un di o ya ba Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra a chi Mɛlkizidɛk. Ba Naa Bur A Li Koosa Wuti Nyinbaan Ni Wɛ

ref: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
text: Mu daŋni mu ŋɔ ma wu damatiwa a gbaari chenani mu ŋɔ ma nan ni, amaa o bɔmu wa mu biksi ma, domi ma sɔɔnɛu wa ma zim.

ref: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
text: Ma ya Yesu nitiŋɛu a vusi, o fu lawa ma titirɛ kibiksi nara kin nama wɛ. Amaa ma ha vɛɛn a daŋni narawa kibiksi ma Koosa laaboor weri te tel kin timau. Ma chi bichɛsa ba naa la ɔsirɛ. Ma ha wa mak ku diili.

ref: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ chi bichɛsa ba naa la ɔsirɛ, ba zim Koosa wuti biksi ti tamnɛ.

ref: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa zim Koosa wuti biksi ti nɛnɛ, ba waa zim kiwere awa kitagna. Ba waa chi ba naa biee a di ku chen.

ref: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
text: Ma nyɛ ya kpa Koosa laaboor weri te tel ti a chiŋsi a kidaŋni ya zim o laaboor weri ti maga a peti. O wa lawa ya sɛm dɔŋ ni chenani ya zi kpa ya tuma a naa ya yɔri a ta a nyɛ ya se su Koosa nɛnɛ,

ref: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
text: awa chenani nibinsa kpa ni a chaksi ba nara aa kurge awa chenani ya kpa ya nisa a saksi dɔŋ ni. O wa lawa ya sɛm dɔŋ ni chenani Koosa zi wa lɛli nisitɛ a li ba sou ni wuzu digi a di nibinsa maga sɛria.

ref: But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
text: O funɛ ya nan magwa a nyɛ ba ka ba bur o tiŋi ni. O chi ba funɛ kpaasi Koosa be ti a mari o daakakli ti chene a nyɛ nara bu na o hiisa gbampulim ni. Chendaha ba vɛ koti a gil ba tum tagna bilɛ tiina.

ref: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
text: Ya chi Koosa ŋmaharɛ. Doon mi ya ni ka kiparɛ bi nɛnɛ, chen ni ba zi wa kumu a di, Koosa nyɛ ya nyusum.

ref: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
text: Kɔha awa sɔta duŋduŋ nyu ya ni, ya ya yɔrirɛ. O wa zi vusi ka Koosa ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ya a kpa din a chɔk ya.

ref: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ya wara lisi di ma chi kɔha awa sɔta bar nyɛni tirɛ. Ya se su di ma kpak chenani Koosa fun yisi ma a li ma tum tagna niu nɛnɛ a tum tum were a nyɛ un.

ref: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
text: O ya wuti tiinnɛ. O wa zi sugli ma tum were naa ma fun tum a nyɛ un awa chenani ma fun biksi di ma daŋni unu a li ma dɔŋtima suŋi ni. Hali ma ha vɛɛna suŋi ba chene.

ref: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
text: Ya daŋni kinkan di ma kpak un ma bambe ni di maa suŋi ba hali ma nyɛni saha wa peti. Maa ya chen, chen ni ma bambi bɛgim naa ma kpak Koosa ni chen, zi ya wutiu.

ref: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
text: Ma ta ya zanyɛrka tima. Ma ka tiŋ ba naa nyɛ ba se su Koosa nɛnɛ a kpak kanyita a li ba tiŋi ni. Ma ya chen, wuzu digi Koosa zi wa lisiu a li o kin ni a nyɛ ma, a chi o funɛ vɔu. Koosa Vɔu Vɔwe, A Ya Ya Wutirɛ

ref: No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham,
text: Koosa funɛ vɔu vɔwi a nyɛ Abraham ni, o fun hɛɛsi o titi sɔn di o zi nyɛ o ya wutiu. O sɔn ti kaali kina maga. Chendaha o fun hɛɛsi un chen.

ref: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
text: Koosa fun vɔu di o zi nyɛ un nyusumu kinkan a nyɛ un bi lulɛ damata.

ref: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
text: Abraham funɛ kpak kanyitau. Chendaha o fun na chenani Koosa fun vɔu a nyɛ un.

ref: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
text: Nibine vɔu wu digi, o yaa hɛɛsi sɔn naa kaali o sɔnnɛ di o biksi nara di o zi nyɛ o ya wutiu. Chenani o hɛɛsi chen kok un a li amɔrɛ diili ni.

ref: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
text: Koosa fun kpa o sɔn a gbaari o vɔu ti ni di o biksi ba naa kaa wa la un di o wa zi chɔk un.

ref: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
text: Koosa ya wuti tiina a vɛ chɛrgi o bambi. Chenani o fun kpa o sɔn ti a gbaari o vɔu ti ni kpami ya kinkan. Ya naa funɛ chɔ la Koosa wati di o suŋi ya koti a kpak nɛnɛ a li chenani ya kpak bambi bɛgim ni di o zi wa kpa o kinau a nyɛ ya wuzu digi.

ref: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
text: Ya bambi bɛgim nɔ chiŋɛu. O wa zi chɛrgi. O chi nyinbaannɛ yaa tiŋ a la di ya la zu Koosa bar nyɛni naa o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni a li o tɔhɔn ti ni.

ref: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
text: Yesu ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra a chi Mɛlkizidɛk hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Waa funɛ te ya suwachak a la zu dooni ya daha.

ref: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
text: Mɛlkizidɛk funɛ ya Koosa naa zenee, dam kɔkɔɔra a nyɛn saha digi. O funɛ ya tɔhɔn naa ba yir Selɛm, koorre. Wuzu digi Abraham fun la chem laal a kpu koorsa. O funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni a la dama chen, Mɛlkizidɛk fun la chem un a sumi Koosa a du un ni.

ref: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
text: Abraham fun purigi o kina maga fi fi a lisi a digi a nyɛ un. Mɛlkizidɛk sɔn, o mun ya, ‘koor naa tum o tuma a deŋli’. O gbanko funɛ ya Selɛm tim koorre. Sɔn ti mun ya, ‘koor naa kpak bara sɔɔna awa nara ni’.

ref: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
text: Nandigi wa zim doonani o nyan awa o niina akoo o nawaalsa nyɛn. Nandigi wa zim saha naa ba fun lul un akoo saha naa o fun sou. Waa ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, a chi Yesu naa ya Koosa be chen.

ref: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
text: Ma na chenani nibine zen mak. Abraham funɛ la chem laal a kpu koorsi ta a pa ba kina a bur wa, hali o fun a purigi au fi fi a lisi a digi a nyɛ un.

ref: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o chiksɛ ta a nyɛ Mosis ni, o fun ŋɔ di Ju tim ta purigi ba kina fi fi a lisi a digi a nyɛ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa ba naa ya Livai bi lulɛ ni. Hali ba wa nara tima maga funɛ ya Abraham bi lulɛwa, Koosa funɛ ŋɔ ba di ba ya chen.

ref: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
text: Mɛlkizidɛk wa fun ya Livai bi lulɛ digi, amaa Abraham funɛ purigi o kina maga fi fi a lisi a digi a nyɛ un. Doone Mɛlkizidɛk fun sumi Koosa a du Abraham naa funɛ la chenani Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ un ni.

ref: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
text: Naa sumi Koosa a du nara ni, waa zen a kaali ba naa o sumi a du chen ni.

ref: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
text: Abraham funɛ lisi a li o kin ta ni a nyɛ Mɛlkizidɛk ni, o chi Livai gbanko funɛ lisi a nyɛ un.

ref: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
text: Abraham be luli ha wa fun lul Livai, amaa o funɛ chi o ha nyɛn Abraham bara nɛɛ chen saha ti.

ref: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
text: Koosa chiksɛ ta awa Ju tim ta Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni. Ba wa fun koti a yisi Ju tima a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. Chendaha Koosa fun tum Yesu Kristo. O wa fun ya Ɛrɔn bi lulɛ digi. O ka funɛ ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrrɛ a chi Mɛlkizidɛk.

ref: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
text: Yesu funɛ wa di o wa chiŋi Ju tima Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa bar chiŋi ni, Koosa fun chɛrgi o chiksɛ a naa o fun kpa a nyɛ Mosis ni chen.

ref: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
text: Lalaknɔwa ya piili a zim kin nama wɛ tama. Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra nifali a chi Mɛlkizidɛk funɛ wa ya wati.

ref: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
text: Nibinsa wa fun lisi un di o ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra. Kpok naa li o miisa naa vɛ kpak bɔ ni nyɛ o du chen.

ref: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
text: Chenani ya fun tiŋ Ju tim ta chiksɛ wa fun nyɛ Koosa bu vɛ na ya galimsa. Amaa lalaknɔ ya kpak bambi bɛgim a li naa nyɛ ya wa dugli Koosa.

ref: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ Yesu burgi ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra, o fun hɛɛsi di chenani o ya chen chiŋi. Ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ un, “Hin ya mu dam kɔkɔɔra fali hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Mu fun hɛɛsi di mu wa zi chɛrgi mu bambi.”

ref: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
text: Ju tim dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta funɛ iiu, domi nihɛɛta funɛ sitiu ka bɔnɛ chiŋi ba bar chiŋɛ ni.

ref: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
text: Yesu ka ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Nandigi wa kuwa chiŋi o bar chiŋi ni.

ref: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
text: Chendaha o koti a yisi ba naa maga se su un di ba wa Koosa wati, a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. O nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ a la sumi Koosa saha maga ba daha.

ref: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
text: Yesu ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra naa ya daŋni. Waa ya tu chaan tiina, a vɛ tum tum tagna hali tama. Koosa funɛ waligi unu a li nibinsa ba naa la tum tum tagna ni a kpa un a zini o zu Koosa tɔha di o kani o jirima bar kani ni.

ref: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
text: O wa Ju tima Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta wa ya nibɛgim. Ba waa yaa fun te kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni di a chiŋi ba tum tagna bar chiŋi ni, a zi kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni di a chiŋi nara tum tagna bar chiŋi ni. Yesu wa zi daŋni o ya chen. O funɛ sou chen, o fun kpa o titi a kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni bu bɛgim duŋduŋ.

ref: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
text: Nan wu birigirɛ ya daŋni ya ŋɔ ma. Yesu naa ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra, kani Koosa naa zene a kaali kina maga, nihɛɛ bar kani ni a li o tɔhɔn ti ni.

ref: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
text: Saha digi Mosis funɛ du lɛurɛ nyabaan ni a purigi un bilɛ, chen ni Koosa zi wa nyɛn dɛ mun niu. Lalaknɔ ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra nyɛn wuti lɛu naa Koosa fun du o tɔhɔn ti ni a tum o tuma a li dɛ mun ti ni ya daha.

ref: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
text: O ha fu vɛɛn nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, o fu wa zi ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra. Ju tim ta piin kpak ba Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa ba naa tiŋ ba chiksɛ tau a kpa nara tima papaha a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
text: Tum naa Ju tim Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta tum, ya chamsirɛ a li chenani Yesu tum a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ya daha. Mosis funɛ saksi di o du Koosa lɛu ti nyabaan ni saha digi, Koosa funɛ ŋɔ unu, “Doso a du un a chi chenani mu fun biksi hin bi naa ba yir Sainai ni chen”.

ref: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
text: Chenani Koosa wa Ju tim ta fun te ya nɔ bɛgim wa fun suŋi ba a peti. Chendaha Yesu fun nyɛ Koosa wa ya ya nɔ bɛgim di o chiŋi o bar chiŋi ni.

ref: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
text: Koosa funɛ na Ju tim ta galimsau saha digi. Ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Wuzu digi mu wa Ju tima ba naa ya Juda bi lulɛ, zi bu ya nɔ bɛgimu.

ref: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
text: O wa zi wa chi chenani mu fun te ba nawaalsi ta suwachak ba li Ijipt pulum ni, ka mu wa ba zi ya nɔ bɛgim. Ba fun chɔk chenani ba ŋɔ di ba zi tumu. Chendaha mu fun chɔk chen a ta.

ref: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: A wuzu ti wa tel, nannɛ mu wa Ju tim ta zi bu ya nɔ bɛgimu. Mu zi wa kpa mu chiksɛ tau a du ba bambe ni, chen ni ba zi tiŋ a nɔu saha maga. Min la zi wa ya ba Koosa. Ba zi wa ya mu narau.

ref: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
text: Nandigi wa zi wa biksi o niinbe chenani mu bambi du wɛ, domi ba naa ya nibirige awa ba naa vɛ ya nibirige maga zi zim minu.

ref: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
text: Mu zi kpak ba sikiu a gil ba tum tagna a nyɛ ba. Mu gbanko wa zi lisi a wɛ”.

ref: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
text: Koosa funɛ ŋɔ di o wa o nara zi bu ya nɔ bɛgimu, o biksi ya di chenani o wa ba fun te tum fun burgi kibuni. Kin naa maga la burgi kibunɛ a gbaari zi ya yɔriu lalaga.

ref: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
text: Ba fun a kpa gar a purigi lɛu ti bilɛ. Ba fun kpa fitina, tebul awa boroboro naa ba fun kpa a dau Koosa suwachak ni a du zɔɔnɔ dɛ ni a yir un Koosa bar lisi.

ref: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
text: Ba funɛ yir lɛu ti dɛ mun dɛ doonani Koosa fun lisi kinkan,

ref: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
text: a fun kpa salma a tɔ biŋbiŋa awa adaga zenzen a dau dooni. Bu bɛgim bun kam ba dam kɔkɔɔrsa nihɛɛ fun kpa poora naa sɔɔra weree a fu biŋbiŋ ti ni a nyɛ Koosa ni. Ba fun kpa diwe ta tama naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ ba a li nyabaan ti ni ka ba yir un ‘mana’ a du salma gbaŋ ni. Gbaŋ nɔ awa Ɛrɔn da naa funɛ tasi o kpachaga chen awa Koosa chiksɛ naa o fun sɔbi a sak gbɛntɛl sulsi daha ni a nyɛ Mosis ni, a maga funɛ nyɛn adaga zenzen ti ni.

ref: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
text: Ju tim ta funɛ kɔɔri lɛu ti peti, wuzu kam ba Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta yaa fun si a la zu zɔɔnɔ dɛ ti di ba tum ba tuma dooni.

ref: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
text: Ba Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta nihɛɛ ti duŋduŋ yaa fun si a la zu dɛ mun dɛ ti bun kam bu bɛgim. O fun kpa kɔɔri ti chɛl a nyɛ Koosa ni o tum tagna awa tum tagna naa nara tima vɛ zim di ba tumu daha.

ref: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
text: Koosa siim weri ti biksi ya a li kin nama ni di lɛu ti zɔɔnɔ dɛ ti funɛ tɔ nara tima nyinbaan, chen ni ba wa zi koti a zu doonani Koosa fun lisi kinkan.

ref: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
text: Ju tim ta waa baga nyɛn kin nawara ba na gbampulim nɛɛ. Diwe bɔnɛ awa kinyɔɔlɛ bɔnɛ funɛ ya kichiilewa, ka nyinbaanasa naa ba sɔ ni kurgɛ. Yesu funɛ wa nyɛ Koosa wa ya ya nɔ bɛgim, o fun biksi ya di kin nama wa bu ya kibirigi.

ref: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
text: Kristo naa ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra, nyinbaan ya kibirigi a kaali nyinbaan buni ti. Waa tum o tuma a nyɛ Koosa ni a li lɛu naa Koosa fun du o tɔhɔn ti ni. O waa zenee a kaali lɛu naa Ju tim ta fun du a li nyabaan ti ni saha digi chen.

ref: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
text: Ju tim ta Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta yaa fun kpa buuna awa nuwala chɛl a nyɛ Koosa ni a kpa nupɔl a fu o bara a kpa tɔ ti a zaasi ba naa chɔk ba chi ti, chen ni ba chaksi ba gbampulim ni.

ref: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
text: Kristo chɛl tum kinkan a kaali chen. Waa la chaksi ya tum tagna a li ya bambe ni, chen ni ya zi kotiu a tum tum were a nyɛ Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ, ni. Ya tum tagna fu kpalimi ya ni, Koosa fu zi wa na ya galimsau a ta ya yɔri. Koosa siim weri naa nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, fun suŋi Kristo di o sou a nyɛ ya. O wa fun tum tum tagin hali tama.

ref: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
text: Nar ŋɔ a chiŋsi ka o ha vɛ siwi, o nara wa zi pa o kina. Ba wa kitiŋ chenani o ŋɔ a chiŋi chen see ba biksi di o sir sou.

ref: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
text: Chen saha ti Mosis fun a biksi Ju tim ta chiksɛ naa maga Koosa fun kpa a nyɛ ba. O fun kpa nupɔla awa buuna a lisi a chɛl a lagmi ni ni. O fun kpa kiduha naa ba yir ‘hisɔp’, awa piisi pusa naa ba kpa tau kisɛm ni, a kpa lagmi dɔŋ ni a vɔu a tau ni wa chɛl ti ni a misi du Koosa chiksɛ tɔn awa nara tima maga ni.

ref: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
text: Mosis gbanko fun kpa ni wa chɛl ti a misi Koosa lɛu naa ba du nyabaan ti ni awa kin naa maga ba kpa a jɛɛmi un.

ref: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
text: Koosa lɛu ti awa kin naa maga nyɛn un ni funɛ ya Koosa tɔhɔn ti kina chamsirɛ. Chendaha Koosa fun ŋɔ ba di ba chaksi a chen. Chenani Yesu fun sou a chaksi Koosa tɔhɔn ti kina kaali sela chɛl ti kinkan.

ref: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
text: Kristo funɛ si o sou ni, o wa fun la zu Koosa lɛu naa nibinsa fun du chen, ka o fun ya Koosa tɔhɔn ti kin chamsa. O ka fun zini a zu Koosa wuti lɛu naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni. Lalaknɔ waa kpalimi a kani ya bar kani ni a li Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
text: Bun kam bu bɛgim Ju tim ta dam kɔkɔɔrsa nihɛɛ ti yaa fun kpa sela chɛlu a zu Koosa lɛu dɛ mun dɛ a nyɛ un di o chiŋi ba tum tagna bar chiŋi ni. Kristo wa fun zini a zu Koosa tɔhɔn ti lɛu ti a kpa o titi a nyɛ Koosa ni bu damata.

ref: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
text: O fu ya chen ni, saha naa Koosa fun naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga hali wa zinan, o fu zi namsi bu damatau a sou. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha funɛ wa dugli, o fun wa sou ya daha bu bɛgim a lisi tum tagna kpok a ta.

ref: We die only once, and then we are judged.
text: Nibinsa maga zi sou bu bɛgim. Ba wa siti, Koosa zi wa di ba sɛriau.

ref: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
text: Chen vɛɛna, Kristo funɛ sou bu bɛgimnɛ a kpa o titi a nyɛ Koosa ni, chen ni o zi kotiu a yisi nidamata a li ba tum tagna ni baa tiŋ un. Kristo wa bur wa, o wa bu sou a chiŋi ya tum tagna bar chiŋi ni. O zi yisi ba naa la pɔ unu, a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti.

ref: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
text: Koosa chiksɛ naa nyɛ Ju tim ta yaa fun kɔɔri kɔɔra a nyɛ Koosa ni bun kam, wa koti a yisi ba naa wa o wati, a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. Chendaha a waa ya kibirige naa Kristo zi ya nyɛ ya simsimsa duŋduŋ.

ref: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
text: Ju tim chiksɛ ta fu kote a yisi ba naa la jɛɛmi Koosa a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti, yɛrɛ ba ha vɛɛn zim di ba galimsa nyɛn ba ni? Ba chiksɛ ta fu lisi ba galimsa, ba fu zi gil di ba kɔɔri kɔɔrau a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
text: Wutirɛ nuwal awa buun chɛl wa kikoti a chiŋi nibinsa tum tagna bar chiŋi ni.

ref: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
text: O fun ŋɔ un dooni, “O Koosa, min nyɛn bar nɔ di mu tum chenani i daŋni, a chi chenani ba fun sɔbi mu wɛ a li i sɛm tɔn ti ni chen.”

ref: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
text: Kristo fun bukoo ŋɔ, “O Koosa, min nyɛn bar nɔ di mu tum chenani i daŋni.” Nan biksi ya di Koosa funɛ lisi Ju tim ta kɔɔra bunɛ tau a ta a kpa chenani Kristo fun sou a nyɛ ya a chiŋsi a bar chiŋi ni.

ref: So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
text: Domi Yesu Kristo funɛ tiŋ Koosa tutɔrɛ a wa sou bu bɛgim duŋduŋ ya daha, Koosa dɔ ya lalaknɔ.

ref: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
text: Wuzu kam Ju tima dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta yaa kɔɔri nara tima koorsau a nyɛ Koosa ni, amaa a wa nyɛ o yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti.

ref: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
text: O pɔ dooni hali wuzu naa Koosa zi nyɛ o dɔma wa burgi o yosa.

ref: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
text: Koosa siim weri ti ya kin nama sɛhɛra. Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ,

ref: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
text: “Nannɛ mu wa Ju tim ta zi bu ya nɔ bɛgimu. Mu zi wa kpa mu chiksɛ tau a du ba bambe ni, chen ni ba zi tiŋ a nɔu saha maga.”

ref: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
text: Ba fun bukoo sɔbi di o ŋɔ, “Mu wa zi bu lisi ba tum tagna wɛ.”

ref: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
text: Koosa yisɛ ya tum tagna a li ya ni. Ya kɔɔri kɔɔra a gbaari chenani o ya chen, a ya yɔrirɛ. Lalaknɔ Ya Na Nyinbaan Di Ya Zu Koosa Lɛu

ref: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
text: Mu niinbisa, domi Yesu funɛ sou a nyɛ ya, ya na nyinbaanu lalaknɔ di ya zu lɛu ti dɛ mun dɛ ti doonani Koosa nyɛn.

ref: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
text: Yesu naa ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔra zen ya naa la nyini ba naa maga la tiŋ un.

ref: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
text: Ma nyɛ ya la zu doonani Koosa nyɛn. Ya se su un nɛnɛ ka ya kpak tu bɛgim, o zi la ya duu. O chi o funɛ kpa Kristo chɛllɛ a misi ya di o chaksi ya bambe, chen ni ya wa zi bu kpak galimsa o suwachak ni. O gbanko chi o kpa ni a sɔ ya bara.

ref: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
text: Ya se su di chenani o fun vɔu a nyɛ ya chen, zi ya wutiu. Ma nyɛ ya kpak chenani ya ŋɔ nara ni di ya kpak bambi bɛgim un niu nɛnɛ.

ref: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
text: Ma nyɛ ya kpami ya dɔŋtima di ya maga kidaŋni dɔŋa a kitum tum were a nyɛ dɔŋ ni.

ref: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
text: Ya bɔnɛ wara lagmi dɔŋ ni a chi chenani ya fu zi du. Ma ta nyɛ ya tiŋ chenani ba tiŋi. Ya kunyan ti zi bur wa lalaga. Chendaha o lawa yaa kpami dɔŋau kinkan a gbaari.

ref: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
text: Ya funɛ nu Koosa wuti sɛmau a kpa ya nyuuna a vɔu tum tagna tumi, kɔɔri digi wa to a la zi nyɛ Koosa yisi ya a li a ni.

ref: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
text: Ya zim chenani Koosa bambi du awa chenani ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu zi bɔ ba naa la tum tum tagnau.” Ba fun bukoo sɔbi di o ŋɔ, “Mu titi zi wa di mu nara sɛriau.”

ref: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
text: Ba naa wa zu Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ, nin ni, yɛrɛ hansura zi kpak ba a mak.

ref: Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light.
text: Maa lisi chenani ma se fun te su Koosa ka ma tiŋ un nɛnɛ. Ma funɛ na namsiu kinkan, amaa ma tiŋi wa fun chɛrgi.

ref: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
text: Nara fun a pini ma zoma a nyɛ ma bara bora bu damata a li gbampulim ni. Ma na ka ma dɔŋtima namsi, ma gbanko ya namsi a nyɛ ba.

ref: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
text: Ma baga funɛ nyɛn ba naa ba fun tɔ dɛ chenu. Ba funɛ wa fuksi ma kina maga a li ma wati, ma tusa ha funɛ vɛɛn sɔɔniu, domi ma funɛ zimu di ma dɔ kin nawara kaali au a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni. Kin ta nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
text: Maa tiŋ o tutɔ wa kanyita ni, chen ni ma zi na kin nawara o vɔu di o nyɛ ma chenu.

ref: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
text: Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “O wa zi vusi ka naa kaa wa chen wa.

ref: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
text: Nara se su Koosa, o wa zi bu na ba galimsa o wati a kpa o mi fali a nyɛ ba. O tuha wa zi sɔɔni a li ba naa bur ba se suuli ni.”

ref: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
text: Ya naa se su Koosa, ya zimu di wuzu digi o zi wa lisiu a li o kina ni a nyɛ ya. Ya kpak bambi bɛgimu kin nama ya ha vɛ naaliu.

ref: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
text: Nidamata ba naa funɛ nyɛn saha digi, nyɛ ba se su Koosa a sɔɔni o tuha.

ref: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
text: Koosa funɛ sɛm a naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga, o fun kpa kin nawara ya vɛ koti a naali, a naami kin naa ya koti a na. Chenani ya se su di Koosa funɛ ya nan, biksi ya di o ya wutirɛ.

ref: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
text: Baal digirɛ ba funɛ yir Abɛl, o se fun su Koosa. Chendaha o kɔɔra naa o fun kpa nyɛ Koosa ni zen a kaali o niinbe Keen kɔɔra. Koosa fun a la o papaha a bu vɛ na o galimsa domi o se su un. Abɛl fun sou, amaa o tuma ha vɛɛn a biksi yau.

ref: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
text: Nibine si vɛ su Koosa, o wa zi sɔɔni Koosa tuha. Naa la daŋni o zim Koosa, o lawa o nyɛ o si su di o nyɛnu a wa bɔ ba naa dɛu un.

ref: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
text: Baal digirɛ ba funɛ yir Nowa, o si fun su Koosa. Chendaha o fun koti a nu ka Koosa ŋɔ un di o kaa kpa nire a su tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Nowa fun tiŋ Koosa nɔ a koori arŋa. O funɛ kɔɔri un peti, o wa o walɛ fun zu o tuha ka Koosa yisi ba a li ni ti ni. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga fun siti, domi ba se wa fun su Koosa. Amaa Nowa si funɛ su unu. Chendaha Koosa bu vɛ na o galimsa.

ref: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
text: Baal digirɛ ba funɛ yir Abraham, o si fun su Koosa. Koosa funɛ yir un, o fun a tiŋ o nɔ a gba a la pulum naa Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ un chen. O funɛ li o titi tɔhɔn ti ni, o wa fun zim doonani Koosa kpa un a la.

ref: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
text: O funɛ la tel pulum naa Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ un chen, o funɛ ya hɔɔrrɛ dooni. O wa o bisa, Aizik awa Jakɔb, funɛ nyɛn lɛwa nɛɛ. Ba gbanko fun a bɔni chenani Koosa fun vɔu a nyɛ Abraham ni chen.

ref: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
text: Amaa Abraham funɛ pɔu di o na tɔhɔn naa Koosa titi fun sa. O tɔhɔn ti kpak zɛchik weri a nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
text: Abraham haan, Sara, funɛ hɛɛsiu a vɛ koti a lul be. Amaa o si funɛ su di Koosa zi kpa o kpoku a nyɛ un o lul a tum chenani o fun vɔu chen.

ref: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Hali Abraham funɛ dugli siwi, bi damata funɛ li un ni. Ba iiu a chi wula naa nyɛn buudaha ni awa hɛchɛ naa nyɛn mɔɔga nɔ ni, ka nara vɛ koti a guri.

ref: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
text: Nara tima maga funɛ siti, ba se ha funɛ vɛɛn su Koosau. Ba funɛ nyɛn, ba wa fun koti a la kin nawara Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ ba chen. O chi ba funɛ na a a li bar boli nee a kpak bambe di ba la a. Ba funɛ nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, ba funɛ biksi narau di ba ya hɔɔrawa dooni.

ref: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
text: Ba naa funɛ biksi nara chen, funɛ nyini ba bar nyɛni.

ref: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
text: Ba wa fun bura lisi pulum naa ba li chen wɛ. Ba funɛ lisi o wɛ, ba fu zi na nyinbaanu a bur la dooni.

ref: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
text: Amaa ba funɛ daŋni ba zu tɔhɔn naa Koosa titi fun sarɛ. Tɔhɔn ti zen kaali pulum naa ba li chen ni kinkan. Koosa tuha sɔɔni di ba yir un ba Koosa. Chendaha o fun saksi ba bar nyɛni a li o tɔhɔn ti ni a nyɛ ba.

ref: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o funɛ ŋɔ Abraham ni, “Aizik daha i zi na be damatau.”

ref: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
text: Abraham funɛ zimu di Koosa funɛ kotiu a lɛli Aizik a li o sou ni. Hali o fun wa dugli sou kinkan, o chi Koosa funɛ lɛli un a li o sou ni.

ref: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
text: Jakɔb si funɛ su Koosa. Chendaha o funɛ wa dugli siwi o fun kpa Josɛf bisa a du Koosa nin ni di oo suŋi ba. Chen saha ti o fun a huuni o daŋkpal ni a jɛɛmi Koosa.

ref: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
text: Josɛf si funɛ su Koosa. Chendaha o funɛ wa dugli siwi, o fun a ŋɔ Ju tim ta ni chenani Koosa zi lisi ba a li Ijipt pulum ni. O gbanko fun a ŋɔ ba doonani ba zi sogli un.

ref: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
text: Mosis nyana awa o niina se funɛ su Koosa. Chendaha ba funɛ lul un, ba fun a kpa un a sogli bar digi ni pɛnsa tora. Ba funɛ na di be ti weru, a vɛ tiŋ chenani Ijipt pulum koor fun sɛm. Ba wa funɛ ɔm un.

ref: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
text: Mosis si funɛ su Koosa. Chendaha o funɛ wa bi, o wa fun la nɔɔni di nara tima yir un Ijipt pulum koor be.

ref: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
text: O funɛ daŋni di o wa Koosa nara lagmi a namsi a kaali chenani o funɛ daŋni o tum tum tagna wuzu bilɛ a na tu sɔɔna.

ref: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
text: O funɛ zimu di wuzu digi Koosa zi wa bɔ un o chimu. Chendaha o fun lisi di Ijipt tima pini o zoma a nyɛ o na namsi naa Koosa zi wa tum wuzu digi daha, chen fu bɔɛɛ wa o dɔ Ijipt tim wu were maga.

ref: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
text: Chenani o si fun su Koosa nyɛ o tiŋ Koosa nɔ a ŋɔ Ju tim ta ni di ba kpu sela a kpa a chɛl a misi ba dɛ zɔɔnɔsa ni, chen ni o malaika naa kpa sou a wa chen, o zi wa valu a gali ba bichɛsa ba naa ba te lul chen niu.

ref: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
text: Chenani o si fun su Koosa nyɛ o purigi ni naa ba yir ni sɛm chen, bilɛ, chen ni Ju tim ta zi koti a ji jibaŋ kinkin tiu. Ijipt tim lanjimasa fun tiŋ ba a la tu ni ti, ka o wa tɔ ba a di ba maga.

ref: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
text: Mu wa koti a sɔbi a ŋɔ ma lalaknɔ Gidiɔn, Berak, Jɛfta, Devid, Samuɛl, awa Koosa witinsa ba naa maga se su Koosa, wɛ.

ref: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
text: Ba fun la chem laal a nyaŋi koor zene. Ba yaa funɛ tum tum werewa a la kin nawara Koosa fun vɔu di o nyɛ ba. Nara fun kpa ba a du doonani gbogumtisa nyɛn,

ref: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
text: Koosa funɛ lɛli nihaana ba naa se su un, nisitɛ a li ba sou ni. Ba fun kpaksi bɔnɛ a ŋɔ ba di ba vɛ Koosa, ba zi na nyinbaanu a chɔ li. Ba ka funɛ na namsiu kinkan a sou, chen ni Koosa zi lɛli bau a du nyɛni naa weri a kaali chenani ba fun te nyɛn ni.

ref: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
text: Ba fun a mɔmsi bɔnɛ a kpa vurmsa a tuksi ba. Ba ka fun kpaksi bɔnɛ a vɔti ba a la tɔ dɛ.

ref: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
text: Ba funɛ ya nibirigewa, amaa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga baga wa fun to di ba nyɛ ba jirima. Ba yaa funɛ gba nyabaan awa kuŋkule nyuuna bar nyɛni ni a nyɛn gbɛntɛl bɔsa awa sela bar chɔɔli ni.

ref: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
text: O funɛ ya o tutɔrɛ di ba pɔ hali ba wa lagmi ya ni ka o zi yisi ya maga a li ya tum tagna ni hali a wa peti.

ref: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
text: Ba naa maga se funɛ su Koosa ka ba vɛ bur gantɛl, iiu kinkan. O chiu di ba baga nyɛn ya ni ka ba go ya. Chendaha o lawa ya kpa tum tagna a naa bɔm wa ya gil a ta. Ya nyɛni chi chɔchɔharɛ. Ma nyɛ ya chɔ a tiŋ nyinbaan naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ ya wa kanyita ni.

ref: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
text: Yesu duŋduŋ si funɛ su Koosa hali o wa peti. Chendaha o lawa ya nyini o se niu a kpaŋi ya titi a tiŋ chenani o tiŋ saha maga. Domi o funɛ zim di o tiŋ Koosa tutɔ ka o tuha wa sɔɔni kinkan, o fun nyɛ ba kpaasi un a mari daakakli ti ni a sou a na hiisa kinkan. Lalaknɔwa o kani Koosa nihɛɛ bar kani ni a li o tɔhɔn ti ni.

ref: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
text: Ma kuɛɛ a daŋni ma bur gantɛl, ma lisi chenani tum tagna tima fun ha un awa chenani o fun di o namsi.

ref: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
text: Ma nyɛ mu lisi ma chenani ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o kpami ma naa ya o bisa. O fun ŋɔ, “Mu be, mu wa buksi hin, i nu mu sɛma nɛnɛ. Mu zulimi hin, ta nyɛ i tuha chɔki.

ref: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
text: Mu biksi ba naa maga mu daŋnirɛ a la zulimi ba naa maga ya mu bisa.”

ref: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
text: Ma tiŋ chenani o buksi ma chen, domi o wa o bisa kpak dɔŋa. Nyama wara buksi ba bisaa?

ref: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
text: Koosa wara buksi ma a chi chenani bisa nyama yaa buksi ba chen, ma wa ya o bisa.

ref: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
text: Ya titi nyama funɛ buksi ya ka ya nyɛ ba jirima. Koosa naa ya ya nyana gbanko a kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ya, buksi ya, o lawa ya nyɛ un jirimau a kaali chenani ya nyɛ ya nyama wa jirimau.

ref: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
text: Ya nyama funɛ buksi ya tama, a tiŋ ba titi tutɔ. Chenani Koosa buksi ya a la suŋi ya kaali chen, chen ni wuzu digi ya zi wa burgi tu chaan timau a chi un.

ref: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
text: Ya nyama funɛ buksi ya akoo Koosa la buksi ya, chen nyɛ ya tusa chɔku tama. O wa peti, ya zimu di o funɛ wer a nyɛ yau, domi o funɛ nyɛ ya tum tum werewa a kpak bara sɔɔna aa gbaari. Ma Doso A Kinu Chenani Koosa Sɛm Ma

ref: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
text: Kin nama daha, ma kpak bambe a tum tum naa kaɛɛ.

ref: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
text: Maa tiŋ Koosa wuti nyinbaan naa weree a chaani, chen ni ma dɔŋtima ba naa vɛ kpak kpok kinkan a tiŋ ma gantɛl, wa zi chel.

ref: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
text: Maa kpak un ma bambe ni a kpak bara sɔɔna awa dɔŋ ni, a ya tu chaan tima. Tu chaan tima duŋduŋ la zi wa na ya kunyana.

ref: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
text: Koosa la suŋi ma di ma ya tu chaan tima aa gbaari. Ma doso ma ta nyɛ ma nandigi wa tɔ o nyinbaan. O bambe vɛ nyɛn un ni, chenani o lisi zi bu ma magau. Chen yɛɛ, yɛrɛ o zi wa du ma ni?

ref: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
text: Ma wa chi Ju tima ba naa funɛ dugli bi naa ba yir Sainai saha digi. Doonee ba fun na kina awa ba se ni a fun koti a kpa ba nisa a gbaŋsi a. Ba waa funɛ na din zen awa birsa awa dobaal zen,

ref: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
text: Chenani ba na wa fun ya nyini hali tama. Mosis bara fun jigiri ka o ŋɔ, “Hansura fun kpak min hali a vɛ weri.”

ref: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
text: Ma wa chi ba. Ma funɛ dugli bi naa ba yir Zaiyɔn ka o nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni. Doonee Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ, awa o malaika damata ka ba vɛ koti a gur a, nyɛn. Ba funɛ yir dooni Jɛrusalɛm titi.

ref: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
text: Ma funɛ dugli Koosa nara ba naa lagmi dooni ka ba sɔbi ba sɔna a du Koosa tɔn naa nyɛn dooni. Waa bu vɛ na ba galimsa a yisi ba a li ba tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. O gbanko zi di nara maga sɛriau.

ref: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
text: Ma funɛ wa zim Yesu naa funɛ nyɛ Koosa wa nibinsa bukoo ya nɔ bɛgim. O chi o kpa o chɛl tirɛ a misi ma bambe ni di o chaksi a. O chɛl ti zenee a kaali Abɛl naa o maana Keen fun kpu, chɛl.

ref: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
text: Ma doso a kinu chenani Koosa sɛm ma. O funɛ sɛm Ju tim ta ni a li bi Sainai ni, ka ba vɛ un, ba wa fun koti a chɔ a li o nin ni. Ya vɛ di ya wa zi nu chenani o sɛm a li o tɔhɔn ti ni, chenani o zi lɛrgi ya diŋsa zi kaali chenani o ya bau.

ref: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
text: O fun ŋɔ di o zi bu wa nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga zigsiu. Chen biksi ya di o zi wa zigsi kin nawara o fun naamiu a ta a yɔri. Kin nawara o zi vɛ wa zigsi, zi nyɛnu.

ref: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
text: Ya waa le la o koor naa kundɔŋ vɛ nyɛni a la zi zigsi un. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un chen daha. Ma nyɛ ya gbanko yaa kiɔm un a kinyɛ un jirima aa kijɛɛmi un, chen ni o tuha zi sɔɔniu.

ref: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
text: Ma maga ya niinbisawa. Chendaha o lawa maa daŋniu saha maga.

ref: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
text: Ma ta suglɛ di ma la hɔɔra aa du ma damsi ni. Bɔnɛ funɛ ya chene saha digi, ba wa fun zim di ba la Koosa malaikasiwa a du.

ref: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
text: Maa lisi ba naa ba fun kpa la tɔ dɛ a chi ma nyɛn ba watɛɛ dooni wɛ. Maa gbanko kilisi ba naa la na namsi a chi ma titi la na namsi wɛ.

ref: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
text: Nibaala awa ba haanasa kinyɛ dɔŋa awa jirima saha maga. Ba naa la daŋni nihaana yɔri yɔri a la tum chɔkumsa tuma, Koosa zi wa na ba galimsau.

ref: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
text: Ma ta daŋni ma ya malɛ tima. Ma nyɛ kin naa ma kpak sɔɔni ma tusa. Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu wa zi kut gil ma a ta.”

ref: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
text: Chendaha ya kpak bambi di ya sɛm, “Koosa zi suŋi yau. Ya wa zi ɔm chenani nibinsa zi ya a du ya ni.”

ref: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
text: Maa lisi ba naa funɛ te wa biksi Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ma, a siti. Ma zim chenani ba funɛ tiŋ un nɛnɛu. Ma nyɛ ma se su Koosa a chi chenani ba se fu su un.

ref: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
text: Yesu Kristo naa funɛ nyɛn ba ni a nyɛn ya ni, ya nibɛgimnɛ. O wa zi chɛrgi hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
text: Nara wa biksi ma a kurgɛ a li chenani ma nihɛɛta fun te biksi ma, ma ta bur wuti nyinbaan ti ni a tiŋ ba. Chiksɛ naa la biksi ma diwe diili wɛ, a wa suŋi ba. Chendaha a vɛ suŋi ma. Chenani Koosa kpa o kpok a nyɛ ma a la suŋi ma, chen kaali ba chiksɛ ta maga kinkan.

ref: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
text: Chendaha Yesu gbanko fun namsi kinkan a li Jɛrusalɛm gantɛl ni, chen ni o chɛl ti zi chiŋi ya tum tagna bar chiŋi niu.

ref: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
text: Ma nyɛ ya la pu un dooni ya wa un bɔni o hiisa.

ref: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
text: Ya wa kpak bar nyɛni a la zi nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni a vusi. Ya pɔ Koosarɛ di o wa kpa ya a la zu o tɔhɔn ti.

ref: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
text: Yesu yaa suŋi ya yaa pɛgi Koosa a la ŋɔ nara o wɛ. Chenani ya ya chen chi kɔɔrrɛ ya kpa a nyɛ un.

ref: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
text: Ma ta suglɛ di ma tum tum were a kisuŋi dɔŋa saha maga. Chenani ya ya chen gbanko kɔɔr naa sɔɔni Koosa tuha kinkan.

ref: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
text: Maa tiŋ ma nihɛɛti ta nɔɔna saha maga. Wuzu digi Koosa zi wa daŋni ba wa ŋɔ un chenani ba fun tum a nyɛ unu. Chendaha ba vɛ koti a hiisi a la nyini ma. Ma tiŋ ba nɔɔna, ba zi tum ba tuma wa tu sɔɔniu. Ma vɛ tiŋ ba nɔɔna, ma zi chɔk ba tusau ka o vɛ suŋi ma.

ref: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
text: Amaa nɔnɔ ya kibirigi a kaali chen. Maa sumi Koosa o nyɛ min nyinbaan mu bur wa ma wati lalaga. Sɛm Ta Mak Bar Nɔwa

ref: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
text: Koosa funɛ lɛli ya kunyan Yesu a li o sou ni. Ya chi o piisisiwa ka o ka ya ya nigbaŋi. O funɛ sou, o fun nyɛ Koosa wa nara bukoo ya nɔ bɛgim. Chenani o fun tum zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
text: Koosa naa ya bara sɔɔna tiina, zi yisi kin naa maga ma daŋniu a nyɛ ma, chen ni ma zi kotiu a tiŋ o tutɔ nɛnɛ. Yesu Kristo la suŋi ya di ya tum tum were a sɔɔni Koosa tuha. Ma nyɛ ya nyɛ Yesu wa jirima kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
text: Mu niinbisa, mu tɔn naa sɔbi a nyɛ ma nan, o wa ziŋ. Mu sumi mau di ma nu mu sɛma a naa la zi kpami ma, wa kanyita ni.

ref: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
text: Ma zimu di ba funɛ kpa Timotiu a tɔ dɛ. Ma nu laaboor weriu di ba lisi unu a li dooni. O wa mu wati lalaga, o wa min zi wa lagmi a wa ma watiu.

ref: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
text: Ma zaami ma nihɛɛta awa Koosa nara ba naa maga nyɛn ma ni, a nyɛ ya. Ma niinbisa ba naa nyɛn Itali pulum ni, ba gbanko zaami mau.

ref: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
text: Mu waa, Jems, naa ya Koosa awa ya kunyana Yesu Kristo tumtumna, zaami ma naa tiŋ Yesu a pisi bawu maga niu. O Lawa Ma Kpak Koosau Nɛnɛ

ref: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma wa na zaamsi, ma nyɛ ma tusa sɔɔni kinkan.

ref: You know you learn to endure by having your faith tested.
text: Ma zimu di ma Yesu se suuli chiŋɛ zaamsi ti ni, ma zi koti a diu.

ref: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
text: Maa di hali a wa peti, ma zi ya nibin wereu a chi chenani Koosa daŋni ma du.

ref: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
text: Zima pɔɔgi ma bɔnɛ, ma sumi Koosa o kpa o zima ti a nyɛ ma. Waa kpa kina a nyɛ nara maga ni a kaali chenani o daŋni a vɛ zɛɛmi ba.

ref: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
text: Ma sumi Koosa, ma nyɛ ma se su un a ta beeli. Nibin beeli chi chenani peha yaa mo ni a la suwachak awa gantɛl chene.

ref: If you are that kind of person, you can't make up your mind, and you surely can't be trusted. So don't expect the Lord to give you anything at all. Poor People and Rich People
text: Nara du chen, ba ya tuhu bilɛ timwa. Ba ta lisi di ba zi na kundɔŋu a li Koosa wati. Malɛ Tima Chi Bugilfita A Naa Vɛ Vusirɛ

ref: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
text: Ma naa ya fara tima, Koosa wa zer ma buudaha, ma nyɛ ma tusa sɔɔni.

ref: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
text: Ma naa ya malɛ tima, saha naa Koosa wa bur ma gantɛl, ma gbanko nyɛ ma tusa sɔɔni, domi ma chi chenani bugilfita yaa vɛ vusi chene.

ref: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
text: Nha wa yɛli a num kinkan. Bugilfita ta yaa zaksiu a chɛsi a bu vɛ weri wa nyini. Chen vɛɛna, malɛ tima wa nyɛn ba kpiŋi ni, ba gbanko zi wa sitiu. Koosa Zi Wa Na Tum Tagna Nitumɛ Galimsau

ref: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
text: Tu sɔɔn nyɛn nibine naa yaa di o zaamsi nɛɛ. O zaamsi saha wa peti, Koosa zi wa kpa o mi fali naa o fun vɔu, a nyɛ ba naa la daŋni un nɛɛ.

ref: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
text: Ya na zaamsi, o wa deŋli di ya ŋɔ di Koosa la zaamsi ya di ya tum tum tagna. Koosa titi wara zaamsi nandigi di o tum tum tagna. Wu tagna gbanko wa kizaamsi un.

ref: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
text: Amaa ya bambe kidaŋnɛ la lɛrgi ya a la mo ya loona a nyɛ ya na zaamsi di ya tum tum tagna.

ref: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
text: Ya tiŋ ya bambe kidaŋnɛ, ya zi tum tum tagnau. Ya tum tagna nyinbaan wa peti, Koosa zi wa na ya galimsau a ta ya yɔri.

ref: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma ta mur ma titi.

ref: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
text: Kiwere naa maga yaa kpak nan a li Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ni watɛɛ. Waa funɛ naami wula maga a du buudaha ni di aa chaani. O wara chɛrgi a chi barsɔn chɛrgi.

ref: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
text: O tutɔ nɛɛ o fun nyɛ ya burgi o bisa a li o wuti sɛm nuuli ni, chen ni waa dɔ ya a kpa ya a chiŋsi kin nawara maga o fun naami suwachak ni. Maa Tum Chenani Koosa Ŋɔ Ma

ref: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma lisi nan. Ma num wa Koosa sɛm nuuli ni, amaa ma ta num wa sɛmi ni aa ta na paan a du nara ni.

ref: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
text: Koosa daŋni yaa tum tum werewa a nyɛ un. Ya na paan, ya wa zi tiŋ chenani o daŋni.

ref: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
text: Ma nu Koosa sɛma a vɛ tiŋ a, ma ya mur ma titirɛ.

ref: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
text: Nar nu Koosa sɛma a vɛ tiŋ a, o chi nibine naa yaa nyini diga ni chene.

ref: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
text: O yaa na o titi diga ti ni aa kaali. O wa bu lisi chenani o fa du.

ref: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
text: Nar ŋɔ di oo tiŋ Koosa a vɛ doso wa o sɛm ni, o mur o titirɛ. O Koosa tiŋi ti ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
text: Ya kunyana Koosa daŋni yaa tiŋ un wa wuti nɛɛ. O lawa yaa suŋi sunsa awa luhaana ba naa la namsi, aa gil tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga tum tagna a ta.

ref: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma naa se su ya kunyan Yesu Kristo naa ya jirima tiina kinkan, o wa lawa ma suŋi bɔnɛ a gil bɔnɛ.

ref: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
text: Ma wa lagmi dɔŋ ni di ma jɛɛmi Koosa ka malɛ tiina laari kiweri a du salma gbina a wa zu, ka fara tiina gbanko laari kizɛrgɛ a wa zu,

ref: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
text: ma nyɛ naa laari kiweri chen jirima kinkan a ŋɔ un, “Kila a la kani bar weri ni,” amaa ma ŋɔ fara tiin ti ni, “Chiŋi akoo kani tɛɛnɛ ti ni dooni.”

ref: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
text: Ma suŋi bɔnɛ aa gil bɔnɛ. Ma wa di ba sɛria ka o vɛ deŋli.

ref: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, maa nu. Koosa funɛ lisi fara tima a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni di ba se su un kinkan a wa dɔ o koor naa o fun vɔu di o kpa nyɛ ba, domi ba waa la daŋni un. Chendaha ba ya kin tima Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
text: Amaa ma wa zɛɛmi fara tima a la nyɛ malɛ tima ba naa kpak tuha sa a nyɛ ma namsi a lɛrgi ma a la sɛria nidiile tim suwachak, wa jirima ni.

ref: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
text: Ba waa la sɛm wu tagna a nyɛ sɔn weri naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ ma chen.
