ref: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
text: Maa tiŋ Koosa koor chiksɛ naa ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni chen, nɛnɛ di ma daŋni dɔŋa a chi ma titi, chen weru.

ref: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
text: Amaa ma nyɛ bɔnɛ jirima aa gil bɔnɛ, ma galimu. Koosa ŋɔ di ma chɔk o chiksi tiu.

ref: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
text: Nar tiŋ o chiksɛ ta maga a chɔk kibɛgim, o chɔk a magawa. Chen tiina galimu Koosa wati.

ref: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
text: Koosa ŋɔ di ma ta tum chɔkumsi tima. O ka vɛɛn ŋɔ di ma ta kpu niinbisa. Ma vɛ tum chɔkumsi tima a la kpu nibine, wuti ma chɔk Koosa chiksɛ ta magawa.

ref: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
text: Saha naa Koosa wa kaa di ya sɛria, nar vɛ ɔm o dɔŋtiin siki, Koosa wa zi wa ɔm un siki. Chenani o kpak ya siki la zi nyɛ o ta na ya galimsa a ta ya yɔri. Ma Nyɛ Ma Se Suuli Awa Ma Yaali Ya Kibɛgim

ref: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
text: Mu niinbisa, nar ŋɔ di o si su Yesu ka o vɛ tum tum were a nyɛ nara ni, o si suuli wa zi yisi un a li chenani Koosa zi ŋɔ di o galimu.

ref: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ vɛ kpak kilaarɛ wa zinan diwe,

ref: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
text: ka ma ŋɔ ba di ba nyɛ ba tusa sɔɔni domi Koosa zi nyɛ ba kilaarɛ awa diweu, chen nyɔɔra ya baŋa ba wati?

ref: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
text: Nara se su Yesu duŋduŋ ka ba vɛ tum tum were a suŋi nara, ba se suuli ti ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
text: Amaa ma nidigi zi ŋɔ o dɔŋtiin niu, “Mu si su Yesu ka i tum tum were.” Mu zi ŋɔ unu, “Biksi min chenani nar si su Yesu ka o vɛ tum tum were. Chenani mu tum tum were biksi nara di mu si su Yesu.”

ref: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
text: Ma la nɔɔni di Koosa bɛgim nyɛnɛɛ. Chen deŋlu. Alizin tagna gbanko la nɔɔni di Koosa bɛgim nyɛnɛɛ. Chendaha ba ɔm a la jigiri.

ref: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
text: Ma ya womniwa kinkan. Mu ŋɔ ma di nar si su Yesu ka o vɛ tum tum were, o zi suuli ti ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
text: Abraham si suuli awa o yaali funɛ ya kibɛgimne. O chen yaali ti la biksi ya di o si funɛ sir su Koosau.

ref: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
text: Sɛm naa ba fun sɔbi a li o sɛm tɔn ti ni chen, funɛ ya wutirɛ. Ba fun sɔbi di Abraham si su Koosa. Chendaha Koosa fun nyɛ o nyinbaan deŋli o suwachak ni. Koosa gbanko fun a yir un o chɛna.

ref: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
text: Chenani nibine si su Koosa awa chenani o tum tum were gbanko nyɛ Koosa bu vɛ na o galimsa.

ref: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
text: Nibine bara vɛ kpak siima, o ya yɔrirɛ. Nibine si su Koosa a vɛ tum tum were, o si suuli ti gbanko ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma zim di Koosa zi wa di ya naa ya nibiksɛ sɛria a kaali ya dɔŋtima. Chendaha o wa lawa ma i a ya nibiksɛ.

ref: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
text: Ya maga yaa butiu kinkan. Nar vɛ buti o sɛm ni, o ya nibin werire kinkan a koti a kpak o titi nɛnɛ.

ref: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
text: Ya yaa kpa kabarikarɛ a du zaaga nɔ ni di o koti a tiŋ ya nɔ a tiŋ doonani ya daŋni o la.

ref: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
text: Ma gbanko kilisi arŋ zen wɛ. Pe zen yaa zer arŋ ti di o val lalaga. Arŋ ti niduse zi kotiu a kpa kiwewe naa yaa nyɛ arŋ ti deŋli aa val, chen ni ba zi nyɛ arŋ zen ti kila doonani ba daŋni o lau.

ref: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
text: Chene nibine zulum ya kiwewe aa la sɛm wu zene. Din wewe yaa kotiu a di kɔ zenzen.

ref: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
text: Nibine zulum chi dinne. Ya zuluma kote a sɛm sɛm tagna a naa kurgɛɛ kinkan a la chɔk chenani ya bambe du. Hali o chi din naa yaa li sintana ni aa chɔk kina maga ya daŋni yaa tum.

ref: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
text: Nara kote a nyaŋi sel zene awa zimebisa wa duma wa ni kina maga.

ref: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
text: Nibine wa to a la zi koti a nyaŋi o zulum. Ya zuluma tagnɛ kinkan. Ya wa koti a kpak a. O chi dum naa kpa o huun aa kpu nibinsiwa.

ref: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
text: Nɔ bɛgim tirɛ ya kpa a pɛgi Koosa. Warɛ ya vɛɛn kpa ŋɔ nɔɔsa a nyɛ nara ni. Mu niinbisa, chen wa deŋli.

ref: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
text: Ma zim di bugul bɛgim wa kibul ni were awa ni tagin.

ref: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
text: Mu niinbisa, ma ha na ka soom nɔn sul nɔnau, akoo a na maŋgo ka o nɔn gɔnda nɔnauu? Adaha teku ni naa kpak yisa wa kikoti a burgi pul ni. Zima Naa Li Koosa Wati, Tum Tum Werewa

ref: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
text: Nandigi kpak nɛha awa zima a li ma niuu? O nyɛ o nyɛni awa o tum were kibiksi nara di o kpak nɛha awa zima a ya jɛrgi.

ref: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
text: Amaa ma kpak handɔta awa tuha sa a ya se tima, ma ta daŋni sɔn a kisuni di ma kpak wuti zimau.

ref: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
text: Nan zima nɔ wa li Koosa wati. O ya tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga kinnɛ awa alizin tagna kin.

ref: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
text: Doonani nara kpak handɔta a ya se tima, ba yaa di dɔŋa wa amɔrɛ kpokwa a la tum tum tagna a naa kurgɛɛ, kinkan.

ref: When peacemakers plant seeds of peace, they will harvest justice.
text: Bara sɔɔn tima nyɛ nara kpak bara sɔɔna wa dɔŋ ni a nyɛn nyɛn weri.

ref: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
text: Yɛrɛ maa yo dɔŋa aa la muksi dɔŋa saha maga? Ma titi daŋni nɛɛ maa yo dɔŋa.

ref: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
text: Ma daŋni kinawa, amaa ma ka wara na a. Chendaha ma saksi di ma yaa kpu nara aa dɔ a. Ma bambe nyɛn a ni, amaa ma ka wara na a. Chendaha maa yo dɔŋa. Ma wara sumi Koosa di o nyɛ ma. Chendaha ma vɛ na a.

ref: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
text: Maa sumi Koosa di o kpa kina a nyɛ ma, chenani ma bambi lise du wa deŋli. Ma duŋduŋ daŋni daha maa sumi Koosa. Chendaha o vɛ kpa a a nyɛ ma.

ref: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
text: Ma naa vɛ tiŋ wuti ti nan, ma wa zim di ma wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ya chɛnsa, ma ya Koosa dɔmwaa? Nara la daŋni ba wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ya chɛnsa, ba waa ya Koosa dɔma.

ref: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
text: Amaa suŋi naa Koosa kpa a nyɛ ya, kpok kaali siim naa o kpa du ya ni chen. Ba funɛ sɔbiu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di, “O suŋi ba naa ya jɛrgɛwa, amaa o burgi o gantɛllɛ a chak sɔn dɔŋtima.”

ref: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
text: Adaha ma kpa ma bambe a du Koosa ni. Maa vɛ sintana, ka o zi chɔ a li ma ni.

ref: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
text: Ma wa dugli Koosa, o gbanko zi wa dugli mau. Ma naa la tum tum tagna, ma sɛm ma nisa a li ma tum tagna ni. Ma bambi bilɛlɛ tima, ma chaksi ma bambe a li ma lisi bilɛlɛ ta ni.

ref: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
text: Maa nyɛ ma tusa chɔk ka ma wi. Ma nyɛ ma mɔmsi burgi wiila awa ma tu sɔɔna ya tu chɔk.

ref: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
text: Ma zer ma titi a bur Koosa gantɛl ni, ka o zer ma buudaha. Ma Ta Di Ma Dɔŋtima Sɛria

ref: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
text: Mu niinbisa, ma ta sɛm wu tagna a nyɛ dɔŋ ni. Ba naa la sɛm sɛm tagna a nyɛ ba niinbisa ni a la di ba sɛria, chen tima sɛm sɛm tagna a nyɛ Koosa chiksɛ ta ni a la di o sɛria. Maa di Koosa chiksɛ ta sɛria ti, ma wara tiŋ a.

ref: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
text: Ma bɔnɛ yaa ŋɔ, “Zinan akoo chɛ ya zi gbau a la tɔ digi a la nyɛn dooni buna a kpiŋi a na nyɔɔra.” Maa nu mu wati.

ref: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
text: Ma wa zim chenani ma nyɛni zi du chɛ. Ma chi burrɛ naa ma na nan. Ma bu nyini, ma wa zi bu na un.

ref: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
text: Ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma chenani ma zi sɛm. Maa sɛm di Koosa la nɔɔni, ya zi koti a nyɛnu aa tum ya tuma nan.

ref: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
text: Amaa lalaknɔ maa kpak ma bambi ni a ŋɔ di ma zi kpiŋiu a na nyɔɔra. Chenani maa sɛm chen ya yɔrirɛ.

ref: If you don't do what you know is right, you have sinned.
text: Nar zim wu were a vɛ di o wara tum a, o galimu.

ref: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
text: Malɛ tima, maa nu mu wati. Ma zi wa namsiu kinkan. Chendaha maa fɔuli a kiwi.

ref: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
text: Kidamata naa ma kpak chen, a chɔku ka chau piin di ma kilaarɛ.

ref: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
text: Ma malɛ tima kɔu. Kɔɔli ti la zi wa ya sɛhɛra a du ma ni a nyɛ Koosa na ma galimsa a ta ba yɔri. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha duglɛu ka ma kpa ma kidamata a chiŋsi chen.

ref: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
text: Ma fun a mur tumtumsa ba naa la tum ma ŋmaha ni a vɛ bɔ ba. Ma wara nu ba fɔulii? Koosa naa kpak kina maga kpok, o nu ba naa la kum ma ŋmaha ti kiparɛ chen wiilau.

ref: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni ma kpak malɛ kinkan ka la ya ma tutɔ. Ma di diwe a zen a chi nuhɛ naa ba yaa chamsi di ba la kɔrigi chen.

ref: You have condemned and murdered innocent people, who couldn't even fight back. Be Patient and Kind
text: Ma funɛ di ba naa nisa vɛ nyɛni sɛriau a kpu ba, amaa ba wa fun lɛli ba nɔ. Ma Kpak Kanyita Hali Ya Kunyana Wa Bur Wa

ref: My friends, be patient until the Lord returns. Think of farmers who wait patiently for the autumn and spring rains to make their valuable crops grow.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma kpak kanyita hali ya kunyan ti wa bur wa. Nipari kpak kanyitarɛ a pɔ doon bumi awa doon naa tiŋ gantɛl ka o zi kum kiparɛ.

ref: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
text: Ma gbanko kpak kanyita. Ma nyɛ ma bambe chiŋi bar bɛgim, domi wuzu naa ya kunyan ti zi wa bur wa, chen duglɛu.

ref: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma ta zulimi dɔŋa, chen ni Koosa wa zi wa di ma sɛria. O zi wa di ma sɛriau lalaga.

ref: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma lisi Koosa witinsa ba naa funɛ nyɛn saha digi, a sɛm Koosa sɛm ta chen. Ma lisi chenani ba fun kpak kanyita a li ba namsi ni. Ma gbanko kpak chen kanyita.

ref: In fact, we praise the ones who endured the most. You remember how patient Job was and how the Lord finally helped him. The Lord did this because he is so merciful and kind.
text: Ba tusa sɔɔnɛu lalaknɔ, domi ba funɛ di ba namsiu. Ma lisi chenani Jop fun di o namsi saha digi. Ma zimu di Koosa funɛ nyɛ o namsi chen, di o ɔm un siki a suŋi un.

ref: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
text: Mu niinbisa, a naa kaali a nama maga, o wa werwa ma hɛɛsi Koosa tɔha akoo tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga koo kundɔŋ maga. Ma la nɔɔni akoo ma wa la nɔɔni, a ta hɛɛsi, chen ni ma wa zi zu Koosa sɛria bar diili dɛ.

ref: If you are having trouble, you should pray. And if you are feeling good, you should sing praises.
text: Nandigi nyɛn ma ni a namsiuu? O sumi Koosa. Nandigi nyɛn ma ni a kpak tu sɔɔnauu? O lɔ boola a pɛgi Koosa.

ref: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
text: Nandigi nyɛn ma ni a wiuu? O yir Yesu nitiŋɛ ta nihɛɛta di ba wa ya nu a biti a du un ni a sumi Koosa a nyɛ un.

ref: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
text: Ba ya chen, ka ba se su di Koosa zi nyɛ wichɔɔrti tiina na alaafɛu, o zi na alaafɛu. Tum tagna naa o tum, Koosa zi gil a nyɛ unu.

ref: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
text: Adaha ma ŋɔ dɔŋ ni ma tum tagna wɛ ka ma sumi Koosa a nyɛ dɔŋ ni, chen ni wichɔɔrta wa kpak ma, Koosa zi tiibi mau. Nibin weri sumi Koosa, o sumi ti kpak kpoku kinkan.

ref: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
text: Saha digi Koosa witiina Ɛlaija funɛ ya nibinere a chi ya. O si fun sɛri o sumi Koosa di doon ta miili. Bunsa tora awa pɛnsa nɔra doon wa fun mi.

ref: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
text: A gantɛl ni o fun bu sumi Koosa di doon mi. Doon fun mi kinkan. Ba fun du kina aa kum.

ref: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
text: Mu niinbisa, ma nidigi wa nyuksi wuti nyinbaan ti ka ma nidigi bu kpa un a bur wa,

ref: From Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To God's people who are scattered like foreigners in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
text: Mu waa, Pita, naa ya Yesu Kristo nitumi, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ma naa nyɛn Pɔntus, Galesia, Kapadosia, Azia awa Baitinia pulumsi ni. Ma ya hɔɔrawa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Koosa funɛ lisi ma a nyɛ o titi ni.

ref: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
text: O funɛ ya ya kunyana Koosa tutɔrɛ saha digi o fun lisi ma chen. O siim weri ti waligi ma di ma burgi o nibin were, chen ni ma zi tiŋ Yesu Kristo nɔu. O sou chɛl ti la zi chaksi ma tum tagnau a li ma ni. Koosa suŋi ma a nyɛ ma bara sɔɔna aa gbaari. Ma Kpak Bambi Bɛgim A Li Kin Naa Koosa Zi Nyɛ Ma Ni.

ref: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
text: Koosa naa ya ya kunyan Yesu Kristo nyana, funɛ ɔm ya siki kinkan. Chendaha ma nyɛ ya pɛgi un saha maga. O funɛ lɛli Yesu Kristo a li o sou ni, a kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ya. Chendaha ya kpak bambi bɛgim,

ref: God has something stored up for you in heaven, where it will never decay or be ruined or disappear.
text: di wuzu digi ya zi la kiwere naa o fun kpa a chiŋsi o dɛ niu a li o tɔhɔn ni. A wa zi kut chɔk dooni.

ref: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
text: Koosa kpa o kpok a nyini ma naa se su un. O funɛ piin saksiu di o yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni hali a wa peti. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa tel, o zi wa ya chenu gbampulim ni.

ref: On that day you will be glad, even if you have to go through many hard trials for a while.
text: Ma nyɛ ma tusa sɔɔni kin nama daha. Lalaknɔ sintana la zaamsi ma a li wu kurgɛ ni, chen ni ma tusa zi chɔku wuzu bilɛ.

ref: Your faith will be like gold that has been tested in a fire. And these trials will prove that your faith is worth much more than gold that can be destroyed. They will show that you will be given praise and honor and glory when Jesus Christ returns.
text: Nibinsa yaa kpa salmau a du din ni di o burgi kiweri. Chen vɛɛna, Koosa nyɛ sintana zaamsi ma, chen ni o zi biksi di ma se suuli naa ma kpak un ni, chen ya wutirɛ. Ma se suuli ti Koosa wati, o kaali salma naa la zi wa chɔku. Saha naa Yesu Kristo wa bur wa nara suwachak ni, Koosa tuha zi sɔɔniu wa ya ni ka o zi nyɛ ya o jirima awa o chaani kinkan.

ref: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
text: Ma ha wa na un, amaa maa daŋni unu ka ma se su un. Chendaha Koosa piin nyɛ ma tu sɔɔna hali a la gali.

ref: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
text: Ma se un daha, o zi wa yisi mau a li ma tum tagna ni hali a wa peti.

ref: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
text: Saha digi Koosa witinsa funɛ biksi nara chenani o zi suŋi ma a ya chen.

ref: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
text: Koosa siim weri naa funɛ nyɛn ba ni, fun a biksi ba di wuzu digi Kristo zi wa namsiu a sou a si a li o sou ni a na jirima kinkan a li Koosa wati. Ba funɛ lisi kin nama wɛ a kpaŋi a dɛu saha naa Kristo zi wa wa.

ref: But they were told that they were serving you and not themselves. They preached to you by the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven. And their message was only for you, even though angels would like to know more about it. Chosen To Live a Holy Life
text: Koosa siim weri ti gbanko wa fun biksi o witinsi ta di ba wa tum a nyɛ ba titi nɛɛ, amaa ba tum a nyɛ ma nɛɛ. Koosa fun a tum o siim weri ti a li o tɔhɔn ti ni di o kpa o kpok ti a nyɛ ba naa funɛ wa biksi ma Koosa laaboor weri ti wɛ. O witinsi ta funɛ sɛm o laaboor weri ti wɛu a daŋni ma zim un. Hali Koosa malaikasa daŋni ba zim o laaboor weri ti munu. Ma Kpak Tu Chaana A Li Ma Tuma Maga Ni

ref: Be alert and think straight. Put all your hope in how God will treat you with undeserved grace when Jesus Christ appears.
text: Adaha ma saksi chenani ma lisi a ya bambe tima a kpak bambi bɛgim a li chenani Koosa zi wa suŋi ma. O be Yesu Kristo wa bur wa nara suwachak ni, Koosa zi ya chenu.

ref: Behave like obedient children. Don't let your lives be controlled by your desires, as they used to be.
text: Saha naa ma fun zɛ zim un, ma funɛ tiŋ ma tutɔ daŋnirɛ. Ma ta tiŋ chen lalaknɔ, amaa maa tiŋ Koosa nɔ nɛnɛ.

ref: Always live as God's holy people should, because God is the one who chose you, and he is holy.
text: Waa ya tu chaan tiina. Chendaha o fun a lisi ma gbanko di ma kpak tu chaana a li ma tum maga ni a nyɛ un.

ref: That's why the Scriptures say, “I am the holy God, and you must be holy too.”
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu ya tu chaan tiinnɛ. Chendaha ma kpak tu chaana.”

ref: You say that God is your Father, but God doesn't have favorites! He judges all people by what they do. So you must honor God while you live as strangers here on earth.
text: Maa sumi Koosa, ma yir un mu nyana. Wuzu digi o zi wa di nibinsa maga sɛriau a li ba tuma ni. O wara daŋni bɔnɛ aa gil bɔnɛ. Chendaha wuzuna a naa kaɛɛ nan, ma nyɛ un jirima kinkan.

ref: Christ was chosen even before the world was created, but because of you, he did not come until these last days.
text: O funɛ te lisi Kristore a zi naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa dugli, Koosa fun tum un di waa tum nan a nyɛ ma.

ref: And when he did come, it was to lead you to have faith in God, who raised him from death and honored him in a glorious way. This is why you have put your faith and hope in God.
text: Kristo funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni, Koosa fun lɛli un a li o sou ni a kpa o jirima awa o chaani kinkan a du un ni. O daha ma se su Koosau ka ma kpak bambi bɛgim un ni.

ref: You obeyed the truth, and your souls were made pure. Now you sincerely love each other. But you must keep on loving with all your heart.
text: Ma funɛ nu Koosa laaboor weri naa ya wuti chen, ma fun a chaksi ma tusa a li ma tum tagna ni a tiŋ un, chen ni ma fun piili a daŋni ma dɔŋtima a li bambe ni. Maa daŋni ba kinkan ma bambe ni.

ref: Do this because God has given you new birth by his message that lives on forever.
text: Koosa sɛma nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. A wa zi kut chɔk. Koosa fun a kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ma a daha.

ref: The Scriptures say, “Humans wither like grass, and their glory fades like wild flowers. Grass dries up, and flowers fall to the ground.
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Nibinsa maga chi kɔsawa a kpak bunɔɔta a chi kɔsa yeligisa. Yeligisa yaa looriu. Kɔsa ka hul.

ref: But what the Lord has said will stand forever.” Our good news to you is what the Lord has said.
text: Amaa Koosa sɛma zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.” Kin nama ya o laaboor weri naa ba fun wa biksi ma chen.

ref: Stop being hateful! Quit trying to fool people, and start being sincere. Don't be jealous or say cruel things about others.
text: Kin nama daha, ma kpa ma tum tagna maga a ta. Ma ta suni wɛ a nyɛ dɔŋ ni a ta kpak handɔta a du dɔŋ ni a ta pini dɔŋ zoma.

ref: You have already found out how good the Lord really is.
text: Ma zimu di Koosa funɛ suŋi ma.

ref: Come to Jesus Christ. He is the living stone people have rejected, but which God has chosen and highly honored.
text: Ma wa dugli ya kunyan ti wati. O chi buŋkul naa Koosa fun lisi di o ya kizen a li o dam ni, amaa nisalɛ funɛ vɛ unu a ta. O kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ma.

ref: And now you are living stones being used to build a spiritual house. You are also a group of holy priests, and with the help of Jesus Christ you will offer sacrifices that please God.
text: Ma naa kpak o mi fali ti, chi buŋkula a naa Koosa kpa sa o dam ti, chen ni o zi kotiu a nyɛn dooni. Ma chi ba naa funɛ ya o kɔkɔɔrsa saha digi, a kpa ma titi a nyɛ Koosa ni Yesu Kristo daha.

ref: It is just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a choice and precious cornerstone. No one who has faith in this one will be disappointed.”
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni di o ŋɔ, “Mu funɛ lisi buŋkul zenu naa mu kpa sa dɛ koŋa ni a li Jɛrusalɛm ni. Ba naa maga se su Kristo, o wa zi buti ba.”

ref: You are followers of the Lord, and this stone is precious to you. But it isn't precious to those who refuse to follow him. They are the builders who tossed aside the stone that turned out to be the most important one of all.
text: Waa ya nizen kinkan a li ba naa se su un ni. Amaa ba funɛ sɔbi ba naa vɛ un chen wɛu a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Buŋkul naa nisalɛ vɛ a ŋɔ di o ya yɔrirɛ, waa ya buŋkul zen a kaali a maga.

ref: They disobeyed the message and stumbled and fell over this stone, because they were doomed.
text: Buŋkul nɔ la zi mo nibinsi naŋgere ba chel,” domi ba vɛu di ba wa zi nu Koosa laaboor weri ti. Koosa tutɔ funɛ ya chen.

ref: But you are God's chosen and special people. You are a group of royal priests and a holy nation. God has brought you out of darkness into his marvelous light. Now you must tell all the wonderful things he has done. The Scriptures say,
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ lisi mau a li birsi ni a wa di o titi bar chaani kinkan ni. Ma chi o koorsiwa awa o kɔkɔɔrsi tawa. O funɛ lisi mau a nyɛ o titi ni di ma ŋɔ nara ni o alaazibu tuma wɛ.

ref: “Once you were nobody. Now you are God's people. At one time no one had mercy on you. Now God has treated you with kindness.” Live as God's Servants Should
text: Saha digi o wa fun dɔ ma, amaa lalaknɔ o dɔ mau. O wa fun a ɔm ma siki saha digi, amaa ma la unu lalaknɔ. Ma Nyɛ Koosa Awa Kaŋkoorsa Awa Nara Maga Jirima

ref: Dear friends, you are foreigners and strangers on this earth. So I beg you not to surrender to those desires that fight against you.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma naa ya hɔɔra a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, mu sumi mau di ma kpa ma tutɔ daŋnɛ a ta. Ya tutɔ daŋnɛ awa chenani Koosa daŋni yaa tiŋ, a chɔ aa du dɔŋ nɛɛ.

ref: Always let others see you behaving properly, even though they may still accuse you of doing wrong. Then on the day of judgment, they will honor God by telling the good things they saw you do.
text: O lawa ma kpak sɔn were ba naa vɛ zim Koosa watiu, chen ni ba wa fiksi ma di ma tum tum tagna, ba zi wa na ma tum were duŋduŋu. Koosa wa wa di o wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria, nifiksi nama zi pɛgi unu ma daha.

ref: You must also obey governors, because they are sent by the Emperor to punish criminals and to praise good citizens.
text: Ma kpak ya kunyan ti sɔn. Chendaha maa tiŋ kaŋkoorsa awa koorsa ba naa maga tiŋ ba gantɛl, nɔ. O vɔti bau di ba lɛrgi ba naa la tum tum tagna diŋsa a kipɛgi ba naa la tum tum were.

ref: God wants you to silence stupid and ignorant people by doing right.
text: O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ ma kpa ma tum were aa tɔ womna ba naa vɛ zim chenani ma tiŋi nɔ sɛma.

ref: You are free, but still you are God's servants, and you must not use your freedom as an excuse for doing wrong.
text: Ma ya nibin hiisewa, amaa ma gbanko ya Koosa yosiwa. Ma ta tum tum tagna aa sogli domi ma ya nihiise daha.

ref: Respect everyone and show special love for God's people. Honor God and respect the Emperor. The Example of Christ's Suffering
text: Maa daŋni ma dɔŋtima ba naa se su Yesu, a kinyɛ Koosa awa koorsi ta nyuhu tiina Siiza awa nara maga jirima. Chenani Kristo Fun Namsi Ma Daha

ref: Servants, you must obey your masters and always show respect to them. Do this, not only to those who are kind and thoughtful, but also to those who are cruel.
text: Ma tumtumsa, ma nyɛ ma kunyama wa jirima kinkan aa tiŋ ba nɔɔna. Ma kunyama ya jɛrgɛ akoo ba ya tu haasa timwa, ma nyɛ ba jirima aa kitiŋ ba chen ni.

ref: God will bless you, even if others treat you unfairly for being loyal to him.
text: Ba kpak tu haasa a nyɛ ma na namsi, ma di un ka Koosa sɔɔni ma tusa.

ref: You don't gain anything by being punished for some wrong you have done. But God will bless you, if you have to suffer for doing something good.
text: Ma tum galimsa awa tum tagna ka nara wa tuksi ma a daha ka ma di a, ba zi pɛgi mauu? Ma ka na namsi ma tum were daha ka ma di a, Koosa zi sɔɔni ma tusau.

ref: After all, God chose you to suffer as you follow in the footsteps of Christ, who set an example by suffering for you.
text: Koosa funɛ yir mau di ma ya chen. Kristo titi funɛ namsiu ma daha a biksi ma chenani ma zi tum a chi o funɛ tum chen.

ref: Christ did not sin or ever tell a lie.
text: O wa fun kut a tum tum tagin. O wa fun suni wɛ a nyɛ nara ni.

ref: Although he was abused, he never tried to get even. And when he suffered, he made no threats. Instead, he had faith in God, who judges fairly.
text: Ba funɛ pini o zoma a vɛ weri, o wa fun bur a pini ba zoma. Ba funɛ nyɛ o namsi, o wa fun a hɔɔsi ba, amaa o funɛ kpa o titu a du Koosa naa la di nara maga sɛria wa wuti ni nin ni.

ref: Christ carried the burden of our sins. He was nailed to the cross, so we would stop sinning and start living right. By his cuts and bruises you are healed.
text: Kristo funɛ sou a li o daakakli ti ni, o fun kpa ya tum tagna a du o titi ni, chen ni ya wa zi bu tum tum tagna, amaa ya zi tum tum wereu. O chɔɔra funɛ nyɛ ma na alaafɛu.

ref: You had wandered away like sheep. Now you have returned to the one who is your shepherd and protector.
text: Ma funɛ chi piisi nyuksɛwa. Lalaknɔ ma funɛ burgiu a wa Kristo naa chi ma nigbaŋi, wati. Waa la nyini ma siimsa.

ref: If you are a wife, you must put your husband first. Even if he opposes our message, you will win him over by what you do. No one else will have to say anything to him,
text: Nihaana, ma gbanko kitiŋ ma baalasa nɔɔna, chen ni ba bɔnɛ se vɛ su Koosa laaboor weri ti, ba zi daŋni ba burgiu a tiŋ un chenani maa tum daha. Ba wa zi burgi a tiŋ un ma sɛm daha,

ref: because he will see how you honor God and live a pure life.
text: amaa ma tu chaana awa chenani ma nyɛ un jirima daha.

ref: Don't depend on things like fancy hairdos or gold jewelry or expensive clothes to make you look beautiful.
text: Ma ta daŋni ma vɔti ma nyuksa a kilaari salma kina awa gar were di ma di tula.

ref: Long ago those women who worshiped God and put their hope in him made themselves beautiful by putting their husbands first.
text: Nihaana ba naa funɛ nyɛn saha digi chen, bɔnɛ fun tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan a kpak bambi bɛgim un ni a tiŋ ba baalasa nɔɔna. Chendaha ba fun a di tula o suwachak ni.

ref: For example, Sarah obeyed Abraham and called him her master. You are her true children, if you do right and don't let anything frighten you.
text: Nihaan digi funɛ nyɛn doon. Ba yir un Sara. Waa funɛ di tula chen ni. O fun a tiŋ o baal Abraham nɔ aa yir un o kunyana. Maa tum tum were a vɛ ɔm wu digi, ma waa chi o lul bisa.

ref: If you are a husband, you should be thoughtful of your wife. Treat her with honor, because she isn't as strong as you are, and she shares with you in the gift of life. Then nothing will stand in the way of your prayers. Suffering for Doing Right
text: Nibaala, ma wa ma haanasa lagmi nyɛn nɛha ni. Ma kpok kaali ba kpok. Chendaha maa nyɛ ba jirima. Ma wa ba piin la Koosa mi fali ti. Ma vɛ ya chen, ma sumi zi tɔu. Ma Zi Namsiu Ma Tum Were Daha

ref: Finally, all of you should agree and have concern and love for each other. You should also be kind and humble.
text: Ma nyɛ mu lagmi a nama a ŋɔ ma. Maa kpak nɔ bɛgim wa dɔŋ ni a li kina maga ni. Ma ya niinbisiwa. Chendaha maa daŋni dɔŋa kinkan a ya jɛrgɛ aa kpak dɔŋa wa siki.

ref: Don't be hateful and insult people just because they are hateful and insult you. Instead, treat everyone with kindness. You are God's chosen ones, and he will bless you. The Scriptures say,
text: Nara tum tum tagna a nyɛ ma, ma ta bur tum tum tagna a nyɛ ba. Ba pini ma zoma a vɛ weri, ma ka ta bur pini ba zoma. Ma ŋɔ ba di Koosa la zi suŋi ma. Koosa funɛ lisi ma a nyɛ o titi ni, o fun a vɔu di ma ya chen, o zi suŋi mau.

ref: “Do you really love life? Do you want to be happy? Then stop saying cruel things and quit telling lies.
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Naa maga la daŋni o nyɛn wa tu sɔɔna, o ta sɛm sɛm tagna a ta suni wɛ a nyɛ nara ni,

ref: Give up your evil ways and do right, as you find and follow the road to peace.
text: a gil o tum tagna aa kitum tum were, chen ni o kpak un o bambi ni a kpak nɔ bɛgim awa nara ni saha maga.

ref: The Lord watches over everyone who obeys him, and he listens to their prayers. But he opposes everyone who does evil.”
text: Koosa la nyini ba naa la tum tum were a la nu ba sumi, amaa o yaa burgi o gantɛlu a chak ba naa la tum tum tagna.”

ref: Can anyone really harm you for being eager to do good deeds?
text: Ma yaa kpak un ma bambe ni a tum tum were kinkan, anti la zi muksi ma?

ref: Even if you have to suffer for doing good things, God will bless you. So stop being afraid and don't worry about what people might do.
text: Hali maa namsɛɛ di ma tum were daha, yɛrɛ ma tusa kaa wa sɔɔni? Chenani nibinsa zi wa ya ma, ma ta nyɛ hansura akoo muksi wa kpak ma.

ref: Honor Christ and let him be the Lord of your life. Always be ready to give an answer when someone asks you about your hope.
text: Ma nyɛ ma kunyan Kristo naa nyɛn ma ni, jirima. Waa ya tu chaan tiina. Nara pɛɛsi ma chenani ma kpak bambi bɛgim un ni, ma saksi a ŋɔ ba saha maga.

ref: Give a kind and respectful answer and keep your conscience clear. This way you will make people ashamed for saying bad things about your good conduct as a follower of Christ.
text: Amaa ma doso a nyɛ ba jirima a ya jɛrgɛ ba wati. Ma kpak tuha kibɛgim awa Koosa ni, chen ni nara pini ma tum were a naa maa tum a nyɛ Kristo ni, zoma, hiisa zi kpak ba naa sɛm chenu.

ref: You are better off to obey God and suffer for doing right than to suffer for doing wrong.
text: O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ ma namsi ma tum were daha, waa bɔɛɛ wa ma namsi ma tum tagna daha.

ref: Christ died once for our sins. An innocent person died for those who are guilty. Christ did this to bring you to God, when his body was put to death and his spirit was made alive.
text: Kristo titi funɛ sou bu bɛgimnɛ ma tum tagna daha, chen ni o zi kpa mau a la Koosa wati. Naa funɛ ya tum were nitumi, waa funɛ chiŋi ya naa ya tum tagna nitumɛ bar chiŋi ni. Ba funɛ kpu o barawa, amaa Koosa siim weri ti funɛ lɛli unu a li o sou ni,

ref: Christ then preached to the spirits that were being kept in prison.
text: a nyɛ o fun sɛm siimsi naa ba fun tɔ dɛ chen.

ref: They had disobeyed God while Noah was building the boat, but God had been patient with them. Eight people went into that boat and were brought safely through the flood.
text: Siimsi nama wa fun tiŋ Koosa nɔ. Ba funɛ nyɛnu saha naa Nowa fun kɔɔri o arŋa saha digi chen. Chen saha ti Koosa kpak ba kanyita, amaa ni funɛ wa su, o fun a yisi nara mɛnasa duŋduŋ ni ti ni.

ref: Those flood waters were like baptism that now saves you. But baptism is more than just washing your body. It means turning to God with a clear conscience, because Jesus Christ was raised from death.
text: Ni naa funɛ wa su Nowa saha ti chen chi chenani nara kpa nire a chaksi ma. Ba wara chaksi ma bara dɔŋwa, amaa maa sumi Koosa wa tu bɛgim, waa la zi yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni maa tiŋ un. Koosa lɛlɛ Kristo a li o sou ni. Chendaha o koti a ya chen.

ref: Christ is now in heaven, where he sits at the right side of God. All angels, authorities, and powers are under his control.
text: O fun lɛli Kristo a zini kaali o tɔhɔn ti a nyɛ o kani o nihɛɛ bar kani ni a dɔ o malaikasi ta maga awa o kpok tima.

ref: Christ suffered here on earth. Now you must be ready to suffer as he did, because suffering shows that you have stopped sinning.
text: Kristo funɛ nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, o funɛ namsiu kinkan. Chendaha ma gbanko kisaksi ma lise ma zi namsiu. Chenani ma namsi zi suŋi mau ma gil ma tum tagna awa ma titi daŋnɛ,

ref: You have already lived long enough like people who don't know God. You were immoral and followed your evil desires. You went around drinking and partying and carrying on. In fact, you even worshiped disgusting idols.
text: Saha digi ma wa ba naa funɛ vɛ zim Koosa, funɛ lagmi dɔŋ ni. Chenani ma funɛ tum tuma a chi ba, chen saha ti funɛ magu. Ma funɛ tum chɔkumsi tuma a la daŋni ma titi daŋnɛ ka ma wa ma chɛnsa lagmi a di diwe a la nyɔ sin di ma dɛɛsi aa la nyɛ vursa wa jirima yɔri yɔri.

ref: Now your former friends wonder why you have stopped running around with them, and they curse you for it.
text: O jɛri ba naa vɛ zim Koosau lalaknɔ di ma wa ba wa lagmi a tum ba tum tagna kinkan. Chendaha baa pini ma zoma.

ref: But they will have to answer to God, who judges the living and the dead.
text: Koosa wa wa di o wa di ba naa kpak miisa awa ba naa sitɛɛ sɛria, o zi pɛɛsi ba chenani ba tumu.

ref: The good news has even been preached to the dead, so that after they have been judged for what they have done in this life, their spirits will live with God.
text: Chendaha Koosa fun nyɛ nara kpa o laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ma ni. Waa la di nara maga sɛria. Chendaha ba siti. Ma bɔnɛ fun siti, amaa ba siimsa kpak miisau a chi Koosa. Ma Kpak Un Ma Bambe Ni Di Ma Tiŋ Koosa Nɛnɛ

ref: Everything will soon come to an end. So be serious and be sensible enough to pray.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha duglɛu. O lawa ma ya bambe timau, chen ni ma zi koti a sumi Koosa wa nɛha niu.

ref: Most important of all, you must sincerely love each other, because love wipes away many sins.
text: O ya wu birigirɛ di ma kpak si sɛm a daŋni dɔŋa kinkan. Yaa daŋni dɔŋa kinkan ka ya dɔŋtima tum tum tagna a du ya ni, o wa zi muksi ya.

ref: Welcome people into your home and don't grumble about it.
text: Ma la dɔŋa aa du dɔŋ damsi ni a ta zulimi.

ref: Each of you has been blessed with one of God's many wonderful gifts to be used in the service of others. So use your gift well.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o papaha kurgɛu a nyɛ ba naa la tiŋ un ni. Ma dɛu kinkin naa o fun nyɛ ma chen a kpa un aa suŋi ma dɔŋtima. Ma maga ya chen, Koosa zi suŋi mau wu damata ni.

ref: If you have the gift of speaking, preach God's message. If you have the gift of helping others, do it with the strength that God supplies. Everything should be done in a way that will bring honor to God because of Jesus Christ, who is glorious and powerful forever. Amen. Suffering for Being a Christian
text: Naa kpak Koosa papaha ti a kaa ŋɔ nara ni o wɛ, ma nyɛ o sɛm sɛm ta wa si sɛm ni. Naa kpak Koosa papaha ti a kaa tum a nyɛ o dɔŋtima ni, ma nyɛ o kpa Koosa kpok ti a tum nɛnɛ a nyɛ ba. Ya ya chen, Koosa zi na pɛgiu a li ya tum maga ni Yesu Kristo sɔn daha. Koosa zi kpak kpoku awa jirima kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔu. Ami. Chenani Nibinsa Zi Namsi Ma Yesu Daha

ref: Dear friends, don't be surprised or shocked that you are going through testing that is like walking through fire.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma ta nyɛ o jɛri ma di ma namsi kinkannɛ lalaknɔ.

ref: Be glad for the chance to suffer as Christ suffered. It will prepare you for even greater happiness when he makes his glorious return.
text: Ma waa la namsi a chi Kristo funɛ namsi. Chendaha ma nyɛ ma tusa sɔɔni. O wa bur wa, ka nara na o jirima awa o chaani kinkan, ma tusa zi wa sɔɔniu hali a la gali.

ref: Count it a blessing when you suffer for being a Christian. This shows that God's glorious Spirit is with you.
text: Nara pini ma zoma maa tiŋ Kristo daha, ma gbanko nyɛ ma tusa sɔɔni. Nan biksi ma di Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri naa kpak o jirima kinkan a du ma bambe ni.

ref: But you deserve to suffer if you are a murderer, a thief, a crook, or a busybody.
text: Ma zimu di ma ta kpu nara a ta ŋmɛɛli a ta tum tum tagna a ta zu nara wɛ ni. O wa ya Koosa tutɔrɛ maa namsi kin nama daha.

ref: Don't be ashamed to suffer for being a Christian. Praise God that you belong to him.
text: Amaa ma ya Yesu Kristo nitiŋɛ. Maa ka saksi ma namsi o daha a ta nyɛ hiisa kpak ma. Maa zaami Koosa di maa tiŋ Kristore.

ref: God has already begun judging his own people. And if his judgment begins with us, imagine how terrible it will be for those who refuse to obey his message. The Scriptures say,
text: Koosa saksi di o di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛriau lalaknɔ. O wa wa, o zi te di ba naa o dɔ chen sɛriau a zi di ba naa se vɛ su o laaboor weri ti chen. O piili ya ni, yɛrɛ o kaa du ba ni?

ref: “If good people barely escape, what will happen to sinners and to others who don't respect God?”
text: Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni, “O bɔmɛɛ di Koosa yisi nibin were, yɛrɛ o zi du tum tagna nitumɛ ba naa vɛ zim un?”

ref: If you suffer for obeying God, you must have complete faith in your faithful Creator and keep on doing right.
text: Chendaha ba naa tiŋ Koosa tutɔ a la na namsi lalaknɔ, o lawa baa tum tum were a kpa ba titi a du Koosa naa funɛ naami kina maga, nin niu. Waa ya wuti tiina.

ref: Church leaders, I am writing to encourage you. I too am a leader, as well as a witness to Christ's suffering, and I will share in his glory when it is shown to us.
text: Mu daŋni mu sumi ma naa ya Yesu nitiŋɛ nihɛɛtau. Mu gbanko ya nihɛɛrɛ, a ya Kristo namsi sɛhɛra tiina. O wa bur wa, mu wa ma zi bɔni o jirima awa o chaani kinkanu.

ref: Just as shepherds watch over their sheep, you must watch over everyone God has placed in your care. Do it willingly in order to please God, and not simply because you think you must. Let it be something you want to do, instead of something you do merely to make money.
text: Ba naa la tiŋ Koosa chi o piisisiwa. Waa nyɛ ma ya ba nigbaŋɛ. Maa nyini ba nɛnɛ. Ma ta nyini ma na nyɔɔra a li ma tum ni, amaa maa kpak un ma bambe ni di ma tum tuma a nyɛ ba kinkan. O wa ya muksirɛ ma tum chen. Ma tutɔrɛ maa tum. Chene Koosa daŋni.

ref: Don't be bossy to those people who are in your care, but set an example for them.
text: Ma ta dɔ ba naa Koosa fun kpa a nyɛ ma aa biksi ba kpok, amaa ma titi tum o tuma a biksi ba chenani ba zi tum.

ref: Then when Christ the Chief Shepherd returns, you will be given a crown that will never lose its glory.
text: Chen ni Kristo naa ya ya nigbaŋi nihɛɛ, wa bur wa, o zi wa bɔ ma chimu ka o zi wa kpa jirima naa vɛ chɔki a nyɛ ma.

ref: All of you young people should obey your elders. In fact, everyone should be humble toward everyone else. The Scriptures say, “God opposes proud people, but he helps everyone who is humble.”
text: Nibaala, chen vɛɛna maa tiŋ ma nihɛɛta nɔɔna a ya jɛrgɛ tima. Ba fun a sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni, “Koosa vɛ ba naa la zer ba titi, amaa o suŋi ba naa ya jɛrgɛu.”

ref: Be humble in the presence of God's mighty power, and he will honor you when the time comes.
text: Ma ya nibin jɛrgɛ a kpa ma bambe a du Koosa naa kpak kpok kinkan ni, chen ni o saha wa tel, o zi zer ma buudahau.

ref: God cares for you, so turn all your worries over to him.
text: Waa la nyini ma. Chendaha ma kpa ma muksi maga a du un ni.

ref: Be on your guard and stay awake. Your enemy, the devil, is like a roaring lion, sneaking around to find someone to attack.
text: Ma dɔm sintana chi gbogumta naa la gba a la wire. O yaa dɛu nandigirɛ di o kpak a se. Chendaha ma ya bambi tima a kidoso aa il.

ref: But you must resist the devil and stay strong in your faith. You know that all over the world the Lord's followers are suffering just as you are.
text: Maa vɛ sintana. Maa nyɛ ma se ka su Koosa kinkan aa chiŋi wa bambi kpok ni. Ma zimu di ma niinbisa ba naa maga se su Koosa aa nyɛn bar damati ni, gbanko namsiu a chi ma.

ref: But God shows undeserved kindness to everyone. That's why he appointed Christ Jesus to choose you to share in his eternal glory. You will suffer for a while, but God will make you complete, steady, strong, and firm.
text: Ma namsi tama wa peti, Koosa naa la suŋi ma kina maga ni, zi kɔɔri ma kpoku kinkan. O funɛ yir mau di ma wa un bɔni o jirima naa vɛ kpak bɔ kinkan, domi ma wa Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: God will be in control forever! Amen. Final Greetings
text: O kpok ti zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami. Pita Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: Silvanus helped me write this short letter, and I consider him a faithful follower of the Lord. I wanted to encourage you and tell you how kind God really is, so you will keep on having faith in him.
text: Silvenus naa ya mu dɔŋtiin wuti tiina, waa suŋi min mu sɔbi tɔn we nɔ a nyɛ ma. Mu tɔn nɔ ni, mu daŋni mu biksi mau a kpami ma di chenani Koosa suŋi ma, o ya wutirɛ. Ma kpak chenani o suŋi ma chen nɛnɛ.

ref: Greetings from the Lord's followers in Babylon. They are God's chosen ones. Mark, who is like a son to me, sends his greetings too.
text: Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa Koosa fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni a li Room ni, zaami mau. Mu niinbe, Maak, gbanko zaami mau.

ref: Give each other a warm greeting. I pray that God will give peace to everyone who belongs to Christ.
text: Maa zaami dɔŋa nɛnɛ. Koosa nyɛ ma maga bara sɔɔna domi ma wa Kristo ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: From Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To everyone who shares with us in the privilege of believing that our God and Savior Jesus Christ will do what is just and fair.
text: Mu waa, Simɔn Pita, naa ya Yesu Kristo tumtumna awa o nitumi, min la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ba naa se su Yesu Kristo a chi ya se su un chen. Ya se suuli ti kaalɛɛ. Waa ya wuti tiina. Chendaha ya se suuli ti awa ma se suuli naa ma kpak un ya kibɛgim. O gbanko ya Koosa a fun yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni.

ref: I pray that God will be kind to you and will let you live in perfect peace! May you keep learning more and more about God and our Lord Jesus. Living as the Lord's Followers
text: Lalaknɔ ma zim Koosa awa ya kunyan Yesu. Chendaha Koosa zi suŋi ma a kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna aa gbaari. Koosa Funɛ Lisi Ma A Nyɛ O Titi Ni

ref: We have everything we need to live a life that pleases God. It was all given to us by God's own power, when we learned he had invited us to share in his wonderful goodness.
text: Waa funɛ yir ya di o wa ya bɔni o jirima awa o chaani kinkan awa chenani o bambi du a fun kpa o kpok awa kin naa maga ya daŋni a nyɛ ya, chen ni ya zi kotiu a tum tum were a tiŋ un nɛnɛ. O ya chene a li chenani ya zim un ni.

ref: God made great and marvelous promises, so his nature would become part of us. Then we could escape our evil desires and the corrupt influences of this world.
text: O vɔwɛ zenu kinkan a kaali kina maga. Ya wa la o kin naa o fun a vɔu di o nyɛ ya chen, ma zi kotiu a chɔ li ma titi daŋnɛ a naa la chɔk tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga, a wa bɔni chenani Koosa duu.

ref: concern for others, and love.
text: a ya nibin were ma dɔŋtima wati a kidaŋni ba.

ref: If you keep growing in this way, it will show that what you know about our Lord Jesus Christ has made your lives useful and meaningful.
text: Ma tum tum were nama a gbaari, a zi nyɛ chenani ma zim ya kunyan Yesu Kristo bukoo zenu a gbaari.

ref: But if you don't grow, you are like someone who is nearsighted or blind, and you have forgotten that your past sins are forgiven.
text: Amaa nara vɛ tum chen, ba chi nyulumiwa ba naa vɛ na nyinbaannɛ a vɛ koti a tiŋ nɛnɛ. O funɛ sugli chenani Koosa fun gil o tum tagna a nyɛ unu.

ref: My friends, you must do all you can to show God has really chosen and selected you. If you keep on doing this, you won't stumble and fall.
text: Mu niinbisa, Koosa fun lisi ma a yir ma. Chendaha ma nyɛ ma se sɛri a gbaari, chen ni ma zi a kpaku nɛnɛ. Ma kpak chen nɛnɛ, ma wa zi kut nyuksi o nyinbaan ti,

ref: Then our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will give you a glorious welcome into his kingdom that will last forever.
text: ka ya kunyan Yesu Kristo zi la ma du koor naa nyɛnɛɛ hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, ni. Waa funɛ yisi ma a li ma tum tagna ni maa tiŋ un.

ref: You are holding firmly to the truth you were given. But I am still going to remind you of these things.
text: Mu zimu di ma piin zim kin nama wɛu ka ma kpak o wuti naa ma la chen ma bambe ni nɛnɛ, amaa mu daŋni ma lisi a wɛu.

ref: In fact, I think I should keep on reminding you until I leave this body.
text: Amaa mu ha vɛɛn nyɛn bar nɔ nan, o deŋlu di mu sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ma, chen ni ma zi bukoo kilisi a wɛu.

ref: And our Lord Jesus Christ has already told me that I will soon leave it behind.
text: Mu zimu di o wa zi vusi ka mu sou a chi chenani ya kunyan Yesu Kristo fun ŋɔ min chen.

ref: This is why I am doing my best to make sure that each of you remembers all of this after I am gone. The Message about the Glory of Christ
text: Mu wa sou, mu tɔn nɔ zi nyɛ ma nyinbaanu maa lisi kin nama maga wɛ. Mu Funɛ Na Kristo Jirima Kinkan Wa Mu Se Ni

ref: When we told you about the power and the return of our Lord Jesus Christ, we were not telling clever stories someone had made up. But with our own eyes we saw his true greatness.
text: Ya funɛ biksi ma chenani ya kunyan Yesu Kristo zi wa bur wa wa o kpok ni, ya wa fun a ŋɔ mura naa kpak nɛharɛ a nyɛ ma. Ya funɛ ŋɔ ma chenani ya fun na o zenu awa o kpok ti kinkanu wa ya se ni.

ref: God, our great and wonderful Father, truly honored him by saying, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ya wa un funɛ nyɛn bi nyuha ni, ya kunyana Koosa fun nyɛ un jirima awa chaani kinkan a nyɛ ta li o bar chaani a ŋɔ, “Mu be daŋni nɛɛnɛ. Waa ya min lɛmlɛm kinkan.”

ref: We were there with Jesus on the holy mountain and heard this voice speak from heaven.
text: Ya wa un funɛ nyɛn dooni, ya fun nu ta ti ka o li buudaha ni.

ref: All of this makes us even more certain that what the prophets said is true. So you should pay close attention to their message, as you would to a lamp shining in some dark place. You must keep on paying attention until daylight comes and the morning star rises in your hearts.
text: Adaha sɛm naa Koosa witinsa fun sɛm saha digi, a ya wuti sɛmwa. O lawa maa nu au nɛnɛ. Ba sɛma chi fitina naa la chaani birsa nɛɛ hali Yesu Kristo wa bur wa. O fitin ti zi wa chiŋi a bar chiŋi niu a nyɛn ma bambe ni.

ref: But you need to realize that no one alone can understand any of the prophecies in the Scriptures.
text: O lawa ma zimu nɛnɛ di chenani Koosa witinsi ta fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni wa fun li ba titi nɛɛ.

ref: The prophets did not think these things up on their own, but they were guided by the Spirit of God.
text: Koosa witinsi ta sɛma wa fun li nibinsa watɛɛ, amaa Koosa siim weri ti funɛ kpa o sɛma a nyɛ ba di ba kpa a biksi nara.

ref: Sometimes false prophets spoke to the people of Israel. False teachers will also sneak in and speak harmful lies to you. But these teachers don't really belong to the Master who paid a great price for them, and they will quickly destroy themselves.
text: Witinsa ba naa funɛ vɛ zim Koosa, fun wa biksi Ju tima saha digi. Nibiksɛ ba naa vɛ zim un gbanko zi lagmi ma niu a sogli a biksi ma. Ba biksi naa vɛ ya wuti a la chɔk, wa tɔ ya kunyana naa siwɛɛ ba daha. Chendaha Koosa zi wa chɔk bau lalaga.

ref: Many people will follow their evil ways and cause others to tell lies about the true way.
text: Nidamata zi wa tiŋ bau aa tum chɔkumsi tuma, chen ni nara zi chɔk Koosa wuti nyinbaan ti sɔnu.

ref: They will be greedy and cheat you with smooth talk. But long ago God decided to punish them, and God doesn't sleep.
text: Nibiksɛ ba naa ya se tima, zi biksi ba titi murau a nyɛ ma, a na nyɔɔra a li ma ni. Amaa Koosa wa zi vusi a wa di ba sɛria a na ba galimsa a ta ba yɔri.

ref: God did not have pity on the angels that sinned. He had them tied up and thrown into the dark pits of hell until the time of judgment.
text: O funɛ kpa o malaikasa ba naa funɛ tum tum tagna, bɔnɛ a du din dam ni. Waa funɛ nyɛ ba kpa ba a ta birsa bar nyɛni ni hali saha naa Koosa wa wa di o di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria.

ref: And during Noah's time, God did not have pity on the ungodly people of the world. He destroyed them with a flood, though he did save eight people, including Noah, who preached the truth.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tima ba naa funɛ nyɛn saha digi aa tum tum tagna kinkan, o fun a kpa ni a su tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ba siti. O fun a yisi Nowa di o ŋɔ nara tima ni di ba bur aa tum tum were. Ni ti funɛ wa su, o wa nara banɔpɛ duŋduŋ funɛ nyaŋi a fosi.

ref: God punished the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah by burning them to ashes, and this is a warning to anyone else who wants to sin.
text: Koosa fun kpa din a du Sɔdɔm awa Gamɔra nara ba naa funɛ tum tum tagna kinkan saha digi, ni di ba maga siti. O funɛ daŋni o biksi ba naa la tum tum tagniwa chenani o zi wa ya a nyɛ ba.

ref: Lot lived right and was greatly troubled by the terrible way those wicked people were living. He was a good man, and day after day he suffered because of the evil things he saw and heard. So the Lord rescued him.
text: Wuzu kam o yaa fun a nau a la nu nara tima tum tagna a naa nyɛ o bambi wi kinkan, wɛ.

ref: This shows that the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their sufferings and to punish evil people while they wait for the day of judgment.
text: Koosa zim chenani o yisi ba naa la tiŋ un nɛnɛ a li ba namsi niu. O gbanko zimu chenani o chiŋsi ba naa la tum tum tagna hali saha naa o wa wa di o di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria a lɛrgi ba diŋsa.

ref: The Lord is especially hard on people who disobey him and don't think of anything except their own filthy desires. They are reckless and proud and are not afraid of cursing the glorious beings in heaven.
text: O zi lɛrgi ba naa la tiŋ ba titi daŋnɛ aa vɛ tiŋ o nɔ, diŋsau hali a vɛ weri. Nibiksɛ na kpak ba bambewa a zer ba titi. Ba wara nyɛ o malaikasi ta wa jirima, amaa ba ka pini ba zomwa.

ref: Although angels are more powerful than these evil beings, even the angels don't dare to accuse them to the Lord.
text: Koosa malaikasa ba naa kpak kpok kinkan a kaali nibiksɛ ta kpok, ba wara fiksi akoo a pini ba Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: These people are no better than senseless animals that live by their feelings and are born to be caught and killed. They speak evil of things they don't know anything about. But their own corrupt deeds will destroy them.
text: Amaa nara nama chi sela zenewa a naa nyɛn dooni nara zi kpaksi a kpu. A wara lisi a chi nibinsa. Kin nawara ba vɛ zim a munwa, ba pini. Chendaha Koosa gbanko zi chɔk bau a chi sel na chɔk chen.

ref: They have done evil, and they will be rewarded with evil. They think it is fun to have wild parties during the day. They are immoral, and the meals they eat with you are spoiled by the shameful and selfish way they carry on.
text: Ba funɛ nyɛ nidamata namsiu. Chendaha Koosa zi nyɛ ba na namsiu. Ba tusa sɔɔniu baa tum tum tagna a su chenani ba bara daŋni nara maga suwachak ni. Ma wa ba lagmɛɛ a di diwe, ba yaa ya chene a nyɛ hiisa kpak ma.

ref: All they think about is having sex with someone else's husband or wife. There is no end to their wicked deeds. They trick people who are easily fooled, and their minds are filled with greedy thoughts. But they are headed for trouble!
text: Saha maga ba dɛu nihaana a daŋni ba, chen ni ba wa ba zi tum chɔkumsi tumau. Baa tum tum tagniwa a vɛ puŋi. Ba kpa ba naa vɛ kpak kpok a mo ba lo. Ba ya se timwa kinkan. Chendaha Koosa fun ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ ba.

ref: They have left the true road and have gone down the wrong path by following the example of the prophet Balaam. He was the son of Beor and loved what he got from being a crook.
text: Ba vɛu di ba wa zi tiŋ Koosa wuti nyinbaan ti a vɛ zim doonani ba laali. Ba chi Belam, Bɔsɔ be, naa funɛ nyɛn saha digirɛ, a la daŋni malɛ naa o na a li o tum tagna daha.

ref: But a donkey corrected him for this evil deed. It spoke to him with a human voice and made him stop his foolishness.
text: Amaa kokum fun wa ŋɔ un a chi nibine ta a burgi o nyuha di o gil o galɛŋ tuma.

ref: These people are like dried up water holes and clouds blown by a windstorm. The darkest part of hell is waiting for them.
text: Nibiksɛ nama chi bugul hulɛwa awa dobaala nawara vɛ kpak dooni. Koosa zi wa kpa bau a du birsa bar nyɛni naa o kpa chiŋsi ba kinkan, niu.

ref: They brag out loud about their stupid nonsense. And by being vulgar and crude, they trap people who have barely escaped from living the wrong kind of life.
text: Ba sɛma la biksi ya di ba ya womniwa a la daŋni sɔna. Saha maga ba yaa sɛm sɛm lɛmawa a nyɛ nara ni a mur ba di baa tum chɔkumsi tuma. Nara nama piili di ba tiŋ Koosa nyinbaan ti a gil ba naa la tum tum tagna.

ref: They promise freedom to everyone. But they are merely slaves of filthy living, because people are slaves of whatever controls them.
text: Nibiksɛ ta la biksi nara di ba naa nuɛɛ a tiŋ ba, ba lisi ba a ta, amaa ba titi ya tum tagna a naa la chɔk ba, yosiwa. Kinkin naa nyaŋi nibine, nyɛ o burgi kin ti yoma.

ref: When they learned about our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they escaped from the filthy things of this world. But they are again caught up and controlled by these filthy things, and now they are in worse shape than they were at first.
text: Nara wa zim ya kunyan Yesu Kristo a nyɛ o yisi ba a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga tiŋi ni ka ba burgi a zu tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim tum tagna ni a nyaŋi ba, chenani ba nyɛn taginɛɛ a kaali chenani ba fun nyɛn.

ref: They would have been better off if they had never known about the right way. Even after they knew what was right, they turned their backs on the holy commandments they were given.
text: Ba funɛ te wa zim Koosa wuti nyinbaan ti a vɛ Koosa chiksɛ ta a li a gantɛl ni. Ba fu vɛ wa zim o wuti nyinbaan ti, chen fu bɔɛɛ.

ref: What happened to them is just like the true saying, “A dog will come back to lick up its own vomit. A pig that has been washed will roll in the mud.”
text: Chenani ba fun tum biksi ya di jɛɛgɛ ta ya wutirɛ. Ya ŋɔ, “Vaha yaa ok, a bur la di un. Kurchu naa ba sɔ ni, yaa bur a la chɔ vɛrɛ ni.”

ref: what God's prophets said would happen. You must never forget what the holy prophets taught in the past. And you must remember what the apostles told you our Lord and Savior has commanded us to do.
text: Mu daŋni maa lisi sɛm naa Koosa witinsi ta fun sɛm saha digi wɛwa awa chenani ya kunyana naa funɛ yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni yaa tiŋ un, fun a biksi o nitumɛ. Ba fun kpa chenani ba nu a wa biksi ma.

ref: But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you
text: O lawa ma zimu di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa dugli, nibinsa ba naa yaa tiŋ ba tutɔ daŋnɛ, zi wau. Ba zi wa mɔmsi mau,

ref: and say, “Didn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.”
text: a ŋɔ, “Ma kunyan ti wa fun vɔu di o zi bur wauu? Leene o ka? Saha naa Koosa fun naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga hali wa zinan, wu digi ha wa chɛrgi.”

ref: They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water.
text: Ba vɛu di ba wa zi zim di saha digi Koosa funɛ sɛm sɛmwa a naami buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. O fun nyɛ tɛɛnɛ si nii ni,

ref: Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood.
text: a fun bu kpa ni ti a su tɛɛnɛ maga o chɔk.

ref: But God has commanded the present heavens and earth to remain until the day of judgment. Then they will be set on fire, and ungodly people will be destroyed.
text: Lalaknɔ Koosa chiŋɛ buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga hali a wuzu naa o kaa di ba naa la tum tum tagna sɛria. O sɛria diili wa peti, o zi kpa dinu a chɔk a maga.

ref: Dear friends, don't forget that for the Lord one day is the same as
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma ta sugli di Koosa na wuzu bɛgimnɛ o chi bun tusuk bɛgimnɛ. O ka na bun tusuga o chi wuzu bɛgim.

ref: The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
text: Nara bɔnɛ lisi di ya kunyan ti vusiu a vɛ tum chenani o fun vɔwi. O daŋni ma gil ma tum tagniwa aa tiŋ un, chen ni o wa zi wa chɔk ma. Chendaha o kpak ma kanyita.

ref: The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
text: Ya kunyan ti wa wa, o kaa wa chi chenani ŋmɛɛr ya wa chene. Chen wuzu ti wa tel, o zi jimu a chi dinful. Buudaha zi wa nyigmiu ka din wa di tɛɛnɛ maga.

ref: Everything will be destroyed. So you should serve and honor God by the way you live.
text: Wuzu digi buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga zi wa chɔku a chi chen, nibin baŋwa ma kaa wa chi? Ma ya tu chaan tima a kitiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ,

ref: You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat.
text: a pɔ di ma na Koosa waali tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga sɛria diili a kpak un ma bambe ni di o wa lalaga. Chen wuzu ti din zi wa chɔk buudahau.

ref: But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ vɔu di o zi naami buudaha fali awa tɛɛnɛu. Ba naa maga nyɛn a ni wa zi bu koti a tum tum tagna. Ya kpak un ya bambe ni di ya pɔ un o ya.

ref: My friends, while you are waiting, you should make certain the Lord finds you pure, spotless, and living at peace.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, chenani ma ya chen, o lawa ma kpak se sɛmu awa tu chaan tima a kpak sɔn weri Koosa suwachak ni ka ma wa un kpak nɔ bɛgim.

ref: Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him.
text: Ma lisi chenani ya kunyan ti nyɛ narawa nyinbaan lalaknɔ di ba gil ba tum tagna aa tiŋ un. Koosa funɛ kpa o zimarɛ a nyɛ ya niinbe daŋni Pɔɔl ni o fun sɔbi chen wɛ a nyɛ ma.

ref: Paul talks about these same things in all his letters, but part of what he says is hard to understand. Some ignorant and unsteady people even destroy themselves by twisting what he said. They do the same thing with other Scriptures too.
text: O sɔbi kin nama wɛu a li o tɔna maga ni a nyɛ nara ni. O bɔmu di nara zim Pɔɔl biksi ti bɔnɛ mun. Chendaha ba naa vɛ zim Koosa a ya bambi bilɛ tima, bɔnɛ kpa un a chɛrgi o vɛ ya wuti ba biksi nara. Ba gbanko kpa Koosa sɛm tɔn ti a chɛrgi a biksi nara. Koosa zi wa chɔk bau.

ref: My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance.
text: Amaa, mu bi daŋnɛ, ma piin zim kin nama wɛu. Maa il a ta nyɛ nibiksɛ ba naa la chɔk Koosa laaboor weri ti, wa mur ma a burgi ma Koosa wuti tiŋi ni ma chel ma bar kpok ni.

ref: Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
text: Ma ka nyɛ ya kunyan Yesu Kristo suŋi ma ma wa zim un nɛnɛ aa gbaari. Ma nyɛ ya nyɛ naa funɛ yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni, jirima kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: The Word that gives life was from the beginning, and this is the one our message is about. Our ears have heard, our eyes have seen, and our hands have touched this Word.
text: Min waa, Jɔn, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a biksi ma nandigi wɛ. Waa funɛ nyɛn dooni a li piili ti ni, a ya miisa maga tiina. Ya funɛ koti a nu o tau a na un awa ya se ni a nyini un a kpa ya nisa a gbaŋsi un. O funɛ biksi ya Koosa wɛu.

ref: The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa miisa maga tiin ti a tum tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Ya funɛ na unu, a ya o sɛhɛra tima a la ŋɔ ma Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ wɛ. Waa funɛ nyɛn Koosa ni saha maga, ka o fun kpa un a biksi ya.

ref: We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.
text: Ya daŋni ya biksi ma kin naa maga o yarɛ ya fun na a nu, chen ni ya wa ma zi lagmiu a li sɛm nan ni. Ya wa Koosa awa o be Yesu Kristo ya maga kpak nɔ bɛgimnɛ.

ref: We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy. God Is Light
text: Ya daŋni ma tu sɔɔniu kinkan. Chendaha ya sɔbi nan wɛ a nyɛ ma. Koosa Chi Fitinne Aa Chaani

ref: Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
text: Nannɛ Yesu fun biksi ya o titi wɛ ka ya gbanko biksi ma. Koosa chi fitinne aa chaani. Birsa tama wa to un ni.

ref: If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth.
text: Ya ŋɔɛɛ di ya wa un ya nibɛgimnɛ ka ya tum birsa tuma, ya suni wɛwa. Ya wa tiŋ wuti nyinbaan.

ref: But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away.
text: Amaa ya nyɛ ya tusa chaani a chi chenani Koosa tuha chaani chen, ya wa ya dɔŋtima zi kpak nɔ bɛgimu ka Koosa be Yesu chɛl zi yisi ya doŋ magau a li ya ni.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Ya sɛmɛɛ di ya wara tum tum tagna, yaa mur ya titirɛ. Ya wa kpak wuti.

ref: If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
text: Ya sɛmɛɛ di ya ha wa tum tum tagna, ya yir Koosa nusun tiinnɛ. Ya wa zim o chiksɛ ta.

ref: My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us.
text: Mu bisa, mu sɔbi nannɛ a nyɛ ma, chen ni ma zi ɔm ma tum tagnau. Amaa ya nandigi tum tum tagna, ya kpak naa la zi sumi ya kunyana Koosa a nyɛ yau. Waa ya Yesu Kristo naa ya tu chaan tiin ti.

ref: When we obey God, we are sure we know him.
text: Yaa tiŋ Koosa nɔ sɛma maga, ya sir zim di ya zim unu.

ref: But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts.
text: Nar ŋɔɛɛ di o zim Koosau, amaa o wa tiŋ o nɔ sɛma, o suni wɛwa. O wa kpak wuti.

ref: We truly love God only when we obey him as we should, and then we know we belong to him.
text: Nar tiŋ o nɔ, waa sir a daŋni un nɛnɛ. Yaa tiŋ chen, ya sir zi zimu di ya wa un ya nibɛgimnɛ.

ref: If we say we are his, we must follow the example of Christ. The New Commandment
text: Nar ŋɔ di o nyɛn Koosa nɛɛ, o tiŋ un a chi o be Yesu funɛ tiŋ un chen. O Lawa Yaa Tiŋ Koosa Chiksi Faliu

ref: My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, o wa ya chiksi falirɛ mu sɔbi a nyɛ ma nan. O ha vɛɛn ya kibuni naa ma fun nu a li o piili ti ni chene.

ref: But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
text: Amaa o vɛɛn ya chiksi falirɛ, domi lalaknɔ birsa saha kaalu, ka chaani saha ka wa di o wa chiŋi o bar chiŋi ni. Yesu wa ma tiŋ chiksi fali nɔwa.

ref: If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark.
text: Nar ŋɔɛɛ di o nyɛn bar chaani nɛɛ a ha o dɔŋtima, o ha vɛɛn nyɛn birsa nɛɛ.

ref: But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them.
text: Amaa naa la daŋni o dɔŋtima, o waa nyɛn bar chaani ti nɛɛ. Kundɔŋ wa to dooni a la zi nyɛ o chel.

ref: If we hate others, we are living and walking in the dark. We don't know where we are going, because we can't see in the dark.
text: Naa ha o dɔŋtima, o nyɛn birsa nɛɛ. O wa zim doonani oo la. Birsa ti tɔ o sewa ka o burgi nyulum.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because your sins have been forgiven in the name of Christ.
text: Bichɛsa, mu sɔbi nannɛ a nyɛ ma, domi Koosa funɛ gil ma tum tagna a nyɛ mau Yesu daha.

ref: Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you have defeated the evil one.
text: Nibahɛɛta, mu sɔbi nannɛ a nyɛ ma domi ma waa piin zim naa funɛ nyɛn dooni a li piili ti niu. Bunɔɔna, mu sɔbi nannɛ a nyɛ ma domi ma funɛ nyaŋi sintanau.

ref: Children, I am writing you, because you have known the Father. Parents, I am writing you, because you have known the one who was there from the beginning. Young people, I am writing you, because you are strong. God's message is firm in your hearts, and you have defeated the evil one.
text: Nibahɛɛta, ma waa piin zim naa funɛ nyɛn dooni a li piili ti niu. Bunɔɔna, mu sɔbi nannɛ a nyɛ ma domi ma kpak kpoku a vɛɛn kpak Koosa sɛma ma bambe gbanko ni. Chendaha ma fun nyaŋi sintana.

ref: Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father.
text: Ma ta daŋni di ma tiŋ chenani tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga tiŋ. Maa daŋni tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga kina, ya kunyana Koosa daŋni ti wa to ma bambe ni.

ref: Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father.
text: Kin nawara nibinsa bara daŋni awa kin nawara ba se daŋni awa kin nawara ba daŋni ba kpak a na sɔn, a nama wa ya ya kunyana Koosa kina. A ya tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga kinawa.

ref: The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever. The Enemy of Christ
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa kin nawara nara daŋni, a maga wa zi vusi, amaa naa tiŋ Koosa tutɔ, waa la zi wa na o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Yesu Dɔm Zen Wɛ.

ref: Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here.
text: Mu bisa, tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha duglɛu. Ma funɛ nu di Yesu dɔm zen zi wa wau chen saha ti. Amaa Yesu dɔm damata piin liu a wa. Chendaha ya zim di tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha ti duglɛu.

ref: These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
text: Ba bɔnɛ funɛ nyɛn ya wati, amaa ba wa fun ya ya walɛ. Ba fu funɛ ya ya walɛ, ba fu zi nyɛn ya watiu. Amaa chenani ba chɔ a li ya wati chen, biksi di ba wa ya ya walɛ.

ref: Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand.
text: Amaa Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a du ma bambe niu ka ma maga kpak zima.

ref: I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth.
text: Mu sɔbi tɔn nɔwa a nyɛ ma, o wa ya di ma wa zim wuti ti. Ma zim wuti ti nɛnɛ, ma zi zim di nusuna wara li un ni.

ref: And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son.
text: Naa vɛ la nɔɔni di Yesu ya Kristo naa Koosa fun tum chen, o ya nusun tiinnɛ. Naa vɛ Koosa awa o be Yesu, o ya Kristo dɔmnɛ.

ref: If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father.
text: Naa vɛ la nɔɔni di Yesu ya Koosa be, o gban vɛ Koosau. Naa la nɔɔni wa Koosa be wɛ, o vɛɛn zim o kunyana Koosau.

ref: Keep thinking about the message you first heard, and you will always be one in your heart with the Son and with the Father.
text: Ma ta suglɛ biksi naa ma fun te nu chen. Ma nyɛ chen nyɛn ma bambe ni, ma wa ya kunyana Koosa awa o be Yesu zi ya nibɛgimu.

ref: I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you.
text: Mu sɔbi tɔn nɔwa a nyɛ ma a ŋɔ ma ba naa la daŋni ba mur ma ma li Yesu wɛ ni.

ref: But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do. Children of God
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a du ma bambe ni. Chendaha nara wa biksi ma a gbaari o biksi ti ni, o ya yɔrirɛ. Koosa siim weri ti ya naa la zi biksi ma kina maga nyinbaan. O biksi maga ya wutirɛ. Nusuna wa to un ni. Adaha ma nyɛ ma wa Yesu ya nibɛgim a chi chenani Koosa siim weri ti fun a biksi ma chen.

ref: Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame.
text: Mu bisa, maa ya chen. Yesu wa bur wa, hansura wa zi wa kpak ya. Hiisa gbanko wa zi wa kpak ya, chen ni ya wa zi wa chɔ li o wati chen saha ti.

ref: You know Christ always does right and everyone who does right is a child of God.
text: Ya zimu di Yesu wɛ maga deŋlu. Chendaha ya zim di ba naa maga tuma deŋlee, ba ya Koosa bisiwa.

ref: Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are.
text: Ma lisi chenani ya kunyana Koosa daŋni ya kinkan. Hali o yir ya o bisa. Ba naa la tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga nyinbaan, wa zim un. Chendaha ba vɛ zim ya.

ref: My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ya ya Koosa bisawa lalaknɔ. Chenani ya zi wa du wuzu digi, Koosa ha wa biksi ya. Amaa Yesu wa bur wa, ya zi wa du a chi unu. Ya se zi wa na unu wuzu digi a chi chenani o du chen.

ref: Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law.
text: Ba naa maga la tum tum tagna, ba chɔk Koosa chire. Tum tagna maga ya Koosa chire.

ref: You know Christ came to take away our sins. He isn't sinful,
text: Ma zimu di Yesu funɛ wau di o wa yisi nara tum tagna maga a ta. O wa tum tum tagin hali tama.

ref: and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
text: Ba naa maga wa Yesu ya nibɛgim, ba tum tum tagin, ba tusa wa yaa sɔɔni tum tagin ti daha. Ba naa maga yaa tum tum tagna, ba tum chene domi ba wa zim un.

ref: Children, don't be fooled. Anyone who does right is good, just like Christ himself.
text: Mu bisa, ma ta nyɛ nandigi mur ma. Ba naa maga la tum tum were, ba ya tu chaan timawa a chi Yesu.

ref: Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done.
text: Ba naa maga la tum tum tagna, ba ya sintana bisiwa. Sintana funɛ tum tum tagnau a li piili ti ni hali wa zinan. Chendaha Koosa fun nyɛ o be wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a wa chɔk sintana tuma.

ref: God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning.
text: Ba naa maga ya Koosa bisa, ba tum tum tagin, ba tusa wa yaa sɔɔni tum tagin ti daha. Koosa fun kpa o mi fali ti a du ba ni di ba ya o bisa. Chendaha ba tusa wara sɔɔni.

ref: You can tell God's children from the devil's children, because those who belong to the devil refuse to do right or to love each other. Love Each Other
text: Chene ma zi zim ba naa ya Koosa bisa awa ba naa ya sintana bisa. Ba naa vɛ tum tum were awa ba naa vɛ daŋni ba dɔŋtima, ba wa ya Koosa bisa. O Lawa Yaa Daŋni Ya Dɔŋ Timau

ref: From the beginning you were told we must love each other.
text: Ya te lagmi Koosa laaboor weri naa ma fun nu dɔŋ ni, o wu birigi ya di yaa daŋni ya dɔŋtima.

ref: Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the devil and murdered his own brother. Why did he murder him? He did it because his brother was good, and he was evil.
text: Ma ta wa chi Keen naa funɛ ya sintana be chen a kpu o niinbe. Ma zim kinkin naa nyuhu ni o fun kpu o niinbe tiuu? O funɛ tum tum tagniwa ka o niinbe ti ka tum tum were. Chendaha o fun kpu un.

ref: My friends, don't be surprised if the people of this world hate you.
text: Mu niinbisa, ba naa la tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga nyinbaan ha ma, ma ta nyɛ chen jɛri ma.

ref: Our love for each other proves we have gone from death to life. But if you don't love each other, you are still under the power of death.
text: Chenani yaa daŋni dɔŋa biksi ya di Koosa funɛ lisi ya a li sintana nin ni a nyɛ ya o mi fali ti. Ba naa maga vɛ daŋni ba dɔŋtima, sintana ha vɛɛn dɔ ba.

ref: If you hate each other, you are murderers, and we know murderers do not have eternal life.
text: Ba naa ha ba dɔŋtima, o chi ba ya nara nikpuulɛwa. Ma zimu di nara nikpuuli wa kpak Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ, ti.

ref: We know what love is because Jesus gave his life for us. This is why we must give our lives for each other.
text: Chenani Yesu fun sou ya daha biksi ya wuti daŋni. O weru wa yaa daŋni ya dɔŋtima chenu, hali a sou ba daha.

ref: If we have all we need and see one of our own people in need, we must have pity on that person, or else we cannot say we love God.
text: Ma ya kin tima a na ma dɔŋtima ka ba namsi ka ma vɛ di ma wa zi suŋi ba, yɛrɛ Koosa daŋni ti kaa nyɛn ma ni?

ref: Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ya zimu di yaa tiŋ chenani o daŋni ya tiŋi, ya zi kpak bambi kpokau o suwachak ni.

ref: He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him.
text: Ya sumi un kundɔŋ, o zi nyɛ yau, domi yaa tiŋ o nɔ sɛmwa a la tum tum naa la sɔɔni o tuha.

ref: God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do.
text: Nannɛ ya zi tum a sɔɔni o tuha. O lawa ya nyɛ ya se su o be Yesu Kristou a kidaŋni dɔŋa a chi chenani o fun ŋɔ ya chen.

ref: If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
text: Ba naa maga tiŋ Koosa nɔ sɛma, nyɛn un ni ka o ka nyɛn ba ni. Ya zimu di nan ya wutirɛ, domi waa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a du ya ni.

ref: Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world,
text: Mu chɛn daŋnɛ, nara ŋɔɛɛ di ba ya Koosa witinsiwa a sɛm wa o kpok ni, ma ta nyɛ ma se su ba lalaga. Witinsa ba naa ya nusun tima, kinkan piin pisiu a kaali bawu maga ni. Chendaha maa doso ba ni.

ref: But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
text: Naa vɛ la nɔɔni Yesu wɛ nan ni, Koosa siim weri ti wa to un ni. O ya Kristo dɔmnɛ. Ma funɛ nu di chen tima zi wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ magau. Ba piin wau.

ref: Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world.
text: Mu bisa, Koosa dɔ ma a fun kpa o siim weri ti a nyɛ ma. O kpok naa nyɛn ma ni chen, waa kpok kaali Kristo dɔm kpok naa nyɛn ba ni.

ref: These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language.
text: Witinsa ba naa ya nusun tima awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ya nibɛgimnɛ. Chendaha ba nu ba sɛma,
