ref: We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
text: amaa Koosa ka dɔ ya. Ba naa maga zim un, ba waa la nu ya sɛma. Ba naa maga vɛ zim Koosa, ba wa zi nu ya sɛma. Chendaha ya zi zim ba naa ya wuti tima awa nusuna. Koosa Ya Daŋni Tiina

ref: My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him.
text: Mu chɛn daŋnɛ, ma nyɛ yaa daŋni dɔŋa. Daŋni li Koosa watɛɛ. Naa la daŋni o dɔŋtiina, waa ya Koosa be aa zim un.

ref: God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him.
text: Koosa ya daŋni tiina. Chendaha naa vɛ daŋni o dɔŋtiina, o wa zim un.

ref: God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life.
text: Koosa funɛ tum o be bɛgim tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, chen ni ya zi kotiu a la o mi fali ti. Chen biksi ya chenani Koosa daŋni ya.

ref: Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven.
text: Ya laata te a daŋni Koosa, amaa waa te o daŋni ya. O funɛ tum o beu di o wa sou a lisi ya a li Koosa paan ni. Chen biksi ya o wuti daŋni.

ref: Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
text: Mu chɛn daŋnɛ, Koosa daŋni ya nan, o weru di ya gbanko kidaŋni dɔŋa.

ref: No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
text: Nandigi ha wa zin na Koosa. Amaa yaa daŋni dɔŋa, Koosa nyɛn ya watiu a la suŋi ya, chen ni ya zi daŋni unu aa gbaari.

ref: God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us.
text: Ya zimu di o nyɛn ya watiu ka ya gbanko nyɛn un ni, domi o siim weri naa o fun kpa a nyɛ ya, waa kut nyɛn ya wati.

ref: God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him.
text: Ya ya Koosa sɛhɛra timwa, domi ya funɛ na o be naa o fun tum a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni di o wa yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni yaa tiŋ un.

ref: God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God
text: Naa la nɔɔni di Yesu ya Koosa bere, Koosa nyɛn tiin watiu ka o gbanko nyɛn Koosa ni.

ref: and are sure God loves us. God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us.
text: Ya zimu nɛnɛ di oo daŋni yau ka ya se su di waa ya daŋni tiina. Naa la daŋni o dɔŋtiina nyɛn Koosa wati ka Koosa nyɛn un ni.

ref: If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment.
text: Ya wa Yesu Kristo lagmi nyɛn ka o suŋi ya, chen ni ya zi daŋni unu hali o wa peti. O ya chene, chen ni ya wa zi wa kpak hansura a wuzu naa Koosa zi wa di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria.

ref: A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
text: Nandigi daŋni ya hali o wa peti, ya wa zi ɔm chenani o zi wa ya ya. Ya ɔm un, chen biksi di ya se wa su di o daŋni yau hali o wa peti.

ref: We love because God loved us first.
text: Ya daŋni unu domi waa te a biksi ya di o daŋni yau.

ref: But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see?
text: Nandigi ŋɔ di o daŋni Koosau a ha o dɔŋtiina, o ya nusun tiinnɛ. O vɛ koti a daŋni o dɔŋtiina naa oo na, yɛrɛ o kaa ya a daŋni Koosa naa o vɛ naali?

ref: The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
text: Nannɛ Kristo fun biksi ya. Naa la daŋni Koosa, o lawa o daŋni o dɔŋtima gbankou.

ref: If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children.
text: Naa si su di Yesu ya Kristo naa Koosa fun tum chen, chen tiina ya Koosa bisi ta digirɛ. Ma daŋni nibine, ma zi daŋni o be ti gbankou.

ref: If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children.
text: Ya daŋni Koosa aa tiŋ o nɔ, ya zimu di ya zi daŋni o bisi ta gbankou.

ref: We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
text: Naa la daŋni Koosa zi tiŋ o nɔu. Chenani o daŋni ya tum wa bɔm.

ref: Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory.
text: Ba naa maga ya Koosa bisa ka ba se su un nɛnɛ zi nyaŋi ba tutɔ daŋnɛu.

ref: No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God. Who Jesus Is
text: Ba naa se su di Yesu ya Koosa bere duŋduŋ la zi ya chen. Chenani Yesu Kristo Sɛhɛrasa Tora Sɛm

ref: Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful.
text: Yesu Kristo funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga, ba wa fun chaksi un wa ni duŋduŋnɛ. Ba funɛ vɛɛn a kpu unu a gbaari ka o chɛl li. Koosa siim weri naa ya wuti tiina, waa ya sɛhɛra a li o chaksi awa o kpuuli ti ni.

ref: They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
text: siim weri ti, Yesu chaksi ti awa o chɛl ti. Sɛhɛrasa tora nama ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son.
text: Ya piinsa nu nara sɛhɛrau. Amaa Koosa be sɛhɛra ti ya kizenzen a kaali nibinsa sɛhɛra kinkan.

ref: If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar.
text: Naa si su Koosa be ti, o be ti sɛhɛra nyɛn un ni, ka o zim o be ti wɛ nɛnɛ. Naa si vɛ su Koosa, waa la ŋɔ di Koosa ya nusun tiinnɛ, domi o wa la nɔɔni a du Koosa sɛhɛra awa o be ti wɛ ni.

ref: God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son.
text: Koosa sɛhɛra ti nɛɛnɛ. Waa funɛ kpa o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ a nyɛ ya. Nan mi fali nɔ nyɛn o be ti nin ni.

ref: And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life. Knowing about Eternal Life
text: Naa wa Koosa be ti ya nibɛgim, waa kpak chen mi fali ti. Naa wa Koosa be ti vɛ ya nibɛgim, o wa kpak chen mi fali ti. Koosa Funɛ Kpa O Mi Fali A Nyɛ Ya

ref: All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life.
text: Mu sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ ma di ma naa se su Koosa be ti zi zimu di ma kpak Koosa mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔu.

ref: We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him.
text: Ya kpak bambe kpok di ya sumi un kin naa nyɛ o tuha sɔɔni, o zi nu ya watiu.

ref: And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
text: Ya zim di o piin nu ya sɛmau, kin nawara ya sumi o wati, o zi nyɛ yau.

ref: Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly.
text: Ma na ma dɔŋtima ka ba tum tum tagna naa Koosa zi vɛ lɛrgi ba diŋsau ba siti, ma sumi un ba gil ba tum tagna ka o kpa o mi fali ti a nyɛ ba. Ba duŋduŋnɛ mu ŋɔ ma, di ma sumi Koosa a nyɛ ba chen. Nara tum tum tagna naa Koosa zi wa lɛrgi ba diŋsau ba siti, ma ta sumi Koosa a nyɛ ba.

ref: Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
text: Kin nawara vɛ deŋli ya wu tagniwa, amaa ma tum tum tagna bɔnɛ, Koosa zi lɛrgi ma diŋsau ma siti.

ref: We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
text: Ya zimu di ba naa ya Koosa bisa chen, tum tum tagna, ba tusa wa zi sɔɔni. Koosa be ti la suŋi ba, chen ni sintana wa kikoti a la ba kpok.

ref: We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
text: Ya zimu di ya ya Koosa bisiwa. Ba naa maga vɛ ya Koosa bisa, ba waa nyɛn sintana nin ni.

ref: We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
text: Ya zimu di Koosa be Yesu ti funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga di o wa lɛli ya sewa, chen ni ya zi zim naa ya wuti tiinau. Ya wa o be Yesu Kristo ti ya nibɛgimnɛ. Waa ya Koosa wuti. Waa la nyɛ ya o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: From the church leader. To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth.
text: Mu waa, nihɛɛ, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ nihaan naa Koosa fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni awa o bisa ba naa mu daŋni kinkan. Mu duŋduŋ laata la daŋni ma chen, amaa ba naa maga zim Koosa wuti, ba daŋni ma chene,

ref: We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
text: domi wuti naa nyɛn ya maga bambe ni. Wuti ti zi kut nyɛnu hali a vɛ peti.

ref: I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love. Truth and Love
text: Mu zaami mau. Ya kunyana Koosa awa o be Yesu Kristo zi suŋi ya magau a kiɔm ya siki a nyɛ ya bara sɔɔna, chen ni ya zi tiŋ wuti tiu nɛnɛ aa kidaŋni dɔŋa. O Lawa Ya Tiŋ Koosa Wuti Biksiu A Kidaŋni Dɔŋa

ref: I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do.
text: Mu tuha sɔɔnɛu kinkan domi mu funɛ na ka i bisi ta bɔnɛ tiŋ wuti ti a chi chenani ya kunyana Koosa fun ŋɔ di ya tiŋ chen.

ref: Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do.
text: Adaha mu sumi hinu ii tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ naa ya fun nu a li o piili ti ni chen. O wa ya chiksi falirɛ. O ŋɔ di o weru wa yaa daŋni dɔŋau,

ref: Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
text: aa kitiŋ o nɔ. O chiksi naa ŋɔ ya di o lawa yaa daŋni dɔŋau, ma gbanko funɛ nu o wɛu a li o piili ti ni.

ref: Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ.
text: Lalaknɔ nusun tima kinkan nyɛnɛɛ. Ba wa la nɔɔni di Yesu Kristo funɛ wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a wa burgi nibine. Ba naa vɛ la nɔɔni chen, ba ya nusun timwa a ya o dɔma.

ref: So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward.
text: Ma nyini ma titi nɛnɛ, chen ni ma wa zi wa chel a chɔk ya tuma. Ma ya chen, ma zi wa na papaha naa Koosa kaa wa kpa nyɛ ma wuzu digiu.

ref: Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you.
text: Nandigi kpa wu digi a gbaari chenani ya fun biksi ma Kristo wɛ chen, Koosa wa to chen tiin ni. Ma kpak chenani ya funɛ biksi ma Kristo wɛ chen, Koosa awa o be Yesu zi nyɛn ma niu.

ref: If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them.
text: Nandigi wa ma wati a wa gbaari chenani ya fun biksi ma Kristo wɛ chen, ma ta la un amaa ma ŋɔ un di o la. Ma ta tiŋ un li ta nyinbaan ni.

ref: Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds. Final Greetings
text: Ba naa nyɛ un jirima, chen tima gbanko ya tum tagin nitumɛwa a chi un. Jɔn Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
text: Mu kpak wu damatiwa a kaa ŋɔ ma, amaa mu wa zi sɔbi a a dau lalaknɔ. Mu daŋni mu wa ma watɛɛ, chen ni ya zi koti a na dɔŋau a sɛm a wɛ. Doone ya maga tusa zi sɔɔni hali a wa peti.

ref: Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
text: Ma towe bisa naa Koosa fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni chen, ba zaami mau.

ref: From the church leader. To my dear friend Gaius. I love you because we follow the truth,
text: Mu waa, nihɛɛ, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ mu chɛn, Gaiyus, naa mu daŋni wuti.

ref: dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit.
text: Mu chɛna, mu sumi Koosau di i na alaafɛ, chen ni i tuma zi weru. Mu zimu di ii tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ,

ref: It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth.
text: domi Yesu nitiŋɛ ta bɔnɛ funɛ wa a wa sɛm min di maa tiŋ wuti nyinbaanu awa ma bambi maga ni. Mu funɛ nu chen, mu tuha fun sɔɔni kinkan.

ref: Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth. Working Together
text: Saha naa mu yaa nu di mu bisa tiŋ Koosa wuti nyinbaannɛ, o yaa sɔɔni mu tuhau kinkan a kaali kina maga. Jɔn Fun Pɛgi Gaiyus

ref: Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before.
text: Mu chɛna, o weru di i yaa kila Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a du aa kisuŋi ba. Ma wa zim ba bɔnɛ, amaa ma yaa la bau a la suŋi ba. Chenani maa tum nan, ma sir tiŋ Koosa nyinbaanu nɛnɛ.

ref: They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve.
text: Ba funɛ sɛm i wɛu a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn bar nɔ ni. Ba funɛ pɛgi hinu a li chenani ii daŋni ba ni. Ba wa saksi di ba li, i suŋi ba ba li nyinbaan ti ni nɛnɛ, chen ni i zi sɔɔni Koosa tuhau kinkan.

ref: When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower.
text: Yesu daha baa gba tɔsa nan. Ba wara daŋni ba naa vɛ zim Koosa nyɛ ba kundɔŋ.

ref: We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
text: Chendaha o weru wa yaa suŋi nara timau. Yaa suŋi dɔŋa chen ni, ya wa ba la biksi nara Koosa wɛ. Diɔtrifis Awa Dimitrius Wɛ

ref: I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us.
text: Min funɛ sɔbi tɔn a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn i tɔhɔn ni, amaa Diɔtrifis wa fun la nɔɔni di o tiŋ mu nɔ. O daŋni o burgi ma nihɛɛrɛ.

ref: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
text: Mu wa wa, mu wa ma zi sɛm chenani o yaa fun a yo o nɔu a la sɛm sɛm tagna a du min ni. Amaa chen duŋduŋ laata ya wu tagna oo tum. O wa zi la Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa yaa val a la wa o wati. Hali o wa zi nyɛ o chɛnsa bɔnɛ la ba du. Ba la ba, o yaa daŋni o duŋni ba a li Yesu nitiŋɛ ta wati.

ref: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
text: Mu chɛna, ta tiŋ chenani o tiŋi. Kitum tum were nyinbaan. Naa la tum tum were, Koosa dɔ un. Amaa naa ka la tum tum tagna, Koosa wa dɔ un.

ref: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
text: Dimitrius ya nibin werire. Nara maga zimu di oo tiŋ Koosa wuti nyinbaannɛ nɛnɛ. Mu titi ŋɔɛɛ di o ya nibin werire ka i zim di mu sɛm ya wutirɛ. Jɔn Mak Tɔn Nɔ Bar Nɔwa

ref: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
text: Mu kpak wu damatiwa a kaa ŋɔ hin, amaa mu wa zi sɔbi a a dau lalaknɔ.

ref: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
text: Mu kpak bambiu di mu wa na hin lalaga. Mu wa pu hin, ya zi sɛm kin nama wɛu.

ref: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
text: Koosa la zi nyɛ hin bara sɔɔna. I chɛnsa ba naa nyɛn bar nɔ, zaami hinu. Zaami ya chɛnsa gbanko a nyɛ ya.

ref: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, mu funɛ daŋni mu sɔbi a sɛm ma chenani Koosa fun a yisi ma awa ya a li ya tum tagna ni yaa tiŋ un. Lalaknɔ o lawa mu sɔbiu a kpami ma ma kpak laaboor weri naa ya fun biksi ma Koosa wɛ chen nɛnɛ. Waa funɛ kpa o wuti nɔ a nyɛ o nara ni bu bɛgim.

ref: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
text: Amaa ba naa vɛ tiŋ Koosa, bɔnɛ fun sogli a wa gbaari ma ni. Ba funɛ chɛrgi Koosa laaboor weri naa biksi ya chenani Koosa fun a suŋi ya, a vɛ Yesu Kristo naa ya ya kunyana bɛgim. Ba funɛ ya chene di ba tum chɔkumsa tuma. Ba fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni saha digi di Koosa zi wa di ba sɛriau a na ba galimsa.

ref: Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them.
text: Ma piin zim kin nama wɛu nɛnɛ. Mu daŋni mu lisi mau saha naa Koosa fun a lisi Ju tima a li Ijipt pulum ni saha digi. A gantɛl nɛɛ o fun a kpu ba naa se vɛ su un.

ref: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
text: Koosa malaikasa ba naa funɛ vɛ o nɔ a li ba bar nyɛni ni, Koosa fun kpak ba a vɔu birsi bar nyɛni ni wa kpok ni hali wuzu zen naa o zi wa di ba sɛria.

ref: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
text: Ma gbanko lisi tɔsi naa ba yir Sɔdɔm awa Gamɔra awa tɔsi naa go a saha digi. Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni yau a chi Koosa malaikasi ta yaali. Ba fun a tum chɔkumsi tuma ka nibaala daŋni nibaala. Koosa fun kpa din naa vɛ kpak bɔ a kpa ba du a lɛrgi ba diŋsa kinkan. Nan la biksi ya di ya ta tiŋ chenani ba tiŋi.

ref: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
text: Chen vɛɛna, nara nama la na kina a naa nyɛ ba tum tum tagna a du ba titi bara ni a chi ba deesi. Baa tiŋ ba tutɔrɛ a la pini Koosa malaikasa zoma.

ref: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
text: Hali Koosa malaikasa nyuhu tiina naa ba yir Maikil, wa tun nan. O wa sintana funɛ di amɔrɛ di anti maa wa dɔ Mosis bara, o titi wa fun pini sintana zoma a di un sɛria, amaa o funɛ ŋɔ di Koosa la zi zulimi un.

ref: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
text: Amaa nara nama pini kin nawara ba vɛ zimi zomwa. Tum naa baa tum a wa kpak nɛha a chi sela, a zi chɔk bau.

ref: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
text: Yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ba ni? Ba tiŋ chenani Keen fun tiŋ saha digirɛ. Ba waa la tum tum tagna di ba na nyɔɔra a chi Beelim funɛ tum saha digi chen. Ba zi sowu a chi chenani Kɔɔra fun sou chen, domi o funɛ burgi o gantɛlu a chak Mosis saha digi.

ref: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
text: Ba wa ma lagmɛɛ di ma di diwe, ba nyini ba duŋduŋnɛ a la di a la nyɔ kinkan. Chen la chɔk ma lagmi ti. Ba chi dobaala naa peha ya a vɛ mi doone. Ba chi bugili naa vɛ koti a nɔɔnirɛ, chen ni nara wa hur a luta a chɛsi a siti.

ref: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
text: Ba chi chenani peha yaa wa mo ni chene ka ba tum tagna naa nyɛ ba na hiisa a chi ni fukla. Ba chi wula naa nyɛn buudaha ni a gba chene. Koosa zi kpa au a du birsi ni hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
text: Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon ba yir un Inɔk. O funɛ ya Adam bi lulɛ nidigirɛ a sɛm ba wɛ saha digi a ŋɔ, “Kina, Koosa wa o malaika damata wa wa,

ref: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
text: ba zi wa di nara maga sɛriau. Ba naa maga la tum tum tagna a sɛm sɛm tagna aa du Koosa ni, o zi na ba galimsau a ta ba yɔri.”

ref: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
text: Nara nama yaa zulimi a la na nara galimsa saha maga. Ba yaa tiŋ ba titi daŋnirɛ a la daŋni sɔna a la pɛgi nara wa ba nyina ni di ba tiŋ ba a nyɛ ba malɛ. Maa Tiŋ Koosa Nɛnɛ Aa Gbaari

ref: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text: Mu bi daŋnɛ, ma lisi chenani ya kunyan Yesu Kristo nitumɛ fun te biksi ma,

ref: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
text: a ŋɔ, “Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga siili saha wa dugli, ba naa la tiŋ ba titi daŋnɛ, zi wa mɔm mau a li ma Koosa nyinbaan tiŋi ni.”

ref: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
text: Nara nama wara tiŋ Koosa siim weri ti nyinbaan, amaa baa ka tiŋ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga nyinbaannɛ. Chendaha ba nyɛ nara purigi dɔŋ ni.

ref: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
text: Amaa, mu bi daŋnɛ, maa tiŋ Koosa nɛnɛ a gbaari, domi ma se su un. Ma nyɛ o siim weri ti suŋi ma maa sumi Koosa.

ref: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
text: Koosa daŋni mau. Chendaha maa tiŋ un nɛnɛ a ta gil o nyinbaan. Maa pɔ ya kunyan ti Yesu Kristo. O wa bur wa, o zi kpak ma sikiu a nyɛ ma o mi fali naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
text: Maa kpak ba naa la beeli wa siki,

ref: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
text: a yisi ba a li bar tagin ni. Ma gbanko kpak ba naa bur Koosa nyinbaan tiŋi ti ni siki. Amaa ma doso wa ba tum tagna ni a ha a. Ma Nyɛ Ya Pɛgi Koosa Naa Kpak Jirima Kinkan

ref: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
text: Waa funɛ tum ya kunyana Yesu Kristo di o wa yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni. Koosa naa ya nibɛgim duŋduŋ kpak jirima kinkan awa kpok a kaali kina maga a li o piili ni a wa zinan kpa la hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami. Ma zaami ba naa maga nyɛn ma wati a nyɛ min.

ref: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
text: Min waa, Jɔn, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ a nyɛ Koosa tumtumsi ni di mu biksi ba chenani la zi ya lalaga. Koosa funɛ te kpa kin nama maga a sogli a zi kpa a a li gbampulim a nyɛ Yesu Kristo ni. Waa funɛ kpa a a nyɛ mu waa naa ya Yesu tumtum ni.

ref: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
text: Mu funɛ kpa Koosa sɛmau, chenani Yesu Kristo biksi ya o titi wɛ awa kin naa maga mu fun na a nu a sɔbi a dau bar nɔ.

ref: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
text: Ba naa karimi tɔn nɔ a ŋɔ ba dɔŋtim ni awa ba naa nu chenani la zi wa ya ka ba tiŋ mu sɛma, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu kinkan. Saha naa kin nama kaa wa ya duglɛu. Jɔn Funɛ Sɔbi A Nyɛ Yesu Nitiŋɛ Naa Purige Bunɔpɛ

ref: From John to the seven churches in Asia. I pray that you will be blessed with kindness and peace from God, who is and was and is coming. May you receive kindness and peace from the seven spirits before the throne of God.
text: Min waa, Jɔn, la sɔbi tɔn nɔ bar digi a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa purige bunɔpɛ a li Azia pulum ni. Koosa naa funɛ nyɛn a ha vɛɛn nyɛn, o zi nyɛnu. O wa siimsa nɔpɛ a naa nyɛn o suwachak ni a li o tɔhɔn ni,

ref: May kindness and peace be yours from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness. Jesus was the first to conquer death, and he is the ruler of all earthly kings. Christ loves us, and by his blood he set us free from our sins.
text: wa Yesu Kristo naa funɛ biksi ya Koosa wuti ti ka o te lɛli un a li o sou ni ya maga suwachak ni, ba maga zi suŋi mau a kinyɛ ma bara sɔɔna. Yesu ya koor zen a kaali tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga koorsa. Waa funɛ daŋni ya a kpa o chɛl a chaksi ya tum tagna a li ya bambe ni,

ref: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
text: a vɔti ya koorsa a lisi ya di ya ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa di ya tum tuma a nyɛ o nyan Koosa ni. Yesu kpak jirima awa chaani wa kpok kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.

ref: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
text: Maa il. O zi wa li dobaala tuhu niu di ba naa nyɛ ba kpa tim a tau o lɔgum hɛna a li o daakakli ti ni chen awa nara maga zi na unu. O wa bur wa, nibin kurgɛ maga zi wiu kinkan. O wa zi vusi. Ami.

ref: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛn a ha vɛɛn nyɛn, o zi nyɛnu. O waa naa kpak kina maga kpok, ŋɔ, “Mu vɛ kpak piili a nyɛn a vɛ kpak peti.” Jɔn Fun Na Yesu A Chi O Deesi

ref: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
text: Mu waa, Jɔn, ya ma niinbe. Mu wa ma la namsi Yesu daha, amaa o suŋi yau ya di un, chen ni ya zi la zu o koor tiu wuzu digi. Ba fun kpa min a la nyɛ mu nyɛn pulum we naa ba yir Patmɔs ka ni go un. Ba funɛ ya chene, domi mu funɛ mɔɔli Koosa laaboor weri ti a ŋɔ nara wuti naa Yesu fun biksi ya a li gbampulim ni chen wɛ.

ref: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
text: Aleedi wuzu digi Koosa siim weri ti fun wa si min ni a nyɛ mu na kin bɔnɛ a chi mu deesi. Mu fun nu ta zaŋa a chi kaabul,

ref: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
text: ka o ŋɔ min, “Sɔbi chenani i kaa na a du tɔn ni a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Ɛfɛsus, Sumana, Pɛgamum, Tiataya, Sardis, Filadɛlfia, awa Laodisia a li Azia pulum ni.”

ref: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
text: Mu fun burgi di mu na naa la sɛm chen a na fitinsa bar chiŋe kina nɔpɛ ka ba kpa salmasa a kɔɔri a.

ref: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
text: Mu funɛ vɛɛn nau ka Yesu naa la yir o titi nibine be, chiŋi a bambaan ni. O funɛ laari takta ziŋnirɛ a kpa salma gar wi a kirisi o bambi.

ref: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
text: O nyuha awa o nyuksa funɛ pilimiu kinkan a chi gar pilim ka o se sɛri a chi din a la chɛr nara se.

ref: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
text: O naŋgere sɛri ti funɛ chi sulminne ba kpa kɔɔri kina a lili kinkan. O ta ti funɛ ku a chi ni bar chuuri kinkan.

ref: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
text: O funɛ kpak wula nɔpɛwa o nindil ni a kpak chisi nɔsa lɛ o li o nɔ ni. O se funɛ chɛru a chi nha biee.

ref: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
text: Mu funɛ na un, mu fun chel tɛɛnɛ ni a chi mu siwɛɛ. O fun wa pu min a kpa o nindil a gbaŋsi min a ŋɔ, “Ta ɔmi. Mu vɛ kpak piili a nyɛn a vɛ kpak peti.

ref: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
text: Mu ya naa nyɛnɛɛ. Mu funɛ sou, amaa lalaknɔ mu zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Min dɔ sou awa ba naa sitɛɛ bar nyɛni.

ref: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
text: Sɔbi kin nawara i na nan a dau. A bɔnɛ ya zinan kiniwa. Bɔnɛ ka zi wa yau.

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Ɛfɛsus ni. Mu waa naa kpak wula nɔpɛ mu nindil ni a val a go fitinsa bar chiŋɛ nɔpɛ ka ba fun kpa salma a kɔɔri a bambaan ni, ma nu chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma.

ref: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
text: Mu zim chenani ma bambe duu awa chenani ma kpak un ma bambe ni di ma tum ma tuma wa kanyita ni a ta kuuli. Mu zimu di ma wa kpak wu digi wa ba naa la tum tum tagna ni a zaamsi ba naa ŋɔ di ba ya Koosa wuti nibiksɛwa a na di ba ya nusun timwa.

ref: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
text: Ma di ma namsiu a wa kanyita ni mu daha a vɛ bur mu tiŋi ni,

ref: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
text: amaa wu digirɛ ma vɛ seen a yaali. Ma wara daŋni min a chi chenani ma fun te daŋni min chen.

ref: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
text: Ma lisi chenani o fun te du a bur kuwa mu wati. Ma vɛ di ma wa zi bur wa, mu zi bur wa ma watiu lalaga a wa zer ma fitin bar chiŋsi a chɔk un.

ref: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
text: Ma waa la ha tum tagna a naa Nikolaitan tima tum a chi chenani mu ha a. Chen weru.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden. The Letter to Smyrna
text: “Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu chenani Koosa siim weri ti sɛm a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni. Ba naa maga vɛ bur mu tiŋi ni, mu zi nyɛ ba nyinbaanu ba di Koosa mi fali bugil nɔnau. O bi Koosa tɔhɔn naa weri nee. Ba naa maga nyɛn dooni, ba tusa sɔɔnɛu kinkan.” Chenani Yesu Fun Sɛm A Nyɛ Sumana Tim Ni

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Sumana ni. “Mu vɛ kpak piili a nyɛn a vɛ kpak peti. Mu funɛ sou, amaa lalaknɔ mu zi nyɛnu hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
text: Mu zim chenani ma namsiu kinkan a ya fara tima, amaa ma ta suglɛ di ma funɛ kpa kidamatiwa a chiŋsi a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni. Bɔnɛ nyɛn ma ni aa chɔk ma sɔna. Sintana dɔ ba, domi ba ŋɔ di ba ya Ju timwa a vɛ yaali.

ref: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
text: Ma ta ɔm chenani ma namsi zi wa gbaari. Sintana zi wa nyɛ ba kpak ma bɔnɛu a tɔ dɛ di o zaamsi ma a nyɛ ma namsi kinkan wuzu fi. Ba daŋni ba kpu ma, ma kpak nɛnɛ ka mu zi kpa Koosa mi fali ti a nyɛ ma.

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Pɛgamum ni. “Mu waa naa kpak chisi nɔsa lɛ ti o li mu nɔ ni, ma nu chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma.

ref: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
text: Mu zimu di doonani ma nyɛn nan, sintana dɔ nidamata. Hali o nitiŋɛ funɛ kpu naa ba yir Antipas domi o funɛ biksi nara mu wɛ, ma wa fun bur a li mu tiŋi ni.

ref: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
text: Amaa wu bɔnɛwa ma vɛ seen a yaali. Bɔnɛ nyɛn ma ni a tiŋ naa ba yir Beelam chen biksi. O funɛ nyɛn saha digi a biksi baal naa ba yir Belak di o la biksi Ju tima chenani ba zi di vursa diwe a tum chɔkumsa tuma.

ref: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
text: Bɔnɛ ka nyɛn ma ni a tiŋ Nikolaitan tima biksi.

ref: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
text: Ma duŋni ba a ta. Ma vɛ duŋni ba, mu zi bur wa ma watiu lalaga a wa lisi chisi naa la li mu nɔ ni a chem ba.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
text: “Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu chenani Koosa siim weri ti sɛm a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni. Ba naa kpak nɛnɛ hali a la tel o bɔ, mu zi kpa diwe naa ba yir ‘mana’ ka o sogliu, a nyɛ ba ba di. Mu zi sɔbi sɔn falɛu a sak buŋkul pilimi ni a kpa nyɛ ba. Ba naa la a chen duŋduŋ la zi zim chenani sɔbi sak o kin ni.” Chenani Yesu Fun Sɛm A Nyɛ Tiataya Tim Ni

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Tiataya ni. “Mu waa naa Koosa be ka mu se sɛri a chi din a la chɛr nara se ka mu naŋgere sɛri ti funɛ chi sulminne ba kpa kɔɔri kina a lili kinkan, ma nu chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma.

ref: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
text: Mu zim chenani ma bambe duu awa chenani ma daŋni Koosa awa dɔŋa awa chenani ma se su un nɛnɛ. Mu gbanko zim di tum were naa maa tum wa kanyita ni lalaknɔ nan kaali te tel tum ta.

ref: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
text: Amaa wu digirɛ ma vɛ seen a yaali. Nihaan digi nyɛn ma ni ba yir un Jɛzɛbɛl. O ŋɔ di waa ya Koosa nitumɛ ta digi, amaa o biksi ma di ma bur a tum chɔkumsi tuma a kidi vursa diwe. Ma waa la nɔɔni o nyɛn ma wati.

ref: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
text: Saha naa mu fun ŋɔ un di o gil o tum tagni chen galɛu. O wara daŋni o bur a li o chɔkumsi tum ta ni.

ref: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
text: Ma nu mu wati. O wa ba naa lagmi dɔŋ ni a tum chɔkumsi tum chen vɛ gil a, mu zi nyɛ wichɔɔrta kpak bau ba chɔ tɛɛnɛ ni a namsi kinkan.

ref: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
text: Mu zi nyɛ o bisa sitiu, chen ni Yesu nitiŋɛ ta maga zi zimu di min ya naa zim chenani nibinsa bambe du. Mu zi bɔ ma magau a li chenani ma tum ni.

ref: I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end.
text: Ma kpak nɛnɛ a sɔɔni mu tuha hali a la tel o bɔ, mu zi vɔti ma mu koorsau ma dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga.

ref: I will give each of them the same power my Father has given me. They will rule the nations with an iron rod and smash those nations to pieces like clay pots. I will also give them the morning star.
text: Mu jirima awa mu chaani a naa mu zi kpa a nyɛ ma, a zi chaaniu a chi chau wul.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
text: “Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu chenani Koosa siim weri ti sɛm a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni.”

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Sardis ni. “Mu waa kpak Koosa siimsa nɔpɛ awa wula nɔpɛ. Ma nu chenani mu kaa ŋɔ ma. Mu zim chenani maa tumu awa chenani nara sɛm di ma tiŋ minu nɛnɛ amaa ma wa tiŋ nɛnɛ.

ref: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
text: Ma kaa tiŋ chen, o wa zi vusi ka ma bur mu tiŋi ni. Ma si chiŋi a kpak si sɛm a tiŋ nɛnɛ, chen ni ma zi peti tum naa Koosa fun kpa nyɛ ma di ma tumu.

ref: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
text: Ma lisi chenani ma fun te nu Koosa laaboor weri ti a nyɛ ma se su un. Ma kpak un nɛnɛ a gil chenani ma vɛ tiŋ min nɛnɛ. Ma vɛ si chiŋi a kpak si sɛm a tiŋ min nɛnɛ, mu zi wa wa ma watiu ka ma vɛ zim mu wɛ. Mu waali ti zi chi chenani ŋmɛɛr ya wa ŋmɛ kina chen.

ref: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
text: Ma naa nyɛn Sardis ni, bɔnɛ ha wa tum tum tagna naa nyɛ ma tusa doŋ. Mu zi nyɛ ma kipilimau ma laari a nyɛ ma wa min lagmi dɔŋ ni chenani ma ya chen daha.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
text: Mu zi nyɛ ba naa kpak nɛnɛu hali a la tel o bɔ laari kipilimau ka mu zi nyɛ ba sɔna naa ba fun sɔbi aa du Koosa mi fali tɔn ti ni kpalimi dooni. Mu si sɛm mu nyana awa o malaikasi ta gbampulim niu a li ba suwachak ni di min dɔ ba.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
text: “Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu chenani Koosa siim weri ti sɛm a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni.” Chenani Yesu Fun Sɛm A Nyɛ Filadɛlfia Tim Ni

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Filadɛlfia ni. “Mu waa naa ya tu chaan tiina awa wuti tiina a kpak koor Devid dam safibe. Mu kpa un a puti mu koor ti bulzɔɔnɔ, nandigi wa kikoti a far un. Mu kpa safibe ti a pur bulzɔɔnɔ ti, nandigi wa kikoti a puti un.

ref: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
text: Mu zim chenani maa tumu awa chenani ma vɛ kpak kpoku, amaa ma tiŋ mu biksi ti a vɛ li mu gantɛl nara bambaan ni. Chendaha mu fun puk mu koor ti bulzɔɔnɔ di ma zu. Nandigi wa to a la zi tɔ un.

ref: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
text: Ma nu mu wati. Ba naa ŋɔ ma di ba ya Ju timwa a ya nusun tima, sintana dɔ ba. Mu zi nyɛ ba wa gbiri ma suwachak niu a nyɛ ma jirima, domi ba zimu di mu daŋni mau.

ref: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
text: Saha naa ba naa maga nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni zi wa namsi kinkan, ka mu zi zaamsi ba, duglɛu. Ma funɛ tum ma tumau wa kanyita ni a chi chenani mu fun biksi ma. Chendaha mu zi wa lisi mau a li namsi ti saha ni.

ref: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
text: Mu zi bur wau lalaga. Ma kpak chenani ma tiŋ min nɛnɛ, chen ni nandigi wa zi lisi Koosa mi fali naa mu zi kpa a nyɛ ma, a li ma ni.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
text: Ba naa kpak nɛnɛ hali a la tel o bɔ, mu zi nyɛ ba burgiu a chi zantɔglsa a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ni hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Mu zi sɔbi Koosa sɔn awa o tɔhɔn ti sɔnu a sak ba ni. O tɔhɔn ti ya Jɛrusalɛm fali naa Koosa zi nyɛ o wa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni wuzu digi. Mu gbanko zi sɔbi mu sɔn faliu a sak ba ni.

ref: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
text: “Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu chenani Koosa siim weri ti sɛm a nyɛ Yesu nitiŋɛ ba naa nyɛn Azia pulum ni.” Chenani Yesu Fun Sɛm A Nyɛ Laodisia Tim Ni

ref: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
text: “Sɔbi kin nama a la nyɛ naa la nyini Yesu nitiŋɛ ta a li Laodisia ni. “Mu waa naa ba ya yir Ami ka mu biksi nara Koosa wuti wɛ. Waa funɛ nyɛ mu naami kina maga a li o piili ti ni.

ref: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
text: Mu zim chenani maa tum. Ma se wa sɛri a li chenani ma daŋni ma tum mu tuma, amaa ma wa bur a li mu tiŋi ni. Mu fu daŋni mu zim di ma se sɛriu akoo ma zi gil mu tiŋirɛ.

ref: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
text: Domi ma se wa sɛri wa mu tiŋi ni, ma nyɛ sɛsirɛ kpak min. Ma nyɛ mu daŋni mu wa chɔk ma.

ref: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
text: Ma ŋɔ ma titi ni, ‘Ya ya kin timawa kinkan a vɛ daŋni kundɔŋ.’ Mu zi ŋɔ ma chenani ma duu wuti. Ma ya fara timwa kinkan a chi nyuluma ba naa laari kizɛrgɛ. Ba naa maga na ma, ma wɛ zi ya ba sikiu.

ref: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
text: Ma wa mu wati a wa yau salma naa ba fun kpa du din ni a chaksi di ma ya kin tima wuti awa gar pilima naa ma laari, chen ni ma wa zi bu na hiisa. Ma wa yau se luri a du ma se ni, chen ni ma zi bukoo kinau.

ref: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
text: Mu zulimi ba naa maga mu daŋni a biksi ba ba tum tagna. Ma gil chenani ma funɛ tum a kpak si sɛm awa mu tiŋi ni.

ref: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
text: Ma nu mu wati. Saha maga mu yaa chiŋi bulzɔɔnɔ nɛɛ a mo a la yir. Ba naa nu mu ta a puk bulzɔɔnɔ ti, mu zi zuu, chen ni ba wa min zi lagmiu a di diwe.

ref: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
text: Ba naa kpak nɛnɛ hali a la tel o bɔ, mu zi nyɛ ba bɔni mu jirima bar nyɛniu Koosa chakpal ni. Mu funɛ kpak nɛnɛ hali a fun la tel o bɔ. Chendaha o nyɛ mu kani dooni.

ref: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
text: A gantɛl ni mu fun bu nyini a na kundɔŋ a chi mu deesi. Koosa tɔhɔn ti bulzɔɔnɔ funɛ puku, ka ta zaŋa naa mu fun te nu chen chi kaabul fun ŋɔ min, “Zini kuwa bar nɔ ka mu biksi hin chenani la zi wa ya.”

ref: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun vaan te mu suwachak a nyɛ mu zini a zu. Mu fun nyini a na Koosa jirima bar nyɛni ka nandigi kani dooni.

ref: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
text: O se funɛ liliu a chi buŋkul were a naa sɛrɛ a chaani, ka doonkagla naa chi suwasi ni go un.

ref: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
text: Bar kanɛ fumlɛ danaasi funɛ go un. Nihɛɛta nara fumlɛ danaasi funɛ kani doonma ni. Ba maga funɛ laari kipilimiwa a chik salma nyuchigsa.

ref: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
text: Din fun nyiksi awa nasɛha a li Koosa jirima bar kani ti. Fitin hɛmsɛ abanɔpɛ funɛ nyɛn o suwachak ni. A waa funɛ ya Koosa siimsa nɔpɛ.

ref: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
text: Bɛɛŋa naa funɛ pɛn a lili a chi diga gbanko funɛ nyɛn o suwachak ni. Sela naasi funɛ chiŋi a go un a gbaari. A maga se funɛ iiu a nyɛn a se awa a naŋkporsi ni.

ref: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
text: Sel digi funɛ chi gbogumtirɛ, digi chi nupɔl, digi ka kpak nyuha a chi nibine, digi ka kpak nyuha a chi tiisi naa pisi o chɛnɛ.

ref: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
text: A maga funɛ kpak chɛnɛ anɔnɔrawa ka se ta su a bara maga. Ba yaa funɛ lɔ boola a vɛ hiisi a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana Koosa naa ya tu chaan tiina a kpak kina maga kpok, i vɛ kpak piili a nyɛn a vɛ kpak peti.”

ref: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
text: Sela naasi ta funɛ lɔ a booliwa a pɛgi naa funɛ kanɛ Koosa jirima bar kani ni, domi o kpak jirima awa chaani kinkanu a nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.

ref: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
text: “Ya kunyana awa ya Koosa, hin naami kina maga. O ya i tutɔrɛ a nyɛn. Chendaha o deŋli wa ya jɛɛmi hin, domi i kpak jirima awa chaani wa kpok kinkan a kaali a maga.”

ref: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
text: Mu fun nyini a na ka naa kanɛ Koosa jirima bar kani ni chen, funɛ kpak tɔnnɛ o nindil ni. Ba fun sɔbi a sak o suwachak awa o gantɛl ni a guti un a tɔ un tɔɔli bunɔpɛ.

ref: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
text: Malaika digi funɛ kpak ta kpok a nyɛn dooni. Waa funɛ mɔɔli a ŋɔ, “Anti ya nizen a la zi wa kasi kitɔɔli nama a puk tɔn nɔ?”

ref: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Nandigi wa ta ba naa nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni akoo tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni koo nisitɛ bar nyɛni ni a ya nizen di ba wa puk un a nyini o tuha ni.

ref: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
text: Mu funɛ na di nandigi wa to a ya nizen a la zi la puk tɔn ti a nyini o tuha ni, sini fun li mu se ni kinkan.

ref: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
text: Nihɛɛti ta digi fun bur a ŋɔ min, “Ta nyɛ i sini kilili. Naa ba yir gbogumti chen a li Juda buuri ni, waa ya koor Devid bi luli. O funɛ kpaku hali o bɔ a nyaŋi o dɔma a koti a kasi kitɔɔlisa nɔpɛ ta a puk tɔn ti. Kina, waa nɛɛnɛ.”

ref: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
text: Mu fun nyini a na ka piisi be nyɛn doon. O chi ba funɛ kɔrigi unu. O fun chiŋi a dugli Koosa jirima bar kani ti ka sela naasi ta awa nihɛɛta nara fumlɛ danaasi ta go dooni. Piisi be ti funɛ kpak nyindaasa anɔpɛ awa se nɔpɛwa. O se nɔpɛ ta funɛ ya Koosa siimsa nɔpɛ naa Koosa fun tum tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni.

ref: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
text: Piisi be ti fun la la tɔn ti a li naa kanɛ Koosa jirima ba kanɛ nindil ni.

ref: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
text: Ba fun lɔ bool fali a ŋɔ, “I ya nizenu wa i la tɔn ti a kasi kitɔɔli ta a puk un. Ba funɛ kpu hin, i fun kpa i chɛl a la nidamata nyuuna a nyɛ Koosa ni. I funɛ lisi bau a li pulumsa awa buurisa maga ni ka ba maga sɛm sɛm kurgɛ.

ref: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
text: I funɛ vɔti ba koorsa a lisi ba di ba ya Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsa a tum tuma a nyɛ ya Koosa ni. Ba zi dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim magau.”

ref: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
text: Mu fun bukoo nyini a na ka Koosa malaikasa nyɛn dooni. Ba funɛ iiu a vɛ kpak guri a chiŋi a go doon maga. Mu funɛ nu a,

ref: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
text: ka a lɔ bool wa ta zaŋa ni a ŋɔ, “Piisa be naa ba fun kɔrigi chen, o deŋlu wa ya jɛɛmi hin, domi i dɔ kina maga a kpak zima awa kpok wa jirima wa chaani kinkan wa tu sɔɔni.”

ref: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
text: Doone mu fun nu kin naa maga funɛ nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga wa nisitɛ bar nyɛni wa teku ni ka a fun lɔ bool a ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi naa kanɛ Koosa jirima bar kani ni awa piisi be ti, domi ba kpak jirima awa chaani wa kpok kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
text: A funɛ lɔ a bool ti peti, sela naasi ta fun ŋɔ, “Ami,” ka nihɛɛti ta fun si a pimi tɛɛnɛ ni a jɛɛmi ba.

ref: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
text: Piisi be ti funɛ la tɔn ti, o fun kasi kitɔɔli naa te chen a puk tɔn ti bar digi. Sela naasi ta digi fun ŋɔ min a chi nasɛha, “Kuwa a wa na.”

ref: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
text: Mu fun la nyini a na zaag pilim ka o nyɛn dooni. Naa funɛ zini zaag ti chen funɛ kpak toha awa hɛmwa ka ba fun kpa salma nyuchiga a chik o nyuha. O fun la chem laal a nyaŋi o dɔm damata.

ref: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Doone piisi be ti fun kasi kitɔɔli alɛ tiina a bu puk tɔn ti tama a gbaari. Sel naa funɛ chi nupɔl chen, fun ŋɔ min, “Kina.”

ref: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
text: Mu fun nyini a na zaag sɛm ka o nyɛn dooni. Ba funɛ kpa chisi zen a nyɛ naa funɛ zini zaag ti chen. Ba gbanko funɛ kpa kpokre di o la a la nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga la chem laal a kpu dɔŋa a nyɛ un.

ref: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
text: Doone piisi be ti fun kasi kitɔɔli atora tiina a bu puk tɔn ti tama a gbaari. Sel naa kpak nyuha a chi nibine chen, fun ŋɔ min, “Kina.” Mu fun nyini a na zaag bilim ka o nyɛn dooni. Naa funɛ zini zaag ti chen funɛ kpak kina kizaamsirɛ o nin ni.

ref: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
text: Mu fun nu ta a li sela naasi ta bambaan ni ka o ŋɔ, “Kila a la bɔ ba naa tum wuzu bɛgim chen wa poge gbaŋpuluma akoo mɛ gbaŋpulumsa tora, amaa doso a ta chɔk nu ti akoo sin.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
text: Doone piisi be ti fun kasi kitɔɔli anaasi tiina a bu puk tɔn ti tama a gbaari. Sel naa kpak nyuha a chi tiisi naa pisi o chɛnɛ, fun ŋɔ min, “Kina,

ref: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
text: Ba fun tɛsi ta zaŋa a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana, hin ya tu chaan tiina awa wuti tiina a kpak kina maga kpok. Yɛrɛ o zi vusi ka i di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria a lɛrgi ba diŋsa chenani ba fun kpu ya chen daha?”

ref: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn Koosa jirima bar kani ti ni a go, bɔnɛ fun kpa takta ziŋɛ pilima a nyɛ ba a ŋɔ ba di ba hiisi tama, hali tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga wa kpu ba niinbisa a chi ba funɛ kpu ba a gbaari peti.

ref: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
text: Doone mu fun nyini a na ka piisi be ti fun kasi kitɔɔli anɔra tiina a bu puk tɔn ti tama a gbaari. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga fun vaan zigsi kinkan ka nha fun biri a chi dinhala. Pɛna fun burgi kisɛm a chi chɛl,

ref: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
text: ka wula fun li buudaha a wa tu tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a chi chenani peha yaa ya soom galsa chesi chen.

ref: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
text: Buudaha funɛ gutiu a chi ba guti kichɔɔli a nyuksi, ka bɛ awa pulumsi naa ni go chen maga fun tur a bar nyɛnɛ ni.

ref: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga koorsa, nibirige ta, kin tima, lanjimasa nyuhu tima, ba naa funɛ ya yosa awa ba naa funɛ ya nihiise, ba maga fun chɔ la zu gbɛntɛl bɔsa awa bɛ bambaanisi ni.

ref: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
text: Ba funɛ sumi di bɛ ta chesi ba ni, chen ni naa kanɛ Koosa jirima bar kani ni wa zi na ba, ka piisi be ti wa zi kpa o paan a du ba ni a na ba galimsa a ta ba yɔri.

ref: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
text: Ba fun ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Wuzu zen naa Koosa kaa wa na paan a lɛrgi ya diŋsa, telu. Anti la zi koti a chem un?”

ref: After this I saw four angels. Each one was standing on one of the earth's four corners. The angels held back the four winds, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree. These angels had also been given the power to harm the earth and the sea. Then I saw another angel come up from where the sun rises in the east, and he was ready to put the mark of the living God on people. He shouted to the four angels,
text: Koosa funɛ nyɛ a kpok di a chɔk tɛɛnɛ awa ni. Doone mu fun na malaika digi fun li nha bar yɛli ka o kpak kin naa o kpa dani nara a nyɛ Koosa ni o nin ni. O fun yir kinkan awa ta zaŋ ni a ŋɔ malaikasa naasi ta ni,

ref: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
text: “Ma nyɛ mu te dani Koosa tumtumsa a li ba tilsa ni ka ma zi chɔk tɛɛnɛ awa ni wa bugila.”

ref: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
text: a ŋɔ, “Ami. Ya pɛgi a zaami hin, domi i kpak zima awa jirima wa chaani wa kpok kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ami.”

ref: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
text: A gantɛl ni mu fun nyini a na ka zɛmɛ kinkan nyɛn dooni. Nandigi wa fun koti a gur ba. Ba funɛ li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga pulumsa awa buurisa maga ni ka ba maga sɛm sɛm kurgɛ. Ba fun laari takta ziŋɛ pilima a kpak bone kpachaga ba nisa ni a chiŋi Koosa jirima bar kani awa piisi be ti suwachak ni.

ref: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
text: Ba fun tɛsi wa ta zaŋ ni a ŋɔ, “Koosa naa la kani o jirima bar kani ni awa piisi be ti, ba maga funɛ yisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni hali a wa peti a kpa ya wa bar nɔ.”

ref: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
text: Sela naasi ta awa nihɛɛti ta awa malaikasa a naa maga fun si a pimi tɛɛnɛ ni Koosa jirima bar kani suwachak ni a jɛɛmi un,

ref: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
text: Nihɛɛti ta digi fun bur a pɛɛsi min, “Ba naa laari takta ziŋɛ pilima nan, anti ya ba? Leene ba li?”

ref: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
text: Mu fun ŋɔ un, “Mu wa zim ba, ŋɔ min.” O fun ŋɔ min, “Ba fun val a wa li namsi kinkan saha a kpa piisi be ti chɛl a chaksi ba kilaarɛ di a ta kpak doŋa.

ref: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
text: Chendaha ba nyɛn Koosa jirima bar kani suwachak ni a la tum o tuma saha maga a li Koosa tɔhɔn nɔ ni. Koosa titi zi kpalimi ba watiu.

ref: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
text: Lɔsa awa nyɔksa wa zi bu kpak ba. Nha awa nunum gbanko wa zi pu ba.

ref: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
text: Piisi be naa duglɛ Koosa jirima bar kani zi ya ba nigbaŋiu a zi te ba suwachak a dɛu ni pali di ba nyɔ. Hali Koosa titi zi fisi siniu a ta a li ba se ni.”

ref: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
text: Doone piisi be ti fun kasi kitɔɔli anɔpɛ tiina a puk tɔn ti maga. Doon funɛ duu balimm kinkan hali a wa tel minti fumtora.

ref: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
text: Chen saha ti funɛ wa kaali, ba fun kpa kaabulsa nɔpɛ a nyɛ malaikasa nɔpɛ ta ni. A waa yaa chiŋi Koosa suwachak ni.

ref: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
text: Salma biŋbiŋa funɛ nyɛn Koosa jirima bar kani suwachak nɛɛ. Malaikasi ta digi fun kpak salma gbaŋa a kpa kin naa sɔɔra weree a du un ni a wa chiŋi o suwachak ni. Kin naa sɔɔra weree chen funɛ ya Koosa nara sumi. Ba fun kpa kin naa sɔɔra weree kinkan a gbaari un di malaika ti kpa fu a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
text: Kin naa sɔɔra weree nyɔɔsa awa Koosa nara sumi fun zini buudaha a li doonani malaika ti chiŋi Koosa suwachak ni chen.

ref: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
text: O funɛ fu kin naa sɔɔra weree chen a peti, o fun zer din ti a biti du salma gbaŋ ti tuha ni a zer a maga a yo ta tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Tɛɛnɛ fun zigsi kinkan ka nasɛha nyiksi a jim kinkan. Malaikasa Nɔra Fun Hu A Kaabulsa

ref: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
text: Doone malaikasa nɔpɛ ta fun saksi di a hu a kaabulsa.

ref: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
text: Malaikasi ta te tel tiina fun hu o kaabul. Doonbe naa funɛ sɛri a chi chɛl vaan chesi tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni ka nasɛha nyiksi a du un ni. O fun purigi tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga butora a lisi bar digi a fu tɛɛnɛ ti awa bugila wa kɔsa a naa nyɛn un ni.

ref: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
text: Alɛ tiin di fun hu o kaabul. Ba fun kpa kundɔŋ a chi bi zen ka din di un a yo du ni ni. O fun purigi ni ti butora a lisi bar digi a chɛrgi un o burgi chɛl,

ref: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
text: chen ni kinyine awa arŋsa a naa maga nyɛn un ni, funɛ chɔku.

ref: When the third angel blew his trumpet, a great star fell from heaven. It was burning like a torch, and it fell on a third of the rivers and on a third of the springs of water.
text: Atora tiin ti fun hu o kaabul. Wul zen naa funɛ chaani kinkan, fun li buudaha a wa chel tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Malaika ti fun purigi pula awa nise butora a lisi bar digi a nyɛ wul ti chɔk a.

ref: The name of the star was Bitter, and a third of the water turned bitter. Many people died because the water was so bitter.
text: Wul ti sɔn funɛ ya ‘Haasa’. Nise awa ni ti fun haasi ka nidamata fun nyɔ un a siti.

ref: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
text: Anaasi tiin ti fun hu o kaabul. Ba fun purigi nha awa pɛna wa wula butora a lisi a chaani bar digi a ta. Ba gbanko fun purigi dusum awa nha a lisi a chaani bar digi a ta.

ref: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
text: Mu fun nyini a na ka tiisi nyɛn dooni a za buudaha bar boli ni. O fun ŋɔ wa ta zaŋ ni, “Malaikasa tora a naa kaɛɛ nan wa hu a kaabulsa, yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ba naa nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni?”

ref: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
text: Anyuun tiin ti fun hu o kaabul. Mu fun na wul naa li buudaha a wa chel tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. O fun kpak kpok a puk bɔ naa vɛ kpak bar peti, bulzɔɔnɔ.

ref: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
text: O fun puk bɔ ti bulzɔɔnɔ ka nyɔɔsa kinkan fun wa li bɔ ti ni a chi din zenzen nyɔɔsa. Nyɔɔsi ta fun du kuum buudaha ni.

ref: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
text: Hɔɔna fun li nyɔɔsi ta ni a tu tɛɛnɛ ka a fun kpok a chi numsa.

ref: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ a di a ta se kpachaga koo kɔsa, amaa a la dusi ba naa malaika ti vɛ dani ba tilsa duŋduŋ.

ref: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
text: Hɔɔni ta wa kpak nyinbaan di a kpu ba, amaa a namsi ba pɛnsa nyuun. Chenani numa wi awa chenani a namsi ba ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
text: Pɛnsa nyuun ta tuhu ni, nara tima zi daŋni ba kpu ba titiu, amaa ba wa kikoti. O chi sou chɔ a ka ba.

ref: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
text: Hɔɔni ta funɛ chi zaagna a naa ba saksi a di a la chem laal. A se funɛ chi nibinsa sewa ka ba fun chiksi salma nyuchigsa.

ref: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
text: A nyuksa funɛ chi nihaana nyuksiwa ka a nyina fun chi gbogumta nyina.

ref: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
text: Ba fun kpa lugɛ lɛusɛ a tɔ a bambe ka a chɛnɛ ta fewi funɛ chɛɛmi a chi zaag damata chɔ.

ref: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
text: A munzuuna funɛ chi num munzuhurɛ. Chenani a kpa a munzuuni tima a dusi nara funɛ ya a kpok a koti a namsi ba pɛnsa nyuun.

ref: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ min chenani nibinsa zi wa te namsi peti. Ba fun ŋɔ min chenani ba zi namsi bilɛ tiina a gbaari.

ref: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
text: Doone malaikasa nɔra tiin ti fun hu o kaabul. Mu fun nu ta a li salma biŋbiŋ naa nyɛn Koosa suwachak bambaan ni.

ref: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
text: Ta ti fun ŋɔ malaika ti ni, “Lisi malaikasa naasi naa ba fun vɔti a du mɔɔga naa ba yir Yufretis nɔ ni a ta.”

ref: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
text: Malaikasa naasi ta fun li mɔɔga ti ni a purigi tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga nara butora a lisi ba digi di a kpu ba. Ba fun kpa a a chiŋsi hali a wuzu naa Koosa fun ŋɔ di a tum chen.

ref: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
text: Nannɛ mu fun na zaagna nizinɛ ta a chi mu deesi. Ba funɛ laari lugɛ lɛusɛwa ba suwachak ni. A funɛ sɛriu a chi din a vɛɛn sɛri a chi sumin. Zaagni ta nyuuna funɛ chi gbogumta nyuuniwa ka nyɔɔsa naa ya kibilim a chi sumin awa din funɛ li a nɔni ni.

ref: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
text: Nyɔɔsa naa ya kibilim a chi sumin awa din a naa funɛ li zaagni ta nɔɔni ni fun kpu tɛɛnɛ nɔ tima ba naa maga malaikasa naasi ta fun purigi butora a lisi bar digi chen.

ref: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
text: Zaagni ta munzuuna funɛ kpak dum nyuuniwa a munzuuna bar peti ta ni. A fun kpa a a nyɛ nara namsi a gbaari chenani ba nɔɔna kpu ba chen.

ref: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tima ba naa maga malaikasa naasi ta ha fun vɛ lisi di a kpu ba, wa fun gil ba tum tagna a bur tiŋ Koosa. Ba yaa funɛ jɛɛmi alizin tagniwa a kpa salma, aniita, lugɛ naa sɛrɛ a chi sumin, buŋkula awa daasa a kɔɔri vursa a naa vɛ naali akoo a nu sɛma koo a val, a jɛɛmi a.

ref: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
text: Doone mu fun nyini a na ka malaika zenzen digi nyɛn dooni, a fun li Koosa tɔhɔn ti. Dobaal funɛ go un ka doonkagla fun go o nyuha. O se funɛ chaaniu a chi nha ka o naŋgere nyiksi a chi din.

ref: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
text: O funɛ kpak tɔn wewe o nin ni ka o puk. O fun tu wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga a kpa o naŋgeri dil a du teku ni ni a kpa naŋgeri gal a chiŋsi jibaŋ ni.

ref: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
text: O fun kaasi a chi gbogumta wi, ka nasɛhasa nɔpɛ a naa funɛ nyɛn doon, tɛsi a bur a nyɛ un.

ref: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
text: A funɛ tɛsi a peti, mu funɛ daŋni mu sɔbi chenani yɛɛ, amaa ta funɛ ŋɔ min a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni a ŋɔ, “Ta sɔbi chenani nasɛhasa nɔpɛ ta sɛmi a dawi. Mu daŋni a sogliu.”

ref: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
text: Malaika naa naŋgeri dil funɛ nyɛn ni ni ka o naŋgeri gal ka chiŋi jibaŋ ni chen, o fun zer o nindil buudaha ni,

ref: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
text: a hɛɛsi Koosa naa nyɛnɛɛ hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Waa ya naa funɛ naami buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ wa teku ni wa kin naa maga nyɛn a ni. Malaika ti fun hɛɛsi a ŋɔ, “Ma ta nyɛ ya sɔɔni.

ref: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
text: Malaikasa nɔpɛ tiin ti funɛ wa hu o kaabul, Koosa naa funɛ te kpa chenani o funɛ lisi a sogli a zi kpa a a li gbampulim a biksi o witinsa ba naa funɛ ya o tumtumsa, o zi tum chenani o daŋni o ya saha digiu.”

ref: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
text: Doone ta naa funɛ ŋɔ min a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni fun bu ŋɔ min, “La zer tɔn we naa funɛ puk chen a li malaika naa naŋgeri nyɛn ni ni awa jibaŋ ni chen nin ni.”

ref: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
text: Mu fun la ŋɔ malaika ti ni di o nyɛ min tɔn we ti. O fun ŋɔ min, “Kpa un a se. O zi lɛmu a chi toon a li i nɔ ni, amaa o zu i tuha, o zi haasiu.”

ref: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
text: Mu fun la tɔn we ti a li o nin ni a se un. Chenani o fun sɛm chen sir ya. O funɛ lɛm a chi toon a li mu nɔ niu amaa o funɛ haasiu mu tuhu ni.

ref: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
text: Mu funɛ se un peti, o fun bu ŋɔ min, “Mu daŋni i la biksi chenani Koosa fun sɛm pulum damata awa ba naa la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ awa ba koorsa wɛu.”

ref: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
text: Ta maksɛ Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam sali ta, domi ba naa vɛ zim Koosa chen, zi wa zu dooniu a chɔk tɔhɔn naa o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni, a val o daha ni pɛn fumnaasi dalɛ ni.

ref: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
text: Ba waa ya bugila lɛ ba yir ‘ɔlif’ awa fitinsa bar chiŋse kina balɛ. A waa nyɛn Koosa naa dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga, suwachak ni.

ref: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
text: Ba naa maga funɛ daŋni ba hɔɔsi a, din fun wa li ba nɔɔniu a kpu ba. Nannɛ ba yaa funɛ kpu ba dɔma.

ref: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
text: Saha naa ba funɛ biksi nara Koosa sɛm wɛ chen, ba funɛ kpak kpoku a nyɛ doon ta miili a nyɛ pulse sɛri a chi chɛl. Ba gbanko funɛ kpak kpoku a nyɛ wichɔɔrta kinkan wa kpaksi nara a chi chenani ba daŋni.

ref: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
text: Ba funɛ biksi Koosa sɛma a peti, sel zen naa kpak kpok kinkan funɛ wa li bɔ naa vɛ kpak bar peti chenu a chem ba yo niu. O fun nyaŋi ba a kpu ba maga,

ref: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
text: a nyɛ ba bara chɔ tɔhɔn zen naa ba fun kpaasi ba kunyana a mari daakakli ti, nyinbaan ni. Ba gbanko funɛ yir tɔhɔn ti Sɔdɔm akoo Ijipt.

ref: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
text: Ba naa funɛ li pulum maga awa buuri a la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ, fun na ba bara ka ba chɔ dooni a ta nyɛ nara la sogli ba. Ba zi ya chenu wuzu butora awa wuzu bore.

ref: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
text: Sel zen ti wa kpu nara balɛ ta, tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga tusa funɛ sɔɔniu, domi ba nyɛ ba na namsiu kinkan. Ba funɛ zɔ zɔɔlɛu a kpa dɔŋa wa kina.

ref: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
text: Wuzu butora awa wuzu bore gantɛl ni, Koosa fun tum o siim weri ti di o lɛli ba a li ba sou ni. Nara funɛ na ba ka ba bu chiŋi, hansura fun kpak ba kinkan.

ref: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
text: Doone nara balɛ ta fun nu ta kpok a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ka o ŋɔ ba, “Ma zini kuwa bar nɔ.” Ba fun zini a kaali buudaha ka dobaal tɔ ba ka ba dɔma nyini ba.

ref: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
text: Tɛɛnɛ ti fun vaan zigsi kinkan a kpu ba nara tusugsa nɔpɛ a purigi tɔhɔn ti bufi a lisi bar digi a chɔk. Ba naa funɛ ka, hansura fun kpak ba hali a la gali ka ba nyɛ Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni wa jirima kinkan.

ref: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
text: Namsi bilɛ tiin ti funɛ wa peti, amaa o wa fun vusi ka naa ya butora tiin chen, wa ya. Malaikasa Nɔpɛ Tiina Fun Hu O Kaabul

ref: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
text: Doone malaikasa nɔpɛ tiina fun hu o kaabul. Mu fun nu ta kpoka a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ka a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana awa Kristo naa o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni kpak kpok a dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Waa la zi dɔ un hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
text: “Ya kunyan Koosa naa kpak kina maga kpok, i wa kpak piili aa nyɛn. Ya zaami hinu di i funɛ kpa i kpok zenu a piili a dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga.

ref: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
text: Saha telu di i na paan a lɛrgi ba diŋsa a di ba naa sitɛɛ, sɛria. Chendaha ba naa vɛ zim hin, fun na paan kinkan a du hin ni. Saha telu di i bɔ i witinsa ba naa ya i tumtumsa, awa i nara ba naa maga ya nizene awa niwisa a la nyɛ hin jirima, chem. Saha telu di i kpa ba naa funɛ chɔk tɛɛnɛ nɔ kina maga a ta ba yɔri.”

ref: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
text: Nihɛɛti ta funɛ sɛm peti, ba fun puk Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni, bulzɔɔnɔ, ka tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga fun na o tuha. Doonee ba fun na adaka awa doonani ba fun sɔbi di Koosa wa nara funɛ ya nɔ bɛgim. Nasɛha fun nyiksi a tɛsi. Tɛɛnɛ fun zigsi ka doonbe chesi kinkan.

ref: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
text: O funɛ wa duuri a daŋni o lul a la wi.

ref: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
