text: Dana zen digi funɛ gbaari un ni a li buudaha ni. Sel zen sɛm funɛ nyɛn doon. O funɛ kpak nyuuna nɔpɛ awa nyindaasa fire a chiksi koorsa nyuchigsa nɔpɛ.

ref: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
text: Sel zen ti fun purigi buudaha butora a lisi bar digi a kpa o munzuha a chaasi wula a ta tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Sel zen ti fun chiŋi nihaan naa la duuri chen suwachak ni, chen ni o lulɛɛ, o zi se be tiu.

ref: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
text: O fun lul nibabichɛla naa la zi wa bi a dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ nara maga wa kpok kinkannɛ. Ba fun wa fuksi be ti bu bɛgim a nyɛ Koosa naa kanɛ o jirima bar kani ni a li o tɔhɔn ti ni.

ref: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
text: Nihaan ti fun chɔ kaali nyabaan. Koosa funɛ kɔɔri bar nyɛni a nyɛ un dooni di o nyɛn ba nyini un wuzu tusuga awa kɔksa lɛ di fumnɔra.

ref: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
text: Doone Koosa malaikasa awa a nihɛɛ naa ba yir Maikil, fun lagmi dɔŋ ni di a la chem sel zen ti awa o malaikasa laal ni a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni.

ref: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
text: Koosa malaikasi ta fun nyaŋi sel zen ti wa o walɛ a fun vɛ nyɛ a bu nyɛn dooni.

ref: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
text: A fun duŋni a a ta tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni di a nyɛn dooni. Sel zen ti awa sintana ya kibɛgimnɛ. Waa ya naa ba yir dum ti a nyɛn a vusi a ya alizin tagna koora. Saha maga o yaa mur tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim magau.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
text: Doone mu fun nu ta kpok a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ka o ŋɔ, “Ya funɛ nyaŋi naa yaa funɛ fiksi ya niinbisa Koosa suwachak niu saha maga a duŋni un a ta. Koosa naa ya ya koor wuti, funɛ kpa Kristo kpok a gbaari o kpok ni a lisi naa la muksi ya chen a li ba bambaan ni.

ref: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
text: Chenani Kristo naa ya Koosa piisi be, fun sou awa chenani ya niinbisa fun biksi nara o wɛ a fun saksi di ba sou a nyɛ un, kin nama funɛ nyaŋi sintana.

ref: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
text: Ma nyɛ ya naa maga nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni tusa sɔɔni, amaa yɛrɛ o kaa wa du ba naa maga nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni domi sintana wa ma nyɛnɛɛ. O na paanu kinkan, domi o zimu di o nyɛni saha wa zi vusi.”

ref: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
text: Sel zen ti funɛ na di ba duŋni un a ta tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni chen, o fun la muksi nihaan naa funɛ lul nibabichɛla, kinkan.

ref: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
text: Ba fun kpa tiisi zen chɛnɛ lɛ a nyɛ haan ti ni di o za a la nyabaan ti, chen ni ba zi nyini un dooni a kok un a li sel ta ni bunsa tora awa pɛnsa nɔra.

ref: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
text: Doon sel zen ti fun nyɛ ni pal kinkan a li o nɔ ni a chi pul, chen ni o zi chuŋi un kaaliu.

ref: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
text: Amaa tɛɛnɛ funɛ puku a nyɔɔsi ni ti maga a suŋi nihaan ti.

ref: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
text: Sel zen ti funɛ na chenani yɛɛ, a paan fun bu si a du nihaan ti ni a gbaari. O fun bur a la chem ba naa maga tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ nɔ a ŋɔ nara ni di Yesu dɔ ba wa laal ni. Ba waa ya nihaan ti nibabichɛla bi lulɛ.

ref: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
text: Chen saha ti sel zen ti fun wa chiŋi teku ni nɔ ni.

ref: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
text: Doone mu fun na sel zen digi ka o li ni ni. O funɛ kpak nyuuna nɔpɛ awa nyindaasa fire a chiksi koorsa nyuchigsa kin fi o nyindaasi ni. Ba fun sɔbi sɛm nawara la pini Koosa zoma a sak o nyuuna nɔpɛ ta ni.

ref: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
text: Sel zen ti funɛ chi wiibagarɛ ka o naŋgere awa o nɔ chi gbogumta. Sel zen naa funɛ chiŋi ni nɔ ni chen, fun kpa o kpok kinkan awa chenani o dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga a kpa du o nin ni.

ref: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
text: Ba fun jɛɛmi un a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Anti zen a tel un akoo a kpak kpok di o wa un yo dɔŋa?” Ba gbanko funɛ jɛɛmi sel zen naa funɛ chiŋi ni nɔ ti ni, domi o funɛ kpa o kpok magau a nyɛ un.

ref: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
text: O fun pini Koosa awa o bar jɛɛmi dama wa ba naa maga nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni zoma.

ref: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
text: Ba fun nyɛ sel zen ti nyinbaan di o la chem Koosa nara wa laal ni a nyaŋi ba a kpa kpok a nyɛ un di o dɔ pulumsa awa buure ba naa maga la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ.

ref: If you have ears, then listen!
text: Ma cheŋi ma diŋsa a nu mu sɛm na nɛnɛ.

ref: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
text: O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ ba zi wa kpaksi ma bɔnɛ a tɔ dɛ a kpa chisi zene a kpu ma bɔnɛ. O lawa ma naa ya Koosa nara di ma namsi wa kanyita niu a kpak nɛnɛ hali o bɔ wa tel. Kin nama zi sir yau.

ref: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
text: Doone mu fun na sel zen digi ka o li tɛɛnɛ ni. O funɛ kpak nyindaasa lɛwa a chi piisi be a sɛm sɛm zene wa ta zaŋ ni.

ref: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
text: O funɛ chiŋi sel zen naa nyuha funɛ huusi a peti chen, gantɛl nɛɛ a kpa o kpok a muksi ba naa maga nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni di ba jɛɛmi sel zen nɔ.

ref: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
text: Sel zen naa funɛ li tɛɛnɛ ni chen, fun tum tum zene damata. Hali o funɛ nyɛ din li buudahau a tu wa tɛɛnɛ nara maga suwachak ni.

ref: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
text: Domi kpok naa sel zen naa nyuha funɛ huusi a peti chen, fun kpa a nyɛ un chen, o fun koti a tum tum zene a la mur tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga. O fun ŋɔ ba di ba kɔɔri vura o chi sel zen naa nyuha funɛ huusi ka o ha vɛɛn kpak miisa chen a jɛɛmi un.

ref: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
text: Ba funɛ kpa kpok a nyɛ naa funɛ li tɛɛnɛ ni chen di o nyɛ vur ti kinyini a koti a sɛm sɛma. Vur ti fun ŋɔ di ba kpaksi ba naa maga vɛ jɛɛmi un, a kpu.

ref: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
text: O gbanko fun kpa o titi dana a dani nara ba naa maga ya nibirige awa ba naa vɛ yaali, kin tima awa fara tima, ba naa ya yosa awa ba naa vɛ yaali a li ba nindilsa awa ba tilsa ni.

ref: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
text: Sel zen ti sɔn awa kɔksa nɔra di fumnɔra danɔra funɛ ya o dani. Nandigi wa fun kpak nyinbaan di o kpiŋi see o kpak dani nɔ a li un ni.

ref: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
text: Ma kpak zima, ma zi zimu di kɔksa nɔra di fumnɔra danɔra chiŋɛ baal digi sɔn bar chiŋi nɛɛ.

ref: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
text: Mu fun nu ta kpok a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni a chi ni chuuri gbɛntɛl ni awa nasɛha jimi kinkan. Ta ta gbanko funɛ chi nidamata la mo gɔnjisa.

ref: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
text: Ba naa wa piisi be ti funɛ chiŋi, ba se funɛ chem doonani Koosa jirima bar kani awa sela naasi ta wa nihɛɛta fumlɛ danaasi ta fun nyɛn, a lɔ bool fali. Kɔk di fumnaasi danaasi tusugsa ba naa piisi be ti fun lisi a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga, ba duŋduŋ funɛ koti a lɔ bool fali ti.

ref: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
text: Ba wa fun kpak haanasa akoo a daŋni nihaana yɔri yɔri a ya tu chaan tima. Piisi be ti funɛ lisi bau a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ni, chen ni ba wa zi bu waligi dɔŋ ni. Ba waa ya ba naa o fun te lisi a nyɛ Koosa ni.

ref: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
text: Doone mu fun nyini a na ka malaika funɛ za buudaha bar boli ni. O fun chuŋi Koosa laaboor weri ti di o la nyɛ pulumsa awa buure ba naa maga la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ a nyɛn tɛɛnɛ nɔ ni.

ref: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
text: O fun ŋɔ ba ta zaŋa ni, “Ma nyɛ Koosa wa jirima a kipɛgi un o zenzen daha. Waa funɛ naami buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ wa teku ni wa nise. Saha telu di o di nara maga sɛria. Chendaha o lawa ma jɛɛmi un chenu.”

ref: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
text: Malaikasa lɛ tiina fun za a tiŋ te tel tiin ti gantɛl a ŋɔ, “Tɔhɔn zen naa ba yir Babilɔn, fun chɔk. Chɔkumsi tuma a naa o nara fun tum, chi sin kpokre o fun muksi ba ba nyɔ.”

ref: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
text: Malaikasa tora tiin ti fun za a tiŋ abalɛ ta mun ni a ŋɔ ta zaŋa ni, “Ba naa maga la jɛɛmi sel zen naa funɛ li teku ni ni chen akoo o vura a nyɛ o kpa o dana a dani ba nindilsa awa ba tilsa,

ref: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
text: Koosa na paan kinkan a du ba ni a lɛrgi ba diŋsa. O paan kinkan chi sin kpokre ba vɛ kpa kundɔŋ a pulsi, naa o muksi ba di ba nyɔ. O zi nyɛ ba kpa bau a du din ni di ba namsi kinkan a li piisi be ti awa o malaikasa naa o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni suwachak ni.

ref: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
text: Din naa nyɛ ba namsi, nyɔɔsa zini buudaha hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ba naa funɛ jɛɛmi sel zen ti awa o vur ti a nyɛ o kpa o sɔn a dani ba, ba waa la namsi a vɛ hiisi.”

ref: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
text: Chendaha o lawa Koosa nara ba naa la tiŋ Koosa chiksɛ nɔ ka ba vɛ bur Yesu tiŋi ni, ba li lalaknɔ namsi wa kanyita niu.

ref: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
text: Doone mu fun nu ta a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ka o ŋɔ, “Sɔbi chenani mu kaa sɛm nan a dau. Ba naa tum tuma aa nyɛ ya kunyan ni a wa siti, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu.” Koosa siim weri ti fun ŋɔ a gbaari, “I sɛma ya wutirɛ.” Koosa zi bɔ ba ba chimu a nyɛ ba hiisi a li ba tum kpok kinkan ni ka ba tusa sɔɔni. Tɛɛnɛ Nɔ Maga Kina Kumi Saha Telu

ref: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
text: Mu fun nyini a na ka Yesu naa ya nibine be fun kani dobaal pilim daha ni. O funɛ chik salma nyuchigrɛ a kpak gorga diili o nin ni.

ref: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
text: Malaika fun li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dama a sɛm ta zaŋa a ŋɔ un, “Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina kumi saha telu. Zer i gorga a la kum.”

ref: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
text: Doone Yesu naa funɛ kani dobaal ti ni chen fun viigi o gorga a kum tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kina.

ref: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
text: Malaika digi fun li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dama naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni. O gbanko funɛ kpak gorga diilire.

ref: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
text: Malaikasa tora tiina naa la nyini din ti, fun li biŋbiŋ ti suwachak a fun tiŋ o gantɛl a sɛm ta zaŋa a ŋɔ a nyɛ naa o sak chen ni, “Zer i gorga diili a teŋsi bugil naa maga ba yir ‘vain’ ka a nɔna sɛri chen maga.”

ref: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
text: Malaika ti fun viigi o gorga ti a teŋsi bugil ta nɔna a lagmi a dau doonani ba hɛmsi a ni. Dooni awa Koosa paan kinkan ya kibɛgimnɛ.

ref: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
text: Doonani ba hɛmsi a ni wa Jɛrusalɛm wa bol. Ba funɛ hɛmsi nɔna naa malaika ti fun lagmi a dau chen, chɛl kinkan fun li. Chɛl ti maga funɛ li, o fun pal a tɔ tɛɛnɛ maali kɔksa lɛ a zini buudaha o wa nibine nyuha mak.

ref: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
text: Doone mu fun nyini a na ka dana zen naa nyɛ alaazibu fun kpak min, fun nyɛn buudaha ni. Malaikasa nɔpɛ fun kpak wichɔɔrta a naa kurgɛ, kina nɔpɛ a nyɛn dooni. Ba wa kaali a nyɛ wichɔɔrtisi ta kpaksi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga, chenani Koosa na paan a du ba ni a lɛrgi ba diŋsa zi wa petiu.

ref: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
text: Mu fun na Koosa nara ba naa maga vɛ jɛɛmi sel zen ti akoo o vura a vɛ nyɛ o kpa o sɔn awa kɔksa nɔra di fumnɔra danɔra a dani ba. Ba fun chiŋi a dugli bɛɛŋa naa funɛ pɛn a lili a chi diga ka din nyɛn o daha ni, a kpak gɔnjisa a naa Koosa fun kpa a nyɛ ba.

ref: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
text: Ba funɛ lɔ Mosis naa funɛ ya Koosa tumtumna awa piisi be ti bool a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana Koosa naa kpak kina maga kpok, alaazibu fun kpak ya i tum zene daha. I waa naa ya tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga koora. Tum naa maga i tum, a ya deŋliu a ya wuti.

ref: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
text: Ya kunyana, i duŋduŋ ya bambi chaani tiina. Chendaha o deŋlu di nibinsa maga kinyɛ hin jirima a ŋɔ ba dɔŋtim ni chenani i zen mak. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zi wa chiŋi i suwachak niu a jɛɛmi hinu, domi ba zimu di i tuma maga deŋlu.”

ref: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
text: Ba funɛ lɔ bool ti peti, mu fun na ka doonani Koosa fun lisi kinkan o bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni, zɔɔnɔ puk.

ref: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
text: Malaikasa nɔpɛ naa funɛ kpak wichɔɔrtisa nɔpɛ ta fun li dooni. A fun laari kipilima kinkan a wer ka salma gar we kirisi a bambe.

ref: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
text: Ba fun la chiŋi sela naasi naa chiŋɛ a go Koosa jirima bar kani ti chen digi suwachak ni. O fun kpa Koosa naa nyɛn hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ, paan kinkan awa chenani o lɛrgi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga diŋsa a du salma gbaŋsa nɔpɛ ni a nyɛ malaikasi ta ni.

ref: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
text: Koosa jirima awa o chaani wa o kpok kinkan funɛ chi nyɔɔsa su o bar jɛɛmi dam ti. Hali malaikasi ta nyɛ wichɔɔrsa nɔpɛ ta kpaksi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga a peti, nandigi wa kizu dooni.

ref: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
text: Doone mu fun nu ta a li Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam ti ni ka o fun ŋɔ malaikasi ta ni, “Ma kpa Koosa paan kinkan awa chenani o lɛrgi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga diŋsa naa nyɛn salma gbaŋsi ta ni a la bini a biti du ba ni.”

ref: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
text: Tel te tiin ti fun te la biti a dau tɛɛnɛ ni. Chɔɔra tagna a naa nyɛ nara namsi kinkan, fun nyɛn ba naa sel zen ti fun dani ka ba jɛɛmi o vur ti chen.

ref: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
text: Malaikasa lɛ tiina fun la biti a du teku ni ni. O fun burgi a chi siwi chɛl di kin naa maga nyɛn un ni siti.

ref: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
text: Atora tiina fun la biti a du pula awa nise ni a maga burgi chɛl.

ref: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
text: Mu fun nu malaika ti ka o ŋɔ Koosa ni, “I waa naa ya bambi chaani tiina a vɛ kpak piili aa nyɛn, chenani i na tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga galimsa deŋlu.

ref: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
text: Ba funɛ kpu i nara awa i witinsa ka ba chɛl li a chɔ tɛɛnɛ ni. O deŋlu wa i nyɛ ba chɛl ba nyɔ lalaknɔ.”

ref: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
text: Doone mu fun nu ta a li biŋbiŋ ti ni ka o ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana Koosa naa kpak kina maga kpok, chenani i na ba galimsa deŋlu a ya wuti.”

ref: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
text: Malaikasa naasi tiina fun la biti a du nha ni, chen ni o nunum kinkan fun na nyinbaan a suksi nara.

ref: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
text: Nha ti funɛ nyɛ o nunum kinkan ti suksi ba, ba fun ŋɔ nɔɔna a nyɛ Koosa ni, domi waa funɛ nyɛ wichɔɔrsi ta kpaksi ba. Ba wa fun gil ba tum tagna a bur a nyɛ un jirima.

ref: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
text: Malaikasa nyuun tiina fun la biti a du kin nawara sel zen naa funɛ li ni ni chen, dɔ. O koor ti bar nyɛni fun wa biri ka o nitiŋɛ fun na paan a dusi ba zuluma.

ref: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
text: Ba fun ŋɔ nɔɔna a nyɛ Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔhɔn ti ni ba chɔɔri ta awa ba namsi kinkan daha, amaa ba wa fun gil a li ba tum tagni ta ni.

ref: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
text: Malaikasa nɔra tiina fun la biti a du mɔɔga zen naa ba yir Yufretis. O maga fun hɛɛsi, chen ni koorsa ba naa kaa li nha bar yɛli a wa chen zi na nyinbaanu a wa.

ref: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
text: Doone mu fun na ka alizin tagna tora a chi sampɔrsa fun wa li sel zen naa duŋni nihaan ti awa sel zen naa li ni ni wa witiina naa vɛ zim Koosa, nɔɔni ni.

ref: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
text: Alizin tagna tora ta funɛ kpak kpok di a tum tum zene damata. Ba fun gba a lagmi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga koorsa awa ba walɛ, chen ni Koosa wuzu zen naa o zi wa di ba sɛria chen wa tel, ba zi chem unu a li laal ni.

ref: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
text: Maa nu chenani ya kunyana ŋɔ ma. O ŋɔ, “Mu zi bur wau ka ma vɛ zim mu wɛ a chi ŋmɛɛr. Chenani Koosa bu vɛ na ma galimsa chi ma gatiwa. Ma doso a kilaari a saha maga, chen ni mu wa wa, ma wa zi kpak bar kpal a chiŋi ma na hiisa nara suwachak ni.”

ref: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
text: Doone malaikasa nɔpɛ tiina fun la biti a du buudaha ni. Mu funɛ nu ta kpoku a li Koosa jirima bar nyɛni ni ka o ŋɔ, “Chenani mu di tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga sɛria duglɛ petiu.”

ref: There were flashes of lightning, roars of thunder, and the worst earthquake in all history.
text: Nasɛha fun nyiksi a jim ka tɛɛnɛ fun zigsi kinkan. Saha naa Koosa fun naami nibinsa hali chen saha ti, tɛɛnɛ ha wa zigsi chen.

ref: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
text: Babilɔn awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga tɔ zene fun chesi Koosa fun lisi chenani Babilɔn tima fun tum tum tagna damata a kpa o paan kinkan a du ba ni a lɛrgi ba diŋsa. O funɛ chi o nyɛ ba sinnɛ di ba nyɔ ba biti o maga a nyɔ.

ref: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
text: Doone malaikasa naa funɛ kpak salma gbaŋsa, digi fun wa ŋɔ min, “Sakaal tiin digi kanɛ mɔɔga damata bambaan ni. Kuwa a wa na chenani Koosa zi lɛrgi o diŋsa.

ref: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga awa ba koorsa wa un funɛ tum sakaal tuma. Chenani ba wa un fun tum chi sinnɛ ba nyɔ a dɛɛsi.”

ref: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun nyɛ malaika ti zer min a la nyabaan. Sel zen sɛm naa funɛ kpak nyuuna nɔpɛ awa nyindaasa fi funɛ nyɛn dooni. Ba fun sɔbi sɛm nawara la pini Koosa zoma kidamata a du un ni ka sakaal tiin ti fun zini un.

ref: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
text: O funɛ niŋi kibilima awa kisɛmwa a laari tula kina naa ba kpa salma awa buŋkul lilɛ a kɔɔri, kidamata. O funɛ kpak salma gbaŋa a li o nin ni a kpa kidoŋa a li o sakaal tum ni a du un ni.

ref: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
text: Ba fun sɔbi o sɔn naa mun soglee a sak o til ni. Ma nyɛ mu kpa un li gbampulim a ŋɔ ma. O mun ya, “min ya Babilɔn naa ya tɔ zen awa sakaal tima wa ba naa maga la tum tum doŋa kinkan niina.”

ref: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
text: Mu funɛ na di nihaan ti nyɔ Koosa nara awa ba naa ba fun kpu domi ba ha vɛ bur Yesu gantɛl tiŋi ni, chɛl a dɛɛsi. Alaazibu fun kpak min kinkan o daha.

ref: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
text: Malaika ti fun ŋɔ min, “Yɛrɛ i nyɛ alaazibu kpak hin chen ni? Nihaan ti awa sel zen naa kpak nyuuna nɔpɛ awa nyindaasa fi ka o zini un, o wa un kpak mun naa soglee. Mu zi ŋɔ hin chenani o duu.

ref: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
text: Sel zen naa i fun na chen, wa bu to o miisa ni. O wa zi vusi ka o li bɔ naa vɛ kpak bar peti chen ni a kaali din dama. Koosa funɛ te sɔbi ba naa maga la zi wa la o mi fali ti, sɔna a du o tɔn ti ni a zi naami tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. Ba naa kaɛɛ a na ka sel zen naa funɛ sou chen bu kpak miisa, alaazibu zi kpak bau kinkan.

ref: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
text: “Ninɛɛgɛ duŋduŋ la zi zim nan mun. Sel zen ti nyuuna nɔpɛ ta ya kuŋkule nyuuna nɔpɛ ka nihaan ti nyɛn a bambaan ni. Sel zen ti nyuuna nɔpɛ ta gbanko ya koorsa nɔpɛwa.

ref: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
text: Koorsi ta nara banyuu wa fun bu dɔ nandigi amaa digi ha vɛɛn dɔu. Ba ha wa vɔu nara banɔpɛ tiin ti koora. Ba wa vɔu un, o wa zi dɔ nara a vusi.

ref: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
text: Sel zen ti funɛ ya koor gbanko. O wa si o sou ni a bu kpak miisa, o zi kpa o titiu a gbaari koorsa nɔpɛ ta ni a dɔ. O wa dɔ peti, o zi la zu din dam tiu.

ref: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
text: “Sel zen ti nyindaasa fi ya nara ba zi wa vɔti ba koorsa. Ba wa vɔti ba, ba wa sel zen ti zi dɔ kerifi bɛgimu.

ref: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
text: Ba fun lagmi a la nɔɔni di sel zen ti zi ya ba kaŋkoorau.

ref: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
text: Ba wa ba walɛ zi la chem piisi be ti wa o nitiŋɛ ba naa o fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni ka ba vɛ bur a li o tiŋi ni, wa laal ni. Waa la zi nyaŋi koorsi ta, domi waa ya koorsa maga kaŋkoora.”

ref: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
text: Malaika ti fun bu ŋɔ min, “Lisi chenani nihaan sakaal ti fun kani mɔɔg damata bambaan ni. A waa ya pulumsa awa buure ba naa maga la sɛm sɛm kurgɛ.

ref: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
text: Sel zen ti wa o nyindaasa fi a naa ya koorsa zi ha nihaan tiu. O wa ba zi lɛrgi o gar were tau a ur o bara ni a kpu un a se a fu un wa din ni.

ref: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
text: O ya Koosa tutɔrɛ ba zi ya chen. O fun kpa un a du ba nyuuni ni di ba la nɔɔni a kpa sel zen ti a ya ba nihɛɛ hali Koosa sɛma wa ya wuti.

ref: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
text: “Nihaan sakaal tiina naa i na chen, ya Babilɔn tɔ zen ka o dɔ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim koorsa maga.”

ref: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
text: A gantɛl ni mu fun na malaika digi ka o li Koosa tɔhɔn ti a tu wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga. O fun kpak kpok kinkan a nyɛ doon maga chaani.

ref: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
text: O fun tɛsi wa ta zaŋ ni a ŋɔ, “Tɔhɔn zen naa ba yir Babilɔn, fun chɔk. Alizin tagna awa zimebisa a nawara doŋee kinkan duŋduŋ nyɛn dooni.

ref: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
text: O wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga funɛ tum sakaal tuma kinkan. O chi o funɛ kpa sin a nyɛ ba ba nyɔ. O wa ba koorsa gbanko fun tum sakaal tuma ka nikpiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn bawu maga ni, fun na nyɔɔra kinkan a li un ni.”

ref: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
text: Doone mu fun nu ta a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ka o ŋɔ, “Ma naa ya mu nara, ma waligi ma titi a li un ni. Ma wa un tum tum tagna, mu zi kpa ma a gbaari un niu a lɛrgi ma diŋsa.

ref: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
text: Mu lisu di o tum tagna iiu kinkan. Nibine zi vɛ koti a guri a.

ref: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
text: Waa funɛ nyɛ nara tum tum tagna kinkan. Ma nyɛ ba la tum tum tagna bilɛ lalaknɔ a nyɛ un. Chenani o wa ba tum sakaal tuma funɛ chi sinnɛ o kpa nyɛ ba. Ma nyɛ ba kɔɔri sin bilɛ lalaknɔ a la nyɛ un.

ref: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
text: O funɛ kpak kin naa maga o daŋniu ka nara fun nyɛ un jirima kinkan. Ma nyɛ ba la namsi un lalaknɔ chen ni o tuha zi chɔku. O yaa kpa o titirɛ a ŋɔ, ‘Min ya koor nihaan. Mu wa ya luhaan naa tuha chɔkɛɛ.’

ref: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
text: Nan daha, wichɔɔrtisa awa lɔsa zi vaan wa kpak unu o tuha chɔk, ka din fu un. Min ya naa ya ma kunyan Koosa zenu kinkan a zi di o sɛria.”

ref: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga koorsa ba naa wa un tum sakaal tuma, zi wa na tɔ zen tiu ka o bu din, ba zi wiu o daha.

ref: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
text: Hansura zi kpak bau kinkan o daha. Ba zi lagmi dɔŋ niu a chiŋi bar boli ni a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Yɛrɛ o du Babilɔn tɔ zen ti ni? Kerifi bɛgim tuhu nɛɛ Koosa na o galimsa a ta un yɔri.”

ref: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
text: Nikpine ba naa nyɛn bawu maga ni zi wiu o daha, domi nandigi wa bu ka a yau ba kina.

ref: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
text: Ba naa nyɛn dooni wa bura yausi ba salma, aniita, buŋkul lilɛ, buŋkul were, gar kurgɛ a naa ya kisɛma awa kibilima, kin nawara ba kpa daasa awa bɔl nyin wa sulmin wa lugɛ wa buŋkul zen a kɔɔri ka a malɛ kpok.

ref: No one will buy their cinnamon, spices, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, cattle, sheep, horses, chariots, slaves, and other humans.
text: Ba gbanko wa bura yausi ba ŋmaga, poora naa sɔɔra weree, tulale naa ba yir firaŋkinsens awa mɛr, sin, nu, sau, mɛ, nuhɛ, piisisa, zaagna, loori munzuta a naa zaagna lɛrgi, awa yosa.

ref: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
text: Nikpiŋɛ ta zi ŋɔ dɔŋ niu, “Ba naa maga nyɛn doon a la daŋni kinkan di ba yausi ya kin nama, situ. Kiwere a naa maga funɛ nyɛn dooni chen, kaalu. A wa zi bu bur wa.”

ref: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
text: Nikpiŋɛ ba naa funɛ na nyɔɔra kinkan a li nara tima ni chen, zi chiŋi bar boli niu a nyini chenani la ya. Hansura zi kpak bau kinkan ba wi o daha.

ref: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
text: Ba zi ŋɔ dɔŋ niu, “Yɛrɛ o du un ni? Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni, fun laari gar kurgɛ a naa ya kisɛma awa kibilima a di tula naa ba kpa salma awa buŋkul lilɛ wa buŋkul were a kɔɔri.

ref: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
text: Kerifi bɛgim tuhu nɛɛ, kin nama maga wa bu toli. “Niduse awa ba nihɛɛta wa ba naa nyɛn arŋsi ni a gba nidamata, fun lagmi a chiŋi a li bar boli ni,

ref: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
text: a na tɔ zen ti ka o bu din. Ba funɛ wi o daha a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, ‘Tɔhɔn nɔ funɛ zen a kaali tɔsi ta maga.’

ref: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
text: Ba fun zɔk hɛɛra a saksi ba nyuuni ni di ba biksi nara di ba tusa chɔku kinkan, a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, ‘Yɛrɛ o du un ni? Ba naa maga dɔ arŋsa yaa funɛ tum au dooni di ba na nyɔɔra a li o kidamati ni. Kerifi bɛgim tuhu nɛɛ, o kina maga wa bu toli.’ ”

ref: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
text: Malaika kpok fun zer buŋkul a chi nam a yo du teku ni ni a ŋɔ, “Nannɛ Koosa fun kpa o kpok a chɔk Babilɔn tɔ zen, ka o bu vɛ nyɛni.

ref: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
text: Ba naa la mo gɔnjisa a la hu gbetelsa awa kaabulsa wa fun bura ya a maga dooni. Ba naa kpak ba sɛmbiri tuma awa ba naa la nam sau wa fun bura ya a maga dooni.

ref: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
text: Nandigi wa zi bu suk fitina dooni akoo a lagmi o chɛnsa di ba wa di diwe o haan fali dam ni. Nikpiŋɛ ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni chen funɛ zim kpiŋi a kaali ba maga. Ba naa la tum tu bilima tumi a nyɛn dooni, fun mur nidamata.”

ref: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
text: Babilɔn tima dɔ ba naa funɛ ya Koosa witinsa awa o nara, sou. Hali ba dɔ ba naa maga ba fun kpu tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni, sou. Chendaha o fun lɛrgi ba diŋsa a ta ba yɔri.

ref: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
text: A gantɛl ni mu fun nu zɛmɛ kinkan ta zaŋa a li Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni ka ba ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa, domi o kpak jirima awa chaani kinkan wa kpok a lisi ya a li ya tum tagna ni hali a wa peti.

ref: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
text: Chenani o di nara sɛria deŋlu a ya wuti. O funɛ na sakaal tiina naa zen chenu galimsa a ta un yɔri, domi o funɛ nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga wa un tum sakaal tumwa. Waa funɛ lɛrgi o diŋna domi o funɛ kpu o tumtumsiwa.”

ref: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
text: Ba fun tɛsi a ŋɔ, “Din naa fu un chen, nyɔɔsa zini buudaha hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa chen daha.”

ref: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
text: Doone mu fun nu ta a li Koosa jirima bar nyɛni ni ka o ŋɔ, “O deŋlu di ba naa maga ya nizene awa ba naa vɛ yaali a ya o tumtumsa a la ɔm un, ba pɛgi un.”

ref: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
text: Mu fun bu nu zɛmɛ kinkan ta a chi ni chuuri kinkan awa nasɛha tɛsi, ka ba ŋɔ, “Ma nyɛ ya pɛgi Koosa. Waa dɔ kina maga a kpak kina maga kpok a ya ya kunyana.

ref: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
text: Waa ya nizen a kaali kina maga. Chendaha o lawa ya pɛgi unu ka ya tusa sɔɔni kinkan. Saha telu di piisi be ti kpa haan naa saksɛ o titi a pɔ.

ref: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
text: Ba funɛ nyɛ o haan ti nyinbaanu di o laari kichaani weri. A waa ya tum were naa Koosa nara fun tumi.”

ref: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
text: Malaikasi ta digi fun wa ŋɔ min, “Sɔbi chenani mu kaa sɛm nan a dau. Ba naa ba zi yir ba wa piisi be ti di diwe a li haan fali dam ti ni, ba tusa zi sɔɔni.” O funɛ sɛm nannɛ a peti a bu sɛm tama a gbaari, “Sɛm na ya Koosa wuti sɛmwa.”

ref: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
text: Mu fun gbiri o suwachak un di mu jɛɛmi un, amaa o funɛ ŋɔ minu, “Ta ya chen, domi mu wa hin awa i niinbisa ba naa funɛ ŋɔ nara ni di Yesu dɔ ba, ya o tumtumsa. Kijɛɛmi Koosa duŋduŋ.” Chenani Yesu fun biksi nara tima wuti ti awa chenani Koosa witinsi ta fun biksi, ya kibɛgimnɛ. Jɔn Fun Na Ka Nandigi Zini Zaag Pilim

ref: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
text: Doone mu fun nyini a na ka Koosa tɔhɔn ti bulzɔɔnɔ puk, ka zaag pilim chiŋi dooni. Ba funɛ yir naa la zini un chen, wuti tiina awa naa tiŋ o ŋɔ. O yaa funɛ la laal ni saha maga a la di o dɔma sɛria awa chenani deŋlee ni.

ref: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
text: O se funɛ chi dinfullɛ ka o fun chik salma nyuchik damata o nyuhu ni. Ba fun sɔbi sɔn naa nandigi vɛ zim o mun a gbaari un ni, a sak un ni.

ref: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
text: Takta ziŋi naa o fun laari chen maga fun tɔ wa chɛl ni ka ba yir un ‘Koosa laaboor weri ti’.

ref: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
text: Lanjima damata ba naa nyɛn Koosa tɔhɔn ti ni fun tiŋ o gantɛl a zini zaag pilima a laari kipilima were.

ref: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
text: Ba nyuhu tiina fun kpak chisi nɔsa lɛ o li o nɔ ni naa o zi lisi a nyaŋi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga. O zi sɔ bau wa kpok kinkan ni a hɛmsi bugil ta nɔna naa malaika ti teŋsi a lagmi a dau, a bar hɛmsi ni. Dooni awa chenani Koosa na paan kinkan a la zi lɛrgi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga diŋsa ya kibɛgimnɛ. Waa kpak kpok a kaali kina maga. Chendaha o zi koti a ya chen.

ref: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
text: Ba fun sɔbi sɔn a sak lanjimasa nyuhu tiin ti takta ziŋi awa o naŋgeri ni. O funɛ ya, ‘Mu ya koorsa maga kaŋkoora.’

ref: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
text: Mu fun na malaika ka o chiŋi nha suwachak ni. O fun tɛsi kinkan awa ta zaŋ ni a nyɛ zimebisa a naa maga la za buudaha ni a ŋɔ, “Ma wa lagmi a di diwe naa Koosa zi nyɛ ma kinkan.

ref: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
text: Doone mu fun na sel zen naa funɛ li ni ni awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga koorsa wa ba lanjimasa ka ba la di ba la chem naa la zini zaag pilim ti awa o lanjimasa wa laa ni.

ref: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
text: Koosa tɔhɔn lanjimasi ta fun nyaŋi tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga lanjimasi ta a kpaksi sel zen ti awa witiina naa vɛ zim Koosa. Witiin ti yaa funɛ tum tum zenewa sel zen ti suwachak ni di o mur ba naa sel zen ti fun kpa o sɔn a dani ba a la jɛɛmi o vura. Koosa tɔhɔn lanjimasi ta fun kpa sel zen ti awa witiin ti a la yo a du bɛɛŋ naa din bu saha maga ni ka ba nyini.

ref: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
text: Koosa tɔhɔn lanjimasi ta nyuhu tiina naa funɛ zini zaag pilim ti, fun lisi chisi nɔsa lɛ ti a li o nɔ ni a kpu tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga lanjimasa ba naa kaɛɛ. Zimebisa fun li buudaha a wa di ba puŋ a ka.

ref: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
text: Doone mu fun na jirima bar kanɛ dooni. Ba funɛ kpa kpok a nyɛ ba naa kanɛ dooni di ba di sɛriasa. Mu gbanko funɛ na ba naa tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga fun kpu domi ba funɛ biksi ba Yesu wuti wɛu. Ba wa funɛ jɛɛmi sel zen ti akoo o vura, a vɛ nyɛ o kpa o sɔn a dani ba tilsa awa ba nisa. Kristo fun lɛli ba a li ba sou ni a nyɛ ba wa un ya koorsa a dɔ bun tusuga.

ref: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
text: Ba naa Kristo fun te lisi a lɛli ba a li ba sou ni, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu. Ba zi ya Koosa awa Kristo dam kɔkɔɔrsau ka ba wa un dɔ bun tusuga. Saha ti wa peti ka Koosa zi wa na ba naa kpalimi chen galimsa a ta ba yɔri, o wa zi wa la nɔɔni di o ya chen a nyɛ ba. Koosa Fun Nyaŋi Sintana Awa O Walɛ A Li Laal Ni

ref: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
text: O zi la gbau a mur tɛɛnɛ nɔ nara a lagmi ba wa ba naa ba yir Gɔg awa Megɔg di ba la chem Koosa nara wa laal ni. Ba zi wa iiu kinkan a chi mɔɔga hɛchɛ naa nandigi vɛ koti a guri.

ref: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
text: Ba zi wa li bawu maga niu a go Koosa tɔhɔn ka o nara nyɛn dooni. O daŋni dooni bar nyɛni kinkan. Koosa zi wa tum dinu o li buudaha a wa chɔk ba naa maga go un.

ref: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
text: Doone ba zi zer sintana naa gbaɛɛ a mur nara a yo du a gbaari sel zen ti awa witiina naa vɛ zim Koosa a li bɛɛŋ naa din di saha maga ni. Doonee ba wa un zi namsi kinkan hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Koosa Fun Di Tɛɛnɛ Nɔ Tim Maga Sɛria

ref: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
text: Doone mu fun na jirima bar kani naa zenee a pilimi a wa na naa kani un ni. Tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga awa buudaha vɛ nyɛn o suwachak ni, amaa bar digi wa fun to a zi sogli.

ref: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
text: Mu fun na tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga ba naa funɛ ya nibirige awa ba baa funɛ vɛ yaali ka Koosa lɛli ba a li ba sou ni. Ba funɛ chiŋi o jirima bar kani suwachak nɛɛ. Ba fun puk Koosa mi fali tɔn ti awa tɔni naa ba fun sɔbi ba tuma a du a tuhu ni. O fun di ba sɛria a li chenani ba sɔbi a du tɔni ta ni.

ref: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
text: Ba naa funɛ siti ka ba sogli ba tɛɛnɛ awa teku ni ni awa ba naa funɛ nyɛn nisitɛ bar nyɛni ni, ba maga funɛ chiŋi dooni. O fun di ba sɛria a li tum naa ba tum ni.

ref: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
text: Ba fun kpa sou kpok awa nisitɛ bar nyɛni a yo du bɛɛŋ naa din nyɛn saha maga ni di a wa chɔk.

ref: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text: Ba naa ba ha vɛ sɔbi ba sɔna a du Koosa mi fali tɔn ti ni, ba fun kpa ba a yo du bɛɛŋ ti gbanko ni.

ref: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
text: Doone mu fun nyini a na ka buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga wa teku ni fun nyuksi. Koosa funɛ naami buudaha awa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga kifalɛ di a chiŋi a bar chiŋi ni.

ref: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
text: Mu fun nu ta kpok a li Koosa jirima bar kani ni ka o ŋɔ, “Kinu mu wati. Lalaknɔ Koosa awa nibinsa zi lagmi nyɛnu. Ba zi ya o narau ka o ka ya ba Koosa.

ref: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
text: O zi fiisi ba sine magau a ta a li ba se ni. Sou awa namsi wa bara wiha wa zi bu nyɛn. Chenani o fun te du wa bu toli.”

ref: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
text: Doone naa funɛ kanɛ a li Koosa jirima bar kani ni, fun ŋɔ min, “Mu naami kina maga kifalɛ.” O fun bu ŋɔ min, “Sɔbi chenani mu kaa ŋɔ hin nan a dau, domi mu sɛma deŋlu a ya wuti.

ref: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
text: Mu tum peteu. Mu vɛ kpak piili a nyɛn a vɛ kpak peti. Mu mi fali naa mu kpa nyɛ nara ni chi nisire. Ba naa maga nyɔksa kpak, mu zi nyɛ ba nyɔ dooniu yɔri.

ref: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
text: Ba naa maga kpak nɛnɛ hali a la tel o bɔ, mu zi nyɛ ba nyɔ dooniu. Mu zi ya ba Koosau ka ba ka ya mu bisa.

ref: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
text: Amaa ba naa bur a li mu tiŋi ni a li nara gantɛl a la tiŋ doŋ tuma a la kpu nara a la tum chɔkumsi tuma a la tum tu bilim tuma a la jɛɛmi vursa a ya nusun tima, mu zi na ba galimsau a kpa bau a yo du bɛɛŋ naa din nyɛn saha maga ni a ta ba yɔri.” Koosa Jɛrusalɛm Fali Wɛ

ref: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
text: Doone malaikasa naa funɛ kpak salma gbaŋsa a kpa wichɔɔrtisa nɔpɛ a du a ni, digi fun wa ŋɔ min, “Kuwa mu biksi hin piisi be ti haan.”

ref: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
text: Koosa siim weri ti fun nyɛ malaika ti zer min a la zini bi ziŋziŋi nyuha. O fun biksi min Jɛrusalɛm fali naa Koosa fun lisi a nyɛ o titi ni ka o li o tɔhɔn ti a tu wa tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga.

ref: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
text: Koosa funɛ kpa o jirima awa o chaani kinkan a du un ni ka tɔhɔn ti funɛ chaani a chi buŋkul lilɛ.

ref: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Ba funɛ sa chechepure mɔgli ziŋirɛ a go un. O funɛ kpak bulzɔɔnɔ fidalɛwa ka malaikasa fidalɛ il bulzɔɔnɔsi ta. Ba funɛ sɔbi Ju tim buure nara fidalɛ sɔna a marigi a ni.

ref: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
text: Bulzɔɔnɔsa tora funɛ chem nha bar yɛli. A batora chem pul mun. A batora ka chem nha bar cheli. A batora ka chem pul nyuha.

ref: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
text: Ba fun kpa buŋkul fidalɛ a sɔbi piisi be ti nitumɛ nara fidalɛ sɔna a sak a ni a kpa a du chechepure ti zɛchik ni.

ref: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
text: Malaika naa funɛ sɛm min funɛ kpak da zaamsirɛ a li o nin ni di o zaamsi tɔhɔn ti awa o chechepure ti wa o bulzɔɔnɔsi ta.

ref: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
text: Tɔhɔn ti bar mɔgle ta funɛ mak dɔŋwa. Malaika ti fun zer o da ti a maksi un. O funɛ ya tusuga awa kɔksa nyuunwa a go a la kaali a chem a vɛɛn ziŋ buudaha ni chen.

ref: Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about
text: O fun maksi chechepure ti biksi di o ya naŋgere kɔksa lɛ di fidanɔrawa. Malaika ti funɛ maksi a chi nara maksirɛ.

ref: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Ba funɛ kpa buŋkul wer naa ba yir ‘jasipa’ a sa chechepure ti a kpa salma naa la lɛli a chi diga a sa damsa.

ref: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
text: Ba funɛ kpa buŋkul were fidalɛ naa ba yir ‘pil’ a kɔɔri bulzɔɔni fidalɛ ta, a kpa salma weri a chi diga a kɔɔri nyinbaanasa.

ref: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
text: Ba wa fun sa Koosa bar jɛɛmi dama, domi ba naa nyɛn dooni funɛ jɛɛmi ba kunyana Koosa naa kpak kpok a kaali kina maga awa piisi be ti bawu maga ni.

ref: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni wa funɛ nyini nha awa pɛn se ni di a nyɛ ba chaani. Koosa awa piisi be ti jirima awa ba chaani kinkan funɛ nyɛ ba chaani.

ref: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zi val o chaani ti niu awa ba koorsa zi kpa ba jirima a wa gbaari un niu.

ref: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
text: Ba wa zi far bulzɔɔnɔsi ta, domi dusum wa toli. A yaa puk chene saha maga.

ref: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
text: Tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga zi kpa ba jirima kinkanu a wa gbaari un ni.

ref: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
text: Tum tagna nitumɛ awa ba naa tum chɔkumsi tuma a la suni wɛ, ba wa zi koti a zu dooni. Ba naa ba fun sɔbi ba sɔna a du piisi be ti mi fali tɔn ti ni duŋduŋ la zi zu dooni.

ref: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
text: Malaika ti fun biksi min Koosa mi fali pul naa la chaani a chi diga. O funɛ li Koosa awa piisi be ti jirima bar kani nɛɛ,

ref: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
text: a pal tu la tɔhɔn ti nyinbaan zen bambaan ni. Koosa mi fali bugila funɛ nyu a chiŋi pul ti nɔsi ni, a ya fun nɔn nɔni fidalɛwa bun bɛgim. A kpachaga nyɛ tɛɛnɛ nɔ tim maga na alaafɛ.

ref: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
text: Kin nawara Koosa fun ŋɔ nɔ a nyɛ wa zi nyɛn tɔhɔn ti ni. Koosa awa piisi be jirima bar kani nyɛn dooni. Chendaha ba tumtumsa duŋduŋ nyɛn dooni a la jɛɛmi ba.

ref: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
text: Ba na Koosa seu ka o fun sɔbi o sɛn a marigi ba tilsa,

ref: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
text: a nyɛ ba ya koorsa hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ. Dusum wa fun to dooni, domi ba kunyan Koosa la nyɛ ba chaani. Ba wara daŋni nha akoo fitinsa nyɛ ba chaani dooni. Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Di O Zi Bur Wau Lalaga

ref: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
text: Doone malaika ti fun ŋɔ min, “Chenani mu kaa ŋɔ hin deŋlu a ya wuti. Ya kunyana Koosa naa funɛ kpa o siim weri ti a du o witinsi ta ni, funɛ tum o malaika di o biksi o tumtumsa chenani la zi wa ya lalaga.”

ref: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
text: Yesu ŋɔ ma, “Ma nu mu sɛm na. O wa zi vusi ka mu bur wa. Ba naa maga se su chenani mu sɔbi a li tɔn nɔ ni, ba tusa zi sɔɔni.”

ref: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
text: Min, Jɔn, funɛ nau a nu kin nama maga. Mu funɛ na a nu kin nama peti, mu fun gbiri malaika naa funɛ biksi min, suwachak ni di mu jɛɛmi un.

ref: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
text: Amaa o funɛ ŋɔ minu, “Ta ya chen, domi mu wa hin awa i niinbisa ba naa ya Koosa witinsa ka ba tiŋ tɔn nɔ sɛma nɔ, ya Koosa tumtumsiwa. Kijɛɛmi o duŋduŋ.”

ref: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
text: O fun bu ŋɔ min, “Ta kpa sɛm naa nyɛn tɔn nɔ ni a sogli, domi o wa zi vusi ka a zi ya wuti.

ref: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
text: Chen saha ti wa tel, ba naa la tum tum tagna awa tum doŋa, ba zi tum au a gbaari. Ba naa ka la tum tum were, ba zi tum au a gbaari. Ba naa ya tu chaan tima gbanko tusa zi chaaniu a gbaari.”

ref: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
text: Yesu ŋɔ, “Ma nu mu wati. O wa zi vusi ka mu wa bɔ ma chim a li chenani ma tum.

ref: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
text: Mu vɛ kpak piili a nyɛn a vɛ kpak peti.

ref: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
text: “Ba naa chaksi ba kilaarɛ di a ta kpak doŋa a kpak nyinbaan di ba zu tɔhɔn ti bulzɔɔnɔ a di Koosa mi fali bugili nɔna, ba tusa zi sɔɔniu.

ref: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
text: Amaa ba naa la tum tum doŋa a la tum tu bilim tuma a la tum chɔkumsi tuma a la kpu nara a la jɛɛmi vursa ka ba tusa sɔɔni awa nusun ni, ba maga zi wa kpalimi tɔhɔn ti dentil niu.

ref: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
text: “Min, Yesu, funɛ tum mu malaika di o mɔɔli kin nama maga a nyɛ ba naa maga la tiŋ min ni. Min ya koor Devid be luli. Mu chaani kinkan chi chau wul.”

ref: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
text: Koosa siim weri ti awa piisi be haan ti la ŋɔ, “Ma wa.” Ba naa maga nu ba sɛma zi gbaari a ŋɔ, “Ma wa.” Ma naa maga nyɔksa kpak, ma wa nyɔ Koosa mi fali ni yɔri. Jɔn Mak Tɔn Nɔ Banɔwa

ref: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
text: Min, Jɔn, la te ba naa maga nu tɔn nɔ sɛma, nin kinkan. Nandigi wa kpa kundɔŋ a gbaari mu sɛm nama ni, Koosa zi kpa wichɔɔrtisa a naa malaikasa nɔpɛ ta kpaku a gbaari chenani o kaa lɛrgi o diŋnau.

ref: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
text: Nandigi lisi kundɔŋ a li tɔn nɔ ni, Koosa wa zi nyɛ o zu o tɔhɔn ti a di o mi fali bugili nɔn a naa wɛ mu ŋɔ ma a li tɔn ti ni.

ref: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
text: Naa chiŋɛ mu sɛm na gantɛl ni ŋɔɛɛ di, “Wuti o wa zi vusi ka mu bur wa.” Nyɛ o ya a chi chenani i ŋɔ chen. Ya kunyana, bur kuwa.
